Deputy Press Secretary contact for Governor Kemp

source_title: Georgia Lands 350 New Jobs in E-mobility Manufacturing | Georgia

facts: Lockheed Martin is celebrating 75 years of continuous operations in Marietta, Georgia.
The company's Cobb County manufacturing facility supports more than 5,600 Georgia jobs.
Governor Brian P. Kemp and First Lady Marty Kemp joined company officials and state, local, and federal leaders to celebrate the milestone on June 17, 2026.
Lockheed Martin announced 1,200 new Marietta-based jobs as part of this milestone.
Since 2019, Lockheed Martin has increased its Georgia workforce by more than 25%.
The company added over 1,200 jobs at its 8-million-square-foot Marietta facility alone since 2019.
Statewide, Lockheed Martin employs more than 6,300 people in Georgia.
In 2025, Lockheed Martin contributed $4.5 billion to Georgia’s economy.
An additional 550 employees are based at Naval Submarine Base Kings Bay.
More than 100 employees are based at Robins Air Force Base.
The C-130J Super Hercules is Georgia’s top export and anchors the state’s second-largest manufacturing industry.
The aerospace and aviation industries generated more than $23.9 billion in GDP in 2024.
At its Marietta center of excellence, Lockheed Martin produces the C-130J Super Hercules and the F-35 Lightning II Center Wing Assembly.
The facility supports the ongoing sustainment of the F-22 Raptor, C-5 Super Galaxy, and P-3 Orion.
The Marietta facility is home to the Skunk Works® division.
The C-130J and F-35 programs alone support more than 20,000 direct and indirect Georgia jobs through robust supplier networks.
Marietta has been the global home of C-130 Hercules production since 1955.
More than 2,700 C-130s have been built at the site and delivered to over 70 operators worldwide.
The manufacturing line is America’s last remaining production capacity for building aircraft for the airlift mission.
Careers at Lockheed Martin in Marietta span advanced manufacturing, engineering, AI, data analytics, IT, finance, operations, and more.
Lockheed Martin invests in Georgia’s future workforce through partnerships with the University System of Georgia, Technical College System institutions, and K-12 STEM education.
These workforce efforts align Georgia’s education pipeline with employer needs as part of Governor Kemp’s Top State for Talent initiative.
Nearly 2,000 veterans, reservists, and military spouses are employed directly by Lockheed Martin in Georgia.
Marietta employees give back 21,000 volunteer hours to schools and organizations each year.
Georgia’s nine military bases offer competitive advantages to Lockheed Martin and other defense and advanced manufacturing companies.
source_title: Georgia Applauds Lockheed Martin’s 75 Years of Production, Impact | Top State for Talent

canonical_name: ARPA-E SCALEUP Ready Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO)
facts: ARPA-E has issued the SCALEUP Ready NOFO to support scaling promising ARPA-E-funded technologies into early commercial products.
The announcement facilitates collaboration among potential performing teams to respond to the program.
The NOFO provides specific program goals, technical metrics, and selection criteria, with terms being controlling.
The program supports innovative energy research and development that complements ARPA-E’s focus on early-stage transformational energy technologies requiring proof of concept.
The NOFO will remain open until closed or modified, with applications accepted at any time while open.
ARPA-E’s mission is to develop and deploy transformational energy technologies supporting U.S. national security and economic competitiveness goals.
ARPA-E funds R&D for energy technologies that reduce foreign energy imports, reduce energy-related emissions including greenhouse gases, and improve energy efficiency across economic sectors.

The authorizing statute directs ARPA-E to ensure the U.S. maintains a lead in advanced energy technologies by accelerating transformational advances industry would not undertake due to technical and financial uncertainty.
Technologies achieving substantial advancement under ARPA-E may face technical and commercial challenges post-funding, risking being stranded in their development path.

Pre-commercial scaling projects aim to translate bench-scale performance to commercially scalable versions, integrate technology with broader systems, provide extended performance data, and validate manufacturability and reliability.
Successful scaling projects enable industry stakeholders to justify financial, personnel, manufacturing facility, and material commitments for commercial deployment.
The program seeks to scale the most promising technologies previously funded by ARPA-E.
Unadopted ARPA-E funded technologies leave intellectual property vulnerable to foreign competitors, harming U.S. national competitiveness in global energy markets.
ARPA-E encourages organizations with entrepreneurial scientists and engineers along with commercialization and financial entities across diverse sectors to participate.
Interdisciplinary and cross-sector collaboration is encouraged to accelerate scientific and technological outcomes previously viewed as extremely difficult or impossible.
ARPA-E is compiling an optional Teaming Partner List for SCALEUP Ready to help form project teams and identify collaborations.
The Teaming Partner List will be available on ARPA-E eXCHANGE starting in October 2024.
The list will be updated periodically as long as the NOFO remains open to reflect new partners.
Organizations can join the Teaming Partner List by providing Organization Name, Contact details, Organization Type, Area of Technical Expertise, and a Brief Description of Capabilities.
Submitters must specify interest as a project team lead or partner, describe sought partners if leading, and describe roles and capabilities if partnering.
Submission consents to publication of the provided information.
ARPA-E does not endorse, evaluate qualifications, pay for information, or compensate respondents for the Teaming Partner List.
Responses submitted via email or other means will not be considered; participation is voluntary and has no bearing on NOFO application evaluation.
This notice does not constitute a NOFO; applicants must refer to the SCALEUP Ready NOFO issued on October 2, 2024 for application instructions and funding terms.
Questions about the NOFO should be directed to the designated program contact.
source_title: DOE: ARPA-E eXCHANGE: Funding Opportunities

entity_name: Augusta EV & Manufacturing Supply Chain Investments
facts: In April, Syensqo began construction on a new battery-grade polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF) manufacturing facility in Augusta that may well become the largest PVDF production facility in North America.
Syensqo was born from a demerger of Solvay.

Chief Technology and Innovation Officer and Chief North America Officer Mike Finelli explained how the decision underscores “the strategic importance of Augusta as a key player in the EV supply chain along the burgeoning Battery Belt.”
Syensqo leaders reinforced that local and state partnerships were deciding factors in choosing Augusta to support the company’s future.
Augusta Mayor Garnett Johnson thanked Syensqo in return, highlighting their more than two decades in the community as “a premier, quality employer.”

The Augusta Economic Development Authority echoed that sentiment, sharing, “Syensqo fits the community’s goal of creating well-paying, skilled manufacturing jobs and shows once again that Augusta-Richmond County is the right place for manufacturing to not only locate but to grow and expand.”
StandardAero has been operating in Augusta since 1974.
The company services approximately 425 aircraft and 500 turbine aircraft engines each year from their current facility.
Projects for a new hangar and engine shop officially kicked off in April 2024.
Vice President and General Manager of StandardAero’s Augusta facility Chris Bodine stated, “The new facility will add over 60% of new space to our existing footprint.”
The current facility was built in the 1970s when aircraft were a lot smaller.
The new facility will allow support for additional super mid-size to large cabin aircraft for airframe and avionics while significantly expanding the engine shop.

President of StandardAero Business Aviation Tony Brancato added that the expansion will allow work on larger Dassault Falcon, Gulfstream, and Bombardier aircraft and meet rapidly growing MRO needs of HTF7000 series engine operators.
The new facility will be adjacent to the Augusta Regional Airport, one of eight regional commercial airports in Georgia that play a key role in supporting the state’s soaring aerospace industry.

Aerospace and defense products totaled $11.1 billion in exports in 2023, making them the largest Georgia export by dollar value and landing Georgia as a top five state for overall aerospace exports in the U.S.

The state’s logistics infrastructure includes Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport, deepwater ports in Savannah and Brunswick, and thousands of miles of rail and highway systems to help companies move products to global markets.
GF Casting Solutions AG has plans for a state-of-the-art, LEED-certified high-pressure die casting facility in Augusta targeting the U.S. e-mobility market.
President Carlos Vasto explained that building the facility is a very long-term investment that will complete their footprint to become truly global in the e-mobility market.
The GF Casting Solutions facility is estimated to begin operations in 2027.
It will produce cast aluminum parts with a special focus on large structural components for the automotive industry.
The facility will be built at the Augusta Corporate Park, a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) site.
GRAD-certified sites are one of the speed-to-market solutions that Georgia offers.
GF Casting Solutions will work with Georgia Quick Start to develop its future workforce.
At the time of the announcement, Vasto shared, “The location is ideal for addressing the thriving automotive industry in the region.”
Over the last few years, Georgia has asserted itself as a core piece of the rapidly developing “Battery Belt” and e-mobility industry in the Southeast.
Georgia possesses an abundance of development-ready sites.
Georgia has a skilled workforce, including cybersecurity talent nurtured at the Georgia Cyber Center.
Georgia has robust logistics infrastructure.
Georgia has a reliable, low-cost energy grid supported by long term investments such as Plant Vogtle in Waynesboro, near Augusta.
The state offers attractive incentives such as a low corporate tax rate, workforce training programs, and a high quality of life.
Communities in every corner of the state benefit from the state’s business-friendly environment.
Augusta was featured on the 2024 International VIP Tour, which gave members of Georgia’s Consular Corps a first-hand look into factors that make Georgia unique.
The tour highlighted how five featured manufacturers built their success in east Georgia.
source_title: Three Investments Spring to Life in Augusta | Georgia

entity_name: Syensqo New Battery-Grade PVDF Manufacturing Facility
facts: Groundbreaking and start of construction on Syensqo’s new battery-grade PVDF manufacturing facility in Augusta occurred on April 25, 2024.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), executive leadership from Syensqo, and local/state representatives attended the groundbreaking ceremony.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia is developing an e-mobility supply chain to position the state as a leader in this industry for years to come.
The new facility will create approximately 100 new full-time, highly skilled, local manufacturing jobs in Richmond County.
At full capacity, the facility’s products will provide material for more than 5 million EV batteries per year.
The project is expected to create hundreds of jobs throughout the value chain.
Syensqo was formed from the demerger of Solvay in December 2023 and operates as a standalone listed company.
Syensqo’s portfolio addresses electrification, lightweighting, advanced connectivity, resource efficiency, improving quality of life, and sustainable sourcing.
The company’s U.S. headquarters in Alpharetta and existing Augusta manufacturing facility currently support more than 800 jobs in Georgia.

Mike Finelli, Chief Technology & Innovation Officer and Chief North America Officer at Syensqo, thanked GDEcD for supporting the expansion and noted it underscores Augusta’s strategic importance as a key player in the EV supply chain along the Battery Belt.
The new facility is located at 3702 Clanton Road in Augusta.
The plant will supply polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF), which is used as a lithium-ion binder and separator coating in electric vehicle batteries.
The Augusta plant will be the largest PVDF production facility in North America.
The facility will hire mechanics, technicians, engineers, and other roles.

Augusta Mayor Garnett Johnson noted Syensqo has been part of the Augusta community for more than two decades as a premier employer and expressed excitement over the expansion adding quality jobs to serve the community for years to come.

Augusta Economic Development Authority Chairman Wayne A. Gossage, Jr. stated the project fits the community’s goal of creating well-paying, skilled manufacturing jobs and confirms Augusta-Richmond County as an ideal location for manufacturing growth.

Senior Project Manager Adela Kelley represented the GDEcD Global Commerce team on the competitive project in partnership with the Augusta Economic Development Authority, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia EMC.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson highlighted that companies are making once-in-a-generation investments to keep up with the electrification of the mobility industry and praised state leadership under Governor Brian Kemp for attracting e-mobility and battery investments.
The Syensqo Augusta site partners with WJBF, Piedmont Hospital, and Security Federal Bank on the Giving Your Best Award to recognize community volunteerism.
Syensqo is supporting Georgia higher education institutions, including Augusta Tech, with workforce development projects.
In 2022, Syensqo opened a New Applications Development Lab at its R&I Center in Alpharetta to support customers in the EV, renewable energy, and aerospace industries.
The Alpharetta lab inauguration was attended by HRH Princess Astrid of Belgium, high-ranking Belgian officials, and State of Georgia leadership.
Over the last four years leading up to 2024, Syensqo has invested more than $30 million in its Alpharetta site.
source_title: Syensqo Breaks Ground on New EV Material Facility in Augusta | Georgia

entity_name: Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing Loan Program
canonical_name: Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing Loan Program (ATVM)
facts: The Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing Loan Program (ATVM) provides loans to support the manufacture of eligible advanced technology vehicles and qualifying components, including newly authorized modes from the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
Expanded uses beyond light-duty vehicles include medium- and heavy-duty vehicles, trains or locomotives, maritime vessels including offshore wind support vessels, aircrafts, and hyperloop.
IRA removed the $25 billion cap on ATVM loan authority.
IRA appropriated $3 billion in credit subsidy to support these loans.
The program is administered by the Loan Programs Office.
Funding amount consists of $3,000,000,000 of credit subsidy appropriations under IRA, resulting in an estimated $40,000,000,000 of direct loans.
Funding mechanism is direct loans.
Recipients include a manufacturer of eligible advanced technology vehicles or of qualifying components.
Period of availability remains open for commitment through 9/30/2028.
Tribal eligibility is Yes.
Formula funding is No.
Cost share requirement is N/A, but the loan amount cannot exceed 80% of eligible project costs.

Loans fund projects that manufacture a range of advanced technology vehicles and their components, including light-duty vehicles, medium- and heavy-duty vehicles, locomotives, maritime vessels including offshore wind vessels, aircrafts, and hyperloop.

Funds may be used for the costs of providing direct loans for reequipping, expanding, or establishing a manufacturing facility in the United States to produce, or for engineering integration performed in the United States of, newly authorized types of advanced technology vehicles only if those vehicles emit, under any possible operational mode or condition, low or zero exhaust emissions of greenhouse gases.
source_title: Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing Loan Program | Department of Energy

facts: Georgia's portfolio of incentives begins with a tax credit to spur job creation.
The incentive portfolio includes an array of other credits for investing in R&D, shipping through ports, and making key expansion investments.
Georgia tax exemptions are just as robust: they can save businesses lots of money in startup and operating costs.
All incentives are anchored by a 5.19% corporate tax rate based on single-factor apportionment.
The state utilizes a tiered system for tax credits.
An Entertainment Industry Tax Credit is available ranging from 20-30%.
source_title: Business Incentives | Select Georgia

facts: POSCO HOLDINGS INC. is making an equity investment alongside Hyundai Motor Group to jointly construct an electric arc furnace steel mill in Louisiana, USA.
The total contribution by POSCO HOLDINGS will be approximately USD 582 million (approximately KRW 858.6 billion).
POSCO HOLDINGS will establish a wholly-owned special purpose company named POS-Louisiana (tentative), which will subsequently invest in 20% of Hyundai Steel Louisiana LLC shares.
The acquisition cost is listed as KRW 858,561,094,870.
The investment represents a 2.6% ratio to equity capital and is classified as a large-scale corporation.
POSLouisiana (tentative) will be incorporated in Delaware, USA.
The scheduled acquisition date is December 31, 2027, which marks the final year of capital payment for the steel mill construction.
Payments will be executed in stages during the construction period, and schedules are subject to change based on project progress.
The exchange rate used for conversion was KRW 1,474.10 per dollar as of December 16, 2025.
The investment aims to respond to changes in the North American steel market and secure a foundation for sustainable automotive steel production.
This disclosure confirms details previously reported by The Financial News on April 22, 2025, regarding POSCO investing in Hyundai Motor’s US steel mills to boost synergy in the EV supply chain.
The ratio of total assets for this investment is 59.1% based on POSCO HOLDINGS INC's statement of financial position as of December 31, 2024.
The board resolution date for this decision was December 16, 2025, with two outside directors present.
Han, Young-Ah, Senior Vice President, signed the report on behalf of POSCO HOLDINGS INC.
source_title: Form 6-K

facts: Total planned investments in Georgia’s e-mobility and battery ecosystem topped $27.3 billion in the five years between 2018-2023.
The first Georgia-made electric car, the EV9, prepares to roll off the line this year at Kia Georgia in West Point.
Georgia Quick Start is helping the state meet the demands of the clean energy boom.
Georgia Quick Start recently unveiled plans to support the training of more than 8,500 Georgians for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
CNBC ranked Georgia as the No. 1 state for infrastructure in the U.S. in 2023.
Georgia has two deepwater ports, one of which is ranked among the fastest growing and the other a top port for automotive Ro/Ro in the country.
Located near the center of the state is the busiest airport in the world, Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.
Georgia's transportation assets are connected to cities across the state by a seamless network of interstates, highways, and rail lines.
Reliable energy grids are diversifying Georgia’s energy mix through renewable options, including solar energy and through Plant Vogtle.
The state is supporting the availability of low cost energy, including solar and hydrogen.
Georgia’s mobility ecosystem consists of original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) Kia, HMGMA, Rivian, and Archer Aviation.
Battery manufacturers in Georgia's ecosystem include SK Battery America and LG Energy Solution.
Metal and battery-material recyclers in the ecosystem include Aurubis AG and Ascend Elements.
Hydrogen fuel cell manufacturer Plug Power is part of Georgia's mobility ecosystem.
Synthetic graphite manufacturer Anovion Technologies is part of Georgia's mobility ecosystem.
At CES 2024 in Las Vegas, Hyundai Motor Group (HMG) unveiled plans for the Clean Logistics Project.
Under the Clean Logistics Project, HMGMA will deploy XCIENT Fuel Cell tractors to manage logistics at the new Metaplant in coastal Georgia.
Georgia’s college and university systems are a prime incubator for the kinds of collaboration and innovation needed to support the quickly evolving e-mobility field.
source_title: Unlocking Innovation in Georgia’s $27 Billion Mobility Industry | Georgia

facts: Between July 1, 2023, and June 30, 2024 (Fiscal Year 2024), GDEcD’s Global Commerce team supported 429 facility expansions and new locations.
These FY2024 expansions and new locations resulted in more than $20.3 billion in investment statewide.
The FY2024 projects committed 26,900 new private sector jobs across Georgia.
83% of the FY2024 expansions and new locations are directed to communities outside the 10-county Atlanta region.
Rural Georgia expansions represent more than 18,300 new jobs and $12.5 billion in investment.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that his administration is committed to prioritizing growth in rural Georgia and investing in education, workforce development, and infrastructure.
Metro Atlanta attracted nearly $7.8 billion in investments as a logistics and business hub during FY2024.
Statewide, new locations account for $12.8 billion in investments, while expansions are responsible for 9,800 new jobs.

Lt. Governor Burt Jones highlighted the necessity of workforce pipeline development through the Technical College System of Georgia, University System of Georgia, College and Career Academies, and private-public partnerships.
Speaker of the House Jon Burns noted tax cuts, regulatory relief, and historic investments in education, workforce development, and infrastructure as key drivers of economic success.
Governor and First Lady Kemp led economic development missions to Korea and Switzerland during FY2024.
These missions focused on strengthening relationships with Hyundai Motor Group and its supplier network while promoting Georgia as a premier business destination.
In the prior year, Governor and First Lady Kemp led similar missions to Israel, France, and Switzerland.
Georgia landed more than 8,100 new jobs and $5.9 billion in investments from international companies expanding or locating in the U.S. market during FY2024.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson cited Georgia's strong partnerships and diverse industry landscape as major advantages for businesses.
The state celebrated over 50 years of continuous business and trade representation in Europe and Japan, which remain top sources of foreign direct investment.
The coming fiscal year will mark 40 years of continuous representation in Korea, a partnership that earned GDEcD the James A. Van Fleet Award from the Korea Society.
Markets where Georgia maintains business representation accounted for 29% of total new jobs and investments in FY2024.
GDEcD Commissioner Kristi Brigman highlighted state achievements across mobility and technology sectors during a decade of excellence.
Manufacturing, including aerospace, automotive, and e-mobility sectors, accounted for over 50% of expansions or new locations in FY2024.
Kia Georgia added the EV9 to its lineup at its facility in Troup County.
Toyota Industries Corporation established a campus in Jackson County.
StandardAero operates facilities in Richmond County.
International life sciences company Gerresheimer announced a recent expansion in Fayette County.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America suppliers announced new locations in Bryan, Candler, Chatham, Coweta, Effingham, Harris, and Laurens Counties.
SOLARCYCLE, an innovator in solar panel recycling, announced plans for a new location in Polk County to support Qcells’ operations.
Investments in food processing and agribusiness totaled more than $1.3 billion in FY2024.
Japanese probiotic beverage company Yakult located in Bartow County to support East Coast operations.
Korean bakery CJ Foodville located in Hall County to support East Coast operations.
Walmart will source milk from local dairy farmers at a future dairy processing facility in Lowndes County.
Technology and corporate hubs are creating at least 4,400 new jobs as a result of FY2024 wins.
Cargill Corporation is establishing a new office hub in the City of Atlanta.
U.S. Soccer’s future headquarters and national training center will be located in Fayette County.
Georgia ranked No. 1 state for business from Area Development magazine for 10 consecutive years.
Georgia earned the No. 1 spot in Site Selection magazine’s annual Prosperity Cup rankings for successful investment recruitment in 2023.
Georgia was awarded a Gold Shovel by Area Development for significant success in attracting new business investment and job creation in 2023.
Business Facilities magazine ranked Georgia the No. 1 state for EV investments.
Business Facilities magazine ranked Georgia a top 10 state for food processing, cybersecurity, aerospace and defense, and AI growth hubs.
source_title: Gov Kemp: Georgia Job Creation Remains Strong | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

canonical_name: Internal Revenue Code Section 30D New Clean Vehicle Credit
facts: The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (P.L. 119-21) or Working Families Tax Cut was enacted on July 4, 2025, and revised dates and provisions in the Internal Revenue Code affecting tax credits.
Internal Revenue Code § 30D provides a credit of up to $7,500 to qualified buyers of new clean vehicles.
To be eligible for the credit, the Qualified Manufacturer (QM) must meet requirements related to qualifying content of critical minerals and battery components (CMBC).
Beginning in 2024, eligible vehicles cannot contain battery components manufactured or assembled by a Foreign Entity of Concern (FEOC).
Beginning in 2025, eligible vehicles also cannot contain applicable critical minerals extracted, processed, or recycled by a FEOC.
In May 2024, DOE published its final interpretive guidance on the definition of a Foreign Entity of Concern.
The Treasury Department concurrently released a final rule on clean vehicle tax credits, including 30D, in May 2024 that cross-references the DOE finalized FEOC interpretive guidance.
DOE is supporting the IRS in conducting an upfront review to ensure vehicles are eligible for the § 30D credit.
The upfront review ensures QMs are compliant with FEOC and CMBC requirements for the calendar year in which the QM anticipates submitting a periodic written report to the IRS.

The qualified manufacturer must submit a Compliance Report to the DOE, including supporting documentation and calculations regarding qualifying content of battery components and applicable critical minerals, as well as associated constituent materials, contained in the clean vehicle battery.
This Compliance Report will be used to establish a compliant battery ledger.
The IRS will make a determination with respect to the submission, with analytical assistance from the DOE, and notify the qualified manufacturer of its determination.

If a Qualified Manufacturer submits information to DOE by July 1 of the year prior to the calendar year for which the compliant battery ledger is being established, DOE and the IRS will complete their review and determination by October 31.
If a Qualified Manufacturer submits information to DOE after July 1, the upfront review will be conducted on a rolling basis but may not be complete prior to the beginning of the calendar year.
IRS Revenue Procedure 2023-38 and Revenue Procedure 2024-26 specify content requirements for submissions.
The 30D Compliance Report Submission Portal (30D Portal) allows Qualified Manufacturers to submit the Annual Compliance Report to the Department of Energy for upfront review.
This review covers FEOC and Critical Mineral and Battery Component (CMBC) compliance requirements.
Qualified Manufacturers are encouraged but not required to use provided templates for their submissions.

Available submission resources include the 2024 FEOC Compliance Report template, 2025 and beyond FEOC and CMBC Compliance Report template, Data sheet template, Data sheet template - Unlocked, Frequently Asked Questions (March 17, 2025), and the 30D Compliance Report Submission Portal User Guide (Revised August 2025).

DOE hosted a virtual informational webinar for Qualified Manufacturers on Thursday, October 3, 2024, at 12:00 p.m. Eastern Time to provide comprehensive guidance on viewing IRS determinations through the portal.
By providing data for the program, manufacturers voluntarily disclose proprietary data to determine eligibility of clean vehicles for IRC Section 30D tax credit purposes.
Manufacturers represent and warrant that they are authorized to disclose this data per the Terms of Use.
The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE), the Internal Revenue Service (IRS), and supporting contractors will have access to the provided data.
The data will be used by DOE, IRS, and contractors to evaluate compliance with FEOC requirements set forth in IRC Section 30D(d)(7).

Compliance evaluation criteria include eligibility requirements, definitions for qualified manufacturer, description of the proposed supply chain, battery components, critical minerals, allocation-based accounting, and other factors described in IRS Section 30D guidance.
Based on this data, the IRS will make a determination on clean vehicle eligibility for IRC Section 30D tax credit purposes with analytical assistance from DOE and notify the manufacturer.
Provided data will be treated as confidential and proprietary to the fullest extent allowable under federal law.

DOE and IRS reserve the right to disclose data if they believe in good faith it is appropriate or necessary to take precautions against liability, protect against fraudulent/abusive/unlawful activity, investigate/defend against third-party claims, comply with audit/inspection/copying requirements, or comply with open records laws.
Data may also be disclosed in any other manner to which the manufacturer consents.
source_title: 30D New Clean Vehicle Credit | Department of Energy

entity_name: DOE Funding Opportunity Announcement DE-FOA-0003371 FY24 Scale-up of Integrated Biorefineries
canonical_name: DOE Funding Opportunity Announcement DE-FOA-0003371 FY24 Scale-up of Integrated Biorefineries
facts: Supports higher risk bioenergy technologies that are ready to scale, ultimately demonstrating feedstock-conversion variations or production pathways to support commercialization.
Aims to meet the Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) Grand Challenge goal of 35 billion gallons per year of SAF production by 2050.
Aims to meet the Clean Fuels and Products Shot goal of 50% projected demand for maritime, rail, off-road, and renewable carbon-based chemicals by 2050.

Funds cost-shared research, development, and demonstration projects with partners in industry, academia, and National Laboratories focused on design, construction, operation, and validation of new technology and feedstock pathways for engineering scale integrated biorefineries.
Intent is to reduce technology uncertainty and risk through cost-shared pilot- and demonstration-scale biorefinery projects.
Supports BETO’s multi-year strategic goals to decarbonize the transportation sector through production of cost-effective SAF and other strategic fuels.
Supports BETO’s multi-year strategic goals to decarbonize the industrial sector through cost-effective and sustainable chemicals, materials, and processes utilizing biomass and waste resources.
Topic Area 1 identifies, evaluates, and selects applications proposing project definition, development, and execution plans for scaling pre-pilot (TRL 5) biofuel technologies to pilot scale (TRL 6).

Topic Area 2 identifies, evaluates, and selects applications proposing project definition, development, and execution plans for scaling pilot (TRL 6) biofuel technologies to demonstration scale (TRL 7-8).
Topic Area 3 supports scale-up activities to produce organic chemicals from renewable biomass and waste feedstocks that can replace those currently derived from petroleum.

Topic Area 3 identifies, evaluates, and selects applications proposing project definition, development, and execution plans for scaling pre-pilot and pilot (TRL 5-6) biochemical technologies to pilot and demonstration scale (TRL 6-8).
Original Full Application submission deadline was January 16th, 2025 at 5:00PM ET.
Amendment 000001 extends the Full Application submission deadline to January 31st, 2025 at 5:00PM ET to allow sufficient time for development during end of calendar year activities.
All proceeding dates, such as Reply to Reviewer Comments and award notification dates for Research, Technology, and Economic Security review, were adjusted by an equal amount of time.
Table 3 was updated to align the Allowable Fuel Types with Table 1.
Required Concept Paper due date is November 7, 2024 at 5PM ET.
Full Application due date is January 31st, 2025 at 5PM ET.
The eXCHANGE system enforces hard deadlines for Concept Paper and Full Application submissions.
APPLY and SUBMIT buttons automatically disable at the defined submission deadlines to consistently enforce a standard deadline for all applicants.

Late submissions due to technical difficulties are accepted only if the applicant encountered difficulties beyond their control, contacted the helpdesk for assistance, and submitted the application through eXCHANGE within 24 hours of the FOA's posted deadline.

facts: An application was submitted to the Executive Secretary of the Foreign-Trade Zones Board by the Georgia Foreign-Trade Zone, Inc., grantee of FTZ 26, requesting temporary/interim manufacturing (T/IM) authority within FTZ 26 at the Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia, Inc. facility in West Point, Georgia.
The application was filed on May 7, 2008.
The KMMG facility is located at 700 Kia Parkway in West Point (Troup County), Georgia, designated as Site 1 T1.
The KMMG facility employs about 2,500 people.
Under the requested T/IM procedures, KMMG would produce up to 350,000 light-duty passenger vehicles (sedans, sport utility vehicles, minivans) annually for the U.S. market and export.
The produced vehicles fall under HTSUS codes 8703.23 and 8703.24.
Foreign components used in production represent about 25% of total material inputs at the facility.

Included foreign components are oils (HTSUS 2710.11), paints (3208.10, 3209.90), plastic tubes/pipes/hoses (3917.31, 3917.40), plastic sheets/strips/plates (3919.90, 3921.90), rubber tubes/hoses (4009.11, 4009.31), rubber belts (4010.31, 4010.33), tires (4011.20), gaskets/washers/o-rings (4016.93, 4016.99), carpet sets (5703.20), safety glass (7007.11, 7007.21), mirrors (7009.10), tube fittings (7307.22, 7307.99), fasteners (7318.14), locks/keys (8301.20, 8301.40), engines (8407.34), engine parts (8409.91), pumps (8413.30), valves (8481.80), and bumpers (8708.10).
The duty rates for the listed foreign components range from free to 8.6%.
FTZ procedures could exempt KMMG from customs duty payments on foreign components used in export production, which is estimated to be 10% of plant shipments.
For domestic sales, KMMG would be able to choose the duty rate that applies to finished passenger vehicles (2.5%) for foreign inputs that have higher rates.
Customs duties could possibly be deferred or reduced on foreign status production equipment.
Public comment is invited from interested parties, with submissions due by June 12, 2008.
A copy of the application will be available for public inspection at the Office of the Foreign-Trade Zones Board's Executive Secretary.
The notice was dated May 7, 2008, and signed by Andrew McGilvray, Executive Secretary.

facts: U.S. Senator Raphael Warnock (D-GA) traveled across Georgia last week to speak with manufacturing leaders, servicemembers, and port and manufacturing workers about job growth in the state.
The tour included stops in West Point, Warner Robins, Garden City, Savannah, and Pooler.

During the tour, Senator Warnock highlighted how the American Jobs Plan and the U.S. Innovation and Competition Act of 2021 will catalyze local economic growth, manufacturing, technological innovation, and job creation across Georgia.

Immediately following a two-day shutdown at Kia Georgia due to a global semiconductor shortage, Senator Warnock visited Kia's West Point factory to meet with local leadership about preventing future production halts.

Senator Warnock secured provisions in the bipartisan U.S. Innovation and Competition Act of 2021 that would provide funding to the federal Creating Helpful Incentives to Produce Semiconductors (CHIPS) for America program to increase domestic semiconductor production.

At Robins Air Force Base on Wednesday, June 2, Senator Warnock met with base and Georgia National Guard leadership to discuss current missions, technological advances, and resources for servicemembers and their families.
The base supports Middle Georgia through good-paying jobs and STEM field training.
The visit followed an announcement by Senator Warnock and United States Air Force Acting Secretary John P. Roth regarding four new operational missions coming to the base.
On Thursday, June 3, Senator Warnock visited the Port of Savannah, which is the fastest growing port in the country and the largest single container terminal in the country.
Georgia Ports officials stated that additional federal funding could strengthen import and export capacity by widening and deepening shipping ports.
Senator Warnock emphasized that passing the American Jobs Plan is vital for maintaining top-level port operations, strengthening Georgia's infrastructure network, and creating good-paying jobs.
He toured the Garden City Terminal while standing on a crane 152 feet above the ground.
Senator Warnock visited the SeaPoint Industrial Terminal Complex located off the Savannah River, which provides deep-water access to the Port of Savannah's main channel.
The SeaPoint complex supports sustainable innovation and high-tech job creation, featuring green technologies such as solar power panels and pollinator gardens housing over 240,000 honeybees.
Senator Warnock stressed the urgency of passing the U.S. Innovation and Competition Act to encourage sustainable solutions that create jobs in coastal Georgia.
At JCB Manufacturing's facility in Pooler, over 75 Georgians help manufacture and assemble vehicles that serve more than 115 dealers across the United States and Canada.

As a member of the Commerce and Agriculture Committee, Senator Warnock expressed continued support for JCB's manufacturing operations and their contribution to Georgia's economic health and agricultural industry.

Senator Reverend Warnock stated that the ripple impact of the semiconductor shortage negatively affects Georgia's economy and emphasized that investing in American products and domestic semiconductor production is necessary to lead.
source_title: ICYMI: PHOTOS: In Jobs for Georgia Tour, Senator Reverend Warnock Highlights Efforts to Support Georgia Jobs, Strengthen Federal Investments in Infrastructure and Industry » Reverend Raphael Warnock

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy announced the termination of 321 financial awards supporting 223 projects.
The terminations result in a savings of approximately $7.56 billion for American taxpayers.
DOE determined the terminated projects did not adequately advance the nation’s energy needs, were not economically viable, and would not provide a positive return on investment of taxpayer dollars.

The awards were issued by the Offices of Clean Energy Demonstrations (OCED), Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy (EERE), Grid Deployment (GDO), Manufacturing and Energy Supply Chains (MESC), Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy (ARPA-E) and Fossil Energy (FE).

Secretary Wright stated that on day one, the Energy Department began reviewing billions of dollars in financial awards, many rushed through in the final months of the Biden administration with inadequate documentation.

Secretary Wright noted President Trump promised to protect taxpayer dollars and expand America’s supply of affordable, reliable, and secure energy, and today’s cancellations deliver on that commitment.
Of the 321 financial awards terminated, 26% were awarded between Election Day and Inauguration Day.
Those awards awarded between Election Day and Inauguration Day alone were valued at over $3.1 billion.
In May 2025, Secretary Wright issued a Secretarial Memorandum entitled, “Ensuring Responsibility for Financial Assistance," establishing a new policy for evaluating financial awards.
The new policy authorized program offices to request additional information from awardees.

The policy required that awards be reviewed on a case-by-case basis to identify waste, safeguard taxpayer dollars, protect America's national security, and advance President Trump's commitment to deliver affordable, reliable, and secure energy for the American people.
Using this review process, DOE evaluated each award and determined they did not meet the economic, national security or energy security standards necessary to justify continued investment.
Award recipients have 30 days to appeal a termination decision.
Some of the projects included in the announcement have already begun the appeal process.
source_title: Energy Department Announces Termination of 223 Projects, Saving Over $7.5 Billion | Department of Energy

facts: Superior Essex Inc. reported first quarter 2008 financial results on May 1, 2008.
First quarter 2008 revenues were $757 million with earnings of $0.37 per diluted share.
Adjusted earnings per diluted share totaled $0.69 for the first quarter of 2008, a 50% increase over the prior year first quarter adjusted earnings per diluted share of $0.46.
Core Business revenues at constant copper increased 12% in the first quarter of 2008 compared to the prior year quarter.
Excluding acquisitions and currency impact, Core Business revenues at constant copper declined approximately 9%, as compared to the first quarter of 2007.
Stephen M. Carter serves as chief executive officer of Superior Essex.
The company's expanded energy/power product lines now exceed $600 million in annualized revenues.
Superior Essex announced plans to consolidate its global Magnet Wire manufacturing base, with benefits expected to begin being realized in late 2008.
The North American Magnet Wire factory consolidation project is expected to be completed by the end of 2008 with estimated annual cost savings of $7 to $9 million.

The company closed its Magnet Wire manufacturing facility in Vincennes, Indiana, incurring $10.1 million in special items including $2.1 million in cash charges and $7.5 million in non-cash accelerated depreciation charges.
Superior Essex is headquartered at 150 Interstate North Parkway, Atlanta, GA 30339.
The company manufactures and supplies wire and cable products for the Communications, Energy, Automotive, Industrial, and Commercial & Residential end-markets.
It is a leading manufacturer of magnet wire, fabricated insulation products, and copper and fiber optic communications wire and cable.
Total debt at March 31, 2008, was $356 million, with net debt of approximately $290 million.
Global Magnet Wire and Distribution operations reported adjusted EBITDA of $22.8 million for the first quarter of 2008, a 36% increase over the prior year.
The Communications Cable segment revenues declined 11% on a constant copper basis in the first quarter of 2008 due to reduced demand from major telecommunications customers.

Copper Rod business segment revenues for the first quarter of 2008 were reduced to $14 million as compared to $72 million in the first quarter of 2007, following a planned rationalization of production output.
Peggy Reilly Tharp serves as Director of Investor Relations for Superior Essex.
Superior Essex Inc. maintains its principal executive offices at 150 Interstate North Parkway, Atlanta, Georgia 30339.
The company is a manufacturer and supplier of wire and cable products for the communications, energy, automotive, industrial, and commercial/residential end-markets.
Superior Essex manufactures magnet wire, fabricated insulation products, and copper and fiber optic communications wire and cable products.
The company distributes magnet wire, insulation, and related products to smaller original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and motor repair facilities.
Superior Essex converts copper cathode to copper rod for internal consumption and for sale to other wire and cable manufacturers and OEMs.
The company operates manufacturing facilities in the United States, Canada, the United Kingdom, France, Germany, Portugal, Italy, Mexico and China.

entity_name: Hyundai Motor Group and SK On EV Battery Facility
canonical_name: SK On and Hyundai Motor Group Joint Venture EV Battery Facility
facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Hyundai Motor Group (HMG) and SK On have selected a site in Bartow County for a new electric vehicle (EV) battery manufacturing facility that will supply Hyundai Motor Group’s plants in the U.S.
Stakeholders estimate the project will create more than 3,500 new jobs through approximately $4-5 billion of investment in Bartow County.

Hyundai Motor Group and SK On recently signed a memorandum of understanding (MOU) regarding the partnership for a new EV battery facility in the U.S., with details of the partnership still in development.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that Hyundai Motor Group and SK On are valued partners and key players in our state's ever-growing automotive industry, and expressed pride in their investment in the No. 1 state for business.
Established in 2021, SK On is the lithium-ion battery subsidiary branch of SK Innovation and currently employs more than 2,000 Georgians at its SK Battery America facility in Commerce.
In July, SK Battery America announced it would partner with the Work for Warriors Georgia program to hire veterans, servicemembers, and their families at its Georgia operations.

Subject to execution of relevant agreements and HMG and SK On's final board decisions, the new facility will be located at Bartow Centre, a zoned manufacturing and industrial site located on Highway 411, aiming to begin operations in 2025.

Bartow County Sole Commissioner Steve Taylor expressed pleasure with the decision, thanked the owners of the former Bartow Centre Industrial property, and noted the project establishes Bartow in Georgia’s EV ecosystem while leveraging the area's advanced manufacturing talent base.

Director of Korean Investment Yoonie Kim represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project in partnership with Georgia EMC, Development Authority of Bartow County, the Cartersville-Bartow County Department of Economic Development, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that HMG and SK have been pioneering partners for Georgia for decades, driving the state's automotive renaissance, and highlighted efforts to create a fully integrated supply chain connecting battery manufacturers with recyclers.
Georgia is positioned as a hub for the electric transportation industry due to its prime location, major ports system, extensive infrastructure, skilled workforce, and pro-business climate.
Excluding this announcement, EV-related projects announced in the state since 2020 total approximately $17 billion in investment and more than 22,800 new jobs in Georgia.

Hyundai Motor Group separately announced in May its plans to invest $5.54 billion in Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, an EV manufacturing facility in Bryan County, breaking ground on the facility in October with Governor Kemp and other officials.

Hyundai Motor Group is a global enterprise with about 250,000 employees worldwide, creating a value chain based on mobility, steel, construction, logistics, finance, IT, and service, with mobility brands including Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis.

SK On was launched as an independent company in 2021 after SK Innovation split off its battery business, aims to become a world leader in the clean energy industry leveraging global production and R&D capabilities, is headquartered in Seoul, South Korea, and has battery plants operating or under construction across the U.S., Europe, and Asia.

links.context: Deputy press contact for Governor Kemp

source_title: Hyundai Motor Group and SK On To Build EV Battery Facility in Bartow | Georgia

entity_name: Department of Energy American Manufacturing Initiative
facts: The landing page for the U.S. Energy & Employment Report (USEER) was published on December 31, 2025.

An interactive web resource provides data on how the Inflation Reduction Act and Bipartisan Infrastructure Law are leading to announcements of historic levels of private sector investments in the United States, dated April 16, 2026.
DOE is making investments through its Office of Manufacturing and Energy Supply Chains, Advanced Materials & Manufacturing Technologies Office, and Loan Programs Office.
DOE is deploying a combination of loans, tax credits, rebates, and grants to shore up manufacturing capabilities through various key sectors.
DOE provides access to smart manufacturing tools and resources to help small and medium-sized domestic manufacturers secure and improve their operations and efficiency.

The Domestic Production Act is being employed as a tool to accelerate domestic manufacturing and deployment of key energy technologies, starting with the acceleration of domestic Heat Pump and heat pump component manufacturing nationwide.
DOE is targeting investments this decade to set the nation on a course to a modernized and upgraded energy system that leads the world.

DOE is securing and sustaining American clean energy supply chains by diversifying and expanding the supply of critical materials, developing alternatives and substitutes to those materials, and keeping those materials in the supply chain.

DOE is prioritizing materials and products circularity through re-use, recycling, and re-manufacturing to reduce environmental impacts and secure manufacturing supply chains while uplifting communities and creating high-paying jobs.
DOE is building the nation’s capabilities and capacity to manufacture current and next generation energy technologies by investing in entrepreneurs and small and medium-sized manufacturers.
Through government-enabled and private-led funding opportunities, DOE is investing in domestic manufacturing to secure the nation’s energy infrastructure and eliminate supply chain vulnerabilities.
Every dollar DOE spends on these investments must catalyze many times that amount in private sector investment.

To help facilitate, expedite, and streamline non-federal funding required by companies, DOE launched the Manufacturing Capital Connector platform to connect companies applying to DOE-administered manufacturing programs, like the Qualifying Advanced Energy Project Credit (48C) Program, to capital providers seeking high-quality projects.
These investments complement the extraordinary funding in the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law (BIL) and Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) and enable DOE to more fully unleash the benefits of new programs.
Nearly 16 million jobs have been created since 2021, with hundreds of thousands of those jobs in clean energy manufacturing.
Clean energy manufacturing roles include heavy equipment operators, autoworkers, pipe fitters, and linemen.

DOE uses market, economic, and supply chain security-related technical analysis, collaborating with experts through its Manufacturing and Energy Supply Chains Office to identify gaps and growth opportunities across the nation's energy supply chains, from raw materials to processing and manufacturing.
These analyses inform investment and program implementation.

DOE is ensuring benefits flow to communities at risk of being left behind, from today’s energy-producing communities facing a major energy transition to communities that have too often borne the downsides of past energy systems without sharing in economic upsides.
DOE is creating high-quality, accessible, and career-track jobs with fair wages and benefits and the free and fair choice to collectively bargain and join a union.

DOE is prioritizing domestic manufacturing needs and restoring U.S. manufacturing growth and global competitiveness across a range of technologies to strengthen solutions for an affordable, reliable, resilient, and secure energy system across America.
source_title: American Manufacturing | Department of Energy

entity_name: Florida Natural Gas and Alternative Fuel Laws & Incentives
canonical_name: Florida Natural Gas and Alternative Fuel Laws & Incentives
facts: Local governments in Florida may use income from an infrastructure surtax to provide loans, grants, or rebates to residential or commercial property owners to install electric vehicle chargers, propane fueling stations, and natural gas fueling stations, provided a local government ordinance authorizing this use is approved by referendum.
Natural gas and propane retailers in Florida must obtain a license from the Florida Department of Revenue.
Effective January 1, 2030, a retailer that does not hold a valid natural gas or propane license is subject to a penalty of 25% of the tax assessed on total purchases.

Before January 1, 2030, sales of natural gas fuel (including propane, compressed natural gas, and liquefied natural gas) are subject to two taxes per motor fuel equivalent gallon adjusted annually based on CPI change from a 2013 baseline: a State Comprehensive Enhanced Transportation System tax with a 2.9¢/gallon base rate, and an additional “privilege of selling natural gas fuel” tax with a 4.6¢/gal base rate rounded to the nearest tenth of a cent.

Beginning on January 1, 2030, propane, compressed natural gas, and liquefied natural gas used to fuel motor vehicles are subject to state motor fuel taxes consisting of an excise tax per gasoline gallon equivalent (GGE), a ninth-cent fuel tax, and a local option fuel tax, plus an additional variable component determined annually by the Florida Department of Revenue.
Starting January 1, 2030, the applicable state motor fuel taxes for alternative fuels are set at $0.02 GGE excise tax, $0.05 ninth-cent fuel tax, and $0.05 local option fuel tax.
Starting January 1, 2031, the applicable state motor fuel taxes for alternative fuels change to $0.04 GGE excise tax, $0.01 ninth-cent fuel tax, and $0.01 local option fuel tax.
To determine the variable tax component, the Florida Department of Revenue will require each propane and natural gas retailer to file monthly electronic reports beginning in February 2030.
For taxation purposes, one GGE is equal to 5.66 pounds or 126.67 standard cubic feet of compressed natural gas; 6.06 pounds of liquefied natural gas; and 1.35 gallons of propane.

Natural gas utilities in Florida may recover costs associated with renewable natural gas projects, including infrastructure and supply investments, through rates approved by the Florida Public Service Commission.

Any motor vehicle equipped with idle reduction technology may exceed gross vehicle or internal bridge weight limits by up to 550 pounds, provided the operator presents written verification of the certified weight and demonstrates the technology is fully functional at all times.
Natural gas vehicles (NGVs) may exceed gross vehicle or internal bridge weight limits by up to 2,000 pounds under the idle reduction exemption.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Natural Gas Laws and Incentives in Florida

facts: Manufacturing Georgia is the result of a collaborative effort involving many stakeholders who are deeply invested in the success of Georgia’s manufacturing industry.
The Georgia Association of Manufacturers (GAM) launched an initiative in 2023 to address outdated perceptions of manufacturing and increase the availability of skilled workers.
The initiative aims to engage future workers, especially students and young professionals, by changing the narrative around the industry.
GAM formed a strategic partnership with Unmudl, a Skills-to-Jobs Marketplace renowned for connecting learners with employers and focusing on mid-level technician skills.

Together, GAM and Unmudl developed a platform that blends social media-like capabilities with cutting-edge technology to directly connect manufacturers with students, job seekers, and the broader public.
The full rollout of Manufacturing Georgia is scheduled for 2025.
source_title: About Us

entity_name: Mines and Metals Capacity Expansion – Piloting By-Product Critical Minerals and Materials Recovery at Domestic Industrial Facilities
canonical_name: Mines and Metals Capacity Expansion – Piloting By-Product Critical Minerals and Materials Recovery at Domestic Industrial Facilities
facts: The page is a Notice of Intent (NOI) to issue Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (IIJA) Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) No. DE-FOA-0003583.
The planned NOFO is titled 'Mines and Metals Capacity Expansion – Piloting By-Product Critical Minerals and Materials Recovery at Domestic Industrial Facilities.'
The goal of the NOFO is to facilitate the development of large pilot-scale facilities to recover domestic critical minerals and materials (CMM).
Targeted materials include rare earth elements (REE), graphite, and other value-added products.
Recovery will occur from operating industrial facilities.
Example industries with byproduct production potential include phosphate, fertilizer, oil and gas, smelting, mining, and waste management.
Goal-related resources will be eligible in this program.
The NOI serves as a disclaimer that DOE may issue a NOFO significantly different than described, or may not issue a NOFO at all.
Any information contained within the NOI is subject to change.
No concept papers, letters of intent, or full applications are being requested nor accepted in response to this NOI.
The Department is not seeking comments on the notice and will not respond to questions concerning it.
Upon release of a NOFO, an avenue for potential applicants to submit questions will be provided.
source_title: DOE: NETL eXCHANGE: Funding Opportunities

facts: On October 27, 2020, Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Toyota Industries Compressor Parts America (TICA) will expand its Pendergrass, Georgia, production facility to meet growing demand for its onboard power electronics.
The company will invest $5.5 million in the project.
The expansion will create at least 27 new jobs in Jackson County over the next 18 months.
Toyota Industries Compressor Parts America’s Pendergrass facility currently manufactures air-conditioning compressors for automotive applications.
The announced expansion will allow a production line of DC-AC inverters.
The expansion will add 15,000 square feet to the plant.
This investment is part of the company’s preparations to supply more parts and products to its onboard power electronics market.
Governor Brian P. Kemp thanked Toyota Industries Compressor Parts America for being a part of Georgia’s $3 billion automotive industry.
Kemp stated the decision to expand is a testament to the hardworking Georgians of Jackson County and the strength of the automotive industry in the area.

Kemp noted the announcement speaks to the importance of Georgia's top-notch logistics network, which continues to attract equipment manufacturers from across the world to expand or plant roots in the Peach State.
Toyota Industries Compressor Parts America works in concert with the adjacent TD Automotive Compressor Georgia facility, which assembles the parts manufactured at the TICA plant.
The combined facilities encompass over 1 million square feet of production space.
The combined facilities represent a total investment of more than $1 billion.

Kota Otoshi, executive officer for the electronics division of TICA’s parent company Toyota Industries Corporation, stated the company would like to expand its electrical products from this plant and its onboard power electronics to its market in North America.
Otoshi expressed a desire to be a corporate citizen who can contribute to the economic and social development of Georgia through the production and sale of its onboard power electronics.
Parent company Toyota Industries Corp. announced the current TICA facility in 2012, which included an initial $350 million investment.
Toyota Industries Corp. supplies automotive compressors for Toyota Motor Corporation, as well as Chrysler, Ford, General Motors, and others.
Jackson County Commissioner for District 1 Jim Hix stated Jackson County is the perfect location for the automotive industry.
GDEcD Project Manager Joseph Huntemann represented the Global Commerce division on this competitive project in partnership with the Jackson County Development Authority.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted the company’s decision to add a new line to their Jackson County facility will mean additional jobs for Georgians.
Wilson cited Georgia's business climate, along with investments in workforce, logistics infrastructure, and quality of life as compelling reasons for continuing to grow there.
Wilson highlighted Toyota Industries Corporation investing in Georgia and in its rapidly growing electric vehicle ecosystem as a strategic growth sector for the state.
Georgia is home to more than 200 automotive-related facilities.

The state is a national leader in advanced manufacturing, outpacing the United States in 10-year GDP growth in the manufacture of products including machinery, electrical equipment and components, and fabricated metals.

links.context: Additional information about Toyota Industries Corp.

source_title: Toyota Industries Compressor Parts America Announces Jackson County Expansion | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: The Industrial Development Authority’s mission is to entice businesses to re-locate to Dade County and support businesses that are already here, so that the people of Dade County may benefit from good jobs and improved quality of life.
The authority aims to retain existing jobs.
The authority aims to create new job opportunities through the expansion of existing industry.
The authority aims to create new job opportunities through the attraction of new industry.
The authority aims to support capital investment by new and expanding businesses.
The authority works cooperatively with state government, local governments, and school board to further economic development.
The authority aims to increase the County’s tax base.
source_title: Industrial Development | Dade County, GA

facts: The GoTrenton! collaborative e-mobility pilot launched in September 2023 in Trenton, New Jersey.
The pilot was conceived and led by Isles, Inc., a community developmental and environmental organization.
It is one of the first subsidized rideshare efforts in the country to serve low-income communities.
Partners include the City of Trenton, ChargEVC, Environment NJ, and New Jersey Clean Cities Coalition (NJCCC), which serves as technical advisor.
NJCCC director Chuck Feinberg noted that 30% of Trenton residents are car-free households, limiting access to jobs, medical appointments, food services, and after-school programs.
Securing grants reduced ride costs to $2 per ride for the program.
Trenton has a poverty rate of 27%, making ride accessibility a key program consideration.
Isles Inc. led citizen engagement initiatives including surveys, listening sessions, community partnership agreements with local businesses, local ambassadors, and a stakeholder advisory committee.
Circuit Electric Mobility Solutions was awarded the GoTrenton! pilot contract for four electric shuttles and a mobile app following competitive solicitation.
The project team plans to expand operations to include additional electric shuttles, carshare vehicles, and vans in future phases dependent on funding and ridership.
During its first few months of limited operations, the program provided more than 1,100 rides serving over 1,600 passengers.
The project increased publicly available EV charging ports in Trenton by 20, with four owned by Isles and the remainder by the City of Trenton.
There are now 50 charging ports at nearly 20 charging stations within five miles of the city center.
Anticipated revenue from the charging stations will be used to subsidize program operations.
Feinberg and NJCCC staff secured over $1 million in funding through proposal requests and grants, drawing on decades of experience.

Coordination benefited from relationships with the New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection, New Jersey Board of Public Utilities, New Jersey Economic Development Authority, Delaware Valley Regional Planning Commission (DVRPC), and the local utility for charging installation.
NJCCC accessed technical assistance from the National Renewable Energy Laboratory as part of a U.S. Department of Energy partnership to advance alternative fuels nationwide.

Chuck Feinberg stated, “Being responsible for the entire state, I usually focus on the big picture, like reducing emissions through our work with large fleets. This project helped us drill down to demonstrate how Clean Cities coalitions can also help underserved communities with day-to-day needs.”

Feinberg and NJCCC co-director Caroline McCallum continue advising on scaling GoTrenton!, including route expansion and connecting residents to good-paying jobs at nearby warehouse and fulfillment centers.
NJCCC is targeting alternative fuel programs in the underserved communities of Newark next.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Electric Mobility Increases Access to Transportation in New Jersey

facts: Applying to a Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) from the Office of Clean Energy Demonstrations (OCED) can take several weeks.
Applicants must review the relevant Funding Notice for all submission deadlines and resources.
Applications must be submitted via the OCED eXCHANGE unless stated otherwise in the NOFO.
Questions regarding a specific NOFO should be sent to the email listed on the Funding Notice page or within the NOFO itself.
The OCED website provides comprehensive information on funding opportunities, mission, programs, news, project portfolio, leadership team, and career opportunities.
Interested applicants may view and download OCED Funding Opportunity Announcements on OCED eXCHANGE.
Applicants must register with required systems to apply to a NOFO, and high demand may cause longer than expected wait times for registration.
Applicants should start and complete the registration processes as early as possible due to potential delays.
Applicants must register with and submit NOFO application materials through OCED eXCHANGE, which is OCED’s online application portal.
Detailed instructions on how to apply are available at Financial Opportunities: Manuals (energy.gov).
The “Apply” and “Submit” buttons on the application portal will be automatically disabled after the listed submission deadlines.
Applicants experiencing technical difficulties with a submission should contact the OCED eXCHANGE helpdesk for assistance.
Each applicant must register in the System for Award Management (SAM) before submitting an application, unless excepted under 2 CFR § 25.110.
Designating an Electronic Business Point of Contact is a required step in SAM registration.
Applicants must provide a valid unique entity identifier (UEI) number in the application.
Applicants must maintain an active SAM registration with up-to-date information while holding an active federal award, application, or plan under consideration.
DOE will not make a federal award until the applicant complies with all applicable UEI and SAM requirements.
If an applicant fails to fully comply with UEI and SAM requirements by the time DOE is ready to make an award, DOE will determine the applicant is not qualified to receive it.
Entities should start the UEI and SAM registration process as soon as possible due to potential delays.
Technical difficulties with UEI validation or SAM registration can be addressed using the Help feature on SAM.gov.
Additional entity validation resources can be found at GSAFSD Tier 0 Knowledge Base - Validating your Entity.
To receive email updates on NOFO amendments, applicants must register with Grants.gov.
Applicants should click the “register” link in the top right corner of the grants.gov page and then the “get registered now” button to complete registration.
Applicants must register in FedConnect to apply.
Some NOFOs require applicants to submit a letter of intent or concept paper before submitting the full application.
Application submission deadlines are located on the cover page of each NOFO.
All letters of intent, concept papers, and applications must be submitted via OCED eXCHANGE unless stated otherwise in the NOFO.
If there are inconsistencies between the NOFO and non-legally binding DOE communications like presentations or staff statements, the NOFO takes precedence.
Applicants may submit questions via email to the address identified in the NOFO Questions/Agency Contacts section.
The Standard Form SF-424 is the government-wide standard form for grant application packages and requires basic applicant information including name, address, telephone number, and type of applicant.
The SF-424 requires a list of sources of proposed funding and a description of the proposed project.
Applicants must provide a Budget and a Budget Justification Workbook with their application.
For any subaward listed in the application, a separate Budget Justification Workbook must be provided for each subaward.
Applicants must use the Standard Form SF-424-A BUDGET INFORMATION - Non-Construction Programs to submit their budget.
