facts: Economic development projects in Georgia have surpassed 2021 fiscal year-end totals with over 35,000 new jobs and almost $13 billion in investments.

Governor Brian P. Kemp, in conjunction with the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), announced that through the third quarter of fiscal year 2022 (July 1, 2021 – March 31, 2022), job creation and investments resulting from economic development projects have already surpassed fiscal year 2021 year-end totals.
These 251 project locations supported by GDEcD’s Global Commerce team will result in the creation of more than 35,400 new jobs and $12.9 billion in investment for the state.
There was a 48% increase in jobs compared to three-quarters through the previous fiscal year and a 6% increase in jobs compared to the prior year’s overall total.
Additionally, there was a recorded increase of 54% in total investment compared to the same time period last year.
Nearly 78% of both projects and investments were outside the 10-county metro Atlanta area, and over half of the jobs created were outside that area.
Key industry sectors include automotive, advanced manufacturing, logistics and distribution, software and technology, and food processing.
Over the past year, Georgia’s electric vehicle (EV) and sustainability ecosystems have grown tremendously.

SK Battery America has partnered with American lithium-ion battery recycling and engineered materials startup Ascend Elements to recycle both cell and module lithium-ion battery manufacturing scrap from SK’s Jackson County facility.
Ascend announced its first commercial-scale battery recycling plant in Covington in January.

Aurubis, one of the leading recyclers of copper, precious metals, and non-ferrous materials worldwide, will invest $340 million in a ‘first-of-its-kind in the U.S.’ state-of-the-art recycling and secondary smelting facility for multi-metal recycling in Augusta.
Electrification solutions and sustainability leader Aspen Aerogels recently announced plans to locate their new 500,000-square-foot advanced manufacturing facility in Statesboro.
Aspen Aerogels will produce PyroThin materials that provide thermal management, mechanical stability, and fire protection properties to EV battery systems at its new Statesboro facility.
The No. 1-ranking workforce training program, Georgia Quick Start, helped secure numerous projects across key industries.
For more than 40 years, Georgia Quick Start has provided specialized training tailored specifically to business needs and is administered through the Technical College System of Georgia.
The Georgia Ports Authority’s (GPA) deepwater and regional ports projects have played a key role in securing diverse projects including Serena & Lily Inc.’s new facility in Effingham County.
For the past 19 consecutive months, the GPA has been reporting record-breaking numbers as the port expedites plans for long-term growth.

The Savannah Harbor Expansion Project (SHEP) was recently completed, deepening a 40-mile stretch between the port and the Atlantic Ocean to accommodate super-sized freighters traveling from the expanded Panama Canal.
Inland ports are attracting investments, such as the Appalachian Regional Port driving Roper Corporation’s decision to invest $118 million in expanding its facilities in Walker County.

The Georgia Department of Transportation maintains more than 1,200 miles of highway as part of Georgia’s highway and interstate system, which connects companies to 80% of the country in two days or less of driving time.
source_title: Georgia economic development surpasses 2021 FY totals

facts: Approximately 475 people were detained during an immigration raid at the Hyundai Megasite in Georgia.
The enforcement operation occurred at the Helga Battery Plant construction site within a sprawling 3,000-acre facility located west of Savannah, Georgia.

Steven Schrank, Special Agent in Charge of Homeland Security Investigations for Georgia and Alabama, stated the raid was part of an ongoing criminal investigation into allegations of unlawful employment practices and serious federal crimes.
The investigation had been ongoing for several months, with authorities receiving leads from community members and former workers.
A majority of the detained individuals were Korean nationals, with Korean media reporting as many as 300, though officials did not provide an exact nationality breakdown.
The detained workers were employed by a network of subcontractors and subcontractors for subcontractors on the site, rather than directly by the parent company.

Of the hundreds encountered on the premises, 475 were found to be illegally present or in violation of their U.S. presence, including those who crossed the border illegally, entered on visa waivers prohibited from working, or overstayed visas.
All detained individuals had their status, documents, and backgrounds checked before being turned over to ICE custody, with most initially transported to the Folkston facility.
Hyundai Motor Group, South Korea's largest automaker, began manufacturing electric vehicles at the $7.6 billion plant approximately one year ago.
The EV manufacturing plant currently employs about 1,200 people.
Hyundai partnered with LG Energy Solution to build an adjacent battery plant through their joint venture, HL-GA Battery Company.
The adjacent battery plant was slated to open the following year.
Construction at the battery site was paused to assist authorities following the raid, while operations at Hyundai's EV manufacturing plant continued without interruption.
Plant spokesperson Bianca Johnson confirmed that EV manufacturing operations were not interrupted by the enforcement action.
Gov. Brian Kemp and other officials had previously touted the Hyundai Megasite as the largest economic development project in Georgia's history.

South Korean Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lee Jaewoong stated that diplomats from the U.S. embassy in Washington and consulate in Atlanta were dispatched to the site, with plans to form an on-site response team.
Lee Jaewoong emphasized that "the business activities of our investors and the rights of our nationals must not be unjustly infringed in the process of U.S. law enforcement."
Hyundai issued a statement saying, "Our top priority is always ensuring the safety and well-being of our employees and partners. We will fully cooperate with the relevant authorities."
HL-GA Battery Company released a statement confirming it was cooperating fully with authorities and had paused construction to assist.
ICE spokesman Lindsay Williams confirmed that federal agents focused their enforcement operation specifically on the construction site for the battery plant.
No criminal charges were announced at the time of the press conference, and officials noted the matter remains ongoing.
There were no substantial uses of force during the operation, with only one detainee experiencing overheating treated on scene and one agent sustaining a minor laceration.
source_title: ICE raid in Savannah, Georgia: 475 people detained

facts: The 2024 legislative session in Georgia concluded on March 28, 2024.
The General Assembly passed several tax bills, including a substantial $500 million in tax cuts for both individual and corporate taxpayers in Georgia.
HB 1181 revises the tax incentive framework by reducing the carryforward period for key income tax credits from 10 years to five years.
HB 1181 affects essential credits like research and development, jobs, and investment tax credits, shortening the time they can be used against future tax liabilities.
HB 1181 sets sunset dates starting in 2025 for these and other specific incentives, requiring renewal through future legislation.
Both income tax rate bills apply to the entire 2024 tax year but take effect on July 1, 2024.
HB 808 raises the exemption threshold for tangible personal property taxes from $7,500 to $20,000, benefiting businesses by lowering tax liabilities and supporting investment.
HB 1162 aligns Georgia’s state income tax laws with the federal Internal Revenue Code (IRC), adopting the same tax rules and regulations as the federal government for that year.
HR 598 and HB 1267 lay the groundwork for creating a dedicated tax court in Georgia, with an operational start date of January 1, 2026, and beginning to accept cases on August 1, 2026.
source_title: Georgia’s 2024 Tax Legislation | Individual Income Tax Reduction

facts: Gov. Brian Kemp signed a state budget that includes more than $2 billion in tax relief, bonuses for teachers and state employees, and plenty of other new spending.
The amended $43.7 billion budget covers fiscal year 2026, which ends in June.
The budget includes $1.2 billion in income tax rebates for Georgians.
Single filers will receive up to $250 in income tax rebates.
Heads of household will get up to $375 in income tax rebates.
Married couples filing jointly will get up to $500 in income tax rebates.
Tax rebate checks are scheduled to go out later this year if legislators approve a separate bill authorizing the refund.
source_title: Gov. Brian Kemp approves taxpayer rebates of up to $500 this summer

facts: Federal agents detained roughly 475 people at a Hyundai plant in Ellabell, Georgia.
Officials called the operation the “largest single site operation” in Homeland Security Investigations history.
The ICE-led raid targeted the HL-GA battery plant, which is part of a mega site developed by the South Korean auto company.
HSI special agent in charge Steven Schrank stated the raid was months in the making.
Federal agents in tactical gear, supported by Georgia State Patrol, FBI, and DEA, entered the facility under a criminal search warrant tied to alleged unlawful employment practices.
The raid occurred on Thursday night, according to NewsNation local affiliate WSAV.
Steven Schrank stated that the detainees were “illegally present in the United States, or in violation of their presence in the United States, working unlawfully.”
A majority of the detained workers were South Korean nationals.
No one was injured during the operation.
ATF Atlanta reported on Twitter that approximately 450 unlawful aliens were apprehended at the Hyundai mega site battery plant in Bryan County, Georgia.
The ATF Atlanta tweet regarding the apprehension numbers was posted on September 4, 2025.
South Korean Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lee Jaewoong described the number of detained South Koreans as “large.”
Lee Jaewoong stated that the detainees belonged to a “network of subcontractors.”
No criminal charges have been announced yet regarding the operation.
Steven Schrank confirmed that the investigation remains an ongoing matter.
The company behind the plant, a joint venture between Hyundai and LG, temporarily paused construction following the raid.
White House Deputy Press Secretary Abigail Jackson stated that the United States looks forward to continuing historic investments and partnerships secured by President Trump.
Abigail Jackson emphasized that any foreign workers brought in for specific projects must enter the United States legally with proper work authorizations.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp touted the $7.6 billion site as one of Georgia’s largest economic developments during a grand opening for its electric vehicle factory in March.
Governor Kemp described the project as a “once-in-a-generation opportunity” for the state.
U.S. Representative Buddy Carter, R-Ga., stated at the grand opening that the hard-working and dedicated local people are the reason for the project's success.

links.context: ATF Atlanta tweet regarding apprehension numbers

source_title: Georgia Hyundai plant: 475 workers arrested in ICE raid

facts: Three workers have died since Hyundai Motor started construction of its $7.6 billion auto plant in Georgia in 2022, the Wall Street Journal reported on Sunday, citing a review of federal records.
Apart from the deaths, more than a dozen workers have suffered serious injuries, including from falling without wearing harnesses and getting crushed by forklifts, according to the WSJ.
A spokesperson for Hyundai Motor North America stated the company conducted a comprehensive audit and strengthened safety oversight across the site.
The company enhanced contractor vetting, training, and accountability to ensure all partners meet Hyundai’s standards and legal requirements.

Dozens of the company’s current and former workers, many of them safety coordinators who helped oversee construction, told the newspaper in interviews that the work environment involved many inexperienced immigrant laborers, often lax safety standards and frequent accidents.
The plant is operated through a joint venture between Hyundai and South Korea’s LG Energy Solution.

The facility has been in the limelight since an immigration raid last month that detained hundreds of South Korean workers in the largest single-site enforcement operation in the U.S. Department of Homeland Security’s history.
Workers interviewed by the WSJ said Hyundai did not ensure that people were properly trained, and safety regulators did little to prevent worksite violations.
Hyundai CEO Jose Munoz told the newspaper he acted immediately and comprehensively to prevent anything like this from happening again.
Jose Munoz traveled to Georgia to tell his team directly that their safety comes before production schedules, before costs, before profits, before everything.
Construction on the electric vehicle and battery plant is ongoing.
The plant is located in Bryan County about 30 miles west of Savannah.
source_title: Three workers died at Hyundai’s Georgia plant since 2022, before US immigration raid, WSJ reports By Reuters

entity_name: Hyundai Motor Group / LG Energy Solution Joint Venture
facts: On September 4, 2025, hundreds of federal agents detained 475 workers at Hyundai Motor Group’s electric vehicle complex in Ellabell, Bryan County, Georgia.
The detention was one of the largest worksite immigration raids in recent U.S. history.
The operation was carried out by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) and the investigative arm of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS).
The raid targeted employees at a battery plant being built as a joint venture between Hyundai and LG Energy Solution.
Most detained workers were South Korean nationals working on temporary visas that did not authorize employment.
23 Mexican nationals were also among those detained.
LG Energy Solution reported that 47 of its direct employees and roughly 250 contractor workers at the joint venture factory were swept up in the operation.
Detainees were transported to the Folkston ICE Processing Center, located more than 100 miles from the plant.

Steven Schrank, special agent in charge of Homeland Security Investigations for Georgia and Alabama, stated no criminal charges were filed at the time and investigators were sorting out employment details.
Schrank noted some workers had been hired by subcontractors rather than directly by Hyundai or LG.
Schrank characterized the workers as “either illegally present in the U.S. or working unlawfully.”
Many South Korean workers had entered the U.S. on short-term business visas or through the visa waiver program, neither of which authorizes paid employment.

Internal ICE arrest records reviewed by the New York Times showed that among a sample of 11 detained workers, six had entered on B-1 or B-1/B-2 business visas and four had used the visa waiver program.
The visa waiver program permits stays of up to 90 days for travel purposes.
Ten of the eleven sampled workers were recorded as “working unlawfully,” and one worker deemed not to have violated his visa was still forced to leave the country.

South Korean companies had long relied on a “grey zone” of loose interpretation around visa categories, sending technicians to install specialized manufacturing equipment under the guise of business visits or supervisory roles.
One equipment technician told Reuters he had previously obtained a B-1 visa by describing himself as a “supervisor” rather than an “equipment specialist.”
Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick stated that Hyundai had “skirted the rules” by using tourist visas and called on the company to “change it.”
Hyundai responded that it has “zero tolerance” for those who do not follow the law and said it was investigating to ensure subcontractors complied with immigration requirements.
Workers reported having their phones confiscated during the raid, leaving them unable to contact their families.
Images showed detainees shackled at the wrists, waist, and ankles.
Workers described overcrowded and unsanitary cells, mouldy beds, and foul-smelling water.
Some detainees alleged that ICE agents failed to inform them of their rights and mocked their nationality.

After a week of tense negotiations between Seoul and Washington, the two governments reached a deal to release the workers and fly them home rather than subject them to formal deportation proceedings.
A chartered Korean Air flight departed Atlanta on September 11, 2025, carrying 316 South Korean nationals along with 10 Chinese nationals, 3 Japanese nationals, and 1 Indonesian.
One South Korean worker chose to remain in the U.S. because he had family in the country.
South Korea’s foreign ministry called on the U.S. Congress to support a new visa category for Korean firms.

South Korean President Lee Jae Myung called the raid “bewildering” and warned that if the U.S. did not ease visa requirements, South Korean businesses would “hesitate to build new factories” in America.
President Lee emphasized that the detained individuals were not permanent employees but technicians helping install equipment.
South Korean Foreign Minister Cho Hyun spoke directly with U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio about the workers’ status.

South Korean lawmakers held emergency parliamentary hearings, with ruling party members declaring that “if the United States truly wants to attract investment from Korean companies, this kind of incident should not happen.”

Construction and operations were halted at the battery facility and at least 22 other Korean factory sites across the automotive, shipbuilding, steel, and electrical equipment sectors as companies paused to verify their employees’ visa statuses.

Seoul launched a formal investigation into whether its citizens had suffered human rights violations during detention, examining visa status, conditions of confinement, and access to counsel and interpreters.
As of mid-2026, no final conclusions from that investigation have been published.
DHS Secretary Kristi Noem defended the raid, arguing it would not deter investment because it created regulatory certainty.
White House border czar Tom Homan signaled on September 7 that additional worksite raids were planned.

President Trump posted on Truth Social on September 15 that skilled foreign workers are “welcome” in the U.S. when brought in to train the domestic workforce, adding that he did not want to “frighten off or disincentivize Investment into America by outside Countries or Companies.”
President Trump later told reporters he was “very much opposed” to the raid, citing an understanding that foreign experts were needed to set up specialized facilities.

Deputy Secretary of State Christopher Landau expressed “deep regret” over the incident during a meeting with South Korean counterparts on September 14 and assured those affected would face no disadvantages in reentering the United States.
In November 2025, Hyundai CEO José Muñoz said publicly that the White House had phoned him to apologize for the raid, calling it an “accident.”
Muñoz said he received “apologies from the president of the United States.”
The White House declined to confirm or deny the apology, issuing a statement that foreign workers “must enter the United States legally and with proper work authorisations.”

On September 30, 2025, the U.S. and South Korea reached an agreement clarifying that South Korean workers could install, service, and repair equipment at investment sites using existing B-1 temporary visas and the ESTA visa waiver program.
U.S. officials acknowledged “practical legislative constraints” that prevented creating a new visa category or increasing quotas.
The U.S. Embassy in Seoul established a new section dedicated to visas for South Korean businesses and opened a communication channel with South Korean missions to improve coordination.
Industry representatives described these measures as a “short-term fix” that did not resolve the underlying uncertainty for workers, who could still be denied entry at U.S. ports.

A formal U.S.-South Korea Business Travel and Visa Working Group met at least three times, including a January 2026 session at which the U.S. highlighted a newly published fact sheet on allowable uses of business visas.

Representatives Young Kim of California and Sydney Kamlager-Dove introduced the “Partner with Korea Act” (H.R. 4687) in July 2025, proposing the creation of 15,000 E-4 highly skilled work visas specifically for Korean nationals with specialized expertise.
As of mid-2026, the bill remains in committee.

Governor Brian Kemp’s office had celebrated the Hyundai Metaplant as a generational economic opportunity for Georgia, and the state had committed roughly $2 billion in taxpayer-backed incentives to the project.
Kemp was not given advance notice of the federal operation and scrambled to contact Trump aides for details.
Kemp’s communications team agonized over the initial statement, at one point removing the word “excited” from a draft to avoid appearing too supportive of Hyundai amid the fallout.

Publicly, Kemp maintained that companies must follow the law while defending the need for specialized foreign workers to install equipment that requires expertise unavailable in the domestic workforce.
Kemp noted that Hyundai had been “following state laws and state rules.”
In October 2025, Kemp traveled to South Korea to shore up the relationship, meeting with Korean industry executives and attending a ceremony marking 40 years of Georgia’s economic presence in Seoul.
Kemp acknowledged that “we certainly had to do some explaining about what happened” but said he had seen no signs that the raid had deterred investment.
At least a half-dozen businesses in the area surrounding the plant reported double-digit percentage drops in revenue.
The Viêt Huong Supermarket in Ellabell, which had purchased a former Piggly Wiggly grocery store to serve the Korean workforce, saw its customer base drop to what its owners described as a “trickle.”
A Korean-American restaurant in nearby Pooler reported an 18% drop in weekly income.
Real estate agent Sean Kim, who had built a business housing Korean workers, saw his livelihood evaporate and moved back to Atlanta by the end of September.
Local business owners reported that Hispanic construction workers and their families also stopped frequenting stores, apparently frightened by the heightened immigration enforcement in the area.
Construction at the 2,900-acre site was suspended in the days following the raid.
On September 6, protesters gathered outside the plant to demonstrate against what they called government overreach.
Hyundai Engineering America served as the general contractor and hired a network of subcontractors, some of which faced allegations of mistreating workers well before the September operation.

In August 2024, two workers named Martin Garcia and Jonathon Rincon filed a federal lawsuit against subcontractor SYS-CON and related entities H&B USA and PPE Trading Co., alleging they had been denied owed raises and overtime pay.
That case was settled in October 2025 for $43,000, paid by PPE Trading Co., after a federal judge initially rejected an earlier settlement proposal.
In April 2023, a worker named Victor Javier Cajija Gamboa, employed by Louisiana-based steel contractor Eastern Constructors Inc., died in a 60-foot fall at the site.
The contractor was subsequently labeled a “severe violator” by federal safety regulators.

Local contractors publicly complained about being shut out of work at the megasite in favor of Korean-affiliated subcontractors, some of which operated from residential addresses with minimal local footprints.
Hyundai CEO José Muñoz said shortly after the raid that the disruption would cause a “minimum two to three months delay” for the battery plant, which had been scheduled to open by the end of 2025.
The company said it would source batteries from other plants in the region while waiting for the Georgia facility to come online.
Replacing the specialized South Korean labor proved difficult.
Hyundai held a job fair at Savannah Technical College in October 2025 that attracted more than 350 applicants, but internal reports indicated high turnover among new hires.
As of late 2025, the main vehicle plant was operating at only one shift per day.

In September 2025, Hyundai announced a $2.7 billion expansion to increase vehicle production capacity to 500,000 units per year, bringing total investment in the auto plant to $10.3 billion, separate from the $4.3 billion joint venture with LG for the battery factory.
Muñoz said the company was “not discouraged” and that its commitment to the site had not changed.
Hyundai had separately pledged to increase its total U.S. investment to $26 billion through 2028.
By April 2026, Muñoz confirmed at the Semafor World Economy conference that the battery plant was “fully operational” and calibrating for production, with an opening scheduled for later that month.
The facility is designed to supply battery cells for electric vehicles assembled at the adjacent Hyundai Metaplant.

Under its agreement with the state and local authorities, Hyundai is contractually obligated to invest $5.545 billion and employ at least 8,500 workers at an average annual salary of $58,105 by 2031, with the jobs retained through 2048.
As of September 2025, the site employed 3,129 workers.
source_title: Immigration Raid at Hyundai Plant: Fallout and Policy Changes - LegalClarity

facts: Georgia manufacturing saw its second consecutive month in decline according to the Purchasing Managers Index (PMI) released by Kennesaw State University’s Econometric Center in June 2016.
June’s PMI reading was 49.3, falling below the 50-point benchmark for growth for the second consecutive month.
The decline was driven by anemic new orders and downward adjustments for production and employment.
Higher monthly employment fell from 31 percent in April to 6.7 percent in June.
Lower monthly employment rose from 6.3 percent in April to 13.3 percent in June.
Higher reported production fell from 52.4 percent in March to 26.7 percent in June.
Higher new orders, which were at 47.6 percent in March, fell to 26.7 percent in June.
Don Sabbarese, director emeritus of the Econometric Center and professor of economics at Kennesaw State University, stated that improved consumer spending had not yet reached Georgia manufacturers.
One third of PMI respondents reported lower new orders and production, while only 6.7 percent reported increased hiring.
June’s new orders, production, and employment metrics registered 11.2, 14.1, and 8.1 points below their respective six-month averages.
The National PMI reading for June was 53.2, an increase of 1.9 points from the previous month.
The national report identified 13 industries expanding in June compared to 12 in May.
June’s national PMI registered increases across all five of its underlying variables.
A PMI reading above 50 indicates that manufacturing activity is expanding, while a reading below 50 indicates it is contracting.

links.context: Contact for full PMI report or to speak with Don Sabbarese

source_title: Georgia manufacturing remains sluggish in June - Coles College of Business

facts: Kia plans to invest over $200 million to expand production for the EV9 SUV creating almost 200 new jobs.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Kia will invest more than $200 million and create nearly 200 new jobs at its West Point plant.
The investment is to accommodate assembly of the company’s highly anticipated all-electric 2024 EV9 three-row SUV.
Through this production line transformation, the EV9 will become the fifth model to be assembled at Kia Georgia.
The EV9 joins the Telluride, Sorento and Sportage SUVs, and the K5 mid-size sedan in assembly at the facility.
EV9 assembly is expected to begin in the second quarter of 2024.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia’s longstanding partnership with Kia has led to generational job creation and growth for the West Point area.
Kemp noted that the project will bring more opportunity to hardworking Georgians and help propel the state toward its goal of becoming the e-mobility capital.
Currently, more than 40 percent of Kia vehicles sold in the U.S. are manufactured in West Point.
The addition of the EV9 to the lineup will further increase the facility’s output.
The EV9 is the brand’s first three-row EV SUV and the first Kia EV to be assembled in North America.
The EV9 is the flagship vehicle for the company’s global Plan S strategy focused on sustainable mobility leadership.
Sean Yoon, President & CEO of Kia Motors North America and Kia Motors America, stated that like Telluride, the EV9 has the potential to be another change catalyst for Kia.
Yoon described the EV9 as the most innovative vehicle they have ever built and a standout in the EV market and on the road.
Kia Georgia is a certified Georgia Made manufacturer.
The facility will continue to utilize the services of Georgia Quick Start, the nation’s top-ranked workforce development program.
Georgia Quick Start operates the Kia Georgia Training Center.
Quick Start’s comprehensive, customized training services will prepare new employees with the skills required to perform successfully on the new assembly line.
Stuart Countess is President & CEO of Kia Georgia.
Countess highlighted partnerships developed statewide with the Governor’s Office, the Department of Economic Development, and Georgia QuickStart.
He also noted local partnerships with the City of West Point and Troup County.
City of West Point Mayor Steve Tramell stated that Kia has been an outstanding corporate citizen and community partner for seventeen years.
Tramell noted that Kia has helped improve the livelihoods of many people in West Point by providing employment opportunities and fostering economic growth.
Senior Regional Project Manager Amanda Fields represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was a partnership with the West Point Development Authority, the City of West Point, Troup County, Georgia Center of Innovation’s Manufacturing and Energy teams, and Georgia Quick Start.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that since Georgia recruited Kia in 2006 to create long-lasting opportunities for West Georgia, Kia has exceeded expectations as a partner by giving back to its adopted community.

Wilson noted that with EV9 now joining Georgia’s growing electrified lineup, they want to thank and congratulate Kia, partners in West Point, and countless other state and local agencies working to meet the needs of today and tomorrow’s leading industries.
To date, Kia has invested more than $1.9 billion in the state of Georgia.
Kia Georgia is responsible for more than 14,000 plant and supplier jobs in the region.
Earlier this year, Governor Kemp and state lawmakers recognized January 31 as “Kia Day,” commending Kia’s vehicle manufacturing and its impact to the state.

The recognition followed the 2006 announcement that a state-of-the-art vehicle assembly facility would be established in West Point, which was one of the biggest single foreign investments in Georgia’s history.
From Georgia, Kia ships vehicles to sales markets around the globe, including the United States, Canada, Mexico, the Caribbean, Middle East and Pacific nations.
As the emerging electric vehicle market continues to grow, Georgia has pursued job creation along the entire supply chain.
This pursuit has resulted in more than $22.7 billion in investment and the creation of over 28,400 jobs since 2020.
Kia Georgia, Inc. is the first manufacturing site in North America for Kia Corporation based in Seoul, Korea.
With an annual capacity of 340,000 units, Kia Georgia is located on 2,200 acres in West Point, Georgia.
The facility began mass production on November 16, 2009.
Kia America is headquartered in Irvine, California.
Kia America serves as the “Official Automotive Partner” of the NBA.
Kia America offers a range of gasoline, hybrid, plug-in hybrid and electrified vehicles sold through a network of over 750 dealers in the U.S.

links.context: Custom email notifications for press releases

source_title: Kia will invest over $200M to expand production in Georgia

facts: On Feb. 24, 2026, Kia Georgia celebrated the start of production for the all-new 2027 Kia Telluride at its West Point facility.
The milestone event marked the production of the state of Georgia’s first-ever hybrid electric vehicle (HEV).
The event also commemorated the plant’s 5 millionth vehicle produced.
A ceremony for the milestones was attended by Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp, team members, and state/community leaders.
Sean Yoon, President and CEO of Kia North America and Kia America, stated that Kia Georgia is the only plant in the world that builds the Telluride.
Yoon noted that reaching five million vehicles and introducing Kia’s first US-assembled HEV reflects Kia's commitment to building the future of mobility in Georgia.
Kia Georgia began production in 2009, with mass production officially starting on Nov. 16, 2009.
Since opening in 2009, Kia Georgia has grown into one of the region’s largest employers and a cornerstone of automotive manufacturing in the Southeast.
The Telluride was introduced in 2019, is exclusively made in Georgia, and has become one of the most successful vehicles in Kia’s lineup.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp praised the proven partnership approach between Kia Georgia, the local community, and the state for creating long-lasting relationships.
Stuart Countess, President and CEO of Kia Georgia, highlighted that the plant's ability to produce world-class gasoline-powered vehicles, EVs, and HEVs demonstrates its versatility and talent.
Kia Georgia, Inc. is the first manufacturing site in North America for Kia Corporation, which is based in Seoul, Korea.
The West Point facility sits on 2,200 acres and has an annual production capacity of 350,000 units.
The plant currently manufactures the Telluride, Sorento SUV (excluding HEV and PHEV models), Sportage SUV (excluding HEV and PHEV models), and the all-electric EV9 and EV6.
source_title: Kia Georgia celebrates trio of major milestones, including Start of Production for 2027 Kia Telluride | lifestyle.ribbon.co

facts: Kia Georgia completed a large-scale hail protection and solar canopy system at its West Point manufacturing facility in Georgia.
The project was announced on April 24, 2026, with an official ribbon-cutting ceremony scheduled for the same day.
The installation spans 3.2 million square feet of vehicle protection area.
The system generates 10 megawatts of on-site solar power capacity used exclusively by Kia Georgia.
It is one of the largest behind-the-meter solar canopy projects in the Southeast.
The project was developed to address increasing weather volatility and protect high-value vehicle inventory following a 2023 hailstorm that damaged more than 13,000 vehicles at the facility.
VPS (Vehicle Protection Structures) delivered the turnkey system, which provides continuous vehicle protection while converting previously unused space into an energy asset.
The solar panels integrated into the canopy were manufactured by Georgia-based Qcells, supporting regional supply chains.
The on-site renewable power generation reduces Kia Georgia's reliance on external energy sources and stabilizes long-term operating costs.

Wade White, Executive Vice President at VPS, stated that combining large-scale protection with on-site solar generation turns the investment into a source of ongoing value and reflects a new standard for manufacturer risk management.

Stuart Countess, President and CEO of Kia Georgia, Inc., noted the solution will protect vehicles, support the team, generate power on-site, and position the company for a more efficient and sustainable future.

Cheryl Davis, Vice President of Customer Service at Georgia Power, highlighted that the project demonstrates strategic energy thinking and helps customers meet renewable energy and sustainability goals while solving unique business needs.

The completion of the project highlights a growing trend among manufacturers adopting integrated infrastructure solutions to address multiple challenges simultaneously, reduce financial exposure, improve efficiency, and better utilize existing space.

VPS, a PlayPower brand and leader in automotive hail, heat, and UV protection since 1991, provided full-service design-build support from initial survey and design through engineering, fabrication, construction, and post-project service.
source_title: VPS and Kia Georgia Complete Landmark Automotive Hail Protection and Solar Canopy Project

facts: Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia (KMMG) is located in West Point, Georgia, and has been operational since 2010.
The plant represented an initial $1.2 billion investment that has since expanded to a cumulative economic impact exceeding $4 billion on the surrounding west Georgia region.
KMMG is the only Kia manufacturing facility in the United States.
The facility initially created 2,500 direct jobs, with production focused on vehicles like the Sorento and Telluride SUVs.
This development absorbed displaced textile workers and spurred ancillary employment in logistics, suppliers, and services.
Infrastructure upgrades such as road expansions and utility enhancements were implemented to support the facility's operations.
In July 2023, KMMG announced a $200 million investment to initiate assembly of the EV9 electric SUV.
The initiative added nearly 200 new positions and enhanced the plant's capacity for sustainable manufacturing.

City-led initiatives between 2015 and 2024 fostered over 1,400 additional jobs and more than $700 million in private capital investments, including expansions in automotive supply chains along Kia Boulevard.
The Downtown West Point Development Authority introduced 50/50 matching facade grants for storefront improvements in 2025 to revitalize the central business district by leveraging Kia's proximity.

West Point is positioned as a hub for advanced manufacturing within the Columbus-Auburn metropolitan area, though sustained growth depends on adapting to automotive industry volatility including supply chain disruptions and electrification demands.

Kia Georgia, Inc. operates one of the largest automotive assembly plants in the United States, employing approximately 3,000 workers in vehicle production with an annual capacity exceeding 350,000 units as of 2024.
The facility was established in 2009 and has expanded multiple times.
Hyundai Dymos America supports Kia's operations by producing automotive seats with around 500 employees.
Johnson Controls manufactures seat systems and door panels with approximately 500 workers.
Powertech America, Inc. specializes in transmissions with about 500 staff.
Daehan Solution provides automotive parts to the local cluster with 90 employees.

entity_name: Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia, Inc. (West Point Plant)
facts: As of September 3, 2009, the first car has yet to roll off the assembly line of Kia Motors’ West Point, Ga., plant.
Progress toward the projected mid-November launch date is moving at full throttle.
J. Randy Jackson, Kia Motors’ human resource director and administrator, shared progress updates at the Opelika Chamber of Commerce’s Business Over Breakfast event at the Saugahatchee Country Club.
“We’re the fastest growing auto company in the world and that’s something I’m proud of,” said Jackson.
The West Point, Ga. plant is scheduled to begin production in mid-November 2009.
At launch, the plant will manufacture 2011 model vehicles, including the popular Sorento CUV.
At full production, the plant hopes to produce 300,000 vehicles a year, according to Kia Motors public relations specialist Patrick Sands.
After January 2010, the plant will begin shipping vehicles by rail and truck to dealership locations in the U.S., Canada, and Puerto Rico.
In August 2009, Kia Motors reported a 60.4 percent increase in vehicle sales.
The opening of Kia’s West Point auto assembly plant is on schedule.
When complete, the plant will sit on a 2,200-acre site, with only about 650 acres designated for the main pad.
“Typically you don’t see an OEM (original equipment manufacturer) site larger than about 1,100 acres but there’s a reason we’re located on such a large site,” said Jackson.
“We hope to expand and we hope to do so quickly,” added Jackson regarding the plant's large footprint.
When Kia Motors began accepting applications for positions at its West Point plant, more than 43,000 people applied from January to February 2008.
As of September 2009, 1,138 persons have been hired to staff the West Point, Ga. plant.
There are about 100 salaried position openings remaining at the plant, according to Jackson.
Jackson hopes to hire more workers in the future.
Opelika Mayor Gary Fuller said he was “impressed with the information Jackson shared” regarding the plant's progress.
“Kia has and will continue to have a significant, positive economic impact on the Opelika and East Alabama communities,” said Fuller.
Fuller hopes the West Point, Ga. plant will serve as an employment option for workers affected by the closing of BFGoodrich’s Opelika plant.
“We’re not going anywhere... We’re here to stay,” said Jackson regarding Kia's commitment to the West Point, Ga. site.
Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia, Inc. (KMMG) is the first manufacturing site in North America for Kia Motors Corporation, which is based in Seoul, Korea.
KMMG is located on 2,200 acres in West Point, Georgia.
The plant was scheduled to begin production in the last quarter of 2009.
At full capacity, the plant will have the ability to produce 300,000 vehicles annually and employ approximately 2,500 team members.
source_title: Kia On Track For November Production Start - Kia Georgia

facts: The 2025 Kia EV9 is the first electric vehicle assembled in the state of Georgia.
The 2025 Kia EV9 joins the award-winning Telluride, Sorento, and Sportage SUVs currently assembled at Kia’s West Point facility.
On May 30, 2024, team members gathered at Kia Georgia’s assembly plant in West Point to celebrate the start of assembly of the all-electric 2025 EV9 three-row SUV.
The 2025 EV9 is the first saleable electric vehicle assembled in the state of Georgia.
The vehicle was introduced by SeungKyu (Sean) Yoon, President and CEO for Kia North America and Kia America.
The vehicle was driven off the assembly line by Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp and James Watson, Kia Georgia’s Vice President of Production.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that the expansion of Kia’s operations in Georgia and the addition of a new product line exemplify what comes from state and local partners working with job creators.
Governor Brian Kemp thanked Kia Georgia team members for choosing the Peach State as the state works to become the 'e-mobility capital of the nation.'
The EV9 joins the award-winning and increasingly popular lineup of SUVs currently being assembled on Kia Georgia’s assembly line: Telluride, Sorento and Sportage.
The launch is the culmination of a more than $200 million expansion at the plant.
The expansion has added approximately 200 new jobs at the plant.
The expansion provides flexibility to assemble both EVs and the company’s traditional products along the same assembly line.
SeungKyu (Sean) Yoon stated that today’s launch of the Kia EV9 in Georgia is the latest example of Kia’s commitment to the U.S. market and the state of Georgia.
Through Plan S, Kia is poised to be a leader in the e-mobility sector for years to come.
The EV9 has achieved prestigious accolades such as World Car of the Year, World EV of the Year, and North American Utility Vehicle of the Year.
Stuart Countess is the President and CEO of Kia Georgia.
The first Kia vehicle rolled off the same assembly line on Nov. 16, 2009.
Stuart Countess stated that the Kia Georgia team continues its mindset of turning challenges into opportunities.
Kia Georgia is adding its flagship EV to the facility and entering a new era with the same quality focus of assembling the best vehicles in the world in West Point.
Kia Georgia, Inc. is the first manufacturing site in North America for Kia Corporation based in Seoul, Korea.
Kia Georgia has an annual capacity of 350,000 units.
Kia Georgia is located on 2,200 acres in West Point, Ga.
Kia Georgia began mass production on Nov. 16, 2009.
Kia Georgia is home to the Telluride SUV, Sorento SUV (excludes HEV and PHEV models), Sportage SUV (excludes HEV and PHEV models) and the all-electric EV9 SUV.

facts: Troup County, Georgia, located in the west-central portion of the state, has experienced economic growth, community revitalization, and improved infrastructure since Kia began production in the community in 2009.

Strategic planning, workforce development, and infrastructure improvements are key priorities to drive sustainable growth for the local economy.

facts: The Georgia Economic Development Department facilitated 426 new facilities and expansions announced in the fiscal year that ended June 30, 2023.
The facilitated projects represent more than $24 billion in investments.
The facilitated projects are expected to create 38,400 jobs.
The number of projects in fiscal 2023 is up 19 percent from the record 358 facilities announced in fiscal 2022.
Total investments in fiscal 2023 are up 13 percent from the $21.2 billion announced in fiscal 2022.
The fiscal 2023 job total is down from more than 51,100 jobs in fiscal 2022.

facts: Daesol Ausys, a South Korean automotive supplier, announced it will invest $72 million to establish a new manufacturing facility in Harris County.
The company specializes in designing and manufacturing motor and electric vehicle interior components and accessories.
According to the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), Daesol Ausys is a “key supplier” for Kia Georgia, Hyundai Motor Group, and General Motors.
The new facility will be located in West Point, Georgia, specifically within the Northwest Harris Business Park.
The facility will specifically produce luggage boards and covers.
The project is expected to create more than 140 new jobs in Harris County.
Operations at the new West Point facility are expected to begin in December 2024.
Governor Brian Kemp announced that Daesol Ausys Georgia will establish the new manufacturing facility in West Point, Georgia.

Min Ho Kwon, CEO of Daesol Ausys, stated the company is committed to delivering world-class services and is excited about the project in the Northwest Harris Business Park and continuing partnerships with the State of Georgia and Harris County.
Governor Brian Kemp noted that West Point has become an automotive capital in its own right and expressed excitement that Daesol Ausys will join other innovators in Harris County.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia’s automotive industry employs over 50,000 hardworking Georgians and will continue to grow as e-mobility suppliers choose all corners of the state for future jobs.
South Korea was one of the top three international sources of job creation in Georgia during Fiscal Year 2023.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that economic development is driven at the local level and congratulated Harris County and community partners for helping bring Daesol Ausys to West Georgia.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson added that the automotive industry is providing generational change in the region and across the state, and expressed excitement for the opportunities Daesol Ausys will provide to people of Harris County.

facts: On May 15, 2023, Essex Brownell announced it will expand its operational presence in the southeastern U.S. by relocating and expanding its distribution center in McDonough, Georgia.
The new facility will be located within the same industrial complex as the current site to ensure uninterrupted service for customers.
Essex Brownell will retain its current staff and create opportunities to double employment at the new location.
Rebecca Hunnicutt, Vice President of Operations and Customer Service, stated that expanding from over 7,000 square meters to over 40,000 square meters was an easy decision due to additional benefits.

Hunnicutt noted that the company has been in McDonough since 1999 and prioritized retaining and growing its existing personnel while maintaining a strong relationship with the local property management team.
Leadership cited business goals of "continuous investment in the region" and "better support for our customers" as key drivers for the expansion.
This marks Essex Brownell's second operational expansion within one year, following the opening of a state-of-the-art facility in Reno, Nevada, in the second quarter of 2022.
Hunnicutt expects current customers served outside the site to experience no service disruption and may perceive faster speeds due to the expansion.
The company aims to reduce redundancies or overlaps with post-COVID supply chains by being larger, closer to customers, more flexible, and faster at delivering products.
Essex Brownell is an international distributor of magnet wire and ancillary products for the construction and repair of motors, generators, transformers, and electrical components.
Through its Essex Active processing facilities, Essex Brownell also provides custom manufacturing and rewinding services alongside discrete material delivery.
The announcement was issued from Fort Wayne, Indiana.
source_title: Essex Brownell Announces Investment At McDonough, Georgia Distribution Center - Superior Essex – Corporate

facts: Superior Essex announced an expanded partnership with Waybridge, a supply-chain operating system for raw materials.
The announcement continues the Vision 2030 measures for Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire through agile digitization of the company's manufacturing facilities in Europe, North America, and Malaysia.
Superior Essex is among the first users of the Waybridge platform, utilizing the technology since August 2021 at two North American magnet wire manufacturing plants in the USA and Mexico.
Waybridge will be deployed in nine new facilities in Canada, Germany, Italy, Serbia, and the USA.
Scott Wilde, VP Strategic Sourcing, highlighted innovation and holistic sustainability as procurement priorities.
Scott Wilde stated that optimizing processes was necessary to provide the best experience for customers and suppliers and to anticipate supply chain problems like stockouts before they impact them.

Since partnering with Waybridge, Superior Essex's view of its entire inventory and workflow process has fundamentally changed, allowing access to predictive information to flexibly solve supply chain problems.
Inefficient supply chains and stockouts can cost companies more than direct revenue loss by increasing logistics costs, damaging brand image, and ultimately affecting future demand.
Causes for stockouts include unexpected demand surges, shipping and supplier delays, human error, and inaccurate inventory data.

Waybridge's Stock & Flow function addresses current and future inventory challenges by aggregating data from shipping forecasts, real-time shipping locations, estimated arrival times, and production schedules to provide predictive capabilities.
In 2021 alone, Waybridge enabled Superior Essex to avoid four material failures that would have directly impacted customers.
Superior Essex reduced enterprise-wide Excel usage in procurement by 90% and email volume around procurement and tracking by 50% through Waybridge's connected platform.

Scott Evans, CEO and founder of Waybridge, noted that Superior Essex was so impressed with the results that they expanded implementation to facilities worldwide, calling them a forward-thinker in raw materials who understands the importance of standardization on a shared platform.

Kyle McCarter, Chief Product Officer of Waybridge, added that Superior Essex provided incredible product feedback since the start of the partnership, helping continuously develop the product for all customers.
Waybridge values Superior Essex as a partner and major participant in global markets as one of the largest magnet wire manufacturers in the world.
Superior Essex currently manages its entire North American material through Waybridge and plans to expand the technology to Asian facilities in the near future.
Superior Essex is the parent brand of Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire, Superior Essex Communications, and Essex Brownell.
The company employs over 3,000 people across 11 countries on three continents.
Superior Essex is the world's leading provider of magnet cables and leads in communication fiber applications and Power over Ethernet (PoE) technology.
The company contributes significantly to disruptive technological advancements in the electrification of the automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.
Superior Essex aims to pioneer smart building technology while setting the pace for sustainability.
Superior Essex is headquartered at 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327 – USA.
source_title: Superior Essex, Essex Furukawa Partner With Waybridge On Agile Digitization Technology - Superior Essex – Corporate

facts: The magnet wire plant in Torreón, Mexico of Essex Solutions has become the company’s fourth facility to achieve sustainability certification in the U.S. as part an ongoing dedication to achieve new levels of environmental stewardship.
The company continues its rebranding to the Essex Solutions identity following the recent buyout by Atlanta-based Superior Essex of the global joint venture Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire.
The Torreón plant joins other sites in Fort Wayne and Franklin, Indiana as well as the copper rod manufacturing plant in Columbia City, Indiana to have completed The Copper Mark Assurance Process.
The Copper Mark Assurance Process is an internationally recognized framework that promotes responsible copper production and ethical practices.

Under the new name, Essex Solutions will encompass all previous Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire locations worldwide.
This includes magnet wire facilities in Japan and Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia that were contributed by Furukawa Electric in October 2020.

Neither party disclosed the acquisition amount, but analysts estimated the transaction to be about $200 million.

Essex Solutions now has more than 2,500 employees across 18 facilities globally with a production capacity of 402,000 metric tons.
Superior Essex has over 3,000 employees in 11 countries and provides magnet wire, communications fiber cable through Essex Brownell, and Power over Ethernet (PoE) technology.
Superior Essex is a subsidiary of LS Cable & System based in Seoul, Korea.
LS Cable & System operates a global network of about 100 subsidiaries and branches in 25 countries active in electric power, automation, machinery, materials and energy.
LS holdings include operations involved in magnetics technology such as superconducting wire, magnet wire and EMI technology.

In 2021 with partners, LS successfully developed a 3-phase coaxial cable featuring improved structural design of the superconducting layer that reduced production costs by more than 10% and increased the power transfer rate by more than 20%.
LS aims to commercialize the developed 3-phase coaxial cable for large-capacity energy transmission.
LS has been working with Korea Electrotechnology Research Institute to jointly research advancements in superconducting transmission and distribution power devices.
LS Cable & System is a major producer of magnet wire, particularly for the Korean automotive market.
LS has supplied its magnet wire products to Hyundai, Kia and General Motors electric vehicles.
In 2021, LS became the first company in Korea to mass product 800V magnet wires.
LS became the sole supplier of magnet wire for the drive motors of the Hyundai IONIQ 5 and Kia EV6 electric vehicles.
Subsidiary LS Mtron unveiled new products at DesignCon 2024 including advancements in EMI connectivity.
The complete 3-layer shielding structure allows stable transmission of 5G mmWave antenna module data and resolves noise issues during high-definition data transmission from high-pixel cameras.

facts: Essex Furukawa released its second annual Sustainability Report on 31 Ogos 2022 related to Vision 2030: Masa Depan Lestari, Kuasa Penggerak Kita.
The 29 muka surat (page) report emphasizes increased reliance on renewable energy and carbon reduction achievements.
Progress is shown toward achieving Zero Waste to Landfill across the global manufacturing footprint.
Future initiatives regarding Scope 3 emissions, supply chain data, and ESG projects are introduced.
Austin Robinson, Global Corporate Sustainability Manager for Essex Furukawa, collected data with help from business unit heads and third-party verification partners.
Robinson stated that achievements only happened with strong support, noting significant company-wide changes and continuous sharing and implementation of best practices.
The company observed a significant increase in social sustainability as more employees recognized their role in the future environment and local community.
Essex Furukawa created its holistic sustainability program with three core values: Disruptive Innovation, Green Manufacturing, and Agile Digitalization.
These core values are designed to drive industry change and innovate the magnet wire industry while considering environmental impact.
The report displays last year's results and how each impacts progress toward the holistic Vision 2030 program.
Key goals revolve around achieving Zero Waste to Landfill, Energy Reduction, Renewable Energy adoption, and Carbon Reduction tied to Green Manufacturing.
Essex Furukawa prioritizes Agile Digitalization while collaborating with customers and R&D leaders to advance Disruptive Innovation in the magnet wire industry.
Daniel Choi, CEO of Essex Furukawa, stated that leadership in Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) requires the transparency offered by the report.
Choi noted the ESG landscape is constantly evolving and the company feels responsible to stakeholders to be agile and accountable.
Choi added that across all perspectives and metrics, they have succeeded and want to lead by example, set standards, and not just follow them.

facts: On May 4, 2021, Superior Essex completed an agreement to become the eighth partner company of The Copper Mark.
The Copper Mark is described as the only comprehensive social and environmental assurance program for the copper industry.

The partnership aligns with Superior Essex's corporate sustainability goals and the newly announced Vision 2030 for the Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire brand to source raw materials used in its magnet wire products responsibly.

Superior Essex Communications, a leading global manufacturer of communication cabling, has been a sustainability leader since 2011 and aims to reduce its product footprint by finding responsibly sourced materials through cooperation with copper suppliers.
The Copper Mark is an assurance framework demonstrating responsible copper production practices and the industry's contribution to UN Sustainable Development Goals.
The framework evaluates copper producers against a set of criteria for responsible production covering 32 distinct environmental, social, and governance issues related to mineral and metal sourcing.
The Copper Mark aims to establish good management practices originating from voluntary sustainability standard requirements typically used in mineral and metal supply chains.
Scott Wilde, Vice President of Global Strategic Sourcing at Superior Essex, stated that aligning responsible material sourcing is crucial for achieving company goals and meeting partner needs.
CEO Brian Kim recently launched the Vision 2030 commitment, framing this partnership as a challenge to continue advancing green production and improving products from responsibly sourced materials.
The commitment highlights how sourcing and sustainability efforts can run in parallel within the organization and externally.
Many partners have shifted their sustainability values, ensuring Superior Essex can be part of their solutions.
Being an early adopter of the Copper Mark highlights confidence in producing magnet wire and communication cables with a lower footprint.

The Copper Mark evaluates copper mining, smelting, and refining management practices across categories including human rights, working conditions, community, environment, and overall governance, including transparency, disclosure, and business integrity.
Company processes are measured against standards defined in the Risk Readiness Assessment made by the Responsible Minerals Initiative, which consists of over 380 cross-industry companies.
Michèle Brülhart, Executive Director of The Copper Mark, stated that Superior Essex's commitment to sustainability and responsible copper production is key to the partnership.
Annie Bevan, Global Sustainability Chief for Superior Essex, said having all parties issue a joint statement demonstrates the excellence of Vision 2030, The Copper Mark, and Superior Essex's future.
Bevan noted that while far from the finish line, this is an extraordinary step toward Vision 2030 goals and hopes to continue reducing impact in products and the company for years to come.
Superior Essex is a parent brand of Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire, Superior Essex Communications, and Essex Brownell.
Superior Essex has over 3,000 employees across 11 countries on three continents.
Superior Essex is a leading global provider of magnet wire and at the forefront of fiber communication applications and Power over Ethernet (PoE) technology.
The company plays a role in creating disruptive technological advancements in the electrification of the automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.
Superior Essex is committed to pioneering smart building technology while also paving the way for continuity.
Superior Essex headquarters address is 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327.
Copper Mark was initially developed and funded by the International Copper Association (ICA) before being incorporated as an independent entity headquartered in the UK.
source_title: Superior Essex Continues Commitment To Sustainability, Partners With Copper Mark - Superior Essex – Corporate

facts: Superior Essex Inc. (SPSX) is an Atlanta-based cable supplier and the world’s top magnet wire provider under South Korea’s LS Group.
The company is mulling an initial public offering on Korea’s main Kospi bourse more than 15 years after leaving the US Nasdaq market.

In 2008, Superior Essex was acquired by Korea’s LS Group in a deal where LS agreed to pay shareholders $45.00 per share in cash, a 50% premium over the year-to-date-average closing price of its common stock.
After the takeover, Superior Essex was delisted from the US market but pursued a return to the Nasdaq market until last year.

IPO market conditions for cable companies in the US have turned negative, with AT&T Inc. inching up only 5.4% over the last year, and US public listing requirements being more complicated than those in Korea.
The Korean IPO market has been on a recovery track since late last year, unleashing more listing opportunities.

LS Corp.’s Chairman Koo Ja-eun stated at Korea’s InteBattery 2024 exhibition in March that the group is seeking to list one or two more affiliates between LS E-Link and LS MnM IPOs, making Superior Essex a strong candidate to go public before LS MnM.
LS Corp. is LS Group's holding company controlling Korea’s top wire and cable producer LS Cable & System Ltd., LS Electric Co., global leading smelter LS MnM Inc., and LS Mtron Co.

Superior Essex should complete its IPO by 2026 because LS raised funds from Korean private equity firm SKS Credit Co. in August last year in a pre-IPO deal giving the investor drag-along rights to force the sale of shares if the IPO fails until that deadline.
Brokerage firms are in talks with LS Group to tap Superior Essex’s IPO in Korea.
Superior Essex is the world’s largest producer of magnet wire and a leader in the North American communications cable market.
In March, it joined the US government’s 55 trillion won project to build high-speed communication networks.
The company has entered the EV motor magnet wire market after fully taking over the minority interest held by Furukawa Electric in the Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire joint venture in March.
Superior Essex reported $1.5 billion in sales in 2023.

Effective April 30, 2024, the completion establishes Superior Essex as the sole owner of the brand, assets, and operations previously conducted by the global joint venture with Furukawa Electric.
Superior Essex plans to relaunch its magnet wire business under a new company name to be announced later in 2024.
The company will continue to operate all current facilities of the Essex Furukawa joint venture worldwide.
Existing facilities include magnet wire facilities in Japan and Malaysia (Kuala Lumpur) that were contributed by Furukawa Electric in October 2020.
Daniel Choi serves as the CEO of Superior Essex.

CEO Daniel Choi stated, “Over the last several years we have been able to accomplish many great things working with the team from Furukawa Electric. I am proud of what was created and thankful for their contributions.”

The acquisition marks a significant milestone for Superior Essex, solidifying its control over global operations and reinforcing its commitment to delivering exceptional products and services to customers worldwide.
With this acquisition, Superior Essex is well-positioned to continue its legacy of innovation and excellence in the wire and cable industry.
CEO Daniel Choi added that Superior Essex plans to continuously respond to market demands in the xEV automotive industry, power, electrification, and renewable energy markets.
The company aims to pave the way for future technologies as a global leader.
Superior Essex will strengthen its position by actively pursuing decarbonization and sustainability.

Superior Essex is instrumental in creating disruptive technological advancements in the electrification of the automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.
The company is committed to pioneering smart building technology while also setting the pace on sustainability.

facts: TOTO is the world’s leading plumbing manufacturer with over $5.34 billion in annual sales.
Opened a $224 million state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Morrow, Georgia.
The new facility marks a major investment in the company’s Americas growth strategy.
Enables faster delivery of strategic high-end one-piece toilets to meet surging market demand.
Shifting more production from Asia-based TOTO Group plants to North America to strengthen its regional supply chain and enhance manufacturing resilience.
Taro Muroi, CEO of TOTO USA, stated the opening is “the next chapter in our U.S. manufacturing story.”
Production at the original Morrow plant began in 1991, uniting TOTO’s manufacturing expertise, innovative technology, local craftsmanship, and commitment to sustainability.
Muroi emphasized the facility strengthens bonds of trust, collaboration, and shared vision with employees, partners, customers, and communities across the Americas.
With the new facility online, TOTO’s production capacity in the Americas rises to approximately one million units annually across its Morrow, Lakewood, and Mexico plants.
The new building in Morrow alone adds production capacity of approximately 300,000 units per year.
Luxury one-piece toilets are a strategic product for the Americas market, previously sourced primarily from TOTO Global Group plants in Asia with extended lead times.
Localized production now ensures timely delivery and supports the company’s growth strategy across the Americas.
In June 2023, TOTO broke ground on the new facility on the site of its original Morrow plant.
Construction concluded in November 2024, with production scheduled to begin in the fall of 2025.
The expansion adds about 161,500 square feet (15,000 m²) of advanced manufacturing space.
The new three-story facility totals over 363,393 square feet (33,800 m²) and sustains 420 local jobs.
The facility features a Tank Bonding Robot, a first for any TOTO Group plant, jointly developed with an equipment manufacturer to precisely position and bond tanks to bowls for one-piece toilets.
An automation fleet of thirty-four advanced robots streamlines processes from molding to finishing while maintaining local artisanal craftsmanship.
Six Automated Guided Vehicles (AGVs) carry three toilets each between molding, drying, glazing, and firing over approximately 44 yards (40 meters).
High-Pressure Casting Equipment overcomes previous production limitations by enabling the manufacture of larger, more complex designs.
Heat-resistant QR codes applied to each product enable real-time tracking, quality control, and big-data analysis to optimize manufacturing processes.
Two planned convection-heated kilns with advanced insulation and digital temperature control will improve energy efficiency and cut CO₂ emissions by approximately 23% compared to current kilns.
The new kilns are planned for installation in 2027, will accommodate taller products, improve firing consistency, and reduce energy loss associated with older kiln technology.
The facility was built with sustainability at its core, aligning with TOTO WILL2030 global strategy centered on Cleanliness, Comfort, Health, the Environment, and Connections with People.

Sustainability initiatives include energy-efficient manufacturing systems embedded throughout production and localized production that reduces shipping distances and lowers transportation-related carbon emissions.
These measures support TOTO’s mission to contribute to the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and maintain its leadership in sustainable innovation.
TOTO began operations in the Americas in 1989 in California and launched U.S.-based manufacturing in Lakewood, Georgia, in 1991.
The company opened its first major manufacturing facility in Morrow in 1996.
For fiscal year 2024, TOTO USA recorded sales of $464 million, underscoring strong demand for its premium bathroom products despite a slowdown in the U.S. housing market.
The company is a key growth driver for the TOTO Global Group.

Muroi stated the expansion cements leadership in luxury bathroom innovation while creating a more agile, resilient manufacturing network to serve customers and support employees with safer, more efficient technology.
source_title: TOTO Opens $224M Georgia Manufacturing Facility, Increasing U.S. Luxury Toilet Production Capacity by 150% | phcppros

facts: Kubota Manufacturing of America was formed in 1988 as Kubota’s North American manufacturing base.
The company manufactures over 200,000 units across its five main manufacturing facilities.
KMA manufactures and assembles lawn tractors, zero-turn mowers, sub-compact tractors, and utility vehicles.
The Gainesville/Jefferson facility offers 3.2 million feet of manufacturing space.
The company employs over 3500 employees.
Approximately one-half of all Kubota branded equipment sold in the United States is manufactured or assembled in the state of Georgia.
KMA operates a Work Based Learning Program to train individuals without prior skills.
Eric Ramey serves as Maintenance Supervisor at KMA in Jefferson, having started his career there in 2012 as a high school junior and intern.
Eric Ramey progressed through roles including High School Intern, Maintenance Apprentice, Mechanical Technician, Electrical Technician, and Maintenance Lead before becoming Maintenance Supervisor.

Brian Arnold serves as President of KMA.
