facts: Trenton Pressing is a Tier 1 automotive stamping and assembly supplier serving BMW, Club Car, ZF, and other global OEMs.
The company required software to handle full operational complexity including APQP, PPAP, shop floor scheduling, and quality control across its operations.
Trenton Pressing operates across 192,000+ square feet of production space.
The facility employs over 200 people.
Operations are distributed across 3 buildings.
Production equipment includes automatic presses ranging from 200 to 660 tons.
The site features 20 or more automated weld cells.
Previously, disconnected systems created data silos and slowed down critical processes at Trenton Pressing.
GatesFlow was built on the production floor at Trenton Pressing using the same address, team, and standards.
The GatesFlow platform unified APQP program tracking, cross-system data mapping, and quality management into a single system purpose-built for Tier 1 operations.
Trenton Pressing completed a $12M facility expansion in 2024.
Following the expansion, Trenton Pressing deployed GatesFlow across all production lines.
The unified platform eliminated data silos between engineering, quality, and production departments.
The system enables IATF 16949 compliance workflows that run end-to-end without manual handoffs.
source_title: Trenton Pressing — Case Study | GatesFlow

entity_name: Hyundai Metaplant America and Hyundai–LG Energy Solution Battery Facility
canonical_name: Hyundai Metaplant America / Hyundai-LG Energy Solution Joint Venture
facts: Federal agents swarmed the Hyundai Metaplant America and the adjacent Hyundai–LG Energy Solution battery facility in Ellabell, Georgia on September 4, 2025.
Authorities detained about 475 workers during the operation, which they called the largest single-site immigration raid in U.S. history.
The operation was carried out by ICE, HSI, the FBI, DEA, ATF, and the Georgia State Patrol under a judicial search warrant.
The enforcement action halted work at the multi-billion-dollar electric vehicle and battery complex.
Officials say more than 300 detainees are South Korean nationals, many accused of overstaying Visa Waiver Program entries or working in violation of B-1 business visitor rules.
As of September 11, more than 300 detainees remain in custody.
A planned repatriation flight arranged by Seoul was delayed with no new date confirmed.
The site employs about 1,400 people and was projected to add up to 8,500 jobs.
The immediate shutdown injected deep uncertainty into Georgia’s fast-growing EV corridor and sent shockwaves through a flagship foreign investment.
U.S. authorities state the raid followed months of investigation into alleged unlawful employment practices, culminating in a court-approved search.

Steven Schrank, the HSI Special Agent in Charge overseeing the operation, confirmed the majority of those arrested are South Korean nationals and described the enforcement action as the end point of a sustained probe.
Detainees are being processed for deportation, primarily to South Korea.
South Korean Foreign Minister Cho Hyun rushed to Washington for urgent talks with U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio.

President Lee Jae Myung had recently visited the White House to advance major trade and investment packages, including a July pledge of $350 billion in new U.S. investment and a purchase of $100 billion in U.S. energy linked to tariff and market access talks.
Lawmakers in Seoul are demanding reciprocal reviews of U.S. citizens working in Korea and pressing for higher visa quotas for skilled Korean workers in the United States.
The Hyundai–LG battery joint venture represents $4.3 billion in investment, part of a broader $7.6 billion EV and battery manufacturing site.
Local suppliers, contractors, and training programs that fed into the Hyundai Metaplant America build-out are in limbo as the company assesses timelines.
Production schedules tied to federal EV incentives and state development targets are at risk, with ripple effects for logistics firms and smaller shops that scaled up for the launch.
James Kim, chairman of the American Chamber of Commerce in Seoul, warned that sentiment in South Korea is very negative regarding the raid's potential to cool future investment.

Federal agencies cite two main categories of violations: ESTA overstays under the Visa Waiver Program and B-1 misuse where workers entered for short-term business activity but allegedly performed manual labor requiring work authorization.
ICE and the Department of Homeland Security maintain the raid was lawful and necessary to address widespread, organized violations tied to a high-profile site.
The operation marks a clear signal of the administration’s stepped-up enforcement focus on critical infrastructure and large foreign-backed projects.
Officials point to whistleblower reports alleging both safety lapses and immigration breaches at the site.
Tori Branum, a Republican candidate in Georgia’s 12th congressional district, publicly claimed she submitted evidence to ICE that helped trigger the probe.
Work that looks like routine site setup or hands-on assembly can cross into “employment” under U.S. law for business visitors.
Business visitors can attend meetings, inspect facilities, or negotiate contracts but cannot perform production-line labor or construction.
Even a few days of unauthorized work can lead to removal and future bars on re-entry.

South Korean officials are pressing for faster consular access and humane processing for detained nationals, delays in deportation while legal options are reviewed, and expanded U.S. visa pathways for skilled Korean technicians supporting EV and battery lines.
American officials counter that the law must be applied evenly regardless of nationality or investment level, citing public safety and rule-of-law concerns around large construction sites.
Business leaders warn the images from Ellabell could deter new projects or push vendors to relocate planned work.
Plant operations remain uncertain, with the immediate work stoppage disrupting commissioning schedules and supplier ramp-ups.
The company must review compliance across contractors and staffing firms, coordinate with federal and state authorities, and assess timelines to restart operations.
Even if production restarts, staffing gaps and heightened oversight may slow output for months.
Local officials worry the region’s job pipeline—community college training, apprenticeship programs, and supplier expansions—could stall if timelines slip.

Legal experts say the case will reshape compliance strategies at foreign-invested manufacturing sites, expecting tighter gate controls, more rigorous document checks, a shift from short-term business entries toward employment-based visas, and higher upfront costs with longer lead times but fewer risks of sudden disruptions.

Practical steps under review include contract audits to map every role to proper work authorization, visitor vetting that flags any hands-on duties before travel, vendor training explaining what is and isn’t “work” under U.S. rules, and contingency plans to keep critical lines running if contractor staff are removed.
Many detainees came for short rotations, leaving spouses and children behind, with some facing language barriers and limited access to legal counsel.
South Korea’s government is coordinating support and charter flights, though logistical delays have increased stress.
Community groups in Georgia are trying to provide translation and basic services for those released pending proceedings.
Immigration enforcement has become a centerpiece of national debate and a rallying cry in Georgia’s tight races.

Right-leaning candidates praise the enforcement as protecting U.S. jobs and fair hiring, while critics warn the raid risks turning a major clean-energy bet into a cautionary tale and could harm U.S. manufacturing goals that depend on global partnerships.

Analysis by VisaVerge.com suggests the episode will accelerate calls in Congress for clearer rules on short-term technical work and stricter oversight of labor brokers that link overseas vendors to U.S. projects.

Talks between Seoul and Washington could produce targeted visa fixes, such as more employment-based slots for specialized technicians, or joint compliance guidance tailored to EV and battery supply chains.
Enforcement agencies appear unlikely to back away from high-visibility sites, especially where whistleblowers allege unlawful work.
Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution have not provided detailed public timelines for resuming full operations, but internal reviews are active and coordination with authorities continues.

The Ellabell operation will be studied as a test case at the crossroads of immigration enforcement, industrial policy, and global investment, showing how a single raid can freeze a complex manufacturing ecosystem, rattle alliances, and reshape hiring practices across an entire sector.
For Hyundai Metaplant America, the immediate task is stabilizing a workforce and supply chain under a bright spotlight.
For policymakers, it’s designing visa rules and worksite oversight that keep factories running while staying within the law.
source_title: Hyundai Raid in Georgia Sparks Political Firestorm Over Jobs

facts: The Georgia Manufacturing Extension Partnership (GaMEP), a program of the Enterprise Innovation Institute at Georgia Tech, received recognition from Gov. Brian P. Kemp for 65 years of service to the manufacturing industry at the Georgia State Capitol on Monday, July 14th, 2025.
The commendation acknowledges GaMEP's commitment to supporting manufacturers across the state through educational opportunities and technical assistance.
GaMEP supports an industry that adds $82 billion to the economy and employs 425,000 residents, according to the National Associations of Manufacturers.
By 1960, more than 4,500 manufacturers had planted roots across Georgia due to strategic efforts by state leaders with economic development assistance from Georgia Tech.

The Georgia General Assembly voted to establish the Georgia Tech Industrial Extension Service (now known as GaMEP) to provide statewide field offices delivering on-site technical expertise to help manufacturers stay competitive.

Tim Israel, GaMEP director and EI2 associate vice president — corporate engagement/firm-based programs, stated that in 2024, Georgia experienced a significant return on investment with GaMEP generating $294 for every state dollar allocated to manufacturing projects.
Over the past decade, GaMEP has provided assistance and education to more than 3,900 manufacturers across 144 counties.
During that same decade, GaMEP helped manufacturers create or retain 14,500 jobs.
Over the past decade, GaMEP helped manufacturers invest $1 billion in capital improvements.
Over the past decade, GaMEP helped manufacturers realize $3.5 billion in sales.
Over the past decade, GaMEP helped manufacturers save nearly $450 million in costs.
GaMEP primarily serves small- to medium-sized manufacturers, with 75% employing less than 250 workers.
GaMEP's top-served industries include fabricated metal product, food, machinery, chemical, and transportation equipment manufacturing.

David Bridges, EI2 vice president, stated that when Georgia manufacturers become more productive and profitable, they hire more people, pay better wages, and stabilize local economies, especially in rural and underserved areas of the state.
David Bridges also noted that GaMEP's work creates pathways for career advancement for frontline workers who might not have had previous opportunities.
GaMEP's reach spans 10 regions across the state, each led by a dedicated region manager who lives and works locally to offer manufacturers direct connections to expert teams and resources.
Lloyd Avram, Georgia Association of Manufacturers CEO and president, called GaMEP a trusted collaborator and valued partner in strengthening manufacturing across Georgia.

In the Coastal Region (Bryan, Bulloch, Camden, Chatham, Effingham, Glynn, Liberty, Long, McIntosh and Screven counties), GaMEP served 269 manufacturers, created/retained 283 jobs, facilitated $23,171,292 in capital improvements, saved $1,645,061 in costs, and realized $13,965,000 in sales, with Roger Wood Foods as a success story.

In the East Region (Burke, Columbia, Glascock, Hancock, Jefferson, Jenkins, Lincoln, McDuffie, Richmond, Taliaferro, Warren, Washington and Wilkes counties), GaMEP served 169 manufacturers, created/retained 3,899 jobs, facilitated $127,754,280 in capital improvements, saved $14,771,582 in costs, and realized $975,465,000 in sales, with Nutritional Resources as a success story.

In the East Central Region (Barrow, Clarke, Elbert, Greene, Jackson, Jasper, Madison, Morgan, Newton, Oconee, Oglethorpe and Walton counties), GaMEP served 209 manufacturers, created/retained 621 jobs, facilitated $19,703,035 in capital improvements, saved $2,535,494 in costs, and realized $29,486,000 in sales, with Creature Comforts Brewing Company and Poly Tech Industries as success stories.

In the Metro Atlanta Region (Cherokee, Clayton, Cobb, DeKalb, Douglas, Fayette, Fulton, Gwinnett, Henry and Rockdale counties), GaMEP served 1,601 manufacturers, created/retained 2,928 jobs, facilitated $235,763,480 in capital improvements, saved $112,083,262 in costs, and realized $844,679,890 in sales, with Compass Technology Group, Construction Specialties, and Highland Forge as success stories.

In the Middle Region (Baldwin, Bibb, Crawford, Houston, Jones, Monroe, Peach, Pulaski, Putnam, Twiggs and Wilkinson counties), GaMEP served 170 manufacturers, created/retained 972 jobs, facilitated $121,814,846 in capital improvements, saved $8,810,950 in costs, and realized $300,213,400 in sales, with Unified Defense as a success story.

In the Northeast Region (Banks, Dawson, Forsyth, Franklin, Habersham, Hall, Hart, Lumpkin, Rabun, Stephens, Towns, Union and White counties), GaMEP served 280 manufacturers, created/retained 1,029 jobs, facilitated $88,443,395 in capital improvements, saved $63,999,228 in costs, and realized $259,453,900 in sales, with Freudenberg Sealing Technologies as a success story.

In the Northwest Region (Bartow, Catoosa, Chattooga, Dade, Fannin, Floyd, Gilmer, Gordon, Haralson, Murray, Paulding, Pickens, Polk, Walker and Whitfield counties), GaMEP served 387 manufacturers, created/retained 1,090 jobs, facilitated $92,948,931 in capital improvements, saved $141,460,651 in costs, and realized $326,366,408 in sales, with Trenton Pressing as a success story.

In the South Region (Appling, Bleckley, Candler, Dodge, Emanuel, Evans, Jeff Davis, Johnson, Laurens, Montgomery, Tattnall, Telfair, Toombs, Treutlen, Wayne, Wheeler and Wilcox counties), GaMEP served 176 manufacturers, created/retained 969 jobs, facilitated $219,300,221 in capital improvements, saved $6,596,254 in costs, and realized $39,632,275 in sales, with Healthy Pet as a success story.

In the Southeast Region (Atkinson, Bacon, Ben Hill, Berrien, Brantley, Brooks, Charlton, Clinch, Coffee, Cook, Echols, Irwin, Lanier, Lowndes, Pierce, Tift, Turner and Ware counties), GaMEP served 166 manufacturers, created/retained 281 jobs, facilitated $20,684,800 in capital improvements, saved $3,098,700 in costs, and realized $77,724,500 in sales, with CJB Industries and South Georgia Pecan as success stories.

In the Southwest Region (Baker, Calhoun, Colquitt, Decatur, Dougherty, Early, Grady, Lee, Miller, Mitchell, Seminole, Terrell, Thomas and Worth counties), GaMEP served 130 manufacturers, created/retained 130 jobs, facilitated $12,266,730 in capital improvements, saved $1,682,790 in costs, and realized $5,002,020 in sales, with Sweet Grass Dairy as a success story.

In the West Region (Chattahoochee, Clay, Crisp, Dooly, Harris, Macon, Marion, Muscogee, Quitman, Randolph, Schley, Stewart, Sumter, Talbot, Taylor and Webster counties), GaMEP served 111 manufacturers, created/retained 713 jobs, facilitated $37,948,131 in capital improvements, saved $3,197,600 in costs, and realized $69,588,348 in sales, with Oneda Corporation as a success story.

In the West Central Region (Butts, Carroll, Coweta, Heard, Lamar, Meriwether, Pike, Spalding, Troup and Upson counties), GaMEP served 234 manufacturers, created/retained 1,658 jobs, facilitated $90,750,763 in capital improvements, saved $89,931,074 in costs, and realized $615,900,002 in sales, with Complete Truck Bodies and Mountville Mills as success stories.
More information about GaMEP's impact by county is available online.
source_title: Georgia Manufacturing Extension Partnership at Georgia Tech Celebrates 65 Years of Service - Tifton CEO

facts: Valeo is completing a €45 million (US$51.75 million) modernization of its Angers lighting plant in France.
The modernization program was carried out between 2023 and 2026.
The investment establishes Angers as Valeo’s European competence center for heavy goods vehicle (HGV) headlights.
The project supports the Valeo Elevate 2028 strategic plan.
The plant employs more than 1,200 people across Angers and Blois.
The facility produces 9,000 headlights and 22,000 modules per day for leading global car manufacturers.
The modernized facility operates 25 injection presses, five varnishing lines, 11 assembly lines, and 181 robots.
Artificial intelligence is utilized in the plant for predictive simulation and real-time verification.
The heavy-duty segment is identified as the main driver of growth for the plant.
Production share for the heavy-duty segment is expected to increase from 20% to 40%.
Two major customers are scheduled to enter service at the plant in 2027 and 2028.
Six of the seven European road transport leaders are projected to use headlights developed by Angers by 2030.
Jean-Luc Terrasse, Chief Executive Officer of Valeo’s Light Division, stated that lighting is at the heart of tomorrow’s mobility challenges regarding safety, design, and intelligent interaction.

Terrasse noted that the investment allows the factory to consolidate its industrial excellence and demonstrates that a French factory can design and produce systems of unprecedented optical complexity at the forefront of European Industry 4.0.
The Angers investment serves as a test case for European industrial policy, demonstrating that complex optical manufacturing is feasible in France.
source_title: Valeo completes 45 million euro maintenance of Angers lighting plant - Automobile Magazine

facts: Valeo is a French global automotive supplier headquartered in Paris.
The company supplies a wide range of products to automakers and the aftermarket.
The Group employs 113,600 people in 29 countries worldwide.
It operates 186 production plants, 66 R&D centers, and 15 distribution platforms.
In 2018, Valeo's sales rose 4% to €19.1 billion.
The company frequently ranks as France's leading patent filer.
The company was founded in 1923 in Saint-Ouen, near Paris, originally as the Société Anonyme Française de Ferodo.
In 1932, the company began producing clutches.
In 1932, it was listed on the Paris Stock Exchange.
The firm produced braking systems in 1961, thermal systems in 1962, and lighting and electrical systems between 1971-1978 and in 1984.

International development began in the 1960s, expanding to Spain, Italy, Brazil (1974), the United States (1980), Tunisia (1984), South Korea and Turkey (1988), Mexico (1989), China (1994), India (1997), Central and Eastern Europe, and Egypt (2005), reaching 33 countries by the end of 2018.
In May 1980, the company was renamed Valeo, which means "I am well" in Latin.
The company acquired German automotive suppliers Peiker and Spheros in 2015, followed by FTE automotive in 2017.
In the early 2000s, Valeo produced parking assistance systems using ultrasonic sensors.
In 2017, Valeo presented a low-voltage prototype electric vehicle at the Consumer Electronics Show in Las Vegas.
In February 2021, Valeo announced an investment of $5 million to open a new assembly plant in Bessemer, Alabama, expected to create 70 jobs.

Since 2009, CEO Jacques Aschenbroich's strategy has focused on innovation linked to CO2 emission reduction, intuitive driving, and geographical expansion in high-growth potential countries like Asia and emerging markets.
From 2009 to 2018, the number of employees increased from 45,000 to 113,600.
From 2009 to 2018, the Group's sales rose from €7.5 billion to €19.1 billion with strong development in China.
Valeo invests 11.8% of its sales in R&D and innovation.
The company employs 17,900 research and development staff.
In 2016 and 2017, the Group ranked as France's leading patent filer.
In November 2025, Valeo announced its 'Elevate 2028' strategy.
The strategy targets steadily increasing profit from 2022 onwards, generating higher cash from 2025 onwards, and returning to sales growth from 2027 onwards.
The goal is to consolidate its position as a global leader in key car technologies across regions like China, India, and North America.
The strategy fully aligns the company for a future of electrified, safer, and software-defined cars.

The company is structured around four business groups: Comfort & Driving Assistance Systems, Powertrain Systems (automotive powertrain components and vehicle electrification), Thermal Systems, and Visibility Systems.
Its Valeo Service activity supplies original equipment spares to automakers and replacement parts to the independent aftermarket.
Original equipment sales by destination region are distributed as follows: Europe and Africa at 46%, Asia at 32%, North America at 20%, and South America at 2%.

In 2006, Valeo, its directors, and its corporate medical function were indicted for homicide, unintentional injury, and failure to assist regarding former asbestos use that caused the death of hundreds of employees.
The group has set up high health-security standards and thoroughly monitors employees and retired personnel.
This policy was ratified in a national agreement with the French government in April 2009.
The group secured four former industrial sites with asbestos-related issues.
source_title: Valeo - Wikipedia

facts: Offers career opportunities in research and development, quality management, production, sales, purchasing, or finance.
Holds numerous worldwide patents demonstrating a commitment to technical excellence and sustainable mobility.
Provides pathways for employees to pursue a management career or become an expert in a product or process.
Accepts applications simply and directly online via its job board.
source_title: Professionals and managers - Career opportunities | Valeo

facts: A French auto parts maker will build a $225 million manufacturing plant in McAllen, according to city leaders.
Valeo is based out of Paris, France.
Valeo is a leading automotive technology company with operations in more than 30 countries worldwide.
The 200,000-square-foot facility will be built near Ware Road and Idela Avenue.
The facility is expected to be fully operational by January 2027.
The facility is expected to create up to 500 new jobs.
The facility will produce transformative automotive technology that advances the future of mobility and strengthens domestic manufacturing capacity.
A news release stated that Valeo's decision to invest in McAllen represents a significant expansion of advanced manufacturing in South Texas.
The investment reinforces McAllen's position within the regional and binational automotive supply chain.

Valeo North America President Jeffrey Shay stated that McAllen offers a highly skilled workforce, a strategic location within the North American automotive corridor, and strong collaboration among local and regional partners.
The investment will support customers with advanced, innovative technologies while contributing to the continued growth of the U.S. automotive industry.
source_title: French auto parts maker Valeo announces $225 million manufacturing plant in McAllen

facts: Rivian is a California-based company and a partner of Volkswagen.
Rivian already operates a vehicle plant in Normal, Illinois, producing the electric delivery van EDV, R1 models, and the mid-sized electric SUV R2.
The Illinois facility's current total capacity is 215,000 vehicles per year.
Rivian has long planned to build a second vehicle plant in Georgia near Atlanta.
The groundbreaking ceremony for the Georgia plant took place in September.
Work at the Stanton Springs North site in Georgia has focused solely on site development, infrastructure, and civil engineering, while building construction has not yet begun.
Rivian revised its plans to increase the Georgia factory's annual capacity to 300,000 vehicles, up from the previously planned 200,000.
The expansion is funded by a $4.5 billion loan from the US Department of Energy.
The loan comprises a credit sum of $4.006 billion and $494 million in capitalized interest.
The DOE loan will only need to be repaid after the construction phase.
Rivian Founder and CEO RJ Scaringe stated, “We’re very excited to partner with the U.S. Department of Energy to grow our manufacturing footprint in Georgia.”

Scaringe noted that the R2 dramatically expands their market opportunity and that thousands of dedicated people will soon work in the Georgia plant to scale American manufacturing and ensure US leadership in innovation and technology.
The new Georgia plant will produce the R2 and the upcoming compact SUV R3.
The R3 is expected to be more affordable than the R2 and will be exported.
Rivian plans to manufacture 50,000 robotaxis for its partner and investor Uber at the Georgia plant.
Production of these models at the Georgia plant is scheduled to begin at the end of 2028.
In the first quarter of 2026, Rivian produced 10,236 vehicles and made 10,365 customer deliveries.
Rivian's revenue in Q1 2026 increased by 11% to $1.38 billion.
Automotive segment revenue declined by 2% to $908 million due to a $100 million drop in regulatory credit sales and a higher proportion of commercial vehicles lowering revenue per unit delivered.
Vehicle deliveries rose by 20% in Q1 2026.
The software and services segment grew 49% to $473 million, driven by the development partnership with Volkswagen following successful winter tests of its series-oriented zonal architecture.
Volkswagen invested an additional $1 billion in Rivian as equity.
VW's stake in Rivian increased from 12.3% to around 16%, making it likely the largest shareholder ahead of Amazon.
Rivian reported a Q1 2026 operating loss of $881 million, compared to $655 million in the prior year quarter, citing lower gross margins and higher operating costs.
Rivian's Q1 2026 net loss was $416 million, a 23% reduction from $541 million in the previous year's quarter.
source_title: Rivian plans Georgia plant 50% larger - electrive.com

facts: French auto supplier Valeo and American partner V. Johnson Enterprises LLC formed a joint venture called Detroit Thermal Systems LLC to acquire the climate control business of Automotive Components Holdings currently located in its Sheldon Road plant in Plymouth Township.
DTS announced that it would move production to a 365,000-square-foot plant at 6505 Cogswell St. in Romulus, pending approval of state and local incentives.
Operational transfers are scheduled to begin in mid-2013 and conclude by the end of 2014.
Ford Motor Co. and ACH signed agreements with DTS for the sale of the business Oct. 25.
V. Johnson Enterprises, owned by former Detroit Piston Vinnie Johnson, will hold 51 percent of DTS, while Valeo will hold the remaining 49 percent.
DTS will produce climate control systems and components for the automotive industry, including supporting current product supply commitments to Ford previously held by ACH.
Ultimately, DTS is expected to employ approximately 500 people.
Valeo employs 73,800 people at 128 plants in 29 countries worldwide.
This announcement marks the fulfillment of the ACH strategy and the 10th ACH sale of an operation or plant.
ACH was started in October 2005 with 17 automotive components plants.
Ford president of The Americas Mark Fields stated that few companies take the longer-term, comprehensive approach taken with the restructuring of ACH.
Mark Fields noted that Ford's goal was to transform operations into businesses that would attract top suppliers while preserving as many jobs as possible.
The sale expands Ford's business with minority-owned suppliers.
DTS intends to apply for certification as a minority business enterprise through the Michigan Minority Business Development Council.
Valeo CEO Jacques Aschenbroich called the acquisition a strategic breakthrough that will strengthen ties with Ford Motor Company in North America and enhance Valeo's presence across North America.
Valeo will become a member of Ford's Aligned Business Framework supplier program through this joint venture.
The joint venture will enable Valeo to broaden its offering of innovative thermal system products and support customers in meeting CO2 emissions reduction challenges.
Detroit Thermal Systems chairman and CEO Vinnie Johnson expressed pride in creating the company and committing to manufacturing world-class climate control systems.
Vinnie Johnson stated that the new organization's growth will help revitalize local communities by creating jobs for minorities, military veterans, and others hard hit by the economic crisis.
DTS will manufacture heating, ventilation and air conditioning units, initially for popular North American Ford vehicles, with future plans to develop offerings for new customers.
The deal gives DTS access to Valeo's leading edge technology in HVAC modules, A/C systems and Powertrain Cooling Systems.
Current ACH employees at the Sheldon Road plant will be offered opportunities to apply for employment at DTS.
The company is committed to promoting a diverse workforce by providing job opportunities for minorities and military veterans.
The DTS plant will be vertically integrated with in-house capability for manufacturing of components and assembly of complete HVAC modules.
Included in the equipment line-up will be 30 precision injection molding presses to manufacture plastic components.
The new plant will have the capacity to produce more than two million HVAC systems annually.
The manufacturing systems will be designed to meet the automotive industry's highest standards for efficiency and manufacturing quality.
The plant will be eco-friendly, with initiatives to minimize energy and water use and maximize material recycling.
source_title: Valeo, Vinnie Johnson Buy Ford Climate Control Biz, Plan Romulus Plant - CBS Detroit

facts: Valeo is the world leader of high and low-voltage electric powertrain solutions.
Valeo is the world No. 2 in thermal management systems that ensure the performance and range of electric vehicles.

The automotive sector has largely bet on electrification to quickly reduce CO2 emissions as part of the 2016 Paris Agreement, which aims to limit global warming to a level below 2°C compared to pre-industrial levels.
600 new models of EVs are due to hit the market in 2025.
Valeo has invested more than 10 billion euros in CO2 emissions reduction technologies over the last decade.
Valeo has nearly 100 years of experience in propulsion and thermal systems.
In terms of OEM sales, Valeo is the world leader in automotive electrification for high- and low-voltage solutions.
Valeo is the inventor of the Stop-Start system and affordable hybrid solutions thanks to its 48V systems that enable electrification at the lowest cost.
A third of existing electric vehicles in the world are equipped with Valeo solutions to reduce CO2 emissions.
Valeo's sales in electrification increased 26-fold between 2009 and 2021.
Valeo is the undisputed world leader in 48V systems with a market share of more than 30% of the world market.
Valeo acquired Valeo Siemens eAutomotive in July 2022.
Valeo's solutions are found on both the standard and premium platforms of more than 20 car manufacturers worldwide.
By the end of 2022, 90 electric and plug-in hybrid car models equipped with Valeo’s high-voltage technologies will be marketed globally.
An EV contains 2.5 times more equipment related to thermal systems than a traditional combustion vehicle.
The market for thermal management systems for electric vehicles (coolant heater, battery cooler, electric compressor, heat pump) will reach 21 billion euros in 2025.
Valeo equipped the first hybrids in 2006 with its cooling solution.
Valeo is the world leader in all cooling solutions (air, liquid or refrigerant) and for all types of cells (cylindrical, prismatic, pouch cells) in series.
The electrification market is witnessing very strong growth of +30% between 2021 and 2025.
Valeo develops solutions for two-, three- or four-wheeled vehicles including electric bicycles, scooters, motorcycles, robot-taxis, urban quadricycles, and delivery droids using its 48V expertise.
Valeo has recorded more than 200 million euros in orders for the electrification of new mobility solutions.
Valeo is targeting more than 500 million euros in revenues in the new mobility market in 2030.
source_title: Vehicle electrification solutions | Valeo

facts: Cooling and air management technologies are critical components of internal combustion engines, but electrified vehicles use a variety of systems depending on whether they are hybrids, plug-in hybrids, or battery-electric.
A 17-minute free webinar covers the major battery cooling systems used in electric vehicles and hybrids.
Batteries are very sensitive to temperature changes, and excessive heat or extreme cold can cause performance issues or damage the battery.
The webinar explores how batteries are affected by temperature and how technology can mitigate it.
Various methods to dissipate heat away from the battery are covered, along with technologies ideal for hybrids, battery electric, and other electrified vehicles.
Battery cooling technology has evolved over the past few decades, becoming increasingly efficient.
Three major types of battery cooling systems in electric vehicles and hybrids are explored, including their advantages and downsides.
The content explains the differences between active and passive, indirect and direct, and air versus liquid cooling.
Manufacturers face challenges in improving charging performance without overheating the battery.
Improvements in electric car battery cooling can lead to faster charging times.
One older cooling technology may start appearing in more new models due to reasons explored in the webinar.
Innovative new systems combine air conditioning and a battery cooling system for electric vehicles.
The ideal battery cooling system depends on the specific vehicle.
Electrified vehicles are a rapidly growing category expected to expand as the industry transitions away from internal combustion engines.
This shift will drive demand for mechanics familiar with high-voltage systems, which require special training to handle safely.
The webinar covers the basic functioning of various cooling technologies likely to be encountered in garages.
Fault codes for EV battery cooling systems are provided to help diagnose whether an issue is caused by the battery cooler or another electric component.
Valeo produces an array of parts for electrified vehicles, including 48v systems for OE manufacturers and the aftermarket alike.
source_title: EV Battery Cooling: Leading Technologies | Valeo Service

facts: Valeo and VoltR are two French companies collaborating on the circularity of micromobility batteries.
The collaboration utilizes Valeo’s experience in electronic battery repair and VoltR’s expertise in battery reconditioning and cell pack manufacturing.
The companies state the initiative will enable material savings of “at least 95 per cent.”
The offer targets fleet managers, vehicle manufacturers, repair networks, and battery producers amid a boom of micromobility electric vehicles in Europe.
Micromobility electric vehicles include electric bicycles, scooters, and other light vehicles.
The process first determines whether used batteries can be repaired.
If batteries need replacement, VoltR provides “remanufactured energy packs with a compatible cell reference to ensure the maintenance of initial certifications and pack performances.”
Valeo and VoltR provide a structured, local warranty and after-sales service for reconditioned energy packs.

Isabelle d’Ambrosio-Pierre, Valeo Service OES & Smart Mobility Vice President, stated the solution addresses environmentally-friendly battery management, economical mobility, and reliable access to repair components.
d’Ambrosio-Pierre noted the battery repair solution is innovative and essential for less carbon-intensive urban mobility.
Valeo collaborates with PanGood to develop an Axial Flux Generator.
Valeo uses Rohm SiC modules for electric drives.
Valeo cooperates on electric motor development with Mahle.
Maxime Bleskine, General Manager of VoltR, stated VoltR’s mission is to minimize the environmental impact of batteries by maximizing their lifespan through remanufacturing.
Bleskine added that partnering with Valeo allows them to industrialize their approach and scale their offering across Europe.
source_title: Valeo and VoltR collaborate on the circularity of micromobility batteries - electrive.com

facts: Forecasts strong sales growth in 2023.
Recorded record orders in 2022.
2022 order volume was helped by demand for car electrification and driving-assistance technology.
These factors helped the company navigate a challenging year for the automotive sector in 2022.
source_title: Valeo earnings: More growth expected as new technologies boost orders - Automotive News

facts: French car parts maker Valeo expected to report lower core profit margin in 2022 amid geopolitical tensions and inflationary pressures, after its 2021 margin missed the group’s target.
RBC analyst Tom Narayan noted that Valeo’s H2/21 results were consistent with the Jan 26 pre-announce, but 2022 guidance was well below consensus expectations.
Analyst Tom Narayan added that this caution aligns with earlier statements from Renault, Michelin, Faurecia, and Stellantis regarding the global chip shortage.
Valeo shares dropped 10% in midday trade, heading for its worst day since March 2020.
Semiconductor suppliers, car parts producers, and carmakers expect some improvement in component shortages this year as chipmakers boost production capacity to meet demand.
Valeo specializes in the design, production, and sale of components and services for the automotive sector.
Valeo forecast its 2022 annual core profit (EBITDA) margin in a range of 11.8% to 12.3%, compared with 13.4% last year.
The company sees 2022 sales between 19.2 billion and 20.0 billion euros, representing a growth of 11-16% from a year earlier.

Valeo said the outlook excluded possible effects from the Ukraine-Russia crisis, which could drag production volumes and hike energy or raw material prices, but included a negative impact from the integration of Valeo Siemens eAutomotive.

CEO Christophe Perillat told a press conference that the direct impact of the Ukraine-Russia crisis should be minimal, stating the company has no sales in Ukraine while those in Russia account for less than 1% of its total sales.
Valeo plans to increase its original equipment sales by around 13% in the medium term to reach a revenue of 27.5 billion euros ($30.73 billion) in 2025, betting on recovery in car production volumes.
The company expects further revenue growth to 40 billion euros by 2030.
source_title: Valeo gives cautious 2022 outlook amid global uncertainties – Wall Street Observer

facts: There’s 7.2 million square feet of manufacturing space set to break ground in mid-2023 or early 2024.
source_title: Atlanta manufacturing pipeline skyrockets to highest level in a decade - Atlanta Business Chronicle

facts: Valeo has officially inaugurated the Valeo Nanjing expansion plant in Yangtze Delta Region, Nanjing, one of China’s major automotive manufacturing bases.
This inauguration marks a milestone in Valeo’s strategy of continuous development in Asia and consolidates its position as a global leader in transmission systems.
Reducing CO2 emissions and boosting the Group’s presence in Asia and emerging countries are the two pillars of Valeo’s global strategy.
The Nanjing plant extension is a further step to accompany the company’s growth in China, alongside their 22 existing plants, four new plants and two other extensions being built this year.
On top of current activity for clutches and torque converters, the Nanjing plant will also be the first site in the world for Valeo to produce double-clutch systems.
In order to meet the increasing global demand for CO2 emission reduction, auto suppliers are developing advanced transmission technologies.
Valeo double clutch system is able to improve energy efficiency during power transmission which leads to better fuel economy.
In 2011, Valeo Nanjing was awarded by the Jiangning Environment Bureau ‘Green Company’ certification for its outstanding environmental behavior.
It has also been recognized by Shanghai GM for its excellent work in the Green Supply Chain Project.
Valeo has received strong support from Nanjing Municipal People’s Government since its establishment.
Valeo’s footprint in China can be traced back to 1994.
In line with its ‘Becoming Chinese in China’ philosophy, Valeo China has been playing an important role in the local market.
Based on its know-how and rich experience, Valeo is gradually improving the production capacity in China.
Today it includes 22 sites.
In 2012, three new plants were opened (Guangzhou, Wuhu and Huadu).
In 2013, three plants are being extended (Foshan, Wuhan and Nanjing) and four other new ones are under construction.

Valeo is an independent industrial Group fully focused on the design, production and sale of components, integrated systems and modules for the automotive industry, mainly for CO2 emissions reduction.
Valeo ranks among the world’s top automotive suppliers.
With operations in 28 countries through its 123 plants, 20 research centers, 36 development centres and 12 distribution platforms, the Group employs 73,000 people.
source_title: Valeo confident of Chinese market • Asia Manufacturing News

facts: Vanguard National Trailer is a national leader in the production of dry-box trailers for industrial companies.
The company is still interviewing for both day and night shift positions.
Open interviews are held Monday through Friday from 9:15 AM to 11:00 AM; applicants should ask for Andy.
Family Day event is confirmed, with lunch scheduled at Noon at Pavilions 5, 6, 7, and 8.
The facility operates on a two-shift schedule, both running Monday through Thursday.
Day shift hours are Monday through Thursday from 6:00 AM to 4:30 PM.
Night shift hours are Monday through Thursday from 5:00 PM to 3:30 AM.
The company is not currently working overtime but this may change as daily production goals increase.
Current production is 25 trailers per day, with a plan to increase to 35 by September.
Newly hired hourly employees receive an Hourly Base Rate, Weekly Attendance Bonus, Monthly Quality Bonus, Retention Bonuses at 90 and 180 Days, and Quarterly Pay Increases.
Benefits include 10 Days Vacation per Year, Holiday Pay, and Medical/Dental/Vision coverage for $20.35/week, a rate that has not increased in over 10 years.
Applications can be submitted in person at 8 Vanguard Drive in Trenton.
The facility will be closed on Memorial Day.
Hiring is being conducted to meet increasing demand for trailers as business conditions are good.
The company is adding 5 assembly positions on the day shift.
Effective Monday, April 20th, employees will resume four-day work weeks.
Production was suspended due to inclement weather for the night shift and the following day, with regular production resuming on Wednesday.
The facility address is 8 Vanguard Drive, Trenton, GA 30752.
Operating hours are Monday through Friday from 8am to 5pm.
source_title: Vanguard National Trailer in Trenton GA, 8 Vanguard Drive, Trenton, GA (2026)

facts: Vanguard National Trailer Corp. and Vanguard CIMC Reefer Trailer, Inc. state that each employee contributes directly to the company's growth and success.
The company offers a full range of benefits including Medical, Dental, Vision, Life Insurance, Disability Insurance, 401k with company match, and Employee Assistance Program.
Vanguard operates a facility located at 8 Vanguard Drive in Trenton, GA.
The company is hiring for an Automation Technician position in the Maintenance department to respond and react to mechanical issues in the production environment.
The company is hiring for a Maintenance Technician position in the Maintenance department to install, maintain, and repair machinery, equipment, physical structures, and pipe and electrical systems.
The company is hiring for a Manufacturing Supervisor position in the Manufacturing department to supervise and coordinate activities of production workers.
The company is hiring for a Production Assembler position in the Production department to perform repetitive line assembly operations to mass-produce products such as dry van trailers.

The company is hiring for a Security Guard position in the Security department to guard, patrol, or monitor premises to prevent theft, violence, or infractions of rules.

canonical_name: U.S. International Trade Commission (ITC) Antidumping and Countervailing Duty Petition
facts: The U.S. truck market is at the center of a trade dispute over alleged dumping by foreign trailer manufacturers from Canada, China, and Mexico.
The U.S. International Trade Commission (ITC) opened an investigation to determine whether imported trailers are sold below fair value and receive government subsidies.
The American Trailer Manufacturers Coalition filed an antidumping and countervailing duty petition regarding the unfair practices.
The coalition is made up of domestic companies including Great Dane, Stoughton, and Wabash.

The coalition argues that trailer imports from Canada, China, and Mexico have increased significantly, displacing domestic production and causing declines in activity, employment, and profitability among U.S. manufacturers.

Hyundai Translead has expanded its presence in Mexico since 2016, importing an average of nearly 57,000 trailers per year between 2017 and 2024, which is double the volume recorded between 2010 and 2015.
Vanguard National Trailer Corporation's parent company, China International Marine Containers (CIMC), increased imports of subassemblies from China.
CIMC opened a plant in Canada in 2020 after U.S. tariffs were imposed in 2018.
Canadian imports from the CIMC plant have risen by 147% between 2021 and 2022 since operations began in 2021.
The coalition claims that low-priced imports created an oversupply in 2022 and 2023, causing new trailer orders to drop sharply in 2024.
The coalition warns that persistent oversupply will keep demand weak and allow imports to continue pressuring domestic manufacturers through unfair pricing.
Estimated dumping margins in the petition are nearly 300% for Canada, 1,400% for China, and more than 430% for Mexico.
The ITC is expected to issue a preliminary ruling on January 5.
The Department of Commerce will release its own ruling around mid-2026.
The final determination is expected by the end of next year, though tariffs could be imposed at any point during the process.
The American Trailer Manufacturers Coalition secured a victory in May when it requested that truck trailers be included among steel and aluminum derivative products subject to Section 232 tariffs.
The request was approved in August, applying a 25% tariff on the value of the steel and aluminum contained in the trailers.
Washington's trade barriers are disrupting land freight flows, raising the cost of heavy equipment, and reshaping traditional route maps for U.S. truckers.

facts: Vernay is based in Griffin, Georgia.
Vernay received the Silver Award in the Smart Transportation Battery Solutions Category of the 2024 Edison Awards for developing the Vernay VoltaVent®.
The Vernay VoltaVent® is a pioneering battery ventilation device designed to enhance safety and efficiency in battery systems.
The VoltaVent® intelligently manages the internal pressure of the battery pack at critical moments, such as during thermal runaway.
The device swiftly vents hot gases to prevent explosions and then reseals.
This innovative flow control solution approach enhances the safety of battery-operated devices and extends their operational life and reliability.
Bob Ferguson, VP of Global R&D, stated that the award acknowledges the remarkable engineering innovation of VoltaVent® and the teamwork in place at Vernay.
Andy Woodward, VP of Sales & Marketing, described the VoltaVent® as a commitment to safety, performance, and sustainability.
Vernay Laboratories has been a technology leader in the design, development, and manufacture of customized flow-control components since its founding in 1946.
The company formulates its own compounds to address each specific application.
Vernay’s products play a vital role in millions of medical devices, automobiles, and consumer products.
The privately held firm produces more than 1 billion components per year.
Vernay draws from a library of more than 33,000 elastomeric materials and compound formulations.
The company operates six plants around the world, in the U.S., the Netherlands, Italy, and China.
source_title: Vernay VoltaVent® Wins Silver Edison Award  - Vernay

entity_name: Georgia Department of Economic Development State of Small Business Program
canonical_name: GDEcD State of Small Business Outreach Program
facts: Governor Kemp announced a new position to support and empower Georgia’s small business community less than a year prior to March 2022.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) established a Director of Small Business Outreach based on a recommendation from Governor Kemp’s Georgians First Commission.
In Georgia, a small business is defined as independently owned and operated with fewer than 300 employees or less than $30 million in yearly gross receipts.
More than 1.6 million Georgians are employed by these small businesses across sectors including manufacturing, retail, arts, hospitality, film production, and more.
Director of Small Business Outreach Allen Fox travels the state to discuss GDEcD services and support available to small businesses.

Approximately 80% of GDEcD’s work involves small businesses through its Global Commerce, International Trade, Tourism, Center of Innovation, Entertainment, Georgia Council for the Arts, and International Relations teams.
In November 2021, Fox launched the “State of Small Business” program featuring a series of regional roundtables connecting GDEcD team members with small business stakeholders.
The inaugural roundtable event was held in Macon to allow state and local organizations to hear directly from small business owners about their efforts, needs, and concerns.
Additional “State of Small Business” roundtables have been presented in Augusta, Butts County, Columbus, the Georgia Black Chamber, Rome, and other locations.
The program continues to bring together businesses, academic institutions, chambers, and organizations to support local small businesses across Georgia.
Interested Georgia communities or organizations can host a “State of Small Business” presentation by contacting Allen Fox via email.
GDEcD plans to celebrate Georgia’s 2022 Small Business Rock Stars during the week of May 2–7, 2022, highlighting outstanding and impactful small businesses in the state.

links.context: State of Small Business program information
Contact for hosting State of Small Business presentations

source_title: GDEcD State of Small Business: A Roundtable Opportunity - Albany CEO

facts: Critical Metals Corp. is an emerging leader in rare earth supply and one of the few companies advancing a fully integrated rare earth supply chain outside of China.
The company is developing world-class capabilities from resource extraction and processing to advanced materials manufacturing.
Operations in Greenland unlock the Tanbreez Project, which contains one of the richest untapped rare earth deposits in the world.
Tanbreez utilizes hydro-electric power and is expected to be one of the greenest mining operations in the world.
The company's essential materials power critical industries including clean energy, electric mobility, defense, aerospace, robotics, and advanced technologies.
The automotive industry is a major consumer of rare earths, with neodymium, dysprosium, and praseodymium used in electric vehicle motors.
The global EV market is expected to reach over 40 million vehicles by 2030, significantly increasing demand for these critical metals.

Tanbreez contains a significant percentage of the global supply of critical minerals restricted by China: Yttrium (45%), terbium (45%), dysprosium (62%), gadolinium (35%), lutetium (80%), and samarium (19%).
The deposit also has high concentrations of zirconium, niobium, tantalum, hafnium, and gallium.
Tanbreez contains the majority of the global supply of zirconium metal, which is critical for hypersonic weapons and is relatively easy to extract from this type of ore.
The mining operation concept involves an open pit mine with onsite dry magnetic separation and a floating dock to load ore directly onto ships alongside a 200-foot-deep fjord.

The mine will sell feldspar and arfvedsonite for construction materials to ensure profitability, while Heavy Rare Earth Elements (HREE) and other key minerals are contained in eudialyte with an average grade of 0.4% total Rare Earth Oxides that include 27% HREE.
The fjord is ice-free year-round, with winter temperatures in the low 30s to high 20s Fahrenheit and summer temperatures in the 50s Fahrenheit.
Ships leave the fjord directly to North America or Europe without transiting a choke point.
Critical Metals Corp. (Nasdaq: CRML) has successfully acquired a strategic Arctic ship housing asset providing long-term accommodations at the Tanbreez site for up to 300 workers.
The company commenced a 10,000-meter drilling campaign at the Tanbreez Rare Earth Project in Greenland in June 2026.
Critical Metals Corp. is proposing an acquisition of European Lithium, with amendments to implementation mechanics not changing the agreed Scheme consideration or principal conditions to completion.
source_title: Home - Critical Metals Corp

facts: Vista Metals plans to invest approximately $60 million in a new specialty aluminum products operation in Bowling Green, Kentucky.
The project will create over 50 full-time jobs across operations, planning, engineering, and site management roles.
Initial site preparation for the facility is already underway, with a phased build-out planned to support core market growth.
Vista Metals intends to acquire more than 100 acres for the new site.
The company will construct a 75,000-plus-square-foot facility in Bowling Green.
Kentucky Governor Andy Beshear congratulated Vista Metals and highlighted Warren County as a leading community for the state's rapid metals industry growth.

CEO Andy Primack cited Bowling Green's strong industrial base, qualified manufacturing talent pool, business-education collaboration, and long-term community development plan as critical factors for the location choice.
Warren County Judge-Executive Doug Gorman stated the expansion will create job opportunities and solidify Warren County's position as a hub for innovation and manufacturing excellence.

President Kelly Thomas noted the investment underscores Vista Metals' commitment to the aerospace industry and robust supply chains, anticipating commercial aircraft build rates will more than double over the next two decades.
The company's strategy aims to enable customers to test and qualify the facility ahead of projected market growth, alongside ongoing investments in people, processes, and equipment at existing sites.
VMC Specialty Alloys LLC, operating as Vista Metals Georgia, functions under a licensing agreement with Fontana, California-based Vista Metals Corp.

Alongside Vista Progressive Metals LLC, which produces aluminum lithium products, the corporate group is a market leader in specialty aluminum products for aerospace, commercial aircraft, defense, automotive, and general industrial applications.
Bowling Green Mayor Todd Alcott emphasized the city's skilled workforce and robust infrastructure as key reasons Vista Metals chose the area.
Officials noted the investment builds upon what is described as the best four-year period for economic growth in Kentucky's history.
source_title: Vista Metals to invest $60 Million in Aluminum Casting Facility in Bowling Green

The $70 million investment is expected to create 220 jobs.
It completes the first phase of the Group’s internationalization program in the automotive sector.
Since 2012 voestalpine has invested more than $140 million in new plants in China, Germany, Romania, South Africa, and the U.S.
The plant will assemble phs-ultraform® components and stamped press parts.
The plant will produce laser-welded blanks and other spare parts for OEMs.

facts: Georgia Ports Authority is doubling the space for auto processing at Colonel’s Island in Brunswick, Ga.
The Savannah Harbor Expansion Project work to deepen the entrance channel to 49 feet and extend it by seven miles is more than 55 percent complete.
The deeper water will allow Garden City Terminal to better accommodate vessels carrying up to 13,0020-foot equivalent container units.
At Garden City Terminal, the design phase for a new mega rail terminal reached its first major milestone in March after receiving overwhelming community support.
Construction contracts on sections of the Garden City mega rail terminal project should be awarded by the end of 2017, with full completion slated for 2020.
In Chatsworth, Ga., construction has begun on GPA’s second inland terminal to improve rail service and open new markets in Alabama, Tennessee, and Kentucky.
Operations at the Appalachian Regional Port in Chatsworth are scheduled to commence next year.
At Colonel’s Island at the Port of Brunswick, 214 acres are in the design or development stages, bringing auto processing space to 547 acres.
The Authority plans to add another 510 acres on the island’s south side to support auto processing, which will double the capacity for cars and heavy machinery in Brunswick.
Georgia has been ranked No. 1 state for business for a fourth consecutive year by Site Selection Magazine.

Gov. Nathan Deal stated that the collaborative framework on state and local levels has led to the creation of hundreds of thousands of jobs, enhanced community outreach by investing millions in local areas, and improved the overall quality of life for all citizens.
In the last four years since 2011, more than 575,000 private sector jobs have been created in Georgia.
The Port of Savannah will commission four new ship-to-shore cranes at Garden City Terminal this spring, with each new crane able to lift 65 long tons to a height of 152 feet above the dock.

GPA Chief Operating Officer Ed McCarthy stated that expanding capabilities allows the Authority to grow along with customers and adjust to logistics marketplace changes such as increasing vessel sizes.
The new cranes are designed by Konecranes of Finland and can work the largest ships calling on the U.S. East Coast.
Garden City Terminal will add another three cranes this spring, bringing the total number to 26 this year, with another four Neo-Panamax cranes on order for commissioning in 2018.
The growing crane fleet working over nearly 10,000 contiguous feet of dock will be able to handle more than 1,000 container moves per hour when all 30 cranes are in place.
Ed McCarthy stated that the terminal infrastructure improvements demonstrate GPA’s commitment to expand capacity and provide greater flexibility to meet customer needs.
Nordic Logistics and Warehousing, a member of AGRO Merchants Group, announced plans to develop a new cold storage and repack facility in Savannah.
In April 2016, AGRO and Nordic completed a 200,000 square-foot expansion to their existing Savannah facility, representing the second investment in the market.
The new AGRO facility will initially be built to 175,000 square feet adjacent to the current Nordic site with additional land available for future expansion.

The facility will have 7 multi-temperature rooms with 7,000 total pallet positions, 25,000 square-feet for repacking and bagging operations, and multi-use space set aside to support onsite fumigation and ripening.
With 25 doors and 85 foot docks, the facility will support efficient product unloading, receiving, inspection, and grading.
Construction of the AGRO facility is planned to begin in the first quarter of 2017 with operations to begin in the fourth quarter.
The new facility will be developed and operated with resource expertise from Lucca Freezer and Cold Storage in Vineland, New Jersey, which was acquired by AGRO Merchants in 2014.
Sonoco announced it will invest $20 million in the development of a new packaging center to support Duracell’s new North America battery packaging operation.
Sonoco’s Display and Packaging unit will be located in Duracell’s new leased facility in the Atlanta area.
Sonoco will install and operate state-of-the-art primary packaging equipment at the new center and provide all packaging materials.
The company will also produce retail merchandising displays which will be packed out at the same facility.
Variety Wholesalers, Inc. will establish its second major distribution center in Coweta County, creating 320 jobs and investing approximately $10.5 million in the Newnan area.
The distribution center will support the company’s stores as it expands its presence in the southeastern, midwestern, and south central United States.
Variety Wholesalers is based in Henderson, N.C., and operates 360 stores in 16 states.
The newly created jobs at the distribution center will include positions in management, operations, transportation, and logistics.
Georgia Quick Start will provide customized training for new distribution center employees.

Wilson Sawyer, chief operating officer of Variety Wholesalers, stated that the opening is the company’s first distribution center outside North Carolina and that they currently operate 48 of their 360 stores in Georgia with a new store opening in Newnan.

Alliance Tire Group opened a new warehouse in Savannah, Georgia, building a state-of-the-art, 202,000-square-foot facility to permit high efficiency operations such as cross-docking and enhanced put-away systems.
John Mancinelli serves as Vice President of Operations for Alliance Tire Americas.
GPA, Cordele Intermodal Services, Glovis America, Hyundai Glovis, and Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia were selected to receive the 2016 Alliance Award by Logistics Management and SMC³.

In the alliance recognized by the award, Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia moves auto parts via rail from the Port of Savannah to the Cordele Intermodal Services inland terminal, and then to West Point, Ga., via motor carrier.
A savings of more than 6 million truck miles are created annually as a result of the agreement between Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia and Cordele Intermodal Services.
Cordele Intermodal Services will handle up to 30,000 TEUs per year for Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia.
The Alliance Award recognition was revealed at Jump Start 2017, a three-day supply chain event.
source_title: GEORGIA'S PORTS GEORGIA PORTS AUTHORITY ANCHORAGE

facts: Voestalpine’s first fiscal quarter through June turned out to be satisfactory for the group.
Order intake from the construction, mechanical engineering, and consumer goods industry market segments weakened.
Chief executive Herbert Eibensteiner stated that central banks’ measures to fight inflation seem to have reached the manufacturing industry.
Demand from the construction, mechanical engineering, and consumer goods segments is expected to remain weak throughout the rest of the 2023/24 business year.
The automotive segment in Voestalpine’s customer portfolio is performing stably.
Voestalpine expects this stable development in its automotive segment to continue for the rest of the business year.
Supply bottlenecks faced by the automotive industry for over two years are increasingly dissipating.
Voestalpine expects the strongest economic slowdown in Europe.
Voestalpine is more optimistic for the economy in North America.
Compared with the June-2022 quarter, revenue decreased by 4.3% to €4.4 billion ($4.8 billion).
EBITDA declined by 42.6% to €505 million.
CEO Herbert Eibensteiner cautioned that the financial comparison is against the best quarter in the group’s history.
source_title: Voestalpine: automotive developing acceptably, other sectors weaken - EUROMETAL

facts: voestalpine Automotive Components is a manufacturing partner for door rings in lightweight automotive construction, offering optimized crash performance, weight and cost reduction, and simpler body assembly.
A door ring is a single part that combines several body components such as the A-pillar, B-pillar, roof frame and sill, and is manufactured from a laser-welded blank using hot forming.
voestalpine Automotive Components is a leader in this high-end technology.
The company operates the world's largest plant for tailor-welded blanks where door ring blanks can be manufactured from different steels with different sheet thicknesses and strengths.
Welded-on patches can further strengthen crash performance at relevant points with pinpoint accuracy.
voestalpine specialists provide support starting with material selection, design and geometry optimization, through to production design and pre-assembling to enable body-in-white integration.
Door rings are manufactured from customized blanks using hot forming, with voestalpine Automotive Components locations on four continents guaranteeing market and customer proximity.
Starting with the automotive industry in China and the USA, door rings are becoming increasingly relevant as a safety-enhancing and advantageous solution for vehicle production.
The company is a specialist in laser-welded blanks and phs forming, leveraging its global presence to serve the market.
Sonja Kronawitter serves as a Senior Key Account Manager at voestalpine.

To ensure hot forming efficiency, voestalpine uses the latest laser technologies, has optimized hot forming technology, and equips its global forming companies with environmentally friendly burners and its own energy supply from renewable sources.
All door ring blanks made of voestalpine steels are available in the greentec steel edition with a reduced CO2 footprint.
The CO2 footprint of greentec steel products is documented with a declaration verified by an independent third party (LRQA).
Customers can use this documentation to prove emission reductions to their customers.
source_title: ManufactuRING future!

facts: Volvo Cars of Marietta is a Volvo dealer located in Marietta, GA, serving drivers around the Atlanta area.
The dealership is part of the Jim Ellis Automotive Group.
It offers the Jim Ellis Express Way, an online platform for shopping inventory, scheduling test drives, and setting up finance or leasing packages.
The dealership delivers purchased vehicles to customers within a 25-mile radius of its location.
The new Volvo lineup at the dealership includes mild hybrids, plug-in hybrids, and fully electric models in the form of luxury-focused SUVs and one wagon model, the V60 Cross Country.
The compact SUV model available is the Volvo XC40.
The midsize Volvo XC60 offers up to 63.3 cubic feet of cargo space with the back row down.
The three-row Volvo XC90 provides six or seven seats for family travel.
The dealership carries certified pre-owned Volvo vehicles that offer savings and a factory-backed warranty.
The Volvo XC60 Plug-In Hybrid delivers horsepower and torque while allowing drivers to swap between electric and gas power or use both together.
The Volvo XC90 Plug-In Hybrid offers powertrain flexibility with three-row versatility.
The compact Volvo EX40 EV provides an all-electric range and zero tailpipe emissions.
The three-row Volvo EX90 EV features over 650 horsepower and high-end tech features like Advanced Sensing.
A team of Volvo financing experts helps customers navigate loan and lease options for new and used vehicles.
Volvo lease terms are shorter than typical auto loans, allowing customers to upgrade to the newest models in a few years.
Financing a new Volvo allows customers to build equity toward ownership with no monthly payments at the end of a term.
The dealership operates a certified Volvo service center staffed by Volvo-certified technicians.
Technicians provide electrical diagnostics and maintenance for EVs and PHEVs like the EX90 and XC60.
Customers can schedule appointments online, request complimentary loaner vehicles subject to availability, arrange for an overnight key drop, or wait on-site.
Advertised prices include applicable factory rebates, factory incentives, and dealer discounts, though some rebates and incentives may require financing through an approved lender.
The dealer documentation fee is $799.
Prices are subject to change without notice based on manufacturer incentive programs.
source_title: Volvo Cars Of Marietta - Volvo Cars Dealer Near Atlanta, GA

facts: Volvo Cars announced today it will add its best-selling XC60 mid-size SUV to the production line of its US car plant in Ridgeville outside Charleston, South Carolina.
The state-of-the-art facility also assembles the fully electric flagship EX90 and the Polestar 3 SUV models.
XC60 production at the US plant is scheduled to start in late 2026.
In the first six months of 2025, XC60 sales in the US rose by almost 23 per cent.

Håkan Samuelsson, CEO of Volvo Cars, stated that adding the XC60 will support and create American manufacturing jobs and reinforces the company's long-term commitment to the US market where it has sold over 5 million cars.
The XC60 recently surpassed the 240 wagon as its all-time best-selling model, with more than 2.7 million XC60s now on global roads – and counting.
Over 33 per cent of Volvo Cars' sales in the US are the XC60, and 25 per cent of those are plug-in hybrids.
The XC60 is currently the fourth best-selling luxury plug-in hybrid in the US and serves as a bridge toward electrification for customers.
Luis Rezende, President of Volvo Cars Americas, expressed gratitude to the state of South Carolina and Governor McMaster for their leadership.
Volvo Cars broke ground on its first US manufacturing plant in 2015.
Volvo Cars has invested USD 1.3 billion in the plant over the last decade.
The body shop and paint shop have been renewed and expanded significantly.
The plant also has a state-of-the-art battery pack production line.
XC60 production will include both mild hybrid and plug-in hybrid forms to secure future local jobs at the plant.
Volvo Cars will continue producing its fully electric, 7-seater flagship EX90 SUV at the South Carolina plant.
Volvo Cars' production plants are located in Gothenburg, Ghent (Belgium), South Carolina (US), Chengdu, Daqing and Taizhou (China).
As of December 2024, Volvo Cars employed approximately 42,600 full-time employees.
