facts: Volvo Cars plans to begin U.S. production of its two most popular crossovers within the next few years.
The plan aims to avoid paying a profit-sapping 30 percent tariff on the imported models.
source_title: Volvo eying U.S. production of 2 top-selling crossovers - Automotive News

facts: Volvo Cars USA operates its first U.S. plant in Charleston, South Carolina.
The project kicked off in 2015 after the company realized the need for a North American presence following its acquisition by Geely in 2010.
Full scale production has been going for one year at the time of the interview.
Around one hundred eighty two cars roll off the line every day.
The first vehicle produced in Charleston is the S60 sedan.
The second vehicle, referred to as the XY 90, will be produced in 2022 and 2023.
Volvo depends heavily on the Port of Charleston for material imports from suppliers and exporting completed cars globally.
The plant represents a one point one billion dollar economic input to the Charleston area.
The impact from suppliers and others is over four billion dollars.
Approximately fifteen hundred people currently work at the facility.
With the launch of the second car, the company aims to have around 4000 employees.
Sustainability initiatives include reducing the carbon footprint from suppliers and achieving full traceability for cobalt sourcing by the end of this year.
Volvo is introducing 25 percent of plastics made from recycled material, targeting completion by 2025.
The company is focusing on industry trends including electrification (fully electric vehicles and hybrids), sustainability, and autonomous drive.
Volvo attended the AIAG SCAC Supply Chain and Quality Conference in North Charleston to identify new vendors and develop workforce partnerships with South Carolina.
Kyle Hooper serves as manager of procurement and excellence at Volvo Cars USA, managing process management, sustainability, and digitalization for supplier collaboration.

links.context: Direct contact for procurement and supplier inquiries

links.url: Supply Chain Now Radio dot com

source_title: Supply Chain Now Radio - Episode-167 - Supply Chain Now

facts: Volvo Cars reached an agreement with the Trump administration that allows the automaker to continue selling vehicles in the U.S., avoiding a ban on Chinese-connected vehicle technology.
The Swedish automaker is majority owned by China’s Geely Holding.
Volvo received specific authorization from the U.S. Department of Commerce to continue importing and selling its vehicles in the United States.
The Biden administration finalized rules in January 2025 that blocked vehicles equipped with software and hardware developed and maintained by Chinese companies over national security concerns.
Connected car technology involves software covering everything from syncing with phones to some automated driving features.
The rules initially apply to 2027 model-year vehicles equipped with software developed and maintained by Chinese companies or companies with Chinese ties.
A separate ban prohibiting the import of vehicle connected hardware begins with 2030 model-year vehicles.
Volvo vehicles are primarily manufactured in Sweden and imported to the United States, with the exception of the EX90 which is assembled at the company’s factory in South Carolina.
Volvo’s ties to China’s Geely and its manufacturing operations in China meant it would have been banned under the new rules.
The approval followed constructive discussions with the Commerce department and other U.S. officials regarding the company’s governance, technology, and data security.
Volvo stated it can now move forward with its expansion plans in the United States.
In September 2025, Volvo announced plans to bring two additional vehicles—the XC60 midsize SUV and a new hybrid vehicle—into production at its South Carolina factory.
In March, Volvo announced it will bring all production of the Polestar 3 EV from its sister company Polestar to its U.S. factory.
The Polestar 3 is currently also produced in Chengdu, China.
The rule governing these restrictions is titled “Securing the Information and Communications Technology and Services Supply Chain: Connected Vehicles.”
Under the rules, Chinese companies are prohibited from testing autonomous vehicles in the United States.

Several Chinese-affiliated companies, including Baidu’s Apollo Autonomous Driving LLC, Pony.ai, and WeRide, currently hold permits to test their autonomous vehicle technology in California with a human safety operator behind the wheel.
The article was updated to clarify that the approval was a special authorization rather than an exemption from the rules.
source_title: Trump administration permits Volvo to keep selling connected cars in the US | TechCrunch

facts: Swedish carmaker Volvo is planning to invest $19.5 million to purchase tooling, machinery and equipment for use at three suppliers in Spartanburg County.
Spartanburg County Council on Monday approved the second of three readings that will provide tax breaks for Volvo Car USA.
The fee-in-lieu-of-tax agreement does not list how many jobs will be created.

The carmaker is also planning to invest $42 million for equipment to be used by suppliers handling tooling and mold preparation at two locations in Greenville County, according to Greenville Area Development Corporation.
Volvo currently operates its first U.S. plant, built in 2015, in Berkeley County near Charleston.
Spartanburg County Councilman David Britt, who chairs the county's economic development committee, stated that Volvo's impact in South Carolina is felt across all 46 counties.
The approved tax breaks provide a reduced property tax rate of 6% for 30 years, compared to the standard corporate rate of 10.5%.
Volvo will receive a total of $330,000 in special source revenue credits over three years under the agreement.
The agreement includes a clawback clause that will retroactively terminate the tax breaks if Volvo does not meet a minimum investment threshold of $15.6 million within a five-year period.

The agreement identifies three Spartanburg County supplier locations for the investment: Auria Solutions at 1 Austrian Way in Spartanburg, Plasman (formerly Minghua) at 1000 Robinson Road in Greer, and Lear Corporation at 1200 Woods Chapel Road in Duncan.

The council also approved the first of three readings for tax breaks for 'Project Double Down,' which plans a $33.5 million investment, though the company name and location remain undisclosed until final approval.

A second reading was approved for a FILOT agreement with TTI Consumer Power Tools Inc. and Fort Prince Building A LLC for a planned $200 million investment that will create 470 new full-time jobs, though the location is unidentified.

A second reading was approved for a FILOT agreement with Spartanburg SF RRL, LLC and tenant Visual Comfort & Co. for a $68.2 million investment creating 105 new full-time jobs at S.C. 195 and Reidville Road.

The council approved the final reading for a FILOT agreement with Crusoe Acquisitions LLC and Jupiter Two Properties LLC for a $12.5 million planned investment with no specified jobs at 991 Robinson Road.
A second reading was approved for a FILOT agreement with Tyger River Industrial Owner, LLC for a $137.7 million investment with no specified jobs at 2000 Victor Hill Road.
A second reading was approved for a FILOT agreement with The Cubes at Fort Prince for a $65.5 million investment with no specified jobs.
A second reading was approved for a FILOT agreement with Dev. Property SC LLC for a $34.9 million investment with no specified jobs at 6629 Pottery Road.
source_title: Spartanburg County: Volvo planning $19.5M investment

facts: Volvo Cars, majority owned by China's Geely Holding(GEELY.UL), received approval from the U.S. government allowing it to continue selling vehicles.

In January 2025, President Joe Biden's administration finalized rules effectively barring nearly all Chinese cars and trucks from the U.S. market as part of a crackdown on vehicle software and hardware from China.
The rules included a ban on most Chinese-developed and maintained software that took effect in March 2026 for the 2027 model year and covered companies with significant Chinese ownership.
Lawmakers have proposed making the rules even tougher.
Volvo Car USA stated it was required to follow a process with the U.S. Department of Commerce to obtain a specific authorization for the continued import and sale of connected cars in the U.S.
With this specific authorization, Volvo Cars can continue its growth plans in the United States.
Volvo Cars sold 121,600 vehicles in the United States in 2025, down 2.9% from 2024.
In September, Volvo Cars said it would begin producing a new hybrid model in the U.S. by the end of the decade.
The new model will be designed for the U.S. market and help Volvo boost capacity utilization at its South Carolina plant.
In April 2025, Volvo Cars CEO Hakan Samuelsson said the company would produce more vehicles in the U.S.
The group announced in July that it plans to start producing its popular XC60 mid-size SUV in South Carolina in late 2026.
Volvo, long positioned as an EV trailblazer with the aim of phasing out all non-electric models by 2030, last year reversed course and said hybrids would remain part of its lineup.
Volvo Cars currently imports all of its cars into the U.S. from Europe except its electric SUV EX90, which it assembles in South Carolina.
The Swedish automaker used to also import cars from China but it halted those after tariffs on Chinese-made cars were put into effect.

Volvo said the Commerce Department gave the company a specific authorization following constructive discussions with government agencies regarding Volvo Cars' governance, technology and data security.
Sweden's Polestar, which is also majority-owned by Geely, separately said it continues to work with U.S. authorities to meet the requirements of the announced regulations.
source_title: Volvo Cars wins U.S. approval to keep importing vehicles with 'connected car' technology

facts: Volvo discontinues sales of the EX30 in the US after the 2026 model year.
The EX30 was unveiled in June 2023 with an originally planned US market launch for summer 2024.
The original US launch was postponed after the Biden administration imposed a 100 percent tariff on electric vehicles manufactured in China, which remained in place under the Trump administration.
The EX30 was initially produced exclusively at Volvo’s Zhangjiakou plant in China.
Volvo delayed most EX30 deliveries to the US until production launched in Ghent, Belgium in April 2025.
Prior to the Belgian production launch, only a few units of the top-tier Twin Motor Performance model were shipped from China to the US.
Since production started in Belgium, Volvo has served the US market alongside European countries.
US dealers were informed that the EX30 will be withdrawn from the US market after the 2026 model year, including both standard and Cross Country versions.
All existing orders for the EX30 will still be fulfilled.
Production for the US market will cease after the summer of 2026.
US dealers had until Friday, 20 March 2026, to place any final orders for the EX30.
The EX30 was available in the US starting at $40,345 (including delivery charges), while in Germany it started at €38,490.
Volvo Cars explained the decision as part of a “thorough evaluation of our business and operational strategies.”
The decision is described as a “direct response to shifting market conditions and financial factors.”
Key factors include the expiry of the US tax credit of $7,500 on 30 September 2025.
Relaxed emissions regulations for internal combustion vehicles introduced by the Trump administration also contributed to the decision.
When Volvo decided to produce the EX30 for the US market in Belgium, the applicable tariff rate was 2.5 percent.
The US imposed an additional 25 percent tariff just as production and deliveries began from Belgium.
The tariff rate was reduced to 15 percent on 1 August 2025 but remains six times higher than the original calculation.
The discontinuation of EX30 sales in the US does not affect its availability in Europe, Canada, and Mexico.
Volvo plans to continue selling the significantly larger EX90, produced in South Carolina, in the US.
Volvo also plans to sell the upcoming EX60 in the US.
Ford has discontinued the all-electric F-150 Lightning and a future electric pickup under the codename T3.
Stellantis has abandoned plans for an electric version of the RAM-1500 pickup and written off $22.2 billion, primarily from its electric vehicle business.
Honda has cancelled three electric models planned for the US market.
General Motors plans to discontinue the recently revived Chevy Bolt once again.
source_title: Volvo discontinues sales of the EX30 in the US - electrive.com

facts: Volvo revealed the all-electric EX60 SUV as part of its revamped product lineup.
The EX60 will be built in Europe, making it subject to tariffs, while Volvo plans to expand US-based production.
Volvo will bring production of its high-volume XC60 gas-powered SUV to its South Carolina plant and build another brand-new vehicle for the US market.
Volvo Cars CEO Håkan Samuelsson stated at a New York City press event that the company is developing a bigger, family-oriented multifuel or hybrid vehicle specifically for the US market.
Samuelsson noted that manufacturing in the US avoids tariffs and allows 0% export duties to Europe, which benefits the Charleston South Carolina factory.
Volvo's current US product portfolio consists of large SUVs, midsize and compact SUVs, and the V60 Cross Country wagon, with gas-powered cars as mild or plug-in hybrids and EVs using the EX prefix.
The new EV launch follows the expiration of the federal tax credit and regulatory emissions credits that previously boosted automaker profits.
Volvo Cars Americas president Luis Rezende reported a drop in EV demand after the 45W credit expired, but noted rising interest due to increased oil prices.
Rezende claimed Volvo is seeing improving metrics in May versus April for EVs and plug-in hybrid EVs compared to regular mild-hybrid cars.
Website traffic for Volvo's EVs and PHEVs is rising, indicating heightened consumer interest in the US.
The EX60 will start at $58,400 when it goes on sale in the US later this year, with orders available online starting Monday.
The cheapest model, the EX60 P6, targets a 307-mile range, while the range-topping EX60 P12 AWD hits 400 miles.
Fast charging on the P6 trim adds 155 miles in just 10 minutes.
Rezende stated that fast-charging time is nearly equivalent to filling up at a gas station, offering an EV that costs the same as a gas car but provides a more premium ride, look, and feel.
Samuelsson and Rezende believe Volvo is well-positioned in the US market ahead of the EX60's later this year launch.
source_title: Volvo reveals EX60 EV to start at $58,400, plans to expand US production

facts: Volvo Cars, majority owned by China's Geely Holding, received approval from the U.S. government allowing it to continue selling vehicles with Chinese "connected technology" in the country.

In January 2025, President Joe Biden's administration finalized rules effectively barring nearly all Chinese cars and trucks from the U.S. market as part of a crackdown on vehicle software and hardware from China.
The rules included a ban on most Chinese-developed and -maintained software that took effect in March 2026 for the 2027 model year.
Lawmakers have proposed making the rules even tougher.
The Commerce Department gave Volvo Cars a specific authorization following constructive discussions with government agencies regarding governance, technology and data security.
With this specific authorization, Volvo Cars can continue its growth plans in the United States.
In September, Volvo Cars said it would begin producing a new hybrid model in the U.S. by the end of the decade.
The new model will be designed for the U.S. market and help Volvo boost capacity utilization at its South Carolina plant.
In April 2025, Volvo Cars CEO Hakan Samuelsson said the company would produce more vehicles in the U.S.
The group announced in July that it plans to start producing its popular XC60 mid-size SUV in South Carolina starting in late 2026.
Volvo, long positioned as an EV trailblazer with the aim of phasing out all non-electric models by 2030, last year reversed course and said hybrids would remain part of its lineup.
Volvo Cars currently imports all of its cars into the U.S. from Europe except its electric SUV EX90, which it assembles in South Carolina.
The Swedish automaker used to also import cars from China but halted those after tariffs on Chinese-made cars were put into effect.
source_title: Volvo Cars wins US approval to keep importing vehicles with 'connected car' technology

facts: Volvo will begin U.S. production of its XC60 SUV in 2025 to avoid Trump’s 15% import tariff and support tariff-free exports to Europe.
The Ridgeville plant is currently running at just 20% capacity and is expected to reach 4,000 jobs and higher output by 2030.
While hybrids remain in demand, Volvo is investing in EV growth with upgrades to the EX90 and plans for a next-generation U.S.-focused hybrid.

Volvo Cars plans to expand production at its $1.3 billion factory in Ridgeville, South Carolina, shifting assembly of its popular XC60 SUV from Sweden to the U.S. in response to new tariffs under President Donald Trump’s trade policy.
The Swedish automaker said the move will help offset a 15% levy on imported vehicles that replaced a previous 2.5% duty.
Producing the XC60 domestically enables Volvo to avoid the higher tariff while also allowing for tariff-free exports back to Europe under preliminary U.S.-EU trade terms.
Volvo’s Ridgeville facility is designed to produce 150,000 vehicles annually, currently operates at approximately 20% capacity, and employs roughly 2,000 employees on a single shift.
The company originally pledged to create 4,000 jobs at the plant, a target it now expects to reach by 2030 as additional production lines are added.
The XC60, offered as both a gas-electric hybrid and a plug-in hybrid, has recorded more than 27,000 U.S. sales this year, representing a nearly 20% increase over the same period last year.
Volvo executives emphasized that American buyers continue to show strong interest in hybrids, as many are reluctant to entirely abandon gas-powered vehicles.
The company has emphasized that long-term growth will stem from EVs, warning that manufacturers who fail to make the transition may struggle to remain competitive.
In Ridgeville, Volvo already builds the $81,000 EX90 electric SUV, though only 2,500 units have sold in the U.S. this year, placing it sixth among the brand’s seven models.
A refreshed EX90 debuting next year will include faster charging, adding more than 150 miles of range in 10 minutes, along with new safety and driver-assist features.
Volvo also plans to launch a next-generation hybrid, designed explicitly for U.S. buyers, before 2030.
By that year, the company aims for at least 90% of its U.S. fleet to be “electrified,” a category that includes hybrids as well as fully electric vehicles.
Volvo holds just 1% of the U.S. auto market and generates 20% of its global revenue in America.
Executives noted that producing vehicles in South Carolina strengthens Volvo’s industrial presence in the U.S. while easing the impact of Trump’s tariffs on imported models and parts.
Volvo SuperTruck 2 is a wholly reimagined, lighter tractor body that is more aerodynamic.
The truck features a dramatically redesigned cab with a wraparound windshield.
It includes a micro-hybrid system among its efficiency technologies.
Volvo Trucks North America (VTNA) achieved greater freight efficiency with the SuperTruck.
The U.S. Department of Energy tasked VTNA to improve freight efficiency.
The diesel-powered Volvo SuperTruck 2 topped 12 miles per gallon.
VTNA showed off the truck online before its public debut at the American Trucking Associations' 2023 Management Conference & Exhibition in Austin, Texas, from Oct. 14-17.
Eric Bond is Volvo Trucks' principal engineer for the SuperTruck 2 project.
The project focus was building the truck around freight efficiency for real-world application.
Validation avoided perfect scenario locations like flat roads, no traffic, or low wind/tailwind.
The SuperTruck 2 was built in Greensboro, North Carolina.
The truck was validated on Interstate 73.
Interstate 73 is representative of customer duty cycles with elevation change and variable traffic.
VTNA exceeded its internal stretch goal of 120% freight efficiency.
The tractor-trailer averaged 12.38 mpg over the complete validations cycle, which included traffic, varying grade, terrain, and posted highway speed limits.
VTNA reported a 134% freight efficiency increase compared to the DOE-set 2009 baseline.
The 2009 baseline was a 6x4 tractor with a standard trailer that achieved 100 ton-miles per gallon.
SuperTruck 2 achieved 234 ton-miles per gallon.
DOE's SuperTruck program challenges OEMs to achieve 100% freight efficiency improvement over their 2009 baselines.
The SuperTruck 2 program is a public-private partnership.
Rival truck makers Peterbilt, Freightliner, and International showed off their Super Truck 2 versions earlier this year.
Peter Voorhoeve is VTNA President.
Partnering with the DOE provides an opportunity to push the technology envelope for heavy-duty transportation and scale solutions into production.
The advanced engineering team worked on SuperTruck 2, pushing limits in creativity, problem-solving, feasibility, and innovation.
Some technologies on SuperTruck 2 will shape the future of trucking and change everything once thought possible.
DOE's SuperTruck 2 program funds research and development to improve efficiency of heavy-duty Class 8 long-haul tractor-trailers.
The program aims to accelerate growth of cost-effective advanced efficiency technologies not currently on the market.
All participating OEMs were asked to demonstrate more than 100% improvement in vehicle freight efficiency (ton-miles per gallon).
Volvo's decarbonization strategy relies heavily on the rapid transition to and adoption of zero-emission vehicles.
Lessons learned from SuperTruck 2 regarding aerodynamics and lightweighting will be directly applicable to the next generation of battery-electric trucks.
These lessons aim to increase efficiency, range, payload, productivity, and value for customers in future battery-electric trucks.
The truck focused on advanced aerodynamics to optimize fuel efficiency.
It features a wedge-shaped cab with a raked and wraparound windshield.
The front end is designed around a downsized cooling package.
It includes a fully aerodynamic trailer with gap fairings, skirts, and a boat tail.
The truck features an adjustable ride height.
Volvo replaced traditional hood and cab-mounted mirrors with a streamlined camera monitoring system to reduce drag by more than 4%.
SuperTruck 2 demonstrated significant aerodynamic gains from an entirely reimagined Body-in-White.
These aerodynamic learnings can be incorporated into future cab updates.
Regulatory pressures exist to reduce tailpipe CO2 emissions at both powertrain and vehicle levels.
Vehicle level targets cannot be met without reducing aerodynamic loads, even with the most efficient powertrain possible.
The tractor-trailer combination was designed to smoothly displace air with minimal resistance.
This design resulted in 50% lower drag than Volvo Trucks' 2009 baseline.
The drag reduction represents a roughly 20% improvement over Volvo Trucks' first SuperTruck.
Around two-thirds of the drag reduction in SuperTruck 1 over the 2009 baseline came from trailer aerodynamic treatments.
Most gains over the first SuperTruck are attributed to the new cab design.
The importance of trailer aerodynamics is highlighted to help fleets reduce fuel consumption from ICE-based tractors and increase range of BEV tractors.
Engineers implemented weight reduction strategies to achieve a significantly reduced curb weight of 27,000 lbs for the combined truck and trailer.
Volvo utilized a 4x2 configuration, which is uncommon in the U.S. but frequently utilized in Europe, using fewer axles for the same payload.
The truck was designed to be applied to a 6x2 or 6x4 configuration, though that was not part of the specific demonstrator validation.
The shorter cab design is lightweight and paired with an aluminum chassis.
It features a lightweight optimized drive axle system with a single composite driveshaft.
Volvo Trucks worked with project partner trailer manufacturer Wabash National to incorporate a custom, lightweight aerodynamic trailer.
The full truck and trailer aerodynamic shape was optimized to appear as one seamless unit.
Volvo worked with project partner tire manufacturer Michelin to include lightweight, smaller 19.5-inch advanced low-friction tires on the SuperTruck 2 and its custom trailer.
The truck features a 48-volt micro-hybrid system that acts as a generator with an integrated starter.
The micro-hybrid system provides power for driver comfort features, including an all-electric HVAC system.
The all-electric HVAC system allows drivers to avoid idling during rest breaks while still having power for amenities.
Some aerodynamic features can be brought into future cab platforms that will offer a range of cab lengths.
While rear door entry may not be feasible in such platforms, the wedge shape of the cab in plan view with a raked and wraparound windshield could be industrialized.
The mirror-less camera monitoring system uses high-definition cameras and monitors instead of traditional side-view mirrors.
This system provides significant drag reduction when it replaces traditional hood and cab-mounted side mirrors.
Regulatory changes are required before eliminating traditional mirrors.
In the short term, a hybrid setup is possible where a regulatory-compliant mirror supplements a fully functional camera monitoring system.
The project team focused on real-world driving conditions for testing.
SuperTruck 2 is the most aerodynamic and efficient truck Volvo has built to date.
Its freight efficiency demonstrates the potential for technology innovations to be developed commercially.
Engineers have already begun implementing learnings from SuperTruck 2 into future truck models.
source_title: Volvo SuperTruck 2 uses aerodynamics, advanced engineering to top 12 mpg | FleetOwner

facts: Vanguard Truck Centers acquired Nacarato Truck Centers to become one of Volvo Trucks’ largest dealer groups in North America.

The acquisition expands Vanguard’s presence to 28 Volvo full-service and parts-and-service-only locations across 11 states: Arizona, Florida, Georgia, Kentucky, Maryland, Missouri, North Carolina, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, and Virginia.
Vanguard assumed full ownership of the Nacarato locations, including all existing stock and parts.
Peter Voorhoeve serves as president of Volvo Trucks North America.
Nacarato Truck Centers was founded in 1976 in Nashville, Tennessee.
Prior to the acquisition, Nacarato operated a network of nine Volvo Trucks dealerships and service centers throughout Tennessee, Florida, Georgia, Kentucky, Maryland, and Virginia.
Tom Ewing is president and chief executive officer of Vanguard Truck Centers.
Mike Nacarato is chief executive officer of Nacarato Truck Centers and will continue to lead the center under its existing brand following the acquisition.
Will Blue serves as chief financial officer of Vanguard Truck Centers.
Lloyd Baldridge serves as chief financial officer of Nacarato Truck Centers.
Pat Daily serves as chief operating officer of Nacarato Truck Centers.

Both dealership groups invested in required training and certification programs to become Volvo Trucks Certified Electric Vehicle Dealers, recognizing battery-electric trucks as important to the future of trucking.

Vanguard’s sales and service teams are fully trained to support customers’ Volvo VNR Electric deployments at five locations: Phoenix, Arizona; Atlanta, Georgia (Forest Park); Charlotte, North Carolina; and Austin and Houston, Texas.
The acquisition adds three additional Volvo Trucks Certified EV Dealer locations in Roanoke, Virginia; La Vergne, Tennessee (serving the Nashville area); and Hagerstown, Maryland.
The acquisition of Nacarato Truck Centers was completed on January 8, 2024.
Vanguard now employs more than 1,200 people following the acquisition.
All 28 Volvo Trucks dealership locations are Certified Uptime Dealers, providing customers with access to almost 500 service bays for preventative service and maintenance.

Vanguard Truck Centers operates 39 full-service commercial truck dealerships and service centers in the United States across Arizona, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Kentucky, Maryland, Missouri, North Carolina, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, and Virginia.
Volvo Trucks North America is headquartered in Greensboro, North Carolina.
Every Volvo truck is assembled at the Volvo Trucks New River Valley manufacturing facility in Dublin, Virginia.

The Dublin, Virginia manufacturing facility meets ISO 9001 standards for quality, ISO 14001 standards for environmental care, and holds dual ISO 50001/Superior Energy Performance certification at the platinum level.
Volvo Trucks North America provides a full range of diesel, alternative-fuel, and all-electric vehicles as part of its complete transport solutions.
Approximately 120,000 Volvo trucks were delivered worldwide in 2025.
Volvo Trucks is part of the Volvo Group, which manufactures trucks, buses, construction equipment, and marine and industrial engines globally.
source_title: Vanguard Truck Centers Acquires Nacarato Truck Centers, Becoming One of Volvo Trucks’ Largest Dealer Groups | Volvo Trucks USA

facts: Volvo posted a profit of $771 million in Q2 2025, less than half its $1.6 billion profit in the year-ago period.
Revenue at Volvo decreased 12% year over year to $12.59 billion from $14.36 billion a year earlier.
The company’s revenue from truck sales declined 7% to $8.37 billion in the three months that ended June 30, down 14% from 9.74 billion krona in the year-ago period.
Volvo posted an operating margin of 8.1% in Q2 2025, compared with 14.5% in Q2 2024.
CEO Martin Lundstedt stated that the societal transformation to zero-emission vehicles is slower than previously anticipated.
Costs of $460 million related to compensation for lower battery volume commitments and impairment of some battery-electric assets negatively impacted reported operating income.
Renegotiated battery delivery contracts saw Volvo pay suppliers $297 million in compensation, with $195 million taken against Q2 earnings.
The company took a $164 million charge against earnings on the value of its battery-electric assets.

CEO Martin Lundstedt warned that enabling conditions such as charging infrastructure and stimulus of demand have not yet been put in place, calling for action from policymakers, state leaders, and other stakeholders.
Volvo sold 52,764 trucks globally in Q2 2025, down 10% from 58,935 trucks in the same period a year earlier.
In the first six months of 2025, Volvo sold 101,597 trucks, a decrease of 11% from 114,405 trucks a year earlier.
Demand in North America has been weak due to uncertainty surrounding tariffs and EPA 2027 emissions regulations, prompting Volvo to reduce production capacity there.
VTNA and Mack will lay off 1,000 staff at manufacturing plants in the U.S., as announced by Lundstedt on April 23.
Volvo peers Traton Group and Daimler Truck have also laid off or are about to lay off employees in North America due to soft demand for Class 8 on-highway vehicles.
Daimler Truck North America will lay off 2,000 production staff temporarily, with returns dependent on an upturn in orders.
The parent company of Freightliner and Western Star sold 38,580 trucks and buses in Q2 2025, down 20% from 48,246 a year earlier.
North America’s over-the-road freight market is in a recession due to lower freight volume and rates, while the vocational market is relatively better.
Overall industrywide heavy-duty truck retail sales in North America through the end of June were down 6% at 135,723, compared with 145,056 in the first half of 2024.
VTNA’s sales in North America decreased by 20% to 12,981 trucks from 16,234.
Order intake in North America across VTNA and Mack decreased by 16% to 8,243 trucks due to a wait-and-see stance by carriers.
VTNA’s orders rose 29% to 4,131 trucks from 3,193 in the year-ago period, while Mack’s orders totaled 4,080 trucks, a 38% slump from 6,556 a year earlier.
VTNA’s North American heavy-duty truck market share was 7.6% in Q2 2025, down from 9.5%, while Mack’s market share rose to 7.2% from 6.1%.
Volvo Group paid $40 million in October 2024 to bring all of Mack’s cab assembly in-house after buying Commercial Vehicle Group’s Kings Mountain, N.C., plant.
VTNA’s on-highway lineup saw a redesign of its flagship VNL tractor and the regional haul VNR tractor.
Mack launched a new flagship on-highway tractor called the Pioneer and repositioned its Anthem predecessor to the regional haul market.
At Volvo Group’s 2024 Capital Markets Day in Dublin, Va., executives announced plans for 25% North American heavy-duty truck market share by 2030.
source_title: Volvo Q2 Profit Halves on US Truck, Battery-Electric Weakness – WTA Global Inc

facts: Volvo Trucks North America announced that Vanguard Truck Centers completed its Volvo Trucks Certified EV Dealer programme at five locations.
The certified locations include sites in Arizona, Georgia, North Carolina, Austin, and Houston in Texas.
These newly certified locations are now officially capable of providing comprehensive sales and service support for Volvo Trucks customers.
The expansion brings the company’s Certified EV Dealer network into three additional states: Arizona, Georgia, and North Carolina.
Volvo Trucks now has a total of 50 certified dealership locations across 24 US states and three Canadian provinces.
To complete the certification programme, dealerships must install onsite charging stations.
Dealerships must also install dedicated EV service bays.
Dealerships are required to acquire diagnostic tools for technicians working with high-voltage systems.
Dealerships must purchase personal protective equipment for technicians working with high-voltage systems.
Dealerships must purchase parts inventory specifically for the VNR Electric model.
Volvo Financial Services (VFS) introduced financing options for fleets to support the cost of installing charging infrastructure.
Volvo Financial Services (VFS) also introduced service and maintenance plans to support the transition.
Peter Voorhoeve serves as president of Volvo Trucks North America.
Peter Voorhoeve highlighted that Vanguard Truck Centers is investing to certify multiple locations simultaneously across the United States.
Peter Voorhoeve noted that cost remains a significant barrier to electrification, particularly at scale.
Peter Voorhoeve stated that new financing options from Volvo Financial Services aim to support customers and dealers in their shift to zero-emission solutions.
source_title: Volvo Trucks certified dealer network adds five new dealer locations in the US - electrive.com

facts: Delivers advanced transport, logistics, and supply chain solutions, supporting sustainable mobility and infrastructure across the U.S., Canada, and Mexico.
Is advancing sustainable logistics through electrification, digitalization, and expanded service offerings in response to industry trends.
Is expanding electric vehicle fleets and charging infrastructure for its logistics operations.
Is making strategic efforts to expand market presence and reach new customer segments.
Is implementing advanced technologies to improve service delivery and customer experience.
Specializes in comprehensive logistics, supply chain management, and transport solutions for commercial vehicles and equipment.
Supports a wide range of industries, including food distribution, construction, and municipal services, with a focus on sustainable and safe transport.
Leverages Volvo Group’s global expertise to deliver innovative logistics services that help customers meet growing market demands.
Operates facilities spanning the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, ensuring robust regional coverage and operational excellence.
Was founded in 1927.
Is publicly traded on the Stockholm Stock Exchange under the ticker VOLV-B.
source_title: Volvo Logistics North America | Advanced Transport & Supp...

facts: A new deal between The Volvo Group and North American-based service station group Pilot Company will make high-performing charging infrastructure for electric battery-operated vehicles publicly available.

Volvo Group intends to enter a strategic partnership combining its market leadership and experience in medium and heavy-duty battery-electric trucks and charging solutions with Pilot Company’s extensive network of more than 750 Pilot and Flying J locations across North America to install high-performing charging infrastructure.
Pilot Company intends to install high-performance charging infrastructure at selected Pilot and Flying J travel centres across the U.S.
The installed charging infrastructure will be open to medium and heavy-duty vehicles of all brands.
Volvo Group and its commercial truck brands in North America – Volvo Trucks and Mack Trucks – will identify selected Pilot and Flying J locations to be prioritized for high-performance charging.
Location prioritization will be based on customer and driver needs, current and anticipated battery-electric truck density, and the availability of public funding to support infrastructure costs.
The initiative will provide customers of any commercial truck brand with the necessary peace of mind to consider adopting electric trucks.
The initiative aims to eliminate range anxiety for fleet customers.
The initiative aims to give North American fleet customers the prerequisites to meet their environmental goals on their decarbonisation journeys.

The intended partnership places Volvo Group and Pilot Company at the forefront of accelerating electrification and developing sustainable and reliable transportation solutions in North America for generations of battery-electric trucks to come.
source_title: North American deal sees Volvo and Pilot Company up the ante on charging stations - TradeTrucks - Used Truck Sales

facts: Volvo has hit an EV milestone with 5,000 electric trucks (ETs) globally.
Volvo announced the delivery of more than 5,000 battery-electric trucks to customers across 50 countries.
This milestone marks a major point in Volvo's electrification journey.
Volvo launched its first ET in 2019.
Since launching its first ET in 2019, Volvo’s fleet has collectively covered close to 170 million kilometres in commercial operations.
The fleet's operation has led to substantial reductions in CO₂ emissions and noise pollution.
Roger Alm, President of Volvo Trucks, stated that transport companies continue to embrace the benefits with electric trucks in a wide range of transport segments.
Volvo’s battery-electric trucks are available here and now, providing customers and transport buyers with a more sustainable alternative that makes business sense.
Many of Volvo's customers are coming back to the company to grow their electric fleets.
Volvo offers eight electric truck models designed to meet the varied demands of urban delivery, regional haulage, construction and waste management.
The eight electric truck models include: Volvo FL Electric, FE Electric, FM Electric, FM Low Entry, FMX Electric, FH Electric, FH Aero Electric, and VNR Electric.
Volvo’s five largest markets for ETs are Germany, the Netherlands, the United States, Norway and Sweden.
Demand for Volvo's ETs is particularly robust across Europe.

The company’s early focus on developing electric trucks has allowed it to cultivate unique expertise in critical areas such as optimising energy usage, tailored financing solutions, servicing strategies and building charging infrastructure.
For drivers, Volvo’s ETs offer enhanced comfort through lower noise levels and reduced vibrations, improving the experience inside the cab and helping driver wellbeing.
Volvo Trucks’ achievements in electrification are a part of a broader three-pronged strategy targeting zero emissions by 2040.

This plan involves the integration of battery-electric vehicles, fuel cell electric vehicles and combustion engines powered by renewable fuels like green hydrogen, biogas (Bio-LNG) and hydrotreated vegetable oil (HVO).
Volvo’s commitment to sustainability stretches beyond the vehicles themselves, encompassing the design, production, and the supply chain.
The goal is to cut CO₂ emissions per vehicle by 75% by 2030 compared to 2018 levels.
Volvo is moving towards full net zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2040.
This climate strategy has been validated by the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) to align with the Paris Agreement’s goal of limiting global warming.
Circular economy principles are also being embedded across Volvo’s operations.
The company aims to achieve 30% average recycled content across its vehicle fleet by 2030.
New models will feature at least 35% recycled materials.
Volvo is transitioning to leather-free interiors, using sustainable alternatives such as Nordico, a material produced from bio-attributed sources and recycled plastics.
Through the acquisition of Bcomp, Volvo is expanding the use of natural fibre composites, offering a lightweight, sustainable substitute for traditional plastics.
All Volvo’s manufacturing plants worldwide are now powered by 100% climate-neutral electricity.
The company is pursuing ambitious targets to lower energy consumption, water use and waste production.
By 2030, Volvo aims for at least 99% of operational waste to be either reused or recycled.
Volvo Construction Equipment’s Braås facility exemplifies this ambition, achieving carbon-neutral operations and landfill-free status, all powered by renewable energy.
Volvo also works to ensure responsible sourcing across its supply chain, aiming for greater transparency and ethical standards.
This includes efforts to phase out animal-based products and promote sustainable materials.

Beyond its own operations, Volvo collaborates with suppliers, companies and industry groups to promote sustainable practices across the automotive sector and to support global decarbonisation efforts.
With the sale of 5,000 electric trucks, Volvo reaffirms its leadership in the transition to more sustainable transport solutions, combining business growth with environmental responsibility.
source_title: Volvo Marks 5000 Electric Truck Sales in Sustainability Push | EV Magazine

canonical_name: Georgia Department of Economic Development HQ2 Proposal
facts: Georgia Department of Economic Development's Brittany Holtzclaw, director of GDEcD's LEAF Team, carried the proposal to Amazon in Seattle.
The Gulch is located just southeast of Philips Arena and was proposed for several projects including a regional multimodal station.
The Gulch could form the heart of a south downtown rebirth led by real estate companies including Newport U.S. RE.
Georgia pitched nearly 10 metro Atlanta sites for Amazon's $5B HQ2 bid.
CNBC ranked Atlanta as the 2nd best city for Amazon HQ2.
Ed Bastian stated that Amazon would be 'well served' coming to Atlanta.
President Joe Biden visited the Volvo Group Powertrain Operations in Hagerstown on a Friday afternoon to deliver remarks about the state of the U.S. economy and thank local manufacturers.
Biden praised the manufacturing team for building heavy-duty engines, transmissions, axles for trucks and buses, and parts for electric cars in the future.
The president spoke for about 22 minutes, addressing the goal of "building the economy from the bottom up and middle out."
Biden's visit followed similar economic trips to Ohio and New York and highlighted his recent signing of the Chips and Science Act to spur semiconductor production in the U.S.
The visit took place on National Manufacturing Day, which is Oct. 7.

Martin Weissburg, chairman of Volvo Group North America and Mack Trucks president, stated that the Hagerstown site exemplifies modern manufacturing by providing advanced transportation technologies to meet customer needs today and in the future.
Over the past 10 years, the Volvo Group has invested nearly $320 million in the site.
The Hagerstown campus produces engines, transmissions, and axles for Mack Trucks, Volvo Trucks, Prevost motorcoaches and Volvo Buses sold in North America.

The facility recently began production of the Modular Power Boxes (MPB) that are at the heart of the Group's new zero-emission battery-electric vehicles, the Volvo VNR Electric and Mack LR Electric models.
Biden opened his remarks with "Made in America" and asked, "Where is it written that America can't be the leader in manufacturing?"
The president emphasized bringing manufacturing back to the U.S., citing competition with China as a matter of national security.
Biden declared himself the "most pro-union president in American history," stating that unions are the "single hardest workers around."
During the visit, Biden met with executives, toured the facility, and met with employees.
source_title: 'Made in America': President Biden touts American manufacturing at Volvo plant in Hagerstown - CBS Baltimore

canonical_name: Volvo Trucks North America - Forest Park Manufacturing Facility
facts: Volvo Trucks has established a manufacturing facility in Forest Park, Georgia.
The Forest Park facility is one of the key production plants for Volvo Trucks North America.
The plant contributes to the development of high-quality, reliable trucks that meet the diverse needs of customers around the globe.
Volvo Trucks maintains a dedication to sustainability and environmental responsibility at its Forest Park operation.
Volvo Trucks has implemented eco-friendly practices in its manufacturing process to reduce its carbon footprint.
The facility uses renewable energy, waste reduction initiatives, and efficient resource management as part of its sustainability efforts.
Volvo aims to set new benchmarks for environmental stewardship in the trucking industry through these manufacturing practices.
The Forest Park facility is equipped with advanced technology and robotics to enhance production efficiency and ensure high-quality output.
Automation in the manufacturing process speeds up production and reduces the likelihood of errors.
A skilled workforce at the facility combines traditional craftsmanship with cutting-edge technology.
Volvo provides continuous training and development to employees to keep pace with the latest innovations in truck manufacturing.
The company adheres to strict safety protocols to protect its employees and maintain a safe working environment within the factory.
The trucks produced incorporate advanced systems designed to prevent accidents and protect drivers and pedestrians alike.
Volvo Trucks actively contributes to local initiatives and programs in Forest Park to enhance its relationship with the surrounding community.
Through partnerships with educational institutions and local organizations, Volvo supports workforce development and promotes careers in manufacturing and engineering.
source_title: Volvo Trucks Manufacturing Facility in Forest Park Georgia Overview and Insights

facts: Wabash announced a significant expansion of its network in the Southeast to strengthen customer access and service in the greater Atlanta region.
The expansion strategy includes opening a new Parts & Service center, expanding its dealer relationship with Fleetco, and adding CS Truck and Trailer to its Preferred Partner Network.
Wabash’s new Atlanta-area Parts and Services center is strategically located at the heart of the Southeast’s trucking corridor.
The new center provides faster access to parts, service, and truck body upfitting for customers.
The facility will offer Wabash’s Ready-to-Mount (RTM) truck bodies for dry freight and platform applications with rapid turnaround times for in-stock chassis.
The Atlanta site joins existing Wabash service centers located in California, Florida, Ohio, Pennsylvania, and Texas.
Additional Wabash service center locations are planned beyond the current network.
Doors to the new Atlanta Parts & Service center officially opened on September 18, 2025.
Fleetco, a long-standing Wabash dealer, will now represent Wabash in the greater Atlanta market specifically for trailer sales.
The Fleetco expansion broadens Wabash’s dealer footprint across the Southeast and provides customers with additional access to new and used equipment through a trusted partner.
Fleetco president Bill Tirrill stated that the partnership allows them to expand their sales force across the region and provide immediate access to new and used trailers.
Wabash added three CS Truck and Trailer locations to its Preferred Partner Network in Georgia.
The Preferred Partner Network model delivers expert service, maintenance, and Wabash Genuine Parts to customers through authorized third-party dealerships.

Dave Hill, vice president of Parts & Services at Wabash, stated that the Atlanta expansions mean faster turnaround, more coverage, and better reliability for fleets operating across Georgia, Alabama, South Carolina, and Tennessee.

Wabash is headquartered in Lafayette, Indiana, and designs, manufactures, and services an extensive range of products including dry and refrigerated trailers, truck bodies, platform trailers, tank trailers, and structural composites.
Customers gain access to a nationwide parts and service network, Trailers as a Service (TaaS)℠, and advanced operational tools through the Wabash Marketplace and Wabash Parts.
source_title: Wabash Expands Southeast Footprint to Strengthen Customer Access and Service

facts: Wabash National Corp. (NYSE:WNC) is refocusing its business on cold chain, home delivery and parts and service.
The company is pushing resources to refrigerated vans needed to move vaccines and food to a homebound nation.
CEO Brent Yeagy stated that Wabash sees the growth in home delivery having staying power even as more Americans receive inoculations against COVID-19.
New technology and products to keep goods refrigerated present significant organic growth prospects, according to CEO Brent Yeagy on the company's fourth-quarter earnings call Wednesday.
Yeagy said, "We've never been faced with a market that has this much potential."
Yeagy added, "All the ways that we could potentially grow are on the table right now."
Growth strategies include expanding upfitting, acquiring technology or another manufacturing platform.
Yeagy stated the company took action to streamline its portfolio by selling assets that do not offer strong strategic fit.

As it finishes pruning its portfolio, Wabash is setting the stage to backfill by continuing diversification with expanded and new revenue streams in transportation, logistics and distribution markets.
Wabash sold its Beall brand of tank trailer to MAC Trailer Enterprises in December.
The company is holding onto its other tank trailer brands.
Yeagy said the company is "absolutely repositioning resources to accelerate the organic efforts to create additional revenue in a way that has never been seen at Wabash National."
Wabash sees expanded use of molded structural composite (MSC) technology beyond the 53-foot refrigerated van market.
MSC weighs less and offers better thermal protection than other trailer construction material.
Wabash worked on an MSC pilot project with C&S Wholesale Grocers in 2020.
Using MSC technology, Wabash is able to make a 48-foot trailer 30% more thermally efficient than a traditional refrigerated trailer.
With this technology, refrigeration units can run fewer hours and for those with all-electric units, fewer batteries are needed.

Yeagy said, "We believe this is likely to be a rapidly expanding market segment where MSC continues to offer unique value to customers with its durability, reduced weight and improved thermal efficiency."
Yeagy added that consistent feedback from interested parties indicates their technology offers benefits they have been unable to find elsewhere.
Wabash is manufacturing Gruau refrigerated inserts for the Ford Transit van to serve the rapidly expanding food/grocery home delivery market.
Traditionally constructed refrigerated cargo vans use spray foam insulation, which can release gas and allow mold to intrude.
Gruau inserts fit specific van models with 30% to 50% greater thermal efficiency.

Yeagy stated this is an important space for Wabash to participate in with its technology as it sets them up to better serve the smaller light-duty upfit market compared to larger, more traditional truck body product models.
President Joe Biden's green agenda, the lingering pandemic, climate change and technology advances combine to create opportunity, according to Yeagy.

Yeagy said at least for the next four years, it opens up a really interesting way that Wabash National can take ideas generated from an innovation standpoint and technology they have to bring sustainable solutions within those spaces.
Traditional truck bodies are the main catalyst to pay for expansion.
The value of Wabash's backlog of trailers awaiting construction rose to $1.5 billion at the end of Q4 compared to $1 billion at the end of the third quarter.
Most of the work is dry vans that dominate the Commercial Trailer Products division.
The average selling price was $27,000, flat with the prior year.
Yeagy said generally shipments are in line with how they thought that would materialize in the fourth quarter.
As they look at 2021, Wabash does not see anything that is a structural impediment moving forward.
Wabash shipped 10,610 trailers in Q4 compared to 14,950 in the same period last year.

Though Yeagy expressed concern during the third-quarter earnings call about being able to find workers to hire during the pandemic, Wabash added 600 hourly employees during the October-December period.
The company is seeking 900 more new hires this quarter, according to Chief Financial Officer Mike Pettit.
Net sales for the fourth quarter 2020 were $404.1 million.
Operating income was $10 million or 2.5% of net sales.
For the full year 2020, total revenue totaled $1.5 billion.
Operating income for the full year was $22.5 million.
Q4 net income was $5.5 million, or 10 cents per diluted share.
For the full year of 2020, net income was $7.8 million, or 15 cents a share.
Operating earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization (EBITDA) was $25.2 million, or 6.2% of net sales in Q4.
Full-year operating EBITDA was $73.6 million, or 5% of net sales.
source_title: Wabash National Refocuses Business On Cold Chain And e-commerce - Wabash National (NYSE:WNC) - Benzinga

facts: Management attributed the fourth quarter's underperformance to prolonged softness in freight, construction, and industrial demand, which led customers to defer capital spending decisions.
The company is proactively idling manufacturing facilities in Little Falls and Goshen to align production capacity with current demand.
Idling the Little Falls and Goshen facilities will reduce fixed manufacturing overhead by approximately $10 million annually.
The Parts and Service segment grew 33% year-over-year, serving as a structural stabilizer that generates higher-margin, more predictable revenue compared to cyclical equipment sales.
Early signs of freight market stabilization are appearing but have not yet reached the magnitude required to drive a sustained recovery in product orders.
The company is monitoring domestic trailer industry antidumping and countervailing duty petitions, which could potentially lead to penalties on international competitors by late 2026.
Strategic focus remains on maintaining a strong balance sheet and liquidity to provide flexibility for a powerful response once the freight market recovery threshold is met.
First quarter 2026 is expected to be the weakest period of the year for both revenue and operating margins as deferred capital spending persists.
Management anticipates full-year 2026 revenue and operating margins will likely exceed 2025 levels, despite limited visibility into the exact timing of a demand rebound.
The company expects to recognize $4 million to $5 million in additional charges in 2026 related to facility idling, with $1 million to $2 million resulting in cash expenditures.
Guidance for Q1 assumes revenue between $310 million and $330 million, with adjusted EPS ranging from negative $0.95 to negative $1.05.
Management believes the industry is building pent-up demand due to multiple years of replacement cycles falling below normalized levels, positioning for a stronger 2027.
The idling of Little Falls and Goshen facilities resulted in $16 million in non-cash charges during the fourth quarter.
A $30 million legal settlement was paid in the fourth quarter, impacting full-year free cash flow.
The Upfit business volume more than doubled in 2025 compared to 2023, with expectations to exceed 2,500 units in 2026 as new centers in Atlanta and Phoenix scale.
Management has paused near-term investments in the Trailers as a Service (TaaS) initiative after establishing the foundational assets in 2025.
Management clarified they are not exiting the refrigerated market; rather, they are repositioning the product for a better cost structure ahead of an expected 2027 market upswing.
The Goshen closure is specifically aimed at overhead optimization by leveraging structural changes made to the truck body manufacturing network over the last several years.
Revenue growth in the Parts and Service segment is expected to continue into 2026, though Q1 margins will likely be the weakest of the year due to startup costs for new upfit locations.
Management expects margins to bounce back after Q1 as upfit operations normalize and new locations like Phoenix begin contributing to the revenue profile.
Wabash expects an initial determination on February 6 regarding potential percentage penalties for international competitors related to antidumping petitions.
While physical collection of duties would occur later in the year, management believes the industry will understand the competitive impact much sooner.
The primary use of available cash will be to pay down the $45 million pulled on the ABL in 2025.
Secondary capital allocation priorities include funding internal CapEx and dividends, followed by potential share repurchases or addressing high-yield bonds due in 2028.
source_title: Wabash National Corporation Q4 2025 Earnings Call Summary

facts: Bollinger Motors Inc of Oak Park, Michigan has partnered with Lafayette, Indiana-based trailer builder, Wabash National Corp to build a last-mile refrigerated electric delivery truck.
Wabash’s lightweight composite EcoNex Technology for truck and trailer bodies will be integrated with Bollinger's Class 4 electric chassis cab.

The EcoNex Technology is made from a composite material that, Wabash claims, is more durable, lighter, and thermally efficient than other commercial products resulting in increased total payload capacity due to lower overall truck weight.
source_title: Bollinger and Wabash to produce refrigerated delivery electric truck

facts: Wabash National Corp. warned investors that its third-quarter 2018 financial results would come in lower than originally anticipated.
Wabash's stock price was down 27% so far in the year of the report.
Q3 2018 revenue was $553.1 million, compared to $612.7 million in Q2 2018 and $425.1 million in Q3 2017.
Q3 2018 operating income was $16.5 million, down from $46.0 million in Q2 2018 and $26.5 million in Q3 2017.
Q3 2018 diluted EPS was $0.08, compared to $0.54 in Q2 2018 and $0.30 in Q3 2017.
Q3 2018 free cash flow was $8.5 million, down from $54.3 million in Q2 2018 and ($340 million) in Q3 2017.
Management attributed the bottom-line decline to operating issues rather than sales problems.
Customers were unable to pick up new trailer units because their trucks were too busy.
Wabash experienced difficulty obtaining chassis from suppliers for its final mile segment.
Higher commodity costs, driven by tariffs and raw material inflation, ate into margins.
The diversified products and final mile segments posted losses in Q3 2018.
Working capital grew significantly due to a surge in product inventories and accounts receivable.
Wabash acquired its final mile business unit in the third quarter of 2017, which caused a large decline in free cash flow that quarter.
Q3 2018 revenue represented the second-highest quarterly revenue in company history.
Customer order volume boosted Wabash's backlog by 80% to $1.3 billion.
Demand for Wabash's products is so high that neither its suppliers nor its customers can keep up with delivery timelines.
CEO Brent Yeagy highlighted the sales boom and steps to address higher input costs due to tariffs.
Management expects to make up for Q3 results in the fourth quarter of 2018.
Continued strong market demand drove backlog to a new record level of $1.3 billion, an increase of 80 percent compared to the prior year quarter.
Trailer and truck body demand remains strong, supporting belief in growing secular demand in e-commerce and home delivery.
The company is taking steps to reduce margin impact from U.S. tariff policy, raw material inflation, supply base disruptions, and a very tight labor market for the remainder of 2018 and into 2019.
Management expects sequential improvement in fourth quarter operating results as it manages business challenges.
The core trailer business expects stronger pricing and a more favorable sales mix.
Wabash maintained full-year guidance for 2018 new trailer shipments to 60,000 to 62,000 units.
Full-year 2018 GAAP earnings guidance is set at $1.41 to $1.46 per diluted share.
Full-year 2018 non-GAAP earnings guidance is set at $1.50 to $1.55 per diluted share.
Wabash takes orders for products a couple of quarters in advance, meaning rising material costs after booking can eat into margins.
The company is working on passing along rising costs to customers.
Strong overall economic conditions and growth of e-commerce provide tailwinds for the trucking industry.
source_title: Trucking Is Booming, So Why Is Wall Street Punishing This Stock? | The Motley Fool

facts: Wabash National Corp. is building a $25 million semi-trailer manufacturing plant in Franklin, Ky., rather than in its headquarters city of Lafayette because a persistently low unemployment rate drove up wages and complicated efforts to hire workers.
CEO Dick Giromini told analysts that the lower costs in Kentucky would help the company be more competitive with its trailers.
Senior Vice President and Chief Operating Officer Joe Zachman stated that the Lafayette plant hit a bottleneck during peak times, causing trouble hiring people.
The new plant will employ 460 workers when it opens in two years.
Wabash National has 2,230 workers at its Lafayette plant following a recent temporary layoff of 280 employees.
The company had a 21-percent market share last year.
source_title: Labor costs drove Wabash National to Kentucky – Indianapolis Business Journal

facts: Wabash announced a significant expansion of its network in the Southeast to strengthen customer access and service in the greater Atlanta region.
The expansion includes opening a new Parts & Service center, expanding its dealer relationship with Fleetco, and adding CS Truck and Trailer to its Preferred Partner Network.
Dave Hill, vice president of Parts & Services at Wabash, stated these moves reflect building an extensive partner ecosystem that puts parts, service, and support closer to customers.
The Atlanta expansions aim to provide faster turnaround, more coverage, and better reliability for fleets operating across Georgia, Alabama, South Carolina, and Tennessee.
Wabash's new Atlanta-area Parts and Services center is strategically located at the heart of the Southeast’s trucking corridor.
The Atlanta site will offer Ready-to-Mount (RTM) truck bodies for dry freight and platform applications with rapid turnaround times for in-stock chassis.
The Atlanta site joins Wabash service centers in California, Florida, Ohio, Pennsylvania, and Texas, with additional locations on the way.
Doors at the new Atlanta center opened on September 18, 2025.
Fleetco, a long-standing Wabash dealer, will now represent Wabash in the greater Atlanta market with trailer sales.
This expansion broadens Wabash’s dealer footprint in the Southeast and gives customers additional access to new and used equipment through a trusted partner.
Bill Tirrill, president at Fleetco Inc., noted over 14 years of experience working closely with Wabash and that the Atlanta expansion allows expanding their sales force across the region.
Fleetco will provide clients immediate access to new and used trailers while improving expertise and commitment expected from the Fleetco/Wabash team.
Wabash added three CS Truck and Trailer locations to its Preferred Partner Network in Georgia.
The Preferred Partner Network brings expert service, maintenance, and Wabash Genuine Parts to more customers through authorized third-party dealerships.
The Preferred Partner Network model is central to Wabash’s strategy of making high-quality parts more accessible through a seamless national network.

Dave Hill added that expanding the dealer and partner network is critical to delivering speed and service as the customer base grows, citing Atlanta as an example of combining service centers, dealer partners, and the Preferred Partner Network.
Wabash combines physical and digital technologies to deliver innovative, end-to-end solutions that optimize supply chains across transportation, logistics, and infrastructure markets.
Headquartered in Lafayette, Indiana, Wabash designs, manufactures, and services an extensive range of products supporting first-to-final mile operations.
Products include dry and refrigerated trailers and truck bodies, platform trailers, tank trailers, and structural composites.
Through the Wabash Marketplace and Wabash Parts, customers gain access to a nationwide parts and service network, Trailers as a Service (TaaS)℠, and advanced tools designed to streamline operations.
source_title: Wabash Expands Southeast Footprint to Strengthen Customer Access and Service

facts: On January 5, 2026, Wabash National Corporation announced and initiated a plan to idle its facilities in Little Falls, Minnesota, and Goshen, Indiana.
The restructuring will result in job reductions of approximately 3 salaried and 53 hourly workers at the Little Falls facility.
The restructuring will result in job reductions of approximately 21 salaried and 193 hourly workers at the Goshen facility.
The idling and restructuring actions are expected to be substantially complete by the end of the second quarter of 2026.
The company expects to incur total charges of $15 million to $20 million, primarily non-cash asset impairment charges.
$12 million to $15 million of the restructuring charges will be recorded in the fourth quarter of 2025.
$3 million to $5 million of the restructuring charges will be recorded in the first half of 2026.
The measures are anticipated to deliver fixed cost reductions in 2026 and beyond without discontinuing any business lines.
Wabash National Corporation operates in the transportation equipment manufacturing industry, producing trailers and related equipment for freight and logistics customers.
The company focuses on efficient, large-scale industrial manufacturing across multiple U.S. facilities.
source_title: Wabash National Announces Plant Idling and Restructuring Plan - TipRanks.com

facts: Wabash National Corporation announced a 10-year agreement with Hydro, a leader in the global aluminum and energy industry.
Under the agreement, Hydro will be a key supplier for dry van trailers, refrigerated trailers with molded structural composite (MSC) technology, flatbed trailers, and truck bodies through 2031.
The agreement also covers requirements for new products Wabash National may release through 2031.
The enhanced supply aligns with Wabash National's recent announcement of additional dry van capacity set to come online at the beginning of 2023.
Future capacity for MSC Technology products is also planned under the agreement.

Richard Mansilla, Vice President, Global Supply Chain at Wabash National, stated the partnership will help address aluminum supply needs to increase production of dry van trailers, refrigerated trailers, and truck bodies with MSC Technology in the near term.
In the longer term, Wabash National plans to work more closely with Hydro on innovation and footprint optimization.
Prior to this agreement, Hydro had supplied Wabash National with aluminum extrusions for over 12 years.
Hydro is a three-time Wabash National supplier award winner and was named Supplier of the Year in 2020.

Jerrod Hoeft, Hydro's VP National Accounts, Transportation, stated that Hydro supports industries through innovative products and views Wabash National as a visionary leadership partner for creating breakthrough customer value.

Wabash National designs and manufactures dry freight and refrigerated trailers, platform trailers, tank trailers, dry and refrigerated truck bodies, structural composite panels and products, trailer aerodynamic solutions, and specialty food grade processing equipment.

Wabash National's products are sold under the brand names Wabash National®, Benson®, Brenner® Tank, Bulk Tank International, DuraPlate®, Supreme®, Transcraft®, Walker Engineered Products, and Walker Transport.
Wabash National Corporation is headquartered in Lafayette, Indiana.
Hydro has operations in 40 countries with over 140 locations and approximately 30,000 employees.
Hydro owns and operates businesses and investments with a base in sustainable industries including aluminum, energy, metal recycling, renewables, and batteries.
source_title: Wabash National Enhances New Capacity with Increased Supply Certainty

facts: Shares of Indiana-based Wabash National Corp. jumped after the maker of truck trailers announced it would diversify its business by purchasing liquid-transportation company Walker Group Holdings LLC for $360 million.
Privately held Walker Group is based in New Lisbon, Wis., and owned by Dallas-based private equity firm Insight Equity Holdings LLC.
Wabash, headquartered in Lafayette, obtained financing from Morgan Stanley and Wells Fargo for up to $450 million to fund the deal and provide additional money.
The deal is subject to regulatory approval and is expected to close during the second quarter.
Shares of Wabash jumped as much as 12.8 percent in afternoon trading before closing up 9.5 percent, to $10.71 each.
Wabash's stock was down from its 52-week high of $11.99 and had fallen sharply over the summer due to broader market tumbles and recession fears after trading above $11.50 last spring.
The stock fell as low as $4.22 per share in early October.

Wabash stated the deal will help it offer new products to its customers and immediately start adding to its profits, noting that the company makes heavy trailers used by freight haulers and semi-trucks.
Wabash's stock performance has been negatively impacted in recent years as high fuel prices and weak economic growth have squeezed over-the-road freight haulers.
Walker Group makes trailers that can carry liquids like dairy products, chemicals, or fuel.
The deal will give Wabash a new source of revenue and a business with fatter profit margins.

Last year, Wabash National posted a profit of $15 million on revenue of $1.19 billion, which was better than the prior year when the company reported a net loss of $167.2 million on revenue of $640.4 million.
During its 2011 fiscal year, Walker Group Holdings generated a profit of $28.2 million on revenue of $344 million.

facts: Wheelabrator Group Inc., a Norican Group company, announced it will consolidate its North American operations by creating a North American hub for centralized sales, service, engineering, and technology development at its LaGrange, Ga., facility.

facts: Wheelabrator started in the foundry industry, where cast parts are often cleaned by shot or sand (abrasive) blasting.
In 1932, The American Foundry Equipment Company located in Cleveland, Ohio developed and marketed an airless abrasive blasting machine to foundries, which became known as the Wheelabrator.
The machines use a rotating wheel to sling shot into parts.
The entire process is contained in a cabinet, greatly improving factory working conditions and enabling automatic handling of parts.
Shortly after the airless centrifugal blast wheel machine was developed, the company moved from Cleveland, Ohio to Mishiwaka, Indiana.
By the 1970s, the company had grown to 1500 employees and operated a 600,000 square foot facility.
In 1983, the shot-making capability was moved to Bedford, Virginia.
In 1984, the foundry was relocated to Walterboro, South Carolina.
The Indiana plant was closed in the mid-1980s because it was too old and large for the volume of business, and the equipment business moved to Georgia.
The company was divided into equipment assembly, which moved to Shenandoah (Newnan) Georgia, and manufacturing, which moved to LaGrange, Georgia.
By 1991, the company consolidated its Georgia plants in LaGrange, except for Blastrac machines which are still built in Newnan.
The company builds many different kinds of airless blast machines for foundries, aircraft and automotive industries, and equipment for the environmental industry.
The firm's primary US location is in LaGrange, Georgia.
Wheelabrator builds its wheelabrator machines in LaGrange and a portable blasting machine called the Blastrac in Newnan, Georgia.
They are affiliated with a foundry in Walterboro, South Carolina (formerly a sister company) that produces cast parts, white iron and steel parts for the wheelabrator machines.
They also produce equipment and parts, including dust collector systems for the wheelabrator machines, in a small plant in Burlington, Ontario, Canada.
The company sells about 70 to 100 machines per year, of which about 30% are standard and sold by distributors.
A Tumblast machine with fancy load and unload system sells for $80 to 90,000 while a small machine might be $25,000.
Wheelabrator is developing a new machine called the Ultrablast which uses a steel flighted mill to transport parts through the blast chamber for continuous operation rather than batch processing.
