facts: Yamaha Motor Corp. is moving its U.S. headquarters from Cypress, California, to Kennesaw, Georgia.
The company has been based in Cypress, California, since 1979 after acquiring the land.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp announced on Tuesday that Yamaha will relocate to Kennesaw between late 2026 and 2028.
Yamaha cited structural reform to improve profitability amid changing market conditions and U.S. tariffs as reasons for the move.
Yamaha already employs over 2,300 people in Georgia.
The company previously moved its marine and motorsports businesses to Kennesaw, Georgia.
Gov. Kemp called the relocation a “loud and clear testament” to the state’s appeal to global job creators.
source_title: Yamaha Relocating US Headquarters from California to Georgia - GV Wire

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp welcomed the announcement that Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. has officially decided to move its U.S. headquarters to Kennesaw, Georgia.
The company is relocating its U.S. headquarters out of California following roughly 50 years of operation there.
The relocation decision followed a meeting between Governor Brian P. Kemp and Yamaha executives in October 2025 at the Southeast U.S./Japan (SEUS-Japan) annual gathering in Tokyo.
Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. manufactures ATVs, boat engines, personal watercraft, and other motorized products.
The company currently employs over 2,300 Georgians across its operations in the state.
The relocation of the Yamaha Motor Corp. U.S. headquarters will begin in 2026 and is expected to continue through 2028.
Governor Brian Kemp stated, "After many years of great partnership, we are honored and proud to welcome Yamaha's American headquarters to the No. 1 state for business."
Governor Brian Kemp noted, "This is another loud and clear testament to what we offer job creators from around the world."
The 1.3-million-square-foot Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America factory in Newnan opened in 1986.
The Newnan factory currently employs roughly 2,000 Georgians.
Yamaha relocated its marine division to Kennesaw in 1999.
The company expanded its Kennesaw facility with a 75,000-square-foot Marine Innovation Center in 2023.
In 2019, the company moved its motorsports operations to Georgia to benefit from close proximity to its existing facilities.
source_title: Yamaha to Move HQ to Georgia - Savannah CEO

entity_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC)
canonical_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America
facts: YMMC is a product manufacturing affiliate of Yamaha Motor Corporation (YMC) and a subsidiary of Yamaha Motor (YMUS).
YMMC develops, tests, fabricates, and assembles ATVs, Golf Cars, Water Vehicles, and Recreational Off-Road Vehicles (Side by Sides).
Products are produced for global distribution.
The Newnan Yamaha Campus spans 1,355,000 square feet across six buildings on 280 acres.
The facility employs over 2,000 team members.
The Newnan facility was founded in 1986.
Production at the facility started in 1988 with golf cars.
Since 1988, the facility has produced over 4 million vehicles.
Yamaha is investing in a new SMART (Sequenced Material and Reduced Transportation) distribution facility, which will be the seventh building on the campus.
The facility is located in Coweta County, Georgia.
Product Sales and Marketing functions are specific to YMC, YMUS, and YGC; YMMC does not directly sell products to the public or service them.
Yamaha partners utilize a network of dealerships for product sales and service.
Yamaha does not manage dealership relationships or certify new dealers.

facts: YMMC and DHL Supply Chain are progressing with studies, permitting, and filings that could lead to the construction of a new distribution facility on YMMC’s Newnan, Georgia campus.
The proposed facility will be 784,700 square feet.
The project represents an estimated $60–90 million investment.
The facility would be solely dedicated to YMMC operations.
Currently, freight traffic flows between YMMC’s main plant and off-site distribution centers, contributing to congestion along Highway 34 and surrounding intersections.
Consolidating logistics operations on the YMMC campus could reduce truck traffic on local roads, ease congestion, improve safety, and incur no added cost to taxpayers.

Bob Brown, President of YMMC, stated that the opportunity is about strengthening their supply chain while also being a good neighbor, and they are working carefully through necessary studies and permitting to ensure the project benefits both Yamaha and the community for years to come.
The facility will serve as a dedicated, world-class logistics hub to support Yamaha’s manufacturing operations.
Consolidating operations on campus will reduce truck traffic on local roads.
The site design will be thoughtful to maintain the character of the surrounding area.
The project would invest in roadway improvements such as new turn lanes and upgraded intersections to improve traffic flow and safety.
Sustainable features will include stormwater management systems, tree buffers, and pervious surfaces to reduce environmental impact.
Pending successful completion of studies and required approvals, construction could begin as early as 2026.
Operations at the new facility could potentially start in the first quarter of 2028.

Dave Rumler, President of Automotive Engineering and Manufacturing at DHL Supply Chain North America, stated that DHL is proud to collaborate with Yamaha on a project that enhances supply chain performance and supports infrastructure improvements that benefit the Newnan community.

links.context: More information about Yamaha Motor Manufacturing and its role in the Georgia community

source_title: DHL Supply Chain to Build New Distribution Facility on Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Campus | Yamaha

facts: Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. is a $17 billion global Japanese brand and the world’s second largest motorcycle manufacturer.
The company announced February 26 that it is moving its U.S. headquarters from Cypress, California to Kennesaw, Georgia.
The relocation will occur by function over the period from the end of 2026 until the end of 2028.
Yamaha plans to sell all fixed assets in Cypress, including land, offices, and warehouses covering 279,000 sq. ft. across three buildings on a 25-acre campus on Katella Avenue.
The company will utilize a sale-leaseback arrangement to ensure business continuity during the transition.
Cypress served as Yamaha’s U.S. headquarters for 47 years after the company acquired the land in 1978 and established an office there in 1979.
Georgia first became involved two decades later when Yamaha relocated its Marine Business Unit to Kennesaw in Cobb County to better serve customers.
The Motorsports Business was also relocated to Kennesaw in 2019.
Yamaha already owns and occupies a 458,760-sq.-ft. office building on 37 acres just off Chastain Road in Kennesaw.
In 2023, Yamaha opened a 75,000-sq.-ft. Marine Innovation Center at the same Kennesaw site.
Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corp. of America has operated a 1.3 million-sq.-ft. assembly plant in Newnan, Coweta County since 1986.
The company leased an additional 25,000 sq. ft. at TownPark Commons in Kennesaw last November.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp announced the corporate relocation on March 10, confirming the state did not offer any incentives to convince Yamaha to move.
Tariff pressures were cited as a driving factor for structural reforms aimed at improving profitability of U.S. operations due to cost increases from U.S. tariffs and market changes.
The relocation decision was influenced by Georgia’s pro-business environment, infrastructure, workforce, and a face-to-face visit from Governor Kemp during economic development travel in Japan.
Cobb County, where Kennesaw is located, has over 800,000 people and is the third largest county in Georgia.
Since 2019, Cobb County has welcomed 40 state-worked projects representing 5,400 new jobs and $550 million of investments.
Since 2021, a total of 103 California-headquartered firms have announced corporate facility investment projects in Georgia.
Georgia’s workforce development strategy includes the MATCH program, which sends letters to high school seniors about university and technical college spots based on GPA.
The state enacted tort reform to stabilize insurance costs and is pursuing legislation to modernize corporate governance litigation to make Georgia more attractive for incorporation.
The Yamaha headquarters win adds to a series of recent automotive headquarters wins in Georgia, including Porsche, Mercedes-Benz, Asbury, and Cox.
Governor Kemp attributes success in attracting automotive companies to a cooperative approach, low cost-of-living, reliable transportation and infrastructure, and a pro-business environment.
source_title: GEORGIA: Thinking Long Term – Site Selection Magazine

facts: Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. announced it will relocate its US subsidiary Yamaha Motor Corporation, U.S.A. (YMUS) from Cypress, California to Kennesaw, Georgia.
The relocation marks the end of nearly half a century at the Cypress location.
Yamaha acquired the Cypress property in 1978 and opened its US headquarters there in 1979.
All real estate at the current Cypress location will be sold, including office buildings, warehouses, and the entire ~25.1-acre (around 102,000 square meters) property.
Yamaha has a history of shifting operations to Georgia: its marine division was relocated to Kennesaw in 1999.
In 2018, a comprehensive reorganization moved the motorsports division (except testing and racing departments) to Georgia.
In 2019, motorcycle and off-road operations were also based in Kennesaw.
Yamaha relocated its golf cart, WaveRunner, ATV, and side-by-side divisions to Georgia.
Yamaha currently employs around 2,600 people in Georgia.
The manufacturing facility in Newnan, Georgia produces all full-size Yamaha ATVs and side-by-side vehicles as well as WaveRunners and golf carts for US and global markets.
Only the corporate administration and financial services division remained at Cypress before this move.
Relocation had been planned for some time; evidence includes the dismantling and shipping of the "Wall of Champions" to Georgia.
Primary reasons for restructuring are rising costs from US tariffs and changing market conditions.
Yamaha's Japanese parent company reported a 30.4 percent decline in operating profit in fiscal year 2025, attributed partly to US tariffs.
The relocation is part of structural reforms aimed at improving profitability of Yamaha’s US operations.
Yamaha aims for a medium-to-long-term profit structure not exclusively dependent on revenue growth, seeking a more resilient organization.
Transition involves a sale-and-leaseback arrangement for the Cypress property to maintain operations during transition; sale price, buyer, and exact timeline are under evaluation.
Move will occur in phases from late 2026 through late 2028.
High Southern California real estate costs and California’s tax burden are key factors in the decision.
Selling the campus will free up tied-up capital and immediately relieve Yamaha's balance sheet.
Leadership change coincides with relocation: Eishin Chihana was appointed CEO of the US division in July 2025.
Chihana previously headed Yamaha in India and has experience in Europe, India, and Southeast Asia.
Consolidating all US functions at a single location aligns with strategic direction under new leadership.
Yamaha remains committed to the US market at the same scale; no product withdrawals or dealer closures have been announced.
A small group of employees will remain stationed in California for test rides and motorsport.
Yamaha has three offices in Georgia and 13 additional locations in the USA, including in Wisconsin, Alabama, and Florida.
With this move, Georgia will definitively become the center of all Yamaha US operations.
No cuts to model lineup or dealer network are planned for US customers.
The move illustrates economic pressure on the US motorcycle industry from tariffs, rising costs, and changing market conditions.
Yamaha is attempting to create a foundation through cost reductions and organizational streamlining to withstand potential sales stagnation.
source_title: Yamaha USA Relocation to Georgia: Headquarters Leaves California

facts: Yazaki North America, part of the Japanese-based Yazaki Group, created its Monozukuri Research Laboratory in Canton in 2014 to increase innovation and build customer trust.
The lab's products include wire harnesses, power distribution and control products, connectors, driver information systems, sensors, and hybrid and electric vehicle products.
Lab Director Doug Neely stated that reducing cost is the No. 1 reason for implementing smart manufacturing tools, with quality being important as well.
The lab's mission remains researching technologies and testing them to determine if they reduce costs and improve efficiency in factories.
Neely noted the goal is to bring more digitization into the factory with more confidence and control during product launches.
Neely added, “We are just a ways off from modeling everything effectively.”
Neely said there is no end in sight to the team’s work because new technologies with smart factory applications are constantly being invented.
Neely stated, “How do we know we have arrived at Industry 4.0 compliance? We will never arrive because when we do, we’ll be in developing I5.0.”
Neely expressed interest in exploring General Motors CEO Mark Reuss’s suggestion to capture knowledge as it happens using video technologies that could include smartphones.

David E. Cole, founder of the Center for Automotive Research in Ann Arbor and former University of Michigan engineering professor, captured Reuss’s suggestion that he wanted to capture the innovation of his young engineers who are very tech savvy, even though they may not have a lot of real-world auto manufacturing experience.
Hitachi Astemo Americas, a Tier 1 auto supplier, created what it calls a Smart Factory Center of Excellence.

Maurice Henson, Senior Director of Operations – Information Technology at Hitachi Astemo Indiana, said the center is composed of cross-functional teams where experts specializing in essential areas for Hitachi regularly meet for developing products.
The Smart Factory Center consolidates various hubs for gathering and spreading knowledge primarily for training purposes and is augmented by video training and knowledge capture.
Henson noted that global and local groups have created many Smart Factory-focused videos posted on SharePoint to highlight how Hitachi Astemo is actively pushing forward with Industry 4.0.
Hitachi Astemo’s Smart Factory initiative uses Amazon Web Services to aggregate data and make it easily available to all employees throughout the global corporation.
An additional connected factory improvement is currently in development approval to create the Hitachi Astemo Standard to accelerate making data available to connected workers.

Sudhanshu Gaur, vice president of R&D at Hitachi America Ltd and chief architect of Smart Manufacturing at Hitachi Astemo, said the company captures knowledge from employees using cloud-based platforms and Artificial Intelligence, with insights shared across the organization to enable informed decision making.
Generative AI enhances information retrieval within Hitachi Astemo’s initiative.

Gaur stated the vision is to provide timely and efficient access to designed information, tailoring the retrieval process to the specific needs and contexts of different teams within their global network.

The company’s online Communities of Practice comprises various manufacturing sites that are actively adopting Digital Transformation initiatives, where practitioners collaborate, exchange ideas, discuss challenges, and share success stories.

Hitachi Astemo captures knowledge from production operations using video snapshots of newly developed applications to demonstrate new ideas and advancements, encouraging other teams and sites to explore similar innovations.

The company records interviews with staff members describing their experiences, insights, and best practices, ranging from engineers discussing complex problem-solving to operators sharing daily routines.
Gaur said the company encourages teams to create short video snippets when facing specific challenges or issues, which are then shared with relevant experts to facilitate efficient troubleshooting.
The company is using AI-driven insights from captured videos to improve production efficiency, automate quality inspections, and augment operators’ daily work routines.
A Hitachi Astemo spokeswoman said the company is looking into how to capture knowledge as it happens using video technologies that could also include smartphones.

Maurice Henson believes most Michigan manufacturers will adopt I4.0 technologies as businesses recognize the benefits of embracing these principles to enhance operations, improve efficiency, and stay competitive globally.
Henson noted that recognizing this principle may mean the difference in how companies survive for the next 25 years in Michigan, which is largely known as the home of the US auto industry.
Henson stated it will likely be necessary for automakers like General Motors and Ford Motor Company to demand I4.0 compliance by their respective supply chains.
Sudhanshu Gaur echoed that a mandate by OEMs, together with MEDC incentives, can certainly drive compliance and create a standardized approach to adopting I4.0 practices.

Gaur noted that I4.0’s focus on data-driven decision-making means seamless data exchange between manufacturers and OEMs can enhance the entire product lifecycle and potentially unlock new business opportunities.
Gaur summarized that a healthy business ecosystem is needed to help set Industry 4.0 implementation goals and provide the right mentorship and training incentives to realize those goals.

The article is the third in a series highlighting Michigan manufacturers addressing Industry 4.0 challenges, following stories on Michigan manufacturers needing to adopt I4.0 and General Motors embracing I4.0 technologies.

A fourth story in February will cover The Michigan Manufacturing Technology Center evaluating technology for SMEs, and a fifth story in March will review educational institutions sharing the I4.0 message with smaller manufacturers.

Mike Brennan is the Editor & Publisher of MITechNews.Com, with 20 years covering manufacturing and business, including work at Boeing in Seattle, Chrysler in Detroit, and tech manufacturing in Silicon Valley.
source_title: Embracing The Future: How Yazaki North America And Hitachi Astemo Are Leading The Industry 4.0 Revolution - MITechNews

entity_name: One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) EV Loan Interest Deduction
canonical_name: One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) EV Loan Interest Deduction
facts: The American-made tax credit applies to the 2026 EV car loan interest deduction.
It replaces a one-time credit under the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) with a recurring electric vehicle loan interest deduction under the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA).
The IRA offers a single tax credit of up to $7,500 for the purchase of EVs and hybrid vehicles, paid at the time of purchase.
The new OBBBA tax benefit is a multi-year deduction of up to $10,000 annually that makes the financing of American-made vehicles more affordable.
The $7,500 Point-of-Sale Credit was a feature of the IRA that allowed EV buyers to transfer their federal tax credit directly to a dealership at the time of purchase.
This credit effectively reduced the price of an EV by $7,500 on the spot if you met the eligibility requirements (battery, income caps, MSRP limits and North American assembly).
The credit provision was replaced on September 30, 2025.
Applicable EVs purchased on or before that date can still claim the credit “retroactively” on 2025 tax returns (filed in 2026) using IRS Form 8936.
The OBBBA interest deduction is an annual, above-the-line incentive that lowers taxable income by allowing owners to deduct up to $10,000 in loan interest each year through 2028.
The “Car Loan Interest Deduction” applies to qualifying loans that originated between January 1, 2025, and December 31, 2028.
To qualify for the deduction, the vehicle must be made in America, and purchased brand-new, after December 31, 2024.
The deduction is intended for personal use vehicles of middle-income earners.
Anyone with a modified adjusted gross income (MAGI) up to $100,000 (Single) or $200,000 (married filing jointly) are eligible for the full deduction.
The benefit decreases by $200 for every $1,000 over these limits up to the ceiling of $150,000 (single) or $250,000 (joint).
To qualify under the “made in America” rule, the vehicle must have its final assembly in the United States.

The OBBBA encourages domestic manufacturing, so unlike the one-time POS credit under the IRA, the new deduction does not apply to vehicles manufactured in Canada or Mexico and there are no strict battery requirements.
A Ford Mustang Mach-E assembled in Mexico qualified for previous credits, but the interest on its loan is not deductible under the OBBBA because it was not assembled in the U.S.
The Tesla Model 3 is assembled in Fremont, California, so it does qualify.
Full deduction if MAGI is under $100,000 for single filers; no deduction above $150,000.
Full deduction if MAGI is under $200,000 for joint filers; no deduction above $250,000.
The $10,000 cap reduces by $200 for every $1,000 earned over the threshold.
Vehicle must be brand-new (original use must begin with you); used/CPO vehicles do not qualify.
The vehicle must have final assembly in the United States.
Gross Vehicle Weight Rating (GVWR) must be under 14,000 pounds (covers all light-duty EVs/SUVs).
Must be used primarily for personal use (over 50%); business-only vehicles are excluded.
Must be a first lien auto loan; leases and “balloon” financing usually do not qualify.
Loan must have originated between Jan 1, 2025, and Dec 31, 2028.
There are no MSRP limits for this deduction.

The most definitive way to check a vehicle’s origin is the Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) on the Safety Compliance Certification Label (silver/white sticker on the driver’s side dashboard or doorframe).
It explicitly lists the “Final Assembly Point.”
To verify whether final assembly is in the United States check the NHTSA VIN Decoder or simply look for a VIN starting with 1, 4, 5 or 7.
Vin numbers starting with 2 (Canada), 3 (Mexico), J (Japan), W (Germany), or S (South Korea), do not qualify for the OBBBA interest deduction.
Ford qualifying VINs start with 1 (F-150 Lightning); non-qualifying start with 3 (Mach-E is Mexico); qualifying US plants in Dearborn, MI.
Rivian qualifying VINs start with 7 (All R1 & R2 models); all U.S. built; qualifying US plants in Normal, IL.
Lucid qualifying VINs start with 7 (All Air & Gravity); all U.S. built; qualifying US plants in Casa Grande, AZ.
GM (Chevy/Cadillac) qualifying VINs start with 1 (Silverado, Blazer, LYRIQ); non-qualifying start with 3 (Equinox EV – Mexico); qualifying US plants in Detroit, MI; Spring Hill, TN.
Toyota qualifying VINs start with 4 or 5 (Grand Highlander/Sienna); non-qualifying start with J (Japan-made imports); qualifying US plants in Princeton, IN.
Honda / Acura qualifying VINs start with 1 or 4 (Prologue, MDX, etc.); non-qualifying start with J or 2 (Japan or Canada); qualifying US plants in Lincoln, AL; Marysville, OH.
Mercedes-Benz qualifying VINs start with 4 (EQE / EQS SUVs); non-qualifying start with W (Germany); qualifying US plants in Vance, AL.
Subaru qualifying VINs start with 4; non-qualifying start with J (Japan); qualifying US plants in Lafayette, IN.
Volkswagen qualifying VINs start with 1 (ID.4); non-qualifying start with W (Germany); qualifying US plants in Chattanooga, TN.
Hyundai / Kia qualifying VINs start with 5 (Ioniq 5, EV9, Ioniq 9); non-qualifying start with K (South Korea); qualifying US plants in Bryan County, GA; West Point, GA.
Tesla qualifying VINs start with 1, 5, or 7; non-qualifying start with L (China); qualifying US plants in Fremont, CA; Austin, TX.
At the dealership you can look for a Monroney sticker on the window of the vehicle, specifically the section titled “Parts Content Information.”
It will explicitly state: “Final Assembly Point:” followed by the city and state (e.g., Normal, IL or Austin, TX).
For the 2026 deduction, this must be a U.S. location.
Required documents to claim the EV interest deduction include Mortgage Interest Statement, IRS Form 1098-V: Proof of the annual interest you paid.
Your lender is required to send you this form by January 31st if you paid more than $600 in interest.
Additional Deductions, IRS Form 1040, Schedule 1-A: Establishes vehicle identity.
This is where you will list the deduction and your 17-digit Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) to qualify under the “Made in America” eligibility requirements.
Monroney Sticker (Window Sticker): Proves the “Final Assembly” was in the USA. Keep a copy (or photo) of the original window sticker as the IRS may ask for this in an audit.
Bill of Sale / Purchase Agreement: Confirms you are the original owner (first use) and that the vehicle was purchased brand-new.
Signed Loan Agreement: Proves the loan is a first lien that was started after December 31, 2024.
Title/Registration: Ownership documentation that proves the vehicle is registered for personal use.
If you refinanced your EV loan in 2026, you would also need the closing statements for the original and the new loan.
You can only deduct interest on the portion of the new loan used to pay off the original purchase principal.
By prioritizing American-made vehicles, the OBBBA ensures that qualifying cars use domestic supply chains, making production more efficient and less vulnerable to global shipping disruptions.
It was designed to incentivize “onshoring”—bringing manufacturing jobs back to American soil.
By tying interest deduction to U.S.-assembled EVs, the government is essentially rewarding you for supporting the domestic clean energy economy.
source_title: Does the 2026 Tax Deduction for American-Made Vehicles Apply to EVs? - Clean Energy CU

facts: YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. was honored with a 2021 GEAR Award by the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).
The award ceremony took place on January 14 at YKK's Macon, Georgia plant.
GDEcD named YKK “Supplier of the Year, employing 200+ people” for its contributions to advancing Georgia’s automotive and mobility industries.
YKK employs over 200 people at its Macon, Georgia facility.
YKK produces a wide range of zippers, hook and loop fasteners, plastic hardware, snaps, and buttons for automobile seat assembly and truck tonneau cover hold-downs.
YKK supplies its automotive fastening products to more than 100 automotive customers.
The company utilizes a vertically integrated structure that allows it to create its own materials and machinery while ensuring quality, dependable supply, and minimized manufacturing costs.
Andrew Capezzuto, Chief Administrative Officer and General Counsel of GDEcD, presented the award and stated that the 2021 GEAR award recognizes companies that are good corporate stewards.
Capezzuto noted that GDEcD facilitates partnerships with local businesses to foster a team approach between communities and companies.
Macon-Bibb County Mayor Lester Miller praised YKK, stating it is about investing in people and community rather than just making zippers.

Robert Stamps, Vice President and Business Development Leader – Transportation Fastening Materials, and Keith Kratz, Product Development Engineer, demonstrated various YKK automotive fastening products following the award presentation.
The ERGO-J® sheet fastener secures motor vehicle seat covering material edges, improves ergonomic efficacy during seat assembly, and reduces carpal tunnel syndrome rates for automotive workers.
The QUICKFIT® concealed hook fastener allows operators to achieve a craftsmanship-like appearance when molded into seat foam and reduces seat weight compared to metal wire tie-down systems.
The 5CH CONCEAL® zipper forms a perfect seam when closed, allowing seat manufacturers to significantly decrease seat back panel weight to improve fuel efficiency.
Jim Reed, President of YKK Corporation of America, expressed gratitude for the automotive group's growth and noted the company reinvests profits into employees through its cyclical process.
YKK aims to reduce its emissions by 50% by 2030 and achieve net-zero emissions by 2050 under its Cycle of Goodness philosophy.
source_title: YKK USA Receives 2021 GEAR Award - YKK Americas

facts: YKK AP America Inc. installed a new goods-to-person (GTP) automation system at its residential window plant in Macon, Georgia.
The system was installed at the 400,000-square-foot purpose-built facility’s shipping department to further reduce the manual movement of heavy windows.
Dennis Howard serves as senior manager of transportation and logistics, and Daymon Loyd serves as director of residential manufacturing.
According to YKK AP officials, the GTP system improves operations and quality and reduces time to load by 39%.
The GTP system uses autonomous robots to move finished products from the end of the line to material storage units and into the GTP field, a fenced area that houses final products.
Daymon Loyd stated: 'Overall, our company is making strategic investments in automation to reduce risk and improve safety.'

Loyd added: 'Specifically, in residential, our goal was to reduce material handling to improve quality. The shipping department is where most material handling is done, so it made sense to start there.'
When the Macon facility opened in early 2024, YKK AP aimed for zero injuries, zero backorders and 100% on-time delivery.
The facility currently achieves 97% on-time delivery rates, and to hit 100%, YKK AP needed to invest in the GTP system to close the gap.
Loyd says the facility handles 80% of its products with GTP; the other 20% are too large for the material storage units.
Howard says integrating the system with YKK AP’s IT infrastructure was the most significant deployment challenge.
Howard notes: 'Additionally, training for the interface of the system (not the end user) served as a challenge, and we had to have an aggressive training schedule.'
The bulk of the training involved troubleshooting technical issues as they arise because that’s one thing you can expect with any new technology.
Howard and Loyd say that while YKK AP did not have to retrain employees, it still had to train them to use the specific system.
Fortunately, the process was seamless thanks to its user-friendly interface.
Howard says the GTP system is vital for companies seeking to improve safety via automation, but they must be open to change.

Loyd notes: 'This was our residential manufacturing team’s first foray into GTP technology, and we had to remain open and flexible to changing the original plans to accommodate the technology correctly.'
He adds that much of this capital equipment was on the manufacturing floor before it was in action, and it took time and trial and error to deploy.
Now that it is, it’s been great for the team to have witnessed the project from start to finish, and now see it operating at its full potential.
source_title: YKK AP America Introduces Autonomous GTP System at Georgia Window Facility

Georgia offers a skilled workforce, favorable business climate, and a central location within reach of 80% of the U.S. population via a two-day truck drive or a two-hour flight.
The strategic placement enables YKK AP to better serve its national and global customer base with fast, efficient distribution.
YKK AP America’s mission is “Building a better society through architectural products.”
The project establishes Georgia as a forward-thinking partner in architectural and industrial innovation.

facts: YKK AP America Inc. started operations at its new manufacturing facility in Macon, Georgia.
The 400,000-square-foot purpose-built facility manufactures residential windows and doors for homebuilders and homeowners throughout the Southeast region.
The facility is located at 100 YKK AP Way, in Macon’s industrial district.
The opening of the facility follows more than 18 months of design and planning.
With a growing residential market, the new facility provides YKK AP with the ability to enhance customer satisfaction by doubling production capacity.
The facility sets the stage for the upcoming new and upgraded StyleView Classic series that will be produced on new manufacturing lines.
All employees have been relocated from the previous manufacturing facility.
Oliver Stepe, president and CEO of YKK AP America, states: 'The new manufacturing facility is an investment in our people and our customers.'

The facility integrates vinyl extrusion, fabrication and glazing, while improving operational efficiencies by bringing all processes under one roof, a strategic floor layout and investment in new equipment.
The company plans to add automated goods-to-person technology to reduce the manual movement of heavy windows across the plant.
To enhance the employee experience, the facility was designed with increased natural light, updated furnishing and outdoor spaces, including a walking trail and basketball/pickleball court.
The facility is also 100% climate controlled.
Oliver Stepe adds: 'Our purpose as a company is to build a better society through architectural products.'

facts: YKK AP America Inc. is a leading manufacturer of award-winning commercial facade systems and residential doors and windows.
The company celebrated the opening of its newly expanded commercial and residential design center, Studio 101, on Thursday, June 22, 2023.
Studio 101 is located at 101 Marietta Street in downtown Atlanta, Georgia.
The design center was first launched in 2021 with a core focus on commercial architectural products and has been expanded to include residential windows and doors.

Studio 101 includes a designated training center designed to provide customers and partners with hands-on training experiences for the proper installation of YKK AP’s entrances, storefronts, curtain walls, window walls, and window and door products.
The training center is also designed as a shared space for the architecture, engineering, and construction (AEC) community to host their own training.
The design center is co-located with YKK AP’s headquarters, which officially relocated from Austell to Atlanta last year.
Oliver Stepe serves as president and CEO of YKK AP America Inc.

YKK AP America Inc. is a subsidiary of YKK AP Inc., a global architectural products company based in Tokyo, Japan.

facts: YKK AP America Inc. announced the creation of its Manufacturing Excellence organization, along with leadership appointments across its commercial and residential manufacturing operations.
The changes are designed to strengthen manufacturing performance, accountability, and alignment as the business continues to grow.
Daymon Loyd has been promoted to lead the Manufacturing Excellence organization, which brings together manufacturing quality, engineering and maintenance under one structure.
The organization is designed to improve coordination, accelerate problem-solving and drive more consistent execution across commercial manufacturing operations.

In this role, Loyd will oversee key YKK AP manufacturing priorities including customer satisfaction, on-time delivery, quality, equipment reliability, process optimization and strategic oversight of the Dublin plant’s investment and modernization programs.

Mike Turner, president, commercial business at YKK AP America Inc., stated Daymon’s appointment reflects the company’s continued commitment to building a strong manufacturing organization well-positioned to support customers and long-term growth.
Mike Turner noted Loyd’s experience across both residential and commercial manufacturing gives him a strong foundation to lead the effort.

Mike Turner emphasized that as the company continues to invest in quality, reliability and service, aligning key functions under focused leadership will strengthen operational execution and enhance the ability to serve the evolving needs of the market.
To support its residential business, YKK AP has promoted Derek Bessenbacher to residential operations manager.
Bessenbacher will provide dedicated leadership and strengthen day-to-day execution in residential manufacturing.
The move reflects the company’s focus on supporting continued growth in its residential operations through greater oversight and accountability.
Josh Jensen, president, residential business at YKK AP America Inc., stated focused plant leadership is critical to delivering the quality, consistency and service customers expect.
Derek Bessenbacher is a 10-year employee who has served in roles of increasing responsibility across manufacturing engineering, process improvement, and quality control.

Josh Jensen expressed full confidence that Bessenbacher will bring the experience and expertise needed to support continued investment in people and structure required to strengthen execution and position the residential business for long-term growth.
source_title: YKK AP makes leadership appointments | LBM Journal

facts: Atlanta-based YKK AP America, a subsidiary of Tokyo-based YKK AP, plans to invest up to $125 million to expand its middle Georgia facility.
The expansion will create 100 additional jobs at the Bibb County manufacturing facility.
State officials declined to divulge whether the state offered any incentives for the expansion or what it might cost Georgia taxpayers.
Jessica Atwell, a Georgia Department of Economic Development communications specialist, confirmed the project is located in a Tier 1 tax credit zone.
Under the Tier 1 credit program, companies that create at least two jobs are eligible for a $4,000 tax credit per new job.
Companies may use 100% of the Tier 1 tax credit toward their tax liability.
Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that YKK AP has a strong history of growth and success in Georgia.
YKK AP opened its first branch in Atlanta in 1986.
YKK AP America established its manufacturing presence in middle Georgia five years after 1986.
The company has expanded its presence in Georgia and built relationships with local communities over the years.
Companies in Georgia's advanced manufacturing industry, like YKK AP, played a key role in bringing jobs and opportunities to the state, creating the second most new jobs in Georgia last year.
The expansion project involves adding machinery and equipment at the facility located in the Macon-Bibb I-75 Industrial Park.
YKK AP currently supports 250 employees in Macon.
YKK AP America president Oliver Stepe said the new facility will help introduce further vertical integration of vinyl extrusion, glass, and window production.
The expanded operations will allow the company to create enhanced efficiencies to manufacture high-quality windows and doors for homebuilders and homeowners.
source_title: Incentives Unknown as Georgia Announces Expansion at YKK AP Facility - Tennessee Star

facts: YKK AP America Inc. will invest up to $125 million in an expansion that includes a new manufacturing facility and additional machinery and equipment in Bibb County.
The expansion will create 100 new jobs in addition to the 250 jobs YKK AP currently supports in Macon, Georgia.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the investment and stated that being the No. 1 state for business means ensuring companies already operating in Georgia can grow and thrive.
Governor Kemp noted that YKK AP recognizes the advantages of doing business in middle Georgia and has chosen to expand its relationship with the Macon community through 100 new jobs.
YKK AP specializes in architectural products for commercial and residential buildings and entered the U.S. market in 1991 with a vertically integrated commercial products factory in Dublin, Georgia.
With headquarters in Atlanta, YKK AP and its Ontario-based subsidiary Erie Architectural Products employ over 1,200 employees in North America, including nearly 900 within the State of Georgia.

President Oliver Stepe stated the new facility will expand the workforce, introduce further vertical integration of vinyl extrusion, glass, and window production, and allow enhanced efficiencies to manufacture high-quality windows and doors for homebuilders and homeowners.
The investment builds toward YKK AP's long-term vision to be the leader in the residential window and door market and positions the company to expand its business in and beyond the Southeast region.
The new state-of-the-art facility will be located at the Macon-Bibb I-75 Industrial Park, a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) certified site.
After completion, YKK AP will relocate its operations and employees from its current site on Ocmulgee Boulevard to the new facility.
The Macon facility will become home to the company's residential division, which manufactures vinyl windows and doors for builders and homeowners.

The facility will serve as a model plant for future North American manufacturing facilities, featuring new technologies, advanced sustainable manufacturing processes, and enhanced work environments for employees.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in early 2024.
YKK AP is currently hiring for numerous positions related to the expansion.
Macon-Bibb County Mayor Lester M. Miller highlighted that one of their longest-running business partners is making another investment in the community and bringing an additional 100 jobs to the area.
Mayor Miller noted that using the new state-of-the-art facility as a blueprint for future facilities illustrates YKK AP's trust in Macon-Bibb County and Georgia as the best place to do business.

Senior Project Manager Candice Scott represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce team on this competitive project in partnership with the Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority and Georgia Power.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that YKK AP opened its first branch in Atlanta in 1986 and chose middle Georgia to establish its U.S. manufacturing presence five years later.

Commissioner Wilson noted that companies in the advanced manufacturing industry, like YKK AP, have played a key role in bringing jobs and opportunities to Georgia, creating the second most new jobs in the state last year.
To earn GRAD certification, a property must undergo due diligence and complete specific certification requirements prior to a visit by a prospective business.
Completing GRAD certification with GDEcD is a proactive way for community planners and landowners to help catalyze economic growth and industrial development for their regions.
Through the GRAD program, Georgia has more than 60 industrial certified sites that are ready for fast-track industrial projects.
source_title: Read more about Governor Kemp: Building Materials Manufacturer YKK AP America Expands Residential Window Production Capabilities with New Facility in Bibb County - EIN Presswire

facts: The Dublin City Council approved a memorandum of understanding with YKK to upgrade its Dublin plant.
The Laurens County Commission approved a tax abatement for YKK’s planned $50 million renovation at its Dublin plant.

County Administrator Bryan Rogers described the project as a “modernization” of the YKK plant that will include new technology and machinery and improvements to the heating-and-air conditioning system.
Rogers stated, “It’s more of a renovation, not an expansion.”

The tax abatement is included in a memorandum of understanding with the company that has already been approved by the Dublin City Council and has the backing of the Dublin-Laurens Development Authority.
Under the agreement, YKK will receive a five-year tax break on the plant improvements.
The company will pay no taxes in the first year of the abatement.
Taxes will increase by 20 percent each subsequent year until reaching 100 percent in the sixth year.
Rogers clarified that the tax abatement applies only to the renovated part of the plant.
Commissioner Trae Kemp called the tax abatement for the $50 million investment a “no-brainer,” and the board passed the measure unanimously.
source_title: Laurens County Commission OKs tax abatement for $50 million project at YKK – Courier Herald Today

facts: Adient plc is the world's largest automotive seating supplier, with products ending up in tens of millions of vehicles annually across Detroit, Shanghai, Stuttgart, and São Paulo.
The company operates more than two hundred manufacturing and assembly plants across twenty-nine countries and employs north of sixty-five thousand people.
In fiscal year 2024, Adient reported consolidated revenue of nearly fifteen billion dollars.

Six years prior to the article's reference point, Adient's stock had cratered ninety-two percent from its all-time high, and it had piled up more than three billion dollars in cumulative losses over two fiscal years.

Adient officially began independent operations on October 31, 2016, following its separation from Johnson Controls International, entering the market as the industry's biggest player with $16.6 billion in revenue.
At its spin-off, Adient was born with a poison pill of $3.5 billion in debt strapped to its balance sheet before it even opened its doors.

In connection with the spin-off, Adient obtained $3.5 billion in debt financing; approximately $500 million stayed for working capital while three billion dollars was distributed directly to Johnson Controls as a pre-separation dividend.

The average interest rate on this debt was 3.4 percent, translating to roughly $125 million in annual interest expense that could never be invested in new manufacturing technology or talent retention.
Adient acknowledged in regulatory filings that its significant leverage put it at a competitive disadvantage relative to less-indebted peers.
R. Bruce McDonald was tapped as Adient's first chairman and chief executive officer, serving as a finance-oriented leader rather than an operations specialist.
Adient spent $100 million to renovate the historic Marquette Building in downtown Detroit and relocate roughly five hundred employees there, including about one hundred new positions.
The Marquette Building had been purchased for $16.9 million from Mexican billionaire Carlos Slim, who acquired it for just $5.8 million in 2014.
Adient also paid approximately $19 million for a nearby parking deck and surface lot as part of its Detroit headquarters plan.
In fiscal year 2017, Adient delivered $16.2 billion in revenue and $877 million in net income, with its stock reaching an all-time high of $84.06 on October 24, 2017.
Bruce McDonald's compensation package for fiscal year 2017 hit $24.3 million, making him metro Detroit's highest-paid public company CEO.

In September 2017, Adient acquired Futuris, an Oak Park, Michigan-based automotive seat manufacturer owned by Clearlake Capital, adding fifteen manufacturing facilities across Asia and North America and expected to contribute $500 million in additional annual revenue.
In January 2018, Adient and Boeing announced the formation of Adient Aerospace, a joint venture to design and manufacture airliner seats, with Adient holding 50.01 percent and Boeing 49.99 percent.
The airline seat market was valued at $4.5 billion in 2017, with forecasts projecting $6 billion by 2026.
Adient Aerospace headquarters were established in Kaiserslautern, Germany, near Frankfurt, with customer service operations in Seattle.
In the second quarter of fiscal 2018, Adient's Seat Structures and Mechanisms division recorded a $279 million non-cash goodwill impairment charge, admitting it overpaid for the asset.
In June 2018, Adient scrapped its Detroit headquarters renovation plan entirely and sold the Marquette Building and surrounding properties at an eleven-million-dollar loss.
Detlef Juerss, Adient's VP of engineering and CTO, later stated that Detroit was not the right timing for the headquarters move.

Fiscal 2018 results showed a devastating net loss of $1.7 billion on revenue of $17.4 billion, with the fourth quarter alone producing a $1.36 billion loss driven by $809 million in restructuring and impairment charges.
The cash dividend was suspended as Adient's stock entered freefall.
Bruce McDonald stepped down as chairman and CEO on June 11, 2018, remaining as an adviser through September 30 while collecting his $1.5 million salary and $250,000 in relocation expenses.
His 2017 equity award of approximately $17 million was forfeited upon departure.
Frederick "Fritz" Henderson served as Adient's interim CEO for roughly three months to stabilize the company while the board hunted for a permanent leader.
Doug DelGrosso became Adient's president and CEO on October 1, 2018, bringing experience from rebuilding Chassix after its March 2015 Chapter 11 bankruptcy.
Under DelGrosso's turnaround strategy, approximately thirteen thousand employee positions were targeted for elimination, representing roughly one out of every six employees at the company.
Premium freight costs were systematically attacked as a key metric to drive down, reversing the ballooning expenses caused by mismanaged launches under previous leadership.
At the Warren Bridgewater plant in suburban Detroit, customer disruptions had been running at more than ten per month during the first quarter of fiscal 2019 but fell below one by the fourth quarter.
Fiscal 2019 results showed the restructuring was working, with the net loss narrowing dramatically from $1.7 billion to $491 million on revenue of $16.5 billion.
The fourth-quarter fiscal 2019 loss was reduced to just $4 million compared to $1.36 billion a year earlier.

On November 14, 2019, DelGrosso announced that roughly 1,300 non-plant salaried employees would not work or be paid during Thanksgiving and New Year's due to a GM strike severely impacting the Americas region.
In March 2020, COVID-19 caused the most severe demand shock in the global automotive industry since the Great Depression, shutting down assembly plants worldwide.
Adient's stock plunged to an all-time low of $6.53 on March 18, 2020, a decline of approximately ninety-two percent from its October 2017 high.
Revenue for fiscal 2020 collapsed to $12.67 billion, a twenty-three percent decline from the prior year, while operating margins contracted to roughly five percent.
Competitors Lear and Magna operated above eleven percent operating margins during the same period, highlighting Adient's competitive disadvantage.
The company accumulated more than three billion dollars in cumulative losses since its spin-off, with interest expense consuming an ever-growing share of diminished earnings.
source_title: Adient (ADNT) - The Complete Story | Spin-off. Turnaround. Auto Seating Empire.

facts: On August 7, 2025, Jerome Dorlack, President and Chief Executive Officer of Adient US LLC, a subsidiary of Adient PLC ADNT, sold 12,000 shares of the company.
Following this transaction, the insider now owns 441,025 shares of Adient PLC.
Adient PLC is a global leader in automotive seating.
The company designs, manufactures, and markets a wide range of seating systems and components for passenger cars, commercial vehicles, and light trucks.
Adient PLC operates in more than 30 countries and supplies products to all major automakers.
Insider transaction history indicates no insider buys over the past year, but two insider sells during the same period.
Over the past year, Jerome Dorlack has sold a total of 12,000 shares and has not purchased any shares.
On the day of the recent sale, shares of Adient PLC were trading at $22.77, giving the stock a market cap of $1.786 billion.
With a trading price of $22.77 and a GF Value of $34.27, Adient PLC has a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 0.66.
GuruFocus considers the stock a "Possible Value Trap, Think Twice" based on its GF Value.

The GF Value is an intrinsic value estimate developed by GuruFocus, calculated using historical multiples such as price-earnings ratio, price-sales ratio, price-book ratio, and price-to-free cash flow that the stock has traded at.
The GF Value calculation includes a GuruFocus adjustment factor based on the company’s past returns and growth.
The GF Value calculation incorporates future estimates of business performance from Morningstar analysts.
source_title: Adient PLC (ADNT) President and CEO Jerome Dorlack Sells 12,000

Through its Corporate Work Study Program (CWSP), Adient partners with Detroit Cristo Rey High School, allowing students like sophomore Diego Vargas to work at Adient while attending school.

Adient expanded its operational footprint by acquiring an automotive seating foam production plant located in Romulus, MI.

facts: Adient will shut down its Athens manufacturing plant at the end of June.
Local officials say the closure will affect roughly 210 employees.
Adient filed a Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification (WARN) notice with the Tennessee Department of Labor regarding the shutdown.
The WARN notice states that the Athens plant will close on June 30.
About 210 jobs will be cut as a result of the closure.
The filing is part of a series of WARN notices Adient has made in the region.
The filing contributes to statewide layoff totals logged so far this year.
Local officials said the WARN notice activated state and local rapid-response procedures designed to get in front of employees early and connect them with resources before the final shutdown date.
The McMinn County Economic Development Authority is coordinating with the local workforce development board and state partners to organize reemployment services and training.
A rapid response team will be on site to provide immediate help to workers navigating the transition.
The EDA will ensure affected employees are informed about employment services, training options, and reemployment assistance.
The EDA is also helping Adient manage the transition.
City of Athens leaders plan to work alongside the EDA throughout the transition so affected families can tap into immediate support.
Tennessee’s Rapid Response system and the American Job Center network provide reemployment support, job-search workshops, and training opportunities for workers who lose their jobs.

The Tennessee Department of Labor and Workforce Development’s WARN technical assistance guide outlines how rapid-response teams can hold on-site briefings, assist with unemployment insurance, and connect eligible employees with training or trade-adjustment programs.
The guide directs workers to local career centers as a key entry point.
Affected Adient employees can apply for unemployment benefits through the state’s career services network.
Employees can look into apprenticeship or retraining paths through the state’s career services network.
Adient is listed among McMinn County’s major employers in the county’s business directory, Make It In McMinn.
Officials stated that their top priority is getting support to the workers while the EDA and the city coordinate next steps.
Employees are being encouraged to monitor official county updates and stay in touch with state career centers for the latest information on services and opportunities.
source_title: Athens Auto Plant Set To Shutter As 210 Jobs Hang In The Balance

facts: Discussions with the Uzbek delegation placed particular emphasis on the strategic significance of the Anaklia Deep Sea Port for Georgia, Uzbekistan, and the Middle Corridor.
Both sides engaged in substantive discussions on opportunities to further deepen bilateral trade and economic cooperation.
Special attention was paid to strengthening transport and logistics opportunities between the two countries.
The ongoing construction of the Anaklia Deep Sea Port project was highlighted as highly important for both Georgia and Uzbekistan.
The Georgian government plans to modernize the country's railway network to boost cargo-handling capacity and enhance freight transportation efficiency.
Modernization of Georgian Railways is expected to improve and increase the capacity of cargo flows.
Discussions centered on increasing bilateral trade and broadening export opportunities for Georgian products to diversify markets.
Members of the Uzbek delegation will be shown the ongoing construction of the Anaklia Deep Sea Port, Poti Free Industrial Zone, and other port infrastructure during the visit.
The visits aim to identify future cooperation opportunities and develop a practical action plan to accelerate trade and economic relations expansion.
The Anaklia Deep Sea Port is one of Georgia's most important strategic projects and will play a pivotal role in strengthening the country's economy and reinforcing its strategic position.
Active construction is currently underway at the Anaklia Deep Sea Port, with the port expected to receive its first vessel in 2029.
The Anaklia Deep Sea Port will remain under state ownership, while international companies will be involved only in managing terminal infrastructure.

Development of the Anaklia Deep Sea Port will follow a model ensuring the state's leading role in strategic infrastructure while creating a competitive environment for multiple partner countries and international companies.

The Georgian government decided to develop the Anaklia Deep-Sea Port under a landlord model, where the state retains ownership of core maritime and port infrastructure and oversees its development and management.
Georgia and Uzbekistan reached key agreements during the Uzbek presidential visit to boost trade, investments, and the Middle Corridor's role.
Uzbek Investment Minister Laziz Kudratov praised Georgia's expanding port infrastructure after visiting the Anaklia Deep Sea Port construction site and Poti's port facilities.
Georgia and Uzbekistan are discussing the establishment of an Uzbek logistics and production hub in Georgia.
source_title: Anaklia Port central to Georgia-Uzbekistan talks, says Georgian Economy Minister  | imedinews

facts: Archer Aviation Inc., a pioneer in electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft, announced plans to build its manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia, next to the Covington Municipal Airport.
Archer announced a new partnership with Molicel to supply battery cells for Archer’s Midnight Aircraft.
The manufacturing facility will be located on a 96-acre property.
Archer intends to initially develop a 350,000-square-foot plant.
The initial plant capacity is projected to produce up to 650 aircraft annually.
Over the long run, the eVTOL aircraft production plant is expected to provide over 1,000 jobs.
Construction of the facility is expected to be finished in the first half of 2024.
Initial production is projected to start in the second half of 2024.
The facility will have the capacity to grow by an additional 550,000 square feet.
The expanded capacity is estimated to enable the production of up to 2,300 aircraft annually.
Archer Aviation thoroughly investigated all available locations across the US to choose the site for its first manufacturing facility.

Important factors in site selection included the availability of skilled workers on the local labor market, accessibility of utilities, capability of conducting smooth flight test operations, construction expenses, and logistics.
A grant from the Georgia REBA is part of the incentive package Archer will receive.
The incentive package also includes tax breaks and land conveyance.
Archer believes Synovus, the biggest bank with its headquarters in Georgia, will offer finance for the project.
Adam Goldstein, Archer’s CEO, stated that after many months of discussion and evaluation, they are excited to select Georgia as the location for their manufacturing facility.
Adam Goldstein noted that the U.S. has long led the world in aerospace technology and Georgia has played a vital role in that industry.

Adam Goldstein stated that eVTOL technology can transform how urban and rural communities live and commute, and the factory can create pathways to highly skilled manufacturing jobs and other ladders of social and economic opportunity.
Adam Goldstein expressed gratitude to Governor Kemp along with Bob Kosek, Sara Gershon, Serra Hall, Frank Turner, their teams, and Archer colleagues for pioneering innovative aviation technologies.
Governor Brian P. Kemp commented that Georgia has made nurturing innovation a priority as they work with businesses to define the future.
Governor Kemp noted that Georgia's established leadership in the aerospace industry was essential to landing Archer Aviation in Newton County.
Governor Kemp highlighted that Archer can take advantage of Georgia's signature workforce training program Quick Start to help fill over 1,000 jobs in Covington in the long term.
Georgia is home to more than 800 aerospace businesses.

The state maintains a strong pipeline for aerospace education with 12 high schools providing industry training, 5 technical institutions offering aviation programs, and 7 universities granting degrees in aerospace engineering.

For more than 10 years, the Georgia Center of Innovation for Aerospace, a strategic division of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, has fostered an ecosystem of opportunity for cutting-edge businesses like Archer.
source_title: Archer Aviation Selects Georgia For Manufacturing Facility Next to Covington Municipal Airport – Candela Green Energy

facts: Archer Aviation announced it has closed financing and development deals for the construction of its electric aircraft manufacturing plant in Covington.
Since March, Archer has been building a 350,000-square foot plant to build electric vertical take-off and landing aircraft next to Covington Municipal Airport, about an hour east of Atlanta.
The plant is expected to be completed next year.
The plant is expected to eventually create more than 1,000 jobs.
Santa Clara, California-based Archer secured a $65 million financing deal with Columbus-based Synovus Financial Corp. for a “substantial majority” of the cost of construction for the first phase.
Archer struck a design-build agreement with Alpharetta-based Evans General Contractors LLC for development of the facility.
Archer is one of the key startups seeking to develop a new market of electric aircraft — known as eVTOL aircraft ― for air taxi service to transport people within cities, avoiding road traffic.
Founded in 2018, it has raised over $1 billion in funding and has been publicly traded since 2021.
The startup company is not yet profitable as it is investing heavily in aircraft development.
The company is developing a piloted four-passenger eVTOL aircraft called Midnight and is working toward Federal Aviation Administration certification of the aircraft in 2025.
It is building several test aircraft in California, and will later move manufacturing to the Georgia plant for high-volume production.
In Covington, Archer has been grading the site and plans to soon begin pouring concrete.
The facility is being built to eventually produce as many as 650 aircraft annually.
There is room for a second phase of construction to expand the plant to as much as 900,000 square feet to build more than 2,000 aircraft per year.
Archer will get an incentive package worth about one-third of its $118 million capital investment commitment, including tax incentives and a Georgia Regional Economic Business Assistance grant.
The company has a deal with Stellantis, the automaker formed in the merger of Fiat Chrysler and Peugeot, as a manufacturing partner and investor.
It also has an agreement to share flight test data with the U.S. Department of Defense and to send several of its aircraft to the U.S. Air Force for military use.
For its planned commercial air taxi service, Archer has partnered with United Airlines, and announced plans last year to launch service in 2025 from Manhattan to Newark Liberty International Airport.

Atlanta-based Delta Air Lines has partnered with a different eVTOL developer, Joby Aviation, with the idea of eventually transporting passengers from their homes to hubs in New York and Los Angeles to connect to Delta flights.
source_title: Archer Aviation Closes Financing for Georgia Electric Aircraft Plant | Aviation Pros

facts: Stock quotes for Archer Aviation collapsed nearly a quarter in the past two weeks.
Risk-tolerant investors are advised to look at Archer as long as the share price is below the recent high of $13.30 per share, reached on May 16.
At the close on Friday, May 30, one share was trading 24% below that high, at $10.09.
Before the open on June 2, Archer's stock had edged 0.4% lower.
The company faces "hugely exciting" opportunities in 2025 as its eVTOL aircraft, Midnight, should make its first commercial flight.
Archer stated that its Midnight aircraft could allow passengers to replace "one-to-two-hour drives with flights that take 5-15 minutes, potentially saving hours versus sitting in traffic."
The company currently has no revenue, no profits, and no finished and certified product.
For the first quarter, the company reported a net loss of $93.4 million.
At the end of the first quarter, the company estimated it was only 15% of the way through the U.S. regulatory approval process.
In 2025, Archer plans to be the first carrier to launch air taxi services in Abu Dhabi.
A successful launch in the UAE would help assure investors that Archer's long-term plans are feasible and potential customers that air taxi flights are safe.

Archer faced pressure from a short-seller campaign in May, with Culper Research publishing a report on May 20 alleging the company "over the past 12 months, has systematically misled, deceived, or outright lied to investors about virtually every supposed milestone related to its development and testing of its eVTOL aircraft, Midnight."
Culper Research believed that Archer’s push for near-term commercialization is "not only premature, but reckless."
On May 21, the day after the report came out, Archer shares fell 4% to $10.82 apiece.
Since the beginning of the year, Archer stock is up a slight 3.5%, but over the last 12 months, it has more than tripled.
Eight Wall Street analysts advise buying Archer ("buys" and "overweights"), while two others advise holding.
The average target price from analysts is $11.75 per share, implying upside of more than 16% versus the last closing price.
source_title: Motley Fool asks: Is it worth investing in eVTOL aircraft developer Archer on a dip? – Oninvest

facts: Archer is starting production of its first Midnight aircraft at its ARC facility in Covington, GA.
Archer plans to build up to 10 Midnight aircraft this year to support ongoing certification testing programs and deployments with key partners.
Abu Dhabi Aviation (ADA) will be Archer’s first Launch Edition customer for the Midnight aircraft.
ADA plans to deploy an initial fleet of Midnight aircraft beginning later this year.
Archer will provide ADA with a team of pilots, technicians, and engineers to support the initial operational ramp.
Archer announced an exclusive partnership with Anduril Industries Inc. to jointly develop a hybrid vertical take off and landing (VTOL) aircraft for critical defense applications.
Archer believes the potential demand for the hybrid VTOL aircraft is stronger than expected.
Archer expects the partnership with Anduril to result in sizable programs of record without needing FAA certification for these specific aircraft.
Archer’s total liquidity is over $1 billion, which includes a Q4 2024 ending cash balance of $835 million and $302 million raised through an equity offering in February.
Archer’s spending in Q4 2024 remained within its guided range and stayed nearly flat for the second straight quarter.
Archer reported total operating expenses of $124.2 million in Q4 2024 (GAAP) and $509.7 million for FY 2024 (GAAP).
Archer reported a net loss of $198.1 million in Q4 2024 and $536.8 million for FY 2024.
Archer held cash and cash equivalents of $834.5 million as of the end of Q4 2024.
Archer reported an adjusted EBITDA loss of $94.8 million in Q4 2024 (non-GAAP) and $368.9 million for FY 2024 (non-GAAP).
Archer expects its adjusted EBITDA to be a loss of $95 million to $110 million for the first quarter of 2025.
Archer’s founder and CEO Adam Goldstein stated the company is on track to deliver its first revenue-generating Midnight aircraft later this year.

The “Launch Edition” commercialization program aims to establish a pragmatic and repeatable playbook to deploy Midnight commercially in dozens of early adopter markets ahead of FAA type certification.
