facts: Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that the state broke economic development records for a third year in a row.
From July 1, 2022, through June 30, 2023, total investments in facility expansions and new locations totaled more than $24 billion.
These investments resulted in 38,400 new jobs statewide.
82 percent of the new jobs were created outside metro Atlanta.
Statewide annual investment totals increased by 131 percent over the last three years.
Investment growth is driven in part by international investments from markets such as South Korea, Japan and Europe.
Corporations choose Georgia for relocations and expansions due to development-ready sites, a skilled and diverse workforce, and logistics infrastructure connecting to a global market.
Companies locating in Georgia have access to workforce development programs and benefit from a low corporate tax rate and business incentives.
The state’s corporate tax rate sits at 5.75 percent.
New and expanding companies can earn Job Tax Credits for creating new jobs in Georgia.
Morgan Stanley continued to grow its workforce across several locations in the metro Atlanta area in 2023.
In fiscal year 2023, expansions accounted for 71 percent of total projects announced across the state.
In fiscal year 2023, new locations accounted for 65 percent of new jobs announced across the state.
Aurubis and Porsche have made Georgia their North American headquarters.
19 FORTUNE 500s including Aflac, Global Payments and UPS use Georgia for their headquarters.
The U.S. Soccer Federation announced it would move its headquarters and construct a first-of-its kind national training center in Georgia.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development runs the “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development” (GRAD) Certification Program to streamline economic development.
GRAD offers businesses and site selectors a portfolio of pre-qualified industrial sites with completed due diligence prior to prospect visits.
GRAD Certified sites were critical in winning key projects, including about half of the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) suppliers and numerous battery supply chain projects.
HMGMA broke ground in October 2022.
Project suppliers for HMGMA have announced investments totaling more than $2.5 billion.
Automotive parts company Hwashin announced a multibillion-dollar investment in Middle Georgia in October 2023 on a GRAD-Certified site.
The GRAD program helped spur a 112 percent increase in statewide advanced manufacturing investments in fiscal year 2023 compared to fiscal year 2021.
Two new sites joined the state’s roster of more than 60 GRAD Certified properties in 2023.
Georgia’s workforce consists of 5.3 million people, strengthened by investments in technical college training, apprenticeship programs, and K-12 education.
Georgia Quick Start is the nation’s number one workforce training program and has trained over one million employees for companies expanding or locating in the state.
Companies like Qcells, Hyundai Motor Group and SK Battery America are benefiting from Georgia Quick Start’s ability to customize training and replicate actual processes for Day One production.
Part of the Technical College System of Georgia (TCSG), Georgia Quick Start currently operates five training centers.
Georgia Quick Start is expanding its advanced manufacturing center and designing new facilities for clean energy manufacturers and suppliers.
In February, TCSG, Quick Start and HMGMA broke ground on the Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia on the same site as the Metaplant.
The training center is inspired by the facility built for Kia Georgia, which opened in 2008 and is still in use today.
Georgia’s top-ranked colleges include two of the nation’s top 20 public universities: the Georgia Institute of Technology and the University of Georgia.
Two of the nation’s top five HBCUs are located in Georgia: Spelman College and Morehouse College.
Georgia offers specialized programs including Cyber and FinTech Academies and the Georgia Film Academy, which prepares students for the state’s $4.1 billion film industry.
Savannah College of Art and Design (SCAD) has campuses in Savannah and Atlanta preparing a diverse, creative workforce.
Georgia’s HOPE Scholarship provides tuition assistance to recent high school graduates and adults seeking further education.
The state made a million-dollar investment in new apprenticeships to train students for careers in healthcare, manufacturing and construction.
The state launched one of the largest state-run direct admissions initiatives in the nation.
Georgia leads in moving people and goods globally through its logistics network supporting cleantech and corporate headquarters.
Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport is the busiest and most efficient airport in the world.
The Port of Savannah is the single largest and fastest-growing container terminal in America.
The Port of Brunswick is one of the most utilized port terminals in the U.S. for roll-on, roll-off cargo.
Georgia has three inland ports that provide regions across the state with direct paths to the Port of Savannah and Brunswick.
The state has 5,000 miles of rail, creating the most extensive rail system and largest intermodal hub in the Southeast.
In fiscal year 2023, suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility OEMs resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state.
Mobility and cleantech projects alone delivered three investments of over $1 billion and six of the largest projects by job creation in fiscal year 2023.
The Georgia Center of Innovation connects public and private partners in strategic ecosystems to foster innovation in mobility and cleantech industries.
Aerospace is growing significantly in Georgia, overlapping with e-mobility through companies like Archer Aviation Inc.
Gulfstream has maintained its headquarters in Georgia since 1967 and continues to expand.
Georgia has the fifth-largest population of active and retired service members in the country, offering opportunities for defense contractors.
source_title: Georgia: Pro-Business Environment, Development-Ready Property, Record-Breaking Investment | Trade and Industry Development

facts: Pratt & Whitney announced a $206 million expansion to its Columbus, Ga., campus at the 2023 Paris Air Show in France on Tuesday.
The expansion promises an additional 400 jobs.

Pratt & Whitney has used Columbus, Ga. (Georgia’s second-largest city, which hugs the Alabama border) as its home base for manufacturing critical components for military and commercial engines since the mid-1980s.
Airbus is a major aerospace customer of Pratt & Whitney.
Airbus supplies passenger jets for Atlanta-based Delta Air Lines and carriers around the world.
Gov. Brian Kemp attended the Paris Air Show where multiple Georgia-related announcements were made.
Kemp touted Pratt & Whitney’s new investment in a news release as a sign that Georgia’s sky-high business ambitions are being rewarded.
Kemp said: “From the flights that go through the world’s most utilized airport to the engines in the very airplanes, the Peach State is crucial to the movement of goods and products.”
Kemp added: “Pratt & Whitney’s decision to expand will only further that reputation.”
Georgia is home to Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport, the world’s busiest airport.
Georgia has more than 800 aerospace facilities, including several operated by Lockheed Martin and private jet-maker Gulfstream.
Last year, Georgia factories shipped $9.2 billion of aerospace products across the globe.
This made Georgia the fifth-largest exporter in the U.S., according to the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
Pratt & Whitney has an existing employee base of roughly 2,000 in Columbus.
Workers at the Columbus campus maintain hundreds of engines and produce compressor airfoils (the metal blades seen whirring in a jet turbine that produce thrust).
The expansion is expected to be completed by 2028.
The project will include 81,000 square feet of new engine maintenance space and new machinery.
The new space and machinery will allow the company to annually overhaul 400 Geared Turbofan (GTF) engines.
Pratt & Whitney President Shane Eddy said: “Our Columbus business has grown from a small manufacturing facility to a large, state-of-the-art manufacturing and overhaul center.”
Eddy added: “This investment will help ensure that we have the infrastructure, machinery and equipment upgrades in place to provide the best products and services to our customers worldwide.”
Pratt & Whitney will likely qualify for job creation tax credits and worker training through Georgia Quick Start.
A Georgia Department of Economic Development spokesperson said negotiations involving discretionary incentives, such as grants, remain active.
The expansion comes on the heels of a new engine repair shop by Delta TechOps, which services Pratt & Whitney’s GTF engines.
The Delta TechOps facility is 155,000 square feet and houses more than 100 mechanics.
These mechanics repair engines used across about 150 airlines.
Officials for Archer Aviation announced progress on the California-based company’s forthcoming 100-acre campus in Covington, Ga., east of Atlanta.
Archer plans to build its first mass production facility for electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft for taxi service at the Covington campus.
The factory is a partnership with European auto brand Stallantis.
The Archer Aviation facility is on track to open in mid-2024.

Archer founder and CEO Adam Goldstein said: “High-volume manufacturing is critical to ensuring we can meet this goal and joining forces with one of the leading mobility companies in the world is helping us realize the once-in-a-generation opportunity we have to redefine urban transportation.”
source_title: Jet engine maker announces big Georgia expansion at Paris Air Show | Stars and Stripes

facts: Archer Aviation has completed the construction of its high-volume manufacturing facility, ARC, in Georgia, US.
The ARC facility is situated adjacent to the Covington Municipal Airport and covers an area of about 400,000ft².
The aircraft manufacturing facility is set to start production in early 2025, aiming to achieve a production rate of two aircraft per month by year’s end.
Archer received the certificate of occupancy for ARC earlier this week, marking a significant milestone towards commencing the tooling load-in for the initial manufacturing line.
Archer founder and CEO Adam Goldstein stated that the completion of the facility is a testament to the industry shifting from R&D into commercialization.
With construction on ARC complete, Archer's team is focused on the start of production planned for early next year, followed by execution and scaling.
Archer teamed up with automobile manufacturing company Stellantis to build ARC, which is focused on mass-producing Archer’s Midnight all-electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft (eVTOL).
The Midnight aircraft is designed to provide a sustainable, low-noise, and safe mode of transportation.
The Midnight aircraft offers an alternative to traditional ground transport in densely populated urban areas.
The facility is the result of an expanded tie-up between Stellantis and Archer Aviation.
Both companies are working to finalize an agreement making Stellantis the exclusive contract manufacturer for the aircraft at the ARC facility.
Stellantis is contributing capital, advanced manufacturing technology, and experienced personnel to scale production to 650 aircraft annually by 2030 at the new facility.

Stellantis chief manufacturing and supply chain officer Arnaud Deboeuf stated that supporting Archer’s electric aircraft growth with deep manufacturing expertise is a great opportunity for both companies.

Archer announced it successfully raised an additional $430m in equity capital this month from longtime strategic partners Stellantis and United Airlines, along with new investors Wellington Management and 2PointZero.
Earlier this year, Archer received almost $400m in further funding from Stellantis to fund the labor costs for its eVTOL aircraft manufacturing program.
source_title: Archer Aviation opens new high-volume aircraft facility in Georgia

facts: Construction of Archer's high-rate manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia, has been completed.
The company received a certificate of occupancy for its 400,000-ft. 2 facility.
The facility is located adjacent to the Covington Municipal Airport.
Archer says it is now ready to begin...
source_title: Archer Completes Construction Of Georgia Production Facility | Aviation Week

facts: Archer Aviation is a startup air taxi developer that has unveiled its prototype four-seat electric aircraft named Midnight.
The Midnight eVTOL craft will fly up to 150 mph and as far as 60 miles, but is optimized for missions of around 20 miles.
Recharges from flights of approximately 20 miles take Archer about 10 minutes.
The unveiling event took place Wednesday evening in a hangar at Palo Alto airport.
Archer CEO Adam Goldstein stated the company is prioritizing getting the vehicle to market over building the longest or fastest flying vehicle.
United Airlines has entered an agreement to purchase as many as 200 Archer Midnights.
United plans to use the Midnight craft to ferry customers quickly from densely populated urban areas to airports.
United and Archer announced their first shared route will connect lower Manhattan with Newark, delivering commuters to the airport in less than 10 minutes.
Archer is targeting late 2024 for certification of the air taxi.
United hopes to begin the commuter service in 2025.
The companies plan to announce additional routes soon.
Archer COO Tom Muniz predicted that air taxis and Archer's technology will change how people move and live.
Midnight features a lean body with a wingspan of nearly 50 feet for stability.
The craft has 12 engines, including six in the rear used only to provide lift during takeoff.
Archer is regarded as a leader in the race toward electric air taxi certification alongside competitors Joby, Vertical Aerospace, Lilium, and Eve Air Mobility.
Joby has backing from Delta Air Lines, Vertical Aerospace has backing from American and Virgin Atlantic, and Eve Air Mobility has backing from Sky West and Republic.
Midnight was designed since day one with a focus on finding the straightest line toward commercialization for short missions of approximately 20 miles in urban spaces.
New York is identified as an obvious target market for the service.
United Airlines venture capital head Mike Leskinen envisions having up to 50 vertiports in the New York-area alone eventually.
United foresees charging approximately $100 per seat for aerial taxi rides initially, with prices lowering as the service scales up.
Los Angeles was called out by Goldstein as a model market where more than 5 million people spend more than one hour per day traveling 20 miles.
A simple engine design and safety redundancy on every part of the Midnight craft are intended to speed Archer's path to FAA certification.
Goldstein projects that Archer will produce hundreds of aircraft per year by 2028 and scale into the thousands annually after that.
Target markets during early commercialization include replacing existing helicopter services and scheduled point-to-point routes in targeted cities.
The company plans to manufacture the Midnight at a plant in Covington, Ga.
Midnight test flights are targeted to begin early next year.
source_title: Archer unveils Midnight four-seater electric vertical takeoff aircraft: Travel Weekly

facts: Archer received the certificate of occupancy for its ~400,000 square foot aircraft manufacturing facility—referred to as ARC—in Covington, GA and held a commemorative ribbon cutting ceremony.

Tooling load-in is already underway ahead of the planned start of initial production beginning in early 2025, with the goal being to ramp production to a rate of two aircraft per month by the end of the year.

Stellantis and Archer are working to finalize an agreement pursuant to which Stellantis would work with Archer as its exclusive contract manufacturer to mass produce Archer’s Midnight eVTOL aircraft at this facility.

As part of the partnership, Stellantis will contribute capital, advanced manufacturing technology and expertise and experienced personnel with the goal of scaling this facility to 650 aircraft annually by 2030.
ARC is connected to the Covington Municipal Airport in Georgia.

Georgia Governor Brian Kemp commented that announcing Archer’s choice to build the new manufacturing facility in Newton County marked just the start of an incredible relationship and reaffirmed Georgia's commitment to creating quality jobs and opportunities for years to come.

Congressman Mike Collins of Georgia, who sits on the House Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure Committee and Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, added that the completion of Archer's manufacturing facility in Covington highlights Georgia’s leadership in innovation, advanced manufacturing and job creation.

Archer founder and CEO Adam Goldstein stated that the completion of the facility is a testament to the industry shifting from R&D into commercialization, praised the team and partners for getting construction done fast and on budget, and noted the focus is now on execution and scaling.

Stellantis Chief Manufacturing and Supply Chain Officer Arnaud Deboeuf commented that supporting Archer’s electric aircraft growth with deep manufacturing expertise is a great opportunity for both companies and a significant step in taking clean mobility to the sky.

ARC is where Archer plans to produce its all-electric vertical take-off and landing aircraft, Midnight, which is designed to offer passengers a sustainable, low-noise and safe alternative to ground transportation in congested cities around the world.

Covington Mayor Fleeta Baggett, State Rep Tim Fleming, Newton County Development President Serra Hall, Newton County Chamber President Debbie Harper, Covington City Manager Tres Thomas, Newton County Commissioner Lanier Sims, and Archer VP of Operations Glen Burks were joined by several local leaders to commemorate the completion of construction with a ribbon-cutting ceremony.

The press release contains forward-looking statements regarding Archer’s future business plans, aircraft performance, certification timelines, production ramp-up, and volume expectations, noting they are predictions subject to risks detailed in SEC filings.
source_title: Archer

facts: On July 31, 2024, Archer Aviation Inc. issued a press release providing an update on the construction progress of its high-volume manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia.
Archer reaffirmed an expected 2024 completion date for the Covington facility.
In preparation for this facility's completion, last week Archer showcased its Midnight aircraft at Atlantic Aviation's new FBO terminal at DeKalb–Peachtree Municipal Airport in Atlanta.

Guests included government officials from Georgia's Office of the Governor, Georgia's Congressional Offices, the City of Atlanta, Georgia's State Legislature, the Federal Aviation Administration and other local governments.
Archer completed the first build phase earlier this year, inclusive of grading and foundation work.
The company is nearing completion of phase two, the erection of the exterior walls and roof.
Phase three, comprising interior fit out, and phase four, the equipment load in and power on, are on track to be completed in the coming months.
Once complete, this facility will be nearly 400,000 square feet.
The facility will support Archer's planned production of up to 650 Midnight aircraft annually.
This production volume would make it one of the largest manufacturing facilities by volume in the aviation industry.
Archer's buildout is taking place in close partnership with Stellantis.
Archer and Stellantis are continuing to progress their plans to mass-produce Archer's Midnight aircraft together.

Adam Goldstein, CEO and founder of Archer, stated that thanks to the hard work of the Archer and Stellantis teams, they are tracking towards completing this manufacturing facility by the end of the year.
Goldstein noted that once complete, Archer will focus on ramping production up to 650 aircraft a year and aims to be the first in the eVTOL industry to achieve scaled manufacturing.
Midnight is Archer's electric vertical take-off and landing aircraft.
The Midnight aircraft is built around Archer's proprietary electric powertrain.
Midnight is designed to offer passengers a sustainable, low-noise and safe alternative to ground transportation.
The aircraft travels at speeds up to 150 mph, turning hour-long ground commutes into minutes in the air.
Midnight is designed for back-to-back flights of 20-50 miles with minimal charge time in between.
The aircraft is 100 times quieter than a helicopter at cruising altitudes and nearly inaudible from the streets below.
Midnight features redundant systems across the aircraft allowing Archer to target similar levels of safety as commercial airliners.
The Midnight is designed to carry a pilot, up to four passengers and carry-on luggage.
source_title: Archer Aviation showcases Midnight aircraft in Atlanta as it nears completion of high-volume manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia | Press Release | Business Air News

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. ramped aircraft manufacturing across its facilities in California and Georgia during Q2 2025.
Six Midnight aircraft were in production during Q2 2025, including three in final assembly.
Archer is building these aircraft across its "golden manufacturing line" in Silicon Valley and its high-volume facility in Georgia.
The FAA continued conducting reviews and inspections of Archer's manufacturing operations as part of the production certificate efforts.
LA 2028 Olympic Games selected Archer as its Official Air Taxi Provider to help align federal and local stakeholders around achieving FAA type certification and scaled operations.
Archer partnered with the U.S. Department of Transportation, the FAA, and the White House on an Executive Order in June 2025 to promote American dominance in eVTOL aircraft.
The June 2025 Executive Order creates a Presidential mandate for early U.S. deployments as soon as next year.
Archer signed definitive agreements with Abu Dhabi Aviation and the Abu Dhabi Investment Office to activate the first of several planned Launch Edition programs globally.
Archer delivered its first Midnight aircraft to the UAE and commenced its flight test program in Abu Dhabi.
Archer expects initial commercial payments for the UAE Launch Edition program later this year.
Archer Defense is seeing strong demand from four major allied defense programs worldwide.
During Q2 2025, Archer acquired a patent portfolio and critical talent from Overair to accelerate its defense program.
During Q2 2025, Archer acquired specialized defense composite manufacturing assets from Mission Critical Composites in Southern California.
Archer reported total operating expenses of $176.1 million (GAAP) and $123.5 million (Non-GAAP) for Q2 2025.
Archer reported a net loss of $206.0 million for Q2 2025.
Archer reported adjusted EBITDA of $(118.7) million for Q2 2025.
Archer held cash and cash equivalents of $1,724.0 million as of Q2 2025.
Stock-based compensation expense for Q2 2025 was $51.8 million.
Stellantis warrant expense for Q2 2025 was $0.8 million.
Adam Goldstein, Archer's founder and CEO, commented on the company's second quarter 2025 results and noted execution from a position of strength with sector-leading liquidity.

links.context: Investor relations website and webcast replay

source_title: ACHR Press Release: Archer Announces Second Quarter Results, High...

facts: Archer Aviation shares were up in Monday afternoon trading as the electric air-taxi company stayed on traders’ radar.
Investors are weighing the company’s 2026 commercial launch targets against its ongoing high cash burn.
Shares last changed hands at $6.87, rising 0.8%.
The stock swung from $6.52 to $6.90.
About 46.2 million shares traded.
Market cap was close to $5.26 billion.
Archer is telling investors it sees first U.S. operations happening this year as part of the White House’s eVTOL Integration Pilot Program.
The company’s eVTOLs—electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft—run on batteries and are built to take off like helicopters but fly short distances like small planes.
The Federal Aviation Administration said in March it picked eight eVTOL pilot projects in 26 states, planning for operations to start by summer 2026.
Archer was listed as a partner for projects tied to the Port Authority of New York and New Jersey, Texas, and Florida.
Joby Aviation and BETA Technologies were also named as rivals in these projects.
Archer said last month it finished Phase 3 of the FAA’s four-step Type Certification process for its Midnight aircraft.
Investors see this as a key point for Archer moving test flights toward revenue.
Type certification means the FAA has cleared the aircraft design for safety requirements.

Archer founder and CEO Adam Goldstein said after first-quarter results that the company is “far more than an air taxi company,” and highlighted defense and AI software as areas where Archer is putting money and focus.
For the first quarter, the company posted $1.6 million in revenue and a net loss at $217.7 million.
Cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments totaled $1.78 billion.
Archer put out a forecast for second-quarter adjusted EBITDA loss between $170 million and $200 million, using the non-GAAP figure to show burn rate.
Archer has “two aircraft that are flying today,” Goldstein said on the earnings call.

He said the company is moving forward with a first batch of eight to 10 aircraft, with some assigned to flight testing, some set aside for the eIPP launch edition and the rest for ramping up production.
Peers were mostly higher.
Joby Aviation added 1.4%, and Vertical Aerospace was up 1.5%.
BETA Technologies eased 0.7%.
Archer said in its annual report that no FAA-certified eVTOLs are in commercial use in the U.S. and certification hold-ups, infrastructure sign-offs, pilot rules and public buy-in could stall growth.
It also told investors it hasn’t made much money from its core business yet and sees more losses and higher costs ahead.
Investors are watching if Archer can turn regulatory milestones, flight tests, and its strong cash pile into real aircraft running—before mounting losses shift the focus back to funding worries.
Traders are watching what happens with the FAA certification steps, transition flight tests with pilots, and any news on U.S. pilot ops.
Archer says it wants to finish the piloted transition phase and begin eIPP flights before year-end.
source_title: Archer Aviation stock moves up as investors watch for 2026 air-taxi test

facts: Construction crews are preparing to break ground for a new $118-million electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft manufacturing facility alongside Covington Municipal Airport in Newton County, outside Atlanta, Ga.
The facility will consist of three separate buildings across more than 96 sprawling acres.
Up to 2,300 aircraft per year will be produced at the plant.
The facility is slated to open in the first half of 2024.
The facility is critical to Georgia’s efforts to remain a leader in the aerospace industry.

Georgia has a robust aerospace education pipeline comprising dozens of high schools with training programs, five technical colleges with aviation programs, and several universities offering degrees in aerospace engineering.
Georgia is currently home to more than 800 aerospace companies.
Santa Clara, Calif.-based Archer Aviation Inc., an aerospace company designing and developing eVTOL aircraft, is building the facility.
Archer Aviation's vision is to unlock the skies and free everyone to reimagine how they move and spend time.
The facility will create 1,000 jobs over a 10-year period.

Adam Goldstein, founder and CEO of Archer, announced the decision to locate the company’s first manufacturing facility in Covington to ramp production for Urban Air Mobility networks across major cities nationwide.
The aircraft manufacturing facility is part of a long-term master plan for the airport and will utilize one of several large parcels of land set aside for development adjacent to it.
The plant will be strategically accessible from Georgia’s robust interstate system via I-20.

Glen Burks, vice-president of manufacturing at Archer, described the facility as a crucial step in bringing eVTOL travel to life and praised Covington as a location and partner for the company's development.
Construction of the plant is expected to take roughly a year.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp stated that fostering innovation is a state priority and that Georgia’s established aerospace leadership was essential to attracting Archer Aviation to Newton County.

The Georgia Center of Innovation for Aerospace, a strategic arm of the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), has spent over 10 years cultivating an ecosystem for innovative companies by building and connecting Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS) networks for Advanced Air Mobility commercialization.

The GDEcD's Global Commerce Industrial Projects team represented the agency on the project in partnership with the Newton County Industrial Development Authority, Metro Atlanta Chamber of Commerce, Georgia Center of Innovation’s Aerospace industry team, Georgia EMC, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia Tech.
GDEcD commissioner Pat Wilson noted that Archer Aviation is at the forefront of aviation technological evolution and will use innovative eVTOL design to sustainably tackle urban mobility issues.

Metro Atlanta Chamber president and CEO Katie Kirkpatrick stated that the region's aviation expertise combined with academic, business, civic, and government partner support makes it an ideal location for the investment.

Lanier Sims, chairman of the Newton County Industrial Development Authority, stated that Archer fits the community’s industry mix and committed to ensuring the local workforce is prepared for the jobs created by Archer Aviation.
source_title: eVTOL facility project critical to unlocking Georgia’s skies

facts: San Jose-headquartered Archer Aviation has been chosen as the official air taxi partner for the upcoming 2028 Olympic Games in Los Angeles.
The company’s ‘Midnight’ electric Vertical Take-Off and Landing (eVTOL) will ferry VIPs, fans, and athletes throughout the event.
The Midnight is Archer’s flagship four-passenger, one-pilot eVTOL.
This craft is much quieter than helicopters and other similar traditional air taxis, and is designed specifically for short-range urban trips.
The eVTOL aircraft is built with redundant, fault-tolerant systems, including 12 total engines and propellers.
Archer targets certification with the FAA at similar levels of safety as commercial airliners.
The Midnight is currently being produced on a small scale at Archer’s plants in California and Georgia.
The upcoming eVTOL will operate from vertiports at key Olympic locations, such as SoFi Stadium, LAX, and Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum.
Adam Goldstein, Archer’s CEO and founder, stated the goal is to transform how people get around Los Angeles and leave a legacy shaping the future of transportation in America during the LA28 Games.
Goldstein expressed excitement about seeing Midnight flying passengers over Los Angeles with the Team USA logo and Olympic/Paralympic rings.
Vertiports are to be set up at key venues including a stadium in Inglewood, the LA Memorial Coliseum on the USC campus, LAX Airport, Hollywood Burbank, Orange County, and Santa Monica visitor hubs.
The partnership with the Olympic committee and Team USA makes Archer a front-runner in implementing air taxis as public service.
The collaboration provides a significant marketing boost for the eVTOL industry and helps normalize flying taxis in urban settings.
The initiative aligns with LA's push for green transit and the Olympics' spotlight on innovation, offering clean, futuristic transport.
The 2028 Olympic Games are expected to welcome over 15 million visitors, some of whom may experience the first-ever air taxi rides in the US.
Archer Aviation hopes to receive FAA certification to offer commercial flights en masse.
Establishing large-scale vertiport infrastructure across LA over the coming years is a critical milestone.
It remains uncertain if the public will trust and use the technology.
The company announced international partnerships with Japan and the United Arab Emirates to provide zero-emission air taxi networks.
Casey Wasserman, LA28 Chairperson and President, stated Archer was partnered with for innovation and creativity, aiming to reimagine the Olympic experience.
source_title: US firm's air taxi set to fly at 2028 LA Olympics, help dodge traffic

facts: Archer Aviation, Inc. is a California-based aerospace company developing electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft designed to serve as sustainable urban air mobility solutions.
The company was founded in 2018 by Adam Goldstein and Brett Adcock.
Archer focuses on the design, development and certification of zero-emissions air taxis aimed at reducing traffic congestion in densely populated metropolitan areas.
The company's flagship prototypes are named “Maker” and “Midnight.”
The prototypes have been engineered to deliver quiet, efficient short-haul flights with ranges of up to 100 miles per charge.
Archer is headquartered in Palo Alto, California.
The company operates a manufacturing facility in nearby Santa Cruz County.
Archer maintains research partnerships with automotive and energy companies.
Archer has a collaboration with Stellantis to integrate advanced battery systems.
source_title: Archer Aviation (NYSE:ACHR)  Shares Down 3.3%   - Here's Why

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. has received an additional $55 million investment from Stellantis N.V. under the companies’ strategic funding agreement following the achievement of its transition flight test milestone last month.
This latest investment builds on Stellantis’ series of open market purchases of 8.3 million shares of Archer’s stock in March of this year that was previously announced.
During 2023, Stellantis invested $110 million into Archer through a combination of open market stock purchases and investments under the companies’ strategic funding agreement.
Archer remains on track to complete construction of its high-volume manufacturing facility in Georgia later this year.
The first phase of the build out is a ~350,000 square foot facility on an ~100 acre site designed to support production of up to 650 aircraft annually.
The facility would make it one of the largest manufacturing facilities by volume in the aircraft industry.
Archer’s goal with this facility remains to establish a factory that can support its planned commercial ramp by leveraging the expertise of Stellantis as its contract manufacturer.

Carlos Tavares, Stellantis CEO, stated: “Few things rival the excitement of seeing a dream take flight. I applaud the innovation, expertise and hard work of the engineering and manufacturing teams from Stellantis and Archer.”

Adam Goldstein, Archer CEO, stated: “The commitment by Stellantis to Archer has been unrivaled, from its foresight to provide the manufacturing expertise and capital needed to accelerate Archer’s business objectives, to the strategic vision and steadfast support from Stellantis CEO Carlos Tavares.”
Stellantis has been a strategic partner to Archer since 2020 through various collaboration initiatives, and as an investor since 2021.
During this time, Archer has leveraged Stellantis’ deep manufacturing, supply chain, and design expertise in connection with its efforts to design, develop, and commercialize its eVTOL aircraft.

Archer’s goal is to transform urban travel, replacing 60–90-minute commutes by car with estimated 10–20-minute electric air taxi flights that are safe, sustainable, low noise, and cost-competitive with ground transportation.
Archer’s Midnight is a piloted, four-passenger aircraft designed to perform rapid back-to-back flights with minimal charge time between flights.
source_title: Stellantis Invests Additional $55M into Archer Following Recent Flight Test Milestone – Drones World Magazine

facts: Archer Aviation announced it will invest $118 million to construct a plant near Atlanta, Georgia.
The company plans to eventually hire up to 1,000 people for the facility.
The aircraft plant will be built adjoining an airport in Covington, Georgia.
Archer Aviation is developing a battery-powered vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) craft with six propellers that holds four passengers and a pilot.
The eVTOL craft is designed for short flights, particularly in urban areas.
United Airlines announced it would fly the craft from downtown Manhattan to Newark Liberty International Airport in New Jersey.
The United Airlines route would cut a trip that takes an hour by car in congested traffic down to 10 minutes.
The U.S. Air Force is evaluating Archer's aircraft for possible use.
Archer Aviation is currently seeking Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) certification for its aircraft.
The company states it could receive FAA certification by 2024.
Archer aims to produce 650 aircraft annually at the Georgia site initially.
Annual production capacity at the Georgia site has the potential to rise to 2,300 aircraft per year.
The company expects to receive an incentive package worth some $40 million from Georgia and local governments.
The incentive package includes the donation of 96 acres of land at Covington Municipal Airport.
The incentive package also includes a cash grant from the state and other tax incentives.
Archer chose Georgia due to the availability of local workers, the ability to conduct test flights, affordable construction costs, and good highway, rail, and air connections.

Archer CEO Adam Goldstein stated that their eVTOL technology can transform how urban and rural communities live and commute, and the factory can create pathways to highly skilled manufacturing hubs and other ladders of social and economic opportunity.
Georgia Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that economic recruiters specializing in aerospace had worked with Archer.
Pat Wilson stated that technology and innovation continue to drive change across strategic industries.
source_title: Electric air taxi company Archer to build Georgia factory, hire 1,000

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. announced on December 19, 2024, that it has received the certificate of occupancy for its high-volume manufacturing facility, ARC.
The ARC facility is located at the Covington Municipal Airport in Georgia.
Stellantis and Archer are finalizing an agreement in which Stellantis would work with Archer as its exclusive contract manufacturer to mass produce Archer’s Midnight eVTOL aircraft at this facility.
Stellantis plans to contribute capital, advanced manufacturing technology, expertise and experienced personnel, with a goal of scaling the facility to 650 aircraft annually by 2030.
Archer’s team is focused on the start of production planned for early next year following the completion of construction.
Adam Goldstein, Archer’s founder and CEO, stated that construction was completed fast and on budget and expressed pride in the Archer team and partners.
A ribbon-cutting ceremony was held to celebrate the completion of construction on the ARC facility.

The ribbon-cutting ceremony was attended by Covington Mayor Fleeta Baggett, State Representative Tim Fleming, Newton County Development President Serra Hall, Newton County Chamber President Debbie Harper, Covington City Manager Tres Thomas, Newton County Commissioner Lanier Sims, and Archer’s Vice President of Operations, Glen Burks.
The facility is creating valuable jobs for local residents and contributing to the local economy.

Stellantis Chief Manufacturing and Supply Chain Officer Arnaud Deboeuf stated that supporting Archer’s electric aircraft growth with deep manufacturing expertise is a great opportunity for both companies.

On December 12, 2024, Anduril Industries Inc. and Archer Aviation Inc. announced an exclusive agreement to jointly develop a next-generation hybrid-propulsion, vertical-take-off-and-landing (VTOL) aircraft for defense applications.
The first product of the Anduril-Archer partnership is planned to target a potential program of record from the United States Department of Defense (DOD).
Archer raised $430 million in additional equity capital to support the development of its defense initiatives and other corporate purposes.

The $430 million funding includes participation from existing strategic partners Stellantis and United Airlines, along with new institutional investors Wellington Management and 2PointZero, a subsidiary of the UAE’s largest listed entity IHC.
In August, the U.S. Department of Defense (DOD) accepted the military airworthiness assessment of Archer’s Midnight aircraft.
Archer Aviation delivered its first Midnight aircraft to the US Air Force (USAF) to evaluate as part of its AFWERX Agility Prime contract valued at up to $142M.
Archer’s Midnight is an all-electric eVTOL aircraft designed for urban air mobility applications, such as air taxis.
The Midnight has a passenger capacity of four and a range of approximately 100 miles on a single charge.
source_title: Archer Completes Construction of High-Volume eVTOL Manufacturing Facility in GA - EVinfo.net

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. announced plans to locate its manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia at a site adjacent to the Covington Municipal Airport.
The company plans to initially build out a 350,000 square-foot facility on a 96-acre site.
The planned facility will be capable of producing up to 650 aircraft per year.
The manufacturing facility is expected to create over 1,000 jobs over the long term.
Construction is expected to be completed in the first half of 2024.
Initial production is expected to begin in the second half of 2024.
The facility will be capable of being expanded by an additional 550,000 square feet.
The expansion is estimated to support production of up to 2,300 aircraft per year.
Archer completed a comprehensive survey of potential sites across the United States to select the location for its first manufacturing facility.
Key site selection considerations included local labor market talent availability, utility availability, ability to conduct seamless flight test operations, construction costs, and logistics.
Archer will receive an incentive package that includes land conveyance, tax incentives, and a Georgia REBA grant.
The company anticipates that financing for the project will be provided by Synovus, the largest bank headquartered in Georgia.

CEO Adam Goldstein stated that Georgia played a vital role in the U.S. aerospace industry and that the factory can create pathways to highly skilled manufacturing jobs and other ladders of social and economic opportunity.
Governor Brian P. Kemp commented that Georgia's established leadership in the aerospace industry was essential to landing Archer Aviation in Newton County.
Governor Kemp noted that Archer can take advantage of Georgia's signature workforce training program Quick Start to help fill over 1,000 jobs in Covington long-term.
Georgia is home to more than 800 aerospace companies and maintains a robust aerospace education pipeline.

The state's education pipeline includes a dozen high schools offering training in the field, five technical colleges with aviation programs, and several universities offering degrees in aerospace engineering.

The Georgia Center of Innovation for Aerospace, a strategic arm of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, has been cultivating an ecosystem for innovative companies like Archer for more than 10 years.
Archer is designing and developing electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft for use in urban air mobility networks.
source_title: ACHR Press Release: Archer Aviation Selects Site in Georgia Adjac...

facts: Ben Hill County Board of Commissioners unanimously approved rezoning 189 Ed Ward Rd. from General Farming (GF) to Wholesale Light Industrial (WLI).
The rezoning is intended to allow the expansion of Arising Industries, Inc.
The approval followed a recommendation from the Planning and Zoning Commission.
A supporting public comment was provided prior to the vote.
Building and Zoning Administrator Jake Hughes stated that the Planning and Zoning Commission had made the recommendation.
The Ben Hill County Board of Commissioners voted on Oct. 7, 2025, to rezone the property from GF (General Farming) to WLI (Wholesale Light Industrial).
source_title: Ben Hill County approves rezoning of 189 Ed Ward Rd. for Arising Industries expansion | Citizen Portal

facts: Koch Industries Inc. is a private company with annual revenues of more than $125 billion.
Koch Industries paid $7.2 billion to acquire Molex in Sept. 2013.
Molex creates electronics to solve technical problems in electric cars.
Koch Industries completed its full acquisition of Guardian Industries in February 2017.
Guardian Industries manufactures glass for automobiles.
Georgia-Pacific is based in Atlanta, Georgia.
Koch companies collectively employ over 120,000 people in over 50 countries, with half located in the United States.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Aurubis will invest $ 340 million (€ 300 million).
The investment is for a state-of-the-art recycling and secondary smelting facility.
The facility will focus on multi-metal recycling.
The facility is located in Augusta.
source_title: Aurubis to Open Recycling Facility in Georgia

facts: Aurubis AG is Europe's largest copper producer.
Aurubis is making a transformative $800 million investment to establish a state-of-the-art copper recycling facility in Richmond, Georgia.
The facility is scheduled to commence operations in 2025 after a four-year construction period.
This will be the first large-scale copper recycling plant in the region.
The facility has an annual processing capacity of 180,000 metric tons of complex copper scrap.
The facility will produce 70,000 tons of refined copper annually.
The U.S. exported 960,000 tons of copper scrap in 2024.
41% of the 2024 U.S. copper scrap exports were shipped to China.
Aurubis is positioning itself to capitalize on demand drivers like data center expansion and AI infrastructure, which require rapid copper component turnover every 3–5 years.
Primary mining accounts for approximately 60% of global copper production.
Georgia's location taps into the southeastern U.S.'s robust automotive and telecommunications sectors, which generate substantial copper scrap volumes.
CEO Toralf Haag emphasized the facility's role in closing the loop for North American manufacturers.
The facility enables manufacturers to reintegrate recycled copper into production cycles without dependency on international exporters.
The plant processes complex copper scrap, including mixed-material waste from electronics and industrial equipment that traditional recyclers often deem uneconomical.
Aurubis can recover copper with 99.9% purity, matching the quality of newly mined metal.
The facility simultaneously extracts precious metals like gold and silver from byproducts.
With a 70,000-ton annual output, the facility could meet nearly 5% of U.S. refined copper demand.
This production capacity could reduce import reliance by an estimated 15% over the next decade.
Over 80% of U.S. copper scrap is historically processed by small-scale regional operators lacking the capacity to handle complex materials.
Aurubis introduces vertically integrated operations that streamline collection, sorting, and refining.
CEO Toralf Haag noted North America's export-centric scrap economy represents a $12 billion opportunity for localized value addition.
The company projects a 20% reduction in logistics costs for domestic manufacturers by shortening supply chains previously dependent on Asian processors.
The Trump administration launched a 2025 investigation into copper import tariffs, including scrap.
Potential imported refined copper tariffs could face 10–25% duties.
Industry analysts estimate tariffs could raise U.S. copper prices by 8–12%.
China imported 393,600 tons of U.S. copper scrap in 2024, representing 41% of total exports.
Forward contracts have already secured 65% of the plant's initial feedstock from automotive and telecom sectors.
Processing 180,000 tons of scrap annually could prevent 500,000 metric tons of CO2 emissions compared to primary copper production.
This emission reduction is equivalent to removing 108,000 gasoline-powered vehicles from roads.
Copper recycling requires 85% less energy than virgin metal production.
The facility potentially qualifies Aurubis for federal clean manufacturing tax credits under the Inflation Reduction Act.
Data centers are projected to consume 1.2 million tons of copper annually by 2030.
Data centers will become a major scrap source due to 3–5 year hardware refresh cycles.
The company is developing proprietary sorting systems to handle server racks and cooling systems.
Aurubis targets a 95% copper recovery rate from the data center waste stream.
Each AI data center rack requires up to 5,000 pounds of copper for power and cooling systems.
Tech giants are investing $200 billion in U.S. data center construction through 2030.
The development follows Rio Tinto's copper shift toward supporting clean energy infrastructure.
Aurubis has identified three additional U.S. sites for potential expansion, targeting regions with high-tech manufacturing clusters in Texas and the Pacific Northwest.
Modular plant design allows capacity scaling up to 300,000 tons annually, contingent on scrap availability and policy support.
Copper maintains 100% of its physical and chemical properties through the recycling process.
Primary ore grades globally continue to decline below 0.5% copper content.
Recycling avoids the $10,000-15,000 per ton capital expenditure associated with new mine development.
Recycling eliminates the 170 tons of mining waste typically generated for each ton of virgin copper.
Recycling uses just 15% of the water required for conventional extraction and refining.
The Richmond facility incorporates X-ray fluorescence sorting, eddy current separation, and AI-driven optical sorting.
These sorting systems can identify and separate copper alloys with up to 99% accuracy.
The facility employs advanced hydrometallurgical processes using biological leaching agents rather than harsh chemicals.
Emerging extended producer responsibility (EPR) legislation in several states will require manufacturers to finance end-of-life management for electronic products.
EPR regulations are expected to increase domestic copper scrap availability by 25-30% by 2030.
SEC's proposed climate disclosure rules may incentivize manufacturers to source recycled copper, which carries approximately 65% lower embedded carbon than primary metal.
As reported by Reuters, this trend aligns with the broader mining and clean energy transition taking place globally.
source_title: Aurubis to Ramp Up New US Copper Recycling Facility

facts: Aurubis AG and SMS group GmbH signed a supply contract for a second plant module at the new Aurubis Richmond recycling plant in Georgia as part of their strategic partnership.
The additional equipment will increase the capacity of the metal recycling plant in Richmond from an annual 90,000 tons to 180,000 tons of complex metal scrap materials.

The core technology for the second module is the top-blown rotary converter (TBRC), which processes complex recycling materials to recover copper, nickel, tin, precious metals, and platinum group metals.
These recovered metals are crucial raw materials for technologies related to the energy transition, sustainable mobility, or digitalization.
Aurubis CEO Roland Harings stated that the expansion delivers on the company's 'Driving Sustainable Growth' strategy while keeping strategically important mineral raw materials in the resource cycle.
Michael Rzepczyk, a member of the SMS Executive Board, noted that the planned doubling of production volume at the new US site takes recycling to the next level in the country.
The recycling plant utilizes a modular system that allows additional components to be added later to expand capacity and respond flexibly to market needs.
Aurubis is investing about €640 million for both phases of the project to build the first secondary smelter specializing in multimetal recycling in the US.
Groundbreaking for the Aurubis Richmond plant took place in mid-2022, with construction currently running at full steam.
Stage one of the plant is scheduled to go online in the first half of 2024.
The second stage, equipped with the newly contracted TBRC, is anticipated to start production in 2026.
The investment in Georgia will comply with the highest environmental standards in the US and create around 200 new jobs.
Aurubis AG is a leading global provider of non-ferrous metals and one of the largest copper recyclers worldwide, processing roughly 1 million tons of recycling material annually.

Aurubis produces more than 1 million tons of copper cathodes annually, along with wire rod, continuous cast shapes, profiles, flat rolled products, precious metals, selenium, lead, nickel, tin, zinc, sulfuric acid, and iron silicate.
Aurubis has around 6,900 employees, production sites in Europe and the USA, and its shares are listed on the German Stock Exchange (MDAX, GCX, STOXX Europe 600).
SMS group GmbH is a Düsseldorf-based plant and machine manufacturer with worldwide sales of around 2.6 billion euros and 14,500 employees globally.
source_title: Aurubis and SMS group forge ahead with the first multimetal recycling plant in the US - SMS group GmbH

facts: Aurubis AG will invest around US$700 million to build the first secondary smelter for complex recycling materials in North America.
Commissioning of the secondary smelter in Augusta, Georgia is planned for the second half of the coming year.
The company noted that first Lady of the United States visited Aurubis Richmond, the first secondary smelter for complex recycling materials in North America.
An expansion of the Aurubis Richmond plant is already under construction.
The plant expansion will allow Aurubis Richmond to process a total of 180,000 tons of complex recycling materials per year as of 2026.
Complex recycling materials processed at the facility include printed circuit boards and insulated copper cable.
Aurubis is creating long-term, secure jobs for more than 200 people by building this state-of-the-art recycling plant.
Aurubis has pledged a community benefit agreement with the Technical Colleges in Augusta and Aiken (South Carolina) as well as with the Richmond County School System.
As part of the agreement, Aurubis will invest more than US$200,000 in scholarships and job training programs for young workers over the next five years.
The company actively promotes women in the metal industry through its international Women4Metals initiative.

links.context: Contact email for comments and feedback

source_title: Aurubis To Invest Around $700 Mln To Build Smelter For Complex Recycling Materials In U.S.

facts: The Supervisory Board of Aurubis AG has resolved further capital expenditure of about € 530 million.
The investment will be used to double the processing capacity at the Aurubis Richmond recycling site in Georgia, US, and for infrastructure adjustments to the current project.
Currently, an Aurubis plant is under construction in Richmond, Georgia.
About € 90 million in additional capital expenditure adjustments for infrastructure requirements and inflation have been approved for this plant.
Further, the plant will receive a second recycling module with a capital expenditure volume of about € 250 million.
This expansion stage is scheduled to start production in 2026.
The previous throughput volume of 90,000 tons of complex recycling materials will be raised to 180,000 tons due to the doubling of the expansion stage.
Aurubis is creating about 80 new jobs in Richmond.
The site will employ about 200 people after the completion of both recycling modules.
Starting in the fiscal year of 2025/26, Aurubis expects the expansion stage to make an additional EBITDA contribution of about € 90 million per year.
Combined with the second expansion stage, Aurubis expects an EBITDA contribution of about € 170 million from the fiscal year of 2026/27 with a total investment volume of € 640 million.
The Richmond plant will profit from low electricity and gas prices.
Construction work for the first construction stage has already started at the Richmond site.
The expansion follows the highest environmental standards and enables environmentally friendly processing of the input materials.
Roland Harings, Chairman of the Aurubis Executive Board, noted that Aurubis is accelerating growth in recycling with the next expansion stage of its US plant.
Aurubis plans to finance the projects largely from current cash flow.
source_title: Aurubis Invests € 530 Million --  Trade Fair

facts: Aurubis AG is a leading global provider of non-ferrous metals and one of the largest copper recyclers worldwide.
Aurubis inaugurated a globally unique plant at its Hamburg site on July 3, 2026.
The new facility is named Complex Recycling Hamburg (CRH).
The CRH facility integrates the capabilities of multiple smelting processes into a single unit.
For the first time, raw materials containing copper, lead and sulfur can be processed in a single plant at this facility.
Production was officially kicked off after around two-and-a-half years of construction.
The project involved an investment of €190 million.
The €190 million investment is expected to deliver a considerable positive financial contribution once fully ramped up.
CRH is one of the most significant growth projects under Aurubis’ current strategy, “Aurubis Performance 2030: Forging resilience. Leading in multimetal.”
The new facility enables Aurubis to process attractive recycling materials for which there are only a limited number of buyers worldwide.
CRH will allow the company to treat more than 30,000 additional tons of recycling material per year.
CRH will also process significantly larger volumes of high-value smelter intermediates with exceptional efficiency.
The facility enhances Aurubis’ ability to recover important metals, such as copper, lead and precious metals.
The facility produces sulfuric acid, which is indispensable for key industrial applications.

The objective of the process innovation is to keep more materials and value within the Aurubis smelter network, optimizing the utilization of existing assets and further enhancing production resilience.
The new plant strengthens Aurubis’ business model with its diversified earnings drivers.
At the heart of the plant is a specially engineered converter that separates the individual elements.
The unit processes batches of around 45 tons at temperatures of up to 1,400 degrees Celsius.
CRH is equipped with cutting-edge technology and sets a new benchmark in efficient, environmentally friendly multimetal production.
Around a third of total investment went towards air pollution control measures, underscoring the plant’s high environmental standards.
The facility is highly automated, enabling very efficient staffing levels while ensuring best-in-class health and safety standards.
Aurubis COO Tim Kurth stated that global demand for critical raw materials like copper is growing rapidly.
Tim Kurth emphasized the need for better access to recycling materials and more innovation in complex material processing.
The CRH project received funding from the European Union.
The project directly contributes to advancing the EU Critical Raw Materials Act.
Project financing was backed by the first investment loan from the European Investment Bank (EIB) under its comprehensive raw materials strategy.

EIB Vice President Nicola Beer stated that securing critical raw materials such as copper and expanding recycling capacities in Europe are central to resilience, sustainable development, and Europe’s industrial competitiveness.
The EIB loan to Aurubis reinforces European value chains and shows quick and decisive action in alignment with the Critical Raw Materials Act.
Projects like CRH support an increase in the processing of complex materials in Europe.

Stefan Rouenhoff, Parliamentary State Secretary at the German Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Energy, said securing critical raw materials is one of the key economic policy challenges of our time.

Dr. Christopher Schwieger, State Councilor of the Hamburg Authority for Economic Affairs, Labor and Innovation, noted that Aurubis continues to invest in industrial hub Hamburg and that Complex Recycling Hamburg showcases how modern industrial production, innovation and sustainability can successfully work in tandem.
The investment is important for the city’s economic future and for securing high-quality jobs.
source_title: Strengthening Europe’s raw materials security: Aurubis inaugurates globally one-of-a-kind recycling plant for strategic metals

facts: Aurubis is building the country's first plant for complex recycling materials in the US state of Georgia.
The Georgia facility will have a processing capacity of 180,000 tons of input material per year.
Aurubis AG is a leading global provider of non-ferrous metals and one of the largest copper recyclers worldwide.
The company processes complex metal concentrates, scrap metals, organic and inorganic metal-bearing recycling materials, and industrial residues into high-quality metals.
Aurubis produces more than 1 million tons of copper cathodes annually.
From its copper cathodes, the company manufactures wire rod, continuous cast shapes, profiles, and flat rolled products made of copper and copper alloys.
Aurubis also produces precious metals, selenium, lead, nickel, tin, and zinc.
The company's product portfolio includes sulfuric acid and iron silicate.
The recycled content in each of Aurubis's copper cathodes is almost 45 percent.
The company processes more than one million tons of recycling material annually.
Aurubis aims to achieve carbon-neutral production well before 2050.
Since 2000, Aurubis has invested more than €780 million in environmental and climate protection, including €47 million in 2021 alone.
In 2019, the company pledged to develop science-based CO2 reduction targets to help limit global warming.
CEO Roland Harings stated that Aurubis views sustainability as an essential part of its conduct and business activities, committing to responsible handling of natural resources.
The company employs approximately 6,900 people and operates production sites in Europe and the USA.
Aurubis's sustainability reporting aligns with Global Reporting Initiative (GRI) standards and the voluntary Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosure (TCFD).
An external audit for the report was conducted to the exacting standards of "The Copper Mark," an industry initiative verifying copper production site sustainability.
source_title: Aurubis AG: Enabling Tomorrow: Aurubis Releases New Sustainability Report

facts: Aurubis AG celebrated the official ribbon-cutting ceremony for its new multimetal recycling facility in Augusta, Georgia, on September 20, 2024.
The facility, named Aurubis Richmond, is the first-ever secondary smelter of its kind for multimetal recycling in the United States.
Construction of the plant required a total investment of around $800 million.
Groundbreaking occurred in June 2022, followed by a strategic decision at the end of 2022 to fast-track the expansion.
Following over two years of construction, the state-of-the-art facility is now operational.
The plant has a capacity to process up to 180,000 tons of complex recycling materials annually.
Recycled materials will include circuit boards, copper cables, and other metal-containing products.
Aurubis Richmond will provide over 230 high-quality jobs to the greater Augusta area.
The company pledged a community benefit agreement with Augusta Technical College, Aiken Technical College, and the Richmond County School System.
As part of the agreement, Aurubis Richmond will invest in scholarships and job training programs for young workers.
Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp attended the ribbon-cutting ceremony to celebrate the facility's economic impact and partnership with the state.
Aurubis CEO Toralf Haag stated that the plant will keep strategically important metals in the U.S. economy, making American supply chains more independent.
David Schultheis, President and Managing Director of Aurubis Richmond, described the facility as a major player in the North American multimetal supply chain.
The new Richmond facility is equipped with advanced technology that meets environmental standards set by both the State of Georgia and federal regulators.
Aurubis aims to be carbon-neutral well before 2050, aligning the plant's operations with its sustainability mission.
The recycled metals will supply essential materials to key industries including renewable energy, electric vehicles, and battery manufacturing.
German-based Aurubis AG is a leading global provider of non-ferrous metals and one of the largest copper recyclers worldwide for over 150 years.

Aurubis produces more than 1 million tons of copper cathodes annually, along with wire rod, continuous cast shapes, profiles, flat rolled products, precious metals, selenium, lead, nickel, tin, and zinc.

facts: Aurubis AG and SMS group GmbH signed a supply contract for a second plant module at the new Aurubis Richmond recycling plant in Georgia.
The signing occurred last Friday relative to March 27, 2023, as part of a strategic partnership in the global multimetal recycling sector.
A modular system design allows for flexible operations and quick growth in the dynamic US recycling market.
Aurubis is considerably accelerating its growth course in the future recycling and circular economy market.
The additional equipment will increase the capacity of the metal recycling plant in Richmond from an annual 90,000 tons to 180,000 tons of complex metal scrap materials.
The Richmond plant is currently under construction.

Aurubis CEO Roland Harings stated the expansion delivers on the company's 'Driving Sustainable Growth' strategy while keeping strategically important and high-demand mineral raw materials in the resource cycle.
SMS group recycling equipment will be installed in the second module of the new US plant.
The core technology for the second module is the top-blown rotary converter (TBRC).
TBRC is state-of-the-art equipment used for processing complex recycling materials to recover copper, nickel, tin, precious metals, and platinum group metals in downstream stages.
Recovered metals are described as crucial raw materials for energy transition, sustainable mobility, and digitalization technologies.
Aurubis CEO Roland Harings noted that bundling SMS group's plant know-how with Aurubis' expertise creates an environment for highly efficient metal recovery.

Michael Rzepczyk, a member of the SMS Executive Board, stated the partnership helps Aurubis assume a leading role in multimetal recycling in the US and takes recycling to the next level by doubling production volume.
The modular technology allows additional components to be added later to expand the plant to meet future requirements flexibly.
Aurubis is investing about €640 million for both phases of the project to build the first secondary smelter specializing in multimetal recycling in the US.
Groundbreaking for the Aurubis Richmond plant took place in mid-2022.
Stage one of the plant is scheduled to go online in the first half of 2024.
The TBRC for the second stage is anticipated to start production in 2026.
Aurubis currently processes roughly 1 million tons of recycling material annually globally, and the Richmond plant will add another 180,000 tons.
The new Georgia plant will comply with the highest environmental standards in the US.
The project is expected to create around 200 new jobs.

facts: Aurubis Rolled Products is a division of Aurubis.
The division produces over 200,000 metric tons of copper and brass foils, strips, sheets and plates annually.
The company operates 5 production sites in Europe and the US.
Products include an extensive range of copper and alloy grades.
Specific alloys produced include oxygen-free copper, brass, and copper-tin alloys.
The document details the company's technologies, industries served, and customer service offerings.
