facts: The Supervisory Board of Aurubis AG has resolved further capital expenditure of about € 530 million.
Funds will be used to double the processing capacity at the Aurubis Richmond recycling site in Georgia, US.
Funds will also cover infrastructure adjustments for the current project.
Aurubis is investing in the CRH (Complex Recycling Hamburg) project in Hamburg to increase recycling capacity.
Aurubis is investing in an expansion of the solar park that generates electricity for the company at the Pirdop site in Bulgaria.
The returns from these three projects result in an expected earnings contribution (EBIDTDA) of about € 130 million.
Aurubis decided to carry out a major IT modernization project involving the switch to the SAP S/4HANA platform.
Roland Harings, Chairman of the Aurubis Executive Board, notes these resolutions show consistent implementation of strategy 'Driving Sustainable Growth'.
Aurubis is accelerating growth in recycling with the next expansion stage of its US plant.
Aurubis is strengthening its core business by investing in innovative metallurgy in Hamburg to process complex secondary materials more efficiently.
Aurubis is expanding industry leadership in sustainable multi-metal production by generating its own power using renewable energies in Bulgaria.
With the introduction of S/4HANA, Aurubis is creating key conditions for automating its entire smelter network even more, leveraging efficiencies and growing further.
Aurubis will significantly increase its growth in the US.
Currently, an Aurubis plant is under construction in Richmond, Georgia.
About € 90 million in additional capital expenditure adjustments for infrastructure requirements and inflation have been approved for this plant.
The plant will receive a second recycling module with a capital expenditure volume of about € 250 million.
This expansion stage is scheduled to start production in 2026.
The rapidly growing availability of complex recycling materials (which lies at more than 5 percent per year) can be explained by the ongoing recycling boom in the USA.
It creates favorable conditions for expanding Aurubis' market position in the US.
The previous throughput volume of 90,000 tons of complex recycling materials will be raised to 180,000 tons due to the doubling of the expansion stage.
The expansion follows the highest environmental standards and enables environmentally friendly processing of the input materials.
Aurubis is creating about 80 new jobs in Richmond.
Aurubis will employ about 200 people at the site after the completion of both recycling modules.
The US investments will be highly profitable due to high synergies, low requirements of additional space and the use of existing infrastructure.
Starting in the fiscal year of 2025/26, Aurubis expects the expansion stage to make an additional EBITDA contribution of about € 90 million per year.
In addition to many other locational benefits, the Richmond plant will also profit from low electricity and gas prices.
Construction work for the first construction stage has already started at the Richmond site.
Combined with the second expansion stage, Aurubis expects an EBITDA contribution of about € 170 million from the fiscal year of 2026/27 with a total investment volume of € 640 million.
To secure the main business, further investments of about € 190 million in the Complex Recycling Hamburg (CRH) project were approved.
In the future, Aurubis will be able to process about 30,000 tons of additional recycling material and internal complex smelter intermediates there.
The company contributes to the circular economy by closing internal and external material cycles.

The process developed specifically for CRH optimizes the utilization of existing facilities and expands the metallurgical capabilities in the Aurubis smelter network, thus differentiating the company from its competitors.
In addition, CRH enables improved precious metal processing with shorter process times.
Heiko Arnold, COO of Aurubis, says the CRH project keeps considerably more added value in-house.
The project also impressively demonstrates their research and development expertise.
No other project integrates into their smelter network so strongly and optimizes so many value chains at the same time.
CRH is the basis for further, important strategic projects at the plant and a clear commitment to the Hamburg site.
The company expects the project to make an annual contribution to earnings of around €40 million once it starts operations in 2025.
The expansion of the already existing photovoltaic plant at the Pirdop site makes up the third project.
The output of the plant there will be increased by 14 MWp to 24 MWp through investments of around € 12 million.
In addition, solar panels worth € 8 million will be purchased for further expansion of the solar park.
The expansion includes the use of stationary, double-sided modules, characterized by higher productivity than conventional V-modules.
The plant is scheduled to start operations in 2024.
Aurubis is thus expanding the country's yet largest solar park to meet its own demands.
After the second expansion stage, the park's output will correspond to the power demand of 9,000 households.
Compared to coal-fired energy generation, the park will save 34,000 tons of CO2 each year.
This adds up to 500,000 tons for the projected operating period of 15 years.
Aurubis' solar park thus constitutes an important building block for further decarbonization in Bulgaria.
The project also contributes to Aurubis' sustainability targets, which include reducing Scope 1 and 2 emissions by 50 percent until 2030.
A capital increase for the planned projects will not be needed, as Aurubis plans to finance them largely from current cash flow.
In the future, the chosen course for growth will increasingly be supported by a flexible and anticipatory dividend distribution.
Aurubis will simultaneously ensure appropriate shareholder participation.
At € 1.80 per share (prev. year €1.60), the Supervisory and Executive Board are proposing the highest dividend in the company's history.
This is the result of the solid 2021/22 earnings (operating EBT) of € 532 million (prev. year adjusted €381 million).
The company expects operating earnings before taxes of € 400 million to € 500 million in the current fiscal year 2022/23.
A detailed commentary on the fiscal results of the year was published in the Annual Report 2021/22 on December 21, 2022.
source_title: Aurubis Invests € 530 Million --  Trade Fair

facts: A Sept. 20 ribbon-cutting ceremony marked the start of operations at the Aurubis Richmond, LLC plant in south Augusta.
Gov. Brian Kemp (R) attended the Friday event.
The event was held just over a week after Area Development Magazine named Georgia the top state to do business for the 11th year in a row.
Kemp stated that even though the national and world economies are ebbing and flowing, Georgia continues to have record economic development.
Kemp said Georgia's pipeline is very good and the state is excited and bullish about the future.
Kemp emphasized working hard on workforce despite the labor market being softened and changed a little bit.
Cal Wray, President of the Augusta Economic Development Authority (AEDA), echoed the Governor’s comments.
Aurubis is a multi-billion dollar international company that chose Augusta for its first U.S. facility.
Wray said Aurubis's presence shows Augusta as a place you can do business and trust.
Aurubis completed everything they promised in two years, including quick construction, quick hiring, and quick partnership with local institutions.
Aurubis partnered on workforce development with Augusta Tech and Augusta University.
Georgia ranked among the best states for access to qualified labor.
Area Development Magazine pointed to Georgia’s Quick Start program, coordinated by the Technical College System of Georgia (TCSG), which can customize workforce training for a specific company.
Kemp cited a program started last year to streamline how high school students apply for post-secondary education.
Kemp said they rolled out the Georgia MATCH program last year, resulting in technical college enrollment up almost 10% and university system enrollment over 6%.
Each year, the Georgia MATCH program sends a letter to high school students listing the TCSG and University System of Georgia (USG) institutions into which the student is eligible to enroll.
Kemp and Wray said existing businesses like Aurubis, Gulfstream in Savannah, and Kia in west Georgia serve as ambassadors to help sell the Peach State to other companies.
Potential businesses can talk to operating companies about workforce efforts, business environment, and local/state partnerships.
Wray said Aurubis is one of GF Casting's biggest cheerleaders.

When GF Casting was announced earlier this year, it created a unique challenge for AEDA because the company will use 147 acres in the Augusta Corporate Park off Mike Padgett Highway, which just about fills the 1,800-acre park to capacity.
Only two real sites remain in the park: a 65-acre and a 115-acre site.
AEDA has a few small sites up Tobacco Road and Doug Barnard.
AEDA identified a 1,000-acre site that they are doing due diligence on for one user.
AEDA is also evaluating other 50-acre to 200-acre sites to have other options.
GF Casting Solutions AG, a division of Georg Fischer AG in Schaffhausen, develops and produces parts for multiple industries including light vehicles, trucks, and aerospace.
Site preparation is underway directly across from the Aurubis facility for the $148 million GF Casting plant.
The GF Casting plant will eventually create 350 jobs.
Wray said the site has been cleared and graded, with foundations expected to go in after city permitting, and a building ready to accept equipment by the second quarter of next year.
AEDA maintains a list of developed and undeveloped sites available on its website under the “Site Selection” tab.
source_title: Continued economic development in Georgia and metro Augusta - Augusta Business Daily

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Aurubis will invest $340 million (€300 million) in a state-of-the-art recycling and secondary smelting facility for multi-metal recycling in Augusta.
Aurubis is one of the leading recyclers of copper, precious metals, and non-ferrous materials worldwide.
This new facility will be the very first of its kind in the U.S.
The project will create 125 jobs in the Augusta area.
Governor Kemp stated this marks the single-largest investment made by a German company in the State of Georgia.
Aurubis will join more than 500 German companies with operations in Georgia.
Aurubis’ commitment to sustainable development and raw material security will play a role in building Georgia’s electric mobility value chain.
From Augusta, the company plans to produce materials found in lithium-ion batteries and other electronics.
Roland Harings is the CEO of Aurubis AG.
The company aims to become a forerunner for multi-metal recycling in the U.S., mirroring its existing role in Europe.
Aurubis will build its facility on 150 acres at Augusta Corporate Park.
Augusta Corporate Park is a “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified” site.
Operations are expected to begin by the first half of 2024.
The company plans to begin hiring for positions as soon as summer 2022.
Steven Kendrick is Chairman of the Development Authority of Augusta.
The Augusta Economic Development Authority and City of Augusta welcome Aurubis as their newest corporate tenant.
Griff Lynch is the Executive Director of the Georgia Ports Authority (GPA).
Exports produced by the Aurubis recycling operation will strengthen the state’s global position via Savannah, which is already one of the nation’s most balanced ports in terms of import-export trade.
Fernanda Kirchner is Senior Project Manager representing the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce division on this project.
The project involves partnerships with the Augusta Economic Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Power.
Sérgio Domingues is Managing Director for the State of Georgia’s Europe Office and played a supporting role in attracting Aurubis to Georgia.
Pat Wilson is GDEcD Commissioner.
The facility will be located in Richmond County.
Augusta Corporate Park has completed due diligence and other specific certification requirements prior to visits by prospective businesses as part of its GRAD certification.
Thousands of Georgia jobs have been created on GRAD sites since the program’s inception in 2008.
source_title: Aurubis to open state-of-the-art recycling, copper smelting facility in Georgia

facts: Pilgrim’s is investing almost $75 million to increase its harvesting and portioning operations at its Ellijay, Ga., facility.
The company notes that this production increase will strengthen its ability to meet consumer needs and preferences, and boost operations and growth.
Fabio Sandri, Pilgrim’s CEO, stated that as consumer demand shifts and customers grow, the investment ensures operations are positioned for the future.
Fabio Sandri described Ellijay as a strong-performing facility with a talented team.
The expansion will allow Pilgrim’s to optimize its portfolio and improve efficiency.
source_title: Pilgrim’s investing $75M to strengthen Georgia facility - poultrytimes.com

facts: Amazon.com, Inc. announced plans to invest $11 billion in Georgia through its cloud division, Amazon Web Services.
The investment aims to bolster infrastructure for cloud computing and artificial intelligence technologies.
The project will be located in Butts and Douglas counties.
AWS expects to create more than 550 well-paying jobs as a result of the investment.
Roger Wehner, vice president of economic development at AWS, stated that the company expects to create more than 550 well-paying jobs.
The expansion of AI and cloud computing has led to increased electricity consumption in the U.S.
The Electric Power Research Institute projects that by 2030, data centers could account for as much as 9% of the electricity produced in the U.S., more than twice their current usage.
Amazon has secured agreements with U.S. utilities to power its data centers across the country.
Amazon secured an agreement with Talen Energy in Pennsylvania to power its data centers.
Amazon secured an agreement with Entergy Corp in Mississippi to power its data centers.
Microsoft Corporation announced an $80 billion investment for fiscal 2025 to train AI models and deploy cloud applications, highlighting a larger trend among tech giants to invest heavily in AI.
Nvidia Corporation CEO Jensen Huang identified a $1 trillion opportunity in accelerated computing for data centers during Nvidia's second-quarter earning call.
In December 2024, Amazon decided to halt the development of its Inferentia AI chip, focusing instead on the Trainium chip to improve cost performance in AI model training.
source_title: Amazon AWS To Invest $11 Billion In Georgia To Boost AI And Cloud Infrastructure: 'Expect To Create More Than 550 Well-Paying Jobs'

entity_name: Georgia State Fiscal Policy & Corporate Tax Structure
facts: Gov. Kemp signed a $37.8 billion state budget for FY 2026, representing a 4.5% year-over-year increase from the prior year.

Georgia offers tax breaks that will cost the state more than $11.1 billion in forgone revenue collections in FY 2026, with some delivering outsized gains to manufacturing, film, and insurance industries.
Senate Bill 366, enacted during the 2024 legislative session, allows General Assembly tax-writing committee chairs to request economic analyses of tax expenditures, permitting at least 12 per year.
House Bill 111, signed by the governor in April, retroactively reduced Georgia's flat corporate and personal income tax rates to 5.19% as of January 2025.
The state's flat income tax rate is scheduled to drop to 5.09% in January 2026 if overall revenue growth meets the required 3% benchmark.
At the close of 2024, Georgia’s Revenue Shortfall Reserve stood at $5.5 billion, equivalent to 15% of prior year revenues.
source_title: Georgia Budget Trends Primer for State Fiscal Year 2026 - Georgia Budget and Policy Institute

facts: Amazon plans to spend at least $11 billion on new data center capacity in metro Atlanta.
The investment responds to huge demand for computing power driven by the rise of generative artificial intelligence.
Amazon Web Services (AWS) will make the investments in facilities located in Butts County and Douglas County.
Butts County is located south of Atlanta, and Douglas County is located west of Atlanta.
Combined, the two data center projects would likely rank as the largest corporate investment in Georgia's history by dollar amount.

The combined investment will exceed the capital expenditure of the state’s largest economic development project to date, which is the $7.6 billion Hyundai Motor Group electric vehicle factory near Savannah.
AWS announced it will procure power to the grid with renewable energy, including solar and wind.
Amazon has committed to purchasing 100% renewable electricity for its operations worldwide.
The Georgia Data Center in Atlanta is described as a hub for sustainable and efficient computing infrastructure.
John Jackson, Douglas County Commissioner, commented on the projects.
Seth Weis, Chief Economic Development Officer for Douglas County, addressed the expansion.
Chris Birch, Chief Economic Development Officer for Butts County, provided comments regarding the data center plans.
Hannah Kellaf, Staff Attorney at Earthjustice, offered comments on the proposed data centers.
source_title: Amazon plans $11 billion data center expansion in Georgia

facts: Lyft, Inc. and May Mobility, Inc. announced the launch of their autonomous vehicle (AV) pilot program in Atlanta on September 10, 2025.
This marks the first public autonomous vehicle deployment for the Lyft and May Mobility partnership.
The service initially launches as a pilot program available to Lyft riders in the area, with plans to expand service hours and vehicle availability in the months ahead.
Riders can match with May Mobility’s autonomous vehicles through the Lyft app when requesting a standard or Wait & Save ride within the service area.
The autonomous vehicles will operate around the Midtown Atlanta area, including Virginia Highlands, Old Fourth Ward, and Inman Park.
The fleet consists of hybrid-electric Toyota Sienna Autono-MaaS vehicles equipped with May Mobility’s patented Multi-Policy Decision Making (MPDM) technology.

Jeremy Bird, Executive Vice President at Lyft, stated the partnership brings commercial autonomous vehicle rides to Midtown Atlanta and surrounding areas, strengthening the region's position in transportation innovation.
Initial deployments include a standby operator present in each vehicle to assist the AV and help acclimate riders to autonomous driving technology.
Standby operators are fully trained to take the wheel and drive manually during initial trips as needed, and will intervene less over time as service is optimized.
Standby operators will also answer questions and monitor vehicle operations to prioritize a safe and comfortable ride before eventually transitioning the service to driverless operations.

Edwin Olson, CEO and founder of May Mobility, stated that the launch marks a pivotal moment in the AV industry as they work to bring driverless operations to Atlanta as part of their goal to deploy more AVs onto the platform over the coming years.
This launch in Atlanta is May Mobility’s second active operation in Georgia.
The company operates a driverless service in Peachtree Corners, with public, on-demand rides along Technology Parkway.
The Peachtree Corners microtransit service is in partnership with Curiosity Lads and T-Mobile.
At the core of the partnership is May Mobility's patented Multi-Policy Decision Making (MPDM) platform, which provides active monitoring, vehicle guidance, and predictive capabilities.
The MPDM platform applies human reasoning to every situation with AI-powered speed and precision, helping each vehicle navigate complex urban environments found in Atlanta.
MPDM achieves high safety performance by analyzing thousands of potential scenarios every second and then choosing the optimal maneuver in any situation.
The technology has proven itself in deployments across the U.S. and Japan, including driverless operations in three cities.
This technology foundation is combined with May Mobility's millions of commercial autonomous miles.
More than $90 billion is expected to be invested in transportation improvement and mobility projects across the region by 2040.
Atlanta's selection as the inaugural market for this partnership reflects the city's and state’s growing reputation as a center for transportation technology advancement.

Rep. Matt Reeves (R-Duluth), Chairman of the House Insurance Rate Study Committee, stated that Georgia represents an ideal market for autonomous vehicle technology and demonstrated how innovative companies can work together to enhance transportation options while maintaining community trust and safety.

The collaboration creates opportunities for local workforce development and positions Atlanta as an attractive destination for technology companies seeking to deploy innovative transportation solutions.
Both companies plan to expand the partnership based on community feedback and operational performance, with potential for additional markets and enhanced service offerings.
May Mobility appointed Balajee Kannan as Senior Vice President of Autonomy Core Engineering.
Lyft was founded in 2012 and has grown into a global mobility platform offering rideshare, taxis, private hire vehicles, car sharing, bikes, and scooters across 4 continents and nearly 1,000 cities.
May Mobility leverages its patented MPDM system to build an industry-leading in-situ AI solution that integrates real-time data every 200 milliseconds.
May Mobility delivers safer, more efficient transportation across diverse environments, including rural cities, dense urban areas and diverse weather conditions.
May Mobility is backed by strategic partnerships with industry leaders, including Toyota Motor Corporation and NTT.
The company has completed nearly half a million rides across 19 deployments worldwide.
source_title: Lyft and May Mobility Deploy Their First Autonomous… | May Mobility

facts: On April 30, 2026, Rivian announced its future manufacturing facility in Stanton Springs North, Georgia, will have an optimized Phase 1 production capacity of 300,000 vehicles annually.
The updated Phase 1 capacity represents a 50% increase from the original 200,000-unit plan.
The expansion comes alongside a revised $4.5 billion DOE loan, down from an original $6.6 billion figure.
The DOE financing consists of a principal amount of $4.006 billion plus interest.
Funds from the DOE loan will be drawable starting in early 2027.
Vertical construction at the Georgia site is scheduled to begin in 2026.
Vehicle production at the facility is targeted for late 2028.
Groundbreaking for the facility occurred in 2025.
Site preparation is currently ongoing.
The location is situated in Stanton Springs North, near Social Circle and Atlanta.
CEO RJ Scaringe emphasized bolstering US manufacturing jobs and maintaining American tech leadership regarding the expansion.
The Georgia facility will focus on Rivian’s midsize and compact vehicle platforms.
Primary models for Phase 1 production include the R2 SUV, R3 crossover, and R3X crossover.
Powertrain options for the vehicles will include single, dual, or tri-motor configurations.
Range targets reach up to 345+ miles on select R2 variants.
The R2 model pricing strategy starts around $45,000–$57,990.
The plant is expected to support up to 50,000 R2-based robotaxis through a partnership with Uber.
Advanced manufacturing techniques from Rivian’s Normal, IL plant will be applied, emphasizing modularity, battery integration, and quality control.
The project is expected to create thousands of direct manufacturing jobs plus thousands more in construction and supply chain roles.
Estimates point to 7,500+ long-term positions in Georgia by 2030.
Local suppliers for batteries, wiring, and components will benefit from the expansion.
The move strengthens domestic supply chains and reduces reliance on Asian imports.
Expanding manufacturing in Georgia hedges against tariffs, geopolitical risks, and logistics disruptions.
Newer Rivian models feature native NACS ports for seamless access to the Tesla Supercharger network.
The Rivian Adventure Network has expanded to over 960 chargers across 145+ sites.
Adapters are available for CCS connectors and broader charging network compatibility.
Home EV charger installation is eligible for a federal 30% tax credit.
Federal EV tax credits remain up to $7,500 for buyers.
R2 production is currently ramping at Rivian’s Normal, IL plant, with first deliveries underway or imminent as of 2026–2027.
The Georgia facility coming online in 2028 could help Rivian achieve positive cash flow and margin improvements through scale.
Potential for a full Phase 2 expansion, additional models, and greater robotaxi output exists in the longer term.
Increased competition from Rivian’s expansion is expected to push Tesla, Ford, GM, and legacy automakers to accelerate affordable EV offerings.
source_title: Rivian Georgia Plant Expansion 2026: US EV Boost - VFuture Media

facts: Waymo is a self-driving car company backed by tech giant Alphabet (GOOGL).
Waymo received its first permit from the New York Department of Transportation to begin testing autonomous vehicles in New York City.
Mayor Eric Adams announced the news on Friday, noting it is the city’s first official rollout of autonomous vehicle testing.
Waymo will test up to eight vehicles in Manhattan and Downtown Brooklyn through late September, with the option to continue the program afterward.
New York law still requires a human driver behind the wheel during all test drives.
The move comes after Waymo filed for permits just two months ago as it looks to expand nationally.
The company has already launched in cities like Austin and has expanded services in the San Francisco area.
Waymo plans to enter other major urban markets, such as Atlanta, Miami, Washington D.C., and Philadelphia.
Waymo’s CEO recently stated that the company had reached over 10 million robotaxi rides.
The Adams administration has been helping Waymo by setting safety rules and launching a permit system to support responsible testing.
Under the terms of its new permit, Waymo must regularly report data to the Department of Transportation.
Under the terms of its new permit, Waymo must coordinate closely with local police and emergency services to help ensure safe operation in one of the world’s busiest cities.
Wall Street analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on GOOGL stock based on 27 Buys and nine Holds assigned in the past three months.
The average GOOGL price target of $216.47 per share implies 8.1% upside potential.

links.context: TipRanks Premium investing tools and data-driven insights

source_title: Alphabet-Backed Waymo (GOOGL) Receives Permit to Test AVs in New York City - TipRanks.com

facts: Fiscal Year 2023 (July 1, 2022 to June 30, 2023) total investments in facility expansions and new locations exceeded $24 billion.
FY23 projects resulted in 38,400 new jobs across 426 projects supported by GDEcD’s Global Commerce team.

Excluding the two largest projects in state history (Rivian and Hyundai Motor Group) from previous year's job creation figures, total jobs as a result of FY23 projects exceeded the previous record by more than 2,800 new positions.
Annual investment totals have increased by 131 percent over the last three years.
Eighty-two percent of new jobs created and more than $20 billion in investments are directed to communities outside the 10-county Atlanta region.
Metro Atlanta attracted $3.6 billion in investment during the last fiscal year.
Expansions account for 71 percent of total projects, while new locations account for 65 percent of new jobs announced across the state.
Between July 2018 and July 2023, at least 40 e-mobility projects have been announced in Georgia.
These e-mobility projects commit more than 30,000 jobs and over $25 billion in investment to the state.
Job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent in FY23 compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
Mobility and cleantech projects alone delivered three investments of $1 billion or more and six of the largest projects by job creation.
The Georgia Center of Innovation connects public and private partners to foster innovation in strategic ecosystems for mobility and cleantech.
Investments in aerospace increased by more than 1,000 percent since FY21, with an ecosystem that overlaps with e-mobility through companies like Archer.

Partnerships on speed-to-market and workforce solutions, including the “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development” (GRAD) certification and Georgia Quick Start programs, spurred a 112 percent growth in advanced manufacturing investments since FY21.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that innovation drives a sustainable economy by developing new techniques for existing industries and building an emerging ecosystem to support cleantech and mobility of the future.

International investments were responsible for 47 percent of expected jobs and 65 percent of total investment in FY23, with top sources including South Korea, Finland, Norway, Japan, Switzerland, and the Netherlands.

links.context: View further details of state-announced economic development projects

source_title: Georgia Breaks Economic Development Records for Third Year in a Row

facts: Big Tex Trailer Manufacturing is closing its Trailer World location in Cordele, Ga., and laying off 100 workers.
The closure and layoffs were disclosed in a Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification Act filing with the state of Georgia.
The Cordele facility is located at 502 S. Midway Road.
The location operates as a BigTex Trailer World dealership offering sales, parts, and service on Big Tex and CM trailers.
The facility will be permanently closed.
The first worker separations began in January.
Big Tex manufactures utility, equipment, dump, and heavy-duty gooseneck trailers, along with various work, cargo, and tilt trailers.
The company is headquartered in Mount Pleasant, Texas.
Big Tex's trailers are manufactured across facilities in Texas, Oklahoma, Georgia, and Idaho.
source_title: Big Tex Trailer World to close Georgia facility and cut 100 jobs | Trucks, Parts, Service

facts: Big Tex Trailers is a utility trailer manufacturing company with manufacturing facilities in Odessa and Mount Pleasant, Texas.
The company operates a trailer manufacturing plant and truck bed plant in Madill, Oklahoma.
Big Tex maintains a network of over 500 trailer dealers across the US, Canada, and Mexico.
The company operates trailer parts distribution centers in Mount Pleasant, Odessa, Madill, and Ocala, Florida.
Big Tex owns 13 sales and service locations in California, Arizona, Texas, Louisiana, and Arkansas.
Big Tex is in the final stages of purchasing a 175,000-square-foot facility and 55 acres on Interstate 75 in Cordele, Georgia.
The Cordele, Georgia facility will be used to manufacture trailers and distribute products to dealers in the eastern US.
The never-occupied, like-new Cordele facility includes two buildings: one dedicated to manufacturing trailers and another that will become the fifth Tex Trail distribution facility.
Big Tex acquired CM Trailer Mfg and its subsidiaries, CM Truck Beds and S&H Trailers, from Ronald and Kay Jackson in 2007.
Between 1988 and 1990, Big Tex acquired a 100,000-square-foot building on Interstate 20 between Odessa and Midland for manufacturing.
Big Tex purchased a second 40,000-square-foot manufacturing facility on Business I-20 to expand into the heavy-duty equipment trailer market.
In 1993, Big Tex acquired an adjacent facility to create Big Tex Sales and Service.
Big Tex became the first utility trailer manufacturer to join the National Association of Trailer Manufacturers (NATM) in 1993.
Land was purchased in Mount Pleasant in 1994, with construction starting and the first trailer produced in March 1995.
Between 1997 and 1999, Big Tex acquired Rheas Trailer Sales of Mount Pleasant and expanded Tex Trail Trailer Parts to Mount Pleasant.
In 2001, a new 15,000-square-foot Tex Trail Trailer Parts building was constructed in Odessa.
A few years after 2001, Big Tex purchased a local fabrication company and renamed it Big Tex Fabrication.
Big Tex acquired 30 acres adjacent to its property, bringing the total land ownership to 93 acres.
The new corporate headquarters in Mount Pleasant is 51,500 square feet.

The new headquarters building features three phases: loading and shipping with drive-through bays that triple shipping capacity, operational offices for dealer sales and order entry, and front-section corporate and administrative offices.
In October, Tex Trail opened its third location in Ocala, Florida.
Tex Trail is scheduled to open its fourth distribution location in Madill, Oklahoma on January 1.
source_title: Trailer manufacturer celebrates opening of its new corporate headquarters in Mount Pleasant, Texas, and looks to the future | Trailer Body Builders

facts: BLUE RIDGE MANUFACTURING, LLC operates in the Truck Trailer Manufacturing industry.
The company's NAICS code is 336212 [Truck Trailer Manufacturing].
BLUE RIDGE MANUFACTURING, LLC is located in Blue Ridge, GA 30513.
The company received a PPP loan ranging from $350,000 to $1,000,000.
The PPP loan was issued by United Community Bank.
BLUE RIDGE MANUFACTURING, LLC potentially retained 64 jobs through the PPP program.
The PPP loan approval date was April 8, 2020.
The business is situated in Congressional District GA - 09.
The company did not receive a second-round PPP loan (PPS [2nd Round] amount is $0).
The loan details and company profile information were published by the U.S. Treasury.
SBA.com® is an independently owned and operated website with no government affiliation, and accuracy disputes should be directed to the U.S. Treasury or U.S. Small Business Administration.
This company profile was generated from publicly available data provided by the U.S. Treasury, last updated July 1, 2021.
source_title: BLUE RIDGE MANUFACTURING, LLC, BLUE RIDGE, GA - PPP Loan Details

facts: Georgia’s leaders have made significant investments over the years to expand the state’s logistics network.
Sandy Lake serves as director of logistics at the Georgia Center of Innovation, part of the state’s Department of Economic Development.
The state’s logistics industry has experienced record-setting growth for the last five to six years.
Current logistics activity levels are more than double pre-pandemic levels.
A 2021 report commissioned by the Center of Innovation and the Selig Center for Economic Growth at the University of Georgia identified over 32,000 logistics establishments in Georgia.
These logistics establishments employ more than 250,000 people across the state.
The logistics industry generates an $83.4 billion economic impact in Georgia.
The industry has generated $2.2 billion in tax revenue for the state and $2.3 billion in tax revenue for local governments.
Georgia offers unique modal agility across road, rail, air, and ocean logistics compared to other states.
The Georgia Ports Authority expanded supply chain agility by deepening the Port of Savannah in 2022 to accommodate larger ships.
The Georgia Ports Authority completed $262 million in improvements to the Port of Brunswick.
Two inland ports have opened in North Georgia as part of state logistics expansions.
The Appalachian Regional Port (ARP) is located in Murray County in Northwest Georgia and opened in 2018.
CSX provides exclusive rail services on a direct, 388-mile route between the ARP and the Port of Savannah’s Garden City Terminal.
Each roundtrip container moved through the ARP offsets 710 truck miles.
The ARP helps avoid 12.5 million truck miles per year on Georgia’s highways.
Carl Campbell serves as executive director of the Murray County Industrial Development Authority.
A manufacturer in Dalton can transport a container to the ARP, unload it, and return to its plant in less than 20 minutes.
The ARP has an annual capacity of 75,000 containers.
There is a plan to double the ARP’s container capacity within 10 years.
Companies seeking proximity to the inland port have chosen Murray County for operations, including General Electric Appliances, Huali Floors, and Green Galaxy Group.
The new GE Appliances facility in Murray County represented a $32 million investment.
The GE Appliances facility created 100 new jobs.
Murray County now operates a 555-acre, GRAD-certified industrial park to market to companies for investment and job growth.
A second inland port operated by the Georgia Ports Authority is scheduled to open in 2026.
Located in Gainesville, the Blue Ridge Connector will link the Port of Savannah to Northeast Georgia through exclusive rail service by Norfolk Southern.
The Blue Ridge Connector will have an annual lift capacity of 200,000 containers.
Tim Evans serves as president and CEO of the Greater Hall Chamber of Commerce.
Gainesville and Hall County host a high concentration of advanced manufacturing in pharmaceuticals, food processing, machinery, and automotive industries.
Many local companies in Hall County import parts and components and export finished goods through the Port of Savannah.
The Blue Ridge Connector creates a new trade route that replaces a 300-mile truck route with a 350-mile rail route.
More than 300 companies in Hall County are engaged in manufacturing and processing, representing nearly one-third of the county’s workforce.
Steve Syfan serves as co-founder and executive vice president of Syfan Logistics.
Headquartered in Gainesville, Syfan Logistics specializes in transporting goods for the food, automotive, and shipping industries.
In 2024, Syfan Logistics saw a 16% increase in load count, marking its biggest year in volume in its four-decade history.
Syfan Logistics achieved its second highest year in revenue in 2024.
According to TRIP, freight moved by trucks annually in Georgia is predicted to increase 67% by weight and 96% in value by 2050.
When the Blue Ridge Connector opens in 2026, Syfan Logistics will shift some freight currently transported from deepwater ports in Savannah and Brunswick to be picked up at the new inland port.
Syfan Logistics is asset-based and employs 100 drivers.
The Georgia Ports Authority recently moved all roll-on/roll-off (Ro/Ro), breakbulk, and bulk product from Savannah to the Port of Brunswick.
In January, Wallenius Wilhelmsen cut the ribbon on its new state-of-the-art facility in Brunswick.
Wallenius Wilhelmsen is an Oslo-based carrier and one of the largest Ro/Ro carriers in the world.
Wallenius Wilhelmsen currently employs 518 people in Georgia.
Ryan Moore serves as president and CEO of the Golden Isles Development Authority.
In 2023, the Golden Isles Development Authority purchased a 1,400-acre property located 7 miles west of the Port of Brunswick to create the Georgia Breakbulk Logistics Park.
The Georgia Breakbulk Logistics Park is designed to carve out a niche in the bulk and breakbulk business for the port.
In December 2024, the Golden Isles Development Authority received a $2 million grant from the Georgia Department of Community Affairs to prepare the industrial park for development.
The grant is part of the OneGeorgia Authority’s Rural Site Development Initiative and will fund entrances and extend water and sewer infrastructure.
A recent Georgia Department of Transportation report estimates freight movement will nearly double in Georgia in the next 25 years.
In July 2024, Gov. Brian Kemp announced a $1.5 billion transportation infrastructure investment, including a $500 million freight program for projects improving efficiency, safety, and reliability.

In October 2024, the U.S. Department of Transportation’s Federal Railroad Administration announced Georgia will receive $65 million for three rail improvement projects as part of a $2.4 billion national grant under the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
Norfolk Southern reports an average annual investment of $195 million in roadway and infrastructure improvements.
Over the past five years, industry-supported rail projects across Georgia totaled $1.2 billion.
These rail projects have supported the creation of more than 900 new jobs.
The Georgia Logistics Summit, organized by the Center of Innovation, will be held on April 23 at the Cobb Galleria in Atlanta.
One summit topic will be "Port to Point: Navigating Beyond the Gates."
Ryan Moore stated that the Georgia Ports Authority is second to none globally in port operations and growth, outpacing every other port in the nation.
source_title: Expanding Logistics: Georgia's Infrastrucure & Supply Chain - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: The Bosch Group increased its sales and earnings in 2023 and is successfully implementing its growth strategy despite a difficult environment.

Stefan Hartung, chairman of the board of management of Robert Bosch GmbH, stated that the company achieved its financial targets in 2023 and strengthened its market position from semiconductors to integrated building systems.
The company aims for long-term average annual growth of between 6 and 8 percent with a margin of at least 7 percent and wants to rank among the top three suppliers in its key markets globally.
In 2023, Bosch generated sales of $99 billion (91.6 billion euros), an increase of 3.8 percent or an exchange rate-adjusted 8.0 percent.
Earnings from operations before interest and taxes (EBIT) amounted to $5.2 billion (4.8 billion euros, up from 3.8 billion euros in 2022).
The EBIT margin from operations was 5.3 percent, which is higher than expected but lower than the long-term target margin of at least 7 percent required by 2026.
CFO Markus Forschner noted that a successful final sprint at the end of 2023 contributed to meeting expectations, but the 2024 business year will be at least as challenging as 2023.
Bosch expects global economic growth of only 2.3 percent in 2024, along with stagnating vehicle production and continued weakness in the industrial technology market.
In North America, sales to third parties increased 6 percent year-over-year to $16.5 billion (15.2 billion euros), while total net sales from the region increased 8 percent to $17.9 billion.
The Mobility business sector in North America increased sales by 15 percent to $10.4 billion, while Industrial Technology increased 33 percent to $1.6 billion.
Energy and Building Technology sales in North America were up nearly 8 percent to $1.3 billion, and the Consumer Goods business sector was down nearly 6 percent to $3.3 billion in 2023.
Mike Mansuetti, president of Bosch in North America, highlighted that more than 42,000 associates in the United States, Mexico and Canada contributed to the company's global success.
In 2023, Bosch invested more than $778 million (719 million euros) in capital expenditures in North America, a more than 50 percent increase over 2022.
Since the beginning of 2022, Bosch has announced more than $3 billion dollars worth of investments in North America across its portfolio.
In the Mobility business sector, Bosch announced and closed an acquisition of a wafer fab in Roseville, Calif., intending to invest $1.5 billion at the site to produce silicon carbide chips by 2026.
In its Industrial business sector, Bosch closed the acquisition of U.S.-based specialist hydraulic company HydraForce in 2023.
In its Energy and Building Technologies business sector, Bosch announced and closed the acquisition of Vancouver, Canada-based Paladin Technologies Inc.
In its Consumer Goods business sector, the company announced a $130 million investment to support the manufacturing of power tools accessories in Lincolnton, N.C.
In its core mobility business, Bosch is launching some 30 production projects for electric vehicles this year alone.
Stefan Hartung estimates that 70 percent of all new cars in Europe will likely be purely electric by 2030, and 40 to 50 percent in China and North America.
For heavy vehicles covering long distances, plug-in hybrids and range extenders will remain in demand for some time.

The Mobility business sector expects further stimulus from vehicle dynamics technology, growing by 10 percent annually with new and redundant braking systems tailored to electrified and automated driving.

Bosch is committed to a vehicle motion management (VMM) system solution that coordinates brakes, steering, powertrain, and dampers, equipping more than 20 major-brand test vehicles with VMM variants for recent winter tests.
Hartung stated the company will put its first VMM order into volume production this year and expects to achieve sales worth hundreds of millions for the VMM solution by 2030.
Bosch reaffirmed that sales with hydrogen technology could reach nearly $5.5 billion globally by 2030, though major growth in Europe or North America is not expected until the next decade.

The market for hydrogen engines is expected to be worth around $1 billion by 2030, with Bosch's injection technology on the road in India this year and working on five production orders from truck manufacturers.
Bosch expects global installed capacity for hydrogen electrolysis to reach a good 170 gigawatts by 2030, with its electrolysis stack on course for market entry next year.

Bosch's heat pump business grew by almost 50 percent in 2023 despite Europe stagnating, and the company announced an IDS Ultra cold climate heat pump system at CES 2024 designed for colder climate zones such as the Northern U.S. that met all requirements of the DOE Cold Climate Challenge.
Research and development expenditure remained stable at $7.9 billion (8 percent ratio), while capital expenditure reached a new high of $5.9 billion.
Upfront investments totaled nearly $13 billion in 2023, with CFO Forschner noting close attention to cost-effectiveness and project adjustments.
At the end of 2023, Bosch employed 429,416 people worldwide, an increase of some 8,078 from the previous year, with the strongest regional growth in the Americas.
source_title: Bosch is banking on innovations, partnerships, and acquisitions – cost reduction remains in focus - Bosch Media Service US

canonical_name: Bosch Automotive Aftermarket Division North America
facts: Robert Bosch LLC announced that Douglas Arnold will join the Bosch Automotive Aftermarket Division North America.
Arnold will take on the role of vice president, independent aftermarket sales.
He will be located in the Broadview, Illinois, office.
Arnold will report directly to Enrico Manuele, regional president, Bosch Automotive Aftermarket North America.
The appointment will be effective August 15, 2016.
Arnold brings over 15 years of experience to Bosch in national brand and private label sales and sales management across a range of distribution channels.
Most recently he served as vice president of traditional sales for Dorman Products Inc.
At Dorman Products Inc., Arnold successfully developed operating plans and set team, pricing and program strategies supporting both national accounts and traditional aftermarket business.
Enrico Manuele stated that Doug possesses the key skills and experience to help lead the Bosch independent aftermarket sales team to even higher levels of performance and success.

facts: Bridgestone Bandag broke ground on the expansion of its retread tire plant in Abilene, Texas.
The project represents a $60 million capital investment into Abilene.
The expansion will add 50,000 square feet to their existing 200,000 square-foot facility in Abilene, Texas.
Misty Mayo, President and Chief Executive Officer of the DCOA, stated that Bridgestone Bandag has continued to invest in Abilene for over 50 years.
The expansion project is expected to retain 179 full-time employees.
The project will create an additional 25 full-time positions for Abilene.
Over the next 10 years, the $60 million project is estimated to add over $21 million in taxes for the City of Abilene, Taylor County, and Abilene Independent School District.
Abilene Mayor Weldon Hurt stated that Bridgestone Bandag continues to grow and invest in the City of Abilene.
Over the next 10 years, the total estimated indirect and direct impact of the retention and expansion is $1.81 billion.
Sam Vinson, DCOA Board Chair, noted that Bridgestone Bandag has chosen to stay, grow, and prosper in Abilene, Texas.
The expansion project will focus on the development of an automated operation for rubber mixing and production.
Billy Fralick, Plant Manager of the Abilene Plant, stated the expansion will allow them to grow production capabilities to meet customer needs.

The Abilene plant expansion supports Bridgestone’s E8 Commitment, which consists of 8 values starting with “E” (Energy, Ecology, Efficiency, Extension, Economy, Emotion, Ease, and Empowerment) to realize a sustainable society.

Chris Daniel, Executive Director of Bandag Manufacturing at Bridgestone Americas, stated the expansion is driven by growing demand for products and the team’s commitment to delivering innovation and superior quality.
The company aims to build greener retreads while helping maximize fuel efficiency for fleets.
The project taps into Abilene’s skilled labor force in the manufacturing industry.
Abilene has four universities, a technical college, a community college, and state-of-the-art training facilities for high school and college students.
Abilene is located directly on I-20, just two and a half hours from the Dallas/Fort Worth metro.
The city of Abilene has a population of 126,518 people and a labor shed of over 140,000.
Bridgestone Americas, Inc. is the U.S.-based subsidiary of Bridgestone Corporation, headquartered in Nashville, Tenn., and employs more than 50,000 people across its worldwide operations.
Bandag has been responsible for keeping approximately 300 million tires out of the waste system since 1957.
The Bandag retreading process has saved four billion gallons of oil since it began 50 years ago.
The Development Corporation of Abilene (DCOA) is the economic development driver of Abilene, leveraging the DevelopAbilene partnership to market the city for business expansion.

facts: Manufacturer Bridgestone is set to invest US$60m to expand the Bridgestone Bandag manufacturing facility in Texas.
The investment will be used to expand the existing 200,000ft2 site by a further 50,000ft2.
The expansion will immediately increase the site’s operational activities by running on six and seven-day production schedules.
New mixing operations will also be constructed as part of the expansion plans.
Upon scheduled completion of the mixing operations in January 2025, the plant’s output will be increased by an estimated 16%.
The expansion at the Abilene plant addresses the growing demand for Bridgestone tread rubber products driven by the rapid growth of the company’s retread business.
Since 1957, Bandag has kept an estimated 300 million tires out of the waste system, helping to reduce material emissions and water and energy usage needed for tire production.
The company’s retreading process has saved four billion gallons of oil to date.

Barry Owens, senior vice president of Bridgestone Americas Manufacturing Group, stated that the plant's growth is celebrated through new investment and physical expansion driven by growing product demand and the team's commitment to innovation and quality.
Owens noted that the company and Abilene team play a vital role in the transformation of mobility by building greener retreads and maximizing fuel efficiency for fleets.

facts: Bridgestone Americas, Inc. (BSAM) announced plans to begin the process of relocating members of its senior management team and other specific functions of its Muscatine, Iowa-based Bridgestone Bandag Tire Solutions (BBTS) operations to locations in the Nashville, Tenn., area, including BSAM's headquarters facility in Nashville.
This decision is part of an overall effort to restructure the way BSAM does business across its North American operations.

The goals are to become a more profitable and customer-focused business; to lower costs by eliminating duplication and inefficiencies; and to harness talent and expertise in a way that best serves the business.
The company is installing new management operating systems to improve efficiencies and business processes, and enhancing the ability to measure business and individual performance.

Kurt Danielson, President, BBTS, said that relocating the headquarters team and certain functions to the Nashville area is the right business decision, and will align our resources and people in a way that will enable us to better serve our customers.
Kurt Danielson stated that BBTS will keep certain business functions in Muscatine and continue to be a part of this community.

BBTS functions remaining in Muscatine include Equipment Manufacturing, Emergency Road Service, Human Resources, Retread Manufacturing Management, Manufacturing Finance, Information Technology Developers and Support, Retread Engineering, Retread Technical Services, and Retread Research and Development.
Approximately 220 BBTS teammates will remain in Muscatine.

BBTS functions relocating to locations in the Nashville area, including the company's headquarters facility, include Purchasing, Marketing, Finance, Accounts Payable, Credit, Business Strategy & Solutions Group, Customer Service, Legal, Safety and members of the Company Owned Operations senior management team.
Thirty-two BBTS teammates will be relocating to Nashville as a result of this decision.
Fifty-five BBTS teammates who will not be relocating to Nashville have been asked to assist with the transition.
The transition is expected to conclude no later than June 30, 2011.
Eighteen positions within BBTS were eliminated.
source_title: Bridgestone Bandag Tire Solutions to Relocate Headquarters Team to Nashville, Tenn., Area | Work Truck Online

facts: Bandag has signed an agreement with Tegeta Motors to establish a state-of-the-art Tegeta Bandag Factory in Rustavi, Georgia.
The facility is backed by an initial investment of $2 million.
It will be Bandag’s first retreading plant in the Caucasus region.
The project is developed in collaboration with Tegeta Motors and supported by Bridgestone Middle East and Africa (MEA).
Construction is scheduled to begin this year, with completion targeted for late 2026.
The plant is designed to produce up to 5,000 retreaded tyres annually in its initial phase.
Capacity is projected to double within five years to meet growing regional demand.
The project reinforces Bandag’s commitment to circular mobility by extending tyre life cycles, reducing waste, and lowering carbon emissions.

Through global expertise and advanced technology, the facility will introduce internationally recognised retreading standards aimed at delivering cost-efficient and sustainable solutions for commercial fleets.
Initially, the plant will focus on retreading tyres for buses and trucks, ensuring compliance with global safety and performance benchmarks.

Jacques Fourie, President, Africa, Middle East Region, Bridgestone, stated that the milestone represents a significant step forward in bringing high-quality retreading solutions to the Caucasus region and created a strong foundation for innovation benefiting fleets and communities.
The factory will be equipped with advanced Bandag machinery sourced from Europe and the United States.
Construction oversight is provided by Bridgestone and Bandag representatives to ensure adherence to global quality standards.
Retreaded tyres offer 30–40 per cent cost savings compared with new tyres while delivering comparable performance and a service life of up to five years.
Ekaterine Kavtaradze, CEO, Tegeta Motors, stated that the launch advances fleet solutions, promotes sustainable mobility, and strengthens their presence in the Caucasus region.
The initiative positions Tegeta Motors as a leading provider of advanced, sustainable automotive services across the Caucasus.
source_title: Bandag to Set Up Advanced Tyre Retreading Facility in Georgia

facts: Bandag signed an agreement with Tegeta Motors to build a state-of-the-art Tegeta Bandag Factory in Rustavi, Georgia.

facts: Club Car has been producing small vehicles, from golf carts to cars, for over 65 years.
The company transitioned to electric vehicles over the last two decades.
A ribbon-cutting ceremony was held Thursday for a new manufacturing facility expansion.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp attended the facility's ribbon-cutting ceremony.
According to Governor Brian Kemp, 90 percent of the world's golf and small vehicles are made in Georgia, with 85 percent of those being electric vehicles.
Electric vehicles and renewable energy are on the rise across the nation, and Thursday marked a celebration for the expansion and new facility for Club Car.
The new manufacturing plant is located on the outskirts of Columbia County in Appling, Georgia.
CEO and President Mark Wagner stated that many customers are seeking products to meet green, sustainability, or carbon footprint reduction goals.
Wagner noted that most sales of their utility vehicles involve replacing gas-powered vehicles.
The new facility drives job growth in Appling and the CSRA region, with additional hiring planned for the future.
Governor Kemp noted that Club Car was founded in 1958 and currently employs 1,700 people worldwide.
Of Club Car's global workforce, 1,300 employees are located in Georgia.
The project created 40 jobs from its inception to the current point.
The facility required approximately eight million dollars of investment to reach this stage.
Club Car has expansion plans inside the new Appling facility.
The site can produce up to 20 electric vehicles per day.
The first vehicle from the Appling manufacturing plant was expected to be completed on the day of the ribbon-cutting ceremony.

facts: Club Car announced plans to expand into a new 105,000 square foot facility in Appling, Georgia.
Mark Wagner, Club Car President, stated the team is dedicated to provide the best vehicles and service for our customers.
The new facility gives us the opportunity to extend operations in the United States, added Wagner.
The new facility is in the White Oak Industrial Park near an existing Club Car facility that opened in 2019.
Club Car plans to use the additional space to support warehousing and manufacturing functions.
Construction of the new facility is expected to be complete by December 2023.

Rick Evans, Chair of the Board of Directors for the Development Authority of Columbia County, said Club Car has a long-standing relationship with Columbia County and values the investments made to expand and manufacture world class golf carts and utility vehicles in Columbia County.
Doug Duncan, Chairman of Columbia County Board of Commissioners, added Club Car is a great community partner and they are excited and thankful for their continued growth in Columbia County.
Club Car has maintained multi-purpose operations in Augusta since it was founded in 1958.
Today, Club Car designs and manufactures golf cars, commercial utility vehicles and personal –use transportation.
With over 60 years of experience of innovation and design in producing small-wheel vehicles, Club Car is a leading manufacturer of gas and electric golf, utility and personal transportation vehicles.

Since 1958, the Club Car product portfolio has grown to include much more than golf cars, now encompassing vehicles for commercial and consumer markets, built with an uncompromised desire for superior safety, quality, and performance.
As a global leader in sustainability and electrification, the Club Car Vehicle Group includes luxury electric vehicle brand, Garia, and lightweight electric utility vehicle brand, Melex.
Garia and Melex product portfolios share similar characteristics of reliability, thoughtful design, and proven performance.
Club Car Vehicle Group is proud to be at the forefront of environmentally responsible Zero Emission Vehicle (ZEV) technologies.

facts: Club Car golf carts are primarily manufactured in the United States, with their main production facilities located in Augusta, Georgia.
The Augusta plant serves as the company’s central hub for both assembly and component manufacturing.
The Augusta facility supports the production of both gas-powered and electric golf carts.
Club Car operates additional international production sites in Mexico and the Netherlands to serve regional markets.
Club Car integrates in-house component production to maintain quality consistency.
Club Car uses lean manufacturing principles to minimize waste and optimize efficiency.
Club Car implements advanced robotics and automation in its assembly lines.
Club Car is committed to environmentally sustainable manufacturing practices.
The Augusta facility produces gas and electric golf carts as well as utility vehicles.
Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania serves as another main manufacturing hub responsible for the assembly of a wide range of gas and electric golf carts.
The Pittsburgh facility integrates state-of-the-art robotics and assembly lines to ensure precision and reliability.
Winder, Georgia is a significant plant focusing on the production of utility vehicles and certain specialty golf carts.
The Winder, Georgia site supports both domestic and export markets with tailored manufacturing capabilities.
Club Car maintains strict quality control protocols throughout its manufacturing processes.
Quality assurance begins with supplier selection, emphasizing partnerships with vendors who provide premium materials and components.
Incoming materials undergo comprehensive testing before entering the production line.
During assembly, Club Car employs a combination of automated inspections and manual checks to detect any deviations from specifications.
Each golf cart undergoes extensive functional testing, including electrical system diagnostics to verify battery and motor performance.
Functional testing also includes brake and steering system assessments for safety compliance.
Structural integrity evaluations are conducted to ensure frame and body robustness.
Final road testing is performed to simulate real-world operating conditions.
Club Car adheres to ISO 9001 for quality management systems.
Club Car emphasizes using American-made components and materials to ensure high quality and reliability in their golf carts.
Club Car has invested in expanding and modernizing its U.S. manufacturing plants to meet growing demand.
Club Car’s manufacturing operations emphasize a locally sourced supply chain centered around the Georgia facility.

facts: More than half the jobs at Constellium's manufacturing plant in White, Georgia, are being wiped out.
The layoffs are a gut punch for a Bartow County community that has come to rely on the auto supplier for steady work.
Constellium Automotive USA LLC is eliminating a mix of production and supervisory roles, from operators to quality managers.
The cuts add up to more than half of the plant's workforce, leaving dozens of workers without jobs.
The White facility opened in 2017 to manufacture advanced aluminum structural parts and crash-management systems for automakers.
According to Constellium, the factory was built to serve assembly plants across the Southeast.
The factory was projected to reach about 150 employees during its initial ramp-up.
The job losses land during what has already been a bumpy stretch for Georgia's industrial economy, with employers trimming payrolls across several sectors.
WARN notices in 2025 tallied thousands of job cuts statewide, particularly in logistics and timber.
Earlier this year, SK Battery America cut nearly 1,000 jobs at its Commerce battery plant, highlighting mounting pressure on suppliers across the region.
Georgia requires employers to file Mass Separation notices when they lay off groups of workers.
The state's Rapid Response network can provide on-site help, training referrals and benefits information to displaced employees.
In many cases, employers are expected to submit a Mass Separation Notice within 48 hours of the layoff event.
Workers affected by the Constellium cuts are encouraged to contact local career centers or the state Rapid Response team to learn about available services and next steps.
Officials have not yet released full details on the scope or timing of the reductions.

links.context: Deeper background on the initial announcement

source_title: White, Ga. Reeling As Constellium Guts Auto Parts Plant Workforce

Eric Krepps, vice president and general manager of Constellium Automotive Structures North America, stated that Georgia has a strong business climate and an advanced technical education network to provide a skilled workforce for their operations.

facts: Constellium SE is a French-based global manufacturer of aluminium rolled products, extruded products, and structural parts based on a large variety of advanced alloys.
Constellium primarily serves the aerospace, automotive, and packaging sectors.
Large clients include Mercedes-Benz, Audi, BMW, Fiat Chrysler Automotive, Ford, Airbus, Boeing.
Constellium's C-TEC research center has been credited for advancing technology in the field of advanced aluminium alloy.
Constellium was created when Rio Tinto sold off Alcan Engineered Products to Apollo Management (51%) and FSI (10%) in 2011.
Prior to that, Alcan Engineered Products was the result of various mergers and acquisitions between Pechiney, Alcan and Alusuisse.
Constellium is listed on the New York Stock Exchange.
In February 2018, Constellium delisted its shares from Euronext Paris.
The company counts close to 11,500 employees worldwide.
Constellium operates more than 28 manufacturing sites in North America, Europe and Asia.
The company is headquartered in Paris with corporate offices in Baltimore and Zurich.
Founded in 1967, Constellium's C-TEC Technology Center is a research and development center designed to promote innovation in aluminum.
Located in Voreppe, France, C-TEC maintains a staff of over 200 R&D technicians and engineers.
During the last 50 years, C-TEC has added more than 600 patent families and trademarks.
Breakthrough innovations include high performance aluminium-lithium alloy for air-and spacecraft.
Innovations also include high-strength aluminium alloys for structural components in the automotive industry.
The company developed aluminium solutions for aerosols that can lighten products by 30 percent.
C-TEC developed high-tech aluminium profiles for hybrid motors.

Launched in 2013, the Advanced Light Metals Processing Research Center, a collaboration between Constellium, Brunel University and Jaguar Land Rover, was opened as an innovation center dedicated to the automotive industry.

In 2016, the partnership between Constellium and Brunel was extended with the opening of the Constellium University Technology Center (UTC), a research center for the design, development and prototyping of aluminium alloys and automotive structural components.
In 2016, Constellium opened an additional research hub dedicated to lightweight automotive aluminium in Plymouth, Michigan.

In 2012, the Group founded a Sustainability Council to choose sustainability targets, track performance and ensure accurate disclosure of data in alignment with the Global Reporting Initiative, Carbon Disclosure Project, and United Nations Global Compact.
The company is also a founding member of the Aluminium Stewardship Initiative (ASI), a global, multi-stakeholder, non-profit organization for the aluminium industry.

ASI sets standards across the entire aluminium value chain, including in areas such as mining, greenhouse gas emissions, waste management, material stewardship, biodiversity, human rights, and responsible strategic sourcing.
With plants in Muscle Shoals, Alabama, and Neuf-Brisach, France, the company has the capacity to recycle the equivalent of 23 billion cans a year.
The company also works with the European Aluminium Association, the US Aluminum Association, and the Can Manufacturers Institute to increase beverage can recycling.
In particular, the company supports the European aluminium industry's target to reach an 80 percent beverage can recycling rate by 2020.
Key people include Ingrid Joerg (CEO) and Jack Guo (CFO).
Revenue was US$8.4 billion in 2025.
Net income was US$275 million in 2025.
Total assets were US$5.354 billion in 2025.
Total equity was US$971 million in 2025.
source_title: Constellium - Wikipedia

facts: Constellium SE (NYSE: CSTM) announced the successful completion and publication of the FlexCAR project.
The FlexCAR project was an innovation effort with ARENA2036, Germany’s leading research campus for future mobility and production technologies.
The FlexCAR platform envisions configuring or updating major vehicle systems such as the drivetrain, energy storage, and interior.
Constellium worked alongside project partners Mercedes-Benz, Siemens, Bosch, and the German Aerospace Center (DLR) to complete the publicly funded, five-year project.
Constellium developed a modular sill concept using aluminum extrusions made from HSA6™ high-strength aluminum alloys featuring significant recycled content.
The sill structure is designed to support various powertrains including battery electric and hydrogen fuel cell.
The sill structure offers enhanced crash performance, design flexibility, and a lower carbon footprint over the vehicle’s lifecycle.

Patrick Böhler, Director of Sales & Product Development for Constellium Automotive Structures, stated that FlexCAR shows how modular thinking combined with aluminum's performance and sustainability benefits can revolutionize vehicle design.
Böhler noted that such collaborations turn ideas into real-world innovations enabling longer lifespans, greater adaptability, and lower environmental impact.
Constellium previously collaborated with ARENA2036 on the Digital Fingerprint innovation project, which was completed in 2024.

The Digital Fingerprint project involved developing a digital twin of an aluminum component that tracks its performance across its full lifecycle from design and production to on-road use and end-of-life.
As part of this project, Constellium engineered a smart aluminum housing for a power control unit, embedding sensors to collect crash and field data in a Mercedes-Benz test vehicle.
This approach supports predictive maintenance, accelerates development cycles, and unlocks the full potential of connected, data-driven manufacturing.
Constellium is a full-service supplier of rolled and extrusion-based aluminum solutions for the global automotive market.
Constellium helps automakers produce lighter, safer, and more fuel-efficient vehicles, as well as electric vehicles with greater range.

ARENA2036 (Active Research Environment for the Next Generation of Automobiles) is a research campus in Stuttgart-Vaihingen, Germany designed as a highly flexible and future-oriented research platform for mobility and production.
Constellium has been a contributing member of ARENA2036 since 2018.
Constellium (NYSE: CSTM) is a global sector leader that develops innovative, value-added aluminum products for aerospace, packaging, and automotive markets.
Constellium generated $7.3 billion of revenue in 2024.
source_title: Constellium Drives Digital and Modular Innovation for Smart Aluminum Automotive Structures with ARENA2036

canonical_name: Continental Tire of the Americas LLC
facts: Continental Tire of the Americas LLC is closing a plant in Barnesville, Ga., by the end of 2026.
The closure will cost Lamar County 235 jobs.
The Barnesville plant has been part of Continental's portfolio since 1988.
The facility is situated alongside a railroad track on Aldora Street in Barnesville.
The plant produces tire cord fabric, hose yarn, and knitted fabric used in tires and ContiTech unit products for non-automotive industries.
In a November release, Continental attributed the closure to “ongoing changes in global market dynamics and supply chains.”
Continental stated the Barnesville plant “has not been cost competitive for many years despite continuous turnaround efforts.”
Spokesperson Allison Zora declined to comment on whether recent trade upheaval directly influenced the closure decision.
Zora stated the decision resulted from a comprehensive review of operations and supply chain aimed at strengthening Continental’s long-term competitiveness, efficiency, quality, and sustainability.
Continental emphasized the Barnesville closure will not reduce its commitment to the U.S. market.
The company has made $1.5 billion in investments in U.S. tire manufacturing facilities located in South Carolina, Illinois, Mississippi, and Indiana.
These U.S. facilities produce 16 million tires per year.
Continental employs a total of 8,800 people across its operations.
The company recently completed an $85 million investment to upgrade a ContiTech factory in Iowa.
A new tire distribution center is scheduled to open in Dallas.
ContiTech continues to maintain some operations in Georgia, but Continental Tire operates no tire facilities within the state.
Layoffs at the Barnesville plant will be phased in starting in March, with final separation dates occurring on the last day of 2026.
The company filed a WARN notice regarding the closures with the Georgia Department of Labor in January.
Affected employees will receive career counseling and access to employment resources.
On-site resume writing and informational workshops are scheduled for February 10-12.
A job fair for displaced workers will be held on March 25.
source_title: German Tire Giant Set to Close Georgia Textile Plant, Shedding 235 Jobs  - Global Atlanta

facts: Continental will discontinue operations at the Aldora Mills textile plant in Barnesville, Georgia by the end of 2026.
The company will support affected employees during the transition.
The U.S. remains a key market for Continental.

facts: Hyundai announced plans to expand production capacity at its new $7.6-billion EV factory in Georgia by two-thirds to a total of 500,000 vehicles per year.
Hyundai broke ground at the Metaplant America in Bryan County, Georgia in the fall of 2022.
Hyundai began producing EVs at the plant less than two years after breaking ground.
The company received some $2.1 billion in tax breaks and other incentives from state and local governments.
Around 1,200 people are currently working at the plant.
The plant produces Hyundai’s Ioniq 5.
The plant produces the larger Ioniq 9.
The Ioniq 9 is to be released this spring.
Hyundai plans to build a $5.8-billion steel mill in Louisiana.
The Louisiana steel mill will produce auto parts for Hyundai's assembly plants in Georgia and Alabama.
Hyundai Motor Group Executive Chairman Euisun Chung said the company has come to Georgia “to stay, to invest and to grow.”
Hyundai Motor Group Executive Chairman Euisun Chung stated, “I have never been more confident about building the future of mobility in America.”
Hyundai Motor Company CEO Jose Munoz said, “This plant couldn’t come at a better time than now.”
Hyundai Motor Company CEO Jose Munoz stated that all cars produced at the plant will be exempted from any tariffs.
