entity_name: Continental Tire the Americas, LLC dba Continental Tires
facts: Strauss Borrelli PLLC is investigating Continental Tire the Americas, LLC dba Continental Tires regarding a potential mass layoff at its Barnesville, Georgia facility.
On January 28, 2026, Continental notified the Georgia Office of Workforce Development of its decision to conduct a mass layoff at its Barnesville, Georgia location.

The investigation focuses on whether Continental failed to provide at least 60 days' advance notice before laying off 235 employees, potentially violating the federal Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification (WARN) Act.
The WARN Act is a federal law passed in 1988 that requires employers with 100 or more employees to provide a 60-day written notice for significant layoffs or plant closings.

The WARN Act aims to reduce the impact of sudden job loss on workers and communities by ensuring they have sufficient time to transition, seek new employment, and pursue training or retraining opportunities.
Generally, employees must receive a WARN Act notice if they are laid off or if their hours are cut by 50% or more in any six-month period because of a plant closing or mass layoff.
A temporary layoff may still violate the WARN Act if it ultimately lasts longer than six months.
Employers who do not follow WARN Act rules, either by giving notice too late or providing unclear notices, may have to pay employees back pay and benefits for the time they were in violation.
Continental Tire the Americas is headquartered in Fort Mill, South Carolina, and operates as the North and South American tire business of Continental AG.
The company designs, manufactures, imports, and distributes tires for passenger vehicles, light trucks, commercial trucks/buses, and select specialty applications.
source_title: Continental Tire WARN Act Investigation - Strauss Borrelli PLLC

facts: State leaders celebrated the groundbreaking of a new Rivian electric vehicle factory east of Atlanta on Tuesday, September 16th.
The facility will be built on a 2,000-acre site.
When completed, the facility will employ 7,500 people to build electric vehicles.
The project represents a $5 billion investment.
The $5 billion project was originally announced in December 2021.
At the time of its announcement, it was the largest economic investment in Georgia state history.
Speakers at the groundbreaking ceremony included Gov. Brian Kemp, Morgan County officials, and Rivian founder R.J. Scringe.
Rivian is actively working to find local sources for car parts to keep prices down.

Rivian founder R.J. Scringe stated they have been working closely with the state to bring suppliers to Georgia or work with new suppliers based in the state, calling it a really big part of the effort.
The first vehicles built at this factory are expected to be available in 2028.

Gov. Brian Kemp stated that an immigration raid resulting in the arrest of around 500 people at a battery plant in Southeast Georgia will not deter foreign investors from locating companies in the state.
Kemp has had conversations with the White House regarding the need for visas for immigrant workers and expressed hope for a solution.
Many workers taken into ICE custody during the raid were South Korean, leading to concern from the South Korean government.
ICE stated the arrested workers came to the U.S. illegally or overstayed their visas, while lawyers for some workers claim they were lawfully present.
source_title: Georgia Today: New Rivian electric vehicle plant; Kemp responds to Hyundai raid; Vaccine access | Georgia Public Broadcasting

facts: Last Nov. 13, OSHA received an employer-reported referral from Continental Tire the Americas LLC after an employee unjamming a machine suffered the amputation of three fingers.
The next day, one employee suffered severe burns requiring hospitalization and another employee suffered a concussion after an industrial rubber-compound mixer exploded.

Inspectors said Continental Tire had deficient safety procedures for energy control, combustible dust preventative engineering, housekeeping, personal protective equipment, emergency egress routes and hazard communication.
As a result of the cited deficiencies, the company faces $341,866 in fines.

OSHA Area Director Aaron Priddy stated that OSHA standards are put in place to prevent workers from suffering life-altering injuries and directed Continental Tire to review safety procedures and employee training to ensure worker safety.
Continental Tire the Americas LLC is based in Fort Mill, South Carolina.
The company manufactures passenger, light truck and commercial tires at facilities in Barnesville, Georgia; Mt. Vernon and Sumter, South Carolina.
Continental is the North American subsidiary of the German-based tire and automotive component manufacturer Continental AG.
source_title: Tire Maker Fined after Separate Incidents - ISSSource

facts: Continental AG’s Tires Group posted $3.6 billion in sales during the third quarter of 2024.
Tire group sales represented a 1.9% increase from the same period in 2023.
Adjusted EBIT margin for the tire business was up 13.3% versus the third quarter of 2023.
Continental CEO Olaf Schick stated that tires are performing well in terms of profitability, with the winter tire business getting off to a good start.
The company posted consolidated sales of $10.3 billion for the third quarter.
Global adjusted EBIT for the company was $918.5 million.
Continental’s ContiTech division faced challenges during Q3 2024 due to continued weak industrial development in Europe and North America.
Continental does not expect the industrial business to recover during the fourth quarter of 2024.
The company adjusted its sales and earnings outlook for ContiTech, lowering overall sales expectations for the Continental Group.
Continental Tire the Americas LLC continued to invest in its U.S. operations during Q3 2024.
The company launched two consumer tires: the Continental TrueContact Tour54 all-season touring tire and the Grabber H/T all-season touring tire.
Continental launched its first electric vehicle-compatible TBR tire, the Conti HAU 5.
The company opened its 24th BestDrive commercial tire location in Charlotte, N.C.
Continental announced its first fully owned distribution center, a 753,000-square-foot facility in the Dallas, Texas, metro area.
Two new iterations of the ContiConnect medium truck tire management tool were rolled out in the U.S.

canonical_name: Continental Tire the Americas LLC Sumter Plant
facts: Continental Tire the Americas LLC has broken ground on the planned expansion of its Sumter, S.C. tire plant.
The expansion will increase the facility's tire production capacity from approximately 4 million units by 2016 to approximately 8 million units by 2021.

The second phase of construction at Sumter will help meet the increasing demand for Continental and General brand tires from both its aftermarket business and original equipment orders from automotive manufacturers.
The plant manufactures both passenger and light truck tires.
Continental will more than double its existing employment of 700 to 1,600 jobs at the facility.
The company expects approximately 800 people to be at work inside the plant by the end of 2015.
Jochen Etzel, CEO of Continental Tire the Americas, stated: "This announcement celebrates Continental’s trust in the Sumter facility to continue delivering quality products to our customers."
Altogether, Continental will invest more than $500 million into the Sumter plant and create about 1,600 new jobs.
With the expansion, Continental has met the total projected investment of more than $500 million announced in October 2011.

South Carolina Governor Nikki Haley stated: "We couldn’t be more grateful for Continental’s continued commitment to its facility in Sumter and to our state. Continental is a huge reason our state produces more tires than any other state in the country, and we’re excited to celebrate a bright future for the partnership between South Carolina and this great company."
Continental continues to partner with readySC and local technical colleges to assist with staffing and training interested candidates.
The company is developing tailored apprenticeship programs for mechatronics and production positions in the facility.
During the nine-month construction project, contractors will add about 720,000 square feet including a new production hall and expanded warehouses for raw materials and finished goods.
The expansion is in addition to 1 million square feet in the existing plant.
The finished goods warehouse will be expanded to nearly one-and-a-half-times its original size.
Production at the Sumter plant began in October 2013.
Continental has invested more than $1.5 billion in the past five years for manufacturing, technology, facilities and product development in the U.S.
The company has recently expanded production capacity in Russia, China and Europe to better serve global demand on a long-term basis.
source_title: Continental begins Sumter plant expansion | 2015-09-10  | Modern Tire Dealer

facts: In 2023, Continental AG’s overall global sales grew 5.1% and its net income grew by more than 1,600%.
Tire sales for 2023 totaled $15.40 billion, down 0.3% from $15.45 billion in 2022.
Continental’s tires sector accounted for about 33% of the company’s overall sales and is its second largest of four sectors.
The company recorded global sales of $45.7 billion in 2023, up from $43.4 billion in 2022.
Continental’s net income was $1.2 billion in 2023, up from $73.5 million the prior year, representing a 1,635% increase.
The company noted its tires business delivered stable profitability despite weak tire replacement markets in Europe and North America.
CEO Nikolai Setzer stated that 2023 was a challenging year due to geopolitical conflicts, war, weak tire replacement markets, and inflation impacting economic development.
Setzer noted that four out of five new cars are equipped with hardware and software solutions from Continental.
Setzer stated that the world’s 10 highest-volume manufacturers of electric vehicles all trust in Continental tires for their original equipment.
Continental is referring to 2024 as “the beginning of our era of execution,” focusing on electrification, digital services, and ultra-high-performance tire growth.
The company plans continued investment to strengthen its setup, particularly in APAC (Asia Pacific) and the Americas.
Continental expects the passenger and light truck replacement tire market in North America to be flat to up 3% in 2024.
Continental expects the commercial tire market in North America to return to positive in 2024, with replacement market gains of 2% to 4%.
Continental estimates a slip in commercial vehicle production in North America between 4% and 6% for 2024.
Continental’s sales outlook for 2024 includes global sales of between $45.2 billion and $48.5 billion.
The company expects its tire sales to range between $15.4 billion and $16.5 billion in 2024.
In Q4 2023, Continental’s global tire volumes were up 1.7%, supported by short-term demand in the replacement market.
In Q4 2023, Continental recorded a negative effect from price/mix, down 1.1%, driven by low demand in truck tire replacement.
For Q4 2023, Continental’s tire sales totaled $3.9 billion, down 2.5% over the final quarter of 2022.
source_title: Continental's Tire Sales Slipped in 2023 | Modern Tire Dealer

facts: Continental has announced the retirement of Dr. Jochen Etzel, Chief Executive Officer of Continental Tire the Americas, LLC, effective May 1, 2025.

Tansu Işık will succeed Dr. Etzel as Head of Continental’s Business Area Replacement Tires the Americas and will report directly to Christian Koetz, Executive Board Member of Continental and Head of Continental Tires.
Dr. Etzel has served in his capacity with Continental’s tire business in the Americas since 2011.
Dr. Etzel played an important role in uniting various Continental tire activities in the region and strengthening their competitive position, contributing to the company’s overall growth and success.

Dr. Etzel began his career as a lawyer in Continental’s corporate law department before taking over a financial manager position responsible for developing the company’s tire business outside of Europe and leading the corporate Mergers and Acquisitions team.
Christian Koetz stated that Jochen’s impact on Continental’s tire business has been immeasurable and thanked him for his leadership, dedication, and outstanding contributions throughout his tenure.
Tansu Işık brings more than 20 years of international experience in marketing, supply chain, strategy, and key account management within Continental’s tires business.

Işık began his career as a Business Development Specialist in Istanbul, Türkiye, before moving to Continental Tires’ Headquarters in Hanover, Germany, to become Head of Pricing for the Europe, Middle East and Africa (EMEA) region.
Işık subsequently moved to Shanghai, China, as Director and then Vice President for Key Account Management (OE) China and the Asia-Pacific region (APAC).
From 2017, Işık worked as Head of Marketing for Continental’s replacement tire business in the EMEA region before leading the Strategic Business Development and Global Marketing organization.

Işık currently serves as Head of Region South & Channels Fleet and OE Trailer for Continental replacement tire business in the EMEA region, overseeing business operations in multiple countries and leading the truck tire business there.

Işık is responsible for the retread and digital solutions business in the EMEA region and holds an Executive MBA from Koç University along with trainings at INSEAD, Duke University, and Ashridge University.
Continental was founded in 1871 and generates sales of €39.7 billion, currently employing around 190,000 people in 55 countries and markets.
The Tires group sector offers a premium portfolio encompassing car, truck, bus, two-wheel, and specialty tires as well as smart solutions and services for fleets and tire retailers.
In fiscal 2024, the Tires group sector generated preliminary sales of €13.9 billion.
Continental's tire division employs more than 57,000 people worldwide and operates 20 production and 16 development sites.
Press contact for Continental Tires – The Americas is Allison Zora, Head of Brand and Public Relations, located at 1794 MacMillan Park Drive, Fort Mill, SC 29707.
ContiConnect Tire Monitoring Integration delivers actionable tire insights for every trailer in a fleet and is seamlessly integrated with leading telematics provider Samsara.
ContiConnect digital tire management provides valuable insights into truck or bus tires to allow for safer, more cost-efficient fleet operations.
source_title: Continental Announces Leadership Transition in its Replacement Tires Business in the Americas

facts: On October 23, 2023, Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Daechang Seat Corp. USA will invest $72.5 million in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
The facility is located in close proximity to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
The project will create over 500 jobs.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that suppliers for the HMGMA project alone reach at least nine counties, and DSC is being added to the growing manufacturing base centered around Chatham County and the Savannah region.
Daechang Seat Corp. (DSC) was established in 1979 in Kyungju, South Korea.
Globally, DSC employs more than 5,000 people across seven countries.
The company’s production process includes fully automatic lines with arc welding and spot-welding machines.
Jinsuk Lee, CEO of Daechang Seat Savannah Corp., expressed appreciation to the State of Georgia, its local communities, and the City of Savannah for their support.
DSC's primary objective is to drive growth through facility expansions to transform into a global entity.
Presently, DSC holds a prominent partnership with Hyundai Transys and Hyundai Mobis at HMGMA.
DSC hopes to collaborate with even more projects in the future.
DSC’s new facility will be located at the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
The company will hire for assembly operators, equipment technicians, mechanical engineers, and electrical engineers.
Hiring will occur through local job fairs and website postings in the future.
Griff Lynch, Georgia Ports Authority President and CEO, noted the facility is only minutes from the Port of Savannah, ideally located to leverage Georgia’s world-class connectivity.
The Georgia Ports Authority aims to provide the best global gateway for U.S. supply chains and welcomes DSC as the latest job creator in Georgia’s automaking sector.

Trip Tollison, Savannah Economic Development Authority President and CEO, announced that Daechang Seat Corp. USA will be the third Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America supplier to locate in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
The project brings 500 new jobs and $72.5 million in capital investment to Chatham County.
Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy Alyce Thornhill represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The GDEcD partnered with the Savannah Economic Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start for this project.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that countless partners are helping transform Georgia into a center of the automotive and mobility industry.
Chatham County has leveraged Savannah's status as an established global gateway to create a bustling community for residents and workers.
As the market transitions to electric vehicles, Georgia has pursued the entire supply chain.
Georgia has created more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs since 2018.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
Daechang Seat Co. is recognized for its quality and technological expertise in the automotive seat sector.
DSC has recently ventured into the electric vehicle sector and continues to expand its scope.
The completion of new manufacturing facilities will provide larger installation spaces, anticipating greater opportunities for growth.

facts: Hyundai is building a new $7.6 billion EV assembly plant in the US state of Georgia.
South Korean tier one supplier Daechang Seat has announced investment to localise production alongside Hyundai.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) broke ground on its facility in October 2022.
The HMGMA facility is located near the port of Savannah.
Hyundai is building a battery plant alongside the production lines on the 1,200-hectare site.
Construction has begun on all production-related buildings at the site.
Oscar Kwon, CEO of HMGMA, stated: “The site is advancing every day as we work diligently to complete what will be one of the most advanced vehicle assembly and battery plants in the world.”
Hyundai is on track to start production at the facility in early 2025.
José Muñoz noted that production might start sooner than early 2025.
In May, Hyundai announced a partnership with LG Energy Solution in a 50/50 joint venture for the battery cell manufacturing facility at the plant.
The battery facility will involve an investment of more than $4.3 billion.
The battery facility will have an annual production capacity of 30 GWh.
The 30 GWh capacity will be able to supply annual production at the assembly plant of 300,000 EVs.
Hyundai is eager to take advantage of incentives for localized EV manufacture offered by the US government’s Inflation Reduction Act (IRA).
The IRA provides tax credits that save EV buyers up to $7,500 on EVs made in North America with locally supplied batteries.
These incentives apply to pickup trucks and SUVs that sell for under $80,000 and sedans that sell for under $55,000.
Hyundai is second only to Tesla in EV sales in the US but has yet to establish EV production in the country supplied by local battery and parts content.
Hyundai currently benefits from IRA law allowing dealers to apply the credit to any leased electric vehicle regardless of where it is made.
Hyundai accelerated its plant production schedule, with executives stating they are doubling down and trying to accelerate the project as much as possible.
The original January 2025 production date may be pulled ahead by three months or more.
Hyundai signed a partnership with Georgia Tech aimed at strengthening research into hydrogen-fuelled vehicles during a press conference in Atlanta.
Supplier Daechang Seat committed $72.5 million to the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center, located in close proximity to HMGMA.

Griff Lynch, president and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority (GPA), stated that Daechang Seat is only minutes from the port of Savannah and ideally located to take advantage of Georgia’s connectivity.
Daechang Seat already operates a plant in Phenix City, Alabama, which opened in 2019.
Daechang Seat announced a $23.4m expansion at its Phenix City plant last year.
Sewon Precision Industry is investing $300m in a metal stamping plant as part of Hyundai's localized production commitments in Georgia.
Seoyon E-HWA plans a $76m plant to make interior and exterior trim parts for Hyundai in Georgia.
Hyundai Mobis is investing $926m in a powertrain plant for the project.
Joon Georgia is investing $317m in a plant to make vehicle frames.
Ecoplastic is investing $205m in a plant to make interior and exterior plastic parts, including bumpers, consoles, trims, and plastic moulds.

According to the Georgia Department for Economic Development, suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) scheme resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in fiscal year 2023.

facts: Gov. Brian P. Kemp announced July 20 that Denkai America will locate its new manufacturing facility and North American Headquarters in Richmond County.
Denkai America is the only North American manufacturer of high-quality electrodeposited (ED) copper foil used in printed electronics.
The company’s initial investment will be $150 million to establish an ED copper foil production facility focused on electric vehicle (EV) battery foil.
The facility will be located on a 115-acre site.
Denkai America aims to invest a total of $430 million and create 250 new jobs over the next five years.

Governor Kemp stated that Georgia is proud to be selected as the home of Denkai America, further strengthening the state’s status as a leader in emerging electric mobility and existing aerospace, defense, and technology industries.

Governor Kemp noted the state has been laser focused on bringing quality jobs to highly-trained Georgians and looks forward to seeing companies like Denkai thrive in Georgia’s top-ranking pro-business environment.
Denkai America’s materials have traditionally been used in printed circuit board (PCB) applications, primarily for the aerospace and defense industries.
The company's materials are also now used in lithium-ion batteries powering electric vehicles.
Operating for more than 60 years, Denkai has team members worldwide with operations in Japan and North America.
Heday Nakajima, Ph.D., Denkai Group President and CEO, stated the investment highlights their commitment to leading ED battery foil manufacturing in North America for many years to come.
Heday Nakajima expressed gratitude to the teams in Richmond County and the State of Georgia for making the project possible.
Nobu Masuda, Denkai America’s President and CEO, stated they are thrilled to expand their manufacturing presence into Augusta, which has the skilled workforce needed for growth.
Construction is expected to begin in September 2022 as the first part of a three-phase investment.
The facility will be fully operational by December 2024.

Steven Kendrick noted they expect Denkai to bring high-skilled jobs to the growing list of companies in Augusta and the Augusta Corporate Park.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated the State of Georgia is committed to harnessing the generational opportunity of electric mobility adoption to ensure Georgians in the automotive industry have access to future jobs at home.

facts: Denkai America, the US-based subsidiary of the Japanese materials company Nippon Denkai, announced its intentions to close and liquidate its copper foil manufacturing plant in Augusta, South Carolina.
The facility is the last such factory in the United States.
The news comes just two years after its parent company heralded the site as the future of foil production, with tens of millions of dollars in investments planned and hundreds of new jobs expected.

The company cited financial problems stemming from semiconductor shortages, a decrease in exports of batteries manufactured domestically, lower demand for smartphones, and other factors for the decision.
The continued losses, coupled with loan payments coming due and dried-up sources of new capital, have rendered the company insolvent.

Both the SK On/Hyundai partnership and the FREYR Battery facility are in the top five largest projects by investment in the state's history.
Battery recyclers Sung-Il Hitech and Denkai America also joined Georgia's growing electric mobility ecosystem.
The aerospace and defense industries continued to benefit from Georgia's logistics infrastructure and engineering talent pool, creating more than 1,500 new jobs.

Archer Aviation Inc., an aerospace company advancing sustainable urban air mobility, and Anduril Industries, a defense technology company, both announced facilities near airports and will also benefit from nearby top-ranked technical colleges and four-year universities.
Advanced manufacturing is a cornerstone industry sector in the state and has announced more than 7,700 new jobs since July 1, 2022.
Major investments or expansions announced thus far in FY 2023 include YKK AP in Macon and Beretta Holding S.A.'s new facility in Bryan County through its subsidiary Norma Precision Inc.

Germany-based Haering Precision celebrated the grand opening of its Lavonia facility in November with a two-day event, including a 'Future Day' for approximately 400 local high school students and members of the community.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Georgia Quick Start and GRAD certified sites are proven solutions that speak directly to the most important hurdles many companies face today.

GDEcD Deputy Commissioner for Global Commerce Kristi Brigman stated that companies connect with partners to solve workforce challenges and use the Georgia Quick Start program to build talent from the ground up.
The Georgia Quick Start program helps Georgians upskill, including those who are part of strong inbound domestic migration trends.

facts: Dinex designs and manufactures complete exhaust and aftertreatment systems for all EPA emission standards, from EPA 10 through EPA 21.
Heavy-duty trucks and buses in North America have been equipped with advanced emission technologies since the introduction of EPA 2007 standards.
New EPA phases up to EPA 2021 have further tightened regulations, transforming exhaust systems into sophisticated emissions control units.
Today's systems rely on a combination of filters, catalysts, sensors, and DEF (Diesel Exhaust Fluid) systems to significantly reduce harmful pollutants.
When operating properly, the exhaust from an EPA 21-compliant vehicle can be cleaner than the surrounding air in many urban environments.
Keeping the system functioning at peak performance requires regular maintenance, as early signs of trouble often point to underlying engine issues before they escalate.
Dinex offers a complete range of high-quality aftermarket parts to support all components of EPA 10, 13, 17, and 21 emission systems.
The company empowers customers with technical training, expert knowledge, and troubleshooting support for advanced emissions systems.
Dinex blends its global OE expertise and continuous innovation with the agility and competitive mindset of the aftermarket.
EPA 2004 regulations were the last set of standards that heavy-duty trucks could meet through engine optimization alone.
With the introduction of EPA 2007, Diesel Particulate Filters (DPFs) became necessary to capture fine particulate matter (PM).
By EPA 2010, most OEMs adopted Selective Catalytic Reduction (SCR) systems to meet strict new NOx limits.
Since then, the cost and complexity of emission systems have grown significantly while their environmental impact has been extraordinary.
Toxic nitrogen oxides (NOx) have been reduced by over 95%, and particulate emissions by more than 99% compared to levels from 30 years ago.
Dinex's 2025 School Bus catalog covers emission systems for the most popular EPA10-21 standards, containing 40 pages with 21 systems and hundreds of quality, competitive spare parts.
The Diesel Oxidation Catalyst (DOC) burns a wide range of pollutant gases in the exhaust stream and supports the Particulate Filter by helping it burn off trapped particles and soot.
A failed DOC can lead to significantly more frequent active regenerations, often caused by EGR coolant leaks or insufficient air supply within the system.
The Selective Catalytic Reduction (SCR) catalyst is where nitrogen oxides (NOx) react with DEF (Diesel Exhaust Fluid) and are reduced to harmless components.
The complete SCR system includes pumps, injectors, and sensors, and unfavorable operating conditions or poor maintenance of the DEF system can damage the SCR substrate.
New EPA standards coming in 2027 will target near-zero emissions across all driving conditions, tighten control over regulated pollutants, and capture ultrafine particles as small as 10 nanometers.
Dinex is working closely with leading global OEMs to develop the next generation of emission technologies for future readiness.
source_title: Emission - Dinex Aftermarket US

Product customers include manufacturers such as GM, Ford, Jaguar, Kawasaki, and John Deere.
Eaton developed the Roots style supercharger in 1949.
The company has produced over 5 million superchargers since 1988.
Eaton partners with local organizations including Athens Tech and the University of Georgia (UGA), as well as various non-profits.
Finished bolt-on products manufactured at the facility increase power and fuel efficiency to passenger cars.
The facility features state-of-the-art automated assembly lines, machining cells, an assembly process, and unit testing capabilities.

facts: Eaton Corporation announced plans to invest $340 million in a new manufacturing facility in Union County.
The project will create 700 new jobs.
The project represents the largest single economic development announcement in the county’s history.
The investment reinforces Union County’s position within the advanced manufacturing and automotive supply chain.
Eaton selected Union County following a competitive, multi-state site selection process.

Key factors for the selection included the availability of a large, modern facility, access to a skilled workforce, and the county’s ability to coordinate state, local, and utility partners quickly and effectively.
Union County worked alongside workforce and training partners, utility providers, and the South Carolina Department of Commerce to demonstrate readiness at scale.
The county helped secure adjacent property to allow for future expansion, signaling a long-term commitment to the project.
The investment brings significant economic impact to Union County by strengthening the local tax base and creating long-term employment opportunities for residents across the region.
source_title: Eaton Announces $340 Million Manufacturing Investment in Union County | Union County Development Corporation

facts: Eaton is investing $500 million to grow its North American manufacturing capacity.
The investment will expand production for the company’s utility, commercial, healthcare, industrial and residential markets.
Most investments will be completed in 2024 and 2025.
Eaton’s plans include expanding multiple manufacturing facilities, including in Texas, Wisconsin and South Carolina.
The company is also expanding distribution centers in Chicago and Dallas.
Mike Yelton, president of the Americas region electrical sector at Eaton, stated that electrical infrastructure has to work harder and smarter to accelerate decarbonization and electrification.
Yelton added that steady investments are being made for customers, distributors and employees to meet growing demand in the Americas, with more to come.
Eaton is doubling the size of its Nacogdoches, Texas manufacturing facility with a 200,000 square foot addition.
The Texas expansion gives the company twice the production capacity for voltage regulators, which are power unit components used by utility customers to modernize grids.
Eaton will continue producing multiple types of transformers at the Texas site to address an ongoing shortage of electrical grid products.
In Wisconsin, the company is expanding its largest facility in Waukesha.
The Wisconsin expansion increases production capacity for three-phase transformers for utility, data center, commercial and industrial use.
In South Carolina, Eaton will increase production of components for cable buses, called busways, to meet growing demand.
Eaton has a well-defined presence in South Carolina, operating in multiple locations including a site in Greenwood that was expanded in 2020.
The company is investing in distribution capacity to support its increased manufacturing power.
Eaton recently opened its largest regional distribution center in Chicago.
source_title: Eaton invests $500M in North American manufacturing | Manufacturing Dive

facts: Gov. Brian Kemp appointed Cobb County businessman Fitz Johnson to the Georgia Public Service Commission (PSC) to replace outgoing Chairman Chuck Eaton.
The PSC regulates Georgia’s public utilities and plays a major role in deciding what Georgians pay for electricity and other utility services.
The commission will decide how much of Plant Vogtle’s cost overruns should fall to Georgia Power’s customers.
Commissioner Tim Echols, the panel’s vice chair, plans to nominate Commissioner Tricia Pridemore to replace Eaton as chair.
Chuck Eaton is leaving the PSC this summer to become a superior court judge in the Atlanta Judicial Circuit.
Fitz Johnson is an Army veteran and Cobb County businessman who previously ran for state schools superintendent and has served on the State Charter Schools Commission.
The PSC is an all-Republican, five-member commission that regulates public utilities.
A pending lawsuit challenges the PSC’s at-large election system as unfair to Black voters, noting only one African American commissioner has served since statewide elections began in 1906.
Eaton earned his law degree by taking night classes at Georgia State University after being elected to the commission and recognizing its judicial nature.
Eaton was narrowly reelected in 2018, edging out Democrat Lindy Miller by about 2 percentage points.
Eaton represents Fulton, DeKalb, Clayton and Rockdale counties on the commission, with his term set to expire in 2024.
Gov. Brian Kemp stated that Fitz Johnson’s record of service, private sector leadership, and community dedication uniquely qualify him to serve on the PSC.
Commissioner Tim Echols noted that Chuck Eaton’s calm nature, judicial competency, and sense of humor made him a great Commission Chair.
source_title: Kemp picks new PSC member as Eaton leaves for superior court judgeship | News From The States

facts: Georgia Governor Brian Kemp announced that a manufacturing facility will be built in Bulloch County to help provide supplies to the new Hyundai plant in Bryan County.
Automotive parts manufacturer Ecoplastic Corporation will create 465 new jobs.
The company will invest over $205 million in the facility.
The company will produce auto parts like bumpers, consoles, trim and molding.
The facility will be located on Highway 301 in south Register, near Statesboro.
The facility is expected to begin operations by Oct. 2024.
Governor Brian Kemp stated the company will serve the Hyundai Metaplant well from Statesboro due to its close proximity to Georgia's ports system.
Ecoplastic Corporation is the second Hyundai supplier to choose Bulloch County for their production facility.
County development leaders have been working with different supply companies since soon after Hyundai’s announcement in May.

Benjy Thompson of the Development Authority of Bulloch County stated that the location is close enough to the mega site to be a good place for suppliers to locate, but far enough away to be attractive to them.
Developers have worked with the company for months to find them the right place, including both the land and the community to support them.
Billy Allen of the Development Authority of Bulloch County stated they have all the right components to attract companies and expect the same success with employees.
The announcement brings the number of supply jobs coming to Bulloch County to more than 1,000 so far.
More than 1,400 new industrial jobs have been created in Bulloch County in the past 11 months.
source_title: Metaplant America supplier to build manufacturing facility in Bulloch Co.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Ecoplastic Corporation will create 456 new jobs.
Ecoplastic Corporation will invest approximately $205 million in a new manufacturing facility in Bulloch County.
Ecoplastic Corporation is an automotive parts manufacturer based in Korea.
The company was established in 1984 and manufactures plastic automobile parts for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and other suppliers.
Ecoplastic is a Tier-1 supplier for Hyundai Motor Group (HMG) and Kia.
It manufactures automotive interior and exterior plastic parts, including bumpers, consoles, trims, and plastic molds.
Han Shang serves as the President and CEO of Ecoplastic Corporation.
The new facility will be located at 4822 Highway 301 South Register near Statesboro.
The plant will be capable of injection molding and painting.
It will produce exterior parts, bumpers and garnishes, interior parts, consoles, and trims for electric vehicles.
The company expects to begin operations by October 2024.
Hiring positions include HR, finance, parts development, quality control, facility maintenance, injection, and paint production.
Billy Allen is the Development Authority of Bulloch County Chair.
Alyce Thornhill is the Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy at the Georgia Department of Economic Development's (GDEcD) Global Commerce team.
The project was supported in partnership with the Development Authority of Bulloch County, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia EMC.
Pat Wilson is the GDEcD Commissioner.
The facility will serve the Hyundai Metaplant from Statesboro, leveraging close proximity to Georgia's ports system.
Since 2020, more than 30 EV-related projects have announced more than $21 billion in investment and over 26,300 jobs in Georgia.
On October 25, Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground in neighboring Bryan County on its first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility.
Offsite suppliers connected to the Hyundai project were predicted to invest approximately $1 billion in other locations around the state.
Ecoplastic Corporation was originally established in June 1984 as Apollo Ind Co., Ltd.
The company established its first Technical Research Center in 1987.
Ecoplastic was publicly listed on the Korean Stock Exchange in March 2000.
It currently employs more than 770 employees.
The company recorded sales revenue of more than $700 million in 2021.

facts: Ecoplastic America CEO Youngdoo "Thomas" Kim and his team are making downtown Statesboro their work home while awaiting the construction of their plant on 301 South.
Kim began his role in Statesboro in January 2024 after spending the last 20 years gaining expertise with global SECO affiliates, which is Ecoplastic America's parent corporation.

The Ecoplastic plant in Statesboro is the company’s first American subsidiary and represents an enormous investment by SECO to supply Hyundai and Kia electric vehicles with components, including for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
Kim envisions the plant becoming a building block for Bulloch County and a source of community pride where future generations will eagerly participate.
Ecoplastic designs and supplies the face of cars (bumpers), a process that takes between one year and six months to two years to design.
The company aims to become a key supplier while fostering job creation for the community and building a positive corporate culture.
Kim prioritizes treating employees as family, valuing a workplace culture of unity, resilience, and familial bonds where colleagues are respected.
Factory construction is scheduled to be complete in July.
Equipment installation will follow factory construction.
Until September, the company will focus on fostering quality through testing to produce defect-free products.
Starting October 1st, 2024, Ecoplastic aims to mass-produce bumpers for the IONIC 5 model.
In 2025, the company plans to produce bumpers for the IONIC 7 model.
By 2026, Ecoplastic aims to produce bumpers for TV and TE (electric pickup truck) models.
By 2027, Kim envisions a stable Ecoplastic operation in the region.
SECO is one of the top 5 suppliers for Hyundai/Kia in Korea.
Kim looks forward to welcoming Ecoplastic career applicants in the near future as the plant prepares for operations.

Ecoplastic Corporation is a Korea-based company established in 1984 that manufactures plastic automobile parts for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and other suppliers.

Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this project.
The project was executed in partnership with the Development Authority of Bulloch County, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia EMC.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that Ecoplastic has decades of experience as a supplier for Kia and Hyundai.

Fouts Bros designed a standardized 3000 Gallon Commercial Tanker in cooperation with partners W.S. Darley, UPF Poly-Tank, and Kenworth.
Through its customization options and small-town Customer Direct Sales Approach (CDSA), the company has manufactured and delivered over 1000 tankers worldwide.
Fouts Defense was awarded U.S. Army and U.S. Navy contracts for eight units designated for the Army and six units for the Navy, with deliveries across the U.S.
The Shreveport Fire Department recently purchased a new Fouts Fire 3000 Gallon Tanker through dealer BFA of LA, Inc.
Stock apparatus is available for quick delivery when grant funding comes through, including for FEMA AFG Grant Applications with a deadline of June 22nd.
The Wrentham Fire Department utilizes a Fouts 3,000 Gallon Tanker powered by a Cummins L9 450 HP engine and outfitted with a Hale 1500 GPM Pump.
The company participated in the SEAFC/GAFC Conference in Savannah, hosting a welcome dinner at Starland Yard and displaying a Medium Duty Rescue at booth B2 with Williams Fire Apparatus, Inc.
The business is located at 138 Roberson Mill Road, Milledgeville, GA 31061, and operates Monday through Friday from 8am to 5pm.

facts: Established in 1952 as a family-owned fire apparatus manufacturer.
Headquarters located in Milledgeville, Georgia, with an additional facility in La Grange, Texas.
Specializes in the production of fire trucks, including tankers and pumpers, designed to meet the needs of fire departments and emergency services across the United States.
Employs approximately 36 workers.
Manufacturing process is optimized for cost-effectiveness to offer competitive pricing without compromising quality.
Developed a standardized line of fire trucks that includes models like the 2000, 3000, and 4000 Gallon Tankers, as well as the FB-94 Pumper and the Fouts FOUR.
Known for rapid delivery times and has built a reputation for delivering quality fire apparatus quickly and efficiently.
Has not reported any funding, indicating a stable, self-sustaining operation.
source_title: Inven 2026

facts: Fouts Defense announced that it has been awarded contracts through the Defense Logistics Agency (DLA) to supply a total of 14 FB-94 fire apparatus to support U.S. military operations.
The contracts include eight units designated for the U.S. Army and six units for the U.S. Navy, with deliveries scheduled to various locations across the United States.
The FB-94 is a flagship model within Fouts Fire’s line of dependable, standardized apparatus designed for rapid deployment, ease of operation, and long-term reliability.
These units are built to support critical response needs while maintaining the efficiency and value Fouts Fire is known for.
Scott Edens, president and CEO, Fouts Bros Inc., says the company is honored to be entrusted with supporting the U.S. Army and U.S. Navy.
Edens states the FB-94 was designed with mission readiness in mind, delivering reliability, standardized operation, and long-term maintainability.
Edens notes the awards reflect confidence placed in their team, manufacturing discipline, and ability to deliver dependable fire apparatus on time and without compromise.
Fouts Bros Inc. is a family-owned company since 1952.
The company continues to focus on providing high-quality, affordable fire apparatus with industry-leading delivery times.
The company’s standardized manufacturing approach allows for consistent quality while meeting the evolving needs of departments and organizations nationwide.

facts: Fox Factory Holding Corp. designs and manufactures performance-defining ride dynamics products for bikes, including traditional and motor versions.
Fox Factory Holding Corp. will move its headquarters to Georgia.
The company expects to create 800 jobs in Georgia.
The company expects to invest $50 million in new facilities in Georgia.
Hall County will be the location for Fox's new manufacturing, warehousing, distribution, and office space.
Hall County is home to Lanier Technical College’s newest campus, which is one of the most advanced technical college campuses in the southeast.
The first phase of the Hall County project will be completed in early 2020.
Fox Factory Holding Corp. is expected to relocate its corporate headquarters from Scotts Valley, California, to its existing offices in Georgia by the end of 2018.
It is not yet known when the jobs will come open.

facts: Fox Factory Holding Corp. is headquartered in Duluth, GA.
On February 10, 2026, the company announced a strategic board refresh and entered into a cooperation agreement with Engine Capital to enhance operational performance and profitability.
The board will appoint two new independent directors, including Alan L. Bazaar, CEO and Co-Chief Investment Officer of Hollow Brook Wealth Management LLC.
The second independent director will be identified by Engine Capital and approved by the Board, with expected expertise in manufacturing excellence and margin improvement initiatives.

A Board-level Transformation Committee will be formed to drive operational excellence and improve profitability, chaired by Sidney Johnson and including both new directors plus an additional existing independent director.

The Transformation Committee will oversee management’s execution of operational improvement initiatives targeting enhanced profitability, working capital efficiency, and disciplined capital allocation.
Chairman Dudley Mendenhall and Ted Waitman will retire from the Board effective at the company’s 2026 annual meeting of stockholders.
Jefferies LLC is serving as financial advisor to Fox Factory, while Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz is serving as legal counsel for the company.
Olshan Frome Wolosky LLP is acting as legal counsel to Engine Capital.
Fox Factory designs, engineers, and manufactures premium shocks, suspension systems, and components for leading powered vehicle and bicycle original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).
The company’s brand portfolio includes FOX, Marucci, and Method Race Wheels, serving professional athletes and enthusiasts globally.
Fox Factory supplies aftermarket products through a global network of retailers, distributors, and direct-to-consumer channels.
The company acquires complementary businesses to integrate engineering and manufacturing expertise beyond its core shock and suspension segment.
CEO Mike Dennison stated the board refresh reflects a shared commitment to unlocking value embedded in the business through stronger operational execution and enhanced financial performance.

Arnaud Ajdler, Managing Partner of Engine Capital, stated the company is well positioned to drive meaningful and sustainable long-term shareholder value with a sharpened focus on execution and disciplined cost management.

facts: Fox Factory Holding Corp. is a designer and manufacturer of performance-defining, ride dynamics products primarily for bicycles and powered vehicles.
The company expects to create up to 800 jobs and invest up to $50 million in new Georgia facilities, including the relocation of its corporate headquarters.
The project will utilize a 23-acre site to diversify the company’s manufacturing platform and provide additional long-term capacity to support growth in its Powered Vehicles Group.

CEO Larry L. Enterline announced the new state-of-the-art campus in Hall County, Georgia, stating it will allow the addition of an additional world-class manufacturing operation for the Powered Vehicles Group that they intend to be ISO9001 and IATF 16949 certified.
The new facility will increase the company’s ability to better serve its OEM customers and on-road aftermarket business.
Fox Factory Holding Corp. is expected to relocate its corporate headquarters from Scotts Valley, CA to its existing offices in Georgia by the end of 2018.
The first phase of the Hall County project is expected to be completed in early 2020.
When complete, the new facility will be used for manufacturing, warehousing, distribution and office space.
Gov. Nathan Deal stated that Georgia provides the business environment and skilled workforce for innovative companies like Fox Factory Holding Corp. to enjoy long-term success and compete worldwide.

Gov. Nathan Deal noted that by relocating its headquarters to Georgia and expanding in Hall County, the company will continue to enjoy the benefits of being the No. 1 state for business, including expansive logistics infrastructure and a low-tax climate conducive to manufacturing.
Gov. Nathan Deal added that Fox Factory Holding Corp.’s investment will produce meaningful results for Georgia and create even more opportunities in the Hall County community.
GDEcD Director of Advanced Manufacturing Mike Grundmann represented the Global Commerce Division in partnership with the Greater Hall Chamber of Commerce (GHCC) and Georgia EMC.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that the decision to locate in Hall County speaks volumes about the quality of the workforce and business-friendly environment, noting Georgia’s business assets make it the perfect home for manufacturers and expressing excitement about the many job opportunities being created for citizens.
source_title: Fox Factory Investing $50M In Georgia - Corporate Headquarters - Business Facilities Magazine

facts: Fox Factory Holding Corp. issued this financial report from Duluth, Ga.

The company designs and manufactures performance-defining ride dynamics products primarily for bicycles, on-road and off-road vehicles and trucks, side-by-side vehicles, all-terrain vehicles, snowmobiles, specialty vehicles and applications, motorcycles, and commercial trucks.
Fox Factory is a direct supplier to leading powered vehicle original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).
The company also supplies top bicycle OEMs and their contract manufacturers, and provides aftermarket products to retailers and distributors.
Net sales for the Powered Vehicles Group (PVG) increased by $13.6 million (12.4%) in the third quarter of fiscal 2023 compared to the prior year.
PVG net sales growth was primarily driven by strong demand in the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) channel, partially offset by the impact of the United Auto Workers (UAW) strike.

Net sales for the Aftermarket Applications Group (AAG) increased by $10.3 million (8.2%) in the third quarter of fiscal 2023, primarily due to revenue from the Custom Wheel House subsidiary acquired in March 2023.
Gross margin expenses included costs associated with retaining a skilled workforce as production slowed due to the UAW strike.

Full year 2023 sales guidance was adjusted to $1.43 billion to $1.47 billion, primarily due to the impact from the United Auto Workers strike and continued inventory destocking in Specialty Sports Group (SSG) markets.
Fox Factory reaffirmed its 2025 vision targeting $2 billion in sales and a 25.0% adjusted EBITDA margin.
source_title: Fox Factory Holding Corp. Reports Third Quarter Fiscal 2023 Financial Results and Announces Agreement to Acquire Marucci Sports

facts: Franklin County Industrial Building Authority (IBA) executive director Tonya Powers identifies manufacturing companies, particularly in the automotive sector, as top prospects for future industrial growth in the county.
An automotive parts manufacturing company is investing $29 million and creating 60 new jobs in Franklin County's industrial park.
Franklin County has a pipeline of prospects that are suppliers to SK Battery, which is located nearby.
The IBA purchased a 93-acre site on rail for a new industrial park capable of supporting a 1 million-square-foot building with combined manufacturing and warehousing components.
The Hartwell Railroad connects to CSX and Norfolk Southern railroads and runs directly across the front of the new 93-acre industrial site.
Sheet metal manufacturer Alliance Supply and Piping was sold a 20-acre site at Gerrard Road Industrial Park for a new product line, creating at least 30 jobs.
Carry-On Trailers is adding a larger facility in the Central Franklin Industrial Park as one of the area's largest employers.
Fox Factory manufactures performance products for ATVs, snowmobiles, and motorcycles in Franklin Springs and operates as a billion-dollar business.
Rocky Ridge custom-built trucks are manufactured in Franklin Springs.
Atlanta Rod and Manufacturing employs 100 people at its 200,000-square-foot Franklin County headquarters, producing 150,000 pounds of bolts daily (nearly 45 million annually).
Atlanta Rod and Manufacturing successfully lobbied the EPA to sunset a waiver allowing foreign fasteners in federally funded projects, securing domestic steel requirements for water infrastructure.
Franklin Springs mayor Levy "Lee" Moore is purchasing close to 300 acres for industrial use to attract technology-based and agriculture-based industry.
Emmanuel College (now Emmanuel University) serves as a local workforce pipeline, with new hires from the school and UGA supporting regional business growth.
Lavonia remains the commercial and industrial hub of Franklin County due to historical infrastructure foresight by previous administrations.
The county is building a 3 million-gallon-a-day wastewater treatment facility costing more than $30 million to support incoming industrial growth over the next 20 years.
source_title: Franklin County | Leveraging Growth - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: The project is billed as the largest economic development project in the history of Georgia.
It is an electric vehicle factory to be built by upstart automaker Rivian.
The factory could grow to be five times as large as the Pentagon.
It is expected to produce as many as 400,000 emissions-free trucks a year.
The project represents a transformational $5 billion investment.
It will create 7,500 new green jobs.
The initiative unites California-based Rivian and Republican Governor Brian Kemp to bring EV production to an area dominated by gas-guzzling pickups.
Opponents have held rallies, organized online, spread conspiracy theories, and threatened local officials.
The debate reflects broader environmental movement tensions between building new emissions-free infrastructure and preserving unspoiled lands.

Michael Burger, executive director of the Sabin Center for Climate Change Law at Columbia University, noted the trade-off is always case-by-case and NIMBYism sometimes wins out regarding solar, wind, and transmission projects nationwide.
Opponents fear the factory will contaminate groundwater.
Many disapprove of the lucrative public incentives offered to Rivian.
Residents worry the huge facility will change the area's bucolic character, increase light pollution, snarl traffic, and spur more development.
The opposition movement has spilled into the Georgia governor's race.
Former Senator David Perdue is challenging Governor Kemp in the Republican primary and has targeted him over the factory.
On March 1, Perdue held a rally in Rutledge, about 50 miles east of Atlanta near the planned factory site.
Perdue was introduced by opposition group leaders and argued the factory is a bad community fit and that Kemp sold out to special interests.
Perdue invoked George Soros, noting his hedge fund owns $2 billion in Rivian stock and he is a frequent conservative target.
Perdue stated: “We can grow the economy without selling out and giving our tax dollars to people like George Soros... We can invest in rural Georgia without kicking our communities to the curb.”
Representatives for Kemp and Rivian said they are sensitive to community concerns and that site selection and incentive packages were done properly.
Bert Brantley, deputy chief of staff to Governor Kemp and an official who helped lure Rivian, acknowledged the real impact on residents and stated they deserve answers to their questions.
James Chen, vice president of public policy for Rivian, said environmental concerns are misplaced and the community should celebrate clean economy jobs.
Chen described Rivian as an American company leading in technology and innovation that wants to operate in a green way.
JoEllen Artz, a 74-year-old retiree near the site and factory opponent, believes the project will wreck the local ecosystem and contaminate the aquifer in a well-water community.
Artz, a Republican supporting Perdue, said: “This company that makes green products is going to destroy what Mother Nature spent million of years putting together.”
The roughly 2,000-acre site is largely undeveloped and contains a 200-year-old house the company may help relocate.
Residents in the area hunt in the woods surrounding the site.
Jeanne Dufort, a local real estate broker supporting Stacey Abrams, attended the opposition rally wearing a supportive T-shirt.
Dufort, who has lived in the area for 21 years, is concerned the factory will irrevocably transform the community's small-town feel.
Dufort stated: “We’re not red or blue... We have carefully defined what we want this community to be, and this is not it.”
Some residents worry Rivian may fail and leave a vacant industrial site, citing its November public offering valuation of nearly $70 billion followed by a stock tumble.
Rivian reported revenue of $55 million and a net loss of $4.7 billion for the previous year, causing its stock to drop sharply.
Governor Kemp allocated $125 million in his proposed budget for land and training costs associated with the factory.
State and local municipalities are expected to provide hundreds of millions of dollars in tax breaks to Rivian over the years ahead.

Chas Moore, an auto repair shop partner, criticized the $125 million allocation to a California corporation while small-business owners lack similar options, stating government should not pick winners and losers.
Ray Austin, a landscaping business owner who attended the rally, expressed skepticism about EV viability for hauling heavy gear.
Austin stated: “I’ll never go electric on a vehicle because I can’t.”
Dena Astin, a kindergarten teacher, expressed concern about potential pollutants in lithium batteries used for EVs.
Astin noted: “There are problems with electric cars, just like there are problems with gasoline fueled cars.”
Bruce LeVell, a Georgia businessman and Trump adviser who introduced Perdue, cast the project as a Democratic effort to sway votes in an overwhelmingly Republican county.
LeVell claimed Soros has tremendous money backing the project and stated: “We don’t need George Soros involved in anything dealing with Georgia.”

Protests have grown vitriolic, including an incident where Edwin Snell excoriated officials at a public meeting and kicked his hat at them, calling the area rural rather than an industrial waste dump.
A Facebook group with over 3,000 members serves as a clearinghouse for negative articles about Rivian and Kemp, with commenters launching personal attacks on local project officials.
Shane Short, executive director of the Development Authority of Walton County, and his family faced online threats, prompting him to skip a planned public meeting.
Short acknowledged people say things they don't mean when angry or anxious but does not believe the factory will change the community's character.
A recent Georgia Chamber poll found that two out of three aware residents in the area support the project.
Short anticipates many local residents will go to work for Rivian and views the factory as a local economic boon.
Chen stated Rivian plans to mitigate environmental impact by using recycled water instead of well water, minimizing light pollution, and blending buildings into the landscape.
Chen asserted: “We believe we will have zero impact on the drinking water aquifers.”
Opponents used GoFundMe to raise nearly $20,000 for their campaign and legal help.
Governor Kemp's budget is expected to be approved despite opposition.
Construction on the site was set to begin in the summer following the March 2022 article.
Rivian expects to begin producing trucks at the facility in 2024.
Dufort stated that stopping the project would require political pressure on the governor, shifts in public opinion, or Rivian pulling out.
source_title: How an Electric Truck Factory Became a Lightning Rod in Georgia — New York Progressive Action Network

facts: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies, in conjunction with its aftermarket division Corteco, has acquired Alto Products Corp.
The acquisition bolsters Freudenberg’s position in the North American and global aftermarket, broadening product and service offerings.
Alto Products Corp. operates a 270,000-square-foot manufacturing facility in Atmore, Alabama.
Alto maintains sales and distribution locations in Mexico, the Netherlands and China.
Alto employs approximately 240 people, with 230 based in the United States.
Alto is known for producing friction plates, steel reaction plates and comprehensive kits for automatic transmissions.
Alto serves the automotive, heavy-duty and marine segments.

Dr. Christian Dickopf, senior vice president of Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies’ Corteco division, stated that the acquisition expands their product portfolio and reinforces their commitment to delivering comprehensive, high-quality solutions to customers.
The acquisition enables FNST to offer complete transmission rebuild solutions by integrating Alto’s friction and steel plates into new product offerings.
This development fosters growth in heavy-duty and recreational vehicle segments and enhances vertical integration.
The move improves risk management and strengthens supply chain resilience through Alto’s U.S.-centric manufacturing.
The acquisition expands FNST’s reach into international markets by leveraging Alto’s presence in Latin America and Asia.
This creates opportunities to consolidate product lines and operations across FNST’s global footprint.

Judy Love, North American vice president of the Corteco division, stated that the partnership strengthens their ability to deliver comprehensive solutions to customers across North America and globally.
Judy Love expressed excitement about welcoming Alto’s skilled team into the Freudenberg organization.

The acquisition marks a strategic move for Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies, reinforcing its commitment to long-term growth, operational excellence and customer-focused innovation in the transmission aftermarket sector.
source_title: Freudenberg-NOK Acquires Alto Products Corp.

facts: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies announced the recipients of its 2016 Supplier Excellence Achievement Level (SEAL) Awards.
Freudenberg-NOK runs the business operations for Freudenberg Sealing Technologies in the Americas and is a leading global specialist in sealing applications across multiple industries.

The SEAL awards are now in their 10th year and recognize raw material and metal fabrication partners who have consistently maintained excellent standards in areas like quality, delivery, cost management, technology and customer service.
The company awards three levels of recognition – Gold, Silver and Bronze – to its top-performing suppliers.
Chemical raw material supplier The Chemours Company (Wilmington, Del.) received a 2016 SEAL Award Gold.
Chemical raw material supplier Solvay Specialty Polymers business unit (Alpharetta, Ga.) received a 2016 SEAL Award Silver.
Chemical raw material supplier H.B. Chemical Corporation (Twinsburg, Ohio) received a 2016 SEAL Award Bronze.
Metal & Metal Fabrication supplier Headly/Tryson Group (Broadview, Ill.) received a 2016 SEAL Award Gold.
Metal & Metal Fabrication supplier Rima Manufacturing Co. (Hudson, Mich.) received a 2016 SEAL Award Silver.
Metal & Metal Fabrication suppliers Elgiloy Specialty Metals and Combined Metals (Bellwood, Ill.) received a 2016 SEAL Award Bronze.

Michael Miller, VP, Americas, Strategic Sourcing / Purchasing at Freudenberg-NOK, stated that the company focuses on technical excellence, superior quality, and value for customers, and that their supply base is critical to achieving this level of service.

Suppliers supporting Freudenberg-NOK’s business operations in the Americas are ranked against an annual performance scorecard to identify top achievers and to encourage adoption of continuous improvement and lean operations.

Freudenberg-NOK’s internal continuous improvement and lean program, Growtth (Get Rid of Waste Through Team Harmony), is a manufacturing benchmark in the industry and incorporates lean supply chain practices as part of its foundation.
source_title: Freudenberg-NOK announces supplier excellence achievement award winners | Sealing & Contamination Control Tips

facts: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies announced it will increase its testing capabilities with the addition of a new materials laboratory at its Findlay, Ohio, manufacturing plant.
The new laboratory is part of an overall plant expansion taking place in 2015.
The lab will provide the company’s Fluid Power Division with new material characterization, failure analysis and mechanical property test capabilities.
The lab will consolidate and expand an existing engineering test laboratory at the facility.
Findlay’s expansion, announced last month, will add 43,000 square feet to the existing factory.
The new lab will account for approximately 2,000 square feet of the total expansion.
The new lab represents an investment of approximately $450,000 in equipment and building expenses.
The entire project will create 25 new jobs at the Findlay plant.
The entire project represents an investment of approximately $8.6 million in construction and equipment upgrades.
The expansion will be complete by winter 2015.
The new laboratory will perform analysis and testing of parts the plant is making from high-performance plastics.
Parts include thrust washers, seals and other components used in the automotive industry.
The company is installing a new engineering test rig in the lab that will measure torque loss.
Freudenberg-NOK says the data will help further improve custom engineered sealing solutions that are reducing CO2 emissions and improving fuel economy.
The Findlay expansion will result in creation of 25 new jobs including chemist, chemical engineer and quality technician positions.
The company worked with the state of Ohio, including the Findlay-Hancock County Economic Development authority, Jobs Ohio and the Regional Growth Partnership on workforce incentives.
source_title: Freudenberg-NOK Expands Material Science Capabilities With New Lab At Findlay Plant

facts: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies has announced plans to expand its automotive sealing facility and hire new employees to meet increasing customer requirements for quality components.

Freudenberg-NOK’s Findlay, Ohio, plant produces approximately 700 high-performance plastic parts for customers including vehicle manufacturers and Tier One and Two suppliers using state-of-the art manufacturing processes.
Company executives recently officially announced that the company will pursue a 43,000-foot factory expansion that could result in retaining 148 jobs and creating 25 new jobs at the plant.
Final plans are contingent upon formal approval of all State of Ohio workforce and incentive programs.
Freudenberg-NOK leaders have worked with JobsOhio, state and local officials on incentives to support the expansion.
The company said it expects to invest approximately $8.6 million in construction and equipment upgrades.
The expansion and construction will be complete by winter 2015.

Dr. Theodore Duclos, president, Freudenberg-NOK, stated that the Findlay facility is a benchmark for lean manufacturing and production of quality components so it is not surprising that escalating customer demand offers tremendous new opportunities for business expansion in Ohio.

Dr. Duclos said they are happy to invest in the success of this facility and the greater Findlay community with this construction, and anticipate that the expansion will help serve existing and new customers with quality sealing products.
Freudenberg-NOK and its Findlay operations are considered a top employer in Hancock County.
The plant was a finalist in the IndustryWeek 2014 Best Plants competition last year, which annually recognizes the best manufacturing plants in North America.
The Findlay plant maintains nearly perfect statistics in the areas of quality, delivery and customer retention.
Findlay has become the company’s lead facility in the successful adoption of a new breakthrough manufacturing approach, Single Cavity Net Shape Molding.
The single cavity approach uses compact, custom-engineered one cavity injection and compression molding machines rather than multi-cavity machines to produce crucial thermoplastic parts.
The process improves part quality and reduces scrap and waste.
Roy Schroeder, Lead Center manager, Fluid Power Automotive, Findlay, said the project is a tremendous testament to the skills of their people and the success of their processes in Findlay.

Schroeder stated the plant expansion will allow them to continue their pursuit of world-class manufacturing processes and products and expand markets, adding that they are excited about the opportunities this addition will bring to the facility and looking forward to its completion and the addition of new employees to the plant family.
source_title: Freudenberg-NOK Plans To Expand Facility In Findlay, Ohio

facts: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies supports the Chrysler Pacifica and Honda Ridgeline with a wide variety of engineered powertrain sealing components.
The Chrysler Pacifica and Honda Ridgeline were named the 2017 North America Utility Vehicle and Truck of the Year winners, announced at the North America International Auto Show in Detroit.
Freudenberg-NOK provides gaskets, O-rings, polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) slides, boots, and oil, valve stem, steering damper, and water pump seals to the Pacifica and Ridgeline.

The company supplies components to the majority of vehicles designated as semi-finalists in the 2017 NACTOY competition, including those produced by General Motors, Ford, Audi, Hyundai, Mercedes Benz, Toyota, Porsche, Jaguar, Kia, Nissan, Honda, Fiat-Chrysler, and Volvo.
Matthew Portu serves as President of Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies.
The 2017 NACTOY award for Car of the Year was presented to General Motors' Chevrolet Bolt EV electric vehicle.
Freudenberg-NOK is engaged in developing new materials and components to support the needs of the e-mobility sector.

Freudenberg-NOK has partnered with the U.S. Department of Energy in 2015 to help establish a national network of electric vehicle charging locations by installing six plug-in stations at its headquarters in Plymouth, Mich.
The company's core business principles include sustainability, responsibility, leadership, and a long-term orientation.

Freudenberg-NOK is a leader in the research and development of materials to address powertrain challenges such as wear and friction, better fuel economy, lower emissions, and the use of alternate fuels.

facts: Dana Incorporated announced a definitive agreement to combine with Eaton’s Mobility business in a transaction valued at approximately $5.1 billion.
The combination creates a global powertrain systems company with an enterprise value exceeding $10 billion.
The combined entity has estimated 2026 pro forma revenue of approximately $11 billion.
The transaction is structured as a Reverse Morris Trust.
Eaton shareholders will own at least 50.1% of the combined company following the close.
Dana shareholders will own approximately 49.9% of the combined company following the close.
The combined company will continue operating under the Dana Incorporated name after the transaction closes.
The combined company will remain listed on the New York Stock Exchange.
The deal is expected to close in the first quarter of 2027, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals.

The combination brings together Dana’s powertrain, thermal, and sealing technologies with Eaton Mobility’s commercial vehicle transmissions, engine and emissions systems, and electrification technologies.
The expanded portfolio will serve commercial and light vehicle manufacturers as well as aftermarket customers.
On a fully synergized basis, the combined company is expected to generate approximately $1.7 billion in adjusted EBITDA in 2026.
Management expects an adjusted EBITDA margin of about 15% for the combined company in 2026.
Management expects to achieve $250 million in annual run-rate synergies within 24 months after closing.
Synergies will be achieved through purchasing efficiencies, manufacturing optimization, engineering improvements, and lower structural costs.
Dana’s updated 2030 revenue target is now $14 billion to $15 billion.
Dana’s updated 2030 adjusted EBITDA margin target is approximately 18%.
Dana’s updated 2030 adjusted free cash flow margin target is 8% to 9%.
These updated financial targets are significantly above the company’s previous targets.
R. Bruce McDonald will serve as Executive Chairman of the combined company, overseeing integration and synergy realization.
Byron Foster will become Chief Executive Officer of the combined company.
Timothy Kraus will remain Chief Financial Officer of the combined company.
The board of directors will include all current Dana directors along with three representatives designated by Eaton.
Following completion, the combined company is expected to maintain a strong balance sheet with approximately 1.2 times net leverage on a fully synergized 2026 basis.

Byron Foster, Incoming Chief Executive Officer of Dana Incorporated, stated that the transaction marks an important milestone in Dana's transformation and positions it as a leading provider of powertrain solutions.

Byron Foster noted that expanding presence in core markets with new products and complementary technologies enhances the ability to deliver greater value to customers while strengthening margins through a more balanced portfolio and meaningful synergies.
Byron Foster emphasized bringing together highly skilled teams whose expertise will drive future success and accelerating the execution of the Dana 2030 strategy.
Paulo Ruiz, Chief Executive Officer of Eaton Corporation, stated that they are pleased to have reached the agreement, which delivers significant value to Eaton and its shareholders.
Paulo Ruiz noted the agreement further aligns their existing portfolio with powerful megatrends and supports Eaton’s 2030 growth strategy to lead, invest, and execute for growth.
Paulo Ruiz expressed confidence that the highly complementary combination will drive meaningful value for customers, employees, and shareholders alike.

Timothy Kraus, Chief Financial Officer of Dana Incorporated, stated that the transaction meaningfully enhances Dana's long-term financial outlook and enables significantly increasing the Dana 2030 targets.
Timothy Kraus noted that prior targets included approximately $10 billion in sales, 14% to 15% adjusted EBITDA margins, and a 6% adjusted free cash flow margin.

Timothy Kraus stated that after funding the approximately $1.1 billion cash distribution to Eaton, they expect to maintain a strong balance sheet with approximately 1.2x net leverage on a pro forma 2026 estimated basis.
source_title: Dana To Combine With Eaton's Mobility Business
