facts: The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) released a study on March 7, 2022, showing that by 2030, nearly half of medium- and heavy-duty trucks will be cheaper to buy, operate, and maintain as zero emissions vehicles than traditional diesel-powered combustion engine vehicles.
The study was published by the DOE’s National Renewable Energy Laboratory (NREL).
Continued improvements with zero emission vehicle and fuel technologies will enable clean trucks to become cheaper and more readily available over the next decade.
Increased use of battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and fuel cell electric vehicles (FCEVs) within the trucking industry will support the decarbonization of America’s transportation sector.
The findings advance President Biden’s goals to accelerate the adoption of electric vehicles, address the climate crisis, and bolster domestic manufacturing.

U.S. Secretary of Energy Jennifer M. Granholm stated that DOE is showing a clear pathway for trucking companies to switch from diesel to electric to cut costs and pollution while combatting climate change.

The Biden Administration’s approach aims to make clean transportation a reality by reducing exposure to volatile fuel prices, investing in American manufacturing, and creating a national charging network to support more electric vehicles on the road.
Medium- and heavy-duty vehicles account for less than 5% of the vehicles on the road but produce over 20% of the emissions from the transportation sector.
The transportation sector currently accounts for more than one-third of U.S. greenhouse gas emissions.
Key findings suggest that cost competitiveness of zero-emissions medium- and heavy-duty vehicles can largely be achieved by 2035.
The costs of zero-emission electric medium- and heavy-duty trucks will be the same, or lower, than those of diesel trucks.
Battery electric trucks are expected to become cost-competitive for smaller trucks before 2030.
Heavy trucks with less than 500-miles of range are projected to be cost-competitive by 2035.

Hydrogen fuel cell electric vehicles are expected to become cost-competitive for long-haul heavy-duty trucks with greater than 500-mile range by 2035 due to advancements for fuel cells and clean hydrogen production.
The report follows the release of DOE’s sweeping supply chain report that lays out dozens of critical strategies to build a secure, resilient, and diverse domestic energy sector industrial base.
Supply chain improvements lead to more affordable batteries for all applications.
The Bipartisan Infrastructure Law is investing more than $7 billion into the supply chain for batteries over the next five years.
The Bipartisan Infrastructure Law also invests $7.5 billion to expand the nation’s electric vehicle charging network up to 500,000 charging stations.
Recently released guidance to states includes eligibility to support charging infrastructure for trucks.
In February 2022, DOE released two notices of intent for electric drive vehicle battery recycling and second life applications, as well as battery materials processing and battery manufacturing.

The 21st Century Truck Partnership is a long-standing collaboration among truck manufacturers, major suppliers, and interagency partners that recently launched a new electrification technical team focused on removing barriers to wide-scale truck electrification.
The Department is working directly with industry through the SuperTruck 3 program, which aims to reduce emissions of freight transportation through electrification.
The Million Mile Fuel Cell Truck (M2FCT) consortium aims to enable more than a million miles of heavy-duty truck use for next generation fuel cells.
DOE is working to ensure an effective integration of these vehicles in the grid.
source_title: DOE Projects Zero Emissions Medium- and Heavy-Duty Electric Trucks Will Be Cheaper than Diesel-Powered Trucks by 2035 | Department of Energy

facts: Hyundai has announced the commencement of production at its Metaplant America facility.
The Metaplant America facility is a state-of-the-art plant representing a significant investment in electric vehicle manufacturing.
The facility utilizes advanced robotics and eco-friendly materials to minimize its carbon footprint while maximizing efficiency.
The facility will create thousands of jobs in the local community.
The facility's operations aim to foster economic growth and support the transition to electric mobility.
The facility will produce a range of electric vehicles, including compact cars and spacious SUVs.
Hyundai's lineup at the facility will feature the latest in electric vehicle technology for performance, safety, and efficiency.
The Metaplant America facility plays a crucial role in Hyundai's strategy to produce high-quality, accessible electric vehicles.
Hyundai is focusing on research and development at the facility to enhance battery technology.
The facility will be used to expand Hyundai's electric vehicle offerings.
The opening of Metaplant America is expected to provide opportunities for skilled workers and support local businesses.
Hyundai plans to work closely with local organizations to ensure the facility's presence benefits the community.
The facility's FAQ section addresses common inquiries regarding production timelines, job opportunities, and sustainability commitments.
source_title: Hyundai's Metaplant America Begins Production | Norman Hyundai Blog

facts: The largest employers in the Auburn, Alabama area include Mando America Corporation.
Auto manufacturing is of increasing importance along Interstate Highway 85 to the east and west of Auburn.
Kia Motors has a large automobile factory in nearby LaGrange, Georgia.
Hyundai Motors has a large automobile factory in Montgomery, Alabama.
The auto manufacturing business and its suppliers have positively affected the local economy around Auburn, Alabama.
Automobile manufacturing is cyclical and adversely affected by increases in interest rates.
Decreases in automobile sales could adversely affect the Kia and Hyundai plants and their suppliers’ local spending and employment.

facts: The Telematics Services Agreement is dated October 31, 2007, and executed between Hughes Telematics Inc. (HTI) and Mercedes-Benz USA, LLC (MBUSA).
HTI maintains a principal address at 41 Perimeter Center East, Suite 400, Atlanta, Georgia 30346.
HTI operates in the business of providing telematics equipment and services.
MBUSA manufactures, distributes, markets, and sells automobiles throughout the United States and Puerto Rico through its vehicle manufacturing Affiliates.

HTI will provide PASS Services (personal assistance safety) and MBTA Services to purchasers and lessees of MCG Vehicles and LCT Enabled Vehicles equipped with factory-installed telematics communicators.
MBUSA will factory-install equipment capable of receiving the telematics services in its vehicles on the terms set forth in the agreement.
HTI must make its HTI Operational System fully operational and available to all Subscribers not later than the Commencement Date.
Services will be provided 24 hours a day, seven days a week, irrespective of where the Subscriber Vehicle is located within the Territory (50 states, D.C., and Puerto Rico).
HTI must ensure that PASS Services and MBTA Services meet or exceed specific service levels in Exhibit B and maintain reasonable competitiveness.
MBUSA will review HTI's written proposals for additional telematics services on a case-by-case basis in good faith.
The agreement covers Embedded Cellular Technology Devices installed as original equipment by MBUSA or its vehicle manufacturing Affiliates.
HTI Telematics Communicators may be provided through one or more Authorized Suppliers approved by MBUSA, with Hughes Network Systems, LLC specifically deemed an Authorized Supplier.
MCG Vehicles are defined as vehicles manufactured by or for MBUSA during calendar year 2010 or thereafter for sale or lease within the Territory.
The agreement outlines provisions for testing protocols and acceptance.
The agreement includes data security and privacy contact point requirements.
The agreement covers marketing plans, on-site engineering staffing, and access to Daimler premises.
The agreement establishes milestone requirements, intellectual property ownership rules, indemnification terms, confidentiality obligations, and dispute resolution procedures.
The agreement includes terms for post-termination transition assistance, subscriber agreements, billing, pricing for second and subsequent years, audit rights, and governing law compliance.
source_title: Telematics Services Agreement

canonical_name: U.S. Department of Energy Fiscal Year 2025 Technology Commercialization Fund Core Laboratory Infrastructure for Market Readiness Lab Call
facts: The U.S. Department of Energy’s Office of Technology Commercialization announced an investment of more than $35 million in federal funds towards 42 projects through the Technology Commercialization Fund Core Laboratory Infrastructure for Market Readiness Lab Call.
The selected projects will leverage over $21 million in cost-share from private and public partners, bringing total funding to more than $57.5 million.

Oak Ridge National Laboratory, Brookhaven National Laboratory, Georgia Institute of Technology, and Pioneer Energy are partnering to advance and validate a cost-effective, environmentally friendly catalytic technology for the direct production of methanol from stranded methane streams.

The methanol synthesis project integrates two innovations recently developed at Oak Ridge and Brookhaven National Laboratories with a highly selective and scalable sulfur tolerant catalyst that yields high purity methanol.
Artificial intelligence and machine learning will be used in the methanol synthesis project to predict and optimize conditions for accelerating scaled, commercially viable performance.
The methanol technology will be validated on a customized fixed bed reactor for a continuous scaled process intensification pathway.
Technoeconomic and life cycle analysis for the methanol project will be performed by Pioneer Energy and Georgia Institute of Technology to guide market deployment.

Argonne National Laboratory and PulseForge are collaborating to commercialize a rapid and energy-efficient photonic processing technology for ceramic lithium battery electrolyte material manufacturing.

The photonic processing technology for lithium battery electrolytes has demonstrated a tenfold reduction in energy consumption and a 200-fold increase in manufacturing speed compared to conventional ceramic sintering technologies.

Argonne National Laboratory and PulseForge will further increase the technology readiness level and adoption readiness level for the lithium battery electrolyte manufacturing system through intensive customer engagement and prototype manufacturing equipment construction.

Oak Ridge National Laboratory is partnering with zinc ion battery manufacturers and a university to improve aqueous zinc ion battery cycling performances at the pouch cell level based on developed aqueous electrolytes.

The zinc ion battery project will assemble and test 1Ah multi-layer zinc ion pouch cells with different energy density levels, identifying failure mechanisms through non-destructive, real-time electrochemical analysis approaches.

Performance of the aqueous zinc ion pouch cells will be improved by optimizing design factors such as electrode porosity/mass loading, anode thickness, and electrolyte amount, as well as operation conditions like external pressures and fixture designs.
Ames National Laboratory is developing an injection molding-based technology for the in-situ fabrication of non-rare earth MnBi bonded magnets during the motor assembly process.
The magnet fabrication project converts fine MnBi powder with a particle size of less than 10 μm into anisotropic granules with a particle size of 300±100 μm for handling in air.
The injection molding approach for non-rare earth magnets aims to enable cost-effective, large-scale production of high-performance rare-earth free industrial motors.

Oak Ridge National Laboratory is leveraging patented technology to manufacture novel, lower energy and cost feedstock composite materials incorporating domestically sourced carbon fiber from existing waste material streams with more than 75% reduced embodied energy and cost.

Recycled carbon fiber recovered from automotive, aerospace, and energy generation scrap will be used as a lower-cost, lower-energy filler for newly developed thermoplastic composites to overcome fiber length degradation caused by conventional re-manufacturing.

An industrial demonstration at the end of the recycled carbon fiber project will prove that the newly produced feedstock materials meet all mechanical and thermomechanical performance of virgin materials while reducing overall cost and embodied energy.
source_title: Fiscal Year 2025 CLIMR Projects: Commercializing Energy Technologies | Department of Energy

facts: Alabama Power is an investor-owned utility (IOU) that operates in Alabama.
The utility provides incentives related to alternative fuels and vehicles.
A residential EV Time-of-Use (TOU) Rate incentive is available for fuel prices.
No residential infrastructure incentives are currently offered.
No residential vehicle incentives are currently offered.
A non-residential EV Time-of-Use (TOU) Rate incentive is available for fuel prices.
A non-residential EV Direct Current (DC) Fast Charging Station Purchase Rebate is offered as an infrastructure incentive.
A non-residential EV Direct Current (DC) Fast Charging Station Installation Rebate is offered as an infrastructure incentive.
No non-residential vehicle incentives are currently offered.
Alabama Power operates in the Birmingham-Hoover Metropolitan Statistical Area.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Alabama Power

facts: Murata, a global leader in advanced electronics, will join industry leaders at CES 2026 January 6-9 in Las Vegas.
Murata's booth location is Booth 4399 in West Hall at the Vehicle Technology & Advanced Mobility Pavilion.
Murata will spotlight how intelligent systems and precision sensing are reshaping mobility, wellness, and connectivity.
Featured Smart Mobility demos include Embedded RFID Tire Tag and Sensing.
Featured Smart Mobility demos include Gyro Compass.
Featured Smart Mobility demos include Headlight Leveling Using MEMS.
Featured Smart Mobility demos include Motion Tracing Demonstration Using Low Frequency Magnetic Fields.
Murata will showcase advanced Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and Ultra-Wideband (UWB) modules for embedded connectivity.
Connectivity demos highlight Wi-Fi Mesh for Range Extension.
Connectivity demos highlight Wi-Fi HaLowTM (Type 2HK/Type 2HL Module).
Connectivity demos highlight Type 2NR Module (Nordic nRF54L15 based).
Connectivity demos highlight UWB Smart Lock.
Connectivity demos highlight Indoor Location Tracking with Bluetooth Channel Sounding.
Connectivity demos highlight Satellite Position Tracking.
Health-Centric Sensing solutions on display include Molecular Analysis Platform 1, 2,3 (15-minute DNA/RNA analysis).
Health-Centric Sensing solutions on display include Vios Monitoring System (continuous hospital-grade telemetry and remote ECG).
Application example of the 3D substrate Multilayer LCP Product and the piezoelectric film sensor PicoleafTM (Smart Ring Demo) will be shown.
Ultrasonic Tech showcase includes Ultrasonic Cleaning Device (clear optics for AD/ADAS in any weather).
Ultrasonic Tech showcase includes Ultrasonic Tracking Powered by pMUT (low-power 3D/6DoF spatial tracking for XR).
Ultrasonic Tech showcase includes Airborne Ultrasound Transmission Metamaterial (bi-directional ultrasound through plastic/metal).
At the time of this announcement, some of the exhibit products are concept products or under development.
Specifications and appearance of exhibit products are subject to change without notice.
Murata Manufacturing Co., Ltd. is a worldwide leader in the design, manufacture, and sale of ceramic-based passive electronic components and 2NRs, communication modules and power supply modules.
Murata is committed to the development of advanced electronic materials and leading edge, multi-functional, high-density modules.
The company has employees and manufacturing facilities throughout the world.
source_title: Powered By Innovation: Murata to Showcase Intelligent System Technologies for Mobility, Wellness, and Connectivity at CES 2026

facts: Novelis Inc. announced it will invest $2.5 billion to build a new low-carbon recycling and rolling plant in Bay Minette, Alabama, USA.
The facility will have an initial capacity of 600 kilotonnes of finished aluminum goods per year.
Mr. Kumar Mangalam Birla serves as Chairman of the Aditya Birla Group and the Novelis Board of Directors.
Novelis serves markets including beverage can, automotive, aerospace, and specialties across all geographies.
More than half of the new facility's capacity will serve growing demand for aluminum beverage can sheet in North America.
Steve Fisher is President and CEO of Novelis Inc.
Novelis plans to efficiently expand capacity at this facility above the 600kt announced to capture ongoing strong demand.
The decision to build a fully integrated, greenfield recycling and rolling plant is backed by strong North American demand for flat-rolled, low-carbon aluminum from can makers and beverage companies.
Aluminum beverage cans have an average "can-to-can" lifecycle of just a couple of months.
A recycled can can be back on store shelves in as little as 60 days.
Ron Lewis is Ball Corporation's chief operating officer, global beverage packaging.
Novelis' new recycling and rolling plant will add domestic production of sustainable aluminum in North America while decreasing the carbon footprint of products.
The facility will be the first fully integrated aluminum mill built in the U.S. in 40 years.
It is expected to create up to 1,000 high-paying, advanced careers in modern manufacturing.
The plant aims to be net carbon neutral for Scope 1 and 2 emissions.
The facility will be powered primarily by renewable energy.
The facility will use recycled water and operate as a zero-waste facility.
The plant will rely on railroad transportation, reducing logistics-related carbon emissions by up to 70% compared to road transport.
The plant will make significant use of advanced automation and digital technologies, including artificial intelligence, augmented reality, and robotics.
With the new recycling center for beverage cans, Novelis will soon be able to recycle 90 billion cans globally, up from its current 74 billion used beverage cans.
Novelis has been working to develop circular economies for aluminum through state and federal public policies.
Novelis is partnering with customers and other stakeholders on new approaches that encourage and incentivize U.S. consumers to recycle more often.
John Murphy is Chief Financial Officer of The Coca-Cola Company.
The new low-carbon recycling and rolling facility will benefit the Coca-Cola system, its customers, and consumers while reducing environmental impact.
Site work is under way as of May 2022.
The company expects to begin commissioning in mid-2025.
In addition to the beverage can market, the facility will serve the automotive market, where aluminum is the fastest growing material as automakers pursue sustainability goals.
Novelis had net sales of $17.1 billion in fiscal year 2022.
Novelis is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited.
Hindalco Industries Limited is the metals flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group, a multinational conglomerate based in Mumbai.
Novelis operates throughout North America, Europe, Asia, and South America.
source_title: Novelis to Build $2.5 Billion Low-Carbon Aluminum Recycling and Rolling Plant

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp and First Lady Marty Kemp announced on June 11, 2024 that they are leading a delegation of state officials on an international mission to the Republic of Korea to reinforce strategic economic development partnerships.
The mission will focus on meetings with existing industries operating or under construction in Georgia, including Hyundai Motor Group, Kia, CJ Foodville, LG Group, SK Group, and Hanwha Qcells.
The state delegation consists of President Pro Tem John Kennedy, Representative Soo Hong, and Representative Lehman Franklin.
The delegation includes representatives from the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), Georgia Environmental Protection Division, and Georgia Ports Authority.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that meeting with industry leadership at their headquarters provides a unique opportunity to learn about their perspective on industry shifts and position Georgia at the forefront of new opportunities.
The State of Georgia has maintained continuous representation in Korea since 1985.
Korea’s Consular Office with jurisdiction across the Southeast, Puerto Rico, and U.S. Virgin Islands has been located in Georgia since 1976.
Korea has been one of the top sources of Foreign Direct Investment and international job creation in Georgia for the last three years.
In fiscal year 2023 alone, Korean companies announced more than $10 billion in investments across the state.
In fiscal year 2023 alone, Korean companies announced the creation of 12,605 jobs across the state.
Total trade between the State of Georgia and Korea totaled over $15.8 billion in calendar year 2023.
Trade with Korea accounted for nearly 10 percent of the state’s total trade in calendar year 2023.

Governor Brian Kemp stated: 'The Republic of Korea was the very first country I visited when I became governor for a good reason – because of the incredible partners located there,' and noted that Korean partnerships are bringing historic investments and jobs to communities across the state, calling Georgia the No. 1 state for business.
source_title: Governor and First Lady Kemp Reinforce Economic Partnerships in Korea | Georgia

facts: On May 5, 2022, the Georgia Department of Transportation (GDOT), The Ray, Kia Georgia, and Panasonic demonstrated a fully operational connected vehicle environment along 18 miles of Interstate 85 known as "The Ray Highway."

The demonstration utilized the Cirrus by Panasonic connected vehicle data management platform to detect real-time roadway events and communicate safety-critical messages to travelers about driving conditions.

The partnership between GDOT, The Ray, Kia Georgia, and Panasonic is focused on raising awareness of the life-saving potential of connected vehicle technology and deploying real-world use cases with measurable benefits.
GDOT and Kia Georgia vehicles equipped with vehicle-to-everything (V2X) technology were showcased during the demonstration.
GDOT can now deliver real-time situational awareness to drivers on equipped roadways in this key logistics and manufacturing corridor, a fast-growing region of West Georgia.
GDOT, The Ray, and Panasonic are providing "ride-along" experiences for stakeholders where drivers will be warned of hazardous conditions ahead on the roadway before they come into view.

These ride-along experiences showcase how connected vehicle technology can detect and warn drivers about weather events, work zones, hard braking, and vehicle crashes in a real-world highway environment.

The experience includes an in-vehicle visualization of traveler warnings, V2X technology capabilities installed on and along roadways, digital communication enabling connected roadway intelligence, and the Cirrus cloud platform's point of view for managing GDOT data to detect events and provide real-time alerts.

Russell McMurry, Commissioner of the Georgia Department of Transportation, stated that it is important for GDOT to lead in deploying transportation technology that benefits all drivers, helps move freight across the region more efficiently and safely, and advances Georgia as the technology epicenter of the east coast.

Russell McMurry emphasized that connected vehicle technology will save lives and expressed determination to see it come to life in Georgia as soon as possible through partnerships with Kia, The Ray, and Panasonic.
Stuart Countess, President and CEO of Kia Georgia, stated that Kia Georgia is proud to partner with the organizations while showcasing products built by the Kia team in West Point, Georgia.

Stuart Countess noted that customer safety is Kia Georgia's number one priority and described the V2X program as a forward-thinking initiative putting Kia at the heart of solutions where vehicles and infrastructure communicate seamlessly to enhance travel safety and efficiency.
Allie Kelly, Executive Director of The Ray, stated that connected vehicle technology creates an opportunity to increase travel efficiency and driver safety simultaneously.

Allie Kelly highlighted the responsibility to invest in physical and digital infrastructure necessary for connected and autonomous vehicles, noting that GDOT and The Ray have made those investments to modernize Georgia's transportation infrastructure.

Allie Kelly explained that these investments offer travelers real-time warnings about critical driving conditions such as crashes, construction work zones, hard braking, and weather impacts like black ice, flooding, and hydroplaning.
Chris Armstrong, Vice President of Cirrus at Panasonic's Smart Mobility Office, stated that the tech industry focuses on building technology with purpose rather than for its own sake.

Chris Armstrong expressed pride in partnering with mission-driven organizations (GDOT, The Ray, Kia Georgia) to solve transportation challenges by bringing perspectives from public, private, and philanthropic sectors united in improving safety.
Chris Armstrong emphasized the unique role each partner plays in championing the wide-scale adoption of this life-saving technology as quickly as possible.

Panasonic Corporation of North America is based in Newark, NJ, and is a leading provider of Consumer Lifestyle technologies, Smart Mobility, Sustainable Energy, Immersive Experiences, and Integrated Supply Chain solutions.
Panasonic is the principal North American subsidiary of Osaka, Japan-based Panasonic Holdings Corporation.
Panasonic was named one of Interbrand's Top 100 Best Global Brands of 2021.
Panasonic serves as a leading technology partner and integrator to businesses, government agencies, and consumers across the region.
source_title: Georgia Department of Transportation Demonstrates Safer, Connected Roadways with Kia Georgia and Panasonic Smart Mobility Technology on "The Ray Highway"

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd. (PAS) announced its appointment to the Governing Body of SOAFEE (Scalable Open Architecture for Embedded Edge), a global board dedicated to driving Software-Defined Vehicle (SDV) advancement.

Since joining SOAFEE as a Technical Steering Committee (TSC) member in 2022, PAS has demonstrated strong leadership in Open-Source Software (OSS) standardization efforts, particularly in the Asia-Pacific region.
PAS developed VirtIO-based solutions—Unified HMI and Virtual SkipGen—which align with SOAFEE's vision of hardware-agnostic software.
These solutions significantly contribute to promoting OSS that enables decoupled development of software from hardware in automotive systems, including next-generation cockpits.
PAS's solutions have already been adopted as SOAFEE blueprints to enable broader utilization and collaboration for future automotive software development evolution.
In November 2024, PAS assumed leadership of the newly established SOAFEE APAC Regional Hub, with operations officially launching in May 2025.
The SOAFEE APAC Regional Hub initiative aims to invigorate SOAFEE activities for members in the Asia-Pacific region, who represent a significant portion of SOAFEE's global membership.
PAS became the ninth Governing Body member among SOAFEE's current membership of over 150 companies.

Masashige Mizuyama, Executive Vice President and CTO of Panasonic Automotive Systems, stated that the company shares SOAFEE's vision to develop a seamless cloud-native environment for fast and continuous deliveries to automotive products and believes device virtualization will play a significant role in achieving it.

PAS will contribute to industry evolution by promoting collaboration with other standardization organizations, supporting global SOAFEE activities growth, and addressing opportunities and challenges in SDV and cloud-native development.
PAS was launched on April 1, 2022, as an operating company responsible for the automotive systems business under the Panasonic Group's operating company system.

On December 2, 2024, PAS moved to a management structure where 80% of its shares are held by funds managed by an affiliate of Apollo Global Management, Inc., and 20% by Panasonic Holdings Corporation.
As a Tier 1 company, PAS provides advanced proprietary technologies such as infotainment systems to automakers in Japan and overseas.
source_title: Panasonic Automotive Systems Joins SOAFEE Governing Body, Strengthening Global SDV Standardization Leadership

facts: Pirelli Tire LLC operates a manufacturing plant located at 100 Pirelli Drive in Rome, Georgia, United States, 30161.
Marco Verzino serves as the VP of NAFTA Quality for the facility.
David Ortoli holds the title of Market Quality Manager for the main office.
The Rome, Georgia plant produces tires, classified under Equipment Type ID 29376 with DOT Code 1JR (formerly JR).

facts: Goodie Nation announced the Goodie Nation Product Strategy Sprint Series in collaboration with Porsche Cars North America, Inc.
The initiative is a 90-day virtual program where management and related functional experts from Porsche will advise 20 founders around creating enterprise products.

Eligible startup spaces for participation include Customer Experience, Future of Work, Logistics & Mobility, Supply Chain & Manufacturing, Big Data, Analytics & Security, and Climate Tech & Sustainability.

Participants will experience virtual lunches with Porsche, weekly accountability group huddles, office hours, storytelling help from Goodie Nation, and invitations to appear on Goodie Nation podcast episodes.
Applications for the program are due by December 10.
Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, at One Porsche Drive, Atlanta, GA 30354 USA.
PCNA has been headquartered in Atlanta since 1998.
PCNA is the exclusive U.S. importer of the Porsche 911, 718 Boxster, 718 Cayman, Macan, Cayenne, Panamera and Taycan.
PCNA supports 193 independently owned and operated Porsche dealerships in the U.S., including supplying parts, service, marketing, and training.
PCNA is an indirect wholly-owned subsidiary of Porsche AG, which is headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany.
Porsche's racing heritage boasts some 30,000-plus motorsport wins to date.
Kjell Gruner serves as President and CEO of Porsche Cars North America, Inc.
Joey Womack serves as CEO of Goodie Nation.
Previous Goodie Nation mentorship programs have included collaborations with companies such as Verizon, AARP and Deloitte.
More than 300 founders currently have the support of Goodie Nation to drive their business.

source_title: Goodie Nation Announces Product Strategy Sprint Series in Collaboration with Porsche Cars North America, Inc.

facts: Deputy Assistant Secretary for Transportation Reuben Sarkar announced SuperTruck II, an $80 million funding opportunity subject to congressional appropriations.
The funding is designated for research, development, and demonstration of long-haul tractor-trailer truck technology.
The announcement was made at the GreenTruck Summit in Indianapolis, Indiana.
More than $12 million in selections were announced for three new cost-shared projects focused on plug-in electric powertrain technologies for medium- and heavy-duty vehicles.

Deputy Assistant Secretary Sarkar stated that improving commercial truck efficiency is critical to reducing petroleum consumption, strengthening the clean energy economy, and further reducing climate change contributions.
Sarkar added that the funding will accelerate innovation and foster rapid market adoption of new energy-efficient vehicle technologies.
The Department of Energy launched its SuperTruck initiative in 2010.

Vehicles developed under SuperTruck I are Class 8 combination trucks, commonly known as 18-wheelers, that dramatically increase tractor-trailer fuel, engine, and drivetrain efficiency through advanced technologies.
As the backbone of domestic freight transportation, 18-wheelers haul 70 percent of all freight tonnage.
SuperTruck II projects will research, develop, and demonstrate technologies to improve heavy-truck freight efficiency by more than 100 percent relative to a manufacturer’s best-in-class 2009 truck.
Emphasis for SuperTruck II is on technology cost-effectiveness and performance.
Achieving Class 8 truck efficiency increases requires an integrated systems approach to ensure various vehicle components work together.

SuperTruck II projects will utilize a wide variety of truck and trailer technology approaches, including improvements in engine efficiency, drivetrain efficiency, aerodynamic drag, tire rolling resistance, and vehicle weight.
Robert Bosch LLC (Farmington Hills, MI) will receive $5 million to develop and demonstrate a medium-duty plug-in hybrid vehicle powertrain that reduces fuel consumption by 50 percent.
Cummins Corporate Research and Technology (Columbus, IN) will receive $4.5 million to develop and demonstrate a Class 6 plug-in hybrid delivery truck that reduces fuel consumption by 50 percent.

McLaren Performance Technologies (Livonia, MI) will receive $2.6 million to develop a Class 6 delivery truck with a scalable, innovative, lightweight, low-cost, and commercially-viable plug-in electric drive system that improves fuel economy by 100 percent.
source_title: DOE Announces $80 Million in Funding to Increase SuperTruck Efficiency | Department of Energy

facts: U.S. International Trade Commission instituted an investigation on February 14, 2025, following a complaint filed on January 13, 2025, under section 337 of the Tariff Act of 1930.
A supplement to the complaint was filed on February 3, 2025.
Complainants include Syensqo SA of Belgium, Solvay Specialty Polymers USA LLC of Alpharetta, Georgia, and Solvay Specialty Polymers Italy S.P.A. of Italy.

The complaint alleges violations of section 337 based upon the importation into the United States, the sale for importation, and the sale within the United States after importation of certain polyvinylidene fluoride resins.
Alleged infringement involves claims 1-7, 10, and 12 of U.S. Patent No. 8,337,725 (the '725 patent).
Complainants allege that an industry in the United States exists or is in the process of being established as required by applicable Federal Statute.
Complainants requested that the Commission institute an investigation and issue a limited exclusion order and a cease and desist order.
The plain language description of the accused products defining the scope of the investigation is polyvinylidene fluoride polymers comprising acrylates in solid form.

Respondents named include Inner Mongolia 3F Wanhao Fluorochemical Industry Co. Ltd. (Fengzhen, Inner Mongolia Province, China), Zhejiang Juhua Co. Ltd. (Quzhou City, Zhejiang Province, China), Zhejiang Fluorine Chemical New Material Co. Ltd. (Shangyu Hangzhou Gulf Chemical Industry Zone, Shaoxing, Zhejiang, China), Hubei Fluorine New Materials Co. Ltd. (Qianjiang Economic Development Zone, Hubei Province, China), and Sinochem Lantian Co. Ltd. (Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province, China).
The Office of Unfair Import Investigations will not participate as a party in this investigation.
Named respondents must submit responses to the complaint and notice of investigation within 20 days after service by the complainant.

Failure of a respondent to file a timely response may be deemed a waiver of the right to contest allegations and may result in an initial determination, final determination, and issuance of exclusion or cease and desist orders without further notice.
The notice was issued on February 14, 2025, by Sharon Bellamy, Supervisory Hearings and Information Officer.

facts: Stryten Energy LLC is a U.S.-based energy storage solutions provider headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.
Stryten Energy announced an investment in REVKIT, a new digital platform that brings together a dynamic community of automotive enthusiasts.
Stryten will become the preferred provider of advanced automotive batteries on REVKIT’s digital platform.
Mike Judd serves as CEO and President of Stryten Energy.

“Stryten Energy’s investment in REVKIT expands our existing footprint within the automotive aftermarket to reach consumers looking for premium lead and lithium batteries to power their custom vehicles,” said Mike Judd.
“As the lead investor in this first round of funding, we will become the preferred provider of automotive batteries for the custom build kits featured on the REVKIT platform,” said Mike Judd.
Over 75 million vehicles in the U.S. are customized annually.
Over 70 percent of parts are installed by owners.
Almost 80 percent use social media to validate their purchases.
The capital investment from Stryten Energy will allow REVKIT to grow its platform, add features, and expand into other categories.
Dan Hart is the Co-Founder and CEO of REVKIT.
“We see how technology helps drive passion,” said Dan Hart.
“REVKIT bridges the gap between offroad enthusiasts and the ever-changing world of vehicle modifications,” said Dan Hart.
“Our user-generated content creates a powerful data warehouse and sales lead generator,” said Dan Hart.
“The funding from Stryten Energy will help generate greater awareness among automotive aftermarket retailers and introduce more businesses to this passionate community,” said Dan Hart.

Future enhancements to the REVKIT platform include expanded vehicle verticals, an enhanced peer-to-peer and B2C marketplace, streamlined B2C interactions via chatbots, and on-demand business intelligence for advertisers.
Stryten Energy provides energy storage solutions across the Essential Power, Motive Power, Transportation, Military, and Government sectors.
Stryten powers vehicles and equipment ranging from submarines to subcompacts, microgrids, warehouses, distribution centers, cars, trains, and trucks.
Stryten’s stored energy technologies include advanced lead, lithium, and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers, and energy performance management software.
REVKIT has been active since 2017 and has served over a million enthusiasts for vehicle modification inspiration.
REVKIT allows users to create virtual garages, itemize upgrades into over a dozen categories, track, find, and buy parts, and share build stories with pictures and videos.
source_title: Stryten Energy Invests in REVKIT to Expand Product Reach to Automotive Enthusiast Community

facts: E-Z-GO introduced the Liberty LSV and updated the Freedom RXV on June 6, 2023.
The Liberty LSV is a street-legal vehicle with a top speed of 25 mph that meets all National Highway Traffic Safety Administration standards for an LSV.
The Liberty LSV can be operated on public roads with posted speed limits of 35 mph or less in most states.
The Liberty LSV features four forward-facing seats, a compact wheelbase, a backup camera, operator protective structure, pedestrian warning system, seatbelts, and an optional infotainment system.
The Liberty LSV adheres to FMVSS 500 and SAE J2358 standards.
The Freedom RXV received a fresh new design for the 2024 model year featuring integrated LED headlights and a modernized ergonomic dash.
The Freedom RXV is available in two- and four-passenger models, with the four-passenger version including a modular canopy with a water management system.

Adam Harris, vice president of E-Z-GO for Textron Specialized Vehicles Inc., stated: "These new models answer our customers' call for vehicles that are equally at home on the golf course and in the neighborhood."
E-Z-GO vehicles are designed and manufactured by Textron Specialized Vehicles Inc., a subsidiary of Textron Inc. (NYSE: TXT).
The Liberty LSV is exclusively powered by Samsung SDI lithium batteries, which are part of E-Z-GO's ELiTE series.
Samsung SDI lithium batteries charge up to twice as fast as traditional lead-acid batteries and require no maintenance.
The Samsung SDI batteries in the Liberty LSV come with an 8-year warranty, described as the industry's longest.
The Freedom RXV is available with either Samsung SDI lithium battery technology or E-Z-GO's exclusive EX1 gas powertrain.
The EX1 gas powertrain is a first-of-its-kind engine purpose-built for golf-car applications to optimize fuel efficiency, featuring smart engine management that turns off accessories when not in use.
Both the Liberty LSV and Freedom RXV are manufactured in Augusta, Ga., where E-Z-GO has operated for 69 years.
E-Z-GO was founded in Augusta, Ga. in 1954 and became part of Textron Inc. in 1960.
E-Z-GO is known for innovative sustainable electric-vehicle and powertrain technology, with models including RXV, TXT, Freedom, Valor, and Express series.
Textron Specialized Vehicles integrates environmental performance into its business processes to reduce emissions.
source_title: E-Z-GO® LAUNCHES NEW VEHICLES -- FOR THE GOLF COURSE AND THE STREET

facts: The Embassy of the Czech Republic hosted an online talk with Monika Vintrlíková, CEO of Albaform, Inc., on September 19, 2023, as part of its "Women in Business" series.

Monika Vintrlíková heads ALBAform, a second-generation family business with 30+ years of experience in forming, welding, and assembling metal components for the automotive, agriculture, and e‑mobility sectors.
Albaform operates manufacturing locations in both the Czech Republic and the United States.
The company's U.S. facility employs over 150 employees.
Vintrlíková discussed the company's founding in 1992, its expansion into the American market, and plans for a future facility in Mexico.
She highlighted the strategic importance of locating manufacturing close to customers to minimize transportation costs.
She advised that Czech companies expanding to the United States should focus on site selection rather than expecting government incentives.
Vintrlíková noted that succeeding in the large American market requires heavy investment in the business.
She expressed a strong preference for the Southeast, specifically praising Georgia’s booming economy and its reliable workforce.
The company collaborates with local technical schools as well as the major universities around Atlanta.
Vintrlíková emphasized the importance of having support from Albaform’s Czech employees when starting out in Atlanta.
She noted the dual language capability of locally based employees to communicate with the factory in the Czech Republic.

Vintrlíková shared that her upbringing in the family business gave her the know-how to be successful as a woman in the automotive industry without facing setbacks while balancing work and family life.
Monika Vintrlíková currently serves as the Honorary Consul of the Czech Republic in Atlanta, Georgia.
The Czech Embassy collaborates with Vintrlíková to bring Czech delegations and companies to her jurisdiction, leveraging her knowledge of the American market.
The "Women in Business" series is part of the Czech Embassy’s Mutual Inspirations Festival 2023 – Eliška Junková.
source_title: Women in Business: Monika Vintrlíková | Embassy of the Czech Republic in Washington, D.C.

facts: Bonnell Aluminum is a subsidiary of Tredegar Corporation.
Tredegar Corporation trades on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol TG.
Brook Hamilton, President of Bonnell Aluminum, will retire in July 2026 after more than two decades of leadership and service.
Carl Czarnik has been promoted to Senior Vice President and General Manager of Bonnell, effective June 15, 2026.
Mr. Hamilton has led Bonnell since 2013, following a career with the company that began in 2005.

Over his tenure, Hamilton played a pivotal role in shaping the organization’s strategy, strengthening its culture, and building lasting customer relationships, helping position Bonnell as a respected leader in the U.S. aluminum industry.
Mr. Czarnik joined Bonnell in 2019 as Vice President of Operations.
As Senior Vice President and General Manager, Czarnik will be responsible for Bonnell’s profit and loss performance, operational execution, and strategic direction.
Prior to joining Bonnell, Czarnik served as Vice President of Operations at Gerdau Long Steel and Vice President of Human Resources for Gerdau North America.

Mr. Czarnik served in the U.S. military from 1987 to 1994, including active duty during Operations Desert Shield and Desert Storm, and was honorably discharged as a Captain after earning the Army Commendation Medal for his distinguished service.
Dr. Arijit (Bapi) DasGupta is president and CEO of Tredegar Corporation.

Dr. DasGupta stated that Hamilton’s leadership has been instrumental in building Bonnell into the strong, respected organization it is today, and expressed deep gratitude for his contributions over the years.

Dr. DasGupta added that Czarnik’s proven leadership, operational discipline, and track record of driving performance position him well to lead Bonnell forward and capitalize on the opportunities ahead.

Bonnell Aluminum is a leading manufacturer of custom-fabricated and finished aluminum extrusions serving the building and construction, automotive and transportation, consumer durables, machinery and equipment, electrical and distribution markets.
Branded product lines include TSLOTS by Bonnell Aluminum and Futura Transitions by Bonnell Aluminum.
The Company has manufacturing facilities located in Newnan GA, Carthage TN, Niles MI, Elkhart IN, and Clearfield, UT.
source_title: Tredegar Announces Bonnell Aluminum Leadership Changes

facts: Decostar manufactures parts for automobile manufacturers including Nissan, BMW, Hyundai, and EZ Go.
The company operates a facility in Carrollton, Georgia.
Decostar has operated at the Carrollton location since 2003.
On May 26, 2016, Region 4 EPA and the State of Georgia conducted a RCRA Compliance Evaluation Inspection at the facility as part of the Organic Air Emissions National Enforcement Initiative.
The inspection identified numerous generator violations including RCRA Organic Air Emission violations under Subparts BB and CC.

Violations included failure to conduct inspections, failure to maintain and operate the facility to prevent a release, failure to provide required training, and failure to establish emergency preparedness procedures.

Specific Subpart CC violations included failure to properly determine air emission control levels, failure to design and install fixed roof or closure devices, failure to monitor hazardous waste for leaks, failure to make repairs within 5 days, and failure to maintain records on tank defects and repairs.
Decostar reached a settlement agreement with the U.S. EPA to address violations of the Resource Conservation and Recovery Act.
As part of the settlement, Decostar will implement a leak detection and repair program to control hazardous air emissions from the facility.
The company will perform a supplemental environmental project valued at over $2.7 million.
The supplemental environmental project includes replacing existing robots and paint applicators with devices that increase transfer efficiency.
Decostar will pay a $377,900 civil penalty, with the remaining penalty amount mitigated by the equipment upgrade.

Within sixty days after the Effective Date of the Order, Decostar must develop a “BB Equipment Program” document to catalog regulated equipment, monitor recordkeeping activities, and implement a tracking program.
The environmental project provides for 4,130-pound reductions in annual hazardous waste generation.
The project also provides for 1,385-pound reductions in annual hazardous air pollution emissions.
Additionally, the project provides for 1,972-pound reductions in annual volatile organic emissions.
Decostar proactively eliminated volatile organic emissions detected during the inspection.
Andy Crossland serves as the Waste Enforcement Branch Chief for the EPA's Waste and Chemical Enforcement Division regarding this case.

links.context: Contact phone number for Andy Crossland

source_title: Decostar Settlement Information Sheet | US EPA

facts: Kumho Tire USA, Inc. announced the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with the Georgia Veterans Service Foundation and the Georgia Department of Veterans Service to formalize a long-term collaborative partnership supporting Georgia's approximately 680,000 veterans and their families.

The partnership spans private industry, state government, and nonprofits to identify and deliver programs creating meaningful impact for Georgia's veteran community, with specific initiatives to be announced as the partnership develops.

The partnership first came into public view earlier this year when GVSF representatives were honored as Co-Captains at Kumho Tire Night at State Farm Arena during an Atlanta Hawks vs. Boston Celtics game.

Edmund Cho, CEO of Kumho Tire USA, Inc., stated that the company believes doing business in America means giving back to America and looks forward to building something lasting for Georgia's veterans alongside GVSF.

Dr. Patricia Ross, Commissioner of the Georgia Department of Veterans Service, expressed pride in welcoming Kumho Tire USA as a committed partner to deliver on the promise of unwavering support for Georgia's veterans.
Joe Higgins, Board Chair of the Georgia Veterans Service Foundation, noted that private sector partnerships strengthen the foundation's work connecting veterans with resources and support.
Kumho Tire USA is the U.S. subsidiary of Kumho Tire Co., Ltd., a leading global tire manufacturer headquartered in Seoul, South Korea.

facts: SAFT America, Inc. operates a nickel-cadmium battery manufacturing facility at 711 Industrial Boulevard, Valdosta, Georgia.
The company entered into a settlement agreement with OSHA on March 19, 1993, to resolve litigation regarding the Occupational Safety and Health Standard for Exposure to Cadmium (29 CFR 1910.1027).
SAFT aims to reduce employee exposure to airborne cadmium below the action level for 70% of exposed employees and to or below the PEL of 5 ug/m(3) for all employees by December 31, 1995.
Employees currently work 12-hour shifts on three consecutive days and are off work for the next three consecutive days.

The parties agreed that SAFT will be in compliance with 29 C.F.R. 1910.1027(c) if an employee's time-weighted-average exposure over every consecutive eight-hour period during a work shift is 5 ug/m(3) or less.
SAFT must install dust-free despiraling by December 31, 1993.
SAFT must install dust-free pay-off at ECC 1 & 2 by December 31, 1993.
SAFT must install shrouds and particulate catch pans at six post "ready" stations on impregnation lines by December 31, 1993.
SAFT must install drying vessel particulate capture modifications for five impregnation vessels by December 31, 1994.
SAFT must install a central HEPA Vacuum System with drops at Impregnation Lines, ECC machines, and Despiral area by December 31, 1994.
SAFT must install a pneumatic seal on the mixing bowl in the Plastic Bonded Electrode (PBE) Mix Facility by December 31, 1994.
SAFT must install HEPA vacuum ventilation or an equally effective dust-removal method at the PBE decortication station by December 31, 1994.
SAFT must enclose and ventilate AA and Sub-C coilers (winders) by December 31, 1995.
SAFT must install dust control ventilation on all PSD Plate Preparation department equipment by December 31, 1995.
SAFT must install enclosures and ventilation on all PBD Electrode Assembly Department equipment by December 31, 1995.
SAFT must enclose and ventilate the PBD Electrode Assembly Department to the extent necessary by December 31, 1995.
SAFT must develop and implement a comprehensive preventive maintenance program for cadmium exposure control equipment as it is installed.
SAFT must discontinue the use of fans that may cause dispersion of cadmium dusts by March 14, 1993.
SAFT must clean the Chemical Plant to remove accumulated cadmium dust by December 31, 1994.
SAFT must clean the Assembly Plant to remove accumulated cadmium dust by December 31, 1995.
SAFT must establish a work practice team by March 22, 1993, to monitor work practice improvements and determine if additional modifications can further reduce employee cadmium exposure.
SAFT must install an air tunnel in the aisleway leading to the lunchroom by December 14, 1993, to remove cadmium dust from employees' clothing without dispersing it.
Employees are required to pass through the air shower prior to entering vending and cafeteria areas for break and lunch.
SAFT must require all employees exposed above the PEL to pass through the air tunnel at the end of their work shift.
If engineering and work practice controls do not reduce all employee exposures to or below the PEL by December 31, 1995, SAFT must comply with wet shower requirements by April 30, 1996.

OSHA will grant a permanent variance from the wet shower requirement if SAFT presents sufficient objective evidence that its air shower is as effective as a wet shower in removing cadmium from employees' hair and skin.
SAFT must provide exposed employees with a list of possible solutions within 15 days of receiving monitoring results showing exposure over the PEL.
Air monitoring results must be referred to the Work Practice Team, Supervisor, and Area Manager for assessment within 15 days.
SAFT must post a report in the affected employee area within 30 working days of receiving monitoring results, detailing findings and corrective actions or reasons for no action.
Lunchroom tables must be wet wiped before each use to maintain them free of cadmium.
SAFT may monitor each job category by sampling a representative fraction of employees expected to have the highest cadmium exposure.

Exposure monitoring is limited to between 7:00 a.m. and 7:00 p.m., unless variations in worksite conditions suggest higher exposures for night shift workers, in which case sampling must occur between 7:00 p.m. and 7:00 a.m.
All air monitoring samples must be sent to an outside accredited analytical laboratory for analysis.
After January 1, 1999, SAFT must fully comply with medical removal requirements for exposed employees.
Between March 22, 1993, and January 1, 1999, SAFT must either fully comply with medical removal requirements or follow a specific medical removal protocol based on biological monitoring test results.
Under the protocol, employees are removed to jobs with airborne cadmium exposure below the action level if B(2)-M levels exceed 300 ug/g Cr coupled with CdU above 3 ug/g Cr or CdB above 5 ug/lwb.
Employees are removed if CdB levels exceed 10 ug/lwb.
Employees are removed if B(2)-M exceeds 750 u/g Cr.

Employees are removed if CdU levels exceed 10 ug/g Cr between July 1, 1993, and July 1, 1995; 9 ug/g Cr between July 2, 1995, and July 1, 1996; 8 ug/g Cr between July 2, 1996, and July 1, 1997; and 7 ug/g Cr thereafter.
SAFT must notify OSHA of its choice between full compliance or the alternative medical removal protocol by July 1, 1993.

By April 14, 1993, SAFT must provide initial medical examinations covering specified subjects to each employee covered by the medical surveillance program who has not had a qualifying examination after April 14, 1992.
Any employee whose most recent examination showed a CdU level above 3 ug/g creatinine must receive a complete initial medical examination by April 14, 1993.
SAFT must provide all employees covered by the medical surveillance program with periodic medical examinations by December 31, 1993.
For employees who received a company-provided medical examination after April 14, 1992, SAFT must provide a complete initial or periodic medical examination within one year of their most recent exam.
If some employees remain exposed above the PEL after December 31, 1995, SAFT must provide a change room in full compliance with the standard by April 30, 1996.

Effective March 22, 1993, while not required to provide a change room immediately, SAFT must ensure employees remove and store protective clothing and equipment to minimize cadmium dispersion and prevent contamination of street clothes.
Robert Reich served as U.S. Secretary of Labor and Respondent in the proceeding.
Charles E. Adkins served as Director of Health Standards Programs at OSHA.
Edwin H. Seeger served as Attorney for SAFT America, Inc.
source_title: SAFT America, Inc. - 03/19/1993 | Occupational Safety and Health Administration

facts: NLR's lithium-ion (Li-ion) battery recycling supply chain research explores opportunities to strengthen and secure U.S. supply chains with detailed assessments, benchmarking, and analyses to identify gaps and ensure manufacturing resiliency.
Growing demand for Li-ion batteries is increasing demand for raw materials—such as cobalt, nickel, and lithium—that are not domestically produced or refined in sufficient quantities.
Li-ion battery recycling is one opportunity to enable supply chain resilience in the U.S and globally.

To understand how the intersection of different markets, operations, and policies will impact global electrification efforts, NLR researchers developed the LIBRA: Lithium-Ion Battery Resource Assessment Model.
NLR's analysis experts have been instrumental in understanding how the growing electric vehicle (EV) market will impact the Li-ion battery recycling supply chain.
NLR's research has informed numerous projects and policies that lay the groundwork for the battery recycling infrastructure of the future.
NLR researchers continue to track the global flow of different critical materials used in today's energy systems.
Data mapped for cobalt illustrates the movement of different materials throughout the Li-ion battery supply chain, from mineral extraction to EV application.
Expanding domestic mineral sources is emphasized as important, with Li-ion battery recycling highlighted as a way to bridge a critical gap in domestic access to battery materials.
NLR researchers recently used LIBRA to estimate how recycling scrap material from consumer battery and cathode manufacturing would impact the raw material supply for new manufacturing.

Manufacturing scrap materials will dominate the Li-ion battery waste stream until 2040 when end-of-life batteries from EVs become available for recycling, kick-starting the recycling industry and closing the materials gap for manufacturing.

Analysis demonstrates that manufacturing scrap and end-of-life batteries can be a substantial source of raw materials for domestic manufacturing, offering greater supply chain resiliency for U.S. battery manufacturers.
NLR researchers used LIBRA to evaluate the impact and importance of battery chemistry and sorting in the recycling supply chain.

Commercially available Li-ion batteries include a range of different chemistries, and findings show that automated sorting can help recyclers selectively process batteries based on the material makeup.
LIBRA emphasized how automated sorting will be particularly impactful for cobalt recovery before 2035, before low-cobalt battery designs become more popular in the marketplace.
At $23 per pound for cobalt in 2021, targeting high-cobalt chemistries can help incentive investments by making Li-ion battery recycling more profitable.
NLR published 'Impact of Automated Battery Sorting for Mineral Recovery from Lithium-Ion Battery Recycling in the United States' in Resources, Conservation, and Recycling (2023).
NLR published 'Battery Energy Storage Scenario Analyses Using the Lithium-Ion Battery Resource Assessment (LIBRA) Model' as an NLR Technical Report (2023).
NLR published 'Economics and Challenges of Li-Ion Battery Recycling from End-of-Life Vehicles' in Procedia Manufacturing (2019).
source_title:
Battery Recycling Supply Chain Analysis
 | Transportation and Mobility Research | NLR

facts: Hyundai Motor America is kicking off the Progress Project for the second year in honor of Black History Month.
The Progress Project by Hyundai provides financial assistance to black-owned small businesses in a region.
This year, the area of focus is Savannah, Georgia – home to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, a new dedicated EV and battery manufacturing plant currently being built in Bryan County.

Brandon Ramirez, director, corporate social responsibility and external relations, Hyundai Motor America, stated that Hyundai’s global vision is progress for humanity, which means giving back to the community where we live and work.
Hyundai selected three Savannah-based small businesses each receiving donations of $15,000.

The Culturist Union: A multi-functional coffee shop and artisan marketplace that hosts events, forums, and all things community for creatives in Savannah will use the donation to create an accessibility ramp at the entrance to ensure that everyone can enjoy the space.

Luxe the Salon: An upscale salon with a relaxing ambiance that specializes in natural hair, luxe, and hair extensions in Savannah will use the donation to update the store’s equipment, furniture, and other features.

Savannah Sauce Company: A community-centered company that makes delicious sauces, jams, and other locally sourced products in Georgia, inspired by the Savannah kitchen of owner Mike Roberson’s grandmother, Henrietta Smith, will use the donation to create a new ice cream flavor from one of the jams.

Savannah Economic Development Authority President and CEO Trip Tollison said it is remarkable to see the impact Hyundai Motor America is already making in our local communities and he is especially excited about the Progress Project.

Trip Tollison stated that the three businesses chosen represent different areas of the service industry that keep our communities running, and this is only the beginning of the community-wide impact Hyundai will create.
Hyundai Motor America focuses on ‘Progress for Humanity’ and smart mobility solutions.
Hyundai offers U.S. consumers a technology-rich lineup of cars, SUVs, and electrified vehicles.
Hyundai's 830 dealers sold more than 724,000 vehicles in the U.S. in 2022, and nearly half were built at Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama.
Hyundai encouraged the public to join its social media throughout February to learn more about the stories of the passionate individuals behind these businesses.
source_title: The Progress Project by Hyundai Commences for a Second Year, Supporting Black-Owned Businesses in Georgia – Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority

Inalfa Roof Systems has announced plans to open a manufacturing plant in Cherokee County, Ga.
The investment for the new facility is $17.1 million.
The new plant is expected to create 300 jobs.
Inalfa Roof Systems supplies sunroofs and open roofing systems for automotive manufacturers including Hyundai Kia, Ford, General Motors, Volvo, Chrysler Group, and BMW Group.
The new Georgia facility will be the company's fourth manufacturing location in the U.S.

facts: Inalfa Roof Systems will open a manufacturing plant in Cherokee County, Georgia.
The new facility is expected to create 300 jobs.
The project represents a $17.1 million investment.
The plant will be located in the Cherokee 75 Corporate Park off Interstate 75.
The site is approximately 30 miles northwest of Atlanta.
Inalfa manufactures vehicle roofing systems, including sunroofs and open roofing systems for automotive manufacturers.
Key customers include Hyundai Kia, Ford, General Motors, Volvo, Chrysler Group, and BMW Group.

Governor Nathan Deal stated that Georgia's central location in the Southeast, deep workforce resources, and logistics infrastructure have attracted well over 300 companies to the state's auto manufacturing sector.
The Cherokee County plant will be Inalfa's fourth facility in the United States.
The Cherokee County plant will be Inalfa's first facility in the Southeast region.
Inalfa's global headquarters are located in Venray, The Netherlands.
Inalfa has a worldwide workforce of about 3,000 employees.
Inalfa's North American headquarters is located in Michigan.
Inalfa operates a total of four plants in Michigan and Mexico.
Danny Colaris serves as vice president of North American Operations for Inalfa Roof Systems.
Colaris stated the company spent four years searching for a location in the South before selecting Cherokee County.
Colaris credited state and local economic development teams, as well as Georgia Power, for helping introduce the company to the site's strategic advantages.
The plant is expected to begin operating in January 2014.
Job opportunities at the new facility will include roles in engineering, operations, supply chain, purchasing, and manufacturing.
The industrial park along Interstate 75 is situated in close proximity to automotive manufacturers throughout the Southeast.

facts: Inalfa Roof Systems is a tier-one automotive supplier serving over 40 vehicle brands globally.
Its customer list includes Daimler Benz, BMW, Chrysler, Ford, General Motors, Volkswagen, Audi, Volvo, Hyundai, Kia, Honda, Land Rover, and Nissan.
The company delivers more than 2.5 million roof modules annually.
It specializes in complex automotive sunroof and reconfigurable roof systems.
Its operations include designing, developing, manufacturing, prototyping, testing, assembling, and supplying sunroof modules.
The company is headquartered in Venray, Netherlands.
It operates as a wholly owned subsidiary of Beijing Hainachuan Automotive Parts Co., Ltd. (BHAP).
BHAP is headquartered in Beijing, China.
Inalfa was established by J.A.M. Burgman in Utrecht, Netherlands in 1946.
Initially, the company manufactured aluminium household supplies including knitting needles, stair rods, and curtain rods.
The company moved to Venray in 1956 and began manufacturing metal profiles, stamped metal parts, and oil furnaces.
In 1974, the company began manufacturing automotive sunroofs, initially for Jaguar and Rover.
By 2011, the company had been owned by two Northern European mid-market buyout firms: AAC Capital Partners and its co-shareholder Parcom Capital.
In 2011, it became a wholly owned subdivision of Beijing Hiananchuan Automotive Parts (BHAP).
Inalfa is known for marketing complex roofing solutions, including the highly reconfigurable roofing of the Citroën C3 Pluriel.
It supplied the sliding rear cargo roof of the GMC Envoy XUV.
It supplied the fully retractable, all-glass panoramic roof of the second generation Lincoln MKZ.
Inalfa operates production facilities in the United States, Japan, Mexico, Brazil, South-Korea, China, and Poland.
Its global headquarters remains in Venray.
source_title: Inalfa - Wikipedia

facts: JTEKT's Cairo facility is the winner of Georgia's Automotive Supplier of the Year Award.
The award recognizes a supplier to an automotive OEM, at any tier, that demonstrates strong growth and projections, which positively impact the overall economic health of the state of Georgia.
This award is one of five the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) presented at the third annual Georgia Automotive Awards ceremony earlier this month.
The awards are designed to recognize automotive leaders throughout the state, and to honor them for the contributions they have made to build a robust automotive ecosystem in Georgia.
JTEKT North America’s Koyo Bearing plant in Cairo manufactures high-volume automotive components utilized in engine valve train and transmission applications.
The Cairo facility employs more than 400 associates in and around Grady County.
The facility produces upwards of 90 million high-quality bearings annually for global customers in the automotive and industrial segments.

Since 2014, JTEKT has invested nearly $30 million in the Cairo facility, installing the latest bearings equipment and technologies to continue to produce world-class products and attract the next generation of associates.
With additional manufacturing facilities in Dahlonega and Sylvania, JTEKT employs nearly 1100 Georgians who manufacture products for the aerospace, industrial and automotive industries.
Nominees in each of the five categories were voted on by representatives from the Georgia Automotive Manufacturing Association, Georgia Tech, Georgia Economic Developers Association and others.
These committee members embody a diverse cross-section of experts with a deep understanding of the automotive industry.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson said, “Congratulations to our 2018 Georgia Automotive Award winners.”

Wilson stated, “We have worked hard to cultivate the nation’s best business environment, and Georgia’s automotive sector continues to remain one of our top industries due to the vast resources and incredible workforce we have to offer.”

Wilson added, “The 2018 winners have had a significant impact on our state’s economy by creating jobs and investing in our communities, and have played a huge role in why are continually named the top state for business.”
source_title: JTEKT Koyo cairo plant wins Georgia automotive supplier of the year

facts: Kautex Textron GmbH & Co. KG (Kautex) is a subsidiary of Textron Inc. (NYSE: TXT).
Kautex announced a significant contract win with a leading global automotive OEM for the supply of a full-BEV thermoplastic composite lower battery housing unit.
The ordered unit is part of Kautex’s Pentatonic battery enclosure portfolio, specifically engineered for electric and hybrid electric vehicles.
The new order underscores Kautex’s growing influence in the e-mobility space and reinforces its position as a Tier One automotive supplier.
The lower battery housing will be used in a major BEV platform that spans several vehicle brands.
Designed using thermoplastic composite materials, the housing seals and protects the battery system while meeting rigorous structural and thermal performance standards.
Performance standards include resistance to crush and bottom impact forces, as well as external and internal fire safety tests.
Integration into multiple vehicle models demonstrates versatility and Kautex’s commitment to scalable EV solutions.
The Pentatonic product line includes lightweight, customizable EV solutions constructed from thermoplastic composites or composite-metal hybrids.
Thermoplastic composite construction enables weight reduction, contributing to improved vehicle efficiency and extended driving range.
Use of high-functionality thermoplastic materials allows significant part integration, resulting in a simplified bill of materials and lower overall cost.
The streamlined engineering reflects an industry trend toward multi-material platforms that blend cost-effectiveness, performance, and environmental sustainability.
Thermoplastic composite construction enhances corrosion resistance, ensuring long-term durability without compromising structural integrity.
Lower carbon footprint during production and vehicle operation supports global efforts to decarbonize the transportation sector.
This contract represents Kautex’s second major business win for the Pentatonic product line.
Kautex previously secured its first Pentatonic order in January 2023 for a full-BEV underbody protection skid plate, which went into serial production in 2024.
The Pentatonic platform has earned multiple industry accolades, including the Society of Plastics Engineers (SPE) Grand Innovation Award in Europe and China’s Lingxuan and Gasgoo Innovation Awards.
Kautex deployed three test vehicles featuring full-BEV and hybrid Pentatonic enclosures across different continents.
Test units are located in the United States, Japan, and Europe, collectively logging over 100,000 miles.
Trials serve as proof of concept for the robustness of Pentatonic enclosures under varying terrain and environmental conditions.
Joerg Rautenstrauch serves as President and CEO of Kautex.
Rautenstrauch stated the contract award marks a significant milestone and reflects dedication to providing cutting-edge solutions meeting highest standards of safety, performance, and sustainability.
Kautex is headquartered in Bonn, Germany, and operates as a Tier One automotive supplier with a global manufacturing footprint encompassing 30 plants in 13 countries.
Historically known for contributions to traditional and hybrid fuel systems, engine camshafts, and plastic industrial packaging.
Proactively expanded offerings toward electrification and automation as the automotive sector shifts.
Investing in development of thermoplastic composite and composite-metal hybrid battery systems, as well as advanced cleaning systems for assisted and autonomous driving.
Strategic pivot positions Kautex as a comprehensive partner for automotive manufacturers transitioning to next-generation vehicle platforms.
source_title: Kautex Textron Secures Major BEV Battery Housing Deal with Top Automaker - The Manufacture Data

facts: Kautex Textron GmbH & Co. KG (Kautex), a Textron Inc. company, has secured a new business award for its Pentatonic battery enclosure solution.
The award further strengthens Kautex's position in the growing electrified passenger vehicle segment.
The program is for a leading automotive OEM.
The award includes the full battery enclosure system – top cover and bottom tray including a thermal management solution.
The system is engineered for a multi-vehicle platform.
It is designed to meet the performance and integration requirements of next-generation hybrid-electric vehicles.
Production will be localized in North America.
Start of production is planned for 2028.
The enclosure will be manufactured using an advanced and highly automated production concept.
The award underscores Kautex’s ability to deliver complex, scalable, tested, and validated battery enclosure systems with a strong focus on sustainability, quality, and industrial efficiency.

It further strengthens the company’s Pentatonic portfolio and reinforces its role as a trusted partner supporting customers’ electrification strategies through innovative product design and manufacturing excellence.

Joerg Rautenstrauch, President & CEO of Kautex, stated: “We are thrilled to announce this latest Pentatonic award, which highlights our ability to deliver fully integrated battery enclosure systems at scale.”
Joerg Rautenstrauch added: “Through advanced automation and production, we are driving efficiency, quality, and innovation to support our customers’ electrification strategies.”
Kautex is a Tier One automotive supplier with 30 plants in 13 countries.
Kautex is a pioneer in the design and manufacture of traditional and hybrid fuel systems, engine camshafts and plastic industrial packaging solutions.

For the era of new mobility, Kautex is expanding its portfolio to offer thermoplastic composite and composite-metal hybrid battery systems as well as assisted and autonomous drive vehicle cleaning systems.
Textron Inc. is a multi-industry company that leverages its global network of aircraft, defense, industrial and finance businesses to provide customers with innovative solutions and services.
Textron owns brands including Bell, Cessna, Beechcraft, Pipistrel, Jacobsen, Kautex, Lycoming, E-Z-GO and Textron Systems.
source_title: Kautex Secures New Business Award for Pentatonic Battery Enclosure Solution, by @businesswire

facts: Truck Hero, Inc. announced the strategic acquisition of Lund International Holding Company.
Lund is a portfolio company of Highlander Partners, LP.
Lund is based in Buford, Georgia.
Lund is a leading designer, manufacturer, and marketer of branded automotive accessories for passenger cars, light trucks, SUVs, CUVs, Jeeps, and heavy trucks.
Transaction terms were not disclosed.
The acquisition adds a complementary product portfolio to diversify Truck Hero's product category mix and increases exposure to a larger addressable market of vehicles.
Lund International was founded in 1965, with roots dating back to the 1920s.
Lund started in Anoka, Minnesota before expanding operations.

Lund offers highly functional, protective, and stylish products in over 30 categories under brands including AMP Research, AVS, Belmor, Bushwacker, LUND, Rampage Products, RoadWorks Manufacturing, Roll-N-Lock, Stampede Automotive Accessories, and TonnoPro.
Bill Reminder serves as President and CEO of Truck Hero.
Mitch Fogle leads the Lund team that built the company's portfolio.
Joe Scharfenberger is a Managing Director at CCMP Capital.
Jeff L. Hull is President and CEO of Highlander Partners.
This transaction marks Truck Hero’s fifteenth acquisition since its formation in 2007.
The acquisition aligns with Truck Hero’s growth strategy of acquiring vehicle accessory businesses to diversify its product portfolio into high-growth segments.
Ropes & Gray LLP advised Truck Hero and CCMP on the transaction.
Katten Muchin Rosenman LLP advised Lund and Highlander on the transaction.
Jefferies provided financing and served as advisors to Truck Hero.
Truck Hero is headquartered in Ann Arbor, MI.
Truck Hero provides a full range of truck bed covers, truck caps, bed liners, floor liners, steps, suspension kits, Jeep parts, off-road accessories, and operates the online retailer RealTruck.
Truck Hero’s industry-leading brands include Advantage, A.R.E., BACKRACK, BAK, BedRug, Extang, Husky Liners, N-FAB, Omix-ADA, Retrax, Rugged Liner, Rugged Ridge, Superlift, TruXedo, and UnderCover.
Lund International’s product lineup includes Ventvisors®, hood shields, Powersteps™, floor mats, fender flares, tonneau covers, storage boxes, nerf bars, and running boards.
Lund offers products under the brand names Lund, AVS, Belmor, AMP Research, Rampage, Bushwacker, Roll-N-Lock, TonnoPro, and Stampede.
source_title: ​Truck Hero, Inc. Announces Strategic Acquisition of Lund International - Industry News - Magazine - Movin' Out
