facts: Novelis Incorporated (Atlanta, Georgia), a subsidiary of Aditya Birla Group (Mumbai, India), is in the planning stage for an aluminum recycling mill expansion in Greensboro, Georgia.
The expansion will add state-of-the-art equipment for recycling aluminum scrap.
This project has medium (70-80%) probability of moving forward as scheduled, with kickoff in October and completion in November.

Century Aluminum (NASDAQ:CENX) (Chicago, Illinois), the largest producer of primary (non-recycled) aluminum in the U.S., announced plans for increased production and capital investment at its primary aluminum smelter in South Carolina.
Century Aluminum announced on June 3 it plans to increase production at its Mount Holly Smelter in Goose Creek, South Carolina, by 50% in the next 12 months.
The company also plans to invest more than $60 million in capital projects and add over 100 new jobs, bringing the number of employees at the facility to over 400.
The producer struck an important power agreement in March with state-owned electric utility Santee Cooper, to run through the end of 2023.

This allowed Century Aluminum not only to continue operations, but to invest in a restart of 50% of the second potline, and an incremental expansion of production up to 170,000 tons per year is planned through 2023.
The Mount Holly smelter has a nameplate capacity of 235,000 tons per year, but has been operating only one of two potlines for years as it negotiated electricity prices with Santee Cooper.
In the U.S., electricity can account for up to 40% of aluminum production costs, according to the Congressional Research Service (CRS).
In 2020, U.S. production of primary aluminum totaled 1.0 million metric tons, reports the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS), well below peak domestic production of 4.7 million metric tons in 1980.
More than two-thirds of U.S. primary aluminum imports in 2020 were from Canada.

The economic downturn in 2020 caused by the COVID-19 pandemic contributed to shutdowns or reduced production by U.S. aluminum-consuming industries, reducing U.S. consumption of aluminum to 2.9 million metric tons, down 41% from 2018.
A recent study by the Economic Policy Institute indicates hope for primary aluminum production in the U.S.
In the two years from the March 2018 implementation of Section 232 and its 10% tariff on aluminum imports, to February 2020, production increased by 37.6% compared with the prior two-year period.
This increase was due to restarts or production increases at five of the six remaining smelters in the U.S.
At its pre-COVID peak, domestic aluminum production reached 1.14 million metric tons at an annualized rate, up from 741,000 in 2017.
Industrial Info is tracking $1.71 billion in aluminum projects that are planned for completion in the U.S. and Canada from June through December, including $802 million worth of smelting projects.
Canada accounts for most of the smelting investment.

Auminerie Alouette is performing a $474 million smelter modernization in Sept-Iles, Quebec, which involves a series of upgrades at the 575,000-ton-per-year smelter: converting an anode baking furnace from using heavy oil to natural gas, replacing diesel equipment and installing robots, among others.
Completion is expected in July.
Alcoa Aluminerie de Deschambault, a subsidiary of Alcoa Corporation (NYSE:AA) (Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania), expects to complete a $63.8 million smelter expansion in Deschambault, Quebec in October.
The expansion will add new equipment to increase production and improve efficiency at the facility.
Joe Govreau is Industrial Info's vice president of research for the Metals & Minerals Industry.
source_title: U.S. Aluminum Production Shows Promise Despite Current Downturn

facts: Novelis launched the "Recycle for Good" campaign challenging fans to recycle 3 million aluminum cans throughout the 2016 football season.

Novelis will recycle the collected cans locally at its Greensboro, Georgia recycling facility and convert the aluminum's value into funding an Atlanta Habitat for Humanity home in Atlanta's historic Westside community.
Novelis serves as the Official Recycling Partner of the Atlanta Falcons.
Sil Colalancia is the director of Recycling for Novelis North America.
Local campaign partners include Novelis, the Atlanta Falcons, Mercedes-Benz Stadium, Atlanta Habitat for Humanity, The Arthur M. Blank Family Foundation, and the Georgia Dome.
A drop-off location for cans will be hosted on the West Plaza by the Georgia Dome starting at the first Falcons home game on September 11.
Novelis will provide recycling bags in tailgate areas around the Georgia Dome and sponsor an internal sorting program to maximize can collection during all 2016 home games.
Rich McKay is the president and CEO of the Atlanta Falcons.

Can collection centers will also be established at Atlanta Habitat for Humanity headquarters and a soon-to-be-determined Westside location for contributions during away games and throughout the season.
Scott Jenkins is the general manager of Mercedes-Benz Stadium.
Mercedes-Benz Stadium aims to become the first professional sports stadium to achieve LEED Platinum certification.
Sil Colalancia stated that aluminum cans are the most valuable material in the recycling bin and that discarding them means throwing away money.
Aluminum beverage cans can be recycled from store-shelf to store-shelf in as few as 60 days.
Recycling aluminum cans saves 95 percent of the energy and greenhouse gas emissions associated with primary aluminum production and reduces landfill waste.
The value of 3 million recycled aluminum cans is sufficient to fund the entire construction of the Atlanta Habitat for Humanity home.
More than $70 million worth of aluminum goes to landfill in the state of Georgia each year.
Lisa Gordon is the president and CEO of Atlanta Habitat for Humanity.
Novelis Inc. is the global leader in aluminum rolled products and the world's largest recycler of aluminum.
The company operates in 11 countries, employs approximately 12,000 people, and reported $10 billion in revenue for its 2016 fiscal year.

Novelis supplies premium aluminum sheet and foil products to transportation, packaging, construction, industrial, and consumer electronics markets across North America, Europe, Asia, and South America.
Novelis is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited, the flagship metals company of the Aditya Birla Group, a multinational conglomerate based in Mumbai, India.
source_title: Atlanta Falcons and Novelis Launch 'Recycle for Good' Campaign :: Novelis Inc.

facts: Novelis Inc. produces and sells rolled aluminum products.
The company operates through four segments: North America, Europe, Asia, and South America.

It produces aluminum sheets and thin products primarily for use in beverage and food cans, as well as sheet products for the automotive, transportation, electronics, architecture, and industrial markets.
The company is also involved in primary aluminum smelting and hydropower generation activities in Brazil.
Novelis Inc. was founded in 2004.
Its corporate headquarters is located in Atlanta, Georgia.
Novelis Inc. is a subsidiary of AV Metals Inc.
The page references the years 2026 and 2025.
source_title: Novelis Inc.. Information sur l'émetteur. (LEI 549300DP3XIGY7CFV648). News et notations de crédit. Tableaux avec les informations comptables et financières.

facts: Novelis Inc plans to build a US$300 million plant in Kentucky targeted at automotive customers looking to minimize vehicle weight amid more stringent efficiency rules.

Novelis Chief Executive Officer Steve Fisher stated the investment reiterates the industry's view on strong penetration of aluminum as the material of choice on vehicles and does not see it as a leap of faith.
The facility will be located in Guthrie, Kentucky.
The plant is expected to create about 125 jobs.
The facility will have a capacity of 200,000 metric tons.
Novelis operates as the US unit of Hindalco Industries Ltd.
Novelis expects to complete the project by 2020.
Indian billionaire Kumar Mangalam Birla is expanding his overseas operations through Novelis.
Automotive companies continue to expand the amount of aluminum used in vehicles, particularly in trucks and SUVs.
An industry report projects that aluminum content in North American passenger vehicles will increase 42 percent by 2028 from 2015 levels.
Last April, Braidy Industries Inc. announced plans for a US$1.3 billion aluminum mill in eastern Kentucky.
Braidy Industries CEO Craig Bouchard stated the mill would be the first of its kind in three decades in the US.
Two years prior to publication, aluminum prices were near their lowest in almost seven years due to a China-led glut.
Novelis CEO Steve Fisher stated he did not expect any near-term relief from the China-led glut at that time.
Chinese smelting cutbacks have helped send aluminum prices back to their highest level since 2012.
The price increase provides incentives for producers in the US and elsewhere up and down the supply chain to restart capacity or undertake new projects.
Via Novelis, Hindalco is among bidders for US producer Aleris Corp.
source_title: Novelis to build US$300m aluminum plant amid auto optimism - The Business Times

facts: Novelis Corporation also operates under the name Novelis Global RES & Tech Ctr.
The company is located at 1950 Vaughn Rd Nw, Kennesaw, GA 30144.
This location is situated in Cobb County within the Atlanta-Sandy Springs-Roswell, GA Metropolitan Area.
Novelis primarily operates in the Foil and Aluminum business/industry within the Primary Metal Industries sector.
The company's business category is Aluminum Sheet, Plate, and Foil (SIC Code 3353; NAICS Code 331315).
NAICS Name for this location is Aluminum Sheet, Plate, and Foil Manufacturing.
Novelis engages in Manufacturing, Importing, and Exporting activities at this branch.
The Kennesaw branch employs approximately 14 people.
Marco Palmieri serves as the President of Novelis Corporation.
There are 3 commercial tenants located at the same address (1950 Vaughn Rd Nw, Kennesaw, GA).
source_title: Novelis Company Profile -Sales, Contacts, Competitors – Buzzfile

facts: Atlanta-headquartered aluminum rolling and recycling major Novelis, Inc. decided to collaborate with the Georgia Institute of Technology (Georgia Tech) to establish a new innovation hub at Georgia Tech.
The hub will focus on innovative research initiatives by connecting Novelis’ technical and business innovators with the Institute’s students and faculty.
Novelis committed $2.5 million to promote transformational research and related educational endeavors at Georgia Tech.
Georgia Tech will recruit a senior faculty member to serve as the Director of the Novelis Innovation Hub.
The director will take charge by 2020 academic year.
As per agreement, Novelis will either identify sponsored research or provide a list of innovation needs.
The Georgia Tech faculty will be invited to offer proposals, which will be evaluated by an advisory board comprising of members of both the entities.
The approved proposals will receive funding from Novelis as well as other external agencies.

The new partnership will provide the opportunity to train a new generation of the best and brightest students on Novelis’ scientific, engineering and business innovations, in order to develop new industry-leading solutions.
Todd Summe, Chief Research and Development Officer, Novelis, Inc. stated that establishing closer ties with Georgia Tech will provide tremendous benefits for both the students and the company.
source_title: Novelis Launches New Innovation Hub at Georgia Tech, Atlanta - 2019-08-21 | ScrapMonster

facts: Novelis and DRT Holdings entered into a joint development agreement to accelerate the adoption of high-recycled-content alloys in aluminum beverage can ends.
The collaboration aims to improve the manufacturing process for sustainable can ends, ensuring seamless adoption across the global metal packaging industry.
Novelis is a world leader in high-recycled-content, low-carbon flat rolled aluminum sheet products.
Novelis currently has an average of approximately 85% recycled content in its beverage packaging globally.
DRT is a leading provider of high-speed manufacturing equipment for beverage can ends, capable of producing up to 3,000 ends per minute.
Novelis and DRT will work together to optimize the design, conversion process and sustainability attributes of beverage can ends made from the same alloy as the can body.
The "uni-alloy" design solution enables beverage can-ends to be made from up to 99% recycled content.
This uni-alloy design reduces the carbon footprint of can ends by more than half.
Greg Schlicht serves as Senior Vice President and Chief Commercial Officer at Novelis.
Greg Schlicht stated that Novelis is committed to accelerating the transition to a more circular aluminum beverage packaging industry and this partnership with DRT is a key step in that journey.

Greg Schlicht noted that working with an industry leader in can end manufacturing will ensure beverage brands and can makers have solutions to accelerate uni-alloy adoption without compromising quality.

The use of high-recycled-content aluminum alloys in can ends represents an important shift in material innovation, reducing carbon emissions, improving recyclability and strengthening the closed-loop recycling system.

Novelis aims to be the leading provider of low-carbon, sustainable aluminum solutions and achieve a fully circular economy by partnering with suppliers and customers in aerospace, automotive, beverage can and specialties industries throughout North America, Europe, Asia and South America.
Novelis had net sales of $17.1 billion in fiscal year 2025.
Novelis is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited, an industry leader in aluminum and copper.
Hindalco Industries Limited is the metals flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group, a multinational conglomerate based in Mumbai.
DRT Holdings was founded in 1949 as the Dayton Reliable Tool & Mfg. Co.
DRT operates three business segments: Metal Packaging, Aerospace, and Precision.
DRT's Metal Packaging segment was the original developer of easy-open ("pull-top") ends used in food and beverage cans.
DRT provides engineering expertise, design-for-manufacturability capabilities, go-to-market support, and systems integration for its customers.
DRT operates eleven locations in the United States and Europe.
Novelis is a Georgia-based company that recycles aluminum and produces flat-rolled aluminum products for industries such as aerospace, automotive and transportation.
source_title: Owler Reports - Novelis: Novelis and DRT Collaborate to Advance Sustainability of Aluminum Beverage Can Ends

facts: An industrial blast occurred at the Novelis Greensboro facility in Georgia on March 1, 2026, halting plant operations.
Greene County Emergency Management Director Joe Bashore identified that the explosion originated from baghouse equipment and caused significant structural damage to the closest building.
The incident resulted in secondary fires spreading to aluminum scrap stockpiles and conveyor belt systems.

Baghouse dust collection systems process particulate-laden air through fabric filter bags, concentrating combustible aluminum dust in confined spaces during metal cutting, shredding, casting, ingot handling, and material transfer.
Aluminum particulate matter has a low minimum ignition energy of 1-5 millijoules and high combustion velocity once ignited.
The dust creates explosion thresholds between 20-40 g/m³, which is significantly lower than many other industrial dusts.
The combustion reaction of aluminum dust generates extremely high temperatures exceeding 3,000 Kelvin with exceptional violence.
Industry data indicates aluminum dust explosions achieve Kst values of 80-150 bar·m/s, classifying them among the most severe industrial explosion hazards.
All 16 workers at the Novelis facility were successfully evacuated without injuries.
The Greensboro facility processes used beverage can (UBC) materials and 5182 scrap aluminum specifically for beverage can end manufacturing.
This specialized material stream feeds directly into Novelis rolling mills for sheet production serving beverage industry customers.

Approximately 50% of all beverage cans worldwide contain recycled aluminum content, with 44.5 million tons of aluminum beverage cans circulating in North America in 2024 and recycling rates reaching 46.0% in 2023.

The UBC recycling process involves collection and sorting, shredding into small metal fragments, density separation to remove contaminants, melting in induction furnaces at approximately 660°C, casting into ingot form (typically 500+ kg units), and transport to rolling facilities for sheet production.
Novelis management indicated the company would leverage three Kentucky facilities (Berea, Guthrie, Russellville) and one New York location (Oswego) to absorb Greensboro production volumes.
The Oswego facility experienced multiple fires in Q4 2025 that disrupted hot-rolling mill operations and automotive sheet production.
OSHA Process Safety Management standards (29 CFR 1910.119) mandate enhanced protocols for facilities operating with minimal crews, including automated monitoring systems and redundant alarm networks.
NFPA 13 standards for sprinkler installation specify enhanced protection requirements for facilities handling combustible metals, particularly in dust collection areas and scrap storage zones.

NFPA 664 standards recommend quarterly visual inspections for baghouse systems, with specific requirements for automatic pulse-cleaning systems with pressure monitoring, daily dust removal to non-combustible containers, isolated exhaust systems with spark detection, and prominent hazard identification on all dust collection equipment.

Industrial accidents involving dust explosions typically trigger property damage assessments, business interruption claims, regulatory investigation processes by OSHA and state authorities, and premium adjustments for high-risk operational profiles.
Regional scrap metal pricing variations typically emerge within 2-3 weeks following capacity constraints, as alternative processors adjust purchasing strategies.
A single facility shutdown typically causes a 3-8% regional premium lasting 4-12 weeks, while multi-facility disruption causes a 10-15% price increase lasting 3-6 months.
Industrial facility recovery from dust explosion incidents typically requires 6-18 months depending on structural damage extent and regulatory approval processes.

Baghouse system replacement involves engineering assessment of structural integrity, equipment procurement with enhanced safety specifications, installation and commissioning under regulatory oversight, and testing and certification before operational restart.

OSHA investigations following dust explosion incidents typically require root cause analysis documentation, corrective action plans addressing identified deficiencies, enhanced safety protocol implementation, and third-party safety audits before restart authorization.

Facility recommissioning follows phased restart procedures including structural repairs and equipment installation, safety system testing and certification, workforce retraining on updated procedures, limited production trials under enhanced monitoring, and full capacity restoration following performance validation.

Capital allocation priorities for safety infrastructure include advanced monitoring systems for real-time hazard detection ($500K-1.5 million cost, 2-3 year ROI), automated suppression technology for dust explosion prevention ($1-3 million cost, immediate risk reduction), redundant safety systems minimizing single points of failure, and predictive maintenance programs ($200K-800K annually, 1-2 year ROI).
Baghouse system upgrades typically cost $2-5 million with a 3-5 year ROI timeline.
source_title: Blast Halts Novelis Plant Operations in Georgia

facts: Novelis Inc. signed a new long-term contract with Coca-Cola Bottlers' Sales & Services Company, the contracting agent for The Coca-Cola Company's authorized North American bottlers.
Under the agreement, Novelis will supply Coca-Cola's authorized North American bottlers with aluminum can sheet for The Coca-Cola Company's family of iconic brands.
Supply will come from Novelis' plant in Bay Minette, Ala., which is currently under construction and expected to begin commissioning in 2025.
The Coca-Cola North American bottlers have committed to purchasing a confidential volume of aluminum can sheet over a multi-year period through the agreement.

Steve Fisher, President and CEO of Novelis, stated the contract builds on a decades-long relationship with the Coca-Cola system and strengthens Novelis' position as the leading provider of aluminum for beverage cans.
The contract validates Novelis' investment in expanding rolling and recycling capacity in North America.
The agreement includes closed-loop recycling, where Novelis takes back manufacturing scrap from the can making process, recycles it, and converts it into new can sheet.
Novelis recycles more than 80 billion used beverage cans per year into new aluminum for beverage packaging.
Recycling aluminum is approximately 95% less energy intensive than making primary aluminum, resulting in 95% less carbon emissions.
Novelis expects demand for aluminum beverage can sheet to grow at a 3% compounded annual growth rate from 2022 to 2031.

Demand growth is driven by consumer preference for sustainable products and size variety, as well as more beverage types being packed in cans including water, energy drinks, soda, beer, wine, hard seltzers and ready-to-drink cocktails.

Dan Coe, Chief Procurement Officer of The Coca-Cola Company, stated the goal is to build a resilient domestic supply chain for sustainable long-term growth and recognize Novelis as a sustainability leader supporting 2030 World Without Waste Targets.
The majority of the Bay Minette plant's capacity for beverage can is already contracted.
Novelis' new low-carbon facility in Bay Minette will be the first fully integrated aluminum manufacturing and recycling plant built in the U.S. in nearly 40 years.
The Bay Minette facility is expected to create up to 1,000 new jobs.
The facility boasts an initial capacity of 600,000 tons of finished goods for the North American can and automotive markets.
The facility will be powered by renewable energy, use recycled water, and operate as a zero-waste facility.
Novelis is working toward a goal of achieving net carbon neutrality at Bay Minette.
Novelis expects to commission the Bay Minette plant in mid-2025.
Novelis Inc. is a global leader in innovative aluminum products and the world's largest recycler of aluminum.
Novelis aims to be the leading provider of low-carbon, sustainable aluminum solutions and achieve a fully circular economy across North America, Europe, Asia, and South America.
Novelis had net sales of $18.5 billion in fiscal year 2023.
Novelis is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited, the metals flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group, a multinational conglomerate based in Mumbai.
Novelis serves customers in the aerospace, automotive, beverage can, and specialties industries.
source_title: Novelis Enters Long - Beijing Ferrochrome Co.,Ltd

facts: Baldwin County Economic Development Alliance subscribed to JobsEQ by Chmura in 2015 to identify target industries that would bring high-wage jobs and diversify the county's economy.
Baldwin County EDA identified the Transportation/Warehousing sector as a key industry, noting an average wage of $49,000 in the sector compared to $39,000 county-wide.
Baldwin County EDA attracted an Aldi distribution center and an Imperial Dade logistics hub, bringing a total of 250 new jobs to Baldwin County.
Imperial Dade is investing $20 million in a 220,000 square foot new logistics hub, expanding on an existing facility.
The bid for the $100 million Aldi distribution center was competitive, with JobsEQ data helping demonstrate a large base of underemployed people in Baldwin County to attract the project.
Baldwin County EDA worked closely with the local school system to create a full-time Career Tech high school launching in the 2024-2025 school year.
The new Career Tech high school will train students in aerospace, maritime, welding, healthcare, information technology, and other programs.
Baldwin County EDA used JobsEQ data to highlight University of South Alabama, Coastal Alabama Community College, and the new Career Tech High School as future talent pipeline sources for Novelis.
In May 2022, Novelis Inc. selected the South Alabama Mega Site in Baldwin County for a new fully integrated aluminum mill facility.
Novelis announced plans to invest more than $2.5 billion into building the zero-waste, renewable energy powered facility.
Novelis' facility will create approximately 1,000 new jobs and generate indirect roles.
The average annual salary projected for Novelis' facility is $65,000.
Novelis plans to expand the facility in the future to capture ongoing demand.
44% of the workforce living within Baldwin County commutes out of the county for work each day.
Baldwin County is the 7th fastest-growing Metro area in the United States, ranking in the Top 3% of 3,143 counties for net incoming domestic migration.
JobsEQ data projects Baldwin County's population to reach nearly 300,000 by 2034.
The South Alabama Mega Site is a 3,000-acre county-owned site marketed for large industrial employers.

Greg Canfield, Secretary of the Alabama Department of Commerce, stated that Novelis' decision to locate its technologically advanced aluminum mill in Baldwin County highlights the state's attractive business climate and talented manufacturing workforce.
Baldwin County EDA uses JobsEQ to compile data on the local labor force, identify worker surpluses, and answer "What-If" analytic questions regarding economic benefits for potential projects.
source_title: How JobsEQ Has Helped Baldwin County EDA Nurture 21% Job Growth in Transportation/Warehousing Since 2015

facts: Novelis Inc. administers its Fiscal 2025 Annual Incentive Plan (AIP) through its Human Resources department.
The AIP performance period commences on April 1, 2024, and ends on March 31, 2025.
Payouts under the plan are made as a lump sum during the first fiscal quarter following the end of the performance year.
Eligibility for the AIP includes employees in job bands 7 and higher, all Corporate and Regional Headquarters employees, and all employees assigned to the Bay Minette Project Plan.
Employees must be employed in an eligible job band or position, or transferred or hired into one during the performance year, to receive a payout.
For JB7+ employees, performance measure weightages are Global Safety (10%), Adjusted Operating EBITDA (50%), and Adjusted Operating Cash Flow (40%).

Employees involved in the Bay Minette project have role-based weightings for the Steering Committee, Project Team, and Support Functions across metrics including Global Safety, Adjusted Operating EBITDA, and Adjusted Operating Cash Flow.
The overall AIP plan threshold for payout on any metric is 75% of the target Adjusted Operating EBITDA.
No payout will be made unless Adjusted Operating EBITDA reaches at least 75% of the target level, with results between threshold and maximum determined by interpolation.
Individual performance ratings (IP, PDP, DFP, OP, SP) act as modifiers on payouts, with JB6-11B employees receiving multipliers ranging from 0% to 130%.
Total annual payouts are capped at 170%, 190%, and 200% for employees rated DFP, OP, and SP respectively.

Participants who terminate employment or transfer to an ineligible position during the performance year will have prorated payouts based on days of employment, except for voluntary termination which results in forfeiture.
Global Safety is measured using Days Away from Work (DAFW) based on a standard OSHA calculation approximating 100 person-years.
A fatality at a Company location during the fiscal year ended March 31, 2025, will result in select participants receiving no payout for the Global Safety measure.
The plan defines Change in Control events involving beneficial ownership thresholds of 35% or more, board replacements, mergers with non-Aditya Birla Group entities, or asset sales.
Novelis reserves the right to interpret, amend, or modify the plan at any time and does not confer a right to continued service.
source_title: June 25, 2025 - EX-10.13 - 10-K/A: Annual report [Section 13 and 15(d), not S-K Item 405] | Novelis Inc. ()

facts: NVH KOREA is a manufacturer and supplier of major auto components founded in 1984 and based in Ulsan, South Korea.
The company manufactures interior NVH systems including isolation dash pad, tunnel pad, and floor module.
The company manufactures exterior NVH systems including undercover, insulator tunnel, and wheel guard.
The company manufactures overhead systems including headlining and sun visor.
The company manufactures engine NVH systems including partition engine room and insulator dash.
The company manufactures door trim systems including door trim.
The company manufactures thermal management systems.
The company manufactures cylinder block covers.
NVH KOREA reported annual revenue of ₹500 - ₹1,000 Cr as on Mar 31, 2025.
Its associated legal entity, NVH INDIA AUTO PARTS PRIVATE LIMITED, reported revenue of $50M - $100M as on Mar 31, 2025.
NVH KOREA had an employee count of 210 as on Jan 31, 2024.
The total employee count at NVH KOREA was 24.0% lower in Jan 2024 compared to Jan 2023.
NVH KOREA is part of Hyundai’s Georgia EV supplier network, which added three more manufacturers as of Sep 15, 2023.
NVH KOREA plans to create over 160 jobs in Henry County, Georgia, with Scannell Properties and Cushman involved, as of Jun 23, 2023.
NVH KOREA plans a $72M plant in Georgia to manufacture battery parts and will hire 140 people, as of Jun 23, 2023.
source_title: NVH KOREA - 2026 Company Profile, Funding, Competitors & Financials - Tracxn

entity_name: Georgia Global Energy Policy Center & EV Supply Chain
facts: Article authored by Craig Lesser (Chairman of The Pendleton Group, former Georgia Commissioner of Economic Development 2004-07) and Jorge Fernandez (Senior Consultant at The Pendleton Group).
Authors propose creating a new "Georgia Global Energy Policy Center" to leverage state assets and partner internationally for energy innovation.
The proposed center would be funded by state government, private industry, federal grants, and foundations.
The center would leverage the expertise of the entire University System of Georgia.
The Georgia Institute of Technology would serve as the hub for the Center of Energy Supply Research.
The University of Georgia would form the Center for Rural Energy Research and Policy.
Georgia Southern University would house the Center of Air and Sea Energy Research and Policy.
Georgia State University would become the Center of Urban Energy Research and Policy.
Georgia aims to compete on the value of its intellectual capacity and connections rather than solely on job counts and inward investment figures.
The proposed center would connect global players to address pressing energy issues and form partnerships with international energy think tanks.
Companies with energy interests in Georgia include AGCO, Delta Air Lines, Fieldale Farms, Georgia Power Co., Gulfstream, Hyundai, Kia, Lockheed, Mercedes Benz, Porsche, and Southern Co.
These companies could utilize the proposed center to partner with university researchers and cooperate on joint energy projects to attract international partners and funding.
Energy-related organizations like AEE and INPO could cooperate with the policy center to pool resources and knowledge.
The existing Center of Innovation for Energy is led by the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
This center works with companies like Stryten Energy, Mitsubishi Turbines, and Siemens Energy & Automation to advance energy technologies for generation, storage, and consumption.
The innovation center has worked with partners for about 10 years to lay groundwork for the EV mobility industry in Georgia.
Georgia has positioned itself as a hub for electric transportation with 35 EV-related projects investing in the state since 2018.
Hyundai Motor Group’s fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing “meta plant” near Savannah is expected to ramp up to full production in 2025.
Rivian’s largest manufacturing facility in the U.S. is slated to start production in Georgia in 2026.
Georgia is rapidly expanding its full EV supply chain with related manufacturing and solutions.
NVH Korea announced a $72 million battery parts factory in Henry County.
Woory Industrial Co., Ltd. plans to open an EV parts R&D center and manufacturing facility in Dublin, Georgia.
Both NVH Korea and Woory Industrial are coming to support the Hyundai Meta Plant.
LG Energy Solution Ltd. is partnering on a $4.3 billion battery operation at the Hyundai Meta Plant.
Anovion Technologies is building an $800 million plant to manufacture synthetic graphite anode materials for lithium-ion batteries in Bainbridge, Georgia.

Georgia’s ambitions to become a hub for clean vehicles underscore its goal to be a clearinghouse for clean energy expertise in EV infrastructure, battery development, commercialization, and charging stations.
Georgia’s existing energy innovation center is focusing on identifying EV charging equipment manufacturers, such as Netherlands-based Heliox.
The state is especially interested in attracting global players in hydrogen energy technology and storage to invest in Georgia.
Georgia could work with Hydrogen Europe on innovation and investment for deploying hydrogen power.
The Center of Innovation for Energy hopes to develop demonstration hydrogen projects and potentially the first hydrogen living laboratory.
This hydrogen lab initiative is similar to the state's net-zero transportation initiative The Ray and the smart city Curiosity Lab.
Georgia is bidding for funding to become a center for hydrogen expertise through a coalition of regional utilities, including Southern Company.
Georgia Power is conducting research into hydrogen fuels.
Suppliers like Atlanta Cylinders Inc., whose parent company is in the Czech Republic, are lining up to support Georgia’s hydrogen initiatives.
Automakers Hyundai and Toyota Motor Corp. are looking to hydrogen as the fuel of the future.
Success with hydrogen initiatives could attract broader industry attention to Georgia similar to the EV mobility industry's focus.
Georgia’s previous initiatives to coalesce expertise around fintech and global health demonstrate its ability to garner local and international industry support.
The proposed policy center would encourage public education programs around new energy technology to engage youth and cultivate students for universities and technical schools.
A growing community of educated professionals would help attract talented entrepreneurs and innovators from around the world to Georgia.
The Georgia Research Alliance has been instrumental in accelerating university research and development growth in the state.
The Georgia Research Alliance supports startup companies and creates jobs by bringing private and public research investment to Georgia.
Alignment between the Georgia Research Alliance and a new energy policy center could result in further economic impact throughout the state.
The Georgia Global Energy Policy Center would be a nonpartisan effort benefiting the entire state.
Research and development on this scale could benefit every Georgian by creating new jobs or inventing efficiencies that impact gas and electric bills.
source_title: Powering Ahead: How Georgia Can Become a Global Energy Thought Leader  - Global Atlanta

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that automotive parts manufacturer NVH Korea will establish a new facility in Locust Grove during an economic development trip to the Paris Air Show.
The project will create over 160 new jobs.
The project will bring $72 million in investment to Henry County.
This is the second economic development project Governor Kemp has announced this week while promoting Georgia to international job creators abroad.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that reliable infrastructure and a great workforce allow job creators to find success in every community in Georgia.
Governor Brian P. Kemp cited the incredible growth of the e-mobility and battery industries in Georgia as a testament to supporting large suppliers like NVH Korea.
Governor Brian P. Kemp welcomed NVH Korea as the latest partner in making Georgia the epicenter of the industrial revolution of their time.
NVH Korea is an automotive parts manufacturer based in the City of Ulsan.
NVH Korea was established in 1984.
The company specializes in automotive noise, vibration, and heat-control parts.
NVH Korea currently produces these parts in Korea, Europe, and India.
The company operates a manufacturing facility that produces floor mats, cargo mats, and cargo trays in Georgia.
The new facility will supply battery parts for electric vehicles (EVs) in the United States.
NVH Korea will supply battery parts to manufacturers such as Hyundai Mobis, Kia Georgia, and Hyundai Motor Group.
Ja Kyum Koo, Chairman and CEO of NVH Korea, stated they chose Georgia as the company’s first EV battery component manufacturing site.
Ja Kyum Koo noted that cooperation between state agencies and company staff helped guide the project.
Ja Kyum Koo described this as the first step in contributing to the electrification of the mobility industry.
Ja Kyum Koo expressed looking forward to joining the community of Locust Grove and Henry County.
NVH Korea’s new facility will be located at Gardner Logistics Park on Colvin Drive in Locust Grove.
The facility will produce EV battery system components that protect, connect, and sense the performance of battery cells.
Operations are expected to begin in the second quarter of 2024.
Interested individuals can learn more about NVH Korea online.
Mayor Robert Price of the City of Locust Grove stated NVH’s investment will increase manufacturing jobs and make them part of Georgia’s electric vehicle ecosystem.
Mayor Robert Price expressed looking forward to a successful partnership with NVH and the opportunities this investment creates.
Carlotta Harrell, Chair of the Henry County Board of Commissioners, stated today’s announcement will add quality manufacturing jobs for citizens and continue to diversify the economy.
Carlotta Harrell expressed excitement to welcome NVH and for the community to join Georgia’s growing electric vehicle manufacturing base.
Project Manager Jacob Lee represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was developed in partnership with the Henry County Development Authority, Georgia EMC, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated there is an economic transformation happening as manufacturers create jobs of the future aligned with cutting-edge technologies.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson welcomed innovators establishing their first U.S. or e-mobility manufacturing presence, like NVH Korea.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted these companies enrich the local industry mix, provide well-paying jobs for Georgians, and build on the statewide mobility ecosystem.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson thanked NVH Korea for selecting Georgia and congratulated community partners in Henry County.

For over a century, Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices, encouraged collaboration and innovation, and positioned itself as a leader in developing emerging technologies for the automotive and mobility industry.
From July 1 through December 31, 2022, the automotive industry was the second-largest source of new jobs and investment in Georgia.
Since 2020, Georgia has pursued job creation along the entire EV supply chain, resulting in more than $22.7 billion in investments and the creation of over 28,400 jobs.
Garrison Douglas serves as Press Secretary for the Office of Governor Brian P. Kemp.
Carter Chapman serves as Deputy Press Secretary for the Office of Governor Brian P. Kemp.
Marie Gordon serves as Communications Director for the Georgia Department of Economic Development.

facts: NVH Korea will open a factory in suburban Atlanta to make parts for electric batteries.
NVH Korea will invest $72 million in a plant in Locust Grove, south of Atlanta.
The plant will hire 140 workers.
The plant will make sensors, connections, and protectors for electric vehicle batteries.
The plant is scheduled to begin operations in 2024.
Chairman and CEO Ja Kyum Koo stated that this is NVH Korea's first venture into making components for electric batteries.
NVH Korea is based in Ulsan, South Korea.
NVH Korea will be the 10th supplier to locate in the region.
Hyundai announced in 2022 that it would build a $5.5 billion plant to assemble electric vehicles and batteries in Ellabell, Georgia, near Savannah.
The Hyundai site could grow to 8,100 employees.
The Hyundai site is slated to begin producing vehicles in 2025.
Suppliers for the region have pledged to invest nearly $2.1 billion.
Suppliers for the region have pledged to hire nearly 4,900 people.
This will be NVH Korea's second plant in Georgia.
NVH Korea's subsidiary AFS America is located in Columbus and makes floor mats and cargo trays.
AFS America supplies the Hyundai plant in Montgomery, Alabama.
AFS America supplies the sister Kia plant in West Point, Georgia.
AFS America supplies General Motors.
NVH Korea operates 19 plants worldwide across South Korea, North America, China, India, Europe, and Russia.
NVH Korea manufactures sound-dampening parts and materials, floor mats, headliners, insulators, and parts to help manage engine and cooling system temperatures.
The state of Georgia will pay to train NVH Korea's workers.
The company could qualify for $1.2 million in state income tax credits.
The tax credits are valued at $1,750 per job over five years.
Workers must make at least $31,300 a year to qualify for the tax credits.
The company could qualify for other incentives, including property tax breaks from Locust Grove and Henry County.
source_title: South Korean firm plans $72M Georgia plant to make battery parts, hiring 140

facts: Korean automotive parts supplier AFS America LLC plans to open a manufacturing facility and warehouse in Columbus, Georgia.
AFS America LLC is a subsidiary of parent company NVH Korea Co., Ltd.
The company will create 65 new jobs at the new facility.
The company will invest more than $3 million in the facility.
AFS America LLC will produce automobile floor mats and cargo trays at the Columbus, Georgia facility.
The company is a key supplier for Hyundai Mobis, Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia (KMMG), Hyundai Motors Manufacturing Alabama (HMMA), and General Motors.
The new facility is expected to begin operations by the fourth quarter of 2012.
The facility is expected to be fully operational by 2013.
Jakyum Koo, Chairman of NVH Korea, stated they are excited to start U.S. operations and take advantage of the highly skilled workforce in the Columbus, Georgia region.
Jakyum Koo expressed gratitude for the support of the Greater Columbus Chamber of Commerce.
Jakyum Koo stated their goal is to create meaningful opportunities in the community and better serve customers in the USA.
Jay Alexander, Chairman of the Greater Columbus Georgia Chamber of Commerce, praised the total team effort behind the announcement.

Jay Alexander noted that the Chamber's private sector focus combined with public sector support from the Development Authority of Columbus, Columbus Consolidated Government, and the state of Georgia made the new jobs and capital investment possible.
Dick Ellis, chairman of the Development Authority of Columbus, Georgia, stated that recruiting automotive supplier companies remains a targeted industry for them.
Dick Ellis noted they have been working with leadership at NVH Korea since November and are pleased the company decided to locate its first U.S. manufacturing facility in Columbus.
Dick Ellis stated their economic development team is focused on assisting AFS America with setting up the facility, hiring, training, and getting them in business quickly.
AFS America's Columbus facility will be located in the Corporate Ridge Business Park.
The location provides strategic access to key customers in Georgia and the Southeast.
The company will benefit from highly-skilled talent in the Columbus Region trained for manufacturing operations.
The company will receive operations-specific workforce training from Georgia Quick Start, described as the nation's top-ranked workforce training program.

The Greater Columbus Georgia Chamber of Commerce, Development Authority of Columbus, and The Valley Partnership partnered with the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) to secure this business opportunity.
Brian Sillitto, Chamber Senior Vice President of Economic Development, is managing the project for Columbus.
Yoonie Kim, GDEcD statewide Project Manager, facilitated the project for the state.
Job applications will be accepted at the Georgia Department of Labor, Columbus Career Center located at 700 Veterans Parkway, Monday through Friday from 8:00 am until 4:30 pm.
AFS America LLC is a manufacturing corporation for automobile floor mats and cargo trays.
Parent company NVH Korea is the number one supplier of automotive interior parts in Korea.
NVH Korea has 1,754 employees globally and facilities in India, China, and Russia.
NVH Korea's revenue in 2011 was $590 million globally.

The company utilizes the latest technologies in precision metal stamping, assembly, tool and die designs.
Oneda specializes in innovative robotic transfer and progressive stamping capabilities with press sizes ranging from 25 to 500 tons.
Oneda Corporation is a subsidiary of Oneda Electric Corporation serving the Automotive, Medical and Consumer Electronics industries.
The company provides competitive performance and cost-effective metal stampings for its served industries.
Oneda offers metal stampings and prototyping services ranging from initial design prototyping to full mass production.
The organization works in partnership with customers to reduce tooling expenses, improve overall unit costs, and increase production capacity through flexible manufacturing processes.
Oneda uses advanced comprehensive engineering and design experience to achieve unique manufacturing techniques in factory automation.
The company has developed tooling technology and manufacturing capabilities to produce several different part numbers at once within the same process.

Oneda Corporation is an IATF16949, ISO9001 and ISO14001 certified company.

canonical_name: Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America
facts: Panasonic Automotive presented its new Friction Free Connectivity solution at the 2019 CES on the Hill event in Washington D.C.
The company is a leading global supplier of automotive infotainment and connectivity systems solutions.
According to the 2018 JD Powers Initial Quality Study, Bluetooth Connectivity problems were ranked as the second most recurring issue in new vehicles.
Panasonic Automotive has found that current connection issues include technical complexity during initial pairing, maintaining connection and overall security concerns.
Panasonic Automotive’s Friction Free Connectivity is a Bluetooth pairing via Wi-Fi solution that requires a smartphone app for a one-time secure setup.
New vehicle owners register within the smartphone app via a streamlined log-in process and establish rights to their vehicle using a Vehicle Identification Number (VIN).
This setup is expected to dramatically improve hands-free adoption rates seen today.
The solution can be integrated into existing vehicle manufacturer applications, as well as ride-sharing and rental car apps for easy automatic Bluetooth pairing while traveling.

The Friction Free Connectivity pairing solution offers enhanced security with its ability to cryptographically trust both the user and the vehicle, making it very difficult for “drive-by-pairing” or accidentally connecting to the wrong vehicle.
Sixteen U.S. states have passed laws requiring the use of hands-free technology while driving, and seven others impose some level of restriction.
According to the 2019 Zendrive “Distracted Driving Snapshot” study, 60% of motorists use their mobile phone while driving.

“Bluetooth is nearly standard today with 86% of new vehicles coming equipped with the technology. However, the complexity of the pairing platform is a barrier to adoption, which perpetuates distracted drivers to the roadways” said Jeffrey Zellen, Senior Engineering Manager, Panasonic Automotive.
Jeffrey Zellen serves as Senior Engineering Manager at Panasonic Automotive.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America is headquartered in Peachtree City, Georgia.
The company maintains sales, marketing, and engineering operations in Farmington Hills, Michigan.

Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America acts as the North American affiliate of Panasonic Corp.’s Automotive Company, which coordinates global automotive and industrial systems and components operations.
Panasonic Corporation of North America is headquartered in Newark, New Jersey, and is the principal North American subsidiary of Osaka, Japan-based Panasonic Corporation.
source_title: Panasonic Automotive Streamlines Vehicle Bluetooth Pairing with Patent Pending Friction Free Connectivity Solution | Panasonic North America - United States

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems America is a premier supplier of automotive and mobility technologies for the world’s leading OEMs.
The company strives to create a safe, comfortable, eco-friendly in-vehicle experience through high-quality solutions.
It provides In-Vehicle Computing Technology, including Cockpit Domain Controllers (CDCs) and High-Performance Computing (HPCs), for intuitive and immersive digital experiences.
It offers Premium Audio products built on decades of award-winning in-car audio expertise to deliver studio sound quality into vehicles.
Its Sound & Voice Innovations technologies reduce unwanted noise, improve communication within the cabin, and enable hands-free control for drivers.
Its Vision & Sensing Technology solutions allow drivers to see today’s roads more clearly, increasing safety and improving situational awareness.
The company partners with leading automakers including Ford, GM, Stellantis, Toyota, Honda, Nissan, and many others.
Panasonic Automotive Systems America operates as a unified entity across North America, seamlessly connecting its operations from coast-to-coast.
Its corporate headquarters is located in Peachtree City, GA.
It maintains a sales office in Farmington Hills, MI.
It operates manufacturing facilities in Reynosa and Monterrey, MX.

facts: Apollo Funds, a private equity firm, has agreed to purchase a majority stake in supplier Panasonic Automotive Systems for 311 billion yen ($2 billion).
Under the deal, Panasonic Holdings will maintain a minority stake in Panasonic Automotive.
Panasonic Automotive will continue its strategic relationship with the Panasonic Group.
The Panasonic Group includes Panasonic Holdings, the holding company, eight operating companies and affiliates within Japan and overseas.
The companies expect the transaction to close by the end of the first quarter of 2025.
Panasonic Group CEO Yuki Kusumi stated that through this partnership, Panasonic Automotive could significantly grow as a global leading player.
Panasonic Automotive will continue its role as a member of the Panasonic Group and collaborate with the Group in procurement and fundamental research.
Apollo aims to help support the next phase of growth for Panasonic Automotive, a key link in the global auto supply chain.
Michael Reiss, a partner at Apollo, stated that supporting growth will still involve maintaining the company’s relationships with customers and partners.
Other suppliers, including Continental and Goodyear, are retooling their strategies and operations as the auto industry transitions to electric and software-defined vehicles.
Panasonic Automotive offers vision and sensing technology, integrated cockpit systems and other in-vehicle electronics for automotive applications.
These products support autonomous vehicles, SDVs (software-defined vehicles) and connected services.
Masashi Nagayasu, representative director and CEO of Panasonic Automotive, said the company has the potential to win through this once-in-a-century transformational period.
Nagayasu stated that by taking advantage of the partnership with Apollo and realizing PAS’ full potential, they aim to become a top global player in the automotive electronics industry.
The company aims to provide added value to cars and the mobility experience with customers.
source_title: US private equity firm buys majority stake in Panasonic Automotive | WardsAuto

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems Executive Officer Keiko Ohta discussed future car design and UX with Professor Masaya Ando of Chiba Institute of Technology following the company's exhibit at Tokyo Auto Salon.
The company showcased concept vehicles including the "WELL Cabin Luxe" and "WELL Cabin Craie2" to demonstrate tangible mobility experiences.
Panasonic introduced a large transparent display in its concept cars to present information on a large scale within the vehicle.
The company demonstrated AI technology capable of analyzing finger gestures for in-car interaction.
Panasonic's UX design philosophy emphasizes that a car must be driven safely before any convenient features are added, making safety the foundational premise of automotive UX.
Context of use is identified as the most important factor in UX design, with different experiences required for weekend drives versus shuttling family members.
Designers aim to shape how people spend time in the vehicle, such as creating spaces for solitary enjoyment or deep conversation between passengers.

Automotive UX must account for finite human attention resources, noting that listening to music while driving uses about 30% of attention while driving uses 70%, whereas conversation splits attention fifty-fifty and can delay reactions to unexpected events.
Interface design must be rooted in an understanding of human cognitive characteristics to prevent increased cognitive load from excessive information display.
The company prioritizes maintaining the driver's psychological "sense of competence" rather than entrusting all driving tasks to technology.
Panasonic is developing a non-commercial AI project called "Human Insight Intelligence" that combines human cognition models with external sensor data.
Human Insight Intelligence uses AI to estimate risk based on sensor information and identifies cognitive gaps where drivers may fail to notice hazards, such as objects moving at a constant speed.
The system aims to provide safety guidance by gently supplementing what the driver has not noticed, rather than constantly notifying them or creating warning displays.
Panasonic has researched Human Insight Technology for many years to understand which environments and stimuli give people a sense of reassurance and comfort.
The company is committed to examining factors that produce comfort and reassurance factor-by-factor to establish logic and evidence for situation-aware design.
Future potential exists in using personal data sensed by cars not only within the vehicle but also in daily life or other industries to create new value.
source_title: Considering “Joy in Motion” from a UX Perspective Professor Masaya Ando Describes People’s Relationship with Cars | Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd.

facts: In April 2027, the company will change its name from Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd. to Mobitera Inc.
CEO Masashi Nagayasu explained the core sentiments behind the rebranding process through dialogue about who the company is and hopes for the new name.
Over several months, the company reviewed roughly 800 proposed new names before finalizing the decision.
The name change decision was made with an eye toward a future IPO to grow as an autonomous company amid a major transformation in the automotive industry.
The company aims to shift from simply manufacturing products to designing 'experiences' that make mobility comfortable and enrich lives.
The new 'Mobitera' logo places the letter 'i' at its center to symbolize keeping individuals at the forefront of all operations.
The brand's character was refined around core values: integrity, teamwork, adaptability, challenge, and productivity.
These values were broken down into specific daily actions (DO) and non-actions (DON'T) to translate abstract commitments into concrete behavior.
To survive in a rapidly changing industry, the company plans to accelerate decision-making and increase the flexibility of its capital policy.
The company's unchanging core DNA includes its founding principles and a relentless commitment to quality in safety and security.
The new name 'Mobitera' combines the Japanese words for opening the road ('Iter') and illuminating ('Tera').
source_title: Beyond the pursuit of what makes us who we are.​ Our resolve in the new vessel called “Mobitera”​ | Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd.

facts: Capgemini Group announced the successful creation of a data ecosystem for longstanding client Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America.
The new platform improves the organization’s capabilities in data-driven decision making and innovation to drive efficiencies and more reliable extracts.
Panasonic Automotive is a division of Panasonic Corporation of North America specializing in innovations that are revolutionizing the driving experience.
Panasonic Automotive streamlined its data processes with the Microsoft Azure platform with Power BI for data visualization.
The platform provides a centralized, stable foundation for its data team to promote innovation and capture more reliable results across the business.
Scott Kirchner, President of Panasonic Automotive, stated that data enables people to see how their piece of the business is working, fostering innovation and accountability.
Capgemini established an aggressive six-month timeline to migrate Panasonic Automotive’s existing data reporting platform to Microsoft Azure.
The migration was built on a single-instance software system.
The new data lake architecture solution centralizes wealth of information across Panasonic Automotive’s business units.
The solution provides more reliable data extracts in a much shorter timeframe, setting the organization up for future artificial intelligence and machine learning use cases.

Venkat Padmanabhan, Account Executive for Panasonic at Capgemini, stated that the collaboration shows Capgemini’s commitment to understanding client goals and utilizing skills in data migration and automotive connectivity to support Panasonic Automotive’s dedication to passion, quality, and innovation.
Panasonic Automotive and Capgemini have worked together for more than five years on streamlining applications with automation and ongoing support from Capgemini’s ADMnext capabilities.

Since migrating to the data lake, the Panasonic Automotive Business Intelligence team spends less than 20% of their time on support-related tasks, down from approximately 50% on the previous platform.
The transformed ecosystem presents well-informed data in significantly less time with increased accuracy and reliability.
source_title: Capgemini Group fuels collaboration with Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America through transformative new data platform - Sogeti Germany

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd. is applying its “Joy in Motion” design perspective to create new mobility value by focusing on the riding and driving experience rather than just vehicle specifications.
The company develops In-Vehicle Infotainment (IVI) systems, which include car navigation systems and audio systems used to provide information and entertainment inside vehicles.
User evaluations show that IVI quality and reliability significantly affect customer satisfaction with vehicles.
Panasonic Automotive Systems aims to provide consistent user experiences across software-defined vehicles (SDVs) instead of competing based on the number of added functions.

The company is dedicating efforts to Human Insight Technology to understand individual users and make vehicle interiors more comfortable, coordinating with universities and other partners for this research.
Panasonic conducts fundamental research into how the brain recognizes visual information through sensation sensors to inform interface design.
The company designs vehicle interfaces to capture no more attention than necessary, creating a “low distraction” state that reduces driver frustration and stress.
Panasonic Automotive Systems is developing Unified HMI technology to integrate vehicle instrument panels, heads-up displays, and monitors into a cohesive framework.
Unified HMI aims to eliminate hesitation or confusion in the vehicle interior as screen numbers increase and will be opened up to others to improve development efficiency.
The company plans to use AI to support users by automating recommendations through emotional and action inferencing, reducing the need for instruction manuals.

AI integration strategies include using facial recognition or smartphone Bluetooth for authentication, automatically adjusting GUIs per driver, enabling individual seat audio zones, prioritizing alerts over music based on driver focus, and using thermography sensing to automatically adjust seat temperatures.
Panasonic Automotive Systems leverages its strengths in software and computing, which are vital for the development of software-defined vehicles (SDVs).

The company plans to expand its contributions beyond traditional cars to total mobility experiences, including Mobility as a Service (MaaS), which uses apps for transportation stages like searching, reservations, and payment.
Masashige Mizuyama serves as Representative Director, Executive Vice President, and Chief Technology Officer (CTO) at Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd.
Keiko Ohta serves as Executive Officer and Japan Chief Executive Officer (Japan CEO) at Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd.
source_title: Entering the Era of Choosing Mobility Based on the Experience It Offers The Future of “Joy in Motion” design and Car Interior UX | Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd.

facts: In June 2023, Panasonic Automotive Systems’ new generation wireless charger was installed as standard equipment in all grades of Toyota’s fully remodeled Alphard/Vellfire.
The charger provides highly efficient charging performance using a proprietary moving coil method and algorithms that support smartphones of various shapes and sizes.
Maximum charging output increased by 1.5 times from the previous 10 W to 15 W for products compliant with Ver. 1.3.2 of the Qi standard.
The charger is compatible with iPhone 7.5 W charging and Galaxy fast charging specifications (10 W).
A unique safety function stops charging if a foreign object between the charger and smartphone generates heat.
The chargeable range has been expanded by 3.6 times compared to the previous model to ensure highly efficient charging even if the smartphone is misaligned.
Panasonic Automotive made aggressive technical appeals to Toyota’s development managers to highlight its Moving Coil technology, which initially was not recognized by Toyota.
Following initial standard installation in the Alphard/Vellfire, the charger's installation rate expanded to other Toyota models.
Sales increased through the KINTO FACTORY service, a vehicle update service for cars already on the road, in cooperation with Toyota-affiliated KINTO Corporation.
As of May 2024, the charger is available as a retrofit option for a total of five Toyota- and LEXUS-brand models.
Panasonic participated in an offline meeting of LEXUS and RAV4 users organized by an SNS influencer to raise product awareness and demonstrate its capabilities.
End-user feedback highlighted that wireless charging often fails when phones are out of position, which the moving coil technology successfully addresses during demonstrations.
Demonstrations at KINTO FACTORY booths effectively attracted customers and generated more orders than expected for LEXUS NX since the service launch.
Future activities will focus on increasing orders at KINTO FACTORY, updating existing vehicles, and expanding factory-installed options in Japan and overseas.
Panasonic plans to actively participate in offline meetings to allow end-users to test the product with their own smartphones and increase interest.
User feedback will be reflected in future product planning and leveraged in proposals to car manufacturers.
The engineering team aims to evolve charging algorithms to achieve higher efficiency and keep pace with smartphone evolution.

entity_name: Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America
