facts: Panasonic Automotive announced two variants of in-vehicle wireless charging technology (moving coil and static coil) at the first-ever virtual CES 2021.
Both variants claim to provide efficient in-vehicle charging that meets or exceeds most current in-vehicle charging systems.

Panasonic launched an automotive wireless charging system aimed at being a more accurate and efficient way to charge compared to the majority of vehicle charging systems that remain wired or tethered.

The moving coil variant includes Exclusive Detection Technology, a patent-pending tracking technology where the wireless charging coil moves into the optimum position to align with the mobile device's charging coil and quickly begins to charge wirelessly.
This moving coil technology has better coupling capability against other wireless charging systems and supports 15W of charging power.
The wireless charger is Qi 1.3 certified for both static/moving coil variants.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America is a division company of Panasonic Corporation of North America.
Panasonic Automotive operates as a global supplier of automotive infotainment and connectivity system solutions.
Panasonic Automotive is headquartered in Peachtree City, Georgia.
The company maintains sales, marketing, and engineering operations in Farmington Hills, Michigan.
source_title: Panasonic Automotive announces in vehicle wireless charging solutions - BusinessToday

facts: GDOT, The Ray, Kia Georgia, and Panasonic demonstrated a fully operational connected vehicle environment along 18 miles of Interstate 85 known as “The Ray Highway.”

The demonstration utilized the Cirrus by Panasonic® connected vehicle data management platform that detects real-time roadway events and communicates safety-critical messages to travelers about driving conditions.

The partnership between the four organizations is focused on raising awareness of the life-saving potential of connected vehicle technology as well as deploying real-world use cases with measurable benefits.
GDOT and Kia Georgia vehicles were equipped with vehicle-to-everything (V2X) technology for the demonstration.
GDOT can now deliver real-time situational awareness to drivers on equipped roadways in West Georgia, a key logistics and manufacturing corridor.
GDOT, The Ray, and Panasonic are providing “ride-along” experiences for stakeholders where drivers will be warned of hazardous conditions ahead on the roadway before they come into view.

These “ride-along” experiences showcase how connected vehicle technology can detect and warn drivers about weather events, work zones, hard braking, and vehicle crashes in a real-world highway environment.
The experience includes an in-vehicle visualization of traveler warnings.
The experience showcases the capabilities of V2X technology installed on and along roadways.
The experience highlights the digital communication that enables the intelligence of connected roadways.
The Cirrus cloud platform manages data for GDOT to detect events and provide real-time alerts to drivers.

Russell McMurry, Commissioner of the Georgia Department of Transportation, stated that it is important for Georgia DOT to lead in deploying transportation technology that benefits all drivers, moves freight across the region more efficiently and safely, and advances Georgia as the technology epicenter of the east coast.
Russell McMurry noted that connected vehicle technology will save lives and expressed determination to see it come to life in Georgia as soon as possible.
Russell McMurry emphasized the power of partnerships with organizations like Kia, The Ray, and Panasonic to advance roadways for Georgians.

Stuart Countess, Kia Georgia’s president and CEO, stated that Kia Georgia is proud to partner with these organizations while showcasing outstanding products being built by the Kia team in West Point, Georgia.

Stuart Countess emphasized that customer safety is the number one priority for Kia Georgia team members and called the V2X program a forward-thinking initiative putting Kia at the heart of solutions where vehicles and infrastructure communicate seamlessly to enhance travel safety and efficiency.
Allie Kelly, Executive Director of The Ray, stated that connected vehicle technology creates an opportunity to increase travel efficiency and driver safety simultaneously.

Allie Kelly noted that enabling connected and autonomous vehicles requires investments in physical and digital infrastructure, which Georgia DOT and The Ray have made to improve and modernize transportation infrastructure.

Allie Kelly highlighted that these investments offer the traveling public real-time warnings about critical driving conditions such as crashes, construction work zones, hard braking, and weather impacts like black ice, flooding, and hydroplaning.

Chris Armstrong, Vice President of Cirrus at Panasonic’s Smart Mobility Office, stated that the tech industry constantly talks about building technology with purpose rather than developing technology for technology’s sake.

Chris Armstrong expressed pride in partnering with mission-driven organizations in Georgia DOT, The Ray, and Kia Georgia to solve transportation challenges by bringing perspectives from public, private, and philanthropic organizations united in improving safety.
Chris Armstrong emphasized championing the wide-scale adoption of this life-saving technology as quickly as possible.

Panasonic Corporation of North America is based in Newark, NJ, and is a leading provider of Consumer Lifestyle technologies, Smart Mobility, Sustainable Energy, Immersive Experiences, and Integrated Supply Chain solutions.
Panasonic Corporation of North America is the principal North American subsidiary of Osaka, Japan-based Panasonic Holdings Corporation.
Panasonic was named one of Interbrand’s Top 100 Best Global Brands of 2021.
Panasonic serves as a leading technology partner and integrator to businesses, government agencies, and consumers across the region.
source_title: Georgia Department of Transportation Demonstrates Safer, Connected Roadways with Kia Georgia and Panasonic Smart Mobility Technology on “The Ray Highway” | Panasonic North America - United States

facts: Griffin, Georgia, is the Perkins manufacturing facility supplying engines to the construction, agricultural, materials handling and electric power markets across North America.
The facility was established in 2004 and produces the compact but powerful 400 Series range of engines.

The facility provides a one-piece flow production system which tracks each individual engine electronically from the start of the assembly process, through the hot testing systems to paint spraying and the final dress for the customer.
Jeff Rohr is the facility manager at Perkins Griffin.
The 400 Series engine provides a seamless power range of 10.2-49.2 kW (13.7-66 hp) within an efficient, space saving 2, 3 or 4 cylinder package.
Production is scheduled to start on the new 404F-E22T/TA common rail engines with direct injection, producing 36-50 kW (48-67 hp).
The engines are assembled on a roller track, with electronic screens providing the operator with build data specific for each individual engine.
Engines are built using electronic torque-controlled tools to ensure that every critical fastening is tightened precisely to specification, every time.
After assembly, the engines pass to one of ten test beds in a single acoustic enclosure for a comprehensive hot test.
After painting, dressing, and 100 percent inspection, the engines are ready to be shipped out to the customer.
Griffin supplies engines to customers across North America, direct from the factory in the fastest possible time, with lead times and delivery times kept to a minimum.

Engines are produced in line with the Caterpillar Production System (CPS), which is used in all Perkins manufacturing facilities, ensuring the same efficient processes, the same tested and validated components, and the same stringent quality controls are implemented at every facility.
The facility employs a highly trained American team focused on North American markets.
The facility address is 325 Green Valley Road, Griffin, Georgia, USA, 30224.
The facility site size is 6 acres (5,100m²).
The facility has 100 employees.
source_title: Perkins Griffin: the 400 Series | Perkins

facts: Perkins unveiled an expanded lineup of power systems, services, and innovative technologies at bauma 2025.
The unveiling reinforced Perkins' commitment to helping OEMs build machines engineered for greater efficiency, productivity, and fuel flexibility.
The upcoming Perkins 2600 Series is a new 13-litre diesel engine platform set for availability in 2026.
The 2600 Series is designed to meet the rigorous demands of construction and other off-road, heavy-duty applications.
The 2600 Series delivers power density, torque, fuel efficiency, and productivity.
The engine will be available in eight power ratings ranging from 340 to 515 kW (456 to 690 hp).
The 2600 Series offers up to 3,200 Nm of peak torque.
The 2600 Series extends Perkins’ leadership in the high-performance, mid-sized engine category.
A new 106 kW variant of the 904 Series platform was displayed at the event.
The new 904 Series variant features a 6% power increase over the existing 100 kW model.
The new 904 Series variant features a 3% boost in torque.
The 904 Series is a compact yet powerful engine ideal for OEMs seeking enhanced performance without increasing machine size.
Perkins introduced a new battery electric power unit technical demonstrator at bauma 2025.
The battery electric power unit was installed in a pipe fusion machine as a plug-and-play solution.
The plug-and-play solution is engineered to help off-highway OEMs transition smoothly from diesel to battery electric powertrains.
This introduction expands Perkins’ growing portfolio of advanced power solutions.
source_title: Perkins Showcases Next-Gen Power Systems - Plant & Equipment News

facts: MiddleGround Capital acquired Peterson Spring, a leading specialty spring manufacturer with thirteen facilities throughout North America and the United Kingdom.
The acquisition marks the closure of the second transaction in MiddleGround Capital's inaugural private equity fund.
Peterson Spring is based in Southfield, Michigan, and designs and manufactures highly engineered springs, wire forms, related components, and sub-assemblies.
Peterson Spring provides mission-critical components to a variety of industries.

MiddleGround Capital co-founder John Stewart stated the investment seeks to build upon the company's strong foundation, position it for growth, and ensure manufacturing employment opportunities for generations to come.
MiddleGround Capital Partner Scot Duncan stated the investment will enable the company to meet growing customer demands by increasing operational efficiency and providing necessary resources.

Co-founders John Stewart and Scot Duncan have over 30 years of combined experience in operational roles at Toyota Motor Company, including leadership positions in engineering, maintenance, production, and manufacturing.
Mr. Stewart, Ms. Mulholland, and Mr. Duncan have been working together in private equity for the past eight years as of April 2019.
source_title: MiddleGround Capital Acquires Peterson Spring - MiddleGround Capital

canonical_name: Northwest Georgia Joint Economic Development Authority
facts: Georgia's upper left hand corner offers superior accessibility, stunning vistas and a work force primed for the manufacturing renaissance.
Economic development in the region is led by the Northwest Georgia Joint Development Authority.
The authority is located just minutes from Chattanooga, Tennessee.
The Northwest Georgia JDA is comprised of Catoosa, Chattooga, Dade and Walker Counties.
The region features high performing schools, top healthcare facilities and a low cost of living.
The counties are located on or near three of the nation’s interstates: I-75, I-59, and I-24.
The region is also located on or near U.S. Highway 27, known as "The Scenic Hometown Highway."
Northwest Georgia has abundant access to water sources in the area.
The region is less than two and a half hours from Atlanta, Nashville, and Knoxville.
Northwest Georgia offers easy access to rail systems and commercial airports.
The area provides a highly skilled workforce.
Higher education opportunities are available at technical colleges as well as research universities.
The counties are located in the right-to-work state of Georgia.
The region is positioned as the best place in the Southeastern U.S. for manufacturing or distribution businesses.

links.context: Contact phone number for Northwest Georgia Joint Development Authority

source_title: Northwest Georgia Joint Economic Development Authority

facts: Piolax is known worldwide for its degree of excellence in automobile parts and is committed to growing along with its customers.
Due to a commitment to supply quality products to the automotive industries, the company established a location in the United States.
Piolax US began production in Canton, Georgia in August 1988.
Piolax is a leading supplier of plastic and metal products that continually strengthens its position by expanding global marketing and production capabilities.
The company provides in-house manufacturing for plastic and metal fastening products.
In August 1988, KHK established the Georgia Corporation.
In January 1990, KHK of America was merged with Piolax Corporation.
In October 1991, the company purchased a 48,000 sq ft building for plastic fastener production.
In April 1993, the facility expanded by 6,000 sq ft for a heat treat factory.
In April 1995, the facility expanded by 23,000 sq ft for plastic fastener and assembly parts production.
In August 1996, the company achieved ISO-9002 registration.
In May 1997, the company achieved QS-9000 registration.
In January 2000, the company implemented a new SAP ERP system.
In August 2001, the company started operations in Mexico with a 30,000 sqft facility.
In November 2001, the facility expanded by 25,000 sqft for assembly, plastic, and warehouse space.
In November 2003, the company achieved TS-16949 registration.
In January 2004, the company achieved ISO-14001 registration.
In July 2004, TS-16949 registration was achieved at the Mexico plant.
In April 2006, the Mexico facility expanded by 30,000 sq ft for production.
In January 2012, the Mexico plant became an independent corporation.
In October 2015, in-house metal surface treatment production started.
In September 2016, the facility expanded by 80,500 sq ft for an automated warehouse.
In December 2017, the company achieved IATF-16949 certification.
In July 2018, the facility expanded by 5,000 sq ft for #3 heat treat machinery for metals production.
In August 2018, the company celebrated its 30th anniversary.
In September 2018, the Michigan Sales & Design office moved to a new location in Plymouth, Michigan.
In July 2019, an additional 26,719 sq ft building was added for plastic and assembly production.
source_title: About PIOLAX| Plastic & Metal Fastening Products Manufacturer

facts: Piolax USA is a Japanese-owned supplier of plastic and metal parts with operations in Canton for nearly 30 years.
Piolax USA is set to hire 30 workers as part of an 82,000-square-foot warehouse expansion planned for next year.
The new hires will augment the existing 255-person workforce at the manufacturing facility.
The manufacturing facility is located in the Canton-Cherokee Business & Industrial Park.
Steve Holcomb, Cherokee Office of Economic Development vice chairman, stated they are extremely proud to have this well-established company in their county.
Piolax operates facilities globally in Mexico, China, Korea, Thailand, and many other countries.
source_title: Japanese Auto Supplier to Add 30 Jobs in Canton - Global Atlanta

facts: Piolax Corporation held a grand opening ceremony for its automated warehouse in Canton, Georgia on September 30th.

Approximately 100 people attended the event, including Consul General Shinozuka, President Shimazu from Piolax Inc. in Japan, Canton Mayor Hobgood, Cherokee County Economic Development Officer President Martin, and other officials and representatives.
Piolax Corporation is a US subsidiary of Piolax Inc., Japan, with Genichi Ishikawa serving as its president.
The company specializes in manufacturing plastic and metal automotive parts.
In light of recent trade expansion in the United States, Piolax Corporation established its own automated warehouse to replace previously rented warehouse space.
The new fully automated warehouse will increase storage capacity.
The facility will reduce more than half the current forklifts used.
The automation initiative will increase safety and efficiency of the warehouse operations.
source_title: Consulate-General of Japan in Atlanta

facts: Piolax Corp. (Georgia) is located at 139 Etowah Industrial Court, Canton, GA 30114 US.
Piolax Ltd. serves as a supplier to Piolax Corp. (Georgia).
Shipment records document transactions between the two entities on 2025-06-26, 2025-02-28, and 2024-08-30.
All recorded shipments are categorized as Bill of Lading documents.
Specific product details for the shipments are redacted in the source data.
source_title: Piolax Corp. (Georgia), 139 ETOWAH INDUSTRIAL COURT CANTONA GA 30114 US | Buyer Report — Panjiva

facts: Piolax Corporation USA was established on August 8th, 1988.
The company manufactures and delivers automobile-related parts for Japanese companies such as Nissan and Honda.
The company supplies automobile-related parts to the Big Three automakers GM, Ford, and FCA.
PIOLAX America installed an electric vehicle charging station in its parking lot.
The Georgia facility is identified as PIOLAX CORPORATION PA (Georgia).
source_title: Report from correspondents – PIOLAX, INC.

facts: Cherokee County, near the top of the Metro Atlanta region, has been hard hit by the recession but prepared itself with investments to enhance employment and correct a 70-plus percent commuter imbalance.
Leaders hope residential subdivisions filling again will keep residents working and shopping closer to home.
Gene Hobgood, mayor of Cherokee’s county seat Canton, is optimistic about the long term despite having enough homes to last 100 years at current rates.
The county doubled in population from 2000 to 2009, reaching 210,000 residents.
Revitalizing downtown Canton is a priority for Mayor Hobgood to stimulate commercial activity.
Canton’s downtown has a new 21,000-square-foot office/retail building, the first new non-governmental building there in 35 years.
Highridge Partners, consisting of locals Stan Rogers and Chuck Ware, invested in the downtown building and already have two attorney offices.
Cherokee County is finishing a Liveable Centers Initiative project funded by the Atlanta Regional Commission to acquire right-of-way for sidewalks and drainage projects from downtown to south Canton.
The LCI grant area extends into Holly Springs, south of Canton.
Holly Springs Mayor Tim Downing aims to create a three-sided town square with City Hall mixed with commercial and residential uses, similar to Smyrna and Cumming.
Implementation funding for the Holly Springs town square was delayed due to the economy and tight lending.
Transportation needs are serious throughout Cherokee County; GDOT recently finished widening Highway 92 through part of the county.
Mayor Downing hopes for more funding from a T-SPLOST referendum in 2012 to improve Highway 20, which runs through Holly Springs and could redirect trucks and commuters from I-575 and I-75.
Improving Georgia 20 and potentially Georgia Highway 140 could significantly relieve congestion in Metro Atlanta by creating an alternative east-west corridor.
GDOT has released contracts to widen a portion of Highway 20 through Gwinnett.
Cherokee County abandoned the Northern Loop outer perimeter concept previously floated by former Gov. Roy Barnes.
Woodstock Mayor Donnie Henriques says two blocks were torn up in downtown to make it more pedestrian friendly, requiring people to cross Highway 5.
A new interchange is going in at Ridgewalk by Rope Mill Road with additional retail space.
Woodstock partnered with the county on the Elm Street Cultural Arts Village, initiated by grassroots nonprofit Elm Street Alliance.
Four acres were purchased downtown for the arts village, including a 100-year-old farmhouse to be renovated.
The arts village is integrated with the Greenprints trail system, another city-county partnership.
Cherokee voters approved a parks bond in 2008, leading to strategic parks development within budget.
Woodstock hopes its eco-cultural destination status will bolster chances of relocating the Georgia Music Hall Of Fame from Macon.
West of Woodstock, widening Highway 92 from I-75 has boosted industrial development.
Cherokee County purchased 100 acres fronting I-75 for industrial growth.
Growth is occurring at Cherokee Commerce Center, a private industrial property owned by Majestic Realty.
Consumer Products Services moved into 50,000 square feet at the center to repair damaged products.
Curt Manufacturing, which distributes trailer hitches, occupies 34,000 square feet of space at the center.
The Cherokee Commerce Center sits right on Interstate 75 with lots of frontage and is performing well.
Misti Martin, executive director of the Cherokee Office of Economic Development, states the county is well positioned for industrial growth.
The district was designated as an Opportunity Zone by the state Department of Community Affairs.
Commission Chairman L.B. “Buzz” Ahrens, Jr. notes that widening Highway 92 dramatically improved logistics and the Opportunity Zone adds a major fiscal incentive for employers.
A key ingredient in Cherokee’s industrial growth strategy is the 400-student Chattahoochee Technical College campus, which opened last year in Ball Ground.
The $12-million tech school campus backs up to the Bluffs of Technology Park, an almost full industrial park expected to grow significantly.
Enrollment at Reinhardt University in Waleska is currently 1,275 and continues to rise.
Kennesaw State University in Cobb County, located just south of Cherokee, is another fast-growing campus part of the Cherokee strategy.
The county is marketing heavy investments in the Cherokee County Airport as a magnet for future growth.
An airport area master plan was developed over the last year to ensure quality business corporate development.
The runway extension from 3,414 to 5,000 feet is almost complete.
The county built a 10,000-square-foot passenger terminal that opened in 2010.
Pam Carnes, Cherokee County Chamber of Commerce president and CEO, believes the Wilbanks-Sosebee Terminal will be a flagship for visitors and corporate traffic.
Plans are underway to build a connector road between Bluffs of Technology Park and the airport via Highway 5.
A white-collar recruitment strategy began 18 months ago to address commuter imbalance by matching resident skill sets and pay ranges with local opportunities.
An employer survey indicates Cherokee can accommodate five to 10 employers with highly skilled employees, leveraging training at Chattahoochee Tech, Reinhardt, and KSU.
Chart Industries, a biomedical company, is building the county’s first LEED-Certified manufacturing facility.
The LEED certification was acknowledged as part of a Going Green effort.
Chart makes gas production and storage equipment and is expanding its presence with a new facility in the Interstate 575/Airport Industrial Park.
Chart purchased the last lot in the industrial park after working on the deal for a long time.
Chart will hire more than 80 people and invest $6 million, creating white-collar jobs following their relocation of labs and regional offices from Cobb a few years ago.
Northside Hospital-Cherokee announced in 2008 it would spend $8 million to expand its emergency room and offer other services.
The hospital expansion will create some 40 jobs and offer robotic-assisted surgery for general, gynecological, and urology procedures.
Mayor Hobgood notes the hospital expansion will create off-campus office jobs beneficial for tax collections.
Piolax, a universal alloy corporation, has expanded in Cherokee County.
Belnick Inc., an office furniture distributor, moved into the vacant Herman Miller facility, which is 327,000 square feet and one of the largest industrial buildings in the county.
Parks are being developed along the Etowah River to link cities’ LCI districts with trails.
Canton is building a new city park on 65 acres on Brown Industrial Parkway along the Etowah with multiple fields and walking areas.
A new trail on the northern end of Heritage Park crosses over the river to continue along it to Boling Park.
Canton is upgrading zoning, subdivision, and development regulations to manage growth quality.
Woodstock’s Greenprints Project will add 60 miles of trails connecting city spaces and public places in southwest Cherokee.
The Greenprints Alliance holds a Streetfest in March to raise funds and awareness for trail projects.
Cherokee County purchased 541 acres on Yellow Creek Road near the Etowah River, increasing green space inventory to 1,500 acres.
This purchase links Boling Park in Canton to Shoal Creek near the Georgia National Cemetery in Sutallee.
Sales tax collections dropped from $250,000 a month four years ago to $175,000 a month due to revenue downturns limiting capital projects.
Cherokee County extended its Special Purpose Local Option Sales Tax in 2010, but it is mainly for debt service.
Canton is backing the Hickory Log Creek Reservoir, a drought-contingency reservoir north of the city.
The city has $60 million in debt, mostly bonded, with a big chunk for the 300-acre reservoir owned by the city and Cobb County-Marietta Water Authority.
Only 25 percent of the reservoir debt is Canton’s responsibility, amounting to more than $22 million.
Top five industries in Cherokee County include Pilgrim’s Pride, Universal Alloy Corporation, Meyn America, Chart Industries, and Piolax Corporation.
source_title: Cherokee County: Optimism And Resolve - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: Pirelli announced an investment in a U.S. factory in Georgia as part of its strategic growth plan for the North American market.
The investment aims to help PTNA sustain its market gains and the 30 percent sales increase realized over the past three years.
Pirelli Tire North America designs, develops, manufactures and markets tires for passenger vehicles in both the original equipment and replacement markets.
Pirelli Tire North America also markets and distributes tires for motorcycles and motor sports.
The company's Modular Integrated Robotized System (MIRS) in Georgia employs state-of-the-art technology to manufacture tires for both export and domestic markets.
Pirelli has just completed its three-year tenure as the official tire supplier to the FIA World Rally Championship.
Pirelli was awarded the exclusive agreement to supply tires for the FIA World Rally Championship in 2008.
Since being awarded the agreement in 2008, Pirelli has supplied a total of 27,995 tires for use on 40 WRC events on snow, ice, asphalt and gravel.
Of those 27,995 tires, only 43 suffered failures on the punishing terrain encountered on world championship rallies during almost 9000 miles of special stages.
In 2011, Pirelli begins its three-year agreement as the official tire supplier to the FIA Formula One World.
source_title: Pirelli Pumps Up U.S. Factory in Georgia

facts: Global electric vehicle market projected to reach $6.5 billion by 2030 according to a Grand View Research report.
Tire manufacturers are transitioning entire passenger/CUV/SUV portfolios to be EV-ready rather than creating EV-only or EV-dedicated SKUs.
Top tire technology focuses include lowering rolling resistance, increasing load capacity, and enhancing durability to handle heavier EVs with instant torque.
Dealers should focus on customer education regarding proper tire maintenance and reinforcing trade-offs and efficiencies when selling and servicing EV tires.

Leading tire manufacturers are shifting their entire passenger/CUV/SUV portfolio to be EV-ready instead of creating dedicated EV-only SKUs, according to Keith Calcagno, chief strategy officer for proprietary brands at American Tire Distributors.
The U.S. vehicle car parc is getting heavier, creating an exceeding emphasis on tires to handle torque, weight, noise, and rolling resistance.
Innovations in tire compounding, structural belt package, and pattern/sidewall designs are pushing the frontier to level up every tire produced.

Apollo Tyres Ltd. is focused on tuning noise and rolling resistance to support range without compromising wear as the market shifts toward EV-compatible products, according to Karl Jin, divisional head of product and pricing.
Many EV owners expect ICE-like tire life despite added weight and instant torque accelerating wear, making customer education equally important.

Atturo Tire Corp. positions any tire meeting the correct size, load index, and speed rating as suitable for hybrid or EV use, with matching the tire to driver intent being paramount, according to president Michael Mathis.

Two key technological advances for EV tires are the heavy-load load index designation addressing increased curb weight and instantaneous torque, and cold-weather suitable tread compound engineering utilizing 3-Peak Mountain Snowflake ratings.

U.S. EV tire development focuses on extending wear life, lowering rolling resistance, and reducing noise because EVs lack engine vibration to mask road noise, according to Ian McKenney, senior product manager at Bridgestone Americas Inc.

Continental Tire the Americas LLC has optimized its tires for over 10 years to achieve lower rolling resistance, low rolling noise, and long service lives with zero compromise on safety, benefiting vehicles with electric drive systems, according to Björn Gläser, head of product for PLT.

EV-specific tires target energy efficiency and range as a main benefit while giving up treadwear mileage, which is the exact same trade-off any OE tire makes, according to David C. Poling, vice president of R&D and technical center at Giti Tire North America Inc.

Proper tire pressure maintenance is a key factor in driving rolling resistance down, increasing vehicle range, and reducing CO2 emissions, according to Jenny Walker, director of global product strategy at Goodyear Tire & Rubber Co.

Noise comfort has become an important development factor as drivers become more aware of tire and road interaction noise, which is particularly relevant for EVs generating as little as half the amount of noise as traditional vehicles due to the absence of an ICE.

U.S. EV tire development is moving in five directions: lower rolling resistance, higher load capacity, lower noise, better wear performance, and a dual-track strategy of EV-specific plus EV-compatible products, according to David Wang, managing director at Gripmax Tires Inc.

Range remains a major purchase consideration for U.S. consumers, making rolling-resistance optimization a core engineering priority alongside addressing heavier weight, instant torque, and exposed tire noise.

Hankook Tire America Corp. focuses EV tire development on managing weight, torque, and noise through advanced compounds for lower rolling resistance, reinforced structures for durability, and tread designs extending wear under instant torque, according to Robert Nasca, product training manager.
Kumho Tire U.S.A. Inc. targets both electric and internal combustion engine vehicles simultaneously with its product portfolio strategy, according to Shawn Denlein, president of sales and marketing.
The U.S. EV tire market is evolving around efficiency, noise reduction, and durability, with improving rolling resistance directly impacting driving range, which remains a top consumer concern.

Manufacturers are focusing on advanced noise reduction technologies such as optimized tread patterns and internal sound-absorbing materials because electric vehicles operate much more quietly than internal combustion vehicles.

Heavier EV weight and instant torque require tires with reinforced structures and enhanced wear resistance to maintain durability and performance, according to Jay Lee, product planning director at Nexen Tire America Inc.
The industry is increasingly exploring advanced materials, sustainable compounds, and smart tire technologies that can monitor real-time performance.

Nokian Tyres Inc. has worked on EV innovations for many years as a Nordic company where electric vehicle sales are thriving, with every tire produced intended to suit the needs of EVs, according to Dave Johnston, director of portfolio, pricing and business planning.

Omni United takes an approach of making all new premium collection tires EV-compatible because tire requirements for EVs overlap with high-performance combustion engine vehicles on durability, stability, high load capacity, low rolling resistance, and low noise.

Pirelli Tire North America Inc. identifies key innovation trends including low rolling resistance compounds to maximize battery efficiency and extend driving range, enhanced load capacity and reinforced structures for heavier platforms, noise reduction technologies like foam inserts, and instant torque durability requiring new tread compounds and patterns.

Prinx Chengshan Tire North America is not launching EV-specific tires for the U.S. market but its next-generation touring, H/T, and ultra-high performance lines will feature lower rolling resistance and popular EV sizes, according to Ken Coltrane, vice president of marketing and product development.

Sailun Tire Americas centers the EV tire conversation on range, durability, and noise, with rapid advancements in low rolling resistance, compounds extending range, reinforced constructions for heavier vehicles and instant torque, and noise-optimized tread patterns enhancing comfort.

Tire Group International LLC observes a clear shift toward lower rolling resistance to maximize range, higher load capacity to support heavier vehicles, reduced road noise, and more durable compounds to handle instant torque and prevent premature wear.
Smarter tire monitoring is emerging as efficiency and performance are directly tied to how the tire is maintained, according to Joaquin Gonzalez, president of Tire Group International LLC.

Toyo Tire U.S.A. Corp. notes that EVs are generally heavier than comparable internal combustion vehicles, making instant torque and added weight tough on tires, while drivers are more concerned about vehicle range due to charging time and infrastructure still being in its infancy.

The largest advancements in tire technology will come from the necessity to improve compounding, construction, and materials to support EV vehicles while reducing environmental impact, according to Todd Bergeson, senior manager of product planning and technical services at Toyo Tire U.S.A. Corp.

For the U.S. replacement market, EV tire demand prioritizes tire mileage and overall cost efficiency more than OE tires, which develop with strong emphasis on noise reduction, rolling resistance, and handling performance, according to Jeffrey Zhang, general manager of international business at Zhongce Rubber Group Co. Ltd./ZC Rubber.
source_title: How Tiremakers Are Advancing EV Tire Technology | Modern Tire Dealer

facts: Pirelli Tire North America specializes in the manufacture and marketing of high-performance car, light truck and motorcycle tires and currently has three highly advanced MIRS (Modular Integrated Robotized System) modules in operation in a new factory in Rome, Georgia, to better serve the American OEM and Replacement markets.
source_title: Pirelli Tires at Sonoma

facts: Pirelli is reinforcing its long-term commitment to the United States with a key step in its product and industrial strategy.
Investment and output expansion details for the Georgia plant will be communicated in the coming months following the finalization of development plans.

The Georgia plant, already dedicated to technologically advanced products for the U.S. market in the High-Value segment and Motorsport, will be enhanced by the production of connected tires featuring Cyber™ Tyre technology.
This development highlights the strategic importance of the U.S. for Pirelli’s global growth roadmap and strengthens the company’s integrated industrial and technological presence in the country.
The announcement was made as Pirelli participated in the SelectUSA Investment Summit, a flagship event promoted by the U.S. Department of Commerce.
Cyber™ Tyre is being showcased at the summit as a core innovation shaping the future of smart mobility.

Cyber™ Tyre is the world’s first hardware-and-software system capable of collecting data and information from sensors embedded in tires, processing them through Pirelli’s proprietary software and algorithms, and communicating in real time with the vehicle’s electronics.
The technology enables new functionalities integrated with driving and control systems to enhance the driving experience and increase safety levels, as well as supporting connected infrastructure.
In the highly advanced U.S. market where digital and smart mobility solutions are rapidly expanding, Cyber™ Tyre represents a distinctive competitive advantage for Pirelli.
At SelectUSA, Cyber™ Tyre was showcased at the Georgia State booth, highlighting Pirelli’s industrial footprint in the state where it has been established for more than 20 years.
Claudio Zanardo, CEO of Pirelli North America, stated that the start of Cyber™ Tyre production in the Rome, Georgia plant is a significant milestone for Pirelli in the country.

The production start reflects Pirelli’s commitment to bringing advanced technologies like Cyber™ Tyre closer to the market and further strengthening its industrial footprint and innovation capabilities in the United States.
To enhance Rome as a high-tech production site, Pirelli is finalizing the introduction of the latest version of the MIRS (Modular Integrated Robotized System) production process.
The MIRS process will be the most advanced manufacturing process for high-end, premium production within the entire Pirelli Group and is exclusive to the Georgia factory.
The new process further enhances robotized production capabilities, increasing productivity, flexibility, and quality.
It is a highly digitalized system that enables a direct link between product design and its industrial application.
This development lays the groundwork for growth in Pirelli’s production capacity in Rome, which is an integral part of the Group’s industrial development plans.
The Rome, Georgia plant specializes in high-value tire production for the North American market.
The facility also hosts a dedicated R&D center, further strengthening its role in technological development and product innovation.
The facility is recognized for responsible sourcing practices, including the use of FSC-certified natural rubber, underscoring the company’s broader environmental commitment across its supply chain.
source_title: PIRELLI WILL START CYBER™ TYRE PRODUCTION IN GEORGIA UNDERLINING THE STRATEGIC IMPORTANCE OF THE U.S. MARKET

facts: The Italian government stepped in to limit China’s control over Pirelli.
Pirelli has been given the go-ahead to begin manufacturing its latest series of tires in the United States with help from the Italian government.
Pirelli’s plant in Rome, Georgia will soon start manufacturing connected tires using Cyber Tyre technology.

The Cyber Tyre tech includes sensors that can collect data and work in conjunction with a vehicle’s on-board electronics, including the ABS and stability control systems, to enhance driving dynamics and safety.
For more than a year, there had been concerns that Pirelli would be blocked from manufacturing in the US because the Chinese company Sinochem holds a 34.1 percent stake in the tire giant.
With the US government clamping down on Chinese technology, it seemed likely Pirelli would have to build these tires elsewhere.
The Italian government stepped in using its ‘golden powers’ rule established to protect companies that are of national strategic interest.

Last month, Italy imposed several curbs on Sinochem, noting that none of the eight board members that Sinochem has previously appointed will be allowed to hold top corporate positions like chairman or chief executive.
Sinochem has been instructed that it cannot exert any influence over Pirelli, and Pirelli does not need to share sensitive information with the Chinese company.
Pirelli’s plant in Georgia can currently produce up to 400,000 tires annually.
The company did not disclose how many tires using its Cyber Tyre tech will be added to its production.
Pirelli North America chief executive Claudio Zanardo said the start of Cyber Tyre production in the Rome, Georgia plant is a significant milestone for Pirelli in this country.

Zanardo stated the move reflects Pirelli’s commitment to bringing advanced technologies like Cyber Tyre closer to the market, further strengthening its industrial footprint and innovation capabilities in the United States.
source_title: Pirelli’s Connected Tires Will Be Made In Georgia, The US Almost Blocked Them Over China - NewsBreak

facts: Pirelli announces that Pietro Berardi, who currently serves as CEO of Pirelli Tire North America, is leaving the company to pursue a new professional challenge.
Pirelli Tire North America will tap Claudio Zanardo as its new CEO, effective November 1st.
Mr. Zanardo will join the North American team from Pirelli's Milan headquarters, where he has most recently served as Senior Vice President Moto and Velo.
Born in 1979, Claudio Zanardo has extensive experience in the automotive industry.
In 2004 he joined Ducati Motor Holding, covering roles of increasing responsibility and, most recently, held the position of Head of Sales Strategies and Planning.
In 2015 he joined Pirelli, taking charge of Marketing and Controlled Retail for Region Europe until assuming responsibility for Driver Italia (Pirelli's franchise network) in 2016.
He was then appointed Country Chief Executive Officer of Italy, before moving to the role, starting from January 2020, of Senior Vice President Moto.
In February 2021 he was also given responsibility for Business Velo.
As CEO of the North American region, Zanardo will be responsible for the car, motorcycle and cycling businesses, as well as manufacturing facilities in Rome, Georgia, and Silao, Guanajuato, Mexico.
"It's an honor to join the Pirelli team in North America, which is one of the most important markets for the company" said Zanardo.

"The local team has done an incredible job of growing our brand and presence in the Region and increasing product offering tailored specifically for this market. I am thrilled to build on this legacy and bring my knowledge of the industry, combined with my recent position at Pirelli's headquarters, to continue fueling our momentum" said Zanardo.
"It has been a true honor to lead the North American team and lay the foundation for continued future growth in this market" said Mr. Berardi.
"While I'm sad to leave a talented team and exciting marketplace, I'm also looking forward to the new challenges and opportunities that await me in my career." said Mr. Berardi.
Pirelli thanks Mr. Berardi for his contribution to the company over his years of service.
Pirelli Communications and Media Relations contact is Maria Stella Narciso.
source_title: Pirelli Tire North America To Name New CEO - Press Release

facts: Pirelli will commence production of its advanced Cyber Tyre at its facility in Rome, Georgia.
Management is set to provide further details during the first quarter 2026 results announcement.
The Georgia plant is Pirelli's sole production facility in the US.
The Georgia plant currently satisfies approximately 5% of Pirelli's exposure in the country.
Cyber Tyre is a small volume product.
Production of the Cyber Tyre in the US indicates a new level of comfort from the US Department of Commerce regarding Pirelli's capital expenditure and technology manufacturing.
Citi analysts interpret this development as a long-term positive for Pirelli's stock.
The move suggests the company may have an improved ability to pursue factory expansion in the United States following recent governance changes.
Analysts believe any future capacity increase can be funded with existing capital expenditure budgets.
Funding future capacity increases will enable a more localized manufacturing footprint over time.
Producing a technology-rich product like the Cyber Tyre locally is a strategic indicator of Pirelli's commitment to the US market.
This production decision could pave the way for broader operational expansion and strengthen the company's regional supply chain.
Producing Cyber Tyres in the US signals increased confidence from US authorities.
The development could be a precursor to larger investments and factory expansions in the country.
Financial analysts view the news as a long-term positive development that may improve Pirelli's ability to expand its US manufacturing base.
source_title: Pirelli's US Cyber Tyre Production Signals Expansion | TrustFinance Blog

facts: Pirelli & C SpA is based in Milan, Italy.
Pirelli & C SpA is the world’s fifth largest tyre manufacturer.
Pirelli operates 22 factories in 13 countries around the world.
Pirelli is currently expanding its operations in a number of locations, including Asia.
North America is one of Pirelli's fast-growing markets for both OEM and replacement tyre sales.
Pirelli increased its production of tyres within North America to significantly improve customer service rates and reduce logistics costs.
Pirelli opened a second plant in Silao, Mexico during 2012.
The opening of the Silao, Mexico plant served as a watershed for Pirelli's logistics in North America.
The Silao plant expansion led to more regional tyre production, growth in local souring, and speedier lead times.
source_title: A stronger grip on the market | Feature | Automotive Logistics

facts: Pirelli Tire North America (PTNA) CEO Claudio Zanardo states the company is in growth mode with a positive first half of 2024 driven by new products and partner cooperations.
The U.S. consumer tire market is flat, but the high-value market for 18- and 19-inch tires and up is showing resilience and growing.
The 19-inch tire market in the U.S. is growing by double digits.
The EV tire market and all-weather segment are growing and supporting Pirelli's growth.

Pirelli makes a concentrated investment in designing and manufacturing EV-specific tires to meet different requirements for weight, torque, and driving dynamics without compromising performance, quality, or handling.
Pirelli's strategy is based on achieving 'perfect fitment' by developing dedicated products specifically for each car model.
Research and development of new materials for EV tires benefits Pirelli's entire tire range.
Pirelli's Elect EV tire line has attained more than 550 OE homologations worldwide for EV tires.
The Elect technology is the driver of growth in the EV tire segment, with growth pursued through both the OE and replacement markets.
In North America, Pirelli is working in partnership with pure electric vehicle players like Rivian and Tesla, as well as traditional automakers.

Pirelli's plant in Mexico recently produced its 50th millionth tire and receives continuous investment in growth, new technologies, machinery, production capacity, and R&D for North American products.
Pirelli's Rome, Georgia plant produces small lots of very high-technology tires and is dedicated to small product runs, contrasting with the high-volume production at its Mexico facility.
Pirelli launched the Pirelli Performance associate dealer program at the end of 2023 and is currently expanding it nationwide with monthly feature additions.
Pirelli's biggest priority for the second half of the year is efficiently serving huge demand, developing new products, and improving product quality and brand power in the North American market.

facts: Pirelli brand is previewing its new TP Commercial Solutions program at the 2017 Mid America Trucking Show (MATS), the largest annual heavy-duty trucking show in the world.

TP Commercial Solutions is ready to launch the sales and marketing of the Pirelli Brand of radial truck tires, agricultural tires and off-the road tires along with the Formula brand in the U.S. and Canada.

Clif Armstrong, president of TP Commercial Solutions, announced that the company has the start of a solid product line-up now available for sale that upholds Pirelli’s iconic brand values and quality after exhaustive testing.
The products underwent a rigorous R&D and testing process with multiple fleets in multiple applications to develop tires for the specific needs of the U.S market.
Pentathlon D is a premium long haul drive tire that is SmartWay verified, offering extremely high-mileage, and thanks to EcoImpact features, fuel savings and low emission benefits.
G:85 Series for On/Off Road includes FG:85 and TG:85 tires specifically designed for the steer and drive on/off road application.
FH:01 Motor Coach is designed for the demanding bus fleet market for long haul and regional all position use.

Formula Driver II All Position rib is part of the Formula Brand, a Pirelli Tier II brand that will be launched in the U.S./Canada, starting with limited sizes and then expanding to a full product line.
TP Commercial Solutions supplies commercial truck tires and fleet solutions with the Pirelli brand to the global market.
TP Commercial Solutions LLC maintains the renowned quality and heritage of the Pirelli family dedicated to the North American market.
TP Commercial Solutions LLC headquarters are in Rome, Georgia.
Pirelli has been a world leader in tire technology for over 140 years.

facts: Mauro Pessi will assume the position of CEO of Pirelli Tire North America, effective October 1, 2009.
Pessi previously served as VP of sales and marketing and CEO of Latin American operations at Pirelli before departing in 2008 to become CEO at Brembo.
Pessi will replace Hugh Pace, who is retiring after having been chairman and CEO of the company for nearly three years.
Hugh Pace will continue his role as chairman of Pirelli Tire North America through year end 2009.
Pirelli Tire North America designs, develops, manufactures, and markets tires for passenger vehicles in both the original equipment and replacement markets.
The company also markets and distributes tires for motorcycles and motorsports.
Pirelli's facility is located in Rome, Georgia.
The Rome, Georgia facility employs a Modular Integrated Robotized System (MIRS) with state-of-the-art technology to manufacture tires for both export and domestic markets.
source_title: Mauro Pessi Named New CEO of Pirelli Tire North America - Auto Service World

facts: Pirelli expands its 2014 roadracing product range by introducing new 125 GP/Moto 3 rear tires to North America.
The new 115/70R17 Slicks come in three compounds (SC0, SC1, & SC2) and match up with the current 100/70R17 slick front.
These new tires were developed in Europe with top Moto3 and 125GP teams and provide improved handling, performance, and grip over the recently discontinued 125/70R17 rear slick size.
Jeff Johnston serves as Pirelli Tire North America Roadracing Manager.
Pirelli offers a complete range of track day, slicks, intermediates and rain tires for almost any racing or track day application for small bore GP, 600 SS or Superbike riders.
These new rear slicks are readily available from Pirelli roadracing tire distributors and vendors.
Founded in 1872, Pirelli is the fifth largest tire manufacturer in the world in terms of sales.
Pirelli is present in over 160 countries, operates 22 manufacturing sites, and has over 36,000 employees worldwide.
Pirelli is a leading producer of premium tires due to its commitment to R&D.
The company has successfully competed in motorsport since 1907 and is the exclusive supplier of the Superbike world championship and many single marque championships around the world.
Pirelli has been the sole supplier for the Formula 1 championship since 2011.

Pirelli Tire North America (PTNA) services NAFTA member countries through its Modular Integrated Robotized System (MIRS) facility and research and development center at its Rome, Georgia headquarters.
PTNA also utilizes a state-of-the-art manufacturing plant in Silao, Mexico and sales and marketing offices in New York City, Montreal, and Mexico City.
The company manufactures, distributes and markets original equipment and replacement tires for export and domestic car/motorcycle applications (US, CA, MX) and Agro/Heavy Truck platforms (MX).

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) and Nobu Hotel Atlanta teamed up to create a first-of-its-kind immersive hospitality experience for travelers.
The collaboration features the new Porsche Icon Suite and Porsche Classic Room at the newly opened Nobu Hotel Atlanta in Buckhead, located just north of PCNA's U.S. headquarters.
Ayesha Coker, Vice President of Marketing of PCNA, stated the goal is to provide an elevated experience for everyone who engages with the brand.
Nobu Hotel Atlanta offers all-electric Porsche Taycan house cars expertly navigated by dedicated personal drivers for guest excursions within a three-mile radius.
Rachael Palumbo, VP Global Hotel Branding at Nobu Hospitality, noted that Porsche's reputation as an automotive legend complements their global vision of delivering luxury hospitality.
The Porsche Icon Suite and Porsche Classic Room are available to book now at Nobu Hotel Atlanta.
Package excursions are subject to availability.
PCNA has been headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia since 1998.

The PCNA campus in Atlanta hosts the first Porsche Experience Center in North America, which features two module-based 1.6 mile driver development tracks, a business center, and Restaurant 356.
The same Atlanta campus houses the U.S. headquarters of Porsche Classic.
PCNA supports 197 independently owned and operated Porsche Centers in the U.S., including supplying parts, service, marketing, and training to them.
These supported centers work to provide Porsche customers with a best-in-class experience aligned with the brand's 75-year history of leadership in vehicle performance, safety, and efficiency.

facts: Porsche Cars North America officially opened its Porsche Experience Center and headquarters in Atlanta today.
The new facility is located off of One Porsche Drive in Atlanta, Georgia.
Porsche invested $100 million into the development of the property, making it the single largest investment the automaker has made in North America.
The 27-acre complex provides office space for PCNA, Porsche Financial Services, Porsche Business Services, Porsche Consulting, and Mieschke Hofmann & Partners.
The site includes a 1.6-mile driver development track, a classic Porsche gallery, a restoration center, a human performance center, a driving simulator lab, and a fine-dining restaurant named 356.
It is estimated that 30,000 guests will visit the Porsche Experience Center and headquarters.
More than 70 locations were considered for PCNA’s new headquarters before Atlanta was selected as the ideal location.
The facility will ultimately employ 450 people with more than 100 positions that are new to Georgia.

Matthias Müller, Chairman of the Executive Board of Porsche, stated that the opening celebrates a significant milestone in expanding Porsche's footprint in the United States and reaffirms the company's commitment to Georgia and Atlanta.

Detlev von Platen, President and CEO of Porsche Cars North America, described the new home as a bold, energizing environment designed to demonstrate the unique capabilities of Porsche sports cars, calling it the first of its kind in the automotive industry.

Atlanta Mayor Kasim Reed cited low cost of doing business, high quality of life, access to the world's busiest and most efficient passenger airport, and the fastest-growing container port in the country as reasons for choosing Atlanta.

facts: The 2020 Porsche Taycan 4S joins the Taycan Turbo and Turbo S at dealerships in preparation for customer deliveries.
Klaus Zellmer serves as President and CEO of PCNA.

The new Taycan 4S sits right at the heart of Porsche's electrified model line-up, delivering effortless performance and engaging dynamics expected of a Porsche sports car with the space and connectivity that make it viable for everyday use.
Dealers have developed innovative ways to support customers during challenging times.
The first of the Taycan 4S’s to arrive in the US will be delivered by dealers to awaiting customers in the coming days.
The jurors of the World Car of the Year Awards gave the Taycan two awards, a first for Porsche: ‘World Performance Car of the Year’ and ‘World Luxury Car of the Year.’
Powering the Taycan 4S is the 93.4-kWh Performance Battery Plus that allows its dual motors to generate up to 562 hp (420kW) using its overboost function and when launch control is activated.
When configured in this way, it can accelerate from a standstill to 60 mph in just 3.8 seconds.
Its top track speed is 155 mph, made possible through its two-speed gearbox.
The EPA estimated range for the Taycan 4S is 203 miles.
Supplemental third party range testing conducted by AMCI will be available on Porsche's website.
The Taycan was unveiled in September of 2019 and the global debut of the Taycan 4S, and subsequent media launch followed at the Los Angeles Auto Show in November.

Customers can collect their cars in person where feasible, or receive them via contactless home delivery as part of the innovative ‘Porsche at Your Service’ program for online sales, vehicle collection, delivery, and servicing flexibility.
The Taycan was designed and engineered from the ground up by a new team at Porsche, and built in a completely new factory located in Stuttgart-Zuffenhausen.

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) announced a new multi-million dollar investment to further develop its North American headquarters and U.S. footprint in Georgia.
The development is located at the northeast corner of Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.
The new 33-acre development adds to PCNA's existing 27-acre headquarters.

The first stage of development, which starts construction this month, will be a standalone Porsche Service Center independently developed, owned, and operated jointly by Hennessy Porsche North Atlanta and Porsche Atlanta Perimeter.
In later stages over the next three years, PCNA will invest in a new Porsche Classic Factory Restoration facility as well as a track extension for the Porsche Experience Center Atlanta.
Kjell Gruner, newly appointed President and CEO of PCNA, stated that Porsche is committed to the United States and its American hometown of Atlanta.
Gruner noted the expansion enhances customer experience by providing faster service at a unique location next to the world’s busiest airport.
The expansion starts a longer-term expansion of the Porsche Experience Center Atlanta, which has welcomed over 270,000 visitors since opening in 2015.
Governor Brian Kemp praised Porsche's growth in Georgia in partnership with two Atlanta area dealers.
Kemp stated that Georgia’s automotive industry is a $3 billion economic driver in the state and called Porsche an important part of their success story.
Mayor Alan Hallman of the City of Hapeville expressed excitement about PCNA launching the expansion of its North American headquarters within the Hapeville community.

Atlanta Mayor Keisha Lance Bottoms thanked Porsche for investing in Atlanta and stated her administration looks forward to working with Porsche’s leadership to strengthen the local economy through highly skilled and middle-wage opportunities.
The Porsche Service Center South Atlanta will be a 20,815 square foot facility on 2.5 acres of land.
The new service center facility will include 13 lift bays to service customer vehicles, a Porsche Driver’s Selection boutique, and a reception area and waiting lounge for customers.
It is one of two standalone service centers for Porsche in the U.S., offering greater service capacity and additional convenience for customers in Atlanta and surrounding cities.
The expected completion date for the service center is the third quarter of 2021.
Phase two of development is expected to begin in 2021 with construction of a parking structure.
Phase two will be followed by construction of the new Porsche Classic Factory Restoration facility and Porsche Experience Center Atlanta track extension, with an expected completion date of mid-2023.
source_title: Porsche commences new multi-million dollar development in Georgia adjacent to its North American Headquarters to expand its U.S. footprint - Porsche Newsroom USA

facts: The Porsche at Aerotropolis project is located in Atlanta, Georgia.
The site is highly visible and adjacent to Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.
In 2006, Ford Motor Company announced the closure of its assembly plant in Hapeville, Georgia, directly adjacent to Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.
Jacoby Development acquired the former Ford site.
Jacoby Development demolished its structures.
Jacoby Development put together an ambitious redevelopment plan for the site.
In 2011, Porsche Cars North America announced the plan to develop a new North American headquarters facility at the site.
The Porsche facility includes a 220,000 SF headquarters office building.
The facility includes the Porsche Technical Training Center.
The facility includes the Porsche Customer and Driving Experience Center.
The Porsche Customer and Driving Experience Center features a 1.6-mile test track and handling circuit.
Porsche opened its $100 million facility in 2015 to great acclaim.
An adjacent hotel opened in late 2017.
These investments have inspired additional investments and commercial developments around the airport.

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Incorporated operates a subscription program called Porsche Passport that allows members to access the latest models of Porsche cars for a monthly fee.
The program is currently only available in the greater Atlanta metropolitan area because Porsche Cars North America's headquarters is located there.
Members use an official mobile application to select vehicles, which are cleaned by a personal concierge and delivered to their home or office with contents transferred from the previous vehicle.

The program includes unlimited mileage, comprehensive liability insurance up to one million dollars for liability, maintenance, repairs, full detail washes, and roadside assistance, but excludes fuel.
The Launch membership plan provides access to four different Porsche models for $2,000.00 per month, or as low as $64.52 per day.
The Accelerate membership plan provides access to seven different Porsche models for $3,000.00 per month, or as low as $96.78 per day.
Both membership plans require a one-time activation fee of $500.00 and a minimum commitment period of 31 days.
The insurance coverage includes a $1,000.00 deductible that the member is responsible for paying on any claim.
Not all applicants are approved for the Porsche Passport program.
Service availability is restricted to a defined part of the greater Atlanta metropolitan area.
The article author previously rode in a Porsche courtesy of Delta Air Lines around the international airport serving the greater Atlanta metropolitan area.
source_title: Any Porsche You Want Any Time You Want for $100 Per Day — But... - The Gate

facts: Gov. Nathan Deal, Atlanta Mayor Kasim Reed, and Detlev von Platen, President and CEO of Porsche Cars North America, announced that the company will build a new headquarters in Georgia.
The project will create approximately 100 jobs over the next three years.
The investment totals up to $100 million.
The complex will be located in the “Aerotropolis Atlanta” development site in Hapeville, adjacent to the International Terminal under construction at Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.
The facility will eventually house a workforce of 400 employees.
The new headquarters will include modern office facilities, Porsche’s Technical Service and Training Center, and a Customer Experience Center featuring a test track facility.
Groundbreaking is planned for fall of this year (2013), with the move-in date scheduled for the second half of 2013.
The headquarters will house PCNA, Porsche Financial Services, Inc., and Porsche Business Services, Inc., relocating them from Chicago.
Georgia Quick Start, the state’s top-ranked workforce training program, has been offered to design and implement a customized training program for the new and existing workforce.
Project partners include the Atlanta Development Authority, the Fulton County Development Authority, the city of Hapeville, Georgia Power, Jacoby Development, and Cushman Wakefield.
The site is the former Hapeville Ford Assembly Plant in South Fulton County.
PCNA sold over 25,000 cars in 2010, finishing 33 percent ahead of the previous year.
PCNA will support continued success with a lineup including iconic sports cars, a new generation of Cayenne SUVs, and the Panamera Gran Turismo.
PCNA was established in 1984 as a wholly owned subsidiary of Stuttgart, Germany-based Porsche AG.
PCNA currently employs approximately 220 people who provide parts, service, marketing, and training for 196 dealers.
Gov. Nathan Deal stated that extending Southern hospitality to the Porsche team adds an incredible new destination to Georgia’s list of must-see places.
Detlev von Platen said the new American headquarters will make the organization stronger and demonstrates a commitment to remain a vital part of the Atlanta region.
Atlanta Mayor Kasim Reed welcomed the Porsche team to the innovative Aerotropolis complex, calling it a testament to the city's vitality.
Hapeville Mayor Alan Hallman expressed excitement about the project creating synergy for the entire Aerotropolis and putting South Fulton County on the map.
Scott Condra, Senior Vice President of Development for Jacoby Development, noted that attracting Porsche demonstrates Atlanta is ready for airport-adjacent development and redevelopment.
Porsche boasts a racing heritage with some 30,000 motorsport wins to date.
source_title: GA: Governor Deal Announces New Porsche Location | Trade and Industry Development

facts: RealTruck is a global aftermarket product and accessory brand and a digital destination for truck, Jeep®, Bronco® and off-road enthusiasts.
RealTruck is continuing to build momentum in the Original Equipment (OE) space by hiring John Thomas as the new general manager of OE.
John Thomas will guide the company's commercial and operational transformation into a world-class supplier to OEM partners.
Carl-Martin Lindahl serves as the CEO of RealTruck.

John Thomas brings more than 25 years of experience in the OEM and aftermarket automotive industry, where he has led, grown, and transformed organizations and built high-performing teams that delivered strong growth and profitability.

Thomas will leverage his expertise to drive business growth, integrate RealTruck's recent acquisition with Vehicle Accessories Group (VAI), and ensure all aspects of operations meet the evolving needs of OEM customers.

In recent years, Thomas served as global chief revenue and marketing officer of Ampure Charging Systems, chief executive officer of Advanced Automotive Advisors LLC, and chief operating officer of Autel Energy.
RealTruck has 30 manufacturing and distribution facilities located in the United States.
RealTruck employs more than 4,500 U.S.-based associates.
RealTruck distributes its products through an omnichannel retail approach that includes its online channel, a 12,000+ dealer network, and automotive OEM partnerships.

entity_name: Bosch Hydrogen Powertrain Systems (Chongqing) Co. Ltd.
canonical_name: Bosch Hydrogen Powertrain Systems (Chongqing) Co. Ltd.
facts: Bosch and Qingling Motors established a joint venture in China named Bosch Hydrogen Powertrain Systems (Chongqing) Co. Ltd.
The new company will develop, assemble, and market fuel cell systems, called Fuel Cell Power Modules, for the Chinese market.
The goal is to pool the technology and market expertise of both partners and contribute to the development of the Chinese fuel cell market and the transformation of the automotive industry there.

According to the Energy Saving and New Energy Vehicle Technology Roadmap 2.0 by the China Society of Automotive Engineers (China-SAE), more than one million vehicles with fuel cell drive systems could be registered in China by 2030.
The joint venture aims to provide all Chinese vehicle manufacturers with fuel cell systems.
Components required for the systems, such as the fuel cell stack, air compressor with power electronics, and control unit with sensors, come from Bosch, mainly from the plant in Wuxi.
Small-scale production of these components will start at the Wuxi plant this year.
In 2021, a test fleet of 70 Qingling trucks equipped with Bosch's Fuel Cell Power Module will hit the road.
The market launch of the fuel cell system is planned for 2022/2023.
Mobility is the largest Bosch Group business sector, generating sales of 55.8 billion euros in 2025 and contributing around 61 percent of total sales.
Bosch Group employs roughly 413,000 associates worldwide as of December 31, 2025, with sales of 91 billion euros in 2025.
Bosch's operations are divided into four business sectors: Mobility, Industrial Technology, Consumer Goods, and Energy and Building Technology.
Bosch employs some 82,000 associates in research and development.
The Bosch Group comprises Robert Bosch GmbH and its roughly 500 subsidiary and regional companies in over 60 countries.
source_title: Emission-free mobility: Bosch and Qingling Motors cooperate on fuel cells - Bosch Media Service

facts: Bosch employs approximately 20,000 associates in the U.S., which includes high-quality, high-value jobs in engineering, manufacturing, software and research and development.

In total, Bosch currently maintains 20 manufacturing locations in the U.S. in Arkansas, California, Florida, Illinois, Indiana, Kansas, Kentucky, North Carolina, Michigan, Minnesota, Oklahoma, Pennsylvania, South Carolina and Tennessee.
Since 2023, Bosch has announced more than $6 billion in U.S.-based acquisitions, resulting in more than 5,000 U.S. jobs.
Paul Thomas serves as president of Bosch in North America and president of Bosch Mobility, Americas.

The structural shift toward electromobility and the extremely high price and competitive pressure in the global automotive industry is resulting in an annual cost gap of around 2.5 billion euros worldwide in the Mobility business sector alone.
Bosch announced last year that it needed to cut about another 13,000 jobs globally due to the structural shift toward electromobility and competitive pressures.
Last year, Bosch won customer orders for solutions for automated driving, the requisite sensor technology, and central vehicle computers worth 10 billion euros.
Bosch's Vehicle Motion Management software provides central control of brakes, steering, powertrain, and chassis and is well received on the market.
At 56 billion euros, sales revenue in the Mobility business sector was up slightly year on year by 0.3 percent (or 3.1 percent adjusted for exchange-rate effects).
For 2025, preliminary figures indicate sales to third parties in North America were 18.7 billion USD, an increase of approximately eight percent year-over-year.
Bosch now assumes that it will begin achieving its target margin of 7 percent in 2027 at the earliest, instead of in 2026.
Stefan Hartung is chairman of the board of management of Robert Bosch GmbH.
Markus Forschner is member of the board of management and chief financial officer of Robert Bosch GmbH.
Increased tariffs negatively impacted results alongside lower sales.
The company made considerable provisions for necessary structural adjustments and associated personnel measures.
Bosch's Strategy 2030 stipulates that the company must be one of the three leading providers in its key markets in all regions of the world.
By the end of 2027, the company plans to have invested a total of 2.5 billion euros in AI, which is already in use throughout the company.
source_title: Bosch has set a course for the future in the difficult 2025 financial year - Bosch Media Service US

canonical_name: Robert Bosch LLC Plymouth Township Technical Center
facts: Michigan Gov. Rick Snyder joined Robert Bosch LLC executives to break ground on the 220,500-square-foot expansion to the site that will nearly double the size of the existing site.
Total investment of $40 million to design, build, equip and furnish site includes estimated $25 million for construction.
Added capacity will allow Bosch to increase efficiencies through co-location and collaboration of associates in Southeast Michigan with up to 200 new jobs expected in the next three years.
The expansion is to be completed in November 2015 and will include several energy-saving technologies, including the installation of a DC Microgrid.
The groundbreaking ceremony took place in Plymouth Township, Michigan, at the existing Technical Center located at 15000 Haggerty Road.
Michigan Gov. Rick Snyder stated that Bosch's commitment to expand its presence in Michigan while enhancing research and development work shows that Michigan is a great place to do business.
Gov. Snyder noted that Michigan's business climate has improved dramatically, making the state a prime location for global companies like Bosch to expand and invest.
Gov. Snyder highlighted that Bosch is an advocate for preparing tomorrow's workforce through hands-on tech training.
The expansion will nearly double the size of the Technical Center, which originally opened in 2007.
When complete, the total site located on 75 acres will have more than 445,000 square feet and capacity for 1,400 people.

The expansion will further enable the growth of key technologies including automotive electronics, start/stop motors, electrical drives, and a variety of safety and driver assistance systems that will advance automated driving.

The new site will provide laboratory and office space to co-locate certain associates in southeast Michigan who currently work in different leased facilities, fostering more innovation and collaboration.
Robert Bosch LLC President Mike Mansuetti stated that breaking ground to begin the expansion at a time when the market offers potential to grow is truly exciting for an innovation-based company.
Mansuetti referenced founder Robert Bosch's belief that education and sustainability are essential for business and society success, aligning with the company imperative of "Invented for life."
The Plymouth Technical Center currently houses 850 people.

In Michigan, Bosch has approximately 3,200 associates across 15 cities including Farmington Hills (headquarters for Robert Bosch LLC), Plymouth Township, Ann Arbor, Bridgeport, Buchanan, Canton, Flat Rock, Kalamazoo, Kentwood, Orion, Novi, Rochester Hills, St. Joseph, and Warren.
With this expansion and co-location of certain groups, Bosch expects to add up to 200 new jobs in Michigan in the next three years.
Mansuetti thanked community and government leaders, noting that Plymouth Township offers a thriving environment for high-tech jobs.
The township allowed Bosch to construct a temporary building on site for about 160 associates to keep jobs in Plymouth Township while the business case for further expansion was built.
The Bosch Community Fund pledged a contribution of $10,000 to the Friends of the Rouge (FOTR) to assist with projects in the Plymouth community.

Since the Bosch Community Fund was established in 2011, it has contributed $360,000 to various community organizations in the Township and more than $439,000 nationwide to FIRST Robotics, including $70,000 to the FIRST Plymouth Canton Schools Team 862 Lightning Robotics.

FIRST (For Inspiration and Recognition of Science and Technology) designs programs that motivate young people to pursue education and career opportunities in science, technology, engineering and math.
Schoolcraft College will work with two Bosch-sponsored FIRST Robotics teams in its labs during the 2015 season as part of a recently established partnership.
This collaboration will benefit students who will work with and be mentored by professionals from both Bosch and Schoolcraft College.
STEM is a key priority for Bosch due to statistics showing the supply of technical talent in STEM-related fields will not meet demand in the years ahead.

The expanded site will showcase energy-efficient technologies including high-efficiency lighting (LED and induction lighting), a photovoltaic solar array, a battery backup system, high-volume low-speed fans, and a Direct Current (DC) Microgrid.
Site architect for the expansion is Harley Ellis Devereaux.
source_title: Bosch Breaks Ground on Technical Center Expansion in Plymouth Township, Michigan - Bosch Media Service US

facts: Sewon America plans to invest $300,000,000.00 to build out new space in Rincon, Georgia.
The company plans to occupy the new space in Rincon on or about March 1, 2025.
Sewon America is at the center of the 21st century automobile industry with excellent manufacturing technology and quality control.
The company's management emphasizes people and the manufacturing field as the driving force for the entire group.
Sewon America is recognized as a global corporation on the forefront of Korea by securing technical competitiveness.
Moon Ki Kim serves as the Chief Executive Officer of Sewon America.
