facts: A sizable crowd gathered off of Newton Road on Tuesday for the groundbreaking ceremony for Anovion Technologies.
The company had previously announced its plan for a Bainbridge facility last month.
Anovion Technologies is headquartered in Chicago.
The company specializes in the production of synthetic graphite, a key component in lithium-ion batteries.
According to Anovion CEO Eric Stopka at the initial announcement, this facility will be the first large-scale factory of its kind in North America.
Development Authority Vice Chairman Andy Bell took to the podium first at the ceremony.
County Commission Chair Pete Stephens delivered the invocation.
Mayor Edward Reynolds spoke at the event and introduced Governor Kemp.

Mayor Edward Reynolds stated that throughout Governor Kemp’s first term, Georgia has consistently broken economic records with jobs and investment coming to every corner of the state, including Bainbridge and Decatur County.
Mayor Edward Reynolds touted the mayor’s record during his introduction, including tax cuts, teacher pay raises, and a crackdown on human trafficking.
Governor Kemp thanked officials present before discussing Anovion at the podium.
Governor Kemp stated that the event represents many great victories for the state and is a cumulation of hard work by local communities.

Governor Kemp noted that projects like this result from months of careful talks and efforts at the state level through the Department of Economic Development and support from members of the general assembly.
Governor Kemp praised Anovion’s business and its stated commitment to build up the American supply chain for lithium-ion batteries, noting much of it is currently based in Asia.
Governor Kemp reiterated that the facility will create 400 new jobs.
Governor Kemp reiterated that the project represents an $800 million investment.

Governor Kemp stated that with the project’s groundbreaking, all four corners of Georgia have seen major investment and significant job creation as a result of leading the next Industrial Revolution in the state.
Anovion Executive Chairman Chip Dunn spoke after Governor Kemp.
Chip Dunn recounted how he met Governor Kemp and how Anovion was persuaded to come to Georgia.
Chip Dunn stated that factors contributing to the decision included Bainbridge’s inland port and access to waterway and land transport.
Chip Dunn stated that Anovion is the “only commercial sale battery-grade synthetic graphite company in North America.”
Chip Dunn discussed the particulars of which components in lithium-ion batteries use synthetic graphite.
Chip Dunn emphasized Anovion’s commitment to being climate-focused.
Chip Dunn focused on the company’s commitment to reduce emissions associated with producing synthetic graphite.

Chip Dunn stated: “Importantly we’re focused on shifting the narrative of a rather environmentally unfriendly process, if we’re being honest, in China, and demonstrate what our vision is, and what it truly means to be climate tech-driven through our state-of-the-art manufacturing facility.”
Anovion hopes to reduce its production’s carbon footprint to zero over time.
Chip Dunn stated: “We think that we can be the first major manufacturing business in the world to be carbon-free. It’s an ambition, but we think we have a chance to make it.”
Congressman Sanford Bishop spoke about how state and national government were involved in bringing Anovion to the state.
Congressman Sanford Bishop discussed how the state has benefitted from federal manufacturing incentives.

Congressman Sanford Bishop stated that federal resources combined with resources from the governor and general assembly are making historic improvements in high-speed internet connecting families, schools, and businesses.
Congressman Sanford Bishop stated: “The Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act is improving our roads, our ports, our railways, bridges and airports, the arteries through which our commerce flows.”
SRTC President Jim Glass delivered the closing remarks at the ceremony.
Jim Glass revealed that Anovion will be working to offer scholarships in the future, with updates to come later.
The facility is projected to be operational by 2025.
source_title: Anovion Technologies holds groundbreaking ceremony | The Post-Searchlight

entity_name: IRA Clean Energy and EV Manufacturing Investments
canonical_name: Inflation Reduction Act Private Clean Energy and EV Manufacturing Investment Wave
facts: Democrats’ climate law launched a huge surge of manufacturing initiatives across critical swing states including Georgia.

Companies say market realities, questions about President Joe Biden’s policies or worries about a second Trump administration have clouded some of the heady expectations raised by the August 2022 passage of the Inflation Reduction Act.
Two-thirds of the announced investment won’t be completed until next year or later.
Seven swing states — Arizona, Georgia, Pennsylvania, Michigan, Nevada, North Carolina and Wisconsin — account for 44 percent of all clean energy investments announced since Biden signed the IRA.

Vice President Kamala Harris has ramped up her pitch to voters on the administration’s efforts to foster a U.S. manufacturing renaissance, while warning that a Trump victory would leave hundreds of planned projects in limbo.
Atlas Public Policy tracks company announcements in clean energy and technology manufacturing, reporting 555 facilities announced with at least $153.4 billion in planned investments as of Oct. 24.
The 555 announced facilities promise to create roughly 160,000 jobs.
Roughly 92 percent of those dollars were pledged for projects that have yet to come online.
About 230 of those facilities announced they would begin operations by at least the end of this year.
Of those, fewer than half are up and running.
An additional 11 have canceled altogether.
Roughly 30 percent of 75 facilities that responded to questions from POLITICO said they had either delayed their projects, named potential delays they are monitoring, or canceled altogether.

The Inflation Reduction Act created an estimated $527 billion in tax incentives for the deployment and manufacturing of low-carbon energy technologies, in an attempt to reclaim U.S. control of supply chains now dominated by competitors such as China.
Vice President Kamala Harris stood before a crowd of factory workers in Dalton, Georgia, to celebrate the thousands of jobs that solar power was bringing to a deep-red corner of the state.
Qcells, a South Korean solar manufacturer, owns a plant in Dalton, Georgia, which Harris highlighted as symbolic for bringing clean energy manufacturing jobs to the carpet capital of the world.
Qcells said it would spend $2.5 billion to expand its Dalton facility and build a new factory outside Atlanta.
Biden administration rules gave automakers a two-year grace period to continue buying graphite from China while still receiving the IRA’s tax credits for electric vehicles.
North American graphite producers and lawmakers had argued against the move, saying it would undermine the business case for domestic producers at a crucial time for the industry.
In Georgia, Anovion Technologies said it was delaying plans for a battery manufacturing facility that had initially promised to bring more than 400 jobs to the southwest corner of the state by 2025.
As of August, Anovion Technologies had hired seven people for its Georgia project, according to chief commercial officer Patrick Donnelly.

Anovion Technologies postponed its Georgia battery facility project because of Biden administration rules that gave automakers a two-year grace period to continue buying graphite from China while still receiving the IRA’s tax credits for electric vehicles.
The White House estimates that businesses have announced a total of $694 billion in manufacturing and clean energy investments since the IRA and CHIPS and Science Act were enacted.
One new tax credit under the IRA rewards companies for the manufacture of clean energy components in the U.S., while others push companies to use domestically made products.
The provisions spurred a wave of factory announcements across the U.S. — with the majority of the projects planned for Republican districts.
More than half of the sites that responded to POLITICO’s inquiry said their facilities remain on target.
Of the roughly 450 announced factories that have selected locations, nearly two-thirds are poised to be in GOP-held congressional districts.
Those projects represent nearly $115 billion in potential investments, or three-quarters of the total.
Over a quarter of the 555 announced facilities tracked by Atlas are poised to be in the seven presidential battleground states.
Most of those facilities and their expansions are still in the planning stage and have not yet begun construction.
Fewer than 25 percent are operating.

In a separate analysis, the Rhodium Group and Massachusetts Institute of Technology estimated that nearly $90 billion in clean energy manufacturing investments have been spent between the quarter the IRA passed and June of this year.
Analysis commissioned by the BlueGreen Alliance estimated that the IRA will create more than 9 million jobs over the next decade.
Schneider Electric announced $440 million in U.S. manufacturing investments since 2020, including plans to expand facilities in Tennessee and South Carolina since the IRA passed.
A recent national survey of nearly 930 businesses found that more than half said they would lose business or revenue if Congress repeals the IRA.
Twenty-seven percent said they would lose projects or contracts, and nearly 9 percent of companies said they would have to relocate to another country should the law be repealed.
source_title: A wave of private energy spending is coming — but may be too late to help Democrats - POLITICO

facts: In business since 1970, Apache Mills leads the way in designing and manufacturing quality floor mats, matting, and OEM materials for the home, commercial, and industrial markets.
The company's headquarters is a 750,000 sq. ft. manufacturing facility located in Calhoun, Georgia.
All four matting divisions (Apache At Home, Apache Commercial, Apache Industrial, and AMI) are serviced from the Calhoun, Georgia headquarters.
The company employs over 600 people across its operations.
Apache Mills uses Wasatch SoftRIP software to streamline workflow and improve production efficiency.
The facility utilizes MacDermid film and a Roland SOLJET PRO III XJ-640 printer to produce film positives for screen printing on various mat materials.
Jeff Young, an art director at Apache Mills, has used Wasatch software for nearly 20 years.
source_title: Apache Mills

facts: DQS Inc., an independent certification body, certified Ascend Elements' electric vehicle (EV) battery recycling facility in Covington, Georgia, to the ISO 9001:2015 standard for quality management systems.
The certification process for the facility included onsite audits and extensive policy and documentation reviews.
Andrew Aberdale, CFO/COO at Ascend Elements, described the certification as a major achievement for a startup organization in an emerging field like battery recycling.
Andrew Aberdale stated that the ISO certification signals the company's commitment to quality, customer service, and rigorous safety procedures.
The Ascend Elements facility in Covington can process 30,000 metric tons of used lithium-ion batteries and scrap per year.
The 154,000-square-foot facility in Newton County contains battery discharge and disassembly processes, battery shredding, lithium extraction, and analytical testing capabilities.
source_title: Ascend Elements' Georgia facility earns ISO 9001:2015 certification

facts: Ascend Elements utilizes a patented Hydro-to-Cathode® direct cathode precursor (pCAM) synthesis process.
The process simplifies lithium-ion battery recycling and transforms used batteries into customized battery materials.
The resulting battery materials can meet or exceed the performance of new materials.
Other processes leach metals out of spent battery materials, but this process leaches out impurities instead.
The process keeps valuable metals in solution and eliminates multiple steps in the recycling flow.
Traditional lithium-ion recycling processes involve many steps that focus on reclaiming intermediate compounds to sell to commodities markets.
The Hydro-to-Cathode direct cathode precursor (pCAM) synthesis process increases efficiency while returning higher-level materials to the supply chain.
The process uses methodology from Argonne National Labs.
The process has demonstrated significant advantages in multiple categories when compared with traditional methods.
Delivering engineered materials at lower cost greatly improves the cost-effectiveness of battery recycling.
Improved economics is the best way to boost battery recycling as the lithium-ion market grows.
The approach will reduce the pressure to mine and process raw materials for critical mineral applications.
The closed-loop approach supports independent and secure supply chains.
source_title: Ascend Elements | Innovation: Patented Hydro-to-Cathode process

facts: The location for its new battery recycling plant in Poland has been identified, though a final investment decision has not yet been made.
Ascend Elements specializes in producing battery materials from black mass.
The company has been active in Poland for approximately one year through a joint venture with Elemental Strategic Metals.
The joint venture operates a recycling plant in Zawiercie, Poland.
Ascend Elements intends to independently build and operate a new planned cathode material (pCAM) plant in Poland.
The new Polish facility will utilize the company's hydro-to-cathode technology, a direct synthesis process that creates pCAM material from used lithium-ion cells.
The plant aims to commercialize the production of sustainable nickel, manganese, and cobalt (NMC) pCAM made from recycled lithium-ion battery materials.

Tomasz Poznar, Senior Vice President of Commercial at Ascend Elements, stated the company is grateful for Polish government support and views the project as a critical step in transforming Europe's battery materials landscape.

The company aims to bridge the growing gap in cathode precursor supply and contribute to the EU’s strategic net-zero goals by building a sustainable circular battery materials supply chain in the region.

Co-founder and Chief Technical Officer Eric Gratz noted that while US tariffs would impact manufacturers relying on virgin materials from Canada and China, Ascend Elements is unaffected because it relies on domestic recycled materials.
Recycled cobalt is expected to become cheaper than imported cobalt due to tariff dynamics.
In February, Ascend Elements canceled a $164 million grant from the US Department of Energy (DOE) for cathode active material production at its Kentucky plant.
The cancellation of US federal funding is paving the way for regions like Europe to welcome US battery recycling expertise and technology previously funded by the US government.

The Polish government is offering a grant of 1.22 billion Polish zloty (approximately 290 million euros) to establish the new plant, which Ascend Elements describes as one of the largest grants ever awarded by the Republic of Poland.

President and CEO Linh Austin stated the grant is an important step in the company's European growth strategy and expressed appreciation for the significant financial support from the Polish Ministry.

Pawel Pudlowski, Vice President of the Management Board at the Polish Investment and Trade Agency (PAIH), described the project as one of the largest recent US investments in Poland, strengthening the country's role in the battery supply chain and supporting regional development and cooperation with local educational institutions.
Ascend Elements already owns and operates a large battery recycling plant in Covington, Georgia.
Construction on the company's planned hydro-to-cathode production plant in Hopkinsville, Kentucky, has been paused but is scheduled to resume after clearing several hurdles.
The Kentucky plant is expected to go into operation at the end of 2026.
Ascend Elements plans to scale up its business selling recycled lithium carbonate to battery manufacturers in both the USA and Europe.
source_title: Poland lures Ascend Elements battery recycling with a grant worth millions - electrive.com

facts: Ascend Elements raised $657.9M in its Series D funding round on 02/20/2024.
The Series D round was priced at $10.65 per share, resulting in a valuation of $1.62B.

Key investors in the Series D round include Agave Partners, Alliance Resource Partners, Alumni Ventures, At One Ventures, Bhp Ventures, Clearvision Ventures, Decarbonization Partners, Fifth Wall, Hitachi Ventures, Irongrey, Just Climate, Mirae Asset, Pulse - Cma Cgm Energy Fund, Qatar Investment Authority, Temasek, and Tenaska.
The Series D round had 61,778,604 shares outstanding.
Ascend Elements raised $60M in its Series C-2 funding round on 10/26/2022.
The Series C-2 round was priced at $9.31 per share, resulting in a valuation of $725.52M.

Key investors in the Series C-2 round include At One Ventures, Doral Energy Tech Ventures, Fifth Wall, Gly Capital Management, Hitachi Ventures, Inmotion Ventures, Lithium Americas Corporation, Mirae Asset, Oman Investment Authority, Orbia Ventures, Shinhan Gib, SK Group, Tdk Ventures, and Trumpf Venture.
Ascend Elements raised $150M in its Series C-1 funding round on 10/26/2022.
The Series C-1 round was priced at $6.20 per share, resulting in a valuation of $725.52M.

Key investors in the Series C-1 round include At One Ventures, Doral Energy Tech Ventures, Fifth Wall, Gly Capital Management, Hitachi Ventures, Inmotion Ventures, Lithium Americas Corporation, Mirae Asset, Oman Investment Authority, Orbia Ventures, Shinhan Gib, SK Group, Tdk Ventures, and Trumpf Venture.
Ascend Elements raised $32.11M in its Series C-1SHDW funding round on 09/21/2021.
The Series C-1SHDW round was priced at $2.52 per share, resulting in a valuation of $121.54M.
Key investors in the Series C-1SHDW round include At One Ventures, Doral Energy Tech Ventures, Hitachi Ventures, Inmotion Ventures, Orbia Ventures, Tdk Ventures, and Trumpf Venture.
Ascend Elements raised $22.62M in its Series B funding round on 04/12/2021.
The Series B round was priced at $1.67 per share, resulting in a valuation of $55.16M.
Key investors in the Series B round include At One Ventures, Doral Energy Tech Ventures, Inmotion Ventures, Orbia Ventures, Tdk Ventures, and Trumpf Venture.
Ascend Elements raised $1.56M in its Series A funding round on 03/15/2020.
The Series A round was priced at $0.52 per share, resulting in a valuation of $5.2M.
Key investors in the Series A round were undisclosed investors.
The Series D round featured Order 1 liquidation preference with a 1.0x multiplier, 1.0x conversion ratio, 8.0% cumulative dividend rate, and non-participating terms.
The Series C-2 and Series C-1 rounds featured Order 2 liquidation preference with a 1.0x multiplier, 1.0x conversion ratio, 8.0% cumulative dividend rate, and non-participating terms.
The Series C-1SHDW and Series B rounds featured Order 2 liquidation preference with a 1.0x multiplier, 1.0x conversion ratio, 8.0% cumulative dividend rate, and non-participating terms.
The Series A round featured Order 4 liquidation preference with a 1.0x multiplier, 1.0x conversion ratio, 0.0% dividend rate, and non-participating terms.
source_title: Invest and Sell Ascend Elements Stock - Forge

facts: Ascend Elements is a U.S.-based company revolutionizing the production of sustainable, engineered battery materials for electric vehicles and other energy storage applications.

The company specializes in a patented Hydro-to-Cathode® direct precursor synthesis process that transforms recycled lithium-ion batteries and manufacturing scrap into high-performance, customized cathode active materials (CAM) and cathode precursor (pCAM).
This approach significantly lowers costs, reduces carbon footprint, and supports the creation of a closed-loop, circular supply chain for critical battery components, primarily in North America.
The company's headquarters serves as the primary center for corporate management, strategic decision-making, research and development leadership, and intellectual property development.
The headquarters features advanced research and development laboratories, including pilot-scale testing equipment for battery material synthesis, characterization, and process optimization.
The headquarters is critical for pioneering and refining the company's proprietary Hydro-to-Cathode® technology, managing its rapid strategic growth, and fostering key industry partnerships.
Ascend Elements is primarily focused on building a robust and sustainable battery material supply chain within North America while addressing global demand for cleaner energy solutions.
The company engages with international partners, customers, and stakeholders in the battery and electric vehicle ecosystems to support the global transition to sustainable energy and transportation.
The headquarters is located at 149 Flanders Road, Westborough, Massachusetts, USA.
The company operates a facility at 1 Ascend Elements Way, Hopkinsville, KY 42240, which is the approximate location for the Apex 1 facility in Commerce Park II.

The Hopkinsville facility was established to create a major North American production hub for sustainable battery materials, leverage regional manufacturing strengths, support the U.S. EV supply chain, and create green technology jobs.
The company operates a facility at 10101 City Pond Road, Covington, GA 30014.

The Covington, Georgia facility acts as a critical hub for battery recycling in the Southeast, processing significant volumes of battery waste and providing essential recycled materials for a circular battery economy.

The company operates an R&D facility in Novi, Michigan, strategically located near the U.S. automotive industry's heartland to facilitate close collaboration with automotive manufacturers, advance EV battery technology, and support material integration into next-generation vehicles.
As of April 2025, the company's leadership structure was highlighted, with no prominent C-suite departures publicly announced over the past 12 months.

Over the past 12 months, Ascend Elements significantly bolstered its executive leadership team with strategic hires in key operational, commercial, and financial roles to scale operations and capitalize on market opportunities.

Andrew Fagg was appointed Chief Financial Officer, with the announcement made via Business Wire on May 8, 2024; he brings extensive experience in financial leadership within growth-stage and publicly traded companies to guide financial strategy during rapid expansion and commercialization of sustainable battery materials.

Srini Sriram was appointed Chief Operating Officer, with the announcement made via Business Wire on February 1, 2024; he will lead operational scale-up focusing on enhancing manufacturing capabilities and efficiency at recycling and production facilities, including the flagship Apex 1 pCAM plant in Kentucky.
Ascend Elements officially broke ground on its 'Apex 1' manufacturing facility in Hopkinsville, Kentucky on October 26, 2023.

The $1 billion Apex 1 plant will produce sustainable cathode precursor (pCAM) and cathode active materials (CAM), significantly bolstering the North American EV battery supply chain and creating hundreds of jobs.
Ascend Elements has been backed by several prominent investors over the years.

The company typically uses standard corporate email formats based on employee names, with patterns including [first_initial][last]@ascendelements.com or [first].[last]@ascendelements.com, and provides info@ascendelements.com for general inquiries.
source_title: Where is Ascend Elements Located? HQ, Global Offices & Company Insights

facts: Ascend Elements team members and partners in Hopkinsville, Ky., celebrated a major milestone in the construction of North America’s first electric vehicle battery cathode precursor (pCAM) manufacturing facility on January 24, 2024.
Turner Construction Company, Kokosing Construction, and SSOE Group worked together to place the final piece of structural steel on the 315,000 square foot pCAM leaching building.
The pCAM building is one of many large structures under construction on the 140-acre Apex 1 campus.

Wenqing Su, VP of Global Capital Projects at Ascend Elements, thanked construction partners for a safe and successful 2023, noting they achieved 244,961 labor hours and no lost time or recordable safety incidents during the year.
pCAM is an engineered material made to precise microstructure specifications for use in electric vehicle batteries.

While most of the world’s pCAM is made in China from primary (mined) metals, U.S.-based Ascend Elements is commercializing an ultra-efficient method to make sustainable pCAM from recycled lithium-ion battery materials.

The company utilizes a patented Hydro-to-Cathode® direct precursor synthesis process that eliminates several intermediary steps in the traditional cathode manufacturing process and provides significant economic and carbon-reduction benefits.
Several peer-reviewed studies have shown Ascend Elements’ recycled battery materials perform as well as similar materials made from virgin (or mined) sources.
When finished, the $1 billion Apex 1 campus will produce pCAM and other engineered battery materials for 750,000 EVs per year.
The Apex 1 project is funded in part by the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law (BIL) and grants from the U.S. Department of Energy.
Construction of the pCAM operations will continue into 2025.

Ascend Elements is a North America- and Europe-based battery materials leader that converts spent lithium-ion batteries and manufacturing scrap into premium precursor cathode material (pCAM) and battery-grade lithium carbonate through its patented Hydro-to-Cathode® process.
The company operates plants at scale with a proven technology platform to deliver circular supply for EV and energy storage markets.
source_title: Ascend Elements | Ascend Elements Celebrates Construction Milestone at America’s First EV Battery Cathode Precursor (pCAM) Manufacturing Facility

facts: Ascend Elements raised $542 million in new equity investments, including $460 million in Series D investments and $82 million of additional investments from earlier this year.
The Series D funding round was led by Decarbonization Partners, Singapore-based investment firm Temasek, and Qatar Investment Authority (QIA).

Other investors include Tenaska, Alliance Resource Partners, PULSE – CMA CGM Energy Fund, BHP Ventures, Fifth Wall, Hitachi Ventures, Mirae Asset, At One Ventures, Agave Partners, Alumni Ventures, and other leading global investors.

The $542 million equity financing is one of the largest cleantech equity private placements in the United States of 2023 to date, and one of the top 10 equity private placements in the United States this year.
CEO Mike O’Kronley stated that sustainable lithium-ion battery materials will power EV batteries and accelerate the global transition to zero carbon emissions.
The funding will be used to build the United States’ first commercial-scale, NMC cathode precursor (pCAM) and cathode active material (CAM) manufacturing facility in Kentucky.
The $542 million equity financing will be paired with two U.S. Department of Energy grants totaling $480 million.
Ascend Elements signed a multi-year, $1 billion agreement to supply sustainable pCAM to a major U.S. customer beginning in Q4 2024.
The contract includes options to expand to a larger quantity with a value of up to $5 billion.

Funding will advance construction of Ascend Elements’ Apex 1 facility in Hopkinsville, Kentucky, which will be North America’s first sustainable cathode precursor (pCAM) and cathode active material (CAM) manufacturing facility.
CAM and pCAM are engineered materials made to precise microstructure specifications for use in electric vehicle batteries.

While most of the world’s pCAM and CAM are made in China from primary metals, U.S.-based Ascend Elements is commercializing an ultra-efficient method to make sustainable pCAM and CAM from black mass, the traditional output of lithium-ion battery recycling facilities.

The patented Hydro-to-Cathode® direct precursor synthesis process eliminates several intermediary steps in the traditional cathode manufacturing process and provides significant economic and carbon-reduction benefits.
Several peer-reviewed studies have shown Ascend Elements’ recycled battery materials perform as well as similar materials made from virgin sources while reducing carbon emissions by up to 93%.

Over the last twelve months, Ascend Elements achieved milestones including signing its first commercial scale pCAM contract, starting construction of its Apex 1 facility, and securing two U.S. Department of Energy grants.

In June 2023, Ascend Elements signed a $1 billion contract to supply sustainable pCAM starting in Q4 2024 with options to expand the multi-year contract to a larger quantity with a value of up to $5 billion.
In October 2022, Ascend Elements began construction of its one-of-a-kind Apex 1 facility on a 140-acre site in southwest Kentucky.
When complete, the 1-million-square-foot facility will produce enough sustainable pCAM for 750,000 electric vehicles per year.
Ascend Elements was awarded two U.S. Department of Energy grants totaling $480 million, part of the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law (BIL) in October 2022.
Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC acted as sole placement agent on the Series D transaction.
Ascend Elements is based in Westborough, Mass., and is the leading provider of sustainable, closed-loop battery materials solutions.

Its proprietary Hydro-to-Cathode® direct precursor synthesis technology produces new pCAM from spent lithium-ion cells more efficiently than traditional methods, resulting in reduced cost, improved performance, and lowered GHG emissions.

Decarbonization Partners is a joint venture between Temasek and BlackRock focused on late-stage venture capital and early growth private equity investing in next-generation companies to help achieve a net zero global economy by 2050.
Dr. Meghan Sharp serves as Global Head of Decarbonization Partners.
Temasek is a global investment company with a net portfolio value of S$382 billion (US$287b) as of March 2023, headquartered in Singapore with 13 offices in 9 countries.
Qatar Investment Authority (QIA) is the sovereign wealth fund of the State of Qatar, founded in 2005 to invest and manage state reserve funds.
Mohammed Al-Sowaidi serves as Chief Investment Officer, Americas at Qatar Investment Authority.

source_title: Ascend Elements | Ascend Elements Raises $542 Million to Accelerate Production of U.S.-engineered Lithium-Ion Battery Materials

facts: The Ministry of Economic Development and Technology in Poland offered Ascend Elements up to USD 320 million (1.22 billion Polish Zloty) to support construction of a sustainable precursor cathode active material (pCAM) manufacturing facility in Poland.
pCAM is a high-value, precisely engineered material used in the manufacture of lithium-ion batteries for electric vehicles and other advanced industrial applications.
The cash subsidy is one of the largest grants ever offered by the Republic of Poland.
The grant operates under the E.U.’s Temporary Crisis and Transition Framework (TCTF) to support Europe’s transition to a net-zero economy.

Linh Austin, President and CEO at Ascend Elements, stated the offer marks a significant step forward in the company's European growth strategy and commitment to building a sustainable battery materials supply chain on both sides of the Atlantic.
Ascend Elements has identified a location in Poland for its first advanced battery materials (pCAM) manufacturing facility in Europe.
CEO Linh Austin met with Poland’s Secretary of State Michał Jaros at the Polish Ministry of Economic Development and Technology in Warsaw to receive the grant offer.

Pawel Pudlowski, Ph.D., Vice President of the Management Board at the Polish Investment and Trade Agency (PAIH), stated battery sector projects are key to PAIH’s investment portfolio and called the Ascend Elements project one of the largest U.S. investments in recent years, strengthening Poland’s role in the battery supply chain and supporting regional development and cooperation with local educational institutions.

Ascend Elements plans to commercialize innovative technology for manufacturing sustainable nickel, manganese, and cobalt (NMC) pCAM made from recycled lithium-ion battery materials at the new Polish facility.

Tomasz Poznar, Ph.D., Senior Vice President of Commercial at Ascend Elements, described the project as a critical step in transforming Europe’s battery materials landscape by bringing Hydro-to-Cathode technology to Poland to bridge the cathode precursor supply gap and contribute to EU net-zero goals.

The company’s Hydro-to-Cathode direct pCAM synthesis technology produces new NMC pCAM material from spent lithium-ion cells more efficiently than traditional methods, improving economics and lowering carbon emissions.

A recently published life cycle assessment found the Hydro-to-Cathode process produces 49% lower carbon dioxide equivalent (CO2e) emissions and 26% lower particulate matter 2.5 (PM 2.5) emissions compared to traditional battery recycling methods.
By 2030, Ascend Elements aims to achieve 86% lower CO2e emissions and 94% lower PM 2.5 emissions through additional decarbonization efforts.

In 2024, Ascend Elements and Elemental Strategic Metals established a joint venture to own and operate the AE Elemental JV battery recycling facility in Zawiercie, Poland, while the new pCAM facility will be solely owned and operated by Ascend Elements.
In the United States, Ascend Elements owns and operates one of North America’s largest battery recycling facilities in Covington, Georgia.

A new lithium recovery line at the Georgia plant is expected to produce up to 3,000 metric tons of Lithium Carbonate (Li₂CO₃) annually, making Ascend Elements the first U.S. company to produce Li₂CO₃ from recycled content.

In Hopkinsville, Kentucky, the company is building an advanced Hydro-to-Cathode pCAM manufacturing facility to produce engineered battery materials for up to 750,000 electric vehicle batteries annually.
The Kentucky facility is expected to be operational in late 2026 and will be North America’s largest NMC pCAM manufacturer when complete.
source_title: Republic of Poland Offers Ascend Elements Up to USD 320mn to Support Construction of Lithium-ion Battery Materials Plant in Poland - Brands Review Magazine

The cash subsidy is one of the largest grants ever offered by Poland’s Ministry of Economic Development and Technology.
As part of the E.U.’s Temporary Crisis and Transition Framework (TCTF), the grant is designed to support Europe’s transition to a net-zero economy.

Ascend Elements plans to commercialize its innovative technology for the manufacture of nickel, manganese, and cobalt (NMC) pCAM made from recycled lithium-ion battery materials at the new Polish facility.

Tomasz Poznar, Ph.D., Senior Vice President of Commercial at Ascend Elements, said the company is deeply grateful for the support of the Polish government and thrilled to expand its presence in Europe.

facts: Aspen Aerogels conditionally received a large Department of Energy loan in October.
Aspen Aerogels is canceling its planned manufacturing facility in Statesboro, Georgia.
The company makes thermal barriers for electric vehicle batteries, including for General Motors.
The conditional commitment from DOE’s Loan Programs Office was for $670 million, dedicated specifically to the Georgia plant.
In its February earnings call, the company told investors it had withdrawn from the negotiation process for the loan.
Instead, the company will focus on expanding its production elsewhere, including at an existing factory in Rhode Island.
The company will also expand production in China and Mexico.
In China, Aspen is planning to increase annual production capacity by $200 million by 2026.
The company’s manufacturing partnerships in China have already proven effective in delivering products for other business arms.
CFO Ricardo Rodriguez said these partnerships are “capable of delivering increased aerogel production capacity” to help make up for the loss of the plant in Georgia.
Rodriguez added there are “literally no limits” to how much Aspen can increase capacity in China.
The earnings call came after the Trump administration’s first 10% tariff increase on China, but before the second, which took effect just last week.
OEM customers aren’t worried about the strategic shift, according to Rodriguez.
Rodriguez stated: “They don’t care where it is made.”
To qualify for various tax credits under the Inflation Reduction Act, EV batteries must meet a threshold of components manufactured in the United States.
The fate of those tax credits remains in flux.
In February, Aspen officially opened two assembly plants outside of Monterrey, in Mexico.
One plant will start the process of creating aerogel rolls.
The second plant will complete assembly of the thermal barrier.
Those factories are currently owned and managed by a consulting company.
Aspen has notified that company of its intent to purchase the “automated fabrication facility.”
This comes as the Trump administration pushes to cut taxes on domestic manufacturing and levy steep tariffs against competing products, including from China and Mexico.
In late January, the president addressed the World Economic Forum.
The president told delegates that under his second administration, there would be “no better place on Earth to create jobs, build factories, or grow a company” than the United States.
The reality of the administration’s first few months has been an environment of regulatory uncertainty, especially for the clean energy industry, that has made it hard to do business.
Aspen Aerogels did not cite political factors among its reasons for the Georgia project cancellation.
The company declined to provide additional comment to Latitude Media on its decision.
Thanks in part to the cancellation of the Georgia plant, and the “reduction of various positions” in the last quarter, Aspen is aiming to reduce its fixed costs by over $35 million per year.
Nixing the DOE debt from the balance sheet is a big plus, Rodriguez said.
Rodriguez said: “It feels great to not take on an incremental $671 million of debt on the balance sheet and to still be able to fulfill all the expected demand.”
Working with external manufacturing — including those in China — has allowed Aspen to “pick up a couple of points of margin” in its other business areas, Rodriguez added.
Doing so for the EV thermal barrier business “really is a no-brainer,” he said.
Canceling a project that was already underway comes with costs of its own.
As of the end of 2024, Aspen had paid around $323.6 million for engineering, design, and construction costs for the Georgia facility.
Some of the factory’s assets will be moved to Rhode Island to increase that facility’s throughput, Rodriguez said.
There’s also the potential, he said, to send some of the equipment “to the external manufacturing facility as well, which we’ll be evaluating.”
The project was expected to come online in 2027.
It was originally designed to supply thermal barriers for more than 200 million EV batteries each year.
The project would have created more than 500 construction jobs and 255 full-time operations jobs.
The site is located in one of the swing states that clinched Trump’s election victory.
Despite Trump’s enthusiasm for domestic manufacturing, his policies since taking office are at times at odds with the goal of onshoring.
The IRA and the lucrative tax credits it offers, like 45X, has lured more clean energy manufacturing to the U.S.
Trump paused funding disbursements related to the law.
GOP lawmakers have said they plan to take a scalpel to the law.
21 House Republicans today issued a letter in support of clean energy tax credits contained within the IRA.
Those Republicans warned that they may vote against the budget bill if the credits are cut.
The Trump administration has also discouraged EV adoption, including through halting a $5-billion federal EV charger program.
Rodriguez told investors that Aspen is concerned about the relatively slow rate of EV adoption in the U.S. and its implications for the company.
Rodriguez stated: “China is at 50% EV penetration already. In North America and Europe, we continue to dabble in this 10% to 15% level.”
He added: “So you do start wondering, is that progress?”
Most of Aspen’s thermal barrier customers are global OEMs.
Rodriguez said these customers are mostly “staying the course” regardless of the Trump administration’s policies.
The customers are also staying cautious about how they deploy capital during his second term in office.
Rodriguez stated: “Four years within a 20-year transition of electrification is not a ton.”
Aspen Aerogels is at least the second loan recipient to walk back its domestic manufacturing plans.
In January, Kore Power, which had received a conditional commitment for an $850 million loan from LPO, scrapped plans to build a battery manufacturing plant in Buckeye, Arizona.
In Kore Power’s case, the company told Latitude Media that it still plans to make its batteries domestically.
Kore Power will do it faster and more cheaply via a retrofit, rather than building its planned greenfield plant.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels cancels its LPO loan and its Georgia factory | Latitude Media

The company's Aerogel Technology Platform addresses thermal runaway barriers.
The company's Aerogel Technology Platform addresses energy infrastructure.
The company's Aerogel Technology Platform addresses battery materials.

Aspen Aerogels provides technical services including unit inspections, installation guides, thickness tables, technical webinars, specification documents, API 521/NFPA guidance, and contractor training.

facts: Northborough-based Aspen Aerogels has cancelled its efforts to construct a manufacturing facility in Statesboro, Georgia.

The company is pivoting to increasing its manufacturing capacity at its existing facility in East Providence and external manufacturing facilities due to anticipated changes in electric vehicle demand.
Aspen Aerogels is evaluating options for the partially constructed facility, including using equipment at the site to upgrade its East Providence location.
The company manufactures a special protective layer for EV batteries.

CFO and treasurer Ricardo Rodriguez stated that in early 2023, pre-empting a reset in EV demand expectations, the company decided to right-time construction of its planned second aerogel manufacturing facility in Statesboro, Georgia, and ramped up external manufacturing capacity for its Energy Industrial business.
The canceled Statesboro plant was originally expected to create more than 250 jobs and be completed in 2023, according to a February 2022 press release from the State of Georgia.
Aspen Aerogels secured $575 million in private funds for the facility through a loan from General Motors Holdings and a sale of more than $25 million in shares of stock.
The company had been approved for $671 million in funding from the U.S. Department of Energy for the site in October.
Aspen Aerogels did not disclose what will happen to the various funds earmarked for the facility.
Aspen Aerogels' fourth-quarter revenue was $124.1 million, up from $84.3 million in the fourth quarter of 2023.
The firm's net income was $11.4 million, compared to a net loss of $500,000 in the same period last year.
The company ended the year with cash and equivalents of $220.9 million.
Aspen Aerogels opened a thermal barrier research center in Marlborough in 2023.

Following the announcement of the Georgia plant cancellation, Aspen Aerogels stock price on the New York Stock Exchange dropped from $12.00 per share on Wednesday to $8.52 on Friday morning, a decrease of 29%.
President Donald Trump has sought to cut funding of federal programs for electric vehicles, including a buildout of the national charging infrastructure, since taking office in January.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels axes plans for Georgia electric-vehicle part manufacturing plant

facts: Aspen Aerogels is focused on its second Aerogel manufacturing facility in Georgia, which will add approximately $1.2 billion of revenue capacity by 2027.

The U.S. Department of Energy Loan Programs Office invited Aspen into the formal due diligence and term sheet negotiation stage for a loan application that is one of the key drivers for restarting construction of Plant 2.

Aspen has made steady progress with the DOE Loan Programs Office but does not have assurance that the DOE will issue a conditional commitment, though it remains deeply engaged and believes it is a strong candidate to partner with them.
Aspen believes it is in the final stages of the due diligence process and expects to provide additional details prior to its next quarterly earnings call.
In Q2 2024, Aspen spent $12.3 million towards slowly advancing progress to fully enclose the main structures at Plant 2 and temperature control in all areas.

To date, Aspen has incurred $300.2 million in cumulative expenses through the end of the second quarter towards Plant 2 in Georgia to position the project for a potential restart of construction after obtaining conditional approval from the DOE Loan Programs Office.
The loan application is pursuant to the DOE’s advanced technology vehicle manufacturing (ATVM) loan program to fund the remaining construction cost of Plant 2.
Aspen only spent $20.5 million in the first half of 2024 towards advancing the construction of Plant 2 in Georgia versus its original expectation of $30 million.
Aspen is not planning to spend more than $15 million advancing the construction of Plant 2 until it receives a potential conditional approval on the loan pursuant to the DOE’s ATVM program.
This investment will ensure the site is advanced enough to preserve all investments made to date and enables the potential reacceleration of construction in the fourth quarter of this year.
Aspen's EV commercial activity remains at peak levels, with expectations to deliver over 100,000 prototype or preproduction parts to over a dozen programs in its development pipeline during Q3 2024.
Aspen announced its sixth design award from a large EU battery manufacturer to supply the next-generation battery platform for Porsche, with an expected start of production in 2025.
The EU battery manufacturer has delivered over two million battery systems since 2019.
Aspen is in final contract negotiations with a major European OEM which it expects to become a formal design award during the third quarter, marking its seventh.
General Motors reiterated during its Q2 earnings call that it is targeting to produce between 200,000 and 250,000 EVs in 2024.
IHS cited 108,000 Ultium-based EVs produced in the first half of 2024, with anticipated acceleration in the second half of the year.
Aspen anticipates that GM will produce 180,000 LTM-based EVs for GM nameplates plus an additional 45,000 Ultium-based EVs for Honda and Acura in 2024.
The potential upside to Aspen's revenue outlook is largely based on GM maintaining its current ramp-up and achieving its targeted production range.

Aspen expects to supply over 225,000 vehicles and enable its EV thermal barrier business to deliver over $240 million of revenue in combination with Toyota, some initial Scania and Stellantis volumes, along with a high level of prototype sales.
The Honda Prologue, produced by General Motors, launched earlier than expected, with almost 3,500 sold in July and an expected annual sales run rate of 42,000 units.
GM revised its external 2024 Ultium production forecast from a range of 200,000 to 300,000 units down to a range of 200,000 to 250,000 units.
Aspen revised its baseline production outlook of GM’s Ultium vehicles down by 10% from 200,000 vehicles to 180,000 to be safe.
The U.S. EV market did not grow year-to-date through the end of July 2024 relative to last year, increasing only around 1%, comparable to overall new vehicle sales growth.
EVs made up around 7% of the U.S. market, with over 1.3 million EVs expected to be sold in the U.S. this year.

facts: Aspen Aerogels, Inc. develops and manufactures high-performance aerogel insulation materials and custom engineered solutions.
The company was founded in 2001 as a spin-out from Department of Energy research.
Aspen Aerogels pursued an initial public offering on the NYSE in 2014 under the ticker ASPN.

The company combines proprietary aerogel formulations with advanced manufacturing processes to deliver products known for low thermal conductivity, lightweight construction, and robust mechanical properties.
The product portfolio spans blanket insulation, boards, and custom shapes built around proprietary brands including Pyrogel, Cryogel, and Spaceloft.
Pyrogel and Cryogel insulation blankets are widely used in oil & gas and petrochemical applications to minimize heat loss and improve operational efficiency.
Spaceloft aerogel products target oilfield tubulars, subsea pipelines, and lightweight structural insulation.
Business segments benefiting from Aspen’s thermal management offerings include power generation, chemical processing, and building materials.
The company leverages its aerogel expertise to develop advanced materials for battery thermal management, aerospace, and specialty markets.
Aspen Aerogels serves customers across North and South America, Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and Asia-Pacific.
The company operates manufacturing facilities in the United States and the Netherlands alongside a network of distribution partners worldwide.
Under the leadership of President and Chief Executive Officer Suzanne H. Watson, the company continues to invest in research and development and expand production capacity.
By aligning product innovation with clean-energy transitions, Aspen Aerogels aims to enable end users to reduce emissions, improve process efficiency, and lower lifetime operational costs.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels (ASPN) Stock Price, News & Analysis

facts: Aspen Aerogels designs, develops, manufactures, and sells aerogel materials primarily for use in the energy industrial, sustainable insulation materials, and electric vehicle (EV) markets in the United States, Canada, Asia, Europe, and Latin America.
The company operates through two segments: Thermal Barrier and Energy Industrial.
Aspen offers PyroThin aerogel thermal barriers for use in battery packs in EVs.
Aspen offers Pyrogel XTE for refineries and petrochemical facilities.
Aspen offers Pyrogel HPS for high temperatures and in demanding thermal applications in refining and chemical processing systems.
Aspen offers Pyrogel XTF for protection against fire.
Aspen offers Cryogel Z for sub-ambient and cryogenic applications.
Aspen offers Spaceloft Subsea for pipe-in-pipe applications in offshore oil production.
Aspen offers Cryogel X201 for cold system designs.
The company serves EV manufacturers, automotive suppliers, lithium-ion battery manufacturers, and e-mobility and energy storage companies.
Aspen Aerogels faces significant challenges as General Motors reduces EV production targets, forcing the company to recalibrate its near-term prospects and financial guidance.
Analysts anticipate a gradual recovery for Aspen as it adapts, with EPS projections at -$0.32 for FY1 and -$0.18 for FY2.
Barclays Capital provides price targets ranging from $4.00 (Underweight) to $7.00 (Equal Weight) for Aspen's stock.
Aspen's Thermal Barrier segment struggles while its Energy Industrial division demonstrates resilience, providing crucial stability to the company's overall financial picture.
The company was founded in 2001 and is headquartered in Northborough, Massachusetts.

facts: LPO announced a conditional commitment for a direct loan of up to $670.6 million to Aspen Aerogels.
The loan will help finance the construction of an aerogel blanket manufacturing facility in Register, Georgia.
Aspen’s PyroThin® aerogel thermal barriers are used as a protective layer within electric vehicle (EV) batteries.
These thermal barriers enable EV manufacturers to achieve critical battery safety goals.
The project helps the U.S. meet expected future demand for EV adoption.
The project reinforces the Biden-Harris administration’s "Investing in America" agenda to onshore and re-shore domestic manufacturing technologies.
This supports the administration’s goal that half of all new vehicles sold in 2030 are zero-emissions vehicles.
Operating at full capacity, the Georgia factory is expected to supply thermal barriers for over 2 million vehicle batteries annually.
The project is expected to create up to 550 construction jobs.
The project is expected to create up to 255 permanent, full-time operations jobs.
This supports the administration’s efforts to deliver good-paying, high-quality jobs to communities across the country.
This project adds to the nearly 16 million jobs created since President Biden and Vice President Harris took office.

While the conditional commitment indicates the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE)'s intent to finance the project, DOE and the company must satisfy certain technical, legal, environmental, and financial conditions before definitive financing documents are entered into and the loan is funded.

facts: Aspen Aerogels Inc (ASPN) held its Q4 earnings call to discuss financial performance and strategic outlook.
Full-year 2025 revenue reached $271.1 million, split between $168.9 million from Thermal Barrier and $102.2 million from Energy Industrial.
The company's business remains heavily tied to EV-related demand.
Q4 2025 sales slowed to $41.3 million, with Energy Industrial contributing $25.3 million and Thermal Barrier $16.1 million.
Management expects the Energy Industrial segment to grow about 20% in 2026.
Energy Industrial growth drivers include a rising subsea project pipeline, more LNG projects, and pent-up maintenance work at refineries and petrochemical plants.
LNG revenue alone is projected to roughly double versus 2025.
The company highlighted growing traction with European automakers, including seven design wins and a recent award with Volvo Cars.
Aspen sees a Europe-only pipeline of roughly $220 million tied to 2027 launches.
The European pipeline is expected to expand to more than $450 million in 2028.
These European programs are expected to add about $10–$15 million of revenue in 2026.
Aspen ended 2025 with $158.6 million in cash and cash equivalents, providing a substantial liquidity buffer.
The company amended its MidCap credit agreement to gain more covenant flexibility.
Aspen generated $6.1 million of cash in Q4 2025.
Management underscored structural fixed cash cost reductions of about $75 million annually.
Adjusted EBITDA breakeven was reduced from roughly $330 million in 2024 to about $270 million in 2025.
The adjusted EBITDA breakeven is targeted at around $200 million for 2026.
Management aims for an adjusted EBITDA breakeven near $175 million in 2027 to improve operating leverage.
For 2025, Aspen posted a gross profit of $46.3 million, translating into a 17% gross margin.
Management expects gross margins to expand as volumes recover and the leaner cost structure takes effect.
The company believes incremental revenue above breakeven could deliver 50%–60% EBITDA margins.
Aspen is developing a battery energy storage systems segment that will leverage its existing technology and domestic manufacturing capacity.
First revenue from the new battery energy storage segment is expected in 2026.
Management identified selective growth opportunities in building and construction as well as defense markets.
The company's 2026 capital expenditure plan is held to roughly $10 million.
Scheduled debt amortization for 2026 is about $35 million, including $24 million of term-loan principal.
Aspen expects to expand its net cash position to more than $70 million by the end of 2026.
The company reported a GAAP net loss of $389.6 million for 2025 and a Q4 GAAP loss of $72.9 million.
Adjusted EBITDA for 2025 was a modest $2.9 million, with Q4 adjusted EBITDA at negative $18 million.
U.S. EV sales slumped in Q4 2025, and General Motors’ decision to ramp down EV production reduced Aspen’s manufacturing absorption, sharply compressing margins.
Management expects EV demand to recover but now sees the production ramp as more measured.
Gross margin in Q4 was significantly hurt by lower production volumes and several discrete year-end items that inflated expenses.
Management noted temporarily elevated costs equal to 48% of revenue in the quarter, alongside multiple one-time adjustments.
The quarter absorbed a $3 million bad debt expense tied to a customer solvency issue, highlighting credit risk from concentrated customer exposure in emerging EV markets.
Energy Industrial revenue of $102.2 million in 2025 was mainly baseload maintenance work and limited LNG activity, marking a step down from the subsea-heavy record years of 2023 and 2024.
Management estimated a $30–$35 million revenue gap versus earlier peaks for the Energy Industrial segment.
Aspen guided to Q1 2026 as the seasonal low point, with revenue expected between $35 million and $40 million and adjusted EBITDA between negative $13 million and negative $10 million.

For 2026, management expects sequential revenue growth driven by roughly 20% expansion in Energy Industrial, $10–$15 million from European OEM programs, normalization of GM EV volumes, and beginning to book battery energy storage revenue.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels Charts Cost-Cut Path Amid EV Slowdown - TipRanks.com

facts: Aspen Aerogels, Inc. announced a commercial award for its PyroThin® EV Thermal Barrier segment.

The company received an additional PyroThin vehicle platform award from The Automotive Cells Company ("ACC"), a battery cell joint venture between Stellantis N.V., Saft-TotalEnergies, and Mercedes-Benz.
Aspen will supply the Stellantis STLA Medium vehicle platform under this award.
Expected start of production for the Stellantis STLA Medium platform is in 2025.

The company received a letter from the U.S. Department of Energy ("DOE") Loan Programs Office ("LPO") confirming its pending application for a loan under the DOE LPO's Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing ("ATVM") lending program is substantially complete.
The DOE invited Aspen to formal due diligence regarding this loan application.
This loan application is in connection with the construction of Aspen's planned second aerogel manufacturing facility in Georgia.
The DOE invitation does not guarantee or imply that Aspen will be invited further in the process, nor that a loan will be issued or terms will align with proposals.
Aspen continues to engage with the LPO and its advisors regarding the application.
Don Young, Aspen's President and CEO, stated these developments are meaningful steps in near-term execution to diversify PyroThin's customer base and optimize cost of capital.
Young noted the European market will drive a meaningful portion of revenues over the next few years as the company maximizes existing assets and commercial arrangements.
Aspen's aerogel technology enables customers to achieve objectives around resource efficiency, e-mobility, and clean energy.
PyroThin® products address thermal runaway challenges within the electric vehicle market.
Aspen Battery Materials is the company's carbon aerogel initiative aimed at increasing lithium-ion battery cell performance to extend EV driving range and reduce EV costs.
Aspen is headquartered in Northborough, Mass., and manufactures products at its East Providence, R.I. facilities.
Forward-looking statements indicate expectations regarding an extended construction and commissioning timeframe for the planned second manufacturing plant in Georgia ("Plant II").
Aspen is managing Plant II construction to align with expected demand from EV customers and may use contract manufacturers to meet Energy Industrial customer demand.

Risks associated with the project include the inability to complete construction and commissioning Plant II at estimated costs, potential cancellation of EV thermal barrier contracts by customers without penalty, costs exceeding projections, disruption in existing production lines or contract manufacturing, and failure to enforce patents.

facts: Beaver Manufacturing Company, Inc. (BMC) is a North American supplier of technical fibre reinforcements for hoses and other industrial applications based in Mansfield, GA.
BMC announced plans to expand its global presence by acquiring Intercord Thüringen GmbH of Germany, a producer of high-quality technical fibres.
The acquisition combines two technologies to increase the company’s customer base worldwide.
The partnership enables BMC to add RFL treating technology to its product line.
The acquisition adds a third production plant for BMC across the globe.
The new manufacturing facility is located in Mühlhausen Thüringen, Germany.
This German facility rounds out BMC’s worldwide footprint alongside existing plants in the US and Mexico.
Michael Dubin, Sr. Vice President & Chief Operating Officer of Beaver Manufacturing Company, Inc., stated that the acquisition represents a significant expansion into the European market.
The acquisition also marks BMC's entry into the tyre and belt industry.
BMC’s plant in Germany will continue to produce RFL treated yarns and cords.
The German facility will also produce hose products that incorporate Beaverloc treatments.
For more than 20 years, Intercord’s technology has produced cords for tyres, hoses, and transmission belts in the automotive and industrial industries.

Ramazan Yasbay, Managing Director of Intercord who will continue as Managing Director of the German facility for BMC, stated that Beaver Manufacturing advanced the U.S. hose yarn industry with environmentally friendly treated yarn solutions.
Yasbay noted that adding Beaver’s technology and product line to theirs will increase innovation in this industry throughout Europe and the Americas.
Despite a sluggish global economy during the pandemic, BMC executives say this partnership will continue its growth.
The partnership is expected to increase production and create jobs into 2021.
Only two years after opening its manufacturing site in Mexico, BMC announced this acquisition.

Executives involved in the deal include Craig Chandler (Vice President of Finance), Michael Dubin (Sr. Vice President & Chief Operating Officer), Ramazan Yasbay (Managing Director for Germany), and Walter vom Stein (former owner of Intercord).
source_title: Beaver Manufacturing expands with acquisition of Intercord Thüringen

facts: State and Middle Georgia officials gathered at the Blue Bird plant in Fort Valley to celebrate the completion of five custom-made buses for the Georgia Department of Corrections.
The event included a partial tour of the enormous bus manufacturing plant located on Ga. 49.
Gov. Nathan Deal attended the event, making several stops in Houston and Peach counties that day.
CEO and President Phil Horlock stated this is the second order of corrections transport buses placed with Blue Bird by the state.
Horlock noted the new bus design was launched in 2012, with the Georgia Department of Corrections serving as its first customer.
Corrections Commissioner Brian Owens reported the department operates a fleet of 24 buses used statewide and is about halfway through replacing them with Blue Bird buses.
Owens specified that despite modifications like white paint, window cages, and toilets, the department prioritizes quality, safety, reliability, and longevity.
Owens stated that among different manufacturers, Blue Bird sets the standards in those four key areas.
Two transport officers participated in the bus design process and attended the event.
Horlock explained that Blue Bird offers 25,000 different parts options, enabling complete customization to buyer specifications.
Following an initial order of 10 transport buses two years ago, orders have come from corrections departments in other states.
Owens commented that with only about 50 corrections commissioners in the U.S., positive product feedback spreads quickly among them.
Blue Bird is expected to grow following its announcement to go public and trade on the Nasdaq exchange.

Texas-based Hennessy Capital Acquisition Corp. plans to acquire Blue Bird in a deal valued at $490 million, including $255 million in cash and stock, while assuming approximately $235 million of Blue Bird’s debt.
Hennessy was established this year as a special purpose acquisition corporation trading under the ticker HCAC.
Upon consummation expected by the end of the year, Hennessy stock will convert to Blue Bird stock.
No operational changes are expected at the company, which currently employs about 1,600 workers.
Blue Bird Corp. manufactures a complete line of Type A, C, and D school buses with various options and configurations.
The company maintains a global presence with sales in more than 60 countries across Africa, Asia, the Caribbean, Latin America, Europe, and the Middle East.
source_title: Custom-made corrections buses roll off Blue Bird line in Fort Valley  | Macon Telegraph

facts: On August 13, 2024, Gurminder Bedi, a Director at Blue Bird Corp, executed a sale of 18,896 shares of the company.
Following this transaction, Gurminder Bedi owns 0 shares of Blue Bird Corp.
The shares were sold at a price of $48.35 per share.
Blue Bird Corp is headquartered in Fort Valley, Georgia.
Blue Bird Corp designs, builds, and sells school buses to a variety of markets across the United States.
Over the past year, Gurminder Bedi has sold a total of 27,615 shares of Blue Bird Corp and has not purchased any shares.
There have been 15 insider sells and no insider buys at Blue Bird Corp over the past year.
As of the date of the sale, Blue Bird Corp's market cap was approximately $1.55 billion.
Blue Bird Corp's price-earnings ratio stands at 15.83, which is slightly above the industry median of 14.965.
According to GF Value, an intrinsic value estimate used by GuruFocus, Blue Bird Corp is currently significantly overvalued with a GF Value of $26.98.
The price-to-GF-Value ratio for Blue Bird Corp is 1.79.

The valuation is based on historical trading multiples like the price-earnings ratio, price-sales ratio, price-book ratio, and price-to-free cash flow, adjusted for the company's past performance and expected future business outcomes.
source_title: Insider Sale: Director Gurminder Bedi Sells 18,896 Shares of Blue Bird Corp (BLBD)

facts: Workers at Blue Bird Corp., a notable Georgia school bus manufacturer, voted in favor to ratify their first union contract, concluding a year-long effort that began when they opted to unionize.
The agreement between United Steelworkers and Blue Bird spans three years and promises a minimum of 12% pay raise for the 1,500 workers covered.
Certain employees, particularly those on the lower end of the pay scale, will see their incomes increase by over 40%.
The company has agreed to contribute to a retirement plan for workers, share profits, and enhance health and safety standards under the new contract.
Blue Bird received a substantial sum of $40 million in federal aid focused on manufacturing electric school buses.

President Joe Biden lauded the agreement, stating, "Congratulations to members of the United Steelworkers and to Blue Bird for proving once again that meeting our clean energy goals is an opportunity to create good-paying union jobs for American workers."
Despite Blue Bird's initial opposition to unionization, CEO Phil Horlock described the contract negotiations as "very collaborative" and expressed optimism about the future.

CEO Phil Horlock stated in a statement obtained by WABE: "We reached an agreement which provides positive outcomes for all parties involved and will continue to drive our One Team, high-performance culture."
The new terms are expected to enhance Blue Bird's ability to attract quality workers by offering competitive pay and developmental opportunities.
This marks a critical achievement for the United Steelworkers in a region typically resistant to organized labor.

Dan Flippo, United Steelworkers District 9 Director, emphasized the importance of worker representation, especially when federal investments are at stake, stating: "Federal investments like these must come with a seat at the table for workers."
Blue Bird is the largest private employer in Peach County, Georgia.
Georgia has a low union membership rate compared to the national average, with only 4.4% of workers belonging to unions versus the national rate that dipped to 10% last year.
The passage of this union contract at Blue Bird could signal a change in the tide for the labor movement within the state and potentially beyond.
source_title: Georgia School Bus Manufacturer Blue Bird Ratifies Historic First

facts: Blue Bird Corporation is headquartered in Macon, Ga.
Georgia has been Blue Bird's home for nearly a century, according to president and CEO John Wyskiel.
The Blue Bird School Bus Foundation hosted a community event at its corporate headquarters in Macon, Ga., and awarded a total of $53,300 to ten local area nonprofit organizations.
Donations from the foundation ranged from $2,500 up to $12,500 per organization.
Safe Kids Central Georgia received the top-level donation of $12,500 to promote child car seat safety and provide free car seats and educational materials to low-income families.

Since its inception in 2015, the Blue Bird School Bus Foundation has donated more than $600,000 in charitable contributions to various community organizations, including more than 85 Georgia-based nonprofits.

The foundation awarded grants to ten specific Georgia nonprofits: Atrium Health Navicent Medical Center, Brave Meadows Therapeutic Riding Center Inc., Fort Valley Lions Club, Girl Scouts of Historic Georgia Inc., Macon Reviving Baseball in the Inner Cities Program by the Boys and Girls Club, Peach Association for Intellectual Disabled Inc. aka Kay Center, The Salvation Army Warner Robins Corps, Safe Kids Central Georgia, Sleep in Heavenly Peace Inc., and United in Pink Inc.
Since December 2023, Blue Bird has donated three all-electric, zero-emission school buses to community organizations.
Peach County Schools in Fort Valley, Ga., received a donated zero-emission bus to add to its fleet as a clean transportation option for day and field trips.
The Jerome Bettis Bus Stops Here Foundation in Pittsburgh, Pa., received a donated electric bus to convert into a mobile computer lab for digital literacy programs.
Hoop Bus in Los Angeles, Ca., received a donated zero-emission bus equipped with basketball hoops to serve as an interactive, mobile court for at-risk youth.
Blue Bird Corporation was founded in 1927 and is recognized as a technology leader and innovator of school buses.
The company designs, engineers, and manufactures school buses with a singular focus on safety, reliability, and durability.
Blue Bird school buses transport approximately 25 million children twice a day.
Blue Bird has sold more than 25,000 propane, natural gas, and electric powered buses.
The company is the proven leader in low- and zero-emission school buses and is transforming the student transportation industry through cleaner energy solutions.

facts: Blue Bird Corporation (Nasdaq: BLBD) is the leader in electric and low-emission school buses.

The company launched its Blue Bird Energy Services business to accelerate the electrification of the student transportation industry by providing turnkey charging infrastructure solutions for electric school bus fleets.
Blue Bird Energy Services helps electric school bus fleet customers meet their charging infrastructure needs.
The company offers various electric school bus charging options ranging from AC Level 2 to DC fast-chargers rated at 30, 60, and 120kW.
Blue Bird has nearly 650 electric-powered, zero-emission school buses in operation today.
The company builds a full range of electric school buses which can carry a maximum of 84 passengers for up to 120 miles on a single charge.
Depending on the charging infrastructure, the buses take between three and eight hours to recharge fully.
Blue Bird develops a complete ecosystem of electric products and services for school districts, bus fleet operators, and its dealer network across the United States.
The company helps customers assess electric vehicle and charging infrastructure needs.
The company helps customers identify funding sources including grants.
The company helps customers understand vehicle and fleet performance through a world-class telematics solution.
The company helps customers safely operate and maintain vehicles through driver and technician training.
The company ensures bus performance through high-quality service over the life of the vehicle.
Through Blue Bird Energy Services, the company provides site engineering, permitting, construction, customizable hardware and software, warranties, and maintenance for charging infrastructure.
Chargers feature single or dual ports to connect and charge simultaneously up to two electric-powered vehicles.
Britton Smith serves as senior vice president of Electrification and Chief Strategy Officer at Blue Bird Corporation.
Blue Bird was founded in 1927.
Blue Bird buses carry the majority of 25 million children twice a day.
The company has more than 20,000 propane, natural gas, and electric powered buses in operation today.

facts: Blue Bird Corp. is based in Fort Valley, Georgia.
The company has more than 100 electric buses on order from school districts throughout the nation.
Its electric buses have already hit the pavement in California, North Dakota, and Washington.
On-order buses will transport students in Colorado, New Jersey, New York, and Quebec this year.
100% electric-powered school buses often cost two to three times the price of diesel counterparts.
Interest in electric vehicles has been growing.
Electric buses have zero emissions, thereby improving air quality.
Electric buses require less maintenance, which saves school districts time and money.
Blue Bird has been working on its electric bus technology for more than 25 years.
The company's current buses have a 120-mile range.
The buses can be recharged overnight.

facts: Atlanta-based Delta Air Lines signed an agreement to purchase 10 million gallons a year of advanced renewable biofuel from Gevo Inc.
Gevo Inc. is a renewable chemicals and biofuels company based in Colorado.
Delta estimates the sustainable aviation fuel will have a carbon footprint that is up to 75% less than conventional jet fuel.
Gevo says its renewable biofuel performs as well as or better than traditional fossil-based fuels in jet engines.
The deal follows Delta’s $2-million investment in Northwest Advanced Bio-Fuels for a feasibility study of a potential biofuel facility in Washington state.
These efforts are part of the airline’s stated goal of reducing emissions 50% by 2050.
Since 2005, Delta estimates it has reduced emissions by 11% as a result of reducing jet fuel consumption.
The company has been investing in fuel-efficient aircraft.
Delta was the first U.S. airline to recycle aluminum cans, plastics, newspapers, and magazines.
