facts: Bonnell Aluminum announced it will invest approximately $18 million over the next 12 months to fund an expansion project.
The investment will include the purchase of a new state-of-the-art aluminum extrusion line at its AACOA facility in Niles, Michigan.
Planned startup for the new line is the second quarter 2017.
Brook Hamilton, President and General Manager for Bonnell Aluminum, stated that growing customer demand prompted the investment to satisfy opportunities with high-quality products and services.
Ira Endres, Vice President Sales and Marketing, noted that AACOA serves the automotive market due to its regional location alongside consumer durables and machinery/equipment markets.
The new line will be comprised of a state-of-the-art 3600-ton extrusion press housing a 9-inch container, handling systems, and ancillary equipment.
Capacity for the new line is estimated at approximately 16 million pounds.
The project includes additional floor space to accommodate future value-added fabrication capacity.
Bonnell Aluminum is a subsidiary of Richmond, Virginia-based Tredegar Corporation (NYSE:TG).

The company produces soft and medium-strength alloy aluminum extrusions for building and construction, automotive, machinery and equipment, consumer durables, transportation, electrical and distribution markets.
On October 1, 2012, Bonnell Aluminum acquired AACOA, a manufacturer, fabricator, and anodizer of aluminum extrusions with operations in Niles, MI, and Elkhart, IN.
source_title: Bonnell Aluminum to Invest $18 Million, Expand Automotive and Specialty Markets - Heat Treat Today

facts: Bonnell Aluminum is a subsidiary of Tredegar Corporation, a publicly traded company that manufactures plastic films and aluminum extrusions.
The company specializes in the production of custom aluminum extrusions and fabricated aluminum parts.
The company was founded in 1953.
The company is headquartered in Newnan, Georgia.
Bonnell Aluminum has manufacturing facilities in Georgia, Michigan, Tennessee, and Indiana.
The company operates a sales office in Shanghai, China.
The company serves a variety of industries, including automotive, construction, and consumer goods.
source_title: Bonnell Aluminum Careers, Jobs, and Salary Information

facts: Tredegar reported first-quarter 2025 net income from continuing operations of $0.7 million ($0.02 per share), down from $2.6 million ($0.08 per share) in the first quarter of 2024.
On an adjusted basis, Tredegar's first-quarter 2025 net income from ongoing operations fell to $3.6 million ($0.10 per share) from $4.7 million ($0.14 per share) a year ago.
The company's first-quarter 2025 net sales reached $164.7 million, representing a 14.4% increase from $144 million in the year-ago quarter.
Tredegar's Bonnell Aluminum segment reported net sales of $133.6 million, a 17% year-over-year increase driven by a 12% rise in sales volume and pass-through of higher metal costs.

EBITDA from ongoing operations in the Bonnell Aluminum segment declined 27% to $9.2 million from $12.5 million in the prior-year quarter due to an unfavorable shift in sales mix, higher input costs, increased labor and maintenance expenses, and elevated SG&A costs.
Specialty products within the Bonnell Aluminum segment led volume growth, jumping 52.8%, primarily due to increased shipments for solar panel applications and TSLOTS framing systems.

The PE Films segment delivered an 8.9% improvement in EBITDA to $7.5 million despite a 4% drop in sales volume, with net sales edging up 3.2% to $25.5 million due to strength in surface protection films.
CEO John Steitz noted that the recovery in Bonnell Aluminum is gaining traction, supported by a tenth consecutive quarterly increase in net new orders, which rose 36% year over year.
Open orders for aluminum extrusions reached 25 million pounds, marking the highest level in two years.

Management highlighted the benefit of new Section 232 tariffs, which raise the rate on aluminum imports from 10% to 25% and expand coverage to downstream products, helping restore market share previously lost to underpriced imports.
The company has not yet seen a decline in demand stemming from U.S. tariff actions on imported electronics, though management remains cautious amid ongoing global trade developments.

The decline in Aluminum Extrusions' EBITDA was also driven by a lower spread between selling prices and metal costs, unfavorable manufacturing yields, and higher expenses related to labor, maintenance, dies, and utilities.
Timing mismatches in aluminum pricing under the FIFO method contributed a $1.7 million benefit versus a $1.3 million charge in the prior year.
Increased SG&A costs in the Aluminum Extrusions segment (up 38.5%) stemmed mainly from compensation, travel, and consulting fees.
PE Films benefited from $1.5 million in incremental contribution from surface protection, offset by a $0.7 million decline from overwrap films due to weaker volume and less favorable pricing.
For 2025, Tredegar forecasts capital expenditures of $17 million for Bonnell Aluminum and $3 million for PE Films, largely directed toward productivity and operational continuity.
Depreciation and amortization expenses are projected at $16 million and $2 million for Aluminum Extrusions, and $5 million for PE Films in 2025.
Tredegar's total debt declined to $56.6 million as of March 31, 2025, from $61.9 million at year-end 2024, with net debt standing at $52.9 million and a net leverage ratio of 1.1x.
The company refinanced its $125 million asset-based lending facility in May for a new five-year term to ensure continued liquidity.
During the quarter, Tredegar completed the sale of its Terphane business and received $9.8 million from a post-closing settlement, contributing to a $9.4 million gain from discontinued operations.
Restructuring activities included the closure of the Richmond, VA, PE Films technical center, which concluded at the end of the first quarter of 2024.
source_title: Tredegar Q1 Earnings Down Y/Y as Aluminum Costs Weigh on Margins — TradingView News

facts: Bonnell Aluminum is headquartered in Newnan, Georgia, and is a leading manufacturer of custom aluminum extrusions.
The company has a 65-year history in the extrusion industry.
Bonnell Aluminum completed the installation of an ASCONA promex EXPERT profile measuring system at its plant in Carthage, Tennessee, to improve extrusion quality.
This is the second profile measurement system of this type installed by the company.
Bonnell Aluminum, a subsidiary of Tredegar Corporation, plans to expand operations focused on producing TSLOTS aluminum structural framing systems.
The company will add a manufacturing and distribution site for the TSLOTS framing system to its existing facility in Newnan, Georgia.
This expansion will enable Bonnell to provide customers based in the Southeastern U.S. with expanded TSLOTS availability.
Bonnell Aluminum is expanding its TSLOTS line of aluminum structural framing systems into the Midwest region of the U.S. with a new location and increased storage capacity.
Customers in the Midwest and East have been requesting expanded TSLOTS availability as the product line has grown.
Futura Industries, a subsidiary of Bonnell Aluminum, installed two new CNC machines due to increased demand for fabrication and precision milling.
The new CNC machines will improve lead times and provide better fabrication speed and reliability.
The 2026 ETF Rick Merluzzi Student Design Competition is sponsored by MI Metals, Inc., Pennex Aluminum, and Bonnell Aluminum.
The AEC and ET Foundation announced the winners of the 2018 Aluminum Extrusion Design Competition at the EDU ’18 event held May 15-17 in Chicago, Illinois.
Seven extrusion designs showcasing the versatility and advantages of aluminum extrusions were recognized at the 2018 competition.
source_title: Bonnell Aluminum Archives - Light Metal Age Magazine

facts: Journeyman-level machinists and state-of-the-art equipment makes for expertise in handling all extrusion machining projects.
CNC machining cells are used to supply customers with Excellence Beyond Extrusion.
The machining department employs high-end tooling in all aspects of its processes.
Processes utilize automatic part positioning, custom-made clamping, in-process error proofing, and diamond tooling.
These capabilities enable production within tight tolerances with superior surface finish qualities.
The company offers state-of-the-art 3, 4, and 5-axis CNC machines.
CNC machining delivers exceptional precision and versatility, creating products with tight tolerances and superior finishes.
Applications for the machining services range from automotive components to construction tools.
Advanced robotic welding systems guarantee consistent, high-strength welds across a broad range of applications.
Integrated robotic welding and CNC machining services work in harmony to reduce cycle times and elevate product quality.

facts: Electric trucks are on roads today, day in and day out.
Cascadia is a class leader in fuel efficiency and reliability due to superior components, innovative technology and unsurpassed driver comfort.
The M2 106 Plus is the #1 best-selling medium-duty truck in North America, offering exceptional productivity, best-in-class visibility and unrivaled versatility.
Freightliner provides a variety of helpful content and driver training resources for customers.
Freightliner operates hundreds of meticulously prepared service centers across North America, staffed by trained experts in both vehicle and customer service.
Freightliner remains future-focused, advancing new technologies and leading the industry through innovation.

facts: Nearly three months after the first effects of the COVID-19 pandemic suspended manufacturing operations, Daimler Trucks North America (DTNA) has reopened all nine of its manufacturing locations in North America.

The reopening of Saltillo and Santiago Truck Manufacturing Plants in Mexico on June 1 marks the beginning of a full restart of operations and the start of resupply for commercial vehicle operators across the world.
The Saltillo and Santiago plants are strong nodes in DTNA’s production network, and help support the trucks manufactured for export to the 35 countries in which DTNA sells commercial vehicles.
Daimler’s supply chain relies heavily on both the U.S. and Mexico for all new truck manufacturing in North America, with components and subassemblies crossing the border in both directions.

DTNA implemented universal safety protocols along with measures tailored for each facility in its network to make workspaces as safe as possible and fully comply with all federal, state and provincial, and local regulations.

At all of its facilities across the U.S. and Mexico, DTNA has also established localized task forces comprised of plant leadership and labor union representatives to routinely assess and fine-tune its protocols.

Among the safety measures implemented are standardized cleaning regimens, redesigned workflows in observance of social distancing, redesigned layouts of common spaces, employee and visitor temperature screening with health questionnaires, and frequent communications to the employees.

Although facilities in Mexico did not open for full production until June 1, following the precedent of sites in the U.S., operations in Santiago and Saltillo did reopen for employee training, testing of safety protocols and limited production the week of May 18 following certification by the federal government.

Anonymized data collected from 130,000 telematics-enabled Freightliner Cascadias provide a real-time picture of the effects of stay-at-home orders and depressed economic activity across the continent.
As the COVID-19 pandemic advanced, the weekly mileage accumulated by the connected, collective Cascadia fleet began to drop markedly, decreasing approximately 15 percent by the end of April.
Through May, DTNA has observed a reversal in the trend, with the weekly mileage increasing every week and projected to return to pre-crisis levels by the end of June.
The pace of manufacturing at DTNA continues to advance with over 500 trucks, buses and specialty chassis produced per day in the past few days.

Roger Nielsen, president and CEO of DTNA, stated: 'Our commitment to the customers and drivers on the road for us, our families, and the world remains steadfast. Whether at our dealers’ sales and service centers, our parts distribution centers or at our manufacturing operations, DTNA is open for business, and we are here for you.'
Daimler Trucks North America LLC is headquartered in Portland, Oregon.
Daimler Trucks North America produces and markets commercial vehicles under the Freightliner, Western Star and Thomas Built Buses nameplates.
Daimler Trucks North America is a Daimler company, the world’s leading commercial vehicle manufacturer.
source_title: Daimler Trucks North America Resumes Production in Mexico, Restarting All Manufacturing Operations - Thomas Built Buses

entity_name: Daimler Trucks Electric Truck Charging Joint Venture
canonical_name: Daimler Trucks, NextEra Energy Resources, and BlackRock Joint Venture
facts: Daimler Truck North America, NextEra Energy Resources and BlackRock have agreed to form a joint venture to build and operate high-performance energy infrastructure for battery electric and fuel cell commercial vehicles in the USA.
The joint venture will likely become a megawatt-charger network.
The partners plan to start joint activities in 2022.
The partners plan to build the first charging stations in 2023.
The planned funding is around $650 million (about €580Mn), which the three companies will provide in equal parts.
The joint venture plans to establish a network of charging stations on freight routes along the east and west coasts and in Texas by 2026.
The network will leverage existing infrastructure and amenities while adding complementary greenfield sites.
The initial focus will be on charging stations for battery-electric medium and heavy-duty commercial vehicles.
The plan is followed by hydrogen refuelling stations for fuel cell trucks.
Technical details regarding the charging infrastructure remain scarce.
The joint venture expects to benefit from the experience of its founding members, most notably Daimler Trucks.
Daimler Trucks is developing electric trucks and hopes to produce the battery-electric Freightliner eCascadia and eM2 in 2022/23.
Daimler Trucks is piloting truck charging sites through its affiliate DNTA.
In cooperation with local utility company Portland General Electric (PGE), DTNA opened a public charging site for commercial vehicles in Portland, OR.

In Europe, Daimler Trucks confirmed the formation of a joint venture with the Traton Group and the Volvo Group to establish and operate a public charging network for battery-electric heavy-duty long-haul trucks last December.

Martin Daum, the Daimler Truck CEO, said the European JV deal was “great news for the transport industry and society, as it underlines all of the partners’ strong commitment to making CO2-neutral trucks a reality. It is remarkable that three fierce competitors in the area of trucks and vehicle technology are taking action together to start establishing the needed charging infrastructure.”

Daimler Trucks pointed out that the planned North American joint venture was “the latest in a line of partnerships proving Daimler Trucks’ continuous efforts to strategically align with top tier players in the field of key transformation technologies.”
Daimler Truck and the Volvo Group are working together in the fuel cell joint venture Cellcentric in Europe.
Daimler Trucks is putting in considerable investment in hydrogen infrastructure.
A recent deal with Total Energies will see Daimler work together with the oil company to develop hydrogen infrastructure for heavy trucks in the European Union.
The other partners in North America appear primarily on board as investors.
NextEra Energy Resources is an investor in electric infrastructure and brings experience optimizing renewable energy and grid integration.
BlackRock Renewable Power claims it operates one of the world’s largest renewable power equity investment platforms.
BlackRock seeks to invest over $9.5 billion across the spectrum of renewable power.
BlackRock is committed to over 350 wind and solar projects across 15 countries and five continents.
In terms of e-mobility, BlackRock is invested in Ionity since the Group partook in a €700-million investment round closed in November 2021.
source_title: NOAM: Daimler Trucks to form electric truck charging JV - electrive.com

facts: Daimler Truck North America LLC (DTNA), the nation’s largest manufacturer of Class 6-8 commercial vehicles, entered into a long-term agreement with Hexagon Purus on November 6, 2023.
The agreement provides complete vehicle integration of the battery electric Freightliner eM2, developed to serve vocational applications.
Hexagon Purus is a leading manufacturer of zero-emission mobility and infrastructure solutions.
Hexagon Purus will incorporate its proprietary zero-emission technology into the integration, including battery systems, auxiliary modules, power modules, and vehicle-level software.
The integration will also include power-take-off (PTO) options to supply power to the vocational body and equipment.

Earlier in 2023, DTNA unveiled a vocational Freightliner eM2 prototype truck with vocational upfit options as part of an innovation project together with selected Truck Equipment Manufacturers Alamo and Altec.
The innovation project aims to lay the groundwork for expanding zero-emission solutions for vocational customers in the utility, sweeper, dump, construction, towing, and refuse segments.
The partnership underscores a joint commitment by DTNA and Hexagon Purus to drive innovation and sustainability in the vocational vehicle sector.
The collaboration will deliver products that complement DTNA’s eCascadia and eM2 battery electric portfolio, which is already in production.
Aaron Scates, vice president of vocational and medium-duty market development at DTNA, stated excitement about the collaboration's potential for the future of electric vocational trucks.
Morten Holum, CEO of Hexagon Purus, expressed pride in continuing their long-standing relationship and supporting DTNA’s transition to zero-emission mobility.
DTNA has been an important zero-emission technology development partner for Hexagon Purus in North America for several years through the Innovation Fleet program.
Prior to this collaboration, DTNA worked with Hexagon's Agility division on the development of Freightliner natural gas fuel tank integration.
Hexagon Purus’ high-voltage battery technology is known for its efficient kWh per meter of frame length, which aligns with the packaging needs of medium-duty vocational applications.
Daimler Truck North America LLC is headquartered in Portland, Oregon.

DTNA designs, engineers, manufactures and markets medium- and heavy-duty trucks, school buses, vehicle chassis and their associated technologies and components under the Freightliner, Western Star, Thomas Built Buses, Freightliner Custom Chassis Corp and Detroit brands.
Daimler Truck North America is a subsidiary of Daimler Truck, one of the world’s leading commercial vehicle manufacturers.

Hexagon Purus is a world leading provider of hydrogen Type 4 high-pressure cylinders and systems, battery systems and vehicle integration solutions for fuel cell electric and battery electric vehicles.
Hexagon Purus' products are used in a variety of applications including light, medium and heavy-duty vehicles, buses, ground storage, distribution, refueling, maritime, rail and aerospace.
source_title: Daimler Truck North America Enters Strategic | decarbonfuse.com

facts: Freightliner is impressed by the development taking place in many markets.
Hardest Working Cities honors and celebrates the communities that are leading the economic recovery.
Diane Hames serves as general manager of marketing and strategy at Daimler Trucks North America.
The Freightliner Trucks Hardest Working Cities program was announced at an event at the NTEA Work Truck Show in Indianapolis.
The new program recognizes the vital role vocational trucks and the men and women who operate them play in stimulating economies in the United States and Canada.
Selecting these cities involved a comprehensive review of public and industry information to determine communities working the hardest to drive the economy.
Approximately 400 metropolitan census areas in the United States and Canada were analyzed for the selection process.

Data from 11 different categories were factored into this process, from total employment and average hours worked to the number of jobs in key industry sectors such as construction, manufacturing and logistics.
Indianapolis was announced as the first recipient of the honor.
A Freightliner Trucks Hardest Working Cities open house was held at Stoops Freightliner - Quality Trailer, part of the 20-location Truck Country dealership group.
Vocational truck owners, truck equipment manufacturers, local officials and Freightliner representatives attended the Indianapolis event.
Freightliner Trucks made surprise coffee and doughnut deliveries to three construction sites on the morning of the announcement.
Freightliner will announce five more Hardest Working Cities throughout 2015 in tandem with congratulatory events at dealerships.
These upcoming events will be held to recognize customers, truck equipment manufacturers, local community leaders and business partners.
Freightliner will support the program through national and local advertising, publicity, social media and a new webpage.
Additional cities will be announced in 2016.
source_title: Freightliner Trucks Hardest Working Cities Salutes Job Creation and Innovation

facts: Daimler Truck North America has an autonomous eCascadia daycab equipped with Torc Robotics’ self-driving technology and Level 4 autonomous sensors and computing power.
The OEM showed off the autonomous eCascadia demonstrator during a virtual media presentation Tuesday broadcast from its North American headquarters in Portland, Oregon.
Torc Robotics is an independent Daimler Truck subsidiary that plans to roll out its diesel-powered AV product to fleets in 2027.

The new EV-AV demonstrator complements traditional Freightliner Cascadia long-haul sleeper tractors packed with autonomous technology and opens a path for Daimler to adapt Torc technology on future powertrains.

Joanna Buttler, Daimler Truck’s head of global autonomous technology, stated the company initially focused on diesel configuration for launch but is combining freight efficiency potential of autonomous trucks with zero emissions and decarbonization via series production electric vehicles.
The ZE-AV combo starts with a battery-electric eCascadia but envisions the same technology fitting with potential hydrogen fuel cell EVs and hydrogen internal combustion engine tractors.
Daimler is the first legacy North American truck OEM to attach autonomous technology to one of its Class 8 EVs.

Torc rival Kodiak Robotics pursued an OEM-agnostic approach to self-driving freight, first featuring an all-electric autonomous Class 8 truck a year ago by upfitting a Peterbilt Model 579EV shown at the Advanced Clean Transportation Expo in 2023.
Autonomous technology provider Aurora has North American partnerships with Volvo Trucks and Paccar’s Kenworth and Peterbilt.
Daimler Truck plans to show off its demonstration EV-AV at this year’s ACT Expo later this month.
Since 2022, more than 55 U.S. fleets have integrated eCascadias into their operations, logging more than 6 million miles, according to DTNA.

The company considers its demonstration autonomous EV a research and advanced engineering project with potential to evolve into a modular, scalable platform that is propulsion-agnostic for flexible use in different trucking applications.
Autonomous trucking is already proving to be more fuel-efficient than human-controlled trucks, with some AV companies noting fuel savings of up to 17% in testing across Texas and the Southwest.
John O’Leary, DTNA president and CEO, said combining zero-emission and autonomous technologies tests solutions for future customer challenges and gives them choices to keep the world moving.

In advance of its 2027 rollout, diesel-powered Freightliner Cascadias with Torc’s AV technology are in tests between Phoenix and Oklahoma City, hauling freight for FleetOwner 500 fleets such as Schneider and C.R. England.
Daimler expects autonomous trucking to generate more than $3 billion in revenue as early as 2030.

Current Torc-driven Cascadias work on long-haul, hub-to-hub freight movement, while the autonomous eCascadia would be more applicable for shorter, repeatable routes based around charging infrastructure.

Daimler hopes to find synergies between ZE and AV infrastructure, studying routes and locations that suit both needs, with future possibilities including combining charging infrastructure with autonomous freight hubs so an electric AV can charge while loaded and unloaded.
Truck charging venture Greenlane, created by DTNA, NextEra Energy Resources, and Blackrock, is building out infrastructure in California with plans to expand west toward Texas and the Northeast.

Greenlane aims to develop a nationwide network of commercial charging and hydrogen infrastructure locations across the U.S., serving battery-electric passenger car and light-duty fleet customers while providing hydrogen refueling for commercial vehicles in the coming years.

Suman Narayanan, DTNA’s chief vehicle engineer for autonomous driving, said the demonstrator will help set up Daimler Truck for any future propulsion technology, noting similarities between fuel cell propulsion and battery-electric vehicles to aid transition to hydrogen fuel cells or internal combustion engines.
source_title: Daimler demonstrates autonomous electric truck potential with Freightliner eCascadia | FleetOwner

facts: Daimler Trucks North America (DTNA) announced an $18M investment to upgrade its current research and development facility in Madras, Oregon.
The upgraded Madras facility will create more than 30 new jobs in an area located approximately 120 miles southeast of Portland.
DTNA produces class 6-8 vehicles under the Freightliner, Western Star, Thomas Built Buses, and Freightliner Custom Chassis nameplates.
The Madras facility supports ongoing testing, research, and development to provide value to DTNA's customers.
The facility's test track is designed to simulate a typical vehicle’s full service life over a variety of road surfaces in less than six months.
The proximity of the Madras facility to DTNA’s Portland headquarters enables office and field engineers to collaborate more easily on projects.

Matthew Markstaller, Property Manager at Daimler Trucks North America, stated that locating their own durability test facility in Madras will further centralize and strengthen the company's engineering presence in Oregon.
Mayor Royce Embanks welcomed DTNA's commitment to Madras and the local economy, noting it as a valuable partnership.

Local and state officials, including the City Council, Business Oregon, EDCO in Jefferson County, ODOT, and Jefferson County, collaborated to secure state grants and utilize an enterprise zone, which influenced DTNA's decision to build and expand in Oregon.
Construction of the upgraded facility, which will include a test track and several buildings, was scheduled to begin in winter 2015 and complete by the end of 2016.
The Madras facility upgrade was enabled by DTNA's significant capital investment alongside support from local and state officials.
Two years prior to this announcement, DTNA had announced plans to construct a new $150M headquarters building on its campus on Swan Island in North Portland.
Daimler Trucks North America LLC is headquartered in Portland, Oregon, and operates as the leading heavy-duty truck manufacturer in North America.
DTNA produces and markets commercial vehicles under the Freightliner, Western Star, and Thomas Built Buses nameplates.
DTNA is a subsidiary of Daimler, the world’s leading commercial vehicle manufacturer.
source_title: Daimler Trucks North America Demonstrates Continued Commitment to Oregon with $18M Upgrade to Madras Testing Facility - Thomas Built Buses

facts: Daimler Truck North America (DTNA) is working with its transport provider Salem Carriers to deliver inbound parts on electric trucks in the US Carolinas.
Salem Carriers transports parts between DTNA’s manufacturing sites, supply chain hubs, and supplier locations across the two states.
Salem Carriers is using DTNA’s Freightliner eCascadia trucks on daily inbound routes.
Operations are supported by a partnership with fleet electrification supplier Electrada for the development, operation, and maintenance of necessary charging infrastructure.
The partnership is backed by Daimler Truck Financial Services (DTFS) to provide charging-as-a-service (CaaS) with a fixed and predictable monthly rate.
Dennis Giff, general manager at Salem Carriers, stated that introducing battery electric vehicles to their operations was a new initiative.
Dennis Giff noted the DTFS/Electrada approach was unified, aligned with operating needs, and set the stage to scale future electric vehicle integration into their services.
source_title: Daimler Truck North America takes charge on cleaner inbound logistics | News | Automotive Logistics

facts: The United Auto Workers union struck a favorable new contract deal for 7,300 Daimler Truck North America workers.
The union had threatened a strike starting at midnight when their last contract expired.
A vast majority of the union employees work at plants in North Carolina, where Daimler makes Freightliner and Western Star trucks and Thomas Built buses.
A smaller number of workers staff parts distribution centers in Atlanta and Memphis.
The UAW first unionized workers at Daimler Truck starting in the 1990s.
Daimler workers have been demanding significant raises, reviving the "record profits mean record contracts" slogan of last year's strike.
The new contract includes raises of at least 25% over four years.
The new contract also includes cost of living allowances and profit sharing.
These gains are the firsts for Daimler Truck workers since they joined the UAW.
Union workers still need to ratify the deal.
Daimler Truck stated it was engaged in good faith negotiations with the UAW toward new contracts that would benefit both sides and allow continued product delivery.
The talks culminated just a week after the UAW won a union election at Volkswagen's plant in Chattanooga, Tenn.
The VW plant win was the union's third attempt at organizing that plant, after two previous narrow defeats.
Workers at the Mercedes-Benz plant in Vance, Ala., will begin voting on whether to join the UAW on May 13.
Daimler Truck split with Mercedes-Benz in 2021.
A favorable outcome for workers in North Carolina could give the UAW a boost in upcoming union drives at Hyundai, Toyota, and Honda plants in the South.
The UAW pledged to spend $40 million on organizing efforts through 2026, with a focus on the South.
source_title: UAW strike at Daimler Truck averted at 11th hour | Northern Public Radio: WNIJ and WNIU

facts: Daimler Truck North America (DTNA) has announced significant investments in its headquarters complex on Swan Island in Portland, Oregon.
The company is set to establish a new Electric Vehicle Supply Equipment (EVSE) training center adjacent to Electric Island.
The new EVSE training center aims to accelerate the testing and development of charging infrastructure for commercial vehicles.
The training center will serve as a resource center for the DTNA dealer network and community stakeholders.
The training center is expected to cost around $3 million.
Funding for the training center is partly provided through a forgivable loan from Prosper Portland, the city’s economic development agency.
DTNA is constructing a new engineering facility covering approximately 110,000 ft² at the DTNA campus.
The new engineering facility will consolidate testing activities and focus on research and development for emerging propulsion technologies, including battery electric and hydrogen-fueled vehicles.
The State of Oregon is supporting the construction of the engineering facility with a $700,000 forgivable loan from the State’s Strategic Reserve fund.
John O’Leary, President and CEO of DTNA, stated: “We appreciate the City and State’s support of our expansion plans to further promote the development and proper testing of advanced technology.”

Portland City Commissioner Carmen Rubio praised DTNA’s investment, stating: “I am thrilled by DTNA’s decision to invest significant resources and continue to lead heavy-duty vehicle technology right here in Portland.”
Portland has been a key location for DTNA, with recent milestones including the start of customer deliveries of the Freightliner eM2 electric truck.
DTNA introduced the eCascadia in 2022.
The company electrified its logistics at the facility towards the end of 2023.
source_title: Daimler Truck North America Invests in Electric Vehicle Infrastructure and Engineering in Portland

facts: Werner Enterprises announced the integration of Daimler Trucks North America’s (DTNA) application programming interface (API) into its fleet management platform.
The integration is anticipated to significantly increase uptime for Werner’s professional drivers and improve efficiencies for its event resolution team.
The move will improve repair velocity at DTNA dealerships.
Director of Equipment Purchasing Tony Wahl stated Werner is working with DTNA to create a cloud-based maintenance system that will greatly reduce manual inputs and phone calls to dealers.
With the new systems, Werner will have better visibility of tractors and their repairs, faster decision making, and cleaner data flow pertaining to tractor repairs.
The goal of the integration is to provide better predictive and scheduled maintenance for Werner's fleet and maximize uptime.
The integration with DTNA’s API launched in September 2021.
The integration was built to follow the industry standard of the Technology Maintenance Council.
Werner will continue to focus on direct API integrations through its innovation arm, Werner EDGE.

DTNA's Director of Customer Experience Erick Nelson stated that preventing unplanned downtime is key to keeping customers’ trucks on the road and ensuring fast and efficient service processes when unexpected issues occur.
The API integration enables Werner’s team to address repair events in an average of two calls compared to a previous average of 12 calls per event.
The integration results in a ten-minute reduction in time per event call.
The integration reduces associated data entry.
Streamlined service operations offer improved repair velocity, allowing Werner’s professional drivers to get back on the road faster with a reduced dwell time of 30 percent on average.

Chief Information Officer Daragh Mahon stated that continuously improving productivity and efficiency is imperative and collaborating with industry experts on effective APIs helps achieve those goals.
These APIs help support faster cloud adoption across Werner's organization and allow investment in building proprietary technology that benefits drivers, shippers, carriers, and associates.
Werner Enterprises delivers truckload transportation and logistics services to customers across the United States, Mexico, and Canada.
Werner had 2020 revenues of $2.4 billion.
Werner operates an industry-leading modern truck and trailer fleet with over 13,000 associates.
Werner provides Dedicated and One-Way Truckload services as well as Logistics services including truckload brokerage, freight management, intermodal, and final mile.
Daimler Trucks North America LLC is headquartered in Portland, Oregon, and is the leading heavy-duty truck manufacturer in North America.
DTNA produces and markets commercial vehicles under the Freightliner, Western Star, and Thomas Built Buses nameplates.
DTNA is a Daimler company, the world’s leading commercial vehicle manufacturer.

source_title: Werner Enterprises, Inc. - Werner Collaborates With Daimler Trucks North America to Maximize Driver Uptime

facts: Daimler Trucks North America (DTNA) announced executive appointments to its Operating Committee on May 9, 2017.
Jeff Allen assumed the position of DTNA’s senior vice president of Production, Quality and Specialty Vehicles effective August 1, 2017.
Stefan Kurschner will lead DTNA’s Aftermarket business units as senior vice president effective July 1, 2017.
Roger Nielsen serves as president and CEO of Daimler Trucks North America.
Jeff Allen currently serves as vice president of operations at Detroit Diesel Corporation (DDC) in Redford, Michigan.
Jeff Allen has over twenty years of leadership and operational responsibility at Daimler Trucks.
Jeff Allen previously led Detroit’s Powertrain Headquarters in Michigan and Detroit’s Axle Plant in Saltillo, Mexico.
Roger Nielsen stated that Jeff Allen is the perfect candidate to lead DTNA’s manufacturing network, Freightliner Custom Chassis Corporation and Thomas Built Buses.
Jeff Allen previously served as vice president of Operations for Thomas Built Buses in Highpoint, NC from 2004 through 2011.
Jeff Allen started his career at DDC in 1992 as a Business Unit Manager.
Jeff Allen accepted an assignment in Brazil with Detroit Diesel Motores do Brazil in 1997.
Jeff Allen returned to Detroit as Director of Assembly from 2000 through 2004.
Jeff Allen will be located in Fort Mill, South Carolina.
Stefan Kurschner currently serves as president and CEO of Daimler Vehículos Comerciales Mexico.
Stefan Kurschner brings over twenty-five years of global Daimler experience, including management positions in Germany, Argentina, Brazil, Spain, USA and Mexico.
Stefan Kurschner has led Daimler Trucks Mexico since September 1, 2013.
DTNA achieved 36.2% of the Mexican Class 8 market in 2016 under Stefan Kurschner’s leadership.
Roger Nielsen stated that Stefan transformed the Mexican market for DTNA over the last four years and revitalized the Daimler Trucks Mexico workforce and Dealer Network.
Stefan Kurschner previously served as president of Detroit Diesel Remanufacturing LLC until August 2013.
In his new role, Stefan Kurschner will lead DTNA’s aftermarket functions including Central Service, Parts Sales & Marketing, Parts Distribution, Dealer Operations, and Remanufacturing.
Stefan Kurschner will be located at the company’s headquarters in Portland, Oregon.
Daimler Trucks North America LLC is headquartered in Portland, Ore.
Daimler Trucks North America is the leading heavy-duty truck manufacturer in North America.
Daimler Trucks North America produces and markets commercial vehicles under the Freightliner, Western Star and Thomas Built Buses nameplates.
Daimler Trucks North America is a Daimler company, the world’s leading commercial vehicle manufacturer.
source_title: Daimler Trucks North America Announces New Executive Appointments to its Operating Committee - Thomas Built Buses

facts: Decostar Industries, a manufacturing division of automotive parts supplier Magna Exteriors and Interiors, will expand its presence in Carroll County.
The expansion will create up to 35 new jobs.
The project includes a $2 million capital investment.
Decostar has leased the 100,000-square-foot Janus International facility in Temple.
The 60 jobs of employees at the former Chiquita facility in Carrollton will be retained.
Decostar currently operates four satellite facilities supporting Mercedes-Benz, BMW, Nissan, and Volkswagen with molded and painted exterior parts.
The new Temple facility will supply assembled components to Nissan, Kia, and other customer assembly lines.
The facility will also supply past-model service parts to distribution centers throughout the world.
New contracts secured by Decostar made the capital investment and headcount increase possible.
Joe Pittel, President of Magna Exteriors and Interiors, stated the company is pleased to bring additional investments and jobs to Carroll County.
Temple Mayor Rick Ford welcomed Decostar to the community and noted the project will have a tremendous economic impact through financial investment and job creation.
Daniel Jackson, President and CEO of Carroll Tomorrow, described the expansion as an example of the community's proactive approach toward existing industries for job creation.
EJane Caraway, senior project manager on the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Existing Industry Team, managed the project for the state.
source_title: Decostar to expand in Temple: Move brings 35 jobs, $2 million investment | Villa Rican | times-georgian.com

facts: DENSO celebrated the opening of its new thermal assembly plant and warehouse facility in Urbandale, Iowa.
The new facility is named DENSO Manufacturing Michigan Iowa Plant (DMIA).
The facility represents a $500,000 investment and created seven new jobs.
The company expects to expand the workforce to 20 employees and nearly double its production within two years.
The Urbandale facility assembles cooling modules for the agricultural and construction industries.
The plant handles pass-through products, including radiator assemblies and cooling modules for the construction, agriculture, and electric power industries.
The plant localizes products that were previously assembled in the United Kingdom for U.S. customers.

This opening is part of DENSO’s recent expansion initiative to invest nearly $2 billion in North America, including more than $750 million in the United States, to strengthen and expand product development and manufacturing capabilities across the region.
DENSO executives and local city officials celebrated DMIA’s grand opening with a ribbon-cutting ceremony.

Attendees included Urbandale Mayor Bob Andeweg, DENSO International America President and CEO Howard Sugi, DENSO Manufacturing Michigan President John Kumagai, DENSO Marston President David Hanson (U.K.), and DMIA Plant Manager Paolo Vercelli.
Howard Sugi stated that while the facility extends existing thermal operations in Michigan and the U.K., it is very much an Iowa facility.
DENSO has operated in Iowa since 1974, when it opened its first facility in Cedar Falls to support heavy-duty, off-highway engine management and others.
Sugi expressed excitement about opportunities for the new site and DENSO’s heavy-duty business in Iowa in the years ahead.

Paolo Vercelli credited the strong support of the Greater Des Moines Partnership, the city of Urbandale, the Urbandale Development Association, and the Urbandale Chamber of Commerce for making the opportunity possible.
DENSO’s motto is “Protecting lives, preserving the planet and preparing a bright future for generations to come.”
source_title: DENSO Manufacturing Michigan Iowa Plant Celebrates Opening Of Urbandale Facility

DENSO's solutions aim to help people feel peace-of-mind in their everyday lives whenever and wherever they are.
DENSO has a strong focus on individual growth development and advancement within its careers approach.

facts: DENSO is ramping up production of stop/start starters at its DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee (DMTN) plant in Maryville, Tenn.
The new stop/start starters are due to hit U.S. roads later this year.
The stop/start technology improves fuel efficiency by cutting the engine when the vehicle is at a stop or in other idle traffic situations.
The new production line will result in approximately 75 positions over the next two years, according to DENSO.
U.S. automakers are adopting stop/start technology to meet upcoming fuel requirements.
Stop/start systems reduce fuel consumption and CO2 emissions by stopping the engine when the vehicle is idling at a stop light or in other traffic situations.
Depending on automaker specifications, the technology can help improve fuel efficiency by approximately 3 to 5 percent.
DENSO has future stop/start system technology that will improve fuel efficiency by more than 7 percent depending on the automaker’s overall systems approach.
source_title: DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee Ramps Up Production for Stop/Start Starters - AftermarketNews

facts: DENSO Manufacturing Michigan, Inc. (DMMI) is a leading producer of automotive thermal products.
The company specializes in air conditioning and engine cooling systems for North American automakers.
DMMI operates from its expansive main facility in Battle Creek, Michigan.
The company is likely advancing its manufacturing automation and sustainability initiatives.
DMMI is expanding its product portfolio to support electric and hybrid vehicles in response to evolving automotive industry trends.
The company is implementing advanced automation and robotics in its manufacturing processes.
DMMI is making strategic efforts to expand market presence and reach new customer segments.
The company is implementing advanced technologies to improve service delivery and customer experience.
DMMI manufactures a comprehensive range of thermal products, including evaporators, condensers, radiators, heaters, and HVAC systems, primarily for the automotive industry.
The company’s main facility in Battle Creek, Michigan, is supported by satellite locations in Alabama and Iowa.
These satellite locations enable efficient distribution and assembly for diverse markets.
DMMI is a wholly owned subsidiary of DENSO, a global automotive parts leader.
The company provides operational support to other DENSO thermal plants across North America.
DMMI's products are integral to vehicles from leading brands such as Toyota, Ford, GM, and Chrysler.
The company was founded in 1984.
DMMI operates as a private company.

facts: The Building 701 Plant Expansion project in Athens, TN doubled the existing facility from 180,000 SF to over 400,000 SF.
The expanded plant manufactures a patented direct fuel injector system for automobiles.
The project includes a new 2400 ton chiller plant.
The project includes steam boilers for humidification.
The project includes hot water boilers for heating.
The project includes process water cooling for four kw centrifugal air compressors.
The project features a new web based DDC control system designed to integrate and upgrade existing controls serving the entire campus and process utilities.
ICT provided mechanical, electrical, plumbing and fire protection design for the new facility.
Denso Manufacturing Tennessee, Inc. serves as both the owner and client of the project.
The project falls under the manufacturing category.

facts: Dongwon Autopart Technology will invest $30 million in a new automotive manufacturing facility in Emanuel County, Georgia.
The new production facility is located at the Highway 297 Industrial Park near Swainsboro.
The project is expected to create 200 new jobs over the next several years.
Hiring for management, system leads, and production personnel at the new facility is expected to begin in 2026.
This initiative marks the largest economic development project for Emanuel County in more than 30 years.
Dongwon Autopart Technology was established in 1971 and supplies automotive components including door, seat, and battery frames for original equipment manufacturers.
The company has operated in the United States since 2003 and currently supports more than 500 direct jobs in the Southeast.
Dongwon Autopart Technology's existing facilities in Hogansville will remain operational.
Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the investment and job creation plans on Wednesday.

Heather Hollstein, human resources manager at Dongwon Autopart Technology, stated the company is proud to be part of growing the state's automotive industry and remains committed to supplying high-quality products to internal and external customers.
State Representative Butch Parrish, chairman of the House Rules Committee, highlighted the project's focus on bringing high-quality jobs to rural communities in southeast Georgia.
Will Donaldson, chairman of the Emanuel County Development Authority, expressed community excitement over the economic development payoff for the area.
Ken Warnock, CEO of the Emanuel County Development Authority, noted his team worked closely with company leadership on the site selection process.
Commissioner Pat Wilson of the Georgia Department of Economic Development stated that the automotive industry is expanding outward along the Interstate 16 corridor toward Georgia's ports.

The project was a collaborative effort involving the Georgia Department of Economic Development Global Commerce team, Emanuel County Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
Alyce Thornhill, director of project implementation and supplier strategy for the Georgia Department of Economic Development Global Commerce team, represented the state on the project.
source_title: New auto production facility bringing 200 new jobs to Georgia

facts: Dongwon Autopart Technology plans to invest $30 million in a new production facility in Emanuel County.

Established in 1971, Dongwon Autopart Technology supplies components such as doorframes, seat frames, and battery frames to automotive original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).

Heather Hollstein, Human Resources Manager at Dongwon Autopart Technology, stated the company is proud to be part of growing the state’s automotive industry and thanked Emanuel County for welcoming them.
Representative Butch Parrish, Chairman of the House Rules Committee, expressed pride in Emanuel County’s work alongside state partners to deliver jobs to Georgians in the region.
Emanuel County Development Authority Chairman Will Donaldson welcomed Dongwon Autopart Technology and noted that their investment in economic development has paid off for the community.

Ken Warnock, CEO of the Emanuel County Development Authority, stated the new state-of-the-art manufacturing facility will be the single largest new economic development project for Emanuel County in over 30 years.

Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy at the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce team, represented the agency on this competitive project in partnership with the Emanuel County Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that the automotive industry is expanding outward along the I-16 corridor as suppliers choose strategic locations with access to OEMs and Georgia’s ports, calling Dongwon Autopart Technology’s second Georgia location a testament to the state’s robust automotive growth.

Manufacturers can exploit opportunities for optimization and added-value manufacturing by instrumenting a mold with cavity pressure sensors and process monitoring equipment.
EVCO Plastics is a globally recognized leader in injection molding and many additional technologies.
EVCO Plastics is a family-run business founded in Wisconsin in 1964.
EVCO Plastics has a workforce of over 1,800 employees spread across eleven facilities in the US, China, and Mexico.
Anna Bartz serves as the Director of Communications at EVCO Plastics.

Following some recent growth, EVCO Plastics now operates more than 260 molding machines.
EVCO Plastics offers excellence in injection molding characterized by state-of-the-art technology, close cooperation, tight tolerances, and high quality demand.
EVCO Plastics provides technologies and services across the entire value chain, such as automation, packaging, labelling, and more.
Precision Molding is one of EVCO's four main divisions.
EVCO's products and solutions cover diverse industries ranging from agriculture and powersports to packaging and consumer goods.

Two EVCO sites are equipped with an ISO Class 8 Clean Room: the headquarters in Deforest, Wisconsin, and the Chinese production facility in Dongguan.

facts: Hühoco Group announced plans to build a new greenfield facility in Cartersville, Bartow County, Georgia on January 26, 2017.
The company will invest $24 million in the new facility and community.
The project is expected to create 200 jobs total.
Hühoco will add 100 new jobs to the new facility over the next three years.
Hühoco currently operates a facility in Mableton, Georgia under the name First American Resources.
The Mableton facility employs around 100 people.
First American Resources specializes in decorative and functional coating systems.
The company's two primary markets are domestic residential building products and automotive.
Hühoco Group worked with Atlanta-based site selection firm Pegasus Partners for the project.

The Cartersville-Bartow County Department of Economic Development partnered with Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) Director of International Investment Nico Wijnberg and Senior Project Manager Walt Farrell.
Georgia Power was also a partner in the development process.

Hühoco Group's product portfolio includes cold strips, paints for coating processes, colour concentrates for plastics, coil coating, lancing and stamping processes, and parts manufactured on multiple Bihler presses.
The company has expanded its portfolio to include noise and damping materials (Anti-Noise Technology) for coated metal strips.
Hühoco Group products are utilized in more than 30 industrial sectors globally, including automotive, human and veterinary medicine, and electronics.
Cartersville Mayor Matt Santini welcomed First American Resources to the community and praised Georgia as a great place for business.
Hühoco CEO Christoph Koslowski stated the company is renewing its commitment to Georgia by adding jobs and investing in an entirely new facility.
Pegasus Partners' Benedikt Ibing emphasized that workforce priorities were central to the site selection process.

facts: A planned nearly $2.6 billion lithium-ion battery plant will not be coming to Coweta County after all.
FREYR Battery plans to exercise its option to pull out of the planned $2.57 billion plant.
The canceled plant would have brought 723 new jobs to Georgia.
The company exited a deal that was originally announced in November 2022.
In a letter to the Coweta County Development Authority dated Jan. 21, FREYR Battery stated the decision was made because it realigned its near-term strategic goals.
The company acknowledged that the Coweta County Development Authority, Coweta County, and the State of Georgia provided extraordinary assistance and support.
The company stated the decision does not reflect concerns about Georgia's support, calling it a great state to do business and noting they would remain advocates based on positive engagements.
FREYR Battery is a Norwegian-based clean energy solutions provider that was seeking to capture more of the U.S. market within the next 10 years, with the Coweta County factory playing a key role.
The company cited rising interest rates, declining battery prices, and changes in corporate leadership as reasons for canceling the plant.
source_title: Plans for nearly $2.6B FREYR Battery plant in Coweta County nixed | FOX 5 Atlanta

facts: FREYR Battery, Inc. is a Luxembourg-headquartered energy solutions company focused on developing and manufacturing clean, next-generation battery cell production capacity.
The company's mission is to accelerate the decarbonization of global energy and transportation systems.
It historically served key markets including energy storage systems (ESS), commercial mobility, and the passenger electric vehicle (EV) segment.
The company operated a Customer Qualification Plant (CQP) in Mo i Rana, Norway.
FREYR advanced its Giga America battery manufacturing project in Coweta County, Georgia.
In December 2024, FREYR completed an acquisition of Trina Solar's U.S. manufacturing assets.
Following the acquisition, the company repositioned itself as a vertically integrated solar and battery storage supplier with an integrated U.S. supply chain.
Effective February 19, 2025, the company rebranded as T1 Energy Inc.
The company's common stock and warrants transitioned to ticker symbols "TE" and "TE WS" on the New York Stock Exchange as of March 3, 2025.
The FREY-WT ticker is no longer active.
At the time of reporting, the company had a market cap of $241.82M and reported 0 fulltime employees.
source_title: FREYR Battery, Inc. WT Stock Price: Quote, Forecast, Splits & News (FREY-WT)

facts: Freyr Battery, a Norwegian battery company, has announced the acquisition of a site in Georgia, US, to build its planned Giga America battery plant.
The company selected the Bridgeport Industrial Park site in Coweta County.

The site was chosen following a rigorous evaluation process based on important operational, logistical, and financial criteria, including the suitability of the site to co-locate modular upstream and downstream projects with the Giga America cell manufacturing facility.
Freyr plans to commence detailed project engineering of Giga America in the months to come.

The company plans the initial phase of the plant to be a cell production module of about 34GWh based on the next-generation of 24M’s SemiSolid production platform at an initial estimated cost of $1.7bn.
The SemiSolid platform enables capital and energy efficient production of lithium-ion batteries at scale.
Freyr is also analysing value accretive upstream and downstream modules besides further cell production lines that are expected to take the total investments to over $2.6bn through 2029.
Following completion of all the construction phases, the Giga America complex is expected to become one of the largest battery cell manufacturing facilities in the world.

Freyr co-founder and CEO Tom Einar Jensen said: “Today’s landmark announcement underscores FREYR’s ambition to develop a very strong and near-term operational footprint in the United States. Expanding into the U.S. has been a foundational aspect of FREYR’s long-term strategy from our inception, and with the recent passage of the Inflation Reduction Act, we expect U.S. demand for ESS, passenger EV and other electric mobility applications to grow rapidly over the next decade,” commented.
Freyr is currently constructing Giga Arctic at Mo i Rana, Norway.
The company is getting financial assistance from the state of Georgia and Coweta County besides the expected production tax credits related to the recently passed Inflation Reduction Act.
Furthermore, Freyr plans to apply with the US Department of Energy for packages that could include a grant and/or direct loans to help in the development of Giga America.

Jensen added: “At FREYR, we are deeply committed to decarbonizing the transportation and energy sectors. As we advance our U.S. expansion plan in cooperation with our key stakeholders, we expect to make meaningful investments to spur job creation and the eventual development of localized, decarbonised supply chains in the U.S. to enhance energy security and economic activity and meet the growing demand for energy storage solutions in the US market.”
Currently, Freyr is analysing clean power supply solutions for the Giga America project with independent power producers.
source_title: Freyr Battery acquires site to build battery plant in Georgia, US - World Construction Network

facts: Norway-based Freyr Battery SA stock price charged 33.19% higher the week ended June 30.
The company assembled and successfully charged its first semi-automated lithium-ion battery unit cells at a Norwegian facility.
CEO Tom Einar Jensen said the successful tests pave the way for the company to ramp up process automation, battery cell production, and testing of cells it produced.
The company anticipates supplying customers with samples in the second half of this year.
Jensen noted an “increasingly robust pipeline of customer opportunities and use cases for our products.”
Shares gapped up and closed 20.26% higher following that announcement.
Freyr Battery SA presented its first “capital markets” day at the New York Stock Exchange as a pitch to attract further investment.
The stock bolted another 3% higher on June 30 after analysts and investors heard the company’s presentation.
Weekly trading volume was triple the average.
Jensen stated that the company is moving toward a totally automated process, which will increase production speed and yields.
Battery applications include transportation, heating, and industrial processes transitioning from fossil fuel-based systems to electric-powered systems.
The company has yet to show any revenue, as its operations are financed through post-IPO offerings, additional rounds of private equity, and grants from Norwegian governmental entities.
Analysts don’t expect the company to be profitable either this year or next.
In his NYSE presentation, Jenner mentioned the company’s planned U.S. gigafactory that will be built in Coweta County, Georgia.
The company is also exploring a potential gigafactory site in Vaasa, Finland.
Batteries produced at the U.S. site will qualify for subsidies under the Inflation Reduction Act.
Investor and miner Glencore plc (OTCMKTS: GLNCY) is a financial backer of Freyr and part of the consortium to produce batteries and bring them to market.
Nidec Corp. (OTCMKTS: NJDCY), Siemens AG (OTCMKTS: SIEGY), and Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE: CAT) are also partners in the consortium.
Freyr Battery SA went public in February 2021 via a SPAC merger.
The company ended its first quarter with $475 million in cash.

2023 capital expenditures are focused on three main projects: a customer qualification plant in Norway to verify product suitability, the Georgia Giga America plant, and maintaining progress at its Giga Arctic facility in Norway.
The Giga Arctic facility will be the company’s first full-scale battery manufacturing facility.
With no earnings and no revenue, and losses forecast into the foreseeable future, Freyr is a poster child for a speculative stock.
Freyr Battery SA could easily become an acquisition target of any number of companies needing batteries.
Potential acquisition suitors could include global automakers, utilities, or big industrial companies.
source_title: Freyr Battery Powers Up After Successful Tests, NYSE Presentation | Nasdaq

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp, in conjunction with the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), announced that Georgia sustained momentum through economic development projects in key industries during the first half of Fiscal Year 2023 (July 1 through December 31, 2022).
The 218 locations or expansions supported by GDEcD’s Global Commerce division are expected to create approximately 17,500 new jobs.
These projects represent more than $13 billion in investment for the state.
Both the number of locations and total investment amount increased over the same period last year.
Areas outside the 10-county metro Atlanta region accounted for 85 percent of jobs created and 92 percent of investments from economic development projects during the period.
Four of Georgia’s largest industries – advanced manufacturing, automotive, aerospace, and food processing – accounted for nearly 75 percent of new jobs and 84 percent of investments.
Planned investments resulting from these projects increased by more than 38 percent compared to the same time last year.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson highlighted proven solutions such as the job training program Georgia Quick Start and GRAD certified sites that support accelerated project timelines.
The automotive industry was the second-largest source of new jobs and investment, after advanced manufacturing.

In October, state and local officials joined company representatives, including Korean Ambassador to the U.S. Tae-yong Cho, for the groundbreaking of the new Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) EV facility.
The HMGMA EV facility will support more than 8,100 direct jobs in Bryan, Bulloch, Chatham and Effingham Counties.
Three off-site HMGMA suppliers announced locations following HMGMA’s groundbreaking.
These supplier additions swiftly surpassed the initial $1 billion in investment predicted to come from them.
International companies accounted for 23 percent of state projects since July 1, 2022.
Historic project examples include the partnership between SK On and Hyundai Motor Group in Bartow County.
Next-generation developer FREYR Battery announced a Coweta County facility.
Both the SK On/Hyundai partnership and the FREYR Battery facility are in the top five largest projects by investment in the state’s history.
Battery recyclers Sung-Il Hitech and Denkai America joined Georgia’s growing electric mobility ecosystem.
The aerospace and defense industries continued to benefit from Georgia’s logistics infrastructure and engineering talent pool, creating more than 1,500 new jobs.
Archer Aviation Inc., an aerospace company advancing sustainable urban air mobility, announced a facility near airports.
Anduril Industries, a defense technology company, announced a facility near airports.
Both companies will benefit from nearby top-ranked technical colleges and four-year universities.
Major investments or expansions announced thus far in FY 2023 include YKK AP in Macon.
Beretta Holding S.A. announced a new facility in Bryan County through its subsidiary Norma Precision Inc.
The advanced manufacturing sector has announced more than 7,700 new jobs in the state since July 1, 2022.
Germany-based Haering Precision celebrated the grand opening of its Lavonia facility in November with a two-day event.
The Haering Precision event included a “Future Day” for approximately 400 local high school students and members of the community.
Companies in the food processing industry announced more than $695 million in investments, largely through expansions.
King’s Hawaiian®, a Georgia partner for more than a decade, announced another expansion of its Oakwood facility.
This marks King's Hawaiian's second expansion in less than a year.

GDEcD Deputy Commissioner for Global Commerce Kristi Brigman noted that working with the Georgia Quick Start program and letting students see jobs in-person helps companies build talent from the ground up.
These programs help Georgians upskill, including those part of Georgia’s strong inbound domestic migration trends.
Two industries with rapidly growing footprints in Georgia -- life sciences and film -- welcomed additional facilities that will support industry breakthroughs and learning opportunities for students.
Georgia Tech hosted a groundbreaking for its Science Square purpose-built district in August.
The Rowen Foundation’s 2,000-acre science and learning campus broke ground in early December.
Athena Studios announced it will add an additional 350,000 square feet to its Athens facility.
The Athena Studios facility includes a structure dedicated to training the local workforce through a partnership with the University of Georgia and Georgia Film Academy.
At the former Fort Gillem outside of Atlanta, BlueStar Studios announced more than 600,000 square feet of new stage space.

BlueStar Studios will also add an additional 45,000 square feet dedicated to technical resources and amenities, which will include refurbished buildings that preserve the base’s historic architecture.

facts: FREYR Battery is a developer of clean, next-generation battery cell production capacity.

The company appointed Todd Kantor, Tore Ivar Slettemoen, and David Manners to its Board of Directors effective immediately to fill vacancies and strengthen the board for growth and capital formation initiatives.

Jason Forcier and Mimi Berdal notified the Board of their intentions not to stand for re-election as Board directors on April 14, 2024, and April 15, 2024, respectively, and subsequently resigned effective immediately.
Todd Kantor noted that FREYR has a cash balance of $276 million at year end 2023 and ownership of a strategic site in Georgia for the Giga America project.
The company is commencing development of the Giga America battery manufacturing project in Coweta County, Georgia, in the U.S.
FREYR Battery's mission is to accelerate the decarbonization of global energy and transportation systems by producing clean, cost-competitive batteries.
The company seeks to serve primary markets of energy storage systems (ESS) and commercial mobility, with an ambition to serve the passenger electric vehicles market (EV).
FREYR is operating its Customer Qualification Plant (CQP) for technology development in Mo I, Rana, Norway.

David Manners commented that development of localized supply chains and production capacity in the Western hemisphere are fundamental to energy security and geostrategic objectives, noting FREYR's vital role as an emerging battery producer.

Tore Ivar Slettemoen developed the concept and founded FREYR Battery and serves as Chairman of Teknovekst AS – Vanir, an institutional investment fund focused on high-growth energy transition investments.
Todd Kantor is the Founder and Managing Member of Encompass Capital Advisors LLC, a cornerstone investor in FREYR.
Mr. Kantor has nearly 25 years of experience in the global energy markets as a portfolio manager, analyst, trader, and investment banker.
Prior to founding Encompass Capital in 2012, Mr. Kantor was a portfolio manager at Citadel LLC’s PioneerPath Capital platform.
David Manners served for 20 years at the U.S. Central Intelligence Agency, including multiple tours abroad and serving as the CIA’s Chief of Station on two occasions.
Tom Einar Jensen is Co-Founder and Executive Chairperson of FREYR.

facts: The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company is one of the world's largest tire companies.
The company employs about 62,000 people and manufactures its products.
The company is seeking an Automotive Technician to join their team in Gadsden, AL.
In this role, you will inspect, diagnose, and repair automotive systems including brakes, suspension, alignment, engine performance, air conditioning, and coolant systems.
You will troubleshoot problems and demonstrate strong diagnostic skills using the latest technology.
You will mentor teammates and continually learn new techniques as automotive technology evolves.
You will maintain high standards of safety, service, and housekeeping while using protective equipment.
The role requires performing manual tasks such as lifting tires/wheels and standing, bending, and squatting for extended periods.
Required qualifications include a minimum of three years' automotive repair experience or three years as a military vehicle maintenance specialist.
Candidates must demonstrate advanced diagnostic and problem-solving skills, including identifying, troubleshooting, and resolving complex mechanical and electrical faults.
A valid driver's license and ability to work a flexible schedule, including some evenings, weekends, and holidays, are required.
Preferred qualifications include ASE A4 (Steering & Suspension) and A5 (Brakes) Certifications, a state inspection license, and MAC certification.
Benefits include a 401(k) with a 2% company match.
The company provides company-paid life insurance and optional short-/long-term disability coverage.
Tuition reimbursement and adoption assistance are offered to help employees' families grow.
Medical, dental, and vision insurance start 31 days after hire.
Benefits include Healthy Choice incentives, mental-health counseling with 6 free EAP sessions, and wellness discounts.
The company offers flexible scheduling and supportive leaders who understand the demands of family life.
Goodyear has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, though it does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.
Data from the US Department of Labor shows trends in total sponsorships: 6 in 2020, 14 in 2021, 33 in 2022, 10 in 2023, 16 in 2024, 29 in 2025, and 2 projected for 2026.

The distribution of different job fields receiving sponsorship shows that 84% represents a job field similar to this job (Engineering and Development, Management and Executive, Sales, Customer Service and Support).

facts: Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company has unveiled tires that have a substantially increased amount of sustainable materials used in their production.
The new tires are made of 70% sustainable materials.
Senior Vice President of Global Operations and Chief Technology Officer Chris Helsel stated this is a significant improvement from current tires.
Normal tires sold today might have up to a 30% sustainable content, predominantly due to natural rubber.
In 2020, Goodyear set the goal of creating a tire made 100% with sustainable materials.
The company analyzed traditional tire materials like petroleum, silica, and carbon black to find eco-friendly alternatives or sustainable sourcing methods.
Carbon black is essential for vulcanization, allowing rubber to stiffen and withstand friction.
Goodyear sources four types of carbon black from methane, carbon dioxide, plant-based oils, and oils pulled from recycled tires via pyrolysis.
These carbon black technologies target reduced carbon emissions, circularity, and the use of bio-based carbons while maintaining performance.
The company harvests silica from rice husk waste residue due to its high silica content.
Goodyear uses soybean oil in place of petroleum in its sustainable tires.
Soybean oil provides better low-temperature performance and maintains rubber pliability across a broader temperature range.
Polyester cords in the tire are made with recycled plastic bottles.
Traction material is made with pine tree resin.
The new sustainable tires last just as long as traditional ones and meet European standards on fuel economy, traction, noise, and tread.
Goodyear is using a marketing strategy asking interested buyers to register for updates online, working similarly to a presale to gauge demand before market release.
Goodyear debuted a demonstration tire made with 90% sustainable material.
The 90% sustainable tire will not be on the market for a few years due to limited material supply.
The company hopes the 70% sustainable tire will increase interest, motivate investors to fund sustainable tires, and increase material supply.
Chris Helsel encouraged motorists to maintain their current tires to improve fuel economy and tire lifespan, noting that proper maintenance is also a form of sustainability.
source_title: Goodyear ready to roll out a more environmentally friendly tire | Ideastream Public Media

facts: Goodyear Tire & Rubber Co. is locating a 1.2 million-square-foot distribution center on U.S. Highway 80 in Forney.
The city will rezone 102 acres for the project, which will be built on agricultural land at Gateway Boulevard.
The project will be part of Dallas-based Petro-Hunt's 2,000-acre Gateway mixed-use development, which also includes homes and apartments.
Hillwood, one of North Texas' largest industrial builders, is developing the project.
Hillwood vice president Todd Finely stated that Hillwood will own the building and Goodyear will lease it from them.
The center will employ 160 people.
City Manager Tony Carson expressed excitement about welcoming one of the nation's most recognizable brands to Forney, noting its evolving role in commerce and innovation in distribution.
Several Forney residents spoke against the project, citing concerns about traffic, pollution and crime.
Warren Ketteman, Forney's executive director of economic development, said the city worked on the warehouse deal for several months under the code name "Project Fireball."
The property tax impact of the project to the city is equivalent to over half the impact of the 714 residential permits issued last year.
Goodyear already has a shipping hub east of Forney, with a current much smaller facility in Terrell that will be moved to this new facility as an expansion bringing more jobs.
Goodyear has $15 billion in annual revenue and ranked 187th on the Fortune 500 list.
The Akron, Ohio-based tiremaker traces its roots to 1839.
source_title: Massive Goodyear distribution center will bring 160 jobs to Forney's Gateway
