facts: Great Dane was recognized by the Georgia General Assembly in commemoration of the company’s 125th anniversary.
Proclamations honoring Great Dane were read in both the House and Senate chambers to mark the milestone.
Governor Brian Kemp issued a special commendation to the company.
The recognition highlights Great Dane’s enduring history of innovation, quality, and leadership in the transportation industry.
The House proclamation was sponsored by State Rep. Ron Stephens from Savannah, GA.
Great Dane was founded in Savannah, GA in 1900.
Rick Mullininx, Great Dane’s President and COO, accepted the House proclamation on behalf of the company during the House session.
Rick Mullininx stated that the commendations are a testament to the hard work, dedication, and innovation of the team throughout each decade.
Rick Mullininx expressed pride in the company's story intertwining with America’s story of ingenuity and looked forward to continuing to lead the industry for generations.
The Senate proclamation was sponsored by State Sen. Ben Watson from Savannah.
Chris Hammond, Great Dane’s Executive Vice President of Sales, accepted the Senate proclamation during the Senate session.
Chris Hammond stated that Great Dane’s 125 years of success reflects the resilience of its teams and the trust built with customers and partners across generations and decades.
Chris Hammond noted that the recognition underscores the company's focus on the future, enabled by technology and customer relationships.
source_title: Great Dane Honored by the Georgia General Assembly for 125 Years of Innovation | Desi Trucking

facts: Great Dane Trailers officially broke ground on the site of its new refrigerated trailer manufacturing plant in Statesboro, Ga.
The groundbreaking ceremony was attended by more than 200 attendees, including local business representatives, state and local political leaders, and company executives.

Georgia Gov. Sonny Perdue, Statesboro Mayor Joe Brannen, and Bulloch County Commission Chairman Garrett Nevil hailed the cooperation among the state, local economic developers, and Great Dane as key reasons for the success of the site selection process.
Company executives were particularly drawn to the location just northwest of Savannah because it remained close to where Great Dane was founded in 1900.

Bill Crown, president and CEO of CC Industries and CEO of Great Dane Limited Partnership, stated that the site selection allowed the company to continue its legacy by keeping operations close to its deep roots in Savannah.
The selected site covers 118 acres.
The plant will encompass 450,000 square feet, including office and showroom space.
The facility will have the capacity to produce more than 5,000 trailers annually.
The plant will employ more than 400 people.
Phill Pines, president and COO of Great Dane Trailers, said the refrigerated trailers that will be built there will be complex and detailed, emphasizing the importance of this to the local workforce.
The new reefer plant will feature the latest technology, making it among the most modern, efficient plants in the world.
Construction is expected to get underway later in 2010.
The plant is scheduled to bring production online in 2012.
Great Dane manufactures dry van, refrigerated, and platform trailers.
The company has division offices in Savannah, Ga., and Chicago, Ill., with manufacturing plants strategically located throughout the United States.
Great Dane utilizes a network of company-owned branches and full-line independent dealers as well as parts-only independent dealers, with distribution points across North and South America.
source_title: Great Dane Trailers Breaks Ground on New Southeast Plant - AftermarketNews

facts: Great Dane Trailers has announced the addition of Superstition Trailers to its North American distribution network.
Superstition Trailers is headquartered in Phoenix, Ariz.
Superstition Trailers operates two locations in Tucson and two locations in Phoenix.
Superstition Trailers grew from a single location in 1994 to become the region's top distributor of trailers, parts, and service.
Superstition Trailers employs more than 125 staff members.
Superstition Trailers recently completed the renovation of a 43,000-square-foot facility in Phoenix.
Superstition's service department is fully equipped to handle all types of trailer repair, fabrication, and installation.
Superstition's service department offers certified brake and federal inspection as well as mobile repair service.
The name "Superstition" was adopted from the Superstition mountain range located east of the Phoenix metro area.
Great Dane Trailers manufactures dry van, refrigerated, and platform trailers.
Great Dane Trailers has headquarters in Savannah, Ga., and Chicago, Ill.
Great Dane Trailers operates nine manufacturing plants in the United States.
source_title: Great Dane Trailers Adds 4 Arizona Locations | Heavy Duty Trucking

facts: Great Dane Trailers announced the reorganization of its management structure.
The changes are part of an ongoing process aimed at enhancing leadership, business fundamentals, and accelerating future growth.
Bill Crown serves as President and Chief Executive Officer of Great Dane Trailers.
Phill Pines is retiring from his role as President of Great Dane.
Jim Pines holds the title of Executive Vice President, Sales and Operations.
Jim Pines will continue to oversee all Sales activities, including Factory Direct Sales, Retail Sales, Aftermarket Parts and Service, and Used Trailers.
Jim Pines will also oversee Great Dane’s Manufacturing Operations.
Jim Pines will report directly to Bill Crown.
Rick Mullininx is a 28-year Great Dane veteran promoted to Executive Vice President, Engineering.
Rick Mullininx will report directly to Bill Crown and oversee Great Dane’s entire Engineering and Product Development organization.
Dean Engelage joins the Great Dane team as Executive Vice President, Strategic Planning and Administration.
Dean Engelage previously spent more than 15 years with CC Industries, which is Great Dane’s parent company.
Dean Engelage will direct Business Development, Marketing, Management Information Systems, Human Resources, and Johnson Refrigerated Truck Bodies.
Dean Engelage will report directly to Bill Crown.
Jim Petrarca serves as Chief Financial Officer overseeing all of the Company’s Finance and Accounting activities.
Jim Petrarca will report directly to Bill Crown.
The Executive Committee responsible to the Board for all Operations and Planning at Great Dane Trailers comprises Jim Pines, Rick Mullininx, Dean Engelage, Jim Petrarca, Sam Gupta, and Bill Crown.
Sam Gupta holds the title of Senior Vice President, Manufacturing.
The organizational changes are effective immediately.
The announcement was issued from Savannah, GA and Chicago, IL.
source_title: Great Dane Trailers Announces New Organizational Structure | Desi Trucking

facts: More than 1 million Volkswagen electric vehicles (EVs) on the road are equipped with CO2 (R744) heat pumps.
A handful of these EVs with CO2 heat pumps are located in Canada.
Volkswagen Canada has offered the ID.4 model since 2021.
Many ID.4 vehicles sold in Canada have been equipped with a CO2 heat pump.
The CO2 heat pump unit is manufactured by Hanon Systems, a South Korean provider of automotive thermal management systems.
The heat pump unit features a 5.5kW (1.6TR) CO2 compressor.
The optional heat pump costs CA$1,500 (US$1,057/€918).
The ID.4 model is built on the Volkswagen Group’s modular electric drive matrix (MEB) platform.
The MEB platform supports a wide range of EVs including the ID.3, ID.5, Audi Q4 e-tron, Q4 Sportback e-tron, Škoda Enyaq, Enyaq Coupé, Cupra Born, and Tavascan.
These vehicles are all available with an optional R744 heat pump.
Volkswagen sold 4,600 ID.4s in Canada in 2025.
The first ID.4s were manufactured in Germany and imported to Canada.
Since 2022, Volkswagen has built the ID.4 at its plant in Chattanooga, Tennessee.
In April 2026, Volkswagen announced it would end ID.4 production at the Chattanooga facility.
The decision is part of a strategy to prioritize production of what the company called “high-demand vehicles.”
A future version of the ID.4 is planned for the North American market, with further details to be announced later.
Because Volkswagen sells CO2-equipped vehicles in Canada, its dealers are required to have R744 air-conditioning service equipment to maintain them.
In the U.S., Volkswagen dealers are not subject to this requirement as they do not offer EVs with CO2 heat pumps.
Audi dealers in the United States are apparently required to have CO2 service equipment.
Audi sells four EVs in the U.S., none of which are currently offered with CO2 heat pumps.
At the ATMO MAC Summit 2025 × TU Berlin, Volkswagen shared how it is improving the efficiency of its CO2 heat pumps.
Johannes Schwerter, a Test Engineer in Volkswagen’s R&D division, stated that the first-generation system was “over-designed” to account for higher temperatures and pressures CO2 operates at.
The first CO2 compressor weighed 7.6kg [16.8lbs].
A second version of the compressor has been introduced that is a half kilogram [1.1lbs] lighter, bringing the weight to 7.1kg [15.7lbs].
The design of the gas cooler is being examined to address ice building caused by harvesting heat from ambient air.
A vertical rather than horizontal orientation of the gas cooler tubes is being considered to allow melted frost to run off the unit.
Professor Stefan Elbel, Head of the Department of Heat Transfer and Heat Conversion at Technische Universität Berlin, stated that CO2 and propane (R290) are critical for the future of MAC.
The shift to natural refrigerants is driven by environmental necessity and regulatory forces.
In May 2026, Sanden announced it is co-developing a propane-based integrated thermal management system for EVs with a “major” Chinese automaker.
China is expected to lead the adoption of natural refrigerants in EVs, according to S&P Global Mobility.
Vehicle HVAC manufacturer Aurora Netherlands has developed a dual-secondary loop propane heat pump HVAC system for electric buses.
Aurora Netherlands had sold 165 units as of November 2025.
In 2024, Ford released an electric version of its Explorer SUV with an optional CO2 heat pump.
The electric Explorer is also built on Volkswagen’s MEB platform.
source_title: Volkswagen Canada Offers the ID.4 EV with a CO2 Heat Pump

facts: Carlyle Group, Bain Capital and auto supplier Mahle have each submitted preliminary bids to buy a 70% stake in South Korea's Hanon Systems worth an estimated 6.6 trillion won ($5.8 billion).
The combined stake in Hanon has been put up for sale by local private equity firm Hahn & Company and Hankook Tire & Technology Co Ltd.
Hanon Systems said in a regulatory filing that nothing has been decided regarding the potential sale.
A spokesperson for Germany's Mahle declined to comment on the bidding process.
Representatives for Carlyle and Bain did not immediately respond to requests for comment.
France's Valeo had also submitted a preliminary bid but the auto supplier denied that it had.
Hanon Systems is the world's second-largest supplier of auto thermal management systems.
Hanon Systems reported revenue of 6.9 trillion won and operating profit of 315.8 billion won last year.
source_title: Carlyle, Bain submit preliminary bids for $5.8 billion stake in Hanon Systems -newspaper | Euronews

facts: Hanon Systems is a global automotive thermal energy management solutions corporation under the Hankook & Company group.
Vice chairman and CEO Lee Su-il distributed a 2026 new year address to global employees outlining management goals and mid-to-long-term growth strategy.
The company achieved annual sales of 10 trillion won for the first time in its history in 2025.
Hanon Systems targets 11 trillion won in sales for 2026.
A core goal is restoring profitability and achieving an operating margin (EBIT) level of 5%.
The company plans to improve profitability and cash flow simultaneously through cost structure improvement and enhanced operational efficiency.
An execution-focused management policy will be established.
Following the completion of an ongoing paid-in capital increase, the debt ratio is expected to improve from about 246% at the end of the third quarter to about 164%.
This financial improvement is expected to create a more stable environment regarding financial soundness and cash flow.
Hanon Systems created a dedicated software organization over the past year.
The company reorganized its global R&D organization and strengthened the efficiency of technology development and collaboration systems.

Hanon Systems is positioning its technical and software capabilities to respond to automakers' multi-powertrain strategies (including battery electric vehicles, hybrid, plug-in hybrid, and EREV) as a core axis of mid-to-long-term growth.
The company is reviewing new business opportunities based on thermal management technologies and long experience, driven by surging power and thermal management demand from AI data centers.
New business opportunities include liquid cooling systems for data centers and ESS thermal management solutions.
These new businesses are expected to contribute to sales growth and improved profitability in the mid to long term.
Hanon Systems participated in AAPEX, the largest aftermarket exhibition in North America, last November.
The company gained experience at major aftermarket exhibitions in the Middle East.
A global aftermarket dedicated organization was newly established in January 2026.
Centered on this new organization, Hanon Systems plans to expand its aftermarket business to make a tangible contribution to sales growth and improved profitability.

Vice chairman and CEO Lee Su-il stated that 2025 focused on improving management efficiency and strengthening fundamentals, while 2026 will focus on completing that work, executing the mid-to-long-term strategy, and turning it into results.
Lee Su-il emphasized responsible management to strengthen fundamentals and continue sustainable growth through expanding new businesses.
source_title: Hanon Systems targets 11 trillion won sales in 2026, pivots to new businesses - CHOSUNBIZ

facts: Panattoni is developing a new premises in the Kladno-South industrial zone for Hanon Systems Thermal Technologies.
Hanon Systems already operates one hall in this location and is expanding with a new production hall while modernizing the older building to meet stringent sustainability criteria.
Hanon Systems Thermal Technologies issued a tender for the project in February 2022 due to growth in the EV industry and the expansion of existing production for new customers.
Negotiations and project preparation took approximately 14 months before Panattoni started construction work in July this year.
The project consists of two transactions: improving the original hall primarily to make it more energy efficient, and constructing a completely new hall.
Hanon Systems will take over the new hall next summer, at which point production is expected to start.
Production operations in the existing hall will remain uninterrupted during the refurbishment and construction period.
The entire project is designed according to high standards of a modern and sustainable industrial building.
The new hall will include heat pumps, a photovoltaic power plant, and a solution for stormwater management.
The Kladno region is highly valued for its excellent infrastructure, including nearby motorways D7, D6, D5, and Václav Havel International Airport.
A planned modernized railway line connecting Prague, the airport, and Kladno is noted as a great promise for the future.
The Kladno region is highly valued in terms of the quantity and availability of skilled labour.

Pavel Sovička, Panattoni’s Managing Director for the Czech Republic and Slovakia, stated that the construction will contribute to the overall economic recovery of the region and build on the industrial history of Kladno.

Ondřej Rys, Kladno’s architect and councillor for city development, welcomed the effective use and development of existing industrial areas and new job opportunities that complement new housing construction in the city.
source_title: Panattoni developing new premises for Hanon Systems - EuropaProperty.com

facts: Hankook & Co. Group is targeting sales of KRW 30 trillion (€20.283 billion) by 2030 through expanding additional businesses based on next-generation technology beyond the automotive industry.
Hyunbum Cho, chairman of Hankook & Co. Group, confirmed the Korean group’s plan to acquire a majority-shareholding in Hanon Systems.
By acquiring management rights in Hanon Systems, Hankook will become a provider of crucial technology for the electric vehicle era, including TMS (thermal management systems) and tires.
The Korean group expects to further strengthen its business portfolio by working with TMS solutions alongside its tire products.
The €1.1-billion transaction will see Hankook increase its stake in the automotive supplier from an existing 19.49% to 50.53%.
Upon finalisation of the deal, expected by year-end, Hankook's total global assets are forecast to reach around KRW26 trillion.
This asset forecast positions Hankook among South Korea’s top 30 companies.
Hanon Systems is considered the world’s second-largest company in TMS for electric vehicles.
Hanon Systems offers services ranging from complete design to parts supply.
The acquired business is set for high growth through technology advances in TMS areas, including compressors and integrated coolant modules.
Hanon Systems operates 53 production locations across 21 countries.
Hanon Systems reported sales of KRW9.6 trillion last year.
Key clients of Hanon Systems include Hyundai Motor Group, Ford, Volkswagen, BMW, and Mercedes-Benz.

facts: Hitachi Astemo, Ltd. is developing cutting-edge autonomous driving technology to enable vehicles to safely and smoothly navigate and overtake other vehicles on narrow roads.
The technology uses 3D sensing results to perceive the vehicle’s surrounding environment.
Hitachi Astemo is focusing on using new stereo cameras that offer improved cost benefits to gather 3D sensing data, aiming to boost the firm’s cost competitiveness.
Autonomous driving technology aims to address social issues such as traffic congestion and provide transportation for individuals with special needs, including the elderly and the disabled.

Successful deployment of autonomous vehicles on public roads requires anticipating pedestrian behavior risks, circumventing sudden dangers, and planning safe driving trajectories using 3D sensing information.

Astemo’s previous technology primarily focused on preventing collisions through predicting the behavior changes of pedestrians and other road users to enable safe deceleration during autonomous driving on general roads.
The company pioneered a hazard prediction and avoidance system that drives in a manner akin to a human driver by predicting high-risk areas and adjusting speed and trajectory accordingly.
The latest development integrates 3D information from LiDAR sensors to recognize the three-dimensional driving environment around the vehicle.
The technology understands intended movement from detected free spaces, predicts the behavior of oncoming vehicles, and performs route prediction.
This capability enables smooth passing automated driving in narrow roads by coordinating with oncoming vehicles.
The company is collaborating with a newly launched high-precision, versatile stereo camera that can detect pedestrians and bicycles with high accuracy.
The stereo camera measures distances by combining long-range detection with a wide viewing angle.
This technology will be introduced at the “Automotive Engineering Exposition 2023 Yokohama” (booth number: 148) taking place at Pacifico Yokohama starting May 23rd.
Hitachi Astemo continues to strengthen its business portfolio, which includes Powertrain & Safety Systems, Chassis, Motorcycle, Software, and Aftermarket businesses.
The company aims for sustainable growth through “green,” “digital,” and “innovation” pillars.
Hitachi Astemo develops xEV systems and highly efficient internal combustion engine systems that reduce emissions.
The company provides advanced mobility solutions including autonomous driving systems, advanced driver assistance systems, and advanced chassis systems to enhance corporate value for customers.
source_title: Hitachi Astemo Unveils Innovative Autonomous Driving Technology for Narrow Roads - Self Drive News

facts: Hitachi Energy announced a historic $1 billion USD manufacturing investment to power America’s energy future through production of critical grid infrastructure.
Power transformers are described as a linchpin technology for a robust and reliable electric grid and winning the AI race.
Bringing production of large power transformers to the U.S. is critical to building a strong domestic supply chain and reducing production bottlenecks amid surging demand.
A state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in South Boston, Virginia, will be established to produce large power transformers.
The new facility will support applications including high-voltage transmission, power generation, AI data centers, and large-scale industrial applications.
The facility will be built alongside Hitachi Energy’s existing campus in South Boston.
This facility will become the largest manufacturing site for large power transformers in the United States.
The South Boston facility alone will create more than 825 new jobs in Southside Virginia.
The new positions will offer long-term employment opportunities in engineering, advanced manufacturing, and operations roles.
The investment is part of a more than $9 billion USD global investment program, which is the largest in the industry.
Hitachi Energy is expanding its manufacturing capacity, R&D, engineering, and partnerships globally to power the world’s energy system to be more reliable, secure, and resilient.
CEO Andreas Schierenbeck stated that Hitachi Energy is uniquely positioned as the global leader in electrification to deliver critical power solutions for the American market.

The company expressed gratitude for the leadership and support of the Trump administration, Governor Youngkin, Virginia’s General Assembly, and the Commonwealth’s congressional delegation in making the production capacity possible.
source_title: Hitachi announces historic $1 billion USD manufacturing investment to power America’s energy future through production of critical grid infrastructure

facts: Hitachi Astemo has developed an autonomous driving technology that helps safely and smoothly pass by oncoming vehicles on narrow roads using 3D sensing results of the surrounding vehicle environment.
Hitachi Astemo aims to strengthen its cost competitiveness through the use of 3D sensing data obtained from new stereo cameras with improved cost benefits.

The demand for autonomous driving is increasing to help solve social issues such as alleviating traffic congestion and supporting the transportation of disabled, elderly, and others with special needs.

For practical implementation of autonomous driving on public roads, it is necessary to predict risks of complex behavior of pedestrians and other moving objects, as well as sudden emergence from hidden areas, to avoid danger in advance.

A vehicle is required to plan a safe driving trajectory and driving control based on 3D sensing information such as LiDAR and stereo cameras to achieve smooth driving in environments mixed with non-autonomous vehicles.

Until recently, Astemo’s technologies were developed to prevent collisions by predicting behavioral changes of pedestrians and other road users in autonomous driving on general roads, and by performing safe and natural deceleration.

Astemo has also developed a hazard prediction and avoidance driving technology that predicts areas with potentially high collision risk, such as the behavior of other moving objects and sudden appearances from blind spots, and drives at a speed and trajectory that can avoid risk in advance.

Hitachi Astemo has developed technologies that enable smooth passing automated driving in narrow roads by coordinating with oncoming vehicles, integrating three-dimensional information obtained from sensing such as LiDAR, recognizing the driving environment around the vehicle in three dimensions, understanding intended movement from detected free space and behavior of oncoming vehicles, and performing route prediction.

Hitachi Astemo is working towards collaborating with a high-precision, versatile new stereo camera launched in March 2023 that can detect pedestrians and bicycles with a high degree of accuracy and measure distances by combining long-range detection with a wide viewing angle.
The new stereo camera stores identification patterns in advance using machine learning techniques, contributing to collision prevention during right and left turns at intersections.
Hitachi Astemo has created software processing that enables advanced image recognition and vehicle control on cost-competitive electronic control units by utilizing its AI and recognition technology.
By collaborating with the new stereo camera, it will be possible to further enhance the cost competitiveness of autonomous driving technology that enables cooperative behavior in narrow roads.

Hitachi Astemo’s autonomous driving technology using cooperative behavior in narrow roads and the new stereo camera will be introduced at the “Automotive Engineering Exposition 2023 Yokohama” (booth number: 148) held at Pacifico Yokohama from May 23.

Hitachi Astemo is committed to strengthening its business and delivering technological innovation through a strategic business portfolio, which includes Powertrain & Safety Systems, Chassis, Motorcycle, Software and Aftermarket businesses.

Aiming for growth based on the pillars of “green,” “digital,” and “innovation,” Hitachi Astemo will contribute to a better global environment by developing xEV systems and highly efficient internal combustion engine systems that reduce emissions.
Hitachi Astemo will deliver enhanced safety and comfort through autonomous driving systems, advanced driver assistance systems, and advanced chassis systems.
source_title: Hitachi Astemo Develops Autonomous Driving Technology That Enables Safe Passing on Narrow Roads

facts: Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. manufactures and markets engine management, electric powertrain, and integrated vehicle controls for major automotive manufacturers worldwide.

Gary Burns was named the new Ecommerce Sales Manager for the Hitachi Astemo Americas Aftermarket Division on Nov. 25, 2022.
In his role, Burns will take the lead on strategy to increase both online brand awareness and online sales of Hitachi Astemo Americas products.
Burns will work under the guidance of Sales Director Ryan Pelkey and closely with Marketing Manager Hannah Hua.
Burns will take a leadership role in establishing new business partnerships and scaling online business for the Aftermarket Division.
Burns' background includes managing Amazon Seller, eBay, and Walmart accounts to support Hitachi Astemo Americas' growing B2B and B2C digital sales.

Gary Burns stated that throughout his career he has been immersed in almost every aspect of digital marketing, advertising, and sales, and is ready to lead the team that expands the brand's digital reach as Hitachi Astemo Americas increases its lineup of world-class quality products.
Prior to joining Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc., Burns held leadership positions at Newegg.com and Xtractor Depot.
Burns has produced photographic and written marketing content, analyzed, budgeted, and forecasted, and sharpened his business development skills.
Burns holds certificates in Google Ads management and web development.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that ports in Savannah and Brunswick are essential to maintaining Georgia’s unprecedented and decade-long status as the No. 1 state for business.
Kemp cited responsible government, top-ranked workforce training program, and world-class infrastructure as key reasons for the state's title and record trade and commerce.
The U.S. Southeast population has grown by 9 percent since 2012, adding 6.5 million people and increasing consumer demand.
The fastest growing states are Texas, Florida, North Carolina, Georgia, South Carolina, and Tennessee.
This demographic trend has resulted in manufacturing shifting to the Southeast, with major brands establishing a presence.
GPA Board Chairman Kent Fountain stated that Governor Kemp’s leadership is helping achieve a pro-business approach with a strong Economic Development team attracting major companies.
Fountain noted ports are huge economic engines and GPA is ensuring responsible growth to bring new jobs and philanthropic partnerships.
Source shifting in Southeast Asia due to a “China plus one” sourcing trend favors U.S. East Coast delivery.
Southeast Asia shipping routes to the U.S. East Coast via the Suez Canal are five days faster than U.S. West Coast routings.
India's growing population and consumer class represent new business opportunities for GPA customers; about a dozen weekly India services currently call GPA.
More global supply chains have shifted from the U.S. West Coast to the U.S. East Coast, with East Coast ports now handling 54 percent of the nation’s global trade.
Griff Lynch stated they are talking to customers and designing a gateway port and inland supply chain for long-term requirements.
Savannah’s ocean carrier customers are upsizing their vessels; 80 percent of container ships entering the port are 11,000 TEU or larger.
Georgia Ports provides one of the nation’s best-connected port operations with 35 weekly ocean carrier services.
Inland connectivity is a GPA growth strategy, with 75 percent of the U.S. population within a 3- to 4-day rail trip from the Port of Savannah.

GPA will focus on a mid-American arc ranging from Dallas to Chicago and Cincinnati, Ohio, as well as other cities within a 200-mile radius of Savannah such as Charlotte, Rocky Mount, N.C., Tampa, Orlando, and Miami.
GPA handled volumes for FY2023 of 5.4 million TEU.
GPA forecasts 4-6 percent growth for the coming years.
At the Port of Brunswick, Roll-on/Roll-off cargo hit a record in FY2023, leading to an overall growth of 17.7 percent over the previous year at 723,515 units.
Georgia Speaker of the House Jon Burns stated that the ecosystem of service providers surrounding ports plays a vital role in getting products to store shelves and factory floors across the country.
Burns noted global trade supports communities in every corner of the state from Dublin to Rome, and Athens to Albany.
Mason Mega Rail Terminal provides rail capacity to serve inland markets and reduce traffic congestion on roads while lowering emissions.
Strong intermodal connections to locations more than 250 miles inland complement Georgia’s motor carrier network.
Mason Mega Rail provides the greatest on-dock rail capacity of any port in the Western Hemisphere.
GPA has seen significant cargo volume growth at the Appalachian Regional Port (ARP) since opening the Northwest Georgia location five years ago.
GPA now provides service to the ARP in only 36 hours.
A new partnership with CSX provides a Carolina Connector service to Rocky Mount, N.C., creating a faster supply chain and opening Ohio Valley opportunities.

Savannah’s position as a first port of call for ocean carriers transiting the Panama Canal means cargo will be available at Rocky Mount before containers could be unloaded from vessels at ports farther up the coast.
Efforts are underway for a new partnership with Norfolk Southern to serve Northeast Georgia.
The Blue Ridge Connector service to Gainesville, Ga., will open in 2026.
GPA recently announced an eight-year, $6 million pledge through Housing Savannah, Inc., and the Community Housing Services Agency, Inc.
The Port of Savannah Area Workforce Housing Initiative is designed to improve quality of life and housing for individuals living in near-port communities.
The program encompasses repair, construction, and purchase of affordable and workforce housing to benefit low- and moderate-income homeowners, first-time homebuyers, and renters.
The initiative will prioritize home repairs for existing neighborhood residents and support eligible applicants with down payment assistance for first-time purchases in the Savannah area.
Garden City Terminal has an annual capacity of 7.5 million TEU as the largest container terminal in the Western Hemisphere.
It can accommodate vessels up to 20,000 TEU.
Renovations at Container Berth 1 were completed in August, creating another big-ship berth option and adding 1.5 million TEU of capacity.
The terminal can now serve seven ships simultaneously, including four vessels with a capacity of 16,000+ TEUs.
In tandem with the expanded berth, GPA ordered eight new STS cranes.
The first four arrived in February and are operational.
The next four (capable of 22-wide, 24-wide reach) arrived in September and will be operational in January.
At Container Berth 2, four more STS cranes are being erected and will be operational in December.
All efforts are focused on getting Berth 2 operational to handle more ship calls and return to normal operations in two months.
New cranes on Berth 9 will be ready early next year.
A newly built, 100-acre container storage yard is centrally located for longer-term storage needs with fewer drays required.

Phase 1 of this new storage yard will open in November 2023 and provide a one-stop storage location for exporters and importers with a dedicated truck gate to speed cargo flows and avoid traffic on local streets.
Berth and container yard renovations are underway at Savannah’s 200-acre Ocean Terminal to serve two large container ships simultaneously and offer 2 million TEU annual capacity.
Renovations to the first 1,325-foot berth will be completed in January 2025, with the second completed in June 2026, for a total of 2,650 feet of berth.
Berth 13 construction starts in October 2023, with this portion closed for ship operations.
650 feet of new berth will be completed by the time the new STS cranes arrive in June 2024.
Eight additional cranes have been ordered as part of the $1 billion refurbishment project.
Entry and exit for truckers will be directly onto I-16/U.S. 17, minimizing neighborhood impact.

The facility is transitioning from a container-Ro/Ro-breakbulk terminal into a container-only model so that the Port of Savannah will handle containers, and the Port of Brunswick will handle Ro/Ro and breakbulk.
By February 2024, all non-containerized cargo at Ocean Terminal will transition to Brunswick.
More gates, extended hours, and improved turn times are the goals to attract more trucking companies to Georgia.
The South Atlantic Chassis Pool 3.0 was recently acquired by Oaktree and will infuse the Port of Savannah with new and high-quality, refurbished chassis.

Built by GPA and opened in September 2023, the Savannah Transload Facility is designed to complement the local Savannah supply chain ecosystem and offer transload services from international marine containers into domestic 53-foot truck trailers.
The facility is managed by NFI, a third-party logistics provider, and adds 10,000 TEU of additional transload capabilities.
Savannah warehouse vacancy rate is currently 4.32 percent according to the most recent figures by CBRE.
The optimal range is 6-8 percent vacancy, so GPA is looking to attract more investment by developers and investors.
GPA is expanding capacity to serve growing Ro/Ro cargo volumes by investing $260 million in Colonel’s Island at the Port of Brunswick.
The investment develops 640,000 square feet of new processing space, 122 acres of additional auto storage, and a fourth Ro/Ro berth.
GPA will host the State of the Port for Brunswick on Oct. 30 at the Jekyll Island Convention Center.
Hyundai Metaplant announced a $9.8 billion investment in Georgia for a new electric vehicle manufacturing site that will generate over 14,000 jobs when opened in late 2024.
Burlington announced a new 2 million square foot warehouse operation.
Bradshaw Home, a kitchenware manufacturer, will open a 1.2 million square-foot distribution center in Bloomingdale, Ga.
Kubota is expanding its location in Gainesville, Ga.
Hwashin, an automotive part manufacturer, is creating 460 new jobs in Laurens County, Ga.
Hanwha Q Cells, LG, and GE have established new locations now operating in the GPA inland port location of Appalachian Regional Port.
Aeronet Worldwide, a logistics services provider, has opened its second location in Georgia, an 180,000 square-foot warehouse eight miles from the Port of Savannah.
In 2023, 38,400 new jobs and $24 billion in new investments were created in Georgia.
The State has been ranked #1 in the nation to do business for 10 years in a row by Area Development Magazine.

From anywhere in the county, it is a less than 2-hour drive to either location.
A 331-acre featured property site is available on HWY 257 (GRAD Select Site).
The total workforce for the area is 301,204.
Market connectivity includes being less than 2 hours from Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.
Key industrial sectors in the region include Distribution, Automotive, and Manufacturing.
Quality of life is characterized by 'True. Southern. Hospitality.'

entity_name: Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority & Georgia State Incentives
facts: The Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority is the primary conduit for business incentives offered to qualified industrial prospects, allowing relocating or expanding companies to offset transition costs and risks associated with investing and creating jobs in Dublin-Laurens County.
Incentives offered through the state of Georgia and local community require signed performance agreements mandating companies meet certain job and investment expectations.

Manufacturers, distributors, and certain qualifying businesses may be offered exemptions from local property taxes for a negotiated time period based on commitments to new jobs and capital investment.
Development Authority staff assists companies in structuring proposed tax incentives to comply with local, state, and federal law.
The Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority can serve as a conduit for low-interest Industrial Revenue Bonds (IRBs) for qualifying projects that obtain external letters of credit for collateral.
Local leadership commits to assist with expedited permitting and plan review for certain projects, to the extent allowed by local and state law.
Qualifying projects are eligible for Laurens County Closing Fund (LCCF) grant funds usable for real property improvements and other expenditures allowed under Georgia law.
Qualifying companies may be eligible to receive donations of land located in established industrial parks based on proposed jobs and investment projections.
Companies generating a significant economic impact may be eligible for assistance with the preparation of a building pad.
In 2005, Georgia became the first state in the Southeast to adopt a Single Factor Gross Receipts apportionment formula for corporate income tax.
This formula treats a company's gross receipts or sales in Georgia as the only relevant factor for determining the portion of income subject to Georgia's six percent corporate income tax.

Georgia is one of only 13 states currently using Single Factor Apportionment, which significantly reduces the effective rate of Georgia income taxation for companies with substantial sales to customers outside Georgia.
Georgia does not use the Throw Back Rule, which many states use to tax income from sales of goods or services to out-of-state customers if the customer's state lacks that tax income.

Companies and headquarters engaged in strategic industries such as manufacturing, warehousing, distribution, processing, telecommunications, broadcasting, tourism, and research and development may qualify for Georgia's Job Tax Credit Program.
Depending on the community's tier, companies must create between five and 25 net new full-time jobs in the first year to qualify for the Job Tax Credit.
Credits may also be accrued for additional jobs created in years 2-5, but jobs created outside of year five may not be claimed unless a new threshold for job creation is met.
Qualified companies can claim tax credits with a value of $750-$3,500 per job, per year, beginning with the first taxable year and continuing for following years the job is maintained.
An additional $500 credit is offered in counties that participate in a multi-county Join Development Authority (JDA).

Increased tax credits, equal to Tier 1 credits, are allowed for companies creating jobs in Less Developed Census Tracts (LDCT), Opportunity Zones (OZ), Military Zones, or Georgia's 40 least developed counties.
Credits may offset 100 percent of state corporate income tax liability in Tier 1 and 2 counties, or 50 percent in Tier 3 and 4 counties.
Unused Job Tax Credits may be carried forward for 10 years from the close of the taxable year in which qualified jobs were established.
In Tier 1 counties, excess credits may be credited to Georgia payroll withholding taxes with a limitation of $3,500 per job, per year.
The Port Tax Credit Bonus is available to taxpayers who increase imports or exports through a Georgia port by 10 percent over the previous or base year.
Base year port traffic must be at least 75 net tons, five containers, or 10 TEUs; if not met, the percentage increase will be calculated using these figures as the base.
The Port Tax Credit Bonus can be used with either the Job or Investment Tax Credit program provided requirements are met, and may offset up to 50 percent of corporate income tax liability.
Unused Port Tax Credits may be carried forward for 10 years provided the increase in port traffic remains above the minimum level and job/investment requirements continue to be met.

The Port Tax Credit Bonus cannot be utilized with the Quality Jobs Tax Credit and is limited to Opportunity Zones, Military Zones, and Less Developed Census Tracts by existing large distribution centers.
An additional $1,250 per job credit is added to the Job Tax Credit for taxpayers with qualified increases in shipments through a Georgia port.
Companies creating at least 50 jobs in a 12-month period where each job pays wages at least 110 percent of the county average are eligible for the Quality Jobs Tax Credit.
The Quality Jobs Tax Credit provides $2,500-$5,000 per job, per year, for up to five years, and new quality jobs created within seven years can qualify.
Quality Jobs Tax Credits may offset payroll withholding once all other tax liability is exhausted and may be carried forward for 10 years.
Georgia offers a research and development incentive providing a 10 percent tax credit of increased RD expenses subject to a base amount calculation.

The R&D base amount equals Current Year Georgia Gross Receipts multiplied by the average ratio of qualified Georgia research expenses to gross receipts for the preceding three taxable years, or 0.300, whichever is less.
For new Georgia companies or those with no prior R&D expenditures in Georgia, the base amount is 30 percent of the current year's Georgia gross receipts.
The R&D credit is applied to 50 percent of net Georgia income tax liability after all other credits have been applied.
In the first five years of a newly formed business entity in Georgia, any excess R&D credit can be applied to state payroll withholding.
Companies employing at least 1,800 new employees and either investing a minimum of $450 million or having a minimum annual payroll of $150 million may claim the Mega Project Tax Credit.

The Mega Project Tax Credit provides $5,250 per job, per year for the first five years of each net new job position, with excess credits eligible for use against payroll withholding and carried forward for 10 years.
Employers purchasing or building qualified child care facilities are eligible for Georgia income tax credits equal to 100 percent of construction costs, spread over 10 years at ten percent each year.
Unused child care construction credits can be carried forward three years, and the facility must be licensed by the state.

Employers providing or sponsoring child care for employees are eligible for a credit against Georgia income tax equal to 75 percent of direct costs, with operating cost credits carried forward five years.
All child care credits can be used against 50 percent of the taxpayer's income tax liability in a given year.
The Georgia Department of Labor coordinates the federal Work Opportunity Tax Credit Program (WOTC) for private-sector businesses hiring individuals from nine target groups facing employment barriers.
Target groups include certain TANF and food stamp recipients, and residents of Empowerment Zones or Rural Renewal Counties.
Participating companies can reduce federal income tax liability with a WOTC between $1,200 to $9,000 per qualified employee depending on the target group.
The most frequently certified WOTC is $2,400 for each adult new hire, requiring GDOL certification before claiming on a federal return.
Qualified equipment purchases or leases are exempt from sales tax when used in the manufacturing process.

Under certain conditions, primary material handling equipment in warehouses and distribution centers, computer equipment, and Class 100 (or less) clean room machinery, equipment, and materials can also be exempted from sales tax.
Effective January 1, 2011, business inventory is exempt from state property taxes at 0.25 mills.
Many Georgia counties exempt up to 100 percent of qualified raw material, work-in-process, and finished goods inventory under the local-option Freeport law.
In most counties, distribution center and warehouse inventories are exempt if destined to be shipped out of state.
Georgia is home to multiple Foreign-Trade Zone (FTZ) sites and facilitates the program to streamline importing/exporting and lower costs for qualified companies.
The FTZ program allows qualified companies to defer, decrease, or eliminate duties on materials imported from overseas that are used in products assembled in Georgia.
GDOL assists company recruitment by posting job notices, collecting and screening applications/resumes, providing interview space, scheduling interviews, and hosting job fairs.
GDOL works with private employment agencies that list jobs with the state to assist in hiring.

Georgia's Quick Start program provides customized training for skill-based jobs at no cost to qualifying companies, providing training space, instructors, and materials, potentially saving millions in training costs.
A company's direct investment in training can be claimed as a retraining tax credit of up to $500 per full-time employee per approved program, with a total maximum of $1,250 per employee per year.
Retraining programs must be approved by the Technical College System of Georgia and focus on quality/productivity enhancements and certain software technologies.
The retraining tax credit offsets up to 50 percent of a company's state corporate income tax liability, can be carried forward 10 years, and can be combined with other tax credits.
The HOPE Scholarship provides tuition assistance at one of Georgia's 35 public colleges or universities for graduating high school seniors with a B or better average.
The HOPE Grant allows all Georgians to receive degree or certificate programs at low cost through Georgia's technical colleges and schools, advantageous for relocating families and company training.

Worksource Heart of Georgia covers 17 counties including Appling, Bleckley, Candler, Dodge, Emanuel, Evans, Jeff Davis, Johnson, Laurens, Montgomery, Tattnall, Telfair, Toombs, Treutlen, Wayne, Wheeler, and Wilcox.
This area receives Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA) funds for On-The-Job training, Youth Services, and Individual Training Accounts.

The shift involves one of Ultium Cells' US plants.

facts: LG Energy Solution has suspended construction of its battery factory in Georgia.
The suspension follows U.S. immigration raids that led to the arrest of hundreds of workers at the site and at Hyundai Motor’s nearby electric vehicle plant.
Nearly 500 agents from Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) and other federal authorities conducted the raids last Thursday.
The raids resulted in 475 arrests, including more than 300 South Korean nationals.
The sweep targeted Hyundai’s U.S. electric vehicle plant, known as HMGMA, and the battery facility it is jointly developing with LGES.
LGES had already pushed back the start of production at the battery plant to 2025, citing weak market conditions.
A company spokesperson told South Korean media it was “too early to say whether recent events would affect the plant’s operations.”
Hyundai has reportedly banned all U.S. business trips for its employees.
South Korea’s government said it was in talks with Washington to secure the release of the detained workers.
Presidential Chief of Staff Kang Hoon-sik stated, “To prevent similar incidents in the future, we will review and improve the accommodation and visa system for those on U.S. project business trips.”
Hyundai and LGES have pledged $7.6 billion in Georgia.
State Governor Brian Kemp described the project as the largest economic development initiative in state history.
The incident has raised concerns over visa access for South Korean specialists and the long-term viability of the investment.
Kang DaeKwun, chief investment officer at Life Asset Management, said the case underscored the challenges of investing in the U.S.
He noted that return on investment was already getting low due to inflation, and now companies face hiring challenges as well.
source_title: LG Energy Solution Halts Battery Plant Construction In Georgia After US Immigration Raids

entity_name: Hyundai-LG Energy Solution Battery Cell Manufacturing Joint Venture
canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution Battery Cell Manufacturing Joint Venture
facts: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution (LGES) will invest an additional USD 2 billion in their battery cell manufacturing joint venture at the Metaplant in Bryan County, Georgia.
This additional investment raises the JV’s total investment value to more than USD 4.3 billion.
The expansion is set to create another 400 new jobs.

Back in May 2023, Hyundai Motor Group and LGES signed a memorandum of understanding establishing LGES as the partner for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America’s (HMGMA) onsite battery cell manufacturing JV.
With today’s announcement, the EV manufacturing facility and the battery JV represent an estimated USD 7.59 billion in investment.
The combined projects will create 8,500 new jobs for the region over the next eight years.
The 30 GWh facility will be able to support the production of 300,000 units of EVs annually at full operations.
Hyundai Mobis will assemble battery packs using cells from the plant.
The assembled battery packs will be supplied to the Hyundai Motor Group’s U.S. manufacturing facilities for the production of Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EV models.
source_title: Hyundai and LG Energy to invest additional $2B in Georgia

entity_name: Hyundai-LG Energy Solution EV Battery Joint Venture
canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution Battery Joint Venture
facts: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution officially announced a plan to establish a 50/50 EV battery joint venture in the United States.
The two South Korean companies signed a memorandum of understanding with an intention to build a 30-gigawatt-hour (GWh) battery factory in Bryan County, Savannah, Georgia.
The joint venture will involve an investment of over $4.3 billion (KRW 5.7 trillion).
Construction of the battery plant is targeted to start in the second half of the publication year.
The construction site is located on a plot adjacent to the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America site, which is currently under construction.
Battery production at the new facility is targeted to start before the end of 2025.
The plant's annual capacity of 30 GWh is expected to be sufficient for approximately 300,000 electric vehicles.
The 30 GWh capacity indicates an average battery pack size of roughly 100-kilowatt-hours (kWh).
Several weeks prior to this announcement, Hyundai Motor Group signed a similar agreement with SK Innovation’s SK On to build a 35-GWh battery joint venture in Bartow Centre, Georgia.
Both the new LG Energy Solution JV and the SK On JV will supply battery cells for the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America plant.
The two battery factories will also supply cells for Hyundai Motor Group's other manufacturing sites.
Battery packs for these vehicles will be assembled by Hyundai Mobis.
Jaehoon Chang, President and CEO of Hyundai Motor Company, stated that Hyundai Motor Group is focusing on electrification efforts to secure a leadership position in the global auto industry.
Chang added that the group will create a strong foundation to lead the global EV transition through establishing a new EV battery cell plant with LG Energy Solution.
Youngsoo Kwon, CEO of LG Energy Solution, stated that two strong leaders in the auto and battery industries have joined hands to drive the EV transition in America.
Kwon noted that LG Energy Solution will commit its best efforts to offering ultimate sustainable energy solutions by advancing product competitiveness and global operational expertise.
Hyundai Motor Group, which includes the Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis brands, is preparing for high-volume electric vehicle production in the US.
The group aims for 600,000 annual EV units based on the recently announced battery plants.
LG Energy Solution noted that the new JV with Hyundai is its seventh battery plant project in the US, which are currently operating or under construction.
The total output of LG Energy Solution's seven US battery plants is roughly 250-300 GWh annually.
Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution have been partnering on electrification for many years, starting with a first non-rechargeable hybrid in 2009.
Their partnership includes several all-electric models, including the Hyundai Kona Electric and Hyundai Ioniq 6.
Both companies are also working on another joint battery project for an upcoming plant in Indonesia with a capacity of 10 GWh annually.
The new Georgia facility will produce lithium-ion cells specifically for electric vehicles.
Construction start is targeted for the second half of 2023 according to the article's summary section.
The facility is expected to supply battery cells to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America in Bryan County, Georgia.
The facility is also expected to supply Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama (HMMA) factory in Montgomery, Alabama.
The facility is expected to supply the Kia Georgia Plant (KaGA).
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group And LGES Confirm 30 GWh Battery JV In Georgia - AutoMoto Tale

facts: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solutions announced a joint venture in late May 2023 to build a $4.3 billion state-of-the-art battery manufacturing plant in the U.S.

Hyundai President and CEO Jaehoon Chang stated the partnership aims to "create a strong foundation to lead the global EV transition" and that with LGES, they are "ready to drive the EV transition in America."
The facility will be located adjacent to the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America near Savannah, Georgia.
Upon projected completion in 2025, the plant is expected to support the production of 300,000 EVs each year.
The project has the potential for future expansion.
This will be Hyundai's second battery manufacturing plant in Georgia and LGES's seventh in the U.S.
Incentives from the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) are drawing clean energy business to the U.S., providing tax credits for companies and consumers.
To make products eligible for IRA tax credits, manufacturers must meet specific sourcing requirements for EV battery components.
To make products eligible for IRA tax credits, manufacturers must complete the final assembly process in North America.
Batteries from the new plant will supply U.S. manufacturing facilities producing new Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EV models.
As of mid-June, the Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EV models were ineligible for IRA tax credits.
The initiative aims to accelerate EV adoption with a goal of EVs or plug-in hybrids contributing 50% of new vehicle sales in the U.S. by 2030.
The project aims to reduce the nation's reliance on Chinese battery supply chains.
The region could see up to 8,100 new jobs available between the Hyundai Metaplant and the new battery manufacturing facility.
Senator Jon Ossoff of Georgia led a trade mission to South Korea and praised the announcement as "yet another huge win for Georgia."
Senator Ossoff stated that "the IRA's manufacturing incentives continue to bring jobs and investment."
source_title: Hyundai-LGES announces $4.3B EV battery plant: ‘Huge win’

entity_name: Hyundai-LG Energy Solution Georgia EV Battery Plant
facts: About 560 Korean officials and workers from Hyundai and LG Chem have been detained from the biggest manufacturing project in Georgia history.

South Korea's government said on Friday (September 5) that its citizens' rights must not be violated in the course of Korean businesses' work in the U.S., noting "many" of its nationals had been detained after an immigration raid at a Hyundai Motor facility in Georgia.

Hundreds of workers at a Hyundai Motor facility being built in Georgia have been detained in a major raid by U.S. authorities, forcing a pause in the construction of a car battery factory that is part of what would be the biggest investment in the state.

The raid on Thursday has dealt a setback to the project and highlights an increasing crackdown by the Trump administration on immigrants and its disruptive impact on businesses, even as the White House tries to spur more inflows from foreign investors.

An agent at the U.S. Department of Homeland Security (DHS) stated that multiple U.S. agencies "conducted a judicially authorised enforcement operation, as we are actively conducting an investigation into unlawful employment practices."
Steven Schrank, special agent in charge of Homeland Security Investigations for Georgia, said in a news briefing aired on U.S. television: "Arrests are being made."
The Atlanta office of the U.S. Justice Department agency ATF reported that up to 450 people had been detained in a post on X.
A South Korean government official stated that the detained individuals were being held at a United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) detention facility.
The Korea Economic Daily reported around 560 workers at the Hyundai Motor facility and LG Energy Solution (LGES) had been detained, citing unidentified industry sources.
Some 300 of the detained individuals are South Korean nationals, according to local media reports.
South Korea's Foreign Ministry expressed concern about the impact of the detention operation on its citizens' economic activities in the U.S.

Lee Jae-woong, a ministry spokesman, said in a statement on Friday: "The economic activities of our companies investing in the United States and the interests of our citizens must not be unduly violated during the course of U.S. law enforcement."
The battery production facility, a joint venture between South Korean battery maker LGES and Hyundai Motor, was due to start operations at the end of this year, according to LGES.
A spokesperson at the Hyundai-GA battery company stated that it was cooperating fully and had paused construction work.
Hyundai Motor confirmed that its production of electric vehicles at the sprawling site was not affected by the raid.

LG Energy Solution stated it was "closely cooperating with the South Korean government and relevant authorities to ensure the safety of our employees and staff at contractors, and to secure their swift release from detention."
LGES shares dropped 2.3% following the incident.
In 2023, Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution announced a $4.3 billion venture to produce EV battery cells, with each company holding a 50% stake.
The plant will supply batteries for Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EV models.

The battery factory is part of Hyundai's $12.6 billion investments in Georgia, including the automaker's just-opened car factory, making it "the largest economic development project in the state’s history."

Social media video footage showed a man wearing a vest with the letters HIS (Homeland Security Investigations) telling workers in yellow safety vests: "We have a search warrant for the whole site. We need construction to cease immediately. We need all work to end on the site right now."
Under President Donald Trump, ICE has become the driving force of the Republican leader's sweeping crackdown on migrants, bolstered by record funding and new latitude to conduct raids.
Trump has stated he wants to deport "the worst of the worst" criminals, but ICE figures have shown a rise in non-criminals being picked up.

The U.S. is adding pressure on Korea, one of the major investors in the United States, at a time when Washington and South Korea are at odds over details of a trade deal that includes $350 billion of investments.
source_title: ICE Raid Shuts Down Hyundai's Giant Georgia EV Battery Plant | GOLDSEA | Asian American Daily

facts: South Korea’s LG Energy Solution (LGES) said its third-quarter operating profit likely rose 34% from a year earlier.
The profit increase was driven by a rush in U.S. electric vehicle (EV) sales ahead of expiring government incentives at the end of September.
LGES is the world’s second-largest EV battery maker.
The company supplies Tesla and General Motors.
LGES estimated an operating profit of 601 billion won ($420 million) for the July–September period.
The preliminary profit figure was up from 448 billion won a year earlier.
The preliminary figure exceeded the 528 billion won profit forecast from LSEG SmartEstimate.
Analysts said the company’s results were lifted by a late-quarter surge in U.S. EV purchases as consumers sought to benefit from the $7,500 federal tax credit that expired on Sept. 30.
A Seoul-based analyst stated: “Strong U.S. sales momentum likely provided a temporary boost before the incentive deadline.”
Excluding U.S. manufacturing incentives received under the Inflation Reduction Act, LGES said it would have posted an operating profit of 236 billion won.
The company will announce detailed earnings on Oct. 30.
LGES shares gained 1.5% on Monday.
The broader South Korean benchmark (.KS11) fell 1.4%.
LGES has warned of a potential slowdown in EV demand early next year following the expiration of U.S. tax credits and the imposition of import tariffs by the Trump administration.
The company is ramping up production of batteries for energy storage systems (ESS) to offset weaker vehicle demand.
LGES signed a $4.3 billion deal to supply Tesla with ESS batteries.
The deal helps the U.S. automaker reduce reliance on Chinese imports affected by tariffs.
Construction at LGES's joint battery plant with Hyundai Motor in Georgia was suspended in early September after an immigration raid led to the arrest of hundreds of South Korean workers.
Hyundai Motor CEO Jose Munoz said the incident would delay the plant’s startup by at least two to three months.

LGES said it would gradually resume U.S. business travel for its employees and subcontractors after the Trump administration agreed to allow South Korean workers to continue equipment installation under existing temporary visas.

The company’s efforts to stabilize operations and diversify beyond EV batteries come as the global battery industry faces shifting demand patterns, tighter trade policies, and increasing competition in the energy storage market.
source_title: LG Energy Solution Forecasts 34% Rise in Q3 Operating Profit on U.S. EV Demand Surge

facts: G-TEKT Corporation is a manufacturer of the Honda Group that produces automotive body stamped parts and thick precision stamped parts.
The company also produces and sells dies, jigs, and tools.
In April 2011, Kikuchi Co., Ltd. merged with Takao Kinzoku Kogyo Co., Ltd. to establish G-Tekt Corporation.
As of March 31, 2025, Honda Motor Co., Ltd. holds a 30.04% investment ratio in the company.
Individual shareholders hold investment ratios of 8.02%, 7.16%, and 2.59%.
The Master Trust Bank of Japan, Ltd. (Trust Account) holds a 6.94% investment ratio.
Custody Bank of Japan, Ltd. (Trust Account) holds a 4.19% investment ratio.
RE FUND 107-CLIENT AC holds a 1.59% investment ratio with Citibank as standing proxy.
STATE STREET BANK AND TRUST COMPANY holds a 1.32% investment ratio with Mizuho Bank as standing proxy.
DFA INTL SMALL CAP VALUE PORTFOLIO holds a 1.09% investment ratio with Citibank as standing proxy.
Hanwa Co., Ltd. holds a 1.03% investment ratio.
The company manufactures electrification parts including battery housing and motor cores.

The company produces body structural parts such as front pillar upper, front pillar lower, center pillars, rear pillars, damper housings, front wheel housings, bulkhead front, dashboard upper, dashboard lower, frame front side, side sills, front sheet, mid floor crossmembers, crossmember B, rail roof front, roof panels, steering beams, rear wheel housings, center inner, parcel rear, rear combination, rear panels, rear seat reinforcement, back panel, and floor.
The company produces automatic transmission components including torque converters (inner/outer), drum inner, EH drum assemblies, carry assemblies, pistons, clutch drums, and clutch hubs.
The company produces continuously variable transmission components including primary pistons/cylinders and secondly cylinders/clutches.
Other products include timing pulleys, holders for hybrids, forged products, rotation sensors, and transmission parts.

In June 2000, the company invested in Jefferson Southern Corporation in Georgia, USA, for the manufacturing and sales of automotive parts; this entity is a subsidiary of Jefferson Industries Corporation.
The company began supplying to Honda Motor Co., Ltd. in May 1964.
In September 1987, Honda Motor Co., Ltd. made a capital participation in the company with an ownership stake of 9.52%.

In May 1984, the company established KTH Parts Industries Inc. in Ohio, US as a joint venture among five companies including American Honda Motor Company Inc. to manufacture and supply automotive parts.
In May 1988, the company established Jefferson Industries Corporation as a joint venture with Takao Kinzoku Kogyo and American Honda Motor Company Inc. in Ohio, US.
The company established G-TEKT North America Corporation in Ohio, USA in April 2013.
In November 2022, the company turned G-TEKT America Corporation into a wholly owned subsidiary.
source_title: G-TEKT Corporation - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: Auto parts manufacturer Joon Georgia Inc. is investing $317 million to build a new assembly plant in Statesboro, GA.
The project is expected to create 317 jobs.
The factory will be the first confirmed supplier for the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
The Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America is a massive $5-billion electric vehicle assembly plant under construction in nearby Bryan County.
Operations at the new Joon Georgia plant are planned to begin in mid-2024.

Jung Ho Sea, CEO of Ajin USA, says the company is excited to be one of the first Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America automotive suppliers to build an automotive parts manufacturing facility in Bulloch County and for its first plant in Georgia.
Joon Georgia is part of Ajin USA, a supplier for multiple Hyundai Motor Group facilities.
The company produces a variety of automotive body parts and electronics for automotive OEMs.
source_title: Automotive Supplier to Bring 317 Manufacturing Jobs to Georgia | ASSEMBLY

facts: Joon Georgia, Inc. is an automotive parts manufacturer.
Joon Georgia, Inc. will create 630 new jobs.
Joon Georgia, Inc. will invest $317 million in Bulloch County.
Plans for the company’s new manufacturing facility mark the first confirmed supplier for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America in Bryan County.
Benjy Thompson is the CEO of the Development Authority of Bulloch County.
Andrew Isenhour is the Deputy Director of Communications at the Office of Governor.
source_title: Economic Development and Developers in the News #85

facts: Northern Meriwether County is anticipating industrial growth sparked by a Kia plant set to open in neighboring Troup County.
The new Kia plant is expected to open in 2009.
Greenville, the county seat, is using the state’s Better Hometown program to spruce up its business district and encourage economic development.
A streetscape project in Greenville has taken precedence to be ready for Kia growth.
Meriwether County is in Phase I of the Entrepreneur Friendly Community program to create an entrepreneur environment by building small business strategies into economic development.
The county hopes to develop a youth entrepreneur program to provide local career options for young people.
Meriwether County has been designated a Work Ready Community.
Gap training for individuals seeking to qualify for certain jobs will be available at no cost at West Georgia Technical College’s Meriwether County Workforce Development Center.

The Work Ready Region program aligns Meriwether with neighboring counties and education partners West Georgia Technical College and Flint River Technical College to prepare residents for advanced manufacturing employment.

The Work Ready Region program is in the grant request stage and targets at-risk and out-of-school youth while integrating science, technology, engineering and mathematics into public school curricula.
The region is concentrating on the advanced manufacturing industry to provide trained employees for the anticipated influx of suppliers that will follow Kia.
Finishing touches are being put on the new Meriwether Industrial Park located at I-85 and Highway 54.
The Meriwether Industrial Park spans 900 acres and is a public-private partnership designed to attract Kia suppliers.
Supplier industry executives find the park's location between the Kia plant and Atlanta’s Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport very attractive.
A 240-acre Greenville Industrial Park, located next to the Workforce Development Center, is also nearing completion.
G&S Metals chose the Manchester Industrial Park in southern Meriwether County for its first Southeastern facility.
G&S Metals opened in 2006 and expects to have 75 employees and invest $7 million by 2009.
G&S Metals, headquartered in Indiana, supplies the automotive industry with primary-grade recycled aluminum.
A Special Purpose Local Option Sales Tax (SPLOST) passed in September will fund water work, roads, bridges, public safety, and recreation to entice new industry.
Luthersville, located seven miles from the new industrial park, is experiencing growth with new business construction after a 25-year gap.
Meriwether County's population was 22,881 in 2006.
Unemployment in Meriwether County was 5.8 percent as of September 2007, compared to Georgia's 4.6 percent.

Largest employers in the county include the Meriwether County School System (850 employees), Roosevelt Warm Springs Institute for Rehabilitation (500), Georgia-Pacific Corp. (335), Angio Dynamics (140), and Warm Springs Medical Center (130).
source_title: Meriwether County: Embracing Past And Future - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) announced on November 1 that YANMAR America Corporation will open a new training and customer experience facility in Cherokee County.
The new facility will create 25 new jobs.
YANMAR America Corporation is investing $20 million into the local community.
YANMAR America Corporation manufactures advanced performance diesel engines, equipment, and gas engine based energy systems.
YANMAR's North, South, and Central American regional headquarters operation is located in Adairsville, Georgia.
This new investment represents an expansion of YANMAR's presence in Georgia.
The 25 jobs will cover administration, marketing, service, training, and management roles.

Cherokee County was selected for its natural beauty, welcoming officials, shared vision for sustainability and agriculture, proximity to the current Adairsville headquarters, and proximity to the Atlanta airport.
The new facility will be located in the Cherokee 75 Corridor at 1 YANMAR Drive, at the corner of Highway 92 and Old Alabama Road.
GDEcD Project Manager Joseph Huntemann represented the Global Commerce division in partnership with the Cherokee Office of Economic Development and Georgia EMC.
Cherokee County Commission Chairman Buzz Ahrens stated that YANMAR is among several new and expanding international corporations choosing Cherokee due to its superb location and access to talent.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson said, 'We are in the business of bringing quality jobs and investment to Georgia, and it is always a great day when we are able to celebrate this good news with statewide partners and our corporate citizens.' She added, 'We are happy to welcome YANMAR’s newest operation, and we are confident they will thrive in our pro-business environment.'

YANMAR America president Tim Fernandez said, 'Cherokee County was a desirable location for our new facility for many reasons. The area itself offers natural beauty, and the Cherokee County officials were very welcoming and share our vision for promoting sustainability and agriculture. The site’s proximity to our current headquarters in Adairsville and the Atlanta airport is also ideal for bringing in our customers from all over the globe.'

Cherokee County Commission Chairman Buzz Ahrens said, 'We are delighted to have YANMAR’s state-of-the-art training facility in Cherokee. They are among several new and expanding international corporations choosing Cherokee because of our superb location and access to talent. We look forward to YANMAR’s success as they help to build our sustainable vision for Cherokee.'
YANMAR America Corporation serves as the regional headquarters for Yanmar Co., Ltd., a global company based in Osaka, Japan.
Yanmar Co., Ltd. was established in 1912 and became the world's first manufacturer to develop a practical small diesel engine in 1933.
YANMAR America Corporation's current headquarters address is 101 International Parkway, Adairsville, GA 30103.
source_title: YANMAR America Corporation will create 25 new jobs in Cherokee County, invest $20M | WBHF

facts: Kumho Tire USA, Inc. is the U.S. subsidiary of Kumho Tire Co., Ltd., a leading global tire manufacturer headquartered in Seoul, South Korea.
Kumho Tire USA's U.S. operations are headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia.

Kumho Tire USA announced the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) formalizing a long-term partnership with the Georgia Department of Veterans Service and the Georgia Veterans Service Foundation to support Georgia's approximately 680,000 veterans and their families.

The partnership first became publicly visible earlier this year when Georgia Veterans Service Foundation representatives were honored as Co-Captains at Kumho Tire Night at State Farm Arena during an Atlanta Hawks vs. Boston Celtics game.
Edmund Cho serves as the CEO of Kumho Tire USA, Inc.
Kumho Tire USA is the official tire sponsor of the Atlanta Hawks.
Specific initiatives for the veteran partnership will be announced as the partnership develops.
source_title: Kumho Tire USA, Georgia Department of Veterans Service, and Georgia Veterans Service Foundation Announce Formal Partnership to Support Georgia's Veterans

facts: Kumho Tire is a high performance tire company that brings quality and innovative technology to consumers worldwide.
With over 50 years of experience, Kumho Tire has transformed its humble beginning in South Korea into becoming one of the largest tire manufacturers with 8 factories.
Kumho Tire first originated over 60 years ago due to a shortage of bus tires.
The Kumho Tire facility in Macon spans over 1 million sq. ft. and is the company's first plant location in the United States.
The Macon facility has over 380 employees on staff.
The Macon facility has the capacity to produce 4 million tires per year.
In 2002, Kumho built the world's first 26" tire.
Kumho Tire is the official product supplier for the NBA.

facts: Kumho Tire USA established its U.S. headquarters in Atlanta, Georgia.
Kumho Tire USA serves as the U.S. sales, marketing, product development, and distribution arm of Kumho Tire Co. Inc.
Kumho Tire was founded in 1960.
Kumho Tire has more than 50 years of history immersed in an innovative approach to product development, market expansion, technical progression, and environmental awareness.
Kumho Tire is positioned as one of the world’s top tire manufacturers.
Kumho Tire products are distributed in 160 countries.
Harry Choi serves as the CEO of Kumho Tire USA.
Julia Kim serves as a press contact for Kumho Tire USA.
