facts: Kumho Tire Georgia Inc. said it will work with the U.S. Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) to resolve alleged safety issues at the tire maker's Macon plant.
source_title: Kumho vows to resolve safety issues at Georgia plant - Tire Business

facts: Production capacity at Kumho Tire USA Inc.'s consumer tire manufacturing plant in Macon, Ga., will increase by an additional 500,000 units per year as part of an expansion initiative that is expected to be completed by the fourth quarter of 2022.
Currently, Kumho's Macon plant can produce 11,000 passenger tires at capacity, according to MTD's 2021 Facts Issue.
Parent company Kumho Tire is investing $305 million to expand its tire manufacturing plant in Vietnam.
According to Kumho officials, upon completion of the expansion in Vietnam, the company forecasts to produce at least 9.3 million tires per year.

The Vietnam expansion project will gradually be funded beginning from the third quarter of 2021 through the first quarter of 2023, adding a new section of the building to vacant lot space within the overall facility compound.
Kumho has eight plants spread across Vietnam, the U.S., China and its native South Korea.
The Macon plant is Kumho's only production facility in the U.S.
The Macon plant opened in 2015.

Il Taik Jung, Kumho Tire's president and CEO, stated that increasing production capacity ahead of growing demand is part of their strategy to ensure product availability for customers and support continued growth of partnerships in the North American market.
source_title: Kumho to Add Passenger Tire Capacity at U.S. Plant | 2021-03-29  | Modern Tire Dealer

facts: Partnership agreement was signed late last year at the Kumho Tire Yongin Central Research Institute in Gyeonggi Province.

Attendees included Kim Young-jin, Executive Vice President and Head of R&D at Kumho Tire; Kim Gi-woon, Executive Vice President and Head of the VPD Center; Dan Clark, CEO of Ansible Motion; and Salman Safdar, Sales Director of Ansible Motion.

Operating under the slogan “Kumho Tire with Ansible Motion: Driving the Future with Digital Tires,” the two companies will collaborate to enhance tire performance validation systems using advanced digital simulation technologies.
The partnership aims to strengthen competitiveness in tire development for electric vehicles (EVs) and high-performance vehicles.
Ansible Motion is a global leader in high-fidelity driving simulator technology that accurately reproduces real-world driving conditions.
Its solutions enable virtual verification of key driving characteristics such as vehicle dynamics, driving safety, and ride comfort.

By integrating Ansible Motion’s latest driving simulators into its R&D processes, Kumho Tire expects to significantly reduce the time and cost associated with physical vehicle testing and prototype development.
Kumho Tire anticipates achieving more precise and efficient performance validation from the early stages of product development.
This initiative is part of Kumho Tire’s broader strategy to respond more rapidly to the evolving mobility landscape while strengthening its digital R&D capabilities.

Kim Young-jin, Executive Vice President and Head of R&D at Kumho Tire, stated: “Collaboration with Ansible Motion marks an important step in advancing Kumho Tire’s R&D paradigm toward a digital-centered approach.”

Kim Young-jin added: “By actively leveraging advanced simulation technologies, we will introduce high-performance, high-value-added products optimized for future mobility and continue to enhance our global competitiveness.”
Dan Clark, CEO of Ansible Motion, commented: “This partnership with Kumho Tire will serve as a meaningful example of how driving simulation technology can accelerate innovation in the tire industry.”
Dan Clark added: “We look forward to contributing to the advancement of the global mobility industry through the technical synergy between our two companies.”
The partnership forms part of Kumho Tire’s ongoing digital transformation and sustainable technology innovation strategy.
Kumho Tire plans to apply advanced driving simulation technologies across its entire R&D process to further improve development efficiency and precision.

Through these efforts, Kumho Tire aims to accelerate the development of next-generation digital tires and secure differentiated technological competitiveness in the EV and high-performance tire markets.
source_title: Kumho Tire Signs Partnership with Ansible Motion to Develop Next-Generation Digital Tires < Business Exchange < Business & Economy < Article - The World Korea

facts: A Korean automotive supplier is setting up shop in Effingham County.
The company is investing $22 million to establish a new distribution center.
The investment will create 70 jobs.
The distribution center will supply "key customers" from the coastal Georgia location.
The Korean automotive supplier is identified as Kyungshin America Corp.
Kyungshin America Corp.'s news release does not explicitly mention Hyundai.
Kyungshin America Corp. is a subsidiary of Incheon, South Korea-based Kyungshin Co. Ltd.
Parent company Kyungshin Co. Ltd. has won supplier awards from Hyundai Motor Group.
Kyungshin Co. Ltd. is building a massive electric vehicle plant in Ellabell.
The Ellabell EV plant will be the parent company's first dedicated EV facility in the U.S.
Effingham County is one of four counties marketing a 2,900-acre site through a joint development authority.
A major Hyundai project is precipitating spillover investment effects in each partner county.
The city of Rincon secured a $300 million investment from longtime Hyundai and Kia supplier Sewon America.
The Sewon America investment will create 740 jobs.
The Sewon America investment was announced in February.
Sewon America will locate at the Grande View Industrial Park.
Kyungshin America is locating at 1001 Gateway Parkway.
The Savannah Gateway Industrial Hub reportedly has industrial sites of up to 500 acres available.
Brandt Herndon serves as the CEO of the joint development authority marketing the site.
Broe Real Estate Group assisted in locating Kyungshin America at the Savannah Gateway Industrial Hub.
Kyungshin manufactures wire harnesses, assemblies, and other electrical components.
The company has been in operation for approximately half a century.
Kyungshin is focusing its efforts on the growing U.S. electric vehicle market.
Details regarding the investment were provided in a release from the Effingham County Industrial Development Authority.
source_title: Effingham County Nets $22M Distribution Center from Korean Auto Supplier - Global Atlanta

facts: Kyungshin Corporation manufactures automotive wire harnesses.
The company supplies its products mainly to Hyundai Motor Company.
Sumitomo Wiring Systems Ltd. and Sumitomo Electric Industries, Ltd. became shareholders in December 2004.
As of December 31, 2024, Kyungshin Holdings Co., Ltd. holds a 50.0% interest ratio.
As of December 31, 2024, Sumitomo Electric Industries, Ltd. holds a 30.0% interest ratio.
As of December 31, 2024, Sumitomo Wiring Systems Ltd. holds a 20.0% interest ratio.
The company produces Connected products including Wiring Harness, FPC Module, Junction Block, Wire to Wire Connector, Wire to Unit Bundling Parts, and Joint.

The company produces Eco-Friendly products including ICCB (In Cable Control Box), Charging Coupler, Power relay assemblies, V2L (Vehicle To Load), HV Wiring Harness, Flexible Busbar, CDM, BEQ, and B12V-48V Convertor.
The company produces Intelligent products including CCU (Communication Control Unit), ICU, Ethernet Service Unit (ESU), SPDU, VPMS module, B-LBM, and Gateway.
In April 2008, the company established Kyungshin America Corporation in Georgia, U.S.A.
The company began supply of wire harnesses for the Hyundai Pony in October 1974.
The company acquired ISO9001 certification for all wire harness plants in July 1997.
The company established Kyungshin Industrial Motherson Ltd. in India in October 1997.
The company acquired QS9000 certification for its wire harness plant in June 1999.
The company established Kyungshin Cable Co., Ltd. as a spun-off company in January 2000.
The company set up a new plant in Hwaseong, Gyeonggi-do in March 2000.
The company acquired QS9000 certification for all offices in July 2001.
The company established Qingdao Kyungshin Electronic Co., Ltd. in China in July 2002.
In June 2003, the company and Lear Corporation of the U.S.A. jointly established Honduras Electrical Distribution Systems, S. de R.L. de C.V. in Honduras.
The company set up a new plant in Gunsan, Jeollabuk-do in August 2003.
In February 2004, the company established Lear-Kyungshin Sales and Engineering LLC in the U.S.A. as a joint venture with Lear Corporation of U.S.A.
The company acquired ISO/TS16949 certification in September 2004.
The company acquired ISO14001 certification in October 2004.
In May 2005, the company relocated its headquarters and research center to Incheon.
The company established Jiangsu Kyungshin Electronic Co., Ltd. in China in December 2005.
The company expanded Qingdao Kyungshin Electronic Co., Ltd. in October 2007.
In February 2010, the company entered into the connector business.
In September 2010, the company adopted a new company name, Kyungshin Corporation.
The company established Qingdao Jimo Kyungshin Electronic Co., Ltd. in China in December 2010.
The company established Kyungshin (Cambodia) Corporation in Cambodia in May 2012.
The company established Anhui Kyungshin Electronic Co., Ltd. in China in September 2015.
The company acquired ISO22301 certification in December 2017.
The company established PT. Dharma Kyungshin Indonesia (DKI) in Indonesia in 2020.
source_title: Kyungshin Corporation - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: Lear Corp. engages in the design, manufacture and supply of automotive seats, electrical distribution systems and electronic modules, as well as related sub-systems, components, and software.
It operates through the Seating and E-Systems segments.

The Seating segment consists of the design, engineering, just-in-time assembly and delivery of complete seat systems, as well as the manufacture of all major seat components, including seat covers and surface materials such as leather and fabric, seat structures and mechanisms, seat foam and headrests.

The E-System segment engages in the design, development, engineering and manufacture of electrical distribution systems, as well as electronic control modules, electrification products, connectivity products and software solutions for the cloud, vehicles and mobile devices.
The company was founded in 1917 and is headquartered in Southfield, MI.
CEO is Raymond E. Scott.
LEA: 2026 outlook targets up to $24B in sales, margin growth, and strong shareholder returns.
Lear Reports Q1 2026: Revenue $5.8B, Net Income $172M, Adjusted EPS $3.87.
LEAR CORP 1Q 2026: Revenue $5.823B, EPS $3.34— 10-Q Summary.
LEA: Q1 2026 net income more than doubled on 5% sales growth and improved margins, with strong cash flow.
Lear declares $0.77 dividend; board re-elected.
Next earnings report date is July 31, 2026.
As of Jul 7, 2026, the company has 164.3K employees.
Lear Corporation EBITDA is 1.40B EUR, and current EBITDA margin is 6.99%.

facts: Shaw Industries is headquartered in Dalton, Georgia.
The flooring company is a unit of billionaire investor Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway Inc conglomerate.
The company is growing so quickly that finding good talent 'can be challenging,' according to Brian Cooksey, director of workforce development.
Brian Cooksey serves as the director of workforce development at Shaw Industries.
Shaw Industries is funding programs in local high schools and even middle schools to get students interested early in the high-tech manufacturing in which it specializes.
source_title: More US regions see job openings outnumbering jobless

facts: Lear Corp., based in Southfield, is a global leader in automotive seating and e-systems.
Lear entered into a definitive agreement to acquire substantially all of Kongsberg Automotive’s interior comfort systems (ICS) business unit for $203.4 million.
Kongsberg Automotive is based in Switzerland and has operations in Novi.

The ICS business unit specializes in comfort seating solutions, with a leadership position in seating product areas experiencing growth within the industry, including massage, lumbar, seat heat, and ventilation.

Ray Scott, president and CEO of Lear, stated that integrating Kongsberg Automotive’s Interior Comfort Systems business unit will build on strengths in design, engineering, and operational excellence to advance their strategy of offering a complete suite of premier comfort seat systems to customers and consumers.
The acquisition represents an opportunity for Lear to capture more content-per-vehicle by providing an optimized solution with a better value proposition in terms of cost, quality, and performance.
Lear looks forward to welcoming the talented ICS workforce to its team.
The purchase seeks to advance Lear’s vertical integration and expand product offerings into specialized comfort seating solutions that improve vehicle performance and packaging.
Automakers are looking for greater product differentiation, increased efficiency, and improved performance, especially in the luxury, SUV, and electric vehicle segments.

Frank Orsini, executive vice president and president of seating at Lear, stated that Lear is integrating seating comfort with more features to meet automaker demands and that the acquisition complements their strategic portfolio of vertically integrated design solutions and comfort capabilities.

The Kongsberg ICS business unit brings operational leadership personnel and more than 3,800 employees globally from its production, engineering, and sales facilities throughout Asia, Europe, and North America.
The ICS business unit is expected to generate approximately $300 million in revenue this year.

Joerg Buchheim, president and CEO of Kongsberg Automotive, stated that the transaction provides room for additional financial maneuvering and allows Kongsberg to fully concentrate on its path towards highly focused and profitable growth.
The transaction is subject to regulatory approvals and other customary closing conditions and adjustments.
The acquisition is expected to close in the first quarter of 2022.
source_title: Lear Corp. Acquires Interior Comfort Systems Unit for $203M - DBusiness Magazine

facts: Lehigh Technologies Inc. is based in Tucker, Georgia.
The company announced the completion of $16 million in financing on August 21, 2012.
The financing was led by Leaf Clean Energy and included investments from Kleiner Perkins Caufield Byers, Index Ventures, and NGP Energy Technology Partners.
The funding will be used to expand the company's geographic reach and research and development initiatives for its micronized rubber powder (MRP) technology.
Lehigh's MRP technology transforms end-of-life tire and post-industrial rubber into new materials.
These materials are incorporated into high-performance tires, consumer and industrial plastics goods, asphalt, coatings, and construction materials.
The MRPs serve as a lower cost, sustainable replacement for higher priced synthetic polymers and associated rubber compounds.
Lehigh's customer base includes the largest tire companies in the world.
Over 140 million tires have been manufactured using Lehigh's MRP.
CEO Dr. Alan Barton stated that support from financing partners enables continued growth and major advancements in MRP technology.
The company is working with customers to support higher levels of MRP use and developing new end uses for the sustainable material.

Ellen Pao of Kleiner Perkins Caufield Byers, who serves as a board member of Lehigh Technologies, noted that the investment highlights the company's ability to deliver technology-based innovation and create new market opportunities.
Leaf Clean Energy Vice President Matthew Fedors stated that industrial customers are motivated by sustainable materials that do not sacrifice quality, safety, cost, or performance.
Lehigh uses energy efficient cryogenic turbo mill technology to break down raw materials into micron scale powders.
The company is privately held.
Leaf Clean Energy is a renewable energy and sustainable technology investment firm listed on the London Stock Exchange's Alternative Investment Market under the symbol LEAF.
Kleiner Perkins Caufield Byers was founded in 1972 and focuses investments in digital, greentech, and life sciences from offices in Silicon Valley, Beijing, and Shanghai.

links.context: Kleiner Perkins Caufield Byers company information

source_title: Lehigh Technologies Raises $16M

facts: Atlanta, Ga.-based Lehigh Technologies produces sustainable raw materials from end-of-life tires and post-industrial rubber.
Lehigh Technologies entered a joint venture with HERA Holding, a European waste resources company, to build the first micronized rubber powder (MRP) production facility in Europe.
The joint venture, named Lehigh Spain, will construct the MRP plant as a HERA facility in Spain.
The new facility will service Russia and the entire regions of Europe, the Middle East, and Africa.

Lehigh Technologies’ MRP is a sustainable raw material made from old tires and other post-industrial rubber that can be repurposed back into a tire and other goods to replace oil- and rubber-based materials.
The proprietary process grinds the material down so small that it has the consistency of flour.
The MRP plant will utilize proprietary technology Lehigh Technologies has been using in the U.S. since 2006.
Construction of the plant is currently underway, with first shipments expected by the end of the first quarter 2017.
Dr. Alan Barton, CEO of Lehigh Technologies, stated that HERA’s shared vision for creating sustainable solutions makes them an ideal partner for Lehigh’s next phase of international expansion.
Dr. Alan Barton noted strong market reception for MRP as companies learn that micronized rubber powder delivers a strong value proposition, marking a critical step to meet rapidly growing demand.
The company currently offers a range of repurposed tire products, including PolyDyne, MicroDyne and EkoDyne.
Lehigh Technologies ships its products to more than 18 countries.
source_title: Lehigh Technologies Bringing Repurposed Rubber Tech to Europe - Tire Review Magazine

facts: Horizon Technology Finance led a $15 million venture loan facility for Lehigh Technologies, Inc.

Horizon Technology Finance provides capital in the form of secured loans to venture capital backed companies in the technology, life science, healthcare information and services, and cleantech industries.
Lehigh Technologies is a specialty materials company that produces micronized rubber powders (MRP).
MRP is described as a lower cost, high-performance, sustainable material.
MRP can be used in applications such as tires, asphalt, coatings, and polyurethanes.
Lehigh Technologies is backed by venture capital firms including Kleiner, Perkins, Caufield & Byers, Index Ventures, and Leaf Clean Energy Company.
Lehigh Technologies has recently expanded into Europe.
Lehigh will use the loan proceeds for working capital and capital expenditures.
Gerald A. Michaud is the President of Horizon Technology Finance.
Dr. Alan Barton is the CEO of Lehigh Technologies.
Lehigh's proprietary manufacturing technology converts waste rubber into micronized rubber powders.
The loan facility will support Lehigh's global expansion initiatives and manufacturing process upgrades.
Dr. Alan Barton stated that Lehigh aims to help customers reduce costs and achieve sustainability goals through its materials.
source_title: Horizon Technology Finance Leads $15MM Venture Loan for Lehigh Technologies - News | ABL Advisor

facts: Linde + Wiemann is a German-based automotive supplier that established and expanded its US manufacturing operations through a multi-phase facility designed to support long-term growth.
The project utilized a design-build partnership approach, collaborating closely with the owner, plant manager, and contractors to align scope, budget, and schedule.
Phase 1 delivered 44,300 SF of production, warehouse, and distribution space.
Phase 1 included a 7,000 SF two-story office building.
The facility was designed to support a larger multi-phase buildout totaling approximately 290,000 SF.
The total buildout plan includes a future freestanding office building.
Phase 2 expanded and renovated an existing 51,333 SF facility to support Linde + Wiemann’s growing manufacturing operations.
Phase 2 increased the building size to more than 138,000 SF.
The expansion created additional space for metal stamping, welding, storage, and distribution.
Phase 2 introduced new manufacturing and warehouse areas while upgrading office and utility spaces to better support daily operations.
A mezzanine level was added for maintenance storage during Phase 2.
Future expansions are planned for the site.
The design and construction were carefully coordinated to prevent disruptions to Phase 1 operations during future expansions.
The design-build team maintained a highly coordinated process from concept through construction, enabling rapid decision-making and minimizing delays.
The initial phase was completed within a seven-month construction schedule.
The project demonstrated the team’s ability to deliver complex industrial facilities on time and within budget.
source_title: Linde Wiemann Manufacturing Expansion | Automotive Production

facts: JBM Group is an Indian auto component major.
JBM Group has acquired a majority stake in Germany's Linde-Wiemann GmbH KG.
The acquisition is being executed through JBM Group's flagship company, Neel Metal Products.
The deal will provide JBM with access to global platforms and German technology inheritance for its Indian operations.
JBM did not disclose the financial value of the acquisition deal.
Linde-Wiemann (L+W) generates revenue of USD 700 million.
L+W operates 17 plants across eight countries: Germany, US, China, Spain, Turkey, Czech Republic, Hungary, and South Africa.
L+W employs over 2,300 people globally.
JBM Group Executive Director Nishant Arya stated the acquisition is a strategic fit perfectly in sync with their existing scope of operations and solutions.
Arya noted the acquisition will further consolidate JBM's global footprint.
JBM intends to leverage L+W's European experience combined with its own auto component expertise.
The combined entity will focus on developing new products that improve vehicle safety and performance through light-weighting.
The combination aims to minimize time to market for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).
The combination will offer an integrated one-stop solution to customers.
Through the acquisition, JBM gains access to L+W's capabilities in electric vehicle components and systems.
Through the acquisition, JBM gains access to L+W's capabilities in battery structure and systems.
Linde-Wiemann CEO Ulrich Schoof stated the new business model will allow L+W to unlock substantial growth by adapting product competencies in chassis and suspension.
Schoof added that L+W will extend its holistic system understanding in e-mobility through knowledge exchange with JBM's electrical bus division.
Linde-Wiemann is headquartered in Dillenburg, Germany.
L+W specializes in hot forming, tubular assemblies, and the use of alternate materials like aluminium for auto system light-weighting.
L+W supplies parts to global automotive OEMs including BMW, Volkswagen, Audi, Skoda, Porsche, Daimler, Ford, and PSA.
source_title: JBM Group Acquires Majority Stake in Germany's Linde-Wiemann | Business News - News18

facts: LINDE + WIEMANN operates approximately 2,300 dedicated employees across 18 production facilities in 8 countries on 4 continents.
The company produces structural components for the vehicle construction industry, including Sills, A- and B-Pillars, and structural components in the Front End, Cockpit, and Chassis fields.
LINDE + WIEMANN utilizes stamping, profile, hot forming, and joining technology to deliver crash-relevant body-in-white components to Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs).
LINDE + WIEMANN US Inc. operates a facility located at 1720 Knox Bridge Crossing Rd, 30553 Lavonia / GA – US.
The Georgia facility covers an area of 4,100 m².
The company's products are designed to enhance vehicle safety while simultaneously reducing their overall weight.
LINDE + WIEMANN has been a trusted partner with OEMs in the international automobile industry for over 85 years.
The company's long-term sustainability goal is to achieve CO2-neutrality by 2039.
LINDE + WIEMANN aligns its climate targets with the Paris Convention's goal of limiting temperature increase to 1.5 degrees Celsius.
Environmental and energy management systems are certified under ISO 14001 and 50001, with annual external audits.
A Sustainability Committee comprising ESG managers drives corporate ESG initiatives and ensures compliance with ethical and legal requirements.
The company aims to reach 100% supplier compliance with its Supplier Code of Conduct and Ethics by 2028, aligning with the German Act on Corporate Due Diligence Obligations in Supply Chains (LkSG).
LINDE + WIEMANN operates a whistleblower platform operated by EQS Group AG for anonymous reporting of misconduct and risks related to human rights and the environment.
The company's governance model is guided by the Declaration of Human Rights and United Nations Global Compact.
source_title: Environmental Social Governance - Linde + Wiemann

Jones Day advised Resilience Capital Partners LLC and Linsalata Capital Partners in their joint acquisition of the assets.

The acquired assets include Belmor Products, Inc., Autotron Accessories, Inc., Deflecta-Shield Accessories Inc., Ventshade Accessories Division, and Nifty Products.

Uses a variety of yarn systems, substrate fabrics, coating applications, and innovative manufacturing techniques.
Supplies carpet and molded components used in many of today's automobiles.

facts: CHEP USA, an industry leader in pallet and container pooling solutions and a part of Brambles Limited, will relocate its headquarters to Atlanta.
The company plans to bring 173 jobs to the new facility over the next five years.
Deal stated that Georgia has many natural benefits and other strategic resources that make the state an attractive location for companies in the logistics industry.
CHEP manages pallet and container supply chain logistics for its customers in the consumer goods, produce, meat, home improvement, beverage, raw materials, petro-chemical, and automotive industries.
The company is a recognized leader in pallet and container pooling services for well-known brands.

CHEP partners with a myriad of companies to develop pallet and container management solutions that ensure reduced product damage, offer enhanced delivery efficiencies, eliminate waste, and reduce supply chain costs.

President of CHEP USA Kim Rumph stated the company is delighted to join the Atlanta business community, citing excellent proximity to many key customers, advantages in recruiting and attracting talent, and an outstanding quality of life for relocating employees.
The CHEP USA headquarters operations will be located in office space in Dunwoody.
The facility will also serve as the base for other leaders from the Americas region and global functions of the pallets business segment.
The space will also serve as a Brambles regional office with members of the Brambles IT leadership team.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) collaborated with the City of Dunwoody and the Metro Atlanta Chamber to secure this business opportunity for Georgia.
GDEcD senior project manager Lauren Salas oversaw this project on behalf of the state.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Chris Cummiskey stated that in addition to the metro Atlanta region's presence as a global logistics hub, the area is also ripe with talent and other significant resources for headquarters operations.
CHEP expects the facility to be operational by the fall of 2012.
CHEP is actively recruiting for open positions in the Atlanta area.
source_title: Logistics provider's new facility in Georgia to create 173 jobs

facts: Magna Powertrain is a major American-based manufacturer of transmission and drivetrain systems.
It operates as a division of the larger Canadian company Magna International.
It was formed from multiple subsidiaries and acquisitions.
In 1988, Magna formed Tesma International to manufacture engine and transmission parts.
Tesma was spun off into a public company in 1995 while retaining affiliation with Magna.
In 2001, Magna founded Magna Steyr through an acquisition to engineer, develop and assemble automobiles for other companies on a contractual basis.

In April 2004, Magna transferred the powertrain operations of Magna Steyr to a new subsidiary called Magna Drivetrain to focus on engineering and assembly of chassis, driveline subsystems, and modules.
Magna stated the new company would better address the growing importance of all-wheel drive powertrains.
In September 2004, Magna acquired the global operations of New Venture Gear from DaimlerChrysler for an estimated price of $435 million and integrated it with Magna Drivetrain.
The acquisition helped establish the newly formed Magna Drivetrain group as a major supplier of four-wheel and all-wheel drive systems manufactured in Europe and North America.
Tesma was privatized again by Magna in December 2004.
In 2005, Magna merged Magna Drivetrain with Tesma to form Magna Powertrain.
In July 2015, Magna Powertrain acquired Getrag, a major transmission manufacturer, for $1.9 billion.
The acquisition gave Magna Powertrain access to Getrag's transmission systems, including recent hybrid designs.

Major customers include General Motors (GMC, Buick, Cadillac, Chevrolet), Mercedes-Benz Group, Tata (Jaguar), BMW (BMW, MINI), Ferrari, Stellantis North America (Jeep, Chrysler, Dodge, Ram), Stellantis Europe (Fiat, Alfa-Romeo), and Ford (Ford, Lincoln).
Magna Powertrain is divided into two manufacturing groups: transmission systems and driveline systems.
The transmission systems group was formed in 2016 from the acquisition of Getrag.
The transmission systems group produces manual, dual-clutch, and hybrid transmissions formerly produced by Getrag.
The driveline systems group produces four-wheel drive and all-wheel drive driveline systems.
They produce transverse and longitudinal drivetrain layouts for a variety of vehicle types.
The company also has a metal-forming group and an engineering services group.
Magna Powertrain has manufacturing plants in Canada, Mexico, the United States, Austria, France, Germany, Italy, Slovakia, China, India, and South Korea.
Magna Powertrain also has offices not located within a manufacturing plant in France, Germany, China, India, Japan, and South Korea.

In November 2006, Magna Powertrain and Amtek Auto Ltd. signed a 50-50 joint venture to establish a manufacturing facility outside of New Delhi for two-piece flexplate assemblies for automotive applications.
In October 2007, Magna Powertrain and RICO Auto Industries Ltd signed a 50-50 joint venture to establish a new manufacturing facility located in Gurgaon.
The Gurgaon facility produces oil and water pumps with aluminum housings for automotive engine applications for Indian and European markets.
In January 2009, WIA Corporation and Magna Powertrain formed a 50-50 joint venture to establish a new manufacturing facility located in Asan, Korea.
The Asan facility produces and supplies all-wheel-drive couplings for Hyundai Motor Group.
In July 2021, LG and Magna Powertrain formed an LG majority (51%-49%) joint venture to establish LG Magna e-Powertrain in Incheon, South Korea.
The goal of the LG Magna e-Powertrain joint venture is to manufacture electric motors, inverters, on-board chargers, and other "e-drive systems."
source_title: Magna Powertrain - Wikipedia

facts: Grupo Antolin is a private automotive company founded in 1950 with headquarters in Burgos, Spain.
The company operates 150 locations across 25 countries as of 2019.
Ernesto Antolín serves as Chairman.
Grupo Antolin manufactures automotive interior parts including headliner substrates, overheads, doors, lighting, cockpits, consoles, and hard and soft trim.
The company reported revenue of M€ 5,037 in 2017.
As of 2018, Grupo Antolin employed 28,000 people globally.
On 31 August 2015, the company announced the acquisition of Magna Interiors, the interiors business of Magna International.

Grupo Antolin acquired an automobile parts manufacturing plant in Nashville, Illinois, originally established in 1987 as Ligma Corporation, a joint venture between Magna International and Lignotock of West Germany.
The company won the Castilla y León Prize for Scientific and Technical Research and Innovation in 2017, awarded by the Junta de Castilla y León.
In 2018, Grupo Antolin opened its 15th plant in Shelby, Michigan, dedicated to manufacturing door panels for RAM vans of the FCA group.
The company's American workforce totaled 5,700 people as of 2018.
An additional plant was announced to open in Alabama.
In May 2022, Ramón Sotomayor served as chief executive officer.
source_title: Grupo Antolin - Wikipedia

facts: Workers at Bridgewater Interiors in Warren, Michigan, members of UAW Local 400, voted Tuesday to ratify a four-year contract the United Auto Workers bureaucracy rushed through without giving the factory’s 1,000 workers the chance to see the full agreement or study its details.
The UAW stated in a social media post Wednesday that workers “voted by 80 percent to ratify a new contract that secures a top rate of $35 an hour by 2030 among other major gains...”
The deal includes a starting wage of only $20 an hour, which would top out at $29 in four years.
Due to high turnover rates, many workers would remain on the low-wage tier for extended periods.
According to the union's own contract “highlights,” workers would not make $35 an hour until 2033—three years after the contract expires.
Inflation is running at 4.2 percent annually, which will wipe out much of any wage increase.
In May, workers previously voted down a UAW-backed deal by 95 percent.
The existing contract expired on May 15 before being extended behind workers' backs by UAW International and Local 400 officials who ignored an overwhelming vote to authorize a strike.
Union officials dangled a $2,000 signing bonus and front-loaded pay increases for lower-seniority workers to exploit economic distress.
The plant's workers produce seats and interiors for Big Three vehicles, including the Ford F-150 and Dodge Ram pickup trucks.
The majority of workers make $21 an hour according to the UAW.
Many workers are hired through temp agencies and earn so little they cannot afford a car.
Workers travel by bus or Uber to reach the plant due to low wages.
One worker stated, “They’ve got this signing bonus, but they are going to take half of it away with taxes and union dues.”
Another worker noted the contract takes away emergency vacation days needed for families.
Workers demanded $35 an hour immediately rather than in five or six years.
A veteran with 23 years of tenure said he is just making $26 an hour and recalled when the company was owned by Johnson Controls.
The company was spun off from Johnson Controls in 2016.
Bridgewater Interiors generates an estimated $2 billion in annual revenue.
CEO Ronald E. Hall Jr. has reportedly taken home $50 million over the past three years.
UAW President Shawn Fain pushed the agreement in a livestream, declaring it ends treating IPS (Independents, Parts and Suppliers) like second-class citizens.
Fain's pay raise brings his annual compensation to nearly $300,000, roughly seven times what a Bridgewater worker earns.
The UAW bureaucracy is attempting to prevent a strike that would disrupt Big Three production and profits.
Fain is aligned with the Trump administration’s trade war policies and increased military production to suppress opposition to living costs and automation-driven job cuts.
Supporters of the WSWS Autoworker Newsletter distributed hundreds of copies of an article on the snap vote at the factory gate Tuesday.
One worker noted that using FMLA days would now come out of vacation time or paid days off.
Another worker pointed out there is no COLA (Cost-of-Living Allowance) and no retro pay back to the contract’s expiration.
Workers lost three personal days under the new agreement.
A worker stated that if they were to strike now, it would shut down a lot of the auto industry.
Another worker denounced the snap vote, noting they got their first glance of the offer yesterday and were asked to vote today.
Contract talks for roughly 3,000 Dana workers under a UAW master wage agreement resume July 7 in Cincinnati, Ohio.

Dana workers across the Midwest and South voted down the first contract in June, led by massive “no” votes of 90 percent or more at Toledo Driveline, Warren Axle, Paris, Tennessee and Columbia, Missouri.
The UAW refused to grant strike authorization for Dana workers and instead extended the contract despite a decisive strike mandate.
Separate United Steelworkers talks over the Fort Wayne, Indiana plant continue; those workers rejected their contract by 158–15.
Under the rejected Dana agreement, new-hire pay is frozen at $20 an hour, rising to just $25 after four years, with senior pay capped at $28.
The Dana contract offered no relief from rotating 12-hour shifts or mandatory weekend work.
Kamara Bond, who worked at the Warren, Michigan facility, was fired twice for reporting unsafe conditions including chemical exposures, high temperatures, and poor ventilation.
Dana reported $102 million in net income in 2025 on $7.5 billion in global sales.
CEO R. Bruce McDonald took home $5.61 million in compensation while the company spent $650 million on stock buybacks—about 23 percent of outstanding stock.
source_title: “It’s hard to save for a car with the wages we’re getting”: Widespread opposition as UAW pushes through second sellout deal at Bridgewater Interiors - World Socialist Web Site

facts: Magna International is constructing a new seat manufacturing plant in Spartanburg County, South Carolina.
The facility represents the company's fourth manufacturing plant in South Carolina.
Magna International is projected to invest $29 million in the facility.
The project will create 480 new jobs over the next five years.
Magna International is headquartered in Aurora, Ontario, Canada.
The company operates facilities across the globe and employs approximately 155,000 people worldwide.
Magna's product capabilities include producing automobile body, chassis, exterior, seating, powertrain, electronic, vision, closure, and roof systems and modules, as well as contract manufacturing.
President of Magna Seating Mike Bisson stated the project marks a new chapter in the ongoing collaboration between Magna and BMW.
Mike Bisson noted that this is Magna's first seating business award with BMW.
The new plant is located in the Tyger River Industrial Park on Highway 290.
The facility will be 230,000 square feet.
The seating plant is expected to be operational in June 2017.
The facility will feature dedicated assembly and sequencing lines to supply seats to BMW’s nearby assembly plant.
Magna currently has three manufacturing locations in South Carolina.
Magna currently employs approximately 1,450 people in the state of South Carolina.
The Coordinating Council for Economic Development has approved job development credits related to this project.
The S.C. Department of Commerce is South Carolina’s leading economic development agency.
The S.C. Department of Commerce works to recruit new businesses and help existing businesses grow in the state.
The S.C. Department of Commerce has recruited world-class companies to South Carolina including BMW, Boeing, Continental, Giti Tire, LPL Financial Holdings, Mercedes-Benz Vans, Toray, and Volvo Cars.
source_title: Magna International constructing fourth manufacturing plant in South Carolina - Who's On The Move

facts: Bank of America-Merrill Lynch upgraded shares of Magna International stock to a "buy" rating and assigned a $63 price target.
Merrill Lynch analysts noted that Magna possesses expertise in numerous automotive component systems made for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).
Magna's knowledge of how automotive parts fit together could afford the company more opportunities in contract manufacturing with traditional OEMs along with new industry entrants.

Magna has a unique competitive advantage over other car parts makers, permitting it to build entire production-ready models of cars, including electric cars, for other manufacturers using its proprietary technology, expertise, and trusted reputation.
Motley Fool automotive specialist John Rosevear described Magna as "the world's only high-quality contract auto manufacturer."
Magna holds contracts to build BMW's new 530e plug-in hybrid vehicle.
Magna holds contracts to build Jaguar's new I-PACE all-electric SUV.

Merrill Lynch views Magna's ability to build production-ready models as setting the company ahead of competition and positioning it as one of the best-positioned suppliers over the long-term to continue winning business and growing revenues ahead of market growth.
Magna International generated $1.7 billion in positive free cash flow over the past 12 months.
Magna reported $2.1 billion in net income under GAAP standards.
Magna is valued at $19.3 billion, giving it a price-to-free-cash-flow ratio of 11.4.
Analysts expect Magna to grow profits at about 10% annually over the next five years, with Merrill Lynch believing growth may exceed this expectation.
Magna stock pays a market average 2% dividend yield with a 19% payout ratio that could be expanded.
The author estimates the stock offers about a 12% total return, potentially more.
Magna carries approximately $2.8 billion in net debt after accounting for cash on hand.
Magna's enterprise value-to-free-cash-flow ratio stands at 13.
source_title: This Just In: Magna International Stock Upgraded | The Motley Fool

facts: MinebeaMitsumi Sales Europe GmbH, Minebea Slovakia s.r.o., Minebea Intec GmbH, myonic GmbH, Paradox Engineering SA, CEROBEAR GmbH, S.A.S. Mach Aero, NMB-Minebea U.K. Ltd., U-Shin Ltd., RO-RA Aviation Systems GmbH, Mast Kunststoffe GmbH, and MinebeaMitsumi Technology Center Europe GmbH form a strong European Network.
The network locations with various core competencies form a strong partner for the development of applications in the automotive sector, industry, health care, aerospace, or medical technology.
Central sales office MinebeaMitsumi Sales Europe GmbH is located close to Frankfurt in Neu-Isenburg.
The central sales office's available product portfolio covers all MinebeaMitsumi products.
Minebea Slovakia s.r.o. in Košice in eastern Slovakia is the newest and largest European production facility in the MinebeaMitsumi Group.
The Slovakian facility spans 20,500 square meters and will have up to 70 production lines in operation by 2022.
The Slovakian facility primarily manufactures drive systems for the automotive sector and industry.
The largest motor development center of the MinebeaMitsumi Group is located in Villingen-Schwenningen.
More than 500 employees at the Villingen-Schwenningen center develop innovative drive technologies, including electronically commutated brushless DC motors and energy harvesting systems.
Minebea Intec GmbH is a leading manufacturer of industrial weighing and inspection technologies.
Minebea Intec GmbH has its headquarters in Hamburg, Germany, and has offered products and services for over 150 years.
Minebea Intec's product portfolio includes platform scales, load cells, vessel and silo scales, X-Ray inspection systems, and user-friendly software solutions.
myonic GmbH is an international industrial company with branch offices in Germany, the Czech Republic, and Austria.

myonic has over 80 years of experience manufacturing precision miniature ball bearings for advanced technologies in the medical and automotive industries, precision engineering, mechanical engineering, and aerospace technology.

Paradox Engineering was established in 2005, is headquartered in Switzerland, and designs pioneering technologies to implement unique, interoperable, and highly scalable network infrastructures for industrial and urban applications.
CEROBEAR is the leading manufacturer of ceramic roller bearings and a pioneer in roller bearing technology.
Ceramic roller bearings are a key product for industries and processes with extreme requirements, such as the semiconductor and aerospace industries, as well as motor sports.
Mach Aero started its career as a grinding specialist mainly for the automotive industry.
Mach Aero is now a leading company for the manufacture of spherical bearings and rod ends, and proposes high-precision machined parts and sub-assemblies mainly for the aerospace industry.
NMB-Minebea U.K., located in Lincoln, is the world leader in the design and manufacture of bearing technologies in sectors where precision, speed, and reliability are essential.
NMB-Minebea U.K.'s core competence includes landing gear bearings, aerospace bearings, and special vehicle suspension bearings for customers in Europe as well as overseas.
Minebea AccessSolutions Inc. supplies the automotive industry worldwide with high-quality and innovative access and control systems that ensure increased safety while maintaining comfort.
Minebea AccessSolutions was founded in 1926 and has a long-standing tradition among Tier-1 automotive suppliers.
Minebea AccessSolutions Deutschland GmbH is located in Erdweg near Munich and serves as a center of competence in the fields of mechatronics and access systems.

facts: Nidec Elesys Americas is based in Suwanee, Georgia.
The company is a prominent player in the automotive electronics manufacturing sector.
Nidec Elesys Americas has a reported annual revenue of $80.8 million.
The company is part of the globally recognized Nidec Group.
Elesys Americas focuses on designing and manufacturing sensors and modules for advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS).
Its facility in Suwanee, Georgia, is a critical part of a global supply chain that includes R&D functions, testing labs, and logistic centers.
The company's product line includes occupant detection systems crucial for airbag deployment accuracy.
The company's product line includes seatbelt reminder systems.
The company's product line includes electronic control units (ECUs) that run safety diagnostics.
The company's product line includes weight sensors to detect passenger size and position.
The company's product line includes battery monitoring systems for electric vehicles.
As of 2023, NHTSA regulations are pushing more stringent rules around driver-assistance and passenger protection systems.
According to a 2022 NHTSA report, seatbelts saved over 14,000 lives in the U.S. alone.
Roughly 2,500 people still die annually because they weren't buckled up.
Elesys offers smart battery management ECU solutions that monitor real-time battery performance to ensure longer lifespan and safer operations for hybrid and electric vehicles.
Direct clients often include Tier 1 suppliers and original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).
Components must meet global safety standards like ISO 26262 and IATF 16949.
Elesys components can be mass-produced while maintaining strict tolerances.
Their ECUs and sensors integrate seamlessly with other systems due to modular design.
Backed by the Nidec brand, Elesys parts are tested for consistent performance across different geographies and climates.
Elesys works closely with automotive partners to tweak existing technologies for use in future patterns, like autonomous driving.
In Suwanee, the company hosts one of the region's most well-regarded testing labs for passive safety technologies.
Engineers carefully simulate crash scenarios, occupant movements, and sensor reliability under various conditions.
Elesys conducts vibration testing to multi-year component lifecycle stress simulations.
This significantly reduces failure rates and ensures compliance with automaker expectations and government safety standards.
Although part of the global Nidec family headquartered in Japan, Elesys Americas operates semi-independently to better respond to North American market needs.
Their Suwanee HQ handles not just manufacturing but also sales and regional support.
This nearshore model helps reduce lead times, enhance communication with U.S. clients, and quickly adapt designs to meet changes in American laws or consumer preferences.
Elesys engineers in Georgia worked directly with a Michigan-based EV maker to incorporate customized battery sensor modules.
The result was a successful launch within a shorter development cycle—months ahead of competitor timelines.
The company aligns itself closely with the Japanese philosophy of Kaizen or continuous improvement.
Employees undergo routine training on new testing techniques, industry best practices, and customer requirements.
The company invests heavily in machine upgrades, automation, and quality management systems.
Elesys's defect rate is among the lowest in the sector according to Auto Alliance benchmarks.
Demand for autonomous vehicles (AVs) is rising and EV sales are climbing.
Elesys is preparing for the transition by investing in sensor fusion technologies that combine LiDAR, radar, and camera input.
Elesys is developing redundant safety systems for Level 4 and Level 5 autonomy.
Elesys is forming partnerships with AI firms to integrate predictive analytics into safety modules.
source_title: Driving Automotive Safety Innovation At Elesys Americas | Distrilist

facts: Nidec Group operates a global network of more than 300 companies in 43 countries.
Nidec Group designs and manufactures a comprehensive range of motors, from small ones found in computers to large ones used in manufacturing equipment.
Nidec Elesys develops, manufactures, and sells automotive safety systems, such as electronic control units (ECU) for electric power steering and vehicle stability assist systems.
Nidec Elesys pioneers advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) for Honda and other OEMs.
Nidec Elesys Americas Corp. is the American subsidiary of Nidec Elesys.
Nidec Elesys Americas Corp. has made significant strides in producing for the North American automotive market.
Nidec Elesys Americas Corp. has won several awards for excellence in quality and delivery.
Nidec Elesys Americas Corp. President Toshihiko Kono stated that the company strives to exceed customer expectations by creating safer, more comfortable mobility.
The company has increased investment in research and development to address advanced automobile industry technology and stricter market demands.
The company believes innovative products for vehicle electrification and autonomous vehicles will result in loyal customers and business expansion.
Nidec Group aims to be the world’s best in “everything that spins and moves” and designs next-generation drive technologies.
source_title: The growth prospects of Nidec Elesys Americas Corp. | The Japan Times

facts: NIFCO KTW is hiring for multiple Molding Process Technician positions in Stephens County.
Compensation for the technician roles is based on experience.
The application deadline for the positions was November 21, 2022.
Technicians are responsible for basic mold setup and molding process operations, including independent setup, startup, and maintenance of tooling.
Responsibilities include performing mold changes and checking auxiliary equipment such as dryers, thermolators, and chillers to ensure they are working and filters are clean.
Technicians must submit work order requests for equipment maintenance and handle the startup and shutdown of presses.
The role requires cleaning and maintaining molds in the area and submitting work orders when starting up a job.
Employees must review job requirements with personnel responsible for running the job and respond to all corrective actions as quickly as possible.
Technicians must keep all processes up to date, recording reasons for changes, hours, and codes on the molding log if changes are made.
The position allows technicians to adjust production parameters within tolerance specifications as permitted and authorized.
Employees are required to report any safety issues or concerns to the molding manager and the maintenance manager.
Technicians must understand current company quality guidelines and report changes in production sequence, workplace design, or parameters.
The role involves checking and verifying that correct mold changes are completed accurately by the mold setter.
Employees must review production releases and ensure accurate completion by the due date.
Technicians are responsible for supporting the implementation of the defined 5S standard and adhering to all safety guidelines outlined by OSHA and Nifco KTW.
The position requires taking responsibility for equipment and operator safety, as well as assisting in the training of new employees.
Technicians must assist in troubleshooting and identifying defects, implementing solutions, and accurately completing paperwork or entering data into a computer.
Employees must continuously participate in improving processes and procedures and forward suggestions for improvement to the shift supervisor and manager.
Technicians are required to immediately inform the shift supervisor about detected quality issues and malfunctions within their work area.
Candidates must have completed Mold Set Up training, Mold Processing and Troubleshooting courses, or possess equivalent experience.
Applicants need knowledge of injection molding and operations, along with the ability to work with all auxiliary equipment including dryers, thermolators, and chillers.
The technician serves as a technical go-to person for associates in the production area.
Employees participate in implementing new production plans and technologies, including validation of new molds.
Technicians are responsible for maintaining cleanliness of their work area and ensuring compliance with order and cleanliness regulations throughout the company.
The role requires good verbal communication between departments and shifts to support the shift supervisor in ensuring all regulations regarding order and cleanliness are complied with.

links.context: Contact phone for Crystal Arnold/Todd Tatum

source_title: NIFCO KTW – Stephens County Development Authority

facts: Founded in Japan in 1967 as a global leader in plastic fasteners and precision components supporting the automotive industry.
Strategic international expansion began in the 1980s, establishing a robust network of production and research facilities across continents.
Aims to enter new strategic markets and increase international sales, targeting a 20% increase by 2025.
Emerging markets in Asia and South America are projected to contribute approximately ¥31.2 billion to revenue by 2025.
Has major global subsidiaries across the USA, Mexico, UK, Poland, Germany, Korea, Taiwan, China, Thailand, Malaysia, Vietnam, and India.
Pursuing new business initiatives focused on solving social issues and expanding technology applications to sectors like home appliances, office equipment, fashion, and sports.
International markets contributed 40% of total sales in 2023.
Investing in strategic partnerships to drive future growth, including leveraging plastic's advantages and collaborating on advanced automation solutions.
Developed school-oriented solutions called 'PLUSchool®' to address labor shortages and promote digital transformation in education.
Expanded technology applications to home appliances, office equipment, fashion, and sports by capitalizing on the benefits of plastic materials.
Made an investment in September 2024 in LexxPluss to jointly develop 'Automation solutions for automotive maintenance.'
Invests approximately $30 million annually in R&D, focusing on enhancing product efficiency and reducing environmental impact.
R&D expenditures increased by approximately 25% between 2020 and 2023.
Developing new automotive products to address safety, comfort, and environmental concerns, including lightweight materials and sustainable processes.
Expanding its product line to include components for electric vehicles (EVs) and advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS).
Focusing development efforts on products that meet stringent environmental regulations for fuel tanks and EVs.
Achieved mass production of packing-less bumper retainers, creating a watertight structure without rubber packing.
Exploring innovations outside the automotive sector by leveraging plastic component expertise in new markets.
The 'PLUSchool®' initiative utilizes battery-less devices for educational settings.
Began demonstration tests for a 'Heatstroke Prevention System for Workplaces' in July 2025.
Extending plastic component expertise to household equipment, consumer electronics, and sports industries.
Transformed automobile manufacturing by replacing metal parts with lighter, non-rusting plastic alternatives.
Maintains an 'AAA' rating in MSCI ESG Ratings, integrating sustainability into its core innovation and growth strategy.
For the fiscal year ending March 31, 2025, reported consolidated net sales of ¥353,038 million, a 5% decrease year-over-year.
Operating profit for FY2024 increased by 12% to ¥49,200 million.
Ordinary profit for FY2024 reached ¥52,147 million, with profit attributable to owners of the parent increasing to ¥44,767 million.
Projects sales of ¥348.00 billion for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026, a -1.4% change.
Anticipates operating profit of ¥49.50 billion for FY2026, a +0.6% increase.
Operating profit margin in the first half of FY2024 surpassed 14%, with a strategic aim to maintain an operating profit ratio exceeding 14% for FY2025.
Revised FY2025 forecasts upward due to improved cost efficiencies and strategic divestitures.
Updated fiscal year ending March 31, 2025 financial projections, anticipating that net sales, operating income, ordinary profit, and net profit will surpass initial estimates.

Positive revision partly attributed to the transfer of businesses like Nifco Germany GmbH and Nifco KTW America Corporation due to challenges in the German OEM sector, alongside improvements in variable costs.
Announced an increased year-end dividend for FY2024 of ¥40 per share, bringing the total annual dividend to ¥75 per share.
Forecast for FY2025 is a dividend of ¥80 per share, a ¥5 increase.
In July 2025, announced a share buyback program intending to repurchase up to 1.56% of its shares, valued at ¥5 billion.
As of July 25, 2025, trailing twelve-month revenue stands at $2.32 billion USD, with a market capitalization of $2.39 billion USD.
Faces risks from intense competition and rapid evolution of EVs and ADAS technologies requiring constant innovation.
Acknowledges a 'great upheaval' in the industry driven by environmental regulations and CASE technologies.
Must adapt its product portfolio quickly to remain relevant amid the automotive sector's shift towards EVs and ADAS.
Evolving environmental and safety standards necessitate ongoing adjustments to product development and manufacturing, potentially increasing R&D and production costs.
Operates over 35 international production plants, making it susceptible to global supply chain disruptions impacting production schedules and delivery commitments.
Securing skilled labor and investing in advanced manufacturing capabilities are crucial for growth, with limitations potentially hindering expansion plans.
Prioritizes resource allocation towards developing products that meet evolving consumer needs for safety, comfort, and environmental sustainability in vehicles.
Demonstrates commitment to risk management through strategic business decisions, such as divesting from challenging markets like the German OEM business.
Cultivates a 'challenge spirit' and 'creativity' to respond flexibly to rapidly changing social and economic landscapes, emphasizing strict compliance and robust risk management frameworks.
Actively positioning itself to capitalize on the shift towards electric vehicles and autonomous driving technologies reshaping the automotive landscape.
source_title: What is Growth Strategy and Future Prospects of Nifco Company? – MatrixBCG.com

facts: Nisshinbo Holdings Inc. is a global manufacturer with a focus on wireless, communications, microdevices, and other electronics businesses.
The company's business is divided into seven segments: wireless communications, microdevices, brakes, precision instruments, chemicals, textiles, and real estate.

Nisshinbo announced it will transfer all outstanding shares of TMD Friction Group S.A. (TMD) and loan receivables against TMD and its subsidiary TMD Friction Holdings GmbH to German company AEQUITA SE & Co. KGaA.
The TMD transfer transaction includes four subsidiaries: TMD Friction Holdings (UK) Limited, TMD Friction UK Limited, TMD Friction do Brasil S.A., and TMD Friction Mexico S.A. de C.V.
The date of the TMD transfer transaction is scheduled to be November 30, 2023.
Nisshinbo is listed on the Prime Market of the Tokyo Stock Exchange as of Dec. 31, 2024.
The Master Trust Bank of Japan holds a 14.91% investment ratio in Nisshinbo.
Fukoku Mutual Life Insurance holds a 5.73% investment ratio in Nisshinbo.
Custody Bank of Japan holds a 4.52% investment ratio in Nisshinbo.
STATE STREET BANK AND TRUST COMPANY holds a 3.76% investment ratio in Nisshinbo.
Teijin Ltd. holds a 2.01% investment ratio in Nisshinbo.
Shikoku Kasei Holdings Corporation holds a 1.65% investment ratio in Nisshinbo.
The Nisshinbo Group Employee Stock Ownership Association holds a 1.48% investment ratio in Nisshinbo.
The Japan Wool Textile Co., Ltd. holds a 1.45% investment ratio in Nisshinbo.
DFA INTL SMALL CAP VALUE PORTFOLIO holds a 1.33% investment ratio in Nisshinbo.
The Nisshinbo Business Partners Stock Ownership Association holds a 1.13% investment ratio in Nisshinbo.
The wireless and communications segment produces communication systems for ADAS, ETC, locators, and GNSS receivers.
The microdevices segment produces electronic devices for car audio, power control units, EPS, BMS, and parking assist.
The brakes segment produces friction materials for brakes.
The precision equipment segment produces EBS valve blocks and molded products for automotive use.
The chemicals segment produces fuel cell separators.
The wireless and communications segment produces high power transformers, reactors, and choke coils as electronics parts for automotive.

These automotive electronics parts are applied to DC-DC converters for 12V output, electric inverters for compressors, EV battery chargers, electric power steering, on-board chargers, and DC/AC inverters.
The wireless and communications segment also produces GPS modules.

The microdevice segment produces ICs and operational amps for automobiles applied to PCU, EPS, BCM, safety/clash avoidance (ADAS), parking assist, entertainment/car navigation/audio, BMS, LED Headlight ECU, V2X, TFT LCD, corner sensor ECU, and center console.
The microdevice segment produces power management ICs for automotive including LDO regulators, watchdog timers, LED controllers, reset ICs, DC-DC converters, and USB switches.
The automobile brakes segment produces brake linings, disc pads, drum brakes, and drum brakes for medium- and heavy-duty trucks.
The automobile brakes segment produces brake components including brake master cylinders, proportioning valves, disc brake calipers, wheel cylinders, and joint brackets.

The precision instruments segment produces molding products such as cross flow fans, Sirocco fans, propeller fans, interior and exterior decorative parts, seat plastic parts, luggage trim, back door trim, pillar garnish, front grill, and wire harness.

The precision instruments segment produces lighting and functional parts including thick wall light guide side turn lenses, lamp outer/inner lenses, lamp LED light guide parts, lamp housings, door mirror power units, meter dial plates, meter panels, connector parts, and HV high-voltage cable protectors.

The precision instruments segment produces precision parts for automobiles including EBS valve blocks, housing for diesel fuel-injection management pumps, cylinders for diesel fuel-injection management pumps, EGR valves, air control valves, supercharged air control valves, exhaust brake valves, intake-air shutter valves, exhaust shutter valves, actuators, check/delay valves, clutch master cylinders, clutch release cylinders, filler caps, fuel cut valves, hoses with check valves, and control valves for automotive steering systems.
The chemicals segment produces bipolar plates for fuel cells.
Nisshinbo Automotive Corporation was established in the U.S.A. in Feb. 1995.
Nisshinbo Automotive Manufacturing Inc. was established in the U.S.A. in Mar. 1997.
In Dec. 2023, Nisshinbo acquired all shares of HVJ Holdings, Inc., making Hitachi Kokusai Electric Inc. a subsidiary as well.
source_title: Nisshinbo Holdings Inc. - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: The company is a global manufacturer with a focus on wireless, communications, microdevices, and other electronics businesses, as well as operations in other fields.
The company's business is divided into seven segments: wireless communications, microdevices, brakes, precision instruments, chemicals, textiles, and real estate.

Nisshinbo announced it will transfer all outstanding shares of TMD Friction Group S.A., a main pillar of its automotive brake business, along with loan receivables against TMD and its subsidiary TMD Friction Holdings GmbH, to German company AEQUITA SE & Co. KGaA.
The transaction includes four TMD subsidiaries: TMD Friction Holdings (UK) Limited, TMD Friction UK Limited, TMD Friction do Brasil S.A., and TMD Friction Mexico S.A. de C.V.
The date of the TMD transaction is scheduled to be November 30, 2023.
The wireless and communications segment produces communication systems for ADAS, ETC, locators, and GNSS receivers.

High power transformers, reactors, and choke coils are applied to DC-DC converters for 12V output, electric inverters for compressors, EV battery chargers, electric power steering, on-board chargers, and DC/AC inverters.
GPS modules are produced as part of the wireless and communications segment.
The microdevices segment produces electronic devices for car audio, power control units, EPS, BMS, and parking assist systems.

ICs and operational amps for automobiles are applied to power control units (PCU), electric power steering (EPS), body control modules (BCM), safety/clash avoidance and prevention (ADAS), parking assist systems, entertainment/car navigation/car audio, battery management systems (BMS), LED headlight ECUs, vehicle-to-everything (V2X), TFT LCDs, corner sensor ECUs, and center consoles.
Power management ICs for automotive applications include LDO regulators, watchdog timers, LED controllers, reset ICs, DC-DC converters, and USB switches.
The automobile brakes segment produces brake linings, disc pads, drum brakes, and drum/brake linings for medium- and heavy-duty trucks.
Brake components produced include brake master cylinders, proportioning valves, disc brake calipers, wheel cylinders, and joint brackets.

Precision instruments division produces molding products such as cross flow fans, Sirocco fans, propeller fans, interior/exterior decorative parts, console parts, seat plastic parts, luggage trim, back door trim, pillar garnish, front grills, wire harnesses, lighting/functional parts, and HV high-voltage cable protectors.

Precision parts for automobiles include EBS valve blocks, housings and cylinders for diesel fuel-injection management pumps, EGR valves, air control valves, supercharged air control valves, exhaust brake valves, intake-air shutter valves, exhaust shutter valves, actuators, check/delay valves, clutch master cylinders, clutch release cylinders, filler caps, fuel cut valves, hoses with check valves, and control valves for automotive steering systems.
The chemicals segment produces bipolar plates for fuel cells and fuel cell separators.
Nisshinbo Automotive Corporation was established in the U.S.A. in February 1995.
Nisshinbo Automotive Manufacturing Inc., a wholly-owned subsidiary, was established in the U.S.A. in March 1997.
The company acquired Enjiasheng Electronic Ltd. in China through a joint investment with Continental Teves AG & Co. oHG in March 2004.
Nisshinbo Brake Inc. and Saeron Automotive Corporation jointly established Nisshinbo Saeron Automotive Changshu Corp. in China in February 2011.
The company purchased all outstanding stock shares in TMD Friction Group S.A. in November 2011, making it a consolidated subsidiary.

In April 2009, the company spun off five business sectors (Textiles, Automobile Brakes, Papers, Mechatronics and Chemicals) and transformed into a holding company, changing its corporate name to Nisshinbo Holdings Inc.
The company is listed on the Prime Market of the Tokyo Stock Exchange as of December 31, 2024.

Major shareholders include The Master Trust Bank of Japan, Ltd. (14.91%), Fukoku Mutual Life Insurance Co. (5.73%), Custody Bank of Japan, Ltd. (4.52%), STATESTREETBANKANDTRUST COMPANY (3.76%), and Teijin Ltd. (2.01%).
The company's history notes the establishment of Hamakita Precision Machine Plant in January 1987.
Kobunsi (Thailand) Ltd. was established in May 1989 and is currently a consolidated subsidiary.
Nisshinbo Somboon Automotive Co., Ltd. was established in Thailand in June 1996 and is currently a consolidated subsidiary.
Searon Automotive Corporation was established in South Korea in March 1999 and is currently a consolidated subsidiary.
The company jointly established Continental Teves with Continental Teves AG & Co. oHG of Germany in December 1999.
Nisshinbo opened its new Toyota plant in January 2005.
Searon Automotive Corp. was listed on the Korea Stock Exchange in October 2005.
The company acquired additional shares in New Japan Radio Co., Ltd. via tender offer in December 2005.
Equity in Japan Radio Co., Ltd. and Nagano Japan Radio Co., Ltd. was increased in December 2006.
The Toyama Plant and Nagoya Plant were closed down in December 2006.
The company acquired 100 percent of Daiwa Shiko Co., Ltd. in July 2007, making it a consolidated subsidiary.
The Harisaki Plant was closed down in July 2007.
Choya Co., Ltd. was made a wholly owned subsidiary via stock swap in July 2007.
Nisshinbo Brake Sales Co., Ltd. was made a wholly owned subsidiary via stock swap in October 2008.
Nisshin Toa, Inc. absorbed Nisshin Tech., Co., Ltd. in October 2008.
Nisshinbo Kikai Hanbai Co., Ltd. was liquidated in October 2008.
Nisshinbo Mobics was liquidated in December 2008.
Nisshinbo Automotive Corporation was dissolved for integration with Nisshinbo Automotive Manufacturing Inc. in December 2009.
Nisshinbo Brake Inc. absorbed Nisshinbo Brake Sales Co., Ltd. in April 2010.
The company acquired additional shares in JRC Nihon Musen through a takeover bid in December 2010, making Nagano Japan Radio Co., Ltd. a subsidiary.
Nisshinbo Business Management (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. was established in March 2012.
Nisshinbo Commercial Vehicle Brake Ltd. (NCB) was established in Thailand in July 2013.
The company acquired all outstanding stock in Nambu Plastics Co., Ltd. in October 2015, making it a consolidated subsidiary.
Japan Radio Co., Ltd. turned Nagano Japan Radio Co., Ltd. and Ueda Japan Radio Co., Ltd. into wholly owned subsidiaries through a stock swap in March 2016.
The company acquired stock shares in Japan Radio Co., Ltd. in October 2017, making it a wholly owned and consolidated subsidiary.
The company acquired stock shares in Ricoh Electronic Devices Co., Ltd. in March 2018.
JRC Mobility Inc. was established in April 2018.
The company acquired stock shares in New Japan Radio Co., Ltd. in September 2018, making it a wholly owned and consolidated subsidiary.
New Japan Radio Co., Ltd. acquired and merged the operations of Ricoh Electronic Device Co., Ltd. and adopted the name Nisshinbo Micro Device Co., Ltd. in January 2022.
The company moved to the Prime Market of the Tokyo Stock Exchange due to TSE reorganization in April 2022.
Nisshinbo Comprehensive Precision Machining (Guragaon) Private, Ltd. was established in September 2022.
In November 2023, the company transferred all shares of TMD FRICTION Group S.A.
In December 2023, the company acquired all shares of HVJ Holdings, Inc., and Hitachi Kokusai Electric Inc. became a subsidiary as a result.

facts: Nivel Parts & Manufacturing Co., LLC announced the acquisition of Snorkel Your ATV LLC on January 17, 2022.
The purchase brings together Nivel’s manufacturing, sourcing, and distribution capabilities with Snorkel Your ATV’s highly regarded product line and new product development expertise.

Kyle Tallman, founder and former owner of Snorkel Your ATV LLC, stated it has been exceptionally enjoyable working with Nivel and expressed respect for the company while looking forward to growing the business further.
Nivel recognizes increasing growth in the UTV and ATV segments.
Through the acquisition of Snorkel Your ATV, Nivel expands its snorkel product line with a well-recognized brand and gains important product insight.

Nivel CEO Brett Hankey commented that they are excited to welcome Snorkel Your ATV into the Nivel family, praising Kyle’s focus on new product development, quality production, and customer-focused sales and service.
Founded in 1968, Nivel is a global manufacturer, distributor, and seller of aftermarket parts and accessories for niche vehicles and heavy-duty equipment.
Nivel is a trusted business partner to thousands of dealers, distributors, and consumers in the US and international markets.
Nivel owns the most valuable brands in the aftermarket niche vehicle parts and accessories categories: MadJax, Red Dot, GTW, Jake’s, Reliance, Seizmik and Highlifter.
Founded in 2008, Snorkel Your ATV LLC is an industry-leading manufacturer that engineers, fabricates and direct-sells over 160 proprietary accessories for ATVs and UTVs.
The primary products are snorkel kits allowing the ATV/UTV to operate with its engine underwater.
Snorkel Your ATV’s products allow ATV/UTV owners to customize, increase performance, and protect their vehicles.
source_title: Nivel Parts & Mfg. Acquires Snorkel Your ATV LLC - Nivel

Nivel is the leading manufacturer and supplier of branded aftermarket parts for personal transportation vehicles, utility terrain vehicles and heavy-duty equipment.
The Company offers over 100,000 SKUs in accessories and maintenance parts across the full vehicle lifecycle to a diversified customer base including dealers, distributors and consumers.
Aaron Sack serves as Managing Director and Head of Morgan Stanley Capital Partners.

The investment represents a continuation of MSCP’s focus on high-growth, consumer enthusiast companies with strong competitive advantages.

facts: OTR Wheel Engineering, Inc. (OTR) is officially unveiling new branding at CONEXPO-CON/AGG 2023.

The new branding includes a new trade name or DBA of “OTR Engineered Solutions,” as well as a new logo, tagline, website, and other branding elements rolled out to better reflect expanded capabilities as a global solutions provider.
Founded in 1987 to provide off-the-road wheels and tires to OEMs and the aftermarket, OTR’s vast tire portfolio includes bias, radial, semi-pneumatic, non-marking, and airless tires.

As its product line grew, OTR began providing value-added services for dealers and OEMs, including product development, sourcing, warehousing, tire mounting, foam filling, flat proofing, wheel inspection, certification, subassembly, and other customized solutions.
John Major, Director of Marketing, stated that thanks to a history of innovation, OTR today has much more to offer beyond wheels and tires.
OTR operates as a partner in the off-the-road market with 34 facilities located across North America, Europe, and Asia to better serve customers.
The company feels the “OTR Engineered Solutions” name and new branding elements better encompass its full capabilities given its evolution.
To complement the branding update, OTR has introduced the tagline “Roll More™.”
John Major credited the company’s success to both innovative products and an unmatched level of service and support.
The company provides services ranging from design and engineering to manufacturing and logistics, driven to help customers “Roll More.”
source_title: OTR unveils new branding at CONEXPO-CON/AGG 2023 - Material Handling Wholesaler

facts: Operates in the industrial machinery manufacturing sector and specializes in off-the-road tire and wheel solutions for original equipment manufacturers and aftermarket customers.
Provides value-added services including tire mounting, sequencing, warehousing, and subassembly for strategic OEM and aftermarket partners.
Serves markets across Construction, Lawn & Garden, Powersports, Agriculture, Forestry, Mining, Material Handling, and Specialty Vehicle.
Maintains a global footprint with 34 manufacturing facilities and warehouse locations across the United States, Canada, Europe, and Asia.
Is based at 195 Chatillon Road Northeast Suite 4, Rome, Georgia 30161, United States.
Employs between 201 and 500 people.
In 2025, expanded its footprint with a Phoenix facility becoming fully operational.
In 2025, expanded its Tulsa, Oklahoma operations.
In 2025, announced the launch of WorkBoss CT tires.
In 2025, announced the launch of the AI-powered TreadIQ platform.
